Name: Grauer & Weil (India) Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1957, Grauer & Weil (India) Ltd specializes in surface finishing solutions including electroplating, functional and decorative coatings, specialty chemicals, and automated plating systems and operates in adjacent engineering and mall segments. Its integrated model delivers customized corrosion protection across industrial, automotive, and aerospace verticals.

The company’s focus spans manufacturing high-performance coatings and systems alongside real estate ventures, positioning it as both a technical solutions provider and an infrastructure player with diversified operational touchpoints.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Grauer & Weil's versatile product portfolio and multi-sector footprint equip it to navigate industrial cycles. Stability in core margins and full-year delivery on steel or real estate demand could restore full-year profitability. Continued operational agility and leveraging its coatings leadership remain critical for maintaining growth momentum in the current fiscal year.

Name: Gulf Oil Lubricants India Ltd
Overview:
Gulf Oil Lubricants India Ltd (GOLIL), a subsidiary of the Hinduja Group, is a leading player in the Indian lubricants industry. The company specializes in manufacturing, marketing, and trading automotive and non-automotive lubricants. With a robust presence in both B2B and B2C segments, GOLIL serves a diverse customer base, including OEMs, fleet operators, and individual consumers. The company is recognized as one of the top three private lubricant companies in India.

GOLIL operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Silvassa and Chennai, equipped with advanced technology to produce a wide range of lubricants. The company's strategic initiatives, such as the UNLOCK 2.0 strategy, focus on expanding market penetration and enhancing product offerings across various segments. These efforts have contributed to GOLIL's growth and strengthened its position in the competitive Indian lubricants market.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company's focus on expanding its presence in the electric vehicle (EV) segment, strengthening OEM alliances, and investing in research and development positions it well for sustained growth. Additionally, GOLIL's emphasis on premium product categories and sustainable solutions aligns with market trends, supporting its long-term growth prospects.

Name: Foseco India Ltd
Overview:
Foseco India Ltd is a leading provider of foundry consumables and services, catering to the metal casting industry. The company offers a wide range of products including gating systems, filters, and coatings, aimed at improving the quality and efficiency of metal casting processes. Foseco India is part of the Vesuvius Group, a global leader in molten metal flow engineering.

In FY25, Foseco India reported a revenue of ₹583 crore, with a net profit of ₹81.9 crore. The company maintained a healthy dividend payout ratio of 33.1%, reflecting its strong financial position and commitment to shareholder returns.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Foseco India Ltd is strategically positioning itself for sustained growth by enhancing its product offerings and expanding its market presence. The company is focusing on innovation and sustainability to meet the evolving demands of the foundry industry. Foseco plans to drive growth by concentrating on key accounts, indirect channels, and continuous innovation. The company aims to collaborate closely with Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and key accounts to develop new and more complex castings, improve processes, reduce overall costs, and help achieve sustainability goals.

Name: Pidilite Industries Ltd
Overview:
Pidilite Industries Ltd is India’s leading maker of adhesives, sealants, construction chemicals, DIY and craftsmen products, with iconic brands like Fevicol and Fevikwik that dominate the market. Its strong R&D base and global presence support continuous innovation and expansion.
 
 Business Segments
 
 Consumer & Bazaar Products: Contribute 80% of revenue — includes household adhesives like Fevicol, Fevikwik, Fevistik, Dr. Fixit, and Fevicryl for retail, construction, and DIY use.
 
 B2B Products: Make up 20% of revenue — covers industrial adhesives, resins, pigments, and construction chemicals for institutional and industrial clients.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Pidilite is positioned for strong growth driven by a real estate rebound and rising urban and rural construction demand. Focus on premium, high-margin products, new launches, and category expansion into paints and industrial adhesives will diversify revenue streams. Digital sales and rural penetration are set to expand, while cost controls and raw material relief support stable margins, with EBITDA expected in the 20–24% range.

Name: Gulshan Polyols Ltd
Overview:
Gulshan Polyols Ltd is a leading manufacturer of specialty chemicals and polyols in India. Established in 1984, the company produces a wide range of polyols, solvents, and specialty chemicals for industries such as paints, coatings, adhesives, construction, and pharmaceuticals.

The company operates multiple manufacturing facilities with advanced production capabilities and adheres to international quality and environmental standards. Gulshan Polyols Ltd focuses on research and development to innovate new chemical solutions and expand its product applications across domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Gulshan Polyols Ltd is strategically expanding its ethanol production capacity to meet the growing demand for biofuels in India. The company has recently commissioned a 500 KLPD grain-based ethanol plant in Madhya Pradesh, one of India's largest units, with technical collaboration from LEAF by Lesaffre.

Name: Elantas Beck India Ltd
Overview:
ELANTAS Beck India Limited, part of the ALTANA Group, is a leading manufacturer of specialty chemicals serving the electrical insulation and construction industries. Its portfolio spans magnet wire enamels, secondary insulation varnishes, and specialty resins for electronics, auto electricals, filters, and high-voltage capacitors. The company also offers construction chemicals for industrial flooring, coatings, repairs, sealants, and adhesives.

Operating through two facilities in Pimpri, Maharashtra, and Ankleshwar, Gujarat, the company serves a wide range of end markets, including home appliances, transformers, automotive, and industrial equipment. Leveraging ALTANA’s global R&D network, ELANTAS focuses on new product development, process optimization, and aligning with emerging application trends in the insulation and specialty chemical space.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Strategic Outlook
Management remains focused on capacity enhancements, process modernization, and increasing penetration into high-margin construction chemicals and specialty resins. Leveraging ALTANA’s international R&D expertise, the company aims to roll out new, application-specific products targeting domestic and export markets in the coming quarters.

Name: Fineotex Chemical Ltd
Overview:
Fineotex Chemicals Ltd is a Mumbai-based specialty chemical manufacturer established in 1979. The company produces a diverse range of performance chemicals and auxiliaries catering to industries such as textiles, construction, water treatment, leather, fertilizers, and paints. Fineotex operates manufacturing facilities in Navi Mumbai, Ambernath, and Selangor, Malaysia, and is recognized for its commitment to quality and sustainability.

As a prominent player in the specialty chemicals sector, Fineotex has established a strong global presence. The company emphasizes research and development to innovate and meet the evolving needs of its clientele, ensuring high-quality standards and compliance with international certifications.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Fineotex Chemicals Ltd is focusing on expanding its product offerings and enhancing operational efficiencies to drive growth. The company is investing in new manufacturing capacities and exploring emerging markets to capitalize on opportunities in the specialty chemicals sector. With a strategic emphasis on innovation and sustainability, Fineotex aims to strengthen its market position and deliver long-term value to stakeholders.

Name: NOCIL Ltd
Overview:
NOCIL Ltd, established in 1961, is India’s leading manufacturer of rubber chemicals, serving primarily the tyre and broader rubber-processing industries. The company, part of the Arvind Mafatlal Group, is a key supplier of essential additives and accelerators that enhance rubber performance and application reliability.

Its operations span domestic production and exports, delivering specialized compounds crucial for the automotive, healthcare, and consumer segments. NOCIL’s position as the country’s largest rubber chemical maker underscores its extensive capabilities and critical role in the value chain.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Supported by its foundational leadership in rubber chemical manufacturing, NOCIL is well-positioned to navigate FY26’s challenges through strategic ramp-up of exports and protective policy measures. Sustained focus on innovation, cost optimization, and global customer penetration should help restore margin resilience and reinforce growth over the year ahead.

Name: Veedol Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Veedol Corporation Ltd., formerly known as Tide Water Oil Company Ltd., is a leading manufacturer and marketer of lubricants in India. The company offers a comprehensive range of automotive, industrial, and marine lubricants, catering to diverse sectors such as automotive, industrial, and marine applications.

With a strong distribution network and a commitment to quality, Veedol Corporation has established itself as a trusted brand in the lubricant industry, serving customers across the country.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Veedol Corporation plans to enhance its production capacities and invest in technological advancements to meet the increasing demand for quality lubricants. The company aims to strengthen its position in the market by focusing on customer satisfaction, operational excellence, and sustainable practices.

Name: S H Kelkar & Company Ltd
Overview:
S H Kelkar and Company Limited is India’s leading fragrance and aroma ingredients manufacturer, known for being at the forefront of innovation in the field. It produces a wide range of fragrance products, including novel aroma molecules, and holds the distinction of being the only Indian-origin firm to file patents in this domain.

Built on strong fundamentals, it supplies to personal care, home care, and food & beverage sectors across India and globally. The company's commitment to quality and R&D has solidified its reputation as a trusted name in fragrance and flavor solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

S H Kelkar and Company Limited is strategically expanding its global presence and product offerings. The company is investing approximately ₹200 crore in capital expenditure over the next two years to establish new and upgrade existing manufacturing facilities, aiming to enhance production capabilities and meet increasing demand. Additionally, S H Kelkar is focusing on reducing its net debt from ₹658 crore to around ₹550 crore, supported by insurance payouts and internal accruals.

Name: Castrol India Ltd
Overview:
Castrol India Ltd is a leading player in the manufacturing and marketing of automotive and industrial lubricants, as well as related services. It offers a comprehensive range of oils and fluids catering to passenger cars, motorcycles, commercial vehicles, and specialized industrial applications. The company also serves niche sectors like marine, energy, IT cooling, and data centres.

The company has a robust market position in India, supported by strong distribution and manufacturing capabilities. With three strategically located plants in Silvassa, Patalganga, and Paharpur, along with partnerships and collaborations such as Jio-BP and Jaguar TCS Racing, Castrol India is actively diversifying into high-growth segments, including premium auto care products and EV fluids.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Castrol India aims to deepen penetration in premium and EV fluid categories, supported by its strong parentage under BP Plc. Focus areas include expanding auto care offerings, leveraging the Jio-BP network for retail distribution, and capitalizing on the growing EV and hybrid markets in India.

Name: R K Swamy Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1973, R K Swamy Ltd (RKSL) is an integrated marketing services provider offering a full spectrum of solutions across advertising, media, market research, customer analytics, and marketing technology. It engages in advertising across multiple media channels, strategic media planning and buying, research and analytics, and customer data-driven campaigns.

Through its subsidiaries, Hansa Customer Equity Pvt Ltd (customer data analytics & MarTech) and Hansa Research Group Pvt Ltd (full-service market research), the company delivers end-to-end brand solutions to clients. It operates under the brands R K Swamy and Hansa, deriving revenues primarily from client marketing budgets, with a strong presence in advertising, digital media, customer experience management, and analytics.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management reiterated confidence in scaling new services, particularly consulting and CX-led offerings, while maintaining growth in established advertising and media verticals. AI-driven efficiencies, healthy new business pipelines, and marquee project opportunities are expected to support revenue momentum.

Name: Shivalik Rasayan Ltd
Overview:
Shivalik Rasayan Limited specializes in the production and distribution of agrochemicals, specialty chemicals, and intermediates, serving industries such as agriculture, pharmaceuticals, textiles, and plastics. Its product portfolio includes insecticides, herbicides, fungicides, and plant growth regulators, supported by state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and stringent quality control measures.

With a strong focus on research and development, Shivalik Rasayan continuously innovates to meet evolving customer needs. The company emphasizes sustainability and responsible practices, leveraging a robust distribution network to ensure product availability across domestic and international markets while establishing itself as a trusted provider of high-quality chemical solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Shivalik Rasayan Ltd is strategically expanding its presence in the agrochemical and pharmaceutical sectors. The company has commissioned a new agrochemical and specialty chemicals plant at Dahej, aiming to increase its Dimethoate production capacity from 1,450 MTPA to 5,000 MTPA. Additionally, Shivalik is developing new products such as Novaluron and Trifloxystrobin, with commercialization expected in the near future.

Name: Sharda Cropchem Ltd
Overview:
Sharda Cropchem Ltd is a rapidly growing global agrochemicals company specializing in the export of crop protection products across markets like Europe, NAFTA, and Latin America. With a strong base in technical-grade formulations, it focuses on developing product dossiers and navigating registrations in diverse geographies.

Operating on an asset-light model, the company also engages in non-agrochemical offerings like conveyor belts and dyes. Its strategic strength lies in deep domain expertise, IPR holdings, and infrastructure-light global penetration—positioning it to respond quickly to shifting demand dynamics.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Sharda Cropchem is positioned for continued growth driven by its extensive registration pipeline, global demand revival, and operational flexibility. With margins strengthened and financial resilience reinforced, the company seems set to sustain momentum through FY26, supported by smart capex deployment and broader market penetration.

Name: Deepak Fertilisers & Petrochemicals Corp Ltd
Overview:
Deepak Fertilisers and Petrochemicals Corporation Limited (DFPCL) is a diversified chemicals and fertilisers company engaged in the production of industrial chemicals, technical ammonium nitrate (TAN), and crop nutrition products. With a strong presence across fertilisers, agri-services, mining chemicals, and real estate, the company has developed an integrated value chain from raw materials to finished products.

Over the years, DFPCL has strengthened its market position through backward integration, specialty product development, and proximity to end customers. Its operations span domestic and international markets, supported by a robust manufacturing footprint on both the east and west coasts of India, enabling logistical advantages and cost efficiencies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Deepak Fertilisers and Petrochemicals Corporation Ltd (DFPCL) is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to bolster its market position and drive long-term growth. The company is expanding its production capabilities, including a significant backward integration project at its Dahej plant in Gujarat, which aims to enhance in-house nitric acid production.

Name: UPL Ltd
Overview:
UPL is a global company engaged in agrochemicals, industrial chemicals, chemical intermediates, specialty chemicals, and seeds. With operations across more than 138 countries, it ranks as the world’s fifth-largest agrochemical and fourth-largest seed manufacturer. The company has access to 90% of the world’s food basket and generates over $5 billion in annual revenue, positioning itself as a key player in global agriculture.

The company operates across the entire agriculture value chain, offering seeds, crop protection, and post-harvest solutions. With a portfolio exceeding 13,600 products, UPL has launched several innovative offerings including Triskele and Trishuk in India, and Evolution and Shenzi in Brazil and Mexico. More than 80% of its revenue comes from branded products as it transitions from a generic post-patent model toward differentiated, sustainable solutions. With over 30 R&D facilities and a research spend of ~3% of sales, UPL continues to invest in innovative, sustainable agricultural practices to serve farmers worldwide.

Beyond products, UPL is advancing sustainability through its CFI 2.0 plan (2022–2025), focusing on cocoa farming communities. Its initiatives include training programs in Côte d’Ivoire and Ghana, aiming to train 45,000 farmers and enroll 26,500 in savings and loan associations by 2025. These programs strengthen both farming practices and community resilience, underscoring UPL’s mission of sustainable agricultural transformation.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

UPL is aiming for steady growth by shifting its focus toward sustainable and differentiated products, backed by strong demand recovery and improved volumes across its businesses. Alongside this, the company is pushing efficiency through cost-conscious expansion and debt reduction, strengthening its financial health. To stay ahead, UPL is investing in advanced technologies like AI-driven digital platforms and precision agriculture tools, enabling smarter farming and faster product development.

Name: P I Industries Ltd
Overview:
PI Industries Ltd is a leading integrated player in the agro-chemicals sector with a strong domestic and export presence. The company has state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Gujarat and is recognized for its innovation-led approach, backed by robust R&D and engineering capabilities.

Over the years, PI has diversified from agri-sciences into a broader life sciences platform, expanding into pharmaceuticals, CRDMO, biologicals, and electronic chemicals. With a healthy order pipeline, strategic acquisitions, and focus on sustainability, it is positioning itself for long-term growth despite near-term market headwinds.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims for mid-single-digit revenue growth in FY26 with stable ~25% EBITDA margins. Growth in H2 FY26 is expected to be stronger as global pricing pressures ease. Strategic focus will remain on expanding biologicals globally, scaling the pharma CRDMO platform, and commercializing innovative products, while maintaining healthy returns and strong cash generation.

Name: Bayer CropScience Ltd
Overview:
Bayer CropScience operates in the agri-care sector, offering a comprehensive range of crop protection chemicals, seeds, and related agricultural solutions. The company’s portfolio includes insecticides, fungicides, herbicides, and hybrid seeds, with a strong focus on enhancing crop yields and quality. Leveraging research-led innovation and a wide distribution network, it caters to the needs of both smallholder and commercial farmers.

The business is structured to address diverse crop protection needs across geographies and cropping cycles. It combines global R&D strengths with localized product development, supported by advisory services that promote sustainable farming practices. By integrating high-quality seeds with crop protection and digital tools, Bayer CropScience aims to deliver holistic farm productivity solutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to sustain growth in FY26 through portfolio expansion, scaling of high-margin products, and deeper penetration in underserved markets. A favorable cropping season outlook, coupled with new product launches and operational efficiency measures, is expected to drive double-digit growth while maintaining strong margins.

Name: BASF India Ltd
Overview:
Sanofi India Limited is one of the leading multinational pharmaceutical companies in the Indian market, with operations spanning seven therapeutic areas, including diabetes, cardiology, thrombosis, central nervous system, and anti-histamines. It is majority-owned by Sanofi Global and its wholly owned subsidiary Hoechst GmbH, holding a combined 60.40% stake. The company follows a calendar-year financial cycle in line with its parent. Its portfolio includes 32 brands, five of which are ranked among the top 300 in the Indian Pharmaceutical Market (IPM), and it serves over 100,000 pharmacies via 3,000 distributors, reaching 28 countries.

The company operates a manufacturing facility in Goa with over 5 billion tablets annual capacity, of which 61% is exported. This facility produces key drugs such as paracetamol-codeine combinations, Depakine, Ramipril, Fexofenadine, and Glimepiride, and is the only plant outside Frankfurt authorized to manufacture Ramipril PC granules. Sanofi India has recently undertaken significant restructuring, including divesting its nutraceuticals business, demerging its consumer healthcare arm into a wholly owned subsidiary, and forging strategic partnerships with Cipla and Emcure to expand reach in CNS and cardiovascular therapies, respectively.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company aims to sustain profitable growth through capacity expansion, operational efficiencies, and deeper customer engagement. Continued investment in high-growth product lines, along with favorable macro drivers like automotive and construction demand, positions BASF India to capture domestic market opportunities. However, margin improvement will depend on stabilizing input costs and optimizing product mix.

Name: Dhanuka Agritech Ltd
Overview:
Dhanuka Agritech Limited is one of India’s leading agrochemical companies, engaged in manufacturing a diverse range of crop protection solutions. Its portfolio covers herbicides, insecticides, fungicides, and plant growth regulators available in multiple formulations such as liquids, powders, granules, and dust. The company serves farmers across India through its strong distribution network and continues to collaborate with global agrochemical majors for technology access.

With four manufacturing plants, NABL-accredited R&D labs, and a robust sales and marketing infrastructure, Dhanuka reaches over 10 million farmers. Its business model emphasizes premium brands, product innovation, and partnerships to bring novel solutions to Indian agriculture. The company has over 300 product registrations and continues to strengthen its market presence through launches and international expansion.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Dhanuka expects growth to be driven by new launches, recovery in fungicides and insecticides aided by rainfall, and increasing paddy acreage. Exports will gain traction as Dahej products and Bayer brands expand globally. While margins may see near-term compression, the long-term outlook remains positive with innovation, partnerships, and a balanced domestic–international portfolio.

Name: NACL Industries Ltd
Overview:
NACL Industries Ltd is a leading integrated agrochemical company engaged in manufacturing and marketing of technicals and formulations across domestic and international markets. The company operates with a diversified portfolio of insecticides, fungicides, herbicides, and plant growth regulators, covering major crops and farmer needs. Its products are distributed across more than 55,000 retail counters in India and exported to over 30 countries.

With strong manufacturing capabilities, including four plants and upcoming brownfield and greenfield expansions, NACL has built a reliable position as a contract manufacturer for global MNCs while also strengthening its own brand presence overseas. The company’s customer-focused R&D, extensive registrations, and strategic shift towards higher-value formulations support its long-term growth aspirations.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management guided for sustained margin improvement through product mix realignment, operating leverage from new capacities, and increasing penetration in international branded markets (Southeast Asia & Africa). With strong promoter backing from Coromandel International, NACL is positioned to accelerate growth and improve profitability in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Dharmaj Crop Guard Ltd
Overview:
Dharmaj Crop Guard Ltd operates in the agrochemical industry, manufacturing and marketing insecticides, fungicides, herbicides, and plant growth regulators. The company has built a strong distribution network across India, catering to farmers through retail outlets and channel partners.

In addition to domestic sales, Dharmaj also exports formulations and technical products to multiple international markets. With a balanced portfolio of branded formulations and B2B supplies, the company maintains a resilient position in the agri-input sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
With India’s agriculture sector expected to sustain steady demand, Dharmaj is positioned to benefit from product launches and exports. Margin expansion may come from backward integration and scale efficiencies in FY26.

Name: Rallis India Ltd
Overview:
Rallis India, with a legacy spanning over 150 years, is a prominent agri-inputs player offering a wide spectrum of solutions from crop protection and seeds to organic plant growth nutrients and contract manufacturing. The firm serves Indian farmers with innovations and strengthens its domestic and international reach with high-value offerings.

In FY 25, Rallis balanced legacy operations with strategic innovation, particularly in soil & plant health solutions and new digital initiatives. The company enters FY 26 with renewed momentum marked by strong Q1 performance and a dual focus on expanding its domestic franchise and strengthening its export and custom manufacturing capabilities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With strong traction and bolstered domestic performance, Rallis is positioned for steady FY 26 growth. Key drivers include new seeds launches (e.g., cotton hybrids for central and southern India), continued strength in Soil & Plant Health segment, controlled inventory in agriculture channels, and scaling of export-oriented products like Metalaxyl-M. Cost discipline, digital adoption, and portfolio innovation are expected to underpin earnings momentum.

Name: Excel Industries Ltd
Overview:
Excel Industries Ltd is a specialty chemical company engaged in manufacturing agrochemicals, industrial chemicals, and custom chemical solutions. Established in 1980, the company supplies products to domestic and international customers.

The company’s offerings cater to agrochemical, textile, paint, and polymer industries. Excel Industries Ltd emphasizes R&D for innovation, regulatory compliance, and quality manufacturing.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Excel Industries Ltd is strategically expanding its footprint in the specialty chemicals sector, with a focus on agrochemicals, biocides, and environmental solutions. The company has secured a five-year contract with a multinational corporation to supply a niche chemical, projected to generate ₹50–70 crore in revenue, marking its entry into contract manufacturing.

Name: Uniphos Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
Uniphos Enterprises Ltd, established in 1969, is an Indian company engaged in the trading of chemicals and other products. The company primarily focuses on trading ethylenediamine, a chemical compound used in various industrial applications.

Headquartered in Vapi, Gujarat, Uniphos Enterprises operates with a lean workforce and has a presence in two Indian states. The company's trading activities are currently at a modest scale, with plans to explore new opportunities both domestically and internationally.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Uniphos Enterprises aims to leverage its investment in UPL Ltd and explore new trading opportunities to enhance its revenue streams. The company's focus on expanding its trading activities and managing its investment portfolio positions it for potential growth in the coming years.

Name: Gujarat Narmada Valley Fertilizers & Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat Narmada Valley Fertilizers & Chemicals Limited (GNFC) operates primarily in two key segments: Chemicals and Fertilizers, with a minor presence in IT services. The company manufactures bulk industrial chemicals and fertilizers used widely across industries, focusing on specialty chemical production and operational efficiency. GNFC benefits from its strategic location in Bharuch and strong government backing, enabling sustained operations and project execution.

The company’s business faced challenges due to an extended shutdown of its TDI-II plant impacting production and profits, yet overall turnover remained stable year-on-year. GNFC is investing heavily in capital expenditure projects aimed at improving capacity and operational contributions, including power plants and process optimizations, signaling a focus on growth and cost efficiencies going forward.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company targets ramping up TDI production to near installed capacity in FY26 with no major shutdowns planned. Capital projects, especially the power plant and nitric acid facility, are expected to enhance margins and drive growth. Continuous process improvements and investment in import-substitute products position GNFC for steady expansion and improved profitability over the medium term.

Name: GSP Crop Science Ltd
Overview:
GSP Crop Science Ltd is an agrochemical company engaged in the development, manufacturing and distribution of crop protection products used in agriculture. Its product portfolio includes insecticides, herbicides, fungicides, plant growth regulators and fertilizers, which help farmers improve crop yield and protect crops from pests, diseases and weeds. The company operates across the entire agrochemical value chain, producing both technical-grade active ingredients and finished formulations.

The company has a strong manufacturing base with multiple facilities and in-house research and development capabilities, allowing it to create innovative and effective agricultural solutions. It serves customers across India as well as international markets, exporting its products to several countries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

GSP Crop Science aims to expand its product portfolio by developing new agrochemical molecules and advanced formulations to meet evolving agricultural needs. The company plans to increase its manufacturing capacity and strengthen backward integration to improve cost efficiency and margins. It is also focusing on expanding its global presence by increasing exports and entering new international markets.

Name: Best Agrolife Ltd
Overview:
Best Agrolife Limited, formerly Sahyog Multibase Limited, is a leading agrochemical company in India and ranks among the top 15 players in the sector. The company manufactures and markets technicals, formulations, intermediates, and public health products, with a diversified portfolio of more than 490 formulations and 115+ technical licenses. Its offerings span herbicides, insecticides, fungicides, and plant growth regulators, catering to domestic as well as global markets.

With three manufacturing facilities across India and a strong distribution network of 8,400 distributors and 10,000+ dealers, Best Agrolife reaches over 1.5 million farmers across 21 states. The company is transitioning from institutional sales towards branded and patented products, aiming to capture higher margins and long-term brand loyalty. Alongside domestic dominance, it is expanding aggressively into export markets with registrations across 90+ countries.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management acknowledged challenges such as seasonal uncertainty, monsoon variability, and long registration cycles abroad. However, they remain confident of sustained margin expansion through higher patented product share, cost control, and stronger brand pull. The focus is on building predictable, industry-comparable performance to attract institutional investors and strengthen long-term market credibility.

Name: Punjab Chemicals & Crop Protection Ltd
Overview:
Punjab Chemicals & Crop Protection Ltd is a leading manufacturer of agrochemicals in India. The company offers a range of products, including insecticides, fungicides, herbicides, and plant growth regulators, catering to the agricultural sector.

Punjab Chemicals & Crop Protection Ltd's products are designed to enhance crop yield and protect plants from pests and diseases, contributing to sustainable agriculture.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Punjab Chemicals & Crop Protection Ltd aims to expand its product portfolio and enhance manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand in the agrochemical industry. The company is also focusing on sustainability and technological advancements to stay competitive.

Name: Heranba Industries Ltd
Overview:
Heranba Industries Ltd is a leading agrochemical company in India, specializing in the manufacture and sale of crop protection chemicals. The company offers a wide range of products, including insecticides, herbicides, fungicides, and plant growth regulators.

Established in 1992, Heranba Industries Ltd has grown to become a prominent player in the agrochemical industry. The company's products are used by farmers to protect crops and enhance agricultural productivity.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Heranba Industries Ltd aims to expand its product portfolio and enhance manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand in the agrochemical industry. The company is also focusing on sustainability and technological advancements to stay competitive in the market.

Name: India Pesticides Ltd
Overview:
India Pesticides Ltd is an agrochemical manufacturer focused on producing technical-grade pesticides and related products. Specializing in key chemicals such as folpet and thiocarbamate herbicides, the company has scaled up to become one of the world's top producers in these segments.

Drawing on robust R&D capabilities and global distribution, India Pesticides serves both domestic and international agricultural markets. Its technicals and formulation divisions contribute to a diverse product mix, positioning the company as a vital supplier in the global agrochemical landscape.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, India Pesticides has earmarked around ₹116 crore for capex to bolster production capacity and scale operations. With rising capacity utilization, expanding exports, and strengthening margins, the company appears poised for continued momentum in the year ahead.

Name: Bhagiradha Chemicals & Industries Ltd
Overview:
Bhagiradha Chemicals & Industries Ltd (BHAGCHEM), established in 1993 and headquartered in Hyderabad, is a prominent Indian agrochemical company. The company specializes in the manufacturing of a diverse range of crop protection products, including insecticides, fungicides, and herbicides. With a strong commitment to research and development, Bhagiradha Chemicals aims to provide innovative solutions to enhance agricultural productivity.

Under the leadership of CEO K.S. Raju, the company has expanded its operations to serve both domestic and international markets. Bhagiradha Chemicals is listed on the NSE under the ticker symbol BHAGCHEM. The company focuses on sustainable practices and aims to contribute significantly to the agricultural sector's growth and development.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Bhagiradha Chemicals plans to enhance its production capabilities through strategic capital expenditures. Phase I of the planned expansion is expected to become operational by June/July 2025, with a 12–18-month ramp-up period. The full impact of this phase is projected to be realized in FY27, positioning the company for sustained growth and increased market share in the agrochemical industry.

Name: Insecticides India Ltd
Overview:
Insecticides (India) Ltd (IIL) is a prominent Indian agrochemical company specializing in the manufacturing of technicals, formulations, biological products, and household insecticides. Established in 1996, the company has developed a robust portfolio catering to both the B2C and B2B segments.

IIL's operations encompass direct sales to end consumers, supply of technicals and formulations to other companies, and exports to over 30 countries, supported by more than 75 product registrations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
IIL aims to achieve a 10% revenue growth in FY26, driven by its premium product strategy and expansion into new markets. The company plans to launch approximately a dozen new products, with two entering the Focused Maharatnas range, indicating strong product innovation.

Name: Advance Agrolife Ltd
Overview:
Advance Agrolife Limited is an agrochemical company engaged in the manufacturing and distribution of a wide range of technical-grade and formulated agrochemical products. Its portfolio includes insecticides, herbicides, fungicides, plant growth regulators and other crop protection solutions used to improve agricultural productivity. The company supplies its products to farmers, distributors and agricultural businesses, helping them protect crops from pests, diseases and weeds.

The company operates manufacturing and formulation facilities supported by research and development capabilities that allow it to develop effective agrochemical solutions. Advance Agrolife focuses on maintaining product quality and regulatory compliance while building a strong distribution network across agricultural regions. Its diversified agrochemical product range enables it to cater to different crop segments and farming requirements.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Advance Agrolife plans to expand its product portfolio by introducing new agrochemical formulations and technical products that address evolving crop protection needs. The company aims to strengthen its distribution network and deepen its presence in key agricultural markets to reach a larger base of farmers. It is also focusing on enhancing manufacturing capacity and investing in research to develop more efficient and sustainable crop protection solutions.

Name: Bharat Rasayan Ltd
Overview:
Bharat Rasayan Ltd, established in 1989, is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in technical-grade pesticides and intermediates for the agrochemical industry. The company offers a comprehensive range of products, including insecticides, herbicides, fungicides, and key intermediates, serving both domestic and international markets. Bharat Rasayan's commitment to quality and innovation has positioned it as a trusted partner in the agricultural sector.

With a strong focus on research and development, Bharat Rasayan continually enhances its product offerings to meet the evolving needs of the agrochemical industry. The company's state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and adherence to stringent quality standards ensure the delivery of high-performance solutions to its clientele.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Bharat Rasayan is poised for continued growth, driven by its strategic focus on expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company's commitment to research and development, coupled with its strong market presence, positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the agrochemical industry. With a focus on innovation and sustainability, Bharat Rasayan aims to strengthen its leadership position and deliver long-term value to stakeholders.

Name: Astec Lifesciences Ltd
Overview:
Astec Lifesciences Ltd., established in 1994 and headquartered in Mumbai, is a prominent player in the agrochemical industry. The company specializes in manufacturing agrochemical active ingredients (technical), bulk formulations, intermediates, and specialty chemicals. 

Astec operates primarily in the business-to-business (B2B) sector, catering to clients globally, including Europe, Japan, and the United States. With a strong emphasis on research and development, Astec has developed capabilities to undertake custom synthesis projects, enhancing its service offerings.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Astec Lifesciences is focusing on expanding its product portfolio and enhancing its research and development capabilities. The company aims to strengthen its position in the global agrochemical market by leveraging its expertise in custom synthesis and active ingredient manufacturing.

Name: Arfin India Ltd
Overview:
Arfin India Ltd, incorporated in 1992 and headquartered in Ahmedabad, is a manufacturer of aluminum-based products serving diverse industrial segments. The company’s product portfolio includes aluminum wire rods, conductor & cable, de-oxidized wire, and ferro alloys, which are extensively used in power transmission, automotive, steel, and engineering industries. Through consistent investments in advanced manufacturing technology, Arfin has established itself as a reliable supplier with strong process control and quality standards.

Its state-of-the-art plant, supported by in-house testing and R&D, allows the company to cater to both domestic and export markets. With India’s growing demand for aluminum conductors in power infrastructure and increasing use of aluminum in transportation, Arfin continues to expand its reach while maintaining sustainability initiatives in energy-efficient production.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Arfin India Ltd is actively pursuing expansion in the non-ferrous metals sector, with plans to increase its installed capacity by 60% over the next decade through strategic partnerships and technological advancements. The company has secured a ₹25.98 crore order for aluminum deoxidizer products, indicating strong demand in the steel sector.

Name: Jain Resource Recycling Ltd
Overview:
Jain Resource Recycling Limited, established in 2022, is a prominent player in India's non-ferrous metal recycling industry. The company specializes in the recycling and manufacturing of lead, copper, and aluminum products, including ingots and alloys. Operating from three state-of-the-art facilities in Gummidipoondi, Chennai, Jain Resource Recycling processes a variety of metal scraps, such as copper (birch, druid), lead (radio, relay, rains, rinks), and aluminum (tread, talon, tense).

As part of the Jain Metal Group, the company leverages over 70 years of experience in the recycling sector. It serves a diverse clientele across various industries, including lead-acid batteries, electrical and electronics, pigments, and automotive sectors. Notable clients encompass Vedanta Limited-Sterlite Copper, Luminous Power Technologies, Yash Resources Recycling, Mitsubishi Corporation RtM Japan, and Nissan Trading Co.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jain Resource Recycling is poised for significant expansion, aiming to diversify its operations by venturing into the recycling of plastics, solar panels, automotive tires, brass e-waste, and copper-aluminum radiator scrap. This strategic diversification is expected to enhance its product portfolio and market reach. The company plans to bolster its manufacturing capabilities by establishing new units focused on both ferrous and non-ferrous segments, including the production of ingots, billets, wires, and tubes.

Name: Ratnaveer Precision Engineering Ltd
Overview:
Ratnaveer Precision Engineering Ltd is engaged in the manufacturing and supply of stainless-steel products including washers, pipes, tubes, and sheets. The company serves multiple industries such as automobiles, construction, kitchenware, and renewable energy, offering products with wide applications in both domestic and international markets. Its ability to produce customized stainless-steel products has allowed it to secure a differentiated positioning among its peers.

Over the years, Ratnaveer has focused on expanding its product mix and export presence to strengthen its growth trajectory. With strong manufacturing facilities and a growing network of global clients, the company is well-positioned to leverage the rising demand for stainless steel in infrastructure and industrial development. The business continues to focus on improving efficiencies, capacity utilization, and value-added products to maintain healthy margins.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Ratnaveer is expected to enhance its global footprint with higher exports and deeper penetration into industrial applications. The company is also planning to expand capacity and launch more value-added stainless-steel offerings, which will likely support margin growth. Its strong balance sheet provides flexibility for future expansion and competitiveness.

Name: Hindustan Zinc Ltd
Overview:
Hindustan Zinc Ltd (HZL), a subsidiary of the Vedanta Group, is India’s only integrated producer of zinc, lead, and silver. With over 50 years of experience, HZL commands an 80% market share in the Indian primary zinc sector and is globally recognized as one of the lowest-cost zinc producers. Its operations span mining, smelting, refining, and exporting, supported by significant backward integration and vast high-grade reserves.
 
 Latest Revenue & Business Segments
 Total Revenue (FY2024-25):
 ₹34,083 crore from operations.
 
 Exports:
 Account for approximately 25% of turnover, roughly ₹8,521 crore.
 
 Product Contributions:
 
 Zinc: Core product, comprising the lion’s share of revenue.
 
 Lead and Silver: Significant secondary contributors (precise split not disclosed for FY25, but silver remains a key high-margin business line).
 
 Market Position:
 
 80% share in Indian zinc mining.
 
 2nd largest zinc-lead smelter global.
 
 6th largest global silver producer.
 
 Key Business Metrics & Capabilities
 Mined Metal Capacity: 1.2 million tonnes per annum (MTPA)
 
 Zinc-Lead Smelting Capacity: 1.123 MTPA
 
 Silver Refining Capacity: 800 tonnes
 
 Mine Life: 25 years, backed by 448 million tonnes in reserves/resources.
 
 Operating Margins: Historical OPM around 51%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
HZL’s growth is driven by significant capacity expansion, with over ₹12,000 crore invested to enhance refining, smelting, and mining capabilities, supporting higher output and margin resilience. Product diversification into zinc- and lead-alloys, supported by ongoing R&D, broadens its portfolio and adds value. Resource security is ensured through continuous exploration, extending operational mine life and scalability.

Name: National Aluminium Company Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1981, National Aluminium Company Limited (NALCO) is a Navratna CPSE under the Ministry of Mines. The company operates one of Asia’s largest integrated Bauxite-Alumina-Aluminium-Power-Coal complexes. It is engaged in the manufacturing and sale of alumina and aluminium, with an expansive portfolio including aluminium ingots, wire rods, billets, and various alumina-based chemical products.

NALCO has strategically located operations and infrastructure, with in-house capabilities across mining (bauxite), refining (alumina), smelting (aluminium), captive power generation, and port-based logistics. It has maintained a low-cost position globally in both bauxite and alumina production. The company achieved its highest-ever revenue and profit in FY25.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

National Aluminium Company Limited (NALCO) is embarking on an ambitious ₹30,000 crore expansion plan aimed at significantly enhancing its aluminium production and power generation capacities by 2030. Key initiatives include increasing aluminium refining capacity by 1 million tonnes at its Angul facility, expanding smelting capacity by 0.5 million tonnes, and setting up a 1,080 MW captive coal-fired power plant. The company is also focusing on sustainable growth by investing in renewable energy projects, including 200–300 MW of solar and wind power capacity within the next three years

Name: MMP Industries Ltd
Overview:
MMP Industries Ltd is engaged in the manufacture and sale of aluminum products including powders, foils, and pastes. The company serves industries like chemicals, construction, packaging, defense, and automotive. It has diversified product applications, giving it resilience against cyclicality in individual industries.

The company also has a joint venture with Toyo Aluminium, Japan, which adds technological expertise and strengthens its position in specialty aluminum products. With its diversified portfolio, MMP is positioned to benefit from both domestic demand and exports.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
The company is scaling its specialty product portfolio, with investments in powders, foils, and conductor divisions to sustain growth momentum. MMP is positioned to capture opportunities in high-margin specialty applications and premium global markets, ensuring sustainable growth over the medium term.

Name: KSH International Ltd
Overview:
KSH International Limited is an Indian manufacturer of specialised magnet winding wires and related conductor products used in a wide range of electrical and industrial applications. It produces enamelled copper and aluminium winding wires, paper-insulated rectangular wires and continuously transposed conductors that serve as critical components in transformers, motors, generators, alternators and other capital goods used across power, renewables, railways, automotive and appliance industries. 

In addition to its strong domestic presence, KSH International has established a global footprint by exporting its magnet winding wires to multiple countries across regions like North America, Europe, the Middle East and Asia. It operates several manufacturing facilities in Maharashtra with advanced production capabilities and certifications that help it deliver customised solutions at scale.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

KSH International plans to expand production capacity further with new facilities and upgraded machinery to capture growing demand from power transmission, renewables, industrial and electric mobility sectors. It aims to broaden its product portfolio with higher-value specialised conductors and enhance export penetration into existing and new international markets. The company will continue investing in technology, quality systems and in-house innovation to maintain competitive advantage and meet evolving OEM requirements.

Name: Hindustan Copper Ltd
Overview:
Hindustan Copper Ltd (HCL), incorporated in 1967, is India’s only vertically integrated copper producer operating from mining to refining. It was carved out of NMDC and remains a government-owned PSU playing a strategic role in India’s mineral ecosystem. The company controls over 80% of the nation’s copper ore reserves.

HCL operates across five major plants located in Maharashtra, Gujarat, Jharkhand, Madhya Pradesh, and Rajasthan. Its product basket includes copper cathodes, copper rods, and concentrates, along with by-products like anode slime, sulphuric acid, and nickel hydroxide. Its diversified operations support India’s copper needs in both industrial and emerging sectors like EVs and renewables.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Hindustan Copper is gearing up for strong future growth by significantly boosting its mining output and modernizing operations. It’s investing about ₹2,000 crore in expanding output and improving mining infrastructure, including underground facilities and beneficiation plants, to increase capacity from around 4 million to over 12 million tonnes per annum. The company is deepening technical collaboration with Chile’s Codelco to enhance exploration and mining efficiencies, while also exploring critical and rare-earth mineral opportunities through partnerships with domestic firms.

Name: Hindalco Industries Ltd
Overview:
Hindalco Industries Ltd., part of the Aditya Birla Group, is a leading global producer of aluminium and copper products. It is the world’s largest aluminium rolling and recycling company and one of Asia’s top primary aluminium producers. The company operates an integrated value chain with strong domestic and global footprints, supported by Novelis, its international subsidiary.
 
Business Segments ):
 
 Novelis (Subsidiary): Contributes ~65% of total revenue — focuses on rolled aluminium products and recycling, with 33 overseas units across the US, Brazil, South Korea, and more.
 
 Copper: Accounts for ~19% of revenue — operates one of the world’s largest custom copper smelters, producing cathodes and copper rods.
 
 Aluminium (India Operations): Contributes ~16% of revenue — covers primary aluminium, flat rolled products, extrusions, and downstream light gauge products.
 
 Chemicals (within Aluminium): Includes calcined alumina and hydrates used in ceramics, grinding media, and water treatment.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
The company is set for robust expansion with a ₹2,000 crore investment in India’s first copper and e-waste recycling plant and a ₹2,000 crore project with Indian Railways supporting Vande Bharat trains and green logistics. Domestic capacities will be further enhanced through a ₹5,000 crore capex over FY24–FY25, while Novelis pursues a $1.8 billion global expansion across the US, Brazil, and South Korea.

Name: Talbros Automotive Components Ltd
Overview:
Talbros Automotive Components Ltd., established in 1956, is a prominent player in the Indian auto components industry. The company specializes in manufacturing automotive and industrial gaskets, heat shields, forgings, suspension systems, anti-vibration components, and hoses. It operates through various joint ventures and has a significant presence in both domestic and international markets.

Headquartered in Gurgaon, Talbros serves a diverse clientele, including original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), the aftermarket sector, and exports. The company is strategically expanding its product portfolio to include components for electric vehicles (EVs) and heat shields, aiming to capitalize on emerging automotive trends.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Talbros aims to achieve a 15% revenue growth with a target turnover of ₹1,450 crore. The company is focusing on expanding its presence in the electric vehicle sector and increasing exports to approximately 22% of total revenues by FY27. Additionally, Talbros plans to grow its heat shield business to around ₹90 crore per annum by FY27.

Name: Munjal Showa Ltd
Overview:
Munjal Showa Ltd, established in 1985, is a prominent auto component manufacturer specializing in the production of shock absorbers and struts for both two-wheelers and four-wheelers. A part of the Hero Group, the company operates through a joint venture with Showa Corporation, Japan, leveraging advanced technology and expertise to deliver high-quality products. 

Munjal Showa caters to major automotive manufacturers, including Hero MotoCorp, Maruti Suzuki, Honda, and Yamaha, both in India and internationally.  The company's manufacturing facilities are equipped with state-of-the-art machinery and adhere to stringent quality standards, ensuring the production of reliable and durable automotive components.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Munjal Showa Ltd aims to strengthen its position in the automotive component industry by focusing on innovation, quality enhancement, and market expansion. The company plans to invest in research and development to introduce new products and improve existing ones, catering to the evolving needs of the automotive sector.

Name: Munjal Auto Industries Ltd
Overview: Munjal Auto Industries Ltd (MAIL) is a leading auto component manufacturing company in India. The company produces exhaust systems, complete spoke rims, and other components for the automotive industry. MAIL is accredited with TS 16949 and ISO 14001 certifications, ensuring adherence to international quality standards.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Munjal Auto Industries Ltd is strategically expanding its product portfolio to include advanced composite molds and components, catering to diverse industries beyond its traditional auto sector. The company is investing in state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and adopting cutting-edge technologies to enhance production capabilities and meet the growing demand for high-quality composite products.

Name: Belrise Industries Ltd
Overview:
Belrise Industries Ltd is a vertically integrated Tier-1 automotive components manufacturer, producing an extensive suite of offerings for two-, three-, and four-wheeler vehicles. With annual revenues nearing ₹7,500 crore and over 15 manufacturing facilities across India, the company serves over 27 OEMs domestically and internationally. Its diversified portfolio includes metal chassis systems, polymer parts, suspension and mirror systems, foundry components, and emerging e-mobility and solar-related subsystems.

Rooted in engineering excellence and agile manufacturing, Belrise has steadily expanded through product innovation and strategic acquisitions, aligning itself with both traditional ICE vehicles and the growing electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Focus areas include higher-margin EV parts, solar energy-linked systems, and exports to global OEMs. Strategic partnerships with international players could further accelerate its transition to a global supplier of choice.

Name: Alicon Castalloy Ltd
Overview:
Alicon Castalloy is a leading manufacturer of aluminum castings, specializing in cylinder heads, intake manifolds, engine brackets, motor housings, battery trays, and other critical components. The company is a pioneer in Low-Pressure Die Casting (LPDC) and Gravity Die Casting (GDC) technologies, with a strong presence across ICE and EV platforms. Its business spans auto (2W, PV, CV, 3W) and non-auto industries, serving marquee OEMs in over 18 countries.

Headquartered in India with four manufacturing plants, including one in Slovakia, the company has a diversified customer base of over 97 clients across automotive, infrastructure, defense, aerospace, medical, and energy sectors. Alicon’s order book exceeds ₹9,000 crore, ensuring long-term revenue visibility, while its investments in R&D and digital manufacturing reinforce its transition into high-value, technology-agnostic structural parts and EV solutions.
Key Takeways:
Outlook & Guidance
Alicon expects growth to resume, with cost optimization and scaling driving margin gains. The long-term vision includes expanding into defense, aerospace, and railways (DAR vertical), broadening structural parts offerings, and strengthening export share. Management reiterated confidence in delivering improved performance in coming quarters but acknowledged near-term uncertainty due to tariffs and supply chain volatility.

Name: Automotive Stampings & Assemblies Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1990, Automotive Stampings and Assemblies Ltd (ASAL), a subsidiary of Tata AutoComp Systems Ltd (TACO), is engaged in the manufacturing of sheet metal stampings, welded assemblies, battery tray assemblies, aluminum cooling tubes, and heavy fabricated parts for passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, electric vehicles, and off-road equipment.

With five operational facilities across Pune, Uttarakhand, Gujarat, and Jharkhand, ASAL has strategically aligned its production with the rising demand in the EV segment while supporting Tata Motors’ leadership across commercial and passenger vehicle markets. The company also serves leading OEMs and Tier-1 suppliers, ensuring a diversified and robust customer base.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking ahead, the company will continue to focus on EV component opportunities, particularly lightweight solutions and precision assemblies. Capacity expansion in fabrication and advanced welding positions ASAL for incremental growth. Supported by OEM demand, debt-light balance sheet, and process automation, management expects sustained profitability and scalability in the coming years.

Name: Steel Strips Wheels Ltd
Overview:
Steel Strips Wheels Ltd is a prominent manufacturer of steel and alloy wheel rims for a wide range of vehicles—including two-wheelers, passenger cars, tractors, commercial vehicles, and off-road equipment. The company operates state-of-the-art facilities across India and serves both domestic and export markets.

Known for its engineering excellence, it also supplies components for electric and premium vehicles, leveraging vertical integration and strong OEM partnerships to maintain quality leadership.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Steel Strips Wheels is targeting high-teens growth in FY26, underpinned by alloy wheel demand, export diversification, and capacity expansion—including alloy wheels and aluminum knuckles. An improved debt-to-equity ratio reinforces financial resilience, positioning the company well for continued momentum and premium mix gains in the upcoming year.

Name: Wheels India Ltd
Overview:
Wheels India Limited, part of the TSF Group, is engaged in manufacturing road wheels, parts, and engineering solutions across automotive and industrial sectors. Its portfolio spans automotive wheels for cars, trucks, and tractors, as well as components for construction and mining equipment, hydraulic cylinders, wind turbines, and railways. Through its subsidiary WCWL, it also manufactures steel wheels for light passenger vehicles.

The company operates 10 manufacturing facilities across India and serves a wide base of over 30 OEMs, including Tata Motors, Ashok Leyland, Mahindra & Mahindra, Maruti Suzuki, Caterpillar, and Vestas Wind Systems. With ~35% market share in M&HCV wheels, 84% in LCV wheels, and 51% in tractors, Wheels India is positioned as a leader in its core business, while simultaneously building a diversified presence in non-automotive engineering solutions.
Key Takeways:
Outlook & Guidance
Management guided for healthy single-digit revenue growth in FY26, with potential to touch ₹5,000 crore if commodity prices remain supportive. EBITDA margins of ~7% are expected to be sustained, with upside from aluminum wheel ramp-up and stronger windmill component demand from Q2 onwards. Export growth is expected to accelerate over the next three years as the company diversifies its base. The strategy remains focused on balancing auto and non-auto segments, strengthening the export footprint, and ensuring steady margin performance through cost efficiencies.

Name: Harsha Engineers International Ltd
Overview:
Harsha Engineers Ltd, established in 2010, is a prominent engineering company specializing in the manufacturing of precision components such as brass, steel, and polyamide cages, along with stamped components. The company operates production facilities across Asia including India and China and Europe (Romania), catering to a diverse range of end-user industries.

The company has demonstrated resilience and adaptability in its operations, navigating challenges in various markets. Notably, Harsha Engineers has been expanding its product offerings and enhancing its manufacturing capabilities to meet the evolving demands of its clientele.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Harsha Engineers Ltd is poised for continued growth, driven by its strategic initiatives and expansion plans. The company aims to capitalize on emerging opportunities in various markets, leveraging its expertise and global presence to enhance its competitive edge. With a focus on innovation and operational excellence, Harsha Engineers is well-positioned to strengthen its position in the engineering sector.

Name: JBM Auto Ltd
Overview:
JBM Auto Ltd, incorporated in 1983, operates in the automotive sector with diversified expertise in manufacturing sheet metal components, tools and dies, and electric buses. The company has evolved into a major force in India’s electric mobility space, establishing one of the largest integrated EV ecosystems globally (outside China). Its product range includes components for passenger and commercial vehicles as well as zero-emission electric buses used across diverse sectors.

The company is recognized as a market leader in electric buses, commanding a 30–35% market share. It serves top-tier clients including Tata, Ashok Leyland, Ford, and Mahindra, and maintains 16 manufacturing facilities across India. JBM Auto operates through three divisions: components, original equipment manufacturing (OEM), and tooling, each contributing to its growing order book and robust expansion roadmap.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

JBM Auto is actively expanding its electric vehicle (EV) capabilities to capitalize on the growing demand for sustainable transportation solutions. The company has established India's largest dedicated integrated EV ecosystem and electric bus manufacturing facility, with a production capacity of 20,000 buses annually.

Name: Minda Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Minda Corporation Ltd is a leading automotive components manufacturer in India with a strong pan-India and international presence. The company operates through two primary verticals: Information & Connected Systems and Mechatronics & Others, offering a diversified product portfolio including wiring harnesses, vehicle access systems, die casting, clusters, and advanced electronics. 

In recent years, the company has strategically expanded into powertrain and power electronics through its stake in Flash Electronics, enhancing its EV product offerings. With a strong order book, ongoing capacity expansions, and technology investments, Minda Corporation continues to consolidate its position as a key player in automotive innovation and localization, aiming to capture growth in emerging segments like electric vehicles and connected systems.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is optimistic about sustained growth driven by increasing EV adoption, premium product offerings, export ramp-up, and technology innovation. Cross-selling synergies with Flash Electronics are expected to accelerate from FY26, driving margin expansion and EPS accretion by FY27.

Name: Jay Bharat Maruti Ltd
Overview:
Jay Bharat Maruti Ltd. is a prominent player in the Indian automotive industry, specializing in the manufacturing of sheet metal stamping, welding assemblies, and module manufacturing. Its diverse product range includes body-in-white (BIW) parts, chassis components, exhaust systems, and precision-engineered components, catering primarily to leading automobile manufacturers. The company operates state-of-the-art facilities with advanced automation and technology, ensuring high standards of quality, reliability, and cost efficiency. Its emphasis on innovation, eco-friendly practices, and customer satisfaction has cemented its position as a trusted partner in the automotive supply chain.

Strategically, Jay Bharat Maruti is focused on domestic expansion, global market entry, and innovation in automotive components. With rising automobile demand in India, the company seeks to leverage its strong operational base while building global partnerships to expand its footprint. By optimizing operations and continuously improving processes, Jay Bharat Maruti aims to enhance productivity and profitability, positioning itself as a leading and sustainable contributor to the growth of the automotive component manufacturing sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jay Bharat Maruti Ltd (JBM) is strategically expanding its manufacturing footprint to meet the growing demand in the automotive sector. The company plans to invest between ₹300–350 crore to establish two new plants in Haryana and Gujarat, aiming to enhance production capacity and better serve its key customer, Maruti Suzuki. Additionally, JBM has increased its stake in JBM Ogihara Die-Tech (P) Ltd. from 39% to 49%, strengthening its capabilities in die manufacturing and tool design

Name: RACL Geartech Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1983, RACL Geartech Ltd is a precision engineering company engaged in the manufacture and export of transmission gears, shafts, sub-assemblies, chassis parts, and industrial gears. The company caters to leading automotive OEMs and industrial customers across Europe, Asia, and North America, serving diverse applications including two-wheelers, passenger vehicles, commercial trucks, ATVs, tractors, and industrial equipment.

The company operates two manufacturing plants in Uttar Pradesh (Gajraula & Noida), three warehouses in Europe, and a wholly owned Austrian subsidiary (RACL GmbH). With more than 22 active global customers and a portfolio of over 900 SKUs, RACL has established a strong export presence, particularly in Europe. Its strategy emphasizes high-precision engineering, customer-driven product development, and long-term supply arrangements with marquee OEMs.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Outlook
Management guided FY26 revenues at ₹500–525 crore (+/-5%), led by commercial vehicles and domestic demand, with conservative assumptions on KTM. Medium-term ambition remains to scale to ₹1000 crore revenue in 3–4 years, driven by diversification into EVs, premium passenger cars, and non-auto projects. Capex of ₹45–50 crore in FY26 will support this trajectory, with adequate capacity already available for near-term growth.

Name: Automotive Axles Ltd
Overview:
Automotive Axles Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in the production of axle assemblies, brakes, and chassis components for commercial vehicles. Established in 1981 and headquartered in Mysuru, the company serves both domestic and international markets.

The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and is committed to delivering high-quality products that meet the evolving demands of the automotive industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Automotive Axles Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the automotive industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, Automotive Axles Ltd is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Sansera Engineering Ltd
Overview:
Sansera Engineering is a specialized manufacturer of precision-engineered components, serving both automotive and non-automotive sectors, including aerospace, off-road, agriculture, and semiconductors. Its integrated operations focus on complex forged, machined, and sub-assembly components, enabling it to cater to high-demand industrial verticals with high-quality manufacturing.

The company has strategically diversified its product applications spanning internal combustion engine components to xEV and non-auto segments to broaden market opportunity and reduce dependence on traditional automotive cycles.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With strong execution in emerging high-growth segments, a healthy order backlog, and improved margins, Sansera is well-positioned for continued momentum into FY26. Its diverse segmental mix and investment in infrastructure set a solid foundation for sustained performance and resilience amid cyclical challenges.

Name: Rolex Rings Ltd
Overview:
Rolex Rings Limited is among India’s top five forging companies, engaged in the manufacturing of forged and machined components. It supplies hot-rolled forged rings, machined bearing rings, and auto components to global OEMs across industries such as automotive, railways, wind turbines, and industrial machinery.

The company has established itself as a trusted supplier in both domestic and international markets, with a strong focus on quality, technology, and customer relationships. Its integrated facilities, automation, and long-term partnerships with global OEMs provide a competitive edge in scaling operations and winning new programs.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Rolex Rings is strategically enhancing its global footprint and operational capabilities to drive sustained growth. The company is expanding into new markets and strengthening its position in existing ones, aiming to increase its revenue by approximately 13.6% annually. Additionally, Rolex Rings is focusing on improving its profit margins and return on equity, with forecasts indicating a potential 18% return in the next three years.

Name: L G Balakrishnan & Bros Ltd
Overview:
L. G. Balakrishnan & Bros Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer of automotive components, primarily specializing in chains, sprockets, and precision metal-formed assemblies. Operating across transmission and metal-forming business segments, the company supplies key powertrain and drivetrain components to OEMs in the automotive and allied sectors.

It combines engineering precision with high-volume production capabilities, catering to market needs through integrated manufacturing and segment-diverse applications.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

L.G. Balakrishnan & Bros is actively scaling up its manufacturing reach by expanding capacity at its major facility, adding over 9,500 m² to enhance production of chains, sprockets, precision components, die-cast parts, and assembled units. Simultaneously, the company is launching a new plant in Nagpur aimed at supplying power transmission chains to both automotive and industrial segments. It is also among the first to operationalize a plug-and-play unit in a newly launched industrial zone, supporting agile expansion and faster time-to-market for automotive belt production

Name: Carraro India Ltd
Overview:
Carraro India Ltd, a subsidiary of Carraro Group (Italy), specializes in the design and manufacture of transmission systems and driveline solutions for tractors, construction equipment, and material handling vehicles. It is a leading supplier of gears and axles to major OEMs in India and abroad.

The company operates as a crucial component supplier to the agriculture and construction machinery sectors, leveraging its global R&D base and local manufacturing capabilities to deliver customized solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Carraro India is positioning itself for sustained growth by significantly enhancing its production capacity, ramping up its manufacturing footprint in Pune with added assembly lines, warehouse space, and advanced machining setups. Strong domestic demand—especially in high-tech segments like four-wheel drive tractors and telehandler axles—is a key driver, supported by deepening localization and improved cost structures. The company is also strengthening its product portfolio through in-house R&D integration and expansion into specialized offerings, such as synchronized transmission systems via strategic joint ventures.

Name: JTEKT India Ltd
Overview:
JTEKT India Ltd is a leading manufacturer of steering systems and auto components, catering primarily to passenger cars and utility vehicles. The company plays a vital role in the Indian automotive supply chain, providing advanced steering solutions to major OEMs.

In FY25, JTEKT India maintained steady growth despite cost pressures and margin contraction. Its investments in capacity expansion and product diversification continue to strengthen its competitive positioning both domestically and in select international markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
JTEKT India expects mid to high single-digit revenue growth supported by expanding exports, stabilizing input costs, and capacity utilization. Margin recovery is anticipated as cost efficiencies improve, with exports and new product lines driving incremental growth.

Name: GNA Axles Ltd
Overview:
GNA Axles Ltd, established in 1993 and headquartered in Hoshiarpur, India, specializes in manufacturing auto components for the four-wheeler industry. The company primarily produces rear axle shafts, spindles, and drive shafts, catering to both domestic and international markets. GNA Axles serves a diverse clientele, including commercial vehicles, passenger vehicles, and off-highway vehicle manufacturers.
StockAnalysis

With a strong export presence, GNA Axles exports its products to regions such as North America, South America, Europe, Asia, and Australia. The company has established itself as a key player in the auto components industry, focusing on quality manufacturing and expanding its global footprint.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
GNA Axles Ltd aims to achieve a revenue growth of 4-5%, supported by its diversified product portfolio and expanding market reach. The company plans to enhance its manufacturing capabilities and invest in research and development to introduce new products and improve existing ones.

Name: Shanthi Gears Ltd
Overview:
Shanthi Gears Ltd is a prominent player in India's industrial gear sector, specializing in the design, manufacture, supply, and servicing of gears and gearboxes. As a subsidiary of Tube Investments of India, part of the ₹77,881 crore Murugappa Group, the company leverages over four decades of engineering expertise to serve a diverse clientele across industries such as mineral processing, mining, off-highway vehicles, and material handling.

Shanthi Gears operates primarily through a Business-to-Business (B2B) model, catering to Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs), and a Business-to-Consumer (B2C) model, which includes dealers and direct sales to end-users. The company has a strong domestic presence and is expanding its footprint in international markets. Its product offerings are integral to various industrial applications, underscoring its role in the industrial gear manufacturing landscape.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Shanthi Gears aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing its global presence. The company's focus on innovation and sustainability positions it well to meet the evolving needs of its diverse customer base. With a strong foundation and strategic initiatives, Shanthi Gears is poised for sustained growth in the industrial gear manufacturing sector.

Name: Shriram Pistons & Rings Ltd
Overview:
Shriram Pistons & Rings Ltd (SPRL) is a leading Indian manufacturer of pistons, piston pins, piston rings, and engine valves, supplying to major OEMs, the aftermarket, and export markets under the SPR and USHA brands.
