The company caters to diverse automotive segments, including passenger and commercial vehicles, 2-wheelers, tractors, and industrial engines, as well as non-automotive applications like marine engines, defence, and railways. Its strong domestic base is complemented by exports to over 45 countries, backed by collaborations with global technology leaders such as KS Kolbenschmidt GmbH, Honda Foundry, Riken Corporation, and Fuji Oozx.

In FY25, SPRL maintained a balanced revenue mix between OEMs (52%), aftermarket (26%), exports (17%), and non-automotive applications (5%). Geographically, domestic sales contributed 85% of revenue, with the remainder from exports led by Europe (31%), South America (26%), and the Middle East (18%). The company operates nine manufacturing facilities across India, running at 75–80% capacity utilization, supported by a network of over 1,200 business partners domestically and 22 global logistics centers.
Key Takeways:
.Growth or Future Outlook
Management remains confident of outperforming the broader auto industry, which remains cautious in the near term. Key growth levers include EV motor-controller integration, alternative fuel component development, and expansion in precision engineering. Capacity is being built ahead of demand, supported by continuous capex and potential M&A. SPRL’s strategy to transform into a multi-product franchise—covering ICE, EV, and alternative fuel platforms—positions it to sustain growth momentum and margin stability in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Z F Steering Gear (India) Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1981, ZF Steering Gear (India) Ltd specializes in the production and assembly of steering systems for various vehicles, including buses and tractors. The company operates manufacturing facilities in Pithampur, Madhya Pradesh, and Shirur, Maharashtra, focusing on both mechanical and hydraulic power steering systems. 

As a Tier-1 supplier, ZF Steering Gear (India) Ltd maintains long-standing associations with significant Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) in the domestic tractor and commercial vehicle markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
ZF Steering Gear (India) Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by leveraging its strategic partnerships and expanding its product offerings in the commercial vehicle segment. The company's focus on innovation and quality positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the Indian automotive market.

Name: Divgi Torqtransfer Systems Ltd
Overview:
Divgi Torqtransfer Systems Ltd is a leading manufacturer of automotive torque transfer systems, including transfer cases and mechatronic transmission components for passenger and utility vehicles. The company serves OEM clients across India and exports critical components globally.

Known for “Make in India” credentials, it offers vertically integrated solutions—from R&D and manufacturing to global delivery—and is uniquely positioned in India’s automotive ancillary segment with advanced transmission technologies.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Divgi is on a growth trajectory with accelerating demand for transmission components and export parts. Its significant JLR and Toyota OEM awards, strong order pipeline, and global competitiveness support the outlook. With continued ramp-ups in EV and transfer case volumes and margin discipline, the company is well poised for stronger growth and profitability in the remainder of FY26.

Name: ZF Commercial Vehicle Control System India Ltd
Overview:
WABCO India Ltd, operating under the ZF WABCO brand and part of the ZF Group’s Commercial Vehicle Control Systems Division, is India’s market leader in advanced braking systems and related air-assisted technologies. Headquartered in Chennai, the company manufactures braking products and systems for trucks, buses, off-highway vehicles, defense, cars, and fleets, along with e-mobility solutions such as e-compressors, electronic braking systems, and electronically controlled air suspension.

With six manufacturing facilities across Chennai, Jamshedpur, and Lucknow, four global engineering & technology centers, and a dedicated test track, WABCO India also operates an extensive aftermarket network of over 270 authorized service partners and 550 distributors. The company is advancing its role in future mobility through collaborations, such as its MoU with IIT Madras to develop the Bharat Multi-Modal Mobility Stack.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
WABCO India is positioned for strong growth, driven by regulatory push for advanced braking systems, rising electrification in commercial vehicles, and sustained export momentum. The new Oragadam facility, strategic partnerships for intelligent transport systems, and a strong product innovation pipeline are expected to accelerate both domestic and international market penetration in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Precision Camshafts Ltd
Overview:
Precision Camshafts Ltd (PCL) is one of the largest camshaft manufacturers globally. Incorporated in 1992, the company produces camshafts and other engine components for the automotive industry.

PCL operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and maintains a comprehensive quality management system. It supplies products to major Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Precision Camshafts Limited is strategically diversifying its product offerings and expanding its market presence. The company aims to increase the share of machined camshafts in its revenue mix, targeting a 50% contribution to total revenues, which is expected to drive margin expansion and strengthen profitability. Additionally, Precision Camshafts is focusing on enhancing its role with customers by offering a comprehensive range of camshaft solutions, including hybrid and assembled camshafts, to meet diverse industry needs.

Name: Uniparts India Ltd
Overview:
Uniparts India, founded in 1994, supplies engineering solutions and components for off-highway vehicles, agricultural machinery, and construction equipment. The company produces precision machined parts, 3-point linkages, and PTO shafts for global OEMs.

With facilities in India and the U.S., it serves over 25 countries and is a leader in small tractor linkage systems.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Uniparts India is strategically positioning itself for robust growth by focusing on expanding its global footprint and enhancing product offerings. The company aims to double its revenue to ₹2,000 crores within five years, driven by new business acquisitions and operational efficiencies. The company is also emphasizing innovation and design capabilities to increase its share of customer spend per vehicle. Additionally, Uniparts plans to enhance its manufacturing and warehousing capacities to support its expansion goals.

Name: Kross Ltd
Overview:
Kross Ltd, established in 1991, specializes in manufacturing high-performance, safety-critical components for commercial vehicle, tractor, and trailer axle industries. The company develops precision-engineered parts essential for load-bearing and safety requirements in heavy transportation. 

Known for its technical expertise and rigorous quality standards, Kross serves both domestic OEMs and international clients across automotive sectors. Over time, Kross has built a reputation for reliability and consistency, reflected in its steady financial performance and trusted supplier relationships.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Kross Ltd is poised for significant growth, with projections indicating a 30–35% increase in revenue for FY25–26, driven by strong demand in its niche tractor and trailer axle segments. The company has recently completed a backward integration program for its new foundry line, enhancing operational margins and positioning itself to capitalize on the expanding Indian transformer market.

Name: The Hi-Tech Gears Ltd
Overview:
The Hi-Tech Gears Ltd is a leading manufacturer of automotive components, specializing in gears and transmission parts. The company serves various sectors, including automotive, agriculture, and industrial applications.

The company focuses on precision manufacturing and quality control, aiming to meet the evolving demands of the automotive industry. It has a global customer base and operates state-of-the-art facilities.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Hi-Tech Gears Ltd is strategically positioning itself for future growth by focusing on operational efficiency, capacity optimization, and market expansion. The company is leveraging its existing manufacturing capabilities to secure new business opportunities, particularly in the electric vehicle (EV) sector, which is experiencing increased adoption. Additionally, Hi-Tech Gears is emphasizing lean manufacturing principles and cash flow efficiency to enhance profitability.

Name: ASK Automotive Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1988, ASK Automotive Limited is India’s largest manufacturer of brake-shoe and advanced braking systems for two-wheelers. The company has established market leadership in the domestic market, supported by strong OEM relationships across all two-wheeler manufacturers.

Its business spans advanced braking systems, aluminum lightweighting precision solutions, and safety control cables, with strategic initiatives to diversify into alloy wheels and sunroof cables. Backed by capacity expansions at Karoli and Bangalore, ASK Automotive is enhancing scale, product depth, and entry into adjacent passenger vehicle opportunities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management reaffirms mid-teen revenue growth  (excluding wheel assembly), supported by strong OEM demand, aftermarket traction, and plant ramp-up. With margins already at record levels, ASK expects sustainable profitability while expanding into new products and geographies. Despite export volatility and regulatory risks, the company remains optimistic about festive season demand and long-term growth visibility.

Name: Endurance Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Endurance Technologies is a diversified automotive component manufacturer with operations across India and Europe. It provides solutions in aluminium die casting, suspensions, transmissions, brakes, alloy wheels, and advanced embedded electronics, particularly in Battery Management Systems (BMS). With 33 manufacturing facilities (19 in India and 14 in Europe), the company caters to both 2W/3W OEMs domestically and 4W OEMs in Europe. Subsidiary Maxwell Energy Systems focuses on electronics solutions for EVs and energy storage.

Revenue is well-diversified, with India (including Maxwell) contributing ~77% and Europe ~23% in 9M FY25. Product-wise, die casting (43%), suspension (26%), and disc brakes (12%) dominate the portfolio. Clientele includes Bajaj Auto, Hero MotoCorp, TVS, Royal Enfield, and international customers like VW and Stellantis. The company is strategically expanding capacity through brownfield and greenfield projects, while also investing in R&D and inorganic growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Endurance Technologies is strategically expanding its product offerings and manufacturing capabilities to align with the evolving automotive landscape. The company is investing approximately ₹136 crore to quadruple its Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) capacity by March 2026, addressing the anticipated surge in demand due to regulatory changes mandating ABS for two-wheelers above 50cc starting in 2026. Additionally, Endurance is enhancing its braking systems and alloy wheel production capacities to support the growing requirements of the automotive sector.

Name: Rane Holdings Ltd
Overview:
Rane Holdings Limited (RHL), established in 1929 and headquartered in Chennai, India, serves as the holding company for the Rane Group. The company owns the "Rane" trademark and provides a range of services to its group companies, including employee training, investor services, business development, and information systems support. 

RHL's strategic role encompasses overseeing and managing the operations of its subsidiaries, which are primarily engaged in manufacturing auto components. These subsidiaries supply a diverse array of products to original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and the aftermarket sector. The company's consolidated financial performance reflects the collective results of these subsidiaries.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Rane Holdings aims to continue its growth trajectory by focusing on enhancing product offerings, expanding market reach, and strengthening its position in the automotive component industry. The company is also committed to improving operational efficiencies and maintaining a robust financial position to support its strategic objectives.

Name: Studds Accessories Ltd
Overview:
Studds Accessories Limited, incorporated around the early 1980s (with the “Studds” brand dating back to 1975), is a prominent Indian manufacturer of two-wheeler helmets and motorcycle accessories. The company designs, manufactures, markets and sells helmets under the “Studds” and “SMK” brands, and also offers accessories such as two-wheeler luggage, gloves, helmet locks, rain suits, riding jackets and eyewear. 

Headquartered in Faridabad, Haryana, it operates multiple manufacturing facilities with advanced automation and in-house design/testing capabilities. It serves the Indian mass and mid-market segment via the Studds brand and the premium segment via SMK, while exporting to more than seventy countries across the Americas, Europe, Asia and other regions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Studds Accessories aims to expand its manufacturing capacity, increase exports and deepen its penetration into both domestic and global markets. It intends to leverage its manufacturing strength and brand presence to launch new design variants, enter newer geographies and strengthen its premium segment via the SMK brand.

Name: Banco Products (India) Ltd
Overview:
Banco Products (India) Ltd is a leading manufacturer and supplier of engine cooling modules, sealing systems, and related components, serving both automotive and industrial sectors domestically and internationally. The company’s expertise in radiators, gaskets, oil coolers, and engine thermal solutions positions it as a critical OEM and aftermarket supplier.

In the latest fiscal period, Banco Products demonstrated a strong recovery and financial discipline, driven by demand uptick, efficient cost management, and growing exposure to emerging segments such as electric vehicles and sustainable alternatives.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Banco Products is well-positioned to benefit from rising demand in both traditional and emerging segments and to leverage cost efficiencies. With its strong product portfolio, increasing adoption in EV cooling, and favorable OEM demand trends, the company is expected to continue delivering healthy growth and profitability in the year ahead.

Name: Federal-Mogul Goetze (India) Ltd
Overview:
Federal-Mogul Goetze (India) Ltd is a leading manufacturer of automotive components, including pistons, piston rings, sintered parts, and cylinder liners. It caters to two/three-wheelers, cars, SUVs, tractors, light and heavy commercial vehicles, and industrial engines.

Established in 1954 as a joint venture with Goetze-Werke, it is now a subsidiary of Tenneco Inc. The company operates advanced manufacturing facilities and has a strong domestic and international presence. Its focus on quality, technology, and innovation has made it a trusted supplier to major OEMs and aftermarket clients.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company plans to introduce new products and technologies for the evolving automotive sector. Focus on enhancing manufacturing efficiency, quality, and international presence remains key. Sustainability initiatives and strategic partnerships will support long-term growth.

Name: Hindustan Composites Ltd
Overview:
Hindustan Composites Ltd is a pioneer in the manufacturing of friction materials and industrial composites in India. The company serves diverse industries such as automotive, railways, and industrial equipment, offering brake linings, clutch facings, and friction sheets. Its longstanding expertise and quality standards have helped it maintain leadership in the friction material space.

Beyond its core friction products, the company also manages significant investments in financial assets, adding a strong treasury component to its balance sheet. This dual revenue model provides stability and resilience, even during cyclical slowdowns in the automotive sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Future growth is expected from the rising demand for friction materials in passenger and commercial vehicles, coupled with expansion in railways. The company’s strong financial position through investments provides it flexibility for future expansions and diversification.

Name: Bosch Ltd
Overview:
Bosch Ltd is a diversified technology and engineering company with strong operations across automotive technology, consumer goods, industrial solutions, energy efficiency, and building technology. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and future-ready solutions like electric vehicles (EVs) and energy efficiency.
 
 Business Segments
Automotive Technology:  Powertrain solutions for ICEs, hybrids, EVs. EV charging infrastructure and solutions. Expansion of multi-brand car service centers and unit repair facilities.
 
Consumer Goods:  Products for trade professionals, DIY users, and crafters.  Solutions for auto diagnosis and repair.
 
Energy & Building Technology:  Security, safety, and communication solutions for infrastructure and commercial uses. Energy efficiency projects in pharma, FMCG, and healthcare.
 
Industrial Technology: Focused on specialized industrial solutions and advanced manufacturing support.
 
 Revenue & Strategy Summary
 Automotive technology remains the core driver, especially powertrain and EV solutions.
 
 Non-automotive divisions (energy & building) gaining traction due to growing demand for safety and energy-saving solutions.
 
 Strong capex plan of INR 2,000 crore by FY25-26 for automotive tech, digital mobility, and network expansion.
 
 Aggressive target to scale car service network to 1,000 outlets by 2025 and 4,000 by 2027.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bosch India is positioning itself at the forefront of the mobility transformation by expanding EV and hydrogen-powered solutions and piloting hydrogen ICE technology. It is also broadening its service network nationwide and diversifying into energy efficiency and building technologies. Continued investment in digital mobility and advanced automotive tech fuels its vision of a balanced, sustainable future.

Name: Tenneco Clean Air India Ltd
Overview:
Tenneco Clean Air India Limited is the Indian arm of Tenneco Inc., a global leader in engineered solutions for clean-air and powertrain systems. The company focuses on designing and manufacturing advanced emission-control components such as catalytic converters, mufflers, filters, and exhaust pipes to help automakers meet stringent pollution norms. It also integrates these clean-air systems with powertrain and ride-performance solutions, making it a key Tier-1 supplier to automotive OEMs in India.

Backed by Tenneco’s global R&D and engineering strength, the Indian subsidiary leverages innovation to develop localized solutions tailored for both domestic and export markets. Through its network of manufacturing plants across various Indian states, it delivers high-quality, technology-intensive products while tapping into India’s growing role as a global manufacturing hub.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Tenneco Clean Air India is poised to benefit from tightening emissions standards, which will drive demand for its advanced after-treatment systems. The company plans to use its modular design approach and asset-light model to efficiently scale up production. It also aims to deepen its export capabilities by serving global customers from its Indian operations, while continuing to invest in innovation through its R&D centers.

Name: NDR Auto Components Ltd
Overview:
Established in 2019, NDR Auto Components Ltd specializes in manufacturing seat frames and seat trims for passenger cars and utility vehicles. The company operates state-of-the-art facilities that cater to leading automotive OEMs, ensuring high-quality standards and timely delivery. With a focus on innovation and precision engineering, NDR Auto Components aims to meet the evolving demands of the automotive industry.

Headquartered in India, NDR Auto Components has rapidly expanded its footprint in the automotive sector. The company is committed to sustainable practices and continuously invests in technology to enhance its manufacturing processes. Through strategic partnerships and a customer-centric approach, NDR Auto Components strives to be a preferred supplier in the automotive components industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
NDR Auto Components Ltd aims to achieve a revenue of ₹1,000 crore by FY26, driven by increased demand and strategic initiatives. The company is focusing on expanding its customer base and enhancing product offerings to achieve this target.

Name: Rane (Madras) Ltd
Overview:
Rane (Madras) Ltd, established in 1960, is part of the Chennai-based Rane Group, a leading supplier of auto components to both domestic and international markets. The company operates through two divisions — Steering & Linkages and Light Metal Castings — along with its overseas subsidiary in Kentucky, USA. Its product portfolio includes steering gears, suspension components, hydraulic pinion housings, pump housings, brake housings, and transmission covers.

With four manufacturing plants across India and an upcoming subsidiary in Mexico, RML serves leading automotive OEMs such as Maruti Suzuki, Tata Motors, Mahindra & Mahindra, and TAFE. The company focuses on passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, and tractors, with exports contributing a sizable portion of its business. Its ongoing amalgamation with Rane Brake Lining and Rane Engine Valve Ltd will further integrate the group’s operations.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking forward, RML expects demand recovery in exports as new product supplies ramp up. Domestic growth will be led by passenger vehicles, supported by strong OEM relationships. The company reiterated focus on aftermarket expansion, international revenue share enhancement, and premium product launches.Capex will continue towards the Mexico facility (~USD 8 mn over three years) and strengthening Indian operations.

Name: Dynamatic Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Dynamatic Technologies (DTL) is a precision engineering firm specializing in hydraulic gear pumps and automotive turbochargers, with a global footprint serving aerospace, automotive, and hydraulics sectors. Operating across India and Europe, it supplies mission-critical components to OEMs worldwide.

Lean execution and a diversified business mix helped the company navigate macroeconomic pressures in FY 25, and early FY 26 shows signs of operational resilience. DTL continues to prioritize strategic orders in aerospace and hydraulic segments, laying a foundation for future ramp-up.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
DTL’s near-term outlook hinges on ramping up aerospace bookings and maintaining growth momentum in hydraulic pumps, especially aftermarket gains. While FY 25 profits were impacted by softness in metallurgy and cost pressures, its robust H1 and Q1 FY 26 performance suggests a foundation for gradual recovery. Continued focus on operational efficiency, strategic segments, and execution will be key to restoring profitability through FY 26.

Name: Jamna Auto Industries Ltd
Overview:
Jamna Auto Industries Limited, established in 1965, is a leading manufacturer specializing in suspension products such as leaf springs, parabolic springs, and lift axles, primarily catering to the commercial vehicle sector. The company operates multiple manufacturing facilities across India, strategically located to serve Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and the aftermarket segment. With a strong presence in the domestic market, Jamna Auto Industries has established itself as a prominent player in the automotive components industry.

The company has embarked on its "Lakshya 50XT" five-year plan, aiming for significant milestones by FY26. This strategic initiative focuses on achieving 50% of revenue from new markets, introducing 50% of revenue from new products, attaining a 50% Return on Capital Employed (ROCE), and distributing 50% of profit after tax as dividends. These objectives underscore Jamna Auto Industries' commitment to innovation, market expansion, and enhanced shareholder value.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Jamna Auto Industries aims to capitalize on the growth opportunities in the commercial vehicle sector, both domestically and internationally. The "Lakshya 50XT" plan is expected to drive innovation and market expansion, positioning the company for sustained growth. With a strong focus on new product development, market diversification, and operational excellence, Jamna Auto Industries is well-positioned to enhance shareholder value and achieve its strategic objectives by FY26

Name: Craftsman Automation Ltd
Overview:
Craftsman Automation Limited, established in 1986 in Coimbatore, is a diversified engineering company with strong in-house capabilities spanning design, process engineering, manufacturing, and assembly. The company operates across three primary business verticals—Powertrain, Aluminium Products, and Industrial & Engineering—serving marquee domestic and global OEMs across automotive, capital goods, warehousing, and other industrial segments.

With 26 manufacturing facilities, including 24 in India and 2 in Germany, the company’s footprint covers over 3.4 million sq. ft., strategically located to ensure just-in-time deliveries. In FY25, Craftsman achieved its highest-ever revenue from operations of ₹5,690 crore with an EBITDA of ₹858 crore, supported by strategic acquisitions, capacity expansions, and deeper penetration into high-growth segments like lightweight aluminium solutions and automated storage systems.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management expects strong growth momentum to continue, supported by increasing localisation in automotive components, rising EV adoption, and expanding market share in storage solutions. With new capacities coming online and synergies from acquisitions, Craftsman aims to deepen penetration in both domestic and export markets, while maintaining healthy margins through operational efficiency and high-value product offerings. The company’s strategic capex pipeline and technology-driven approach are expected to sustain revenue growth and market leadership over the medium term.

Name: Subros Ltd
Overview:
Subros Limited, established in 1985, is a leading manufacturer of thermal products for the automotive industry in India. The company operates as a joint venture with Denso Corporation (Japan) and Suzuki Motor Corporation (Japan), holding significant stakes alongside the Suri family of India.

With a robust manufacturing infrastructure, Subros operates multiple state-of-the-art facilities across India, strategically located to serve its diverse customer base efficiently. The company's commitment to innovation is underscored by its in-house research and development capabilities, enabling the development of cutting-edge thermal solutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Subros Limited is poised for continued growth, driven by its strategic initiatives and market expansion efforts. The company's focus on innovation, coupled with its strong industry partnerships, positions it well to capitalize on emerging trends in the automotive sector.

Name: Sharda Motor Industries Ltd
Overview:
Sharda Motor Industries Ltd is a diversified manufacturer operating primarily in the auto components sector, specializing in products used across automotive and white goods applications. It serves OEMs and various industries, supplying chassis components, drivetrain systems, and household appliance parts. The company leverages manufacturing capabilities and a wide product portfolio to meet infrastructure needs across sectors.

Sharda Motor Industries has demonstrated steady performance through strategic operational execution and cost management. Its business model, grounded in component manufacturing, enables consistent engagement with core industrial sectors and positions it as a resilient player amidst market fluctuations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Sharda Motor Industries is well-positioned for sustained growth, buoyed by demand in both automotive and white goods sectors. Its efficient cost structure and diverse business operations provide a solid foundation for margin improvement. Continued focus on operational discipline and market diversification is expected to support future growth into FY26 and beyond.

Name: Rico Auto Industries Ltd
Overview:
Rico Auto Industries Ltd is a leading engineering company specializing in the manufacturing and supply of high-precision aluminum and ferrous components and assemblies to automotive Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) globally. Established in 1983, the company offers integrated services encompassing design, development, tooling, casting, machining, and assembly. 

Headquartered in Gurugram, India, Rico Auto operates multiple manufacturing facilities across the country. The company's product portfolio caters to both two-wheeler and four-wheeler segments, providing components that meet stringent quality standards.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Rico Auto Industries Ltd is optimistic about achieving a turnover of ₹2,650 crore in FY26, representing a 20% growth from the previous fiscal year. The company aims to enhance its EBITDA margins to 12-13% by the end of the fiscal year, driven by improved operational efficiencies and strategic initiatives.

Name: Sedemac Mechatronics Ltd
Overview:
SEDEMAC Mechatronics Ltd is a technology company focused on developing control electronics solutions for automotive and industrial applications. The company designs and manufactures electronic control units (ECUs), sensors, and advanced control systems that help improve engine performance, fuel efficiency, and emission control. Its products are widely used in two-wheelers, three-wheelers, small engines, and other mobility solutions, supporting the shift toward smarter and more efficient vehicles.

The company combines hardware and software capabilities to deliver integrated mechatronics solutions tailored to customer requirements. It works closely with original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) to provide customized control systems and embedded technologies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its product portfolio by developing advanced control systems and next-generation electronics for mobility and industrial applications. It aims to benefit from increasing demand for electronic content in vehicles, including emission control and efficiency solutions. The company is also focusing on strengthening its relationships with OEMs and expanding into new domestic and international markets.

Name: India Motor Parts & Accessories Ltd
Overview:
India Motor Parts & Accessories Ltd (IMPAL) is one of the largest all-India distributors for automobile spares, representing around 28+ manufacturers. The company caters to more than 35,000 dealers across India, offering a wide range of automotive spare parts and accessories.

IMPAL's product portfolio includes engine group components, brake systems, fasteners, radiators, suspensions, axles, auto electricals, wheels, steering linkages, instrument clusters, and more.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
India Motor Parts & Accessories Ltd aims to expand its product offerings and enhance distribution capabilities to meet the growing demand in the automotive sector. The company is also focusing on digital transformation to streamline operations and improve customer experience.

Name: Pricol Ltd
Overview:
Pricol Limited, established in 1974 and headquartered in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, is a leading manufacturer of automotive components, specializing in instrument clusters and allied products. The company serves both Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and the replacement market, positioning itself as a significant player in the automotive industry.

With a strong market presence, Pricol has expanded its product portfolio to include sensors, oil pumps, telematics solutions, and electronic components. The company's commitment to innovation and quality has enabled it to maintain a competitive edge in the dynamic automotive sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Pricol aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and strengthening relationships with OEMs. The company is also focusing on enhancing its export capabilities to tap into international markets, positioning itself for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: S J S Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
S J S Enterprises Ltd is an Indian manufacturing company engaged in industrial products, building materials, and engineering solutions. The company serves construction, infrastructure, and industrial sectors with high-quality products.

Headquartered in India, S J S Enterprises emphasizes technological innovation, operational efficiency, and sustainable practices. Its diverse manufacturing capabilities allow it to meet the requirements of varied industry clients.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
SJS Enterprises strengthened its core operations by optimizing production efficiency and expanding its service offerings. Focus on supply chain enhancements and customer retention strategies led to improved operational throughput. The company also implemented technology upgrades in manufacturing and distribution, enhancing overall productivity and responsiveness to market demand.

Name: Lumax Auto Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Lumax Auto Technologies Ltd is a leading Indian auto-components manufacturer specializing in advanced plastics, structural and control systems, mechatronics, and emerging alternate-fuel solutions. Its diverse portfolio caters to two-wheelers, passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, and aftermarket segments across OEMs.

Strategically positioned with 30 manufacturing facilities, the company continues to expand through targeted acquisitions—such as its full acquisition of IAC India and Greenfuel stake—and by building capabilities in EV interiors, alternate fuels, and sustainable mobility solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Lumax is on track for sustained momentum with bolstered presence in emerging mobility segments, margin stability, and strategic infrastructure expansion. With earnings softened by temporary pricing corrections anticipated to rebound in H2, the outlook into FY26 reflects strong execution, capital efficiency, and strategic leadership in clean mobility.

Name: Exicom Tele-Systems Ltd
Overview:
Exicom Tele-Systems Ltd, founded in 1994 and based in Gurugram, is a leading provider of power management solutions, specializing in critical power systems and EV charging infrastructure. Its core operations include manufacturing rectifiers, AC/DC converters, Li-ion energy storage units, and smart EV chargers for residential, business, and public use.

With a presence in over 450 cities across India and exports overseas, Exicom serves telecom, enterprise, and EV sectors. It leverages an integrated model combining hardware, software, and service capabilities to drive energy efficiency and support the clean energy transition.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Exicom is focusing on scaling its EV charger business, targeting a 50% share of revenue by 2030. A new manufacturing facility in Hyderabad and the acquisition of Australian firm Tritium are set to bolster this push, supporting global expansion and capacity ramp-up. Continued operational improvements in the core power segment are expected to help improve margins and return to profitability.

Name: Motherson Sumi Wiring India Ltd
Overview:
Motherson Sumi Wiring India Ltd (MSWIL) is a leading Indian player in the wiring harness segment, formed through the demerger of the domestic wiring business of Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd in FY22. The company is a joint venture between Sumitomo Wiring Systems and Samvardhana Motherson Group, with each partner holding 25.1% and 33.4% stakes, respectively. As the market leader in India with over 40% share, MSWIL supplies critical wiring solutions to all major automotive OEMs across segments.

The company operates 26 wiring harness manufacturing facilities strategically located near OEM clusters, along with 2 new plants at Noida and Chennai. Employing more than 45,000 people, MSWIL’s product portfolio is entirely concentrated on wiring harnesses, supplying to top ICE and EV manufacturers. The company remains debt-free and benefits from strong operational and backward integration support from its parent entities.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Motherson Sumi Wiring India Ltd (MSWIL) is strategically expanding its production capacity to meet the increasing demand for advanced wiring solutions in both electric and internal combustion engine vehicles. The company is enhancing its manufacturing footprint with the establishment of three new greenfield facilities, which are expected to increase its production capacity by approximately 10% once fully operational.

Name: India Nippon Electricals Ltd
Overview:
India Nippon Electricals Ltd (INEL) is a joint venture company established in the mid-1980s, specializing in the design and manufacture of ignition systems for two-wheelers, three-wheelers, and portable engines. 

It stands out for producing both digital and analog ignition products and related components like ignition coils, regulators, sensors, and instrument clusters. The company operates multiple manufacturing facilities strategically located in Tamil Nadu, Pondicherry, and Haryana to optimize responsiveness and ensure timely delivery to OEMs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With demand from the two- and three-wheeler sectors on the upswing and its expanding product portfolio, INEL is well positioned for incremental growth. Its strategic investment in EFI development could potentially unlock new revenue streams as emissions norms tighten.

Name: Lumax Industries Ltd
Overview:
Lumax Industries Limited, founded in 1945, is a leading player in the Indian automotive lighting sector. As the flagship company of the DK Jain Group, Lumax has established itself as a market leader in automobile lighting in the Indian automotive industry. 

In 1984, the company entered into a technical collaboration with Stanley Electric Co. Ltd., Japan (SECL), which currently holds around 37.5% equity stake in the company. Other 37.5% is held by Indian promoters (D.K. Jain and family). The company has 29 manufacturing facilities across 7 states in India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Lumax Industries is strategically expanding its footprint to capitalize on the growing demand for automotive lighting solutions, particularly in the electric vehicle (EV) sector. The company has commenced operations at a new greenfield plant in Pune, aiming to enhance its production capabilities and meet the increasing requirements of Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) in Western India. This expansion is in response to the rising adoption of EVs and the need for advanced lighting technologies.

Name: Fiem Industries Ltd
Overview:
Fiem Industries Ltd, established in 1989, is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in automotive lighting and signaling equipment. The company offers a diverse range of products, including LED lighting systems, rearview mirrors, and plastic components, catering to both two-wheeler and four-wheeler segments.

With a robust presence in the Indian automotive industry, Fiem Industries has expanded its footprint globally through subsidiaries in Japan and Italy. The company emphasizes innovation, quality, and customer satisfaction, aiming to be a preferred supplier for automotive lighting solutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Fiem Industries aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and exploring new markets. The company plans to enhance its presence in the electric vehicle segment and strengthen its position in the global automotive lighting market. With a commitment to innovation and quality, Fiem Industries is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the automotive industry.

Name: Igarashi Motors India Ltd
Overview:
Igarashi Motors India Ltd, established in 1992, is a leading manufacturer specializing in Permanent Magnet DC (PMDC) motors, primarily catering to the automotive sector, particularly passenger vehicles. The company was originally a joint venture between Crompton Greaves Ltd (India), Igarashi Electric Works Ltd (Japan), and International Components Corporation (USA). Over time, Igarashi Electric Works Ltd consolidated its stake, making it the sole shareholder. 

Headquartered in India, Igarashi Motors operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and has a strong presence in both domestic and international markets. Igarashi Motors India Ltd has established itself as a key player in the automotive components industry, contributing significantly to the advancement of electric mobility solutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Igarashi Motors India Ltd aims to strengthen its position in the automotive components industry by focusing on innovation and expanding its product offerings. The company plans to leverage advancements in electric vehicle technologies to enhance its product portfolio and cater to the evolving needs of the automotive sector.

Name: Sandhar Technologies Limited
Overview:
Sandhar Technologies Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer of automotive components, specializing in precision engineering and assembly solutions. Established in 1987, the company serves global OEMs across segments such as two-wheelers, four-wheelers, and electric vehicles. Sandhar operates multiple manufacturing facilities worldwide, including in India, Spain, Mexico, and Poland.

The company’s product portfolio includes mirrors, locks, lighting systems, die-cast components, and EV parts. Sandhar is expanding into electric vehicle components, including battery chargers, motor controllers, and DC-DC converters. The company is planning strategic investments for capacity expansion and potential acquisitions to drive future growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Sandhar Technologies Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the automotive industry. With a strong financial performance and commitment to excellence, Sandhar is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd
Overview:
Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd (Motherson) is a leading global automotive component manufacturer, widely recognized for its dominance in exterior rear-view mirrors and polymer-based interior and exterior modules. The company offers a broad portfolio of products and services across wiring harnesses, vision systems, metal products, technology solutions, retail and services, and aerospace applications.
 
 Business Segments
 Automotive Components: Exterior mirrors, polymer modules, wiring harnesses, vision systems, and metal products.
 
 Technology & Software: Digital innovation and operational efficiency enhancements.
 
 Retail & Services: B2B supply, distributor networks, and direct sales/services across market segments.
 
 Aerospace Solutions: Integrated products and services catering to aerospace industry applications.
 
 Revenue Mix (Indicative Approximate Percentages)
 Automotive Components: ~75% — Core revenue from mirrors, wiring harnesses, modules, and related products.
 
 Technology & Software Solutions: ~10% — Focused on digital transformation and tech-driven efficiency.
 
 Retail & Services: ~10% — Support for automotive and other industrial clients through distribution and service networks.
 
 Aerospace: ~5% — Niche but growing segment offering integrated aerospace solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Motherson is strengthening its leadership in automotive wiring harnesses through product diversification and premium positioning, supported by technology and software innovation. Growth is being fueled by strategic acquisitions, operational restructuring, and stable customer demand. Improved margins through efficiency gains further solidify its growth outlook.

Name: Suprajit Engineering Ltd
Overview:
Suprajit Engineering is a diversified manufacturer engaged in producing automotive and non-automotive cables, components, lighting solutions including halogen lamps and LED products, as well as mechanical products like seat latches, steering locks, and headrest mechanisms. The company operates across multiple geographies with a growing presence in both OEM and aftermarket segments.

With a strong global footprint, Suprajit is focused on expanding its product portfolio beyond cables into braking systems, sensors, and electronics while maintaining leadership in control cables. Strategic acquisitions and restructuring initiatives are strengthening its supply chain flexibility and operational efficiency, positioning it for sustainable long-term growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management remains confident of sustaining outperformance against industry growth, delivering a turnaround in SCS , and leveraging its global footprint to mitigate tariff risks. While Phoenix Lamps and Electronics face near-term softness, order wins, technology upgrades, and diversification beyond cables are expected to drive stronger growth and margin expansion in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Gabriel India Ltd
Overview:
Gabriel India is a prominent auto-component manufacturer under the ANAND Group, recognized for its broad portfolio of ride-control solutions including shock absorbers, struts, front forks, and sunroof systems. Serving OEMs, aftermarket players, and exporting markets, it maintains extensive reach across vehicle types and geographies.

Leaning into its deep automotive ecosystem, in FY 25 the company strengthened its capabilities through strategic reorganization, geographic expansion, and diversification into adjacent systems. As FY 26 unfolds, Gabriel India is progressing on capacity augmentation, bolstered product offerings, and integration of its subsidiaries to scale operations while enhancing profitability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With strong franchise in OEM and aftermarket, expanded product suite, and organizational simplification, Gabriel India is set for accelerated growth. The company is expected to deliver double-digit revenue and earnings growth through FY 26–27, driven by integration synergies, new segment entries, and broad demand recovery across automotive segments. Analysts remain bullish, revising EPS and profitability expectations upwards

Name: Uno Minda Ltd
Overview:
Uno Minda Ltd is a diversified automotive component manufacturer supplying over 25 product types including switches, lighting, alloy wheels, seating, and acoustic systems to OEMs across two-wheelers, passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, and EV segments. The company serves both internal combustion and electric vehicle markets and maintains strong domestic market leadership supported by strategic expansions and technological advancements.

With a solid growth trajectory in domestic auto production and exports, Uno Minda has consistently outperformed industry volumes through broad-based revenue growth across segments. Its expansion into EV-related technologies and new capacity additions across plants bolster its long-term competitive positioning amid evolving automotive trends.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects continued broad-based growth driven by ramp-ups in new plants, EV segment acceleration, and premiumization trends. EBITDA margin guidance remains stable around 11%, factoring in startup costs from new projects. With ongoing capacity expansions and EV technology integration, Uno Minda is well positioned for sustained growth aligned with evolving automotive market dynamics.

Name: Varroc Engineering Ltd
Overview:
Varroc Engineering Ltd is a global Tier-1 automotive component manufacturer, offering a wide range of products including exterior lighting, powertrain components, body systems, and electrical & electronics modules for two-, three- and four-wheelers. The company operates through 37 manufacturing plants and 7 R&D centres across three continents, backed by over 6,100 employees and a strong intellectual property portfolio.

With a robust footprint across OEMs, Varroc delivers high-precision components spanning traditional ICE vehicles and emerging electric mobility platforms, reflecting its commitment to innovation, global scale, and diversified portfolio across segments and geographies.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Varroc is confident of delivering a ~10% CAGR in revenues over the medium term, supported by strong order inflow from ICE and EV segments. It aims to improve EBITDA margins toward 10–10.7% by FY27, driven by operational efficiencies, cost control, and backward integration. Key strategic thrusts include scaling EV content, global localization, supply chain de-risking, and leveraging green energy to lower operational cost, all while strengthening cash generation and return ratios.

Name: Rane Brake Lining Ltd
Overview:
Rane Brake Lining Ltd, part of the Rane Group, manufactures friction materials including brake linings, disc pads, clutch facings, and railway brake blocks. It serves OEMs, railways, and the aftermarket segment.

The company’s products are supplied to domestic automotive leaders as well as international markets. Its expertise in friction technology and long-standing OEM relationships strengthen its positioning.It is a leading manufacturer of friction products in India—including brake linings, disc pads, clutch facings, and railway brake blocks. The company operates four advanced manufacturing facilities across Chennai, Hyderabad, Puducherry, and Trichy, offering environmentally compliant products with strong R&D support. Backed by decades of technical expertise, global collaborations, and an extensive distribution network across OEM, aftermarket, and rail segments, Rane Brake Lining is well-positioned to capture growth across both domestic and export markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Demand across passenger vehicles, two-wheelers, rail, and export markets provides mid-term growth visibility. The strength in aftermarket channels and global market penetration positions Rane for continued expansion in both domestic and international segments.

Name: Automobile Corporation Of Goa Ltd
Overview:
Automobile Corporation Of Goa Ltd is a leading manufacturer of automobile bodies in India. The company specializes in the design, development, and manufacture of bus bodies, truck cabins, and other vehicle bodies, catering to both domestic and international markets.

Automobile Corporation Of Goa Ltd's products are used by various commercial vehicle manufacturers, providing them with high-quality and durable vehicle bodies.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
ACGL's strong operational linkages with Tata Motors Ltd (TML) are expected to further enhance its scale, with anticipated higher demand for buses from TML. Additionally, the company has declared a final dividend of ₹20 per equity share for FY2024–25, underscoring its commitment to delivering value to shareholders. With a diversified portfolio and strategic partnerships, ACGL is well-positioned for sustained growth in the competitive auto ancillary sector.

Name: Indo Farm Equipment Ltd
Overview:
Indo Farm Equipment Ltd is an Indian manufacturer of tractors, construction equipment, and agricultural machinery. Established with a focus on affordable mechanization, the company has grown into a recognized name in India’s farm equipment industry.

With a product range spanning tractors, cranes, and engines, Indo Farm serves both domestic farmers and export markets. Its strategy combines cost efficiency with durable, locally-suited machinery.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Financially, Indo Farm Equipment has demonstrated robust performance, with a 31% year-on-year revenue growth, reaching ₹91.26 crore in June 2025. The company's strategic initiatives, including capacity expansion and diversification into mobile tower cranes, position it for sustained growth in the competitive agricultural and construction equipment sectors.

Name: Ather Energy Ltd
Overview:
Ather Energy is one of India’s most prominent electric two-wheeler companies, known for producing premium, high-performance scooters integrated with smart connectivity and digital features. The company has pioneered urban mobility solutions that combine hardware innovation with software-driven intelligence, creating a differentiated user experience in the electric vehicle space.

Beyond vehicles, Ather has invested in developing its proprietary charging infrastructure, Ather Grid, making it one of the few companies in India to offer a comprehensive EV ecosystem. By focusing on design, technology, and customer engagement, Ather has successfully positioned itself as a brand for aspirational urban commuters.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company is expected to broaden its product range by launching more affordable models, scaling its charging infrastructure, and expanding into newer geographies. This will help Ather transition from a niche premium brand into a mainstream EV player.

Name: Force Motors Ltd
Overview:
Force Motors Ltd stands as a fully integrated automotive manufacturer renowned for its leadership in vans, light commercial vehicles, and specialty vehicles, with a robust presence in engine manufacturing for global OEMs. The company serves diverse markets across the Middle East, Asia, Africa, Latin America, and beyond, supported by strategic collaborations with premium brands and a history stretching back to 1958.

In recent years, Force Motors has solidified its reputation through technological integration and partnerships, notably supplying engines to BMW and Mercedes-Benz, while maintaining a dominant position in the Indian LCV passenger segment and expanding product lines like Urbania and Gurkha.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Force Motors is well-positioned to sustain its growth journey. With a debt-free balance sheet, strong internal accruals, and annual capex plans (~₹400–500 crore) financed without external borrowing, the company aims to fund expansion and innovation in emerging domains like EV platforms, fleet intelligence (like its AI-powered iPulse system), and exports of Urbania models.

Name: Escorts Kubota Ltd
Overview:
Escorts Ltd. is a leading Indian engineering conglomerate engaged in manufacturing agricultural machinery, construction and material handling equipment, and railway equipment. The company has a diversified product portfolio including tractors, implements, engines, cranes, and other construction machinery, catering to domestic and export markets. It leverages strong brand partnerships, notably with Kubota, and focuses on innovation and channel expansion to strengthen its presence in key regional markets.

The company continues to balance its legacy businesses with growth in exports and non-tractor segments, backed by strategic investments in product launches and greenfield capacity expansion.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management remains optimistic on sustained growth driven by exports, new product launches, and channel expansion, particularly leveraging Kubota’s global network. Challenges include commodity inflation, regional market disparities, and slow farm mechanization reforms. The company expects improving regional sales mix and margin recovery from H2 FY26 onward.

Name: Ola Electric Mobility Ltd
Overview:
Ola Electric is a leading manufacturer of electric two-wheelers and core EV components, including battery packs, motors, and frames, produced at its integrated Ola Futurefactory. Since inception in 2017, the company has evolved rapidly, focusing on developing its Gen 3 platform which drives better performance and profitability while rolling out motorcycles alongside scooters.

The company is also scaling its cell manufacturing capacity with a 5 GWh Gigafactory to support long-term vehicle production targets. With a dual strategy in scooters and motorcycles, Ola is expanding its product mix and strengthening in-house technology to improve margins and operational efficiency while building a direct-to-consumer sales channel.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management targets operational free cash flow positivity  with continued margin expansion driven by product mix improvement and cell manufacturing scale. The company expects steady EV penetration growth following early adopter phase moderation, with sustained focus on cost reduction, product innovation, and direct customer engagement.

Name: Eicher Motors Ltd
Overview:
Eicher Motors Limited is an iconic player in India’s automobile industry, best known for owning Royal Enfield, a global leader in the middleweight motorcycle segment. The company exports its motorcycles to over 60 countries and complements bike sales with a strong portfolio of branded apparel, accessories, and riding gear. Through its joint venture Volvo Eicher Commercial Vehicles (VECV) with AB Volvo, Eicher Motors also manufactures trucks, buses, and auto components, supported by robust manufacturing capacity and a wide dealer network.
 
Business Segments 
 
 Royal Enfield Motorcycles: ~85% of total revenue — includes mid-sized motorcycles, apparel, accessories, and riding gear.
 
 VECV (Commercial Vehicles JV with Volvo): ~15% of total revenue — includes trucks, buses, auto components, and engines for domestic and export markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Royal Enfield is poised for growth through a combination of product diversification, capacity expansion, and international market penetration. The launch of its first electric motorcycle in 2025, supported by a dedicated EV manufacturing hub in Cheyyar, complements continued investment in ICE superbikes to maintain leadership in the mid-weight segment.

Name: Atul Auto Ltd
Overview:
Atul Auto Ltd, established in 1986, is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in the production and export of auto rickshaws. Headquartered in Rajkot, Gujarat, the company has a significant presence in the domestic market and exports its vehicles to over 15 countries. Atul Auto is recognized for its commitment to quality and innovation in the three-wheeler segment.

The company's product portfolio includes a range of passenger and cargo vehicles, catering to diverse customer needs. Atul Auto operates a robust distribution network with over 400 touchpoints across India, ensuring widespread availability and service support.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Atul Auto aims to capitalize on the growing demand for electric three-wheelers by expanding its EV portfolio and enhancing production capabilities. The company plans to increase its focus on the L5 EV segment, which has shown promising growth.

Name: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd
Overview:
Maruti Suzuki India Limited (MSIL) — a joint venture between the Government of India and Suzuki Motor Corporation — is India’s largest passenger vehicle manufacturer with a commanding domestic market share of ~47.4% in the PV segment.
 
 Market Position by Segment
 Passenger Cars: 62.6% market share — India’s clear leader in compact and small cars.
 
 Utility Vehicles (UVs): 24.9% — competing strongly in the fast-growing SUV/UV space.
 
 Vans: 89.6% — near-total dominance in this niche segment.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Maruti Suzuki plans substantial capital investments in new manufacturing capacity, EV and hybrid technology, and battery production to support long-term growth. The company is expanding its product mix to include SUVs, electric, and hybrid vehicles to cater to evolving consumer preferences. Strategic exports and technology partnerships aim to mitigate domestic market volatility, while digital transformation and strengthened aftersales services enhance customer engagement and loyalty.

Name: Olectra Greentech Ltd
Overview:
Olectra Greentech Limited, incorporated in 2000 and headquartered in Hyderabad, is India’s largest pure electric bus manufacturer and a leading producer of composite polymer insulators. The company operates primarily through two divisions — the E-Vehicle division, which contributes the majority of revenue, and the Insulator division. Olectra’s E-Vehicle portfolio includes 7m, 9m, and 12m electric buses, as well as electric tippers and inter-city coaches, catering mainly to state and central government transport bodies.

The company entered the e-bus market in 2016 through a long-term technology partnership with BYD, valid until 2030, while progressively localizing components. With over 2,448 electric buses and 51 electric tippers delivered across India, Olectra has logged 30+ crore kilometers in operations. It is currently expanding manufacturing capacity through a greenfield EV plant in Seetharampur, aiming to scale production significantly over the next two years.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Olectra targets production of 2,500 buses , supported by capacity expansion, strong government EV adoption policies, and a healthy order book. With its dominant market position, long-term BYD partnership, and growing export potential in the insulator segment, the company is poised for accelerated revenue growth and margin stability.

Name: Bajaj Auto Ltd
Overview:
Bajaj Auto is a global leader in two- and three-wheeler manufacturing, recognized as the world’s largest three-wheeler producer and the fourth-largest motorcycle manufacturer. The company exports to 79 countries and has major market shares across Africa, Latin America, South Asia, the Middle East, and ASEAN.
 
 Business Segments and FY24–25 Performance
 Total Revenue:
 ₹50,995 crore in FY25, with automotive operations contributing ₹49,982 crore.
 
 Motorcycles:
 Strong volume growth, with domestic market share of 18.2% in FY24. Global reach boosted by premium brands (Pulsar, Dominar, KTM). FY25 export volumes were 1.67 million motorcycles, with significant increases in Asia (+51%) and Latin America (+30%).
 
 Three-Wheelers (Commercial Vehicles):
 Achieved an 80% domestic market share in FY24 (RE and Maxima brands). FY25 exports were 189,221 units.
 
 Premium Segment / KTM:
 Bajaj is moving from minority to majority owner of KTM, giving it global reach in premium motorcycles and new synergies in R&D and manufacturing. KTM, Husqvarna, and GASGAS are now part of the strategic portfolio, positioning Bajaj at the forefront of the high-performance motorcycle market.
 
 Electric Vehicles (EVs):
 Under Chetak Technology Ltd., Bajaj is sharply scaling EV investments—₹400–500 crore in FY24. Efforts focus on expanding the Chetak scooter plant in Pune, new Aurangabad electric 3-wheeler plant, and EV component capacity (~₹700–750 crore in capex). Chetak EV sales tripled year-on-year, with Bajaj targeting a retail sales rise from 6,000 to 10,000 units monthly and carving out a 20% EV market share in FY24 (nearly double that of FY23)
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Bajaj Auto plans aggressive capacity expansion, particularly for electric vehicles, supported by new plants in Pune, Aurangabad, and Thailand. The company aims to strengthen exports and recover market share post-pandemic, while leveraging its premium portfolio through KTM and Husqvarna for global market access and R&D synergies. Investments in EV supply chains, internalization of key components, digitalization, and customer experience initiatives will complement product innovation.

Name: Maharashtra Scooters Ltd
Overview:
Maharashtra Scooters Ltd. (MSL) is an unregistered Core Investment Company (CIC) with a primary focus on holding strategic investments in Bajaj Group companies, alongside a small-scale manufacturing operation producing pressure die-casting dies, jigs, fixtures, and castings for the two- and three-wheeler industry. The company’s investment portfolio comprises significant equity stakes in Bajaj Auto, Bajaj Finance, Bajaj Finserv, and Bajaj Holdings & Investment, along with investments in debentures and mutual funds.

During FY25, MSL transitioned away from manufacturing operations following the implementation of a Voluntary Separation Scheme (VSS) for all factory workers and staff, which was fully accepted and executed. Post-VSS, only the CEO and select finance personnel remain on the payroll, marking a shift towards being almost entirely an investment-focused entity.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
MSL’s future is anchored in its role as an investment arm of the Bajaj Group. With operational costs drastically reduced post-VSS, profitability will be driven almost entirely by investment income. While manufacturing revenues will cease, the company’s robust equity holdings and fixed-income investments provide predictable returns, making it a stable but low-growth entity unless strategic redeployment of capital is pursued.

Name: Hyundai Motor India Ltd
Overview:
Hyundai Motor India Ltd is India’s second-largest carmaker and top passenger car exporter, with 13 models across sedans, hatchbacks, SUVs, and EVs. It runs two large plants in Chennai and is expanding capacity with a new plant in Talegaon to reach over 1 million units annually by 2026. Strong localization (93%) and an extensive sales and service network give it an edge in cost and reach.
 
 Business Segments:
 
 Domestic Sales: ~77.5% of revenue comes from the Indian market. SUVs dominate sales, contributing 68.5% of domestic volumes, led by Creta.
 
 Exports: About 22.5% of revenue comes from exports, with steady annual shipments of ~163,000 units to over 80 countries.
 
 EVs: Still a small part but growing — 3,969 units sold in FY25, with major expansion plans through six new EVs by 2030.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Hyundai India is poised for sustained growth through capacity expansion at the new Talegaon plant and Chennai debottlenecking, targeting 1.07 million units annually to support both domestic and export demand. The product pipeline includes 26 new models by 2030, with six EVs, hybrids, and refreshed SUVs driving volume, premiumization, and technology adoption. A strong focus on EV infrastructure, including local battery assembly and 600 fast-charging stations, complements the company’s electrification strategy.

Name: Hero MotoCorp Ltd
Overview:
Hero MotoCorp, formerly Hero Honda, is India’s leading and the world’s largest manufacturer of two-wheelers by unit volume, maintaining this leadership for over two decades. With iconic brands like Splendor, Passion, Glamour, Pleasure, and Maestro, Hero MotoCorp has a dominant domestic market presence across entry, deluxe, premium motorcycles, and scooters.
 
 Business Segments
 Entry Segment: Core strength — 62% market share (FY22).
 
 Deluxe Segment: Well-established — 58% market share (FY22).
 
 Premium Segment: Emerging — 3.7% market share (FY22) with models like Glamour Xtec 125, collaboration with Harley-Davidson.
 
 Scooter Segment: Competes with 7.7% market share (FY22) — brands include Pleasure, Maestro, and special editions.
 
 Electric Vehicles (EV): New Vida brand launched; partnership with Ather Energy & BPCL for EV expansion.
 
 International Business: Exports contribute 7% of total revenue, with a growing global footprint.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Hero MotoCorp is accelerating its EV journey with plans to expand its Vida electric scooter brand to more cities and invest Rs 1,500 crore primarily in EVs and premium motorcycles. The company is modernizing over 500 dealerships and launching exclusive premium retail stores under its “Hero 2.0” strategy. Strategic tie-ups with Harley-Davidson and Gogoro, along with R&D investments, are set to position Hero as a key player in both traditional and sustainable mobility markets.

Name: Mahindra & Mahindra Ltd
Overview:
Mahindra & Mahindra Ltd (M&M) is a diversified Indian conglomerate with a leading presence in automotive, farm equipment, and allied businesses. The company operates globally, with 66 manufacturing facilities—41 in India and 25 abroad—and is recognized for strong execution, technological advancement, and an expanding international footprint.
 
 Business Segments (FY2025 Revenue)
 * Automotive Division: Revenue Share: 62% Fourth-largest in Indian passenger vehicles, No.2 in commercial vehicles, and the largest in light commercial vehicles (LCV)
 
 * Farm Equipment Division: Revenue Share: 34% Dominates the domestic tractor industry with a 43.3% market share, leading for 39 consecutive years
 
 * Other/Allied Businesses: Revenue Share: 4% Includes financial services, IT, auto components, hospitality, infrastructure, steel trading, logistics, defense, energy, and more
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
The company is aggressively investing in electric vehicles, supported by global financing partnerships with BII and Temasek, targeting the launch of five new EV SUVs by 2026. Expansion includes a new EV-dedicated manufacturing plant and capital raising of $250–$500 million to scale production. Strong market leadership in tractors and small commercial vehicles provides stable cash flows to fund growth, while diversification into allied services and digital initiatives enhances overall group performance. Rising exports and strategic joint ventures further strengthen technological capabilities and global footprint.

Name: Tata Motors Ltd
Overview:
Tata Motors Group, part of the Tata Group conglomerate, is a leading global automobile manufacturer with a diversified portfolio spanning passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles (CVs), SUVs, trucks, buses, and defense vehicles. It operates across India, the UK, Europe, China, North America, and other key global markets.
 
 Revenue Mix & Key Segments
 Jaguar Land Rover (JLR): 67% of total revenue — the luxury car arm with a strong global footprint.
 
 Tata Commercial Vehicles: 19% — market leader in India’s CV space.
 
 Tata Passenger Vehicles: 11% — includes hatchbacks, sedans, SUVs, and EVs.
 
 Vehicle financing & Others: Remaining share.
 
 Market Share & Position
 Commercial Vehicles (India): ~45% market share  — clear segment leader.
 
 Electric Vehicles (India): ~88% share — India’s EV pioneer.
 
 Global Reach:
 
 Revenue split: India (34%), China (18%), North America (19%), UK (14%), Europe (14%).
 
 Units sold: India (70%), China (10%), US (8%), Europe (7%), UK (6%).
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Tata Motors is pursuing an aggressive EV roadmap with rapid rollout of localized electric vehicles and next-generation EV architectures to drive future growth. JLR’s “Reimagine” strategy, focused on premiumization and full-scale electrification—including the first all-electric Range Rover in 2026—positions the brand for leadership in luxury and sustainability. Strong financial health, record cash flows, and positive net cash enable increased investments in R&D, green technologies, and product innovation across new segments like SUV-coupés and lifestyle EVs. Expansion of exports, local manufacturing, and targeted penetration in key global markets, combined with a commitment to net-zero emissions, underpins Tata Motors’ sustainable and future-ready growth strategy.

Name: TVS Motor Company Ltd
Overview:
TVS Motor Company is a leading Indian two-wheeler and three-wheeler manufacturer with a strong domestic and growing international presence. It offers a wide portfolio across scooters, motorcycles, mopeds, three-wheelers, and electric vehicles (EVs), with a strategic focus on sustainable mobility and technological innovation.
 
 Business Segments
 Scooters: Popular models like TVS Jupiter 125, Zest 110, Scooty Pep+.
 
 Motorcycles: Strong brands such as TVS Apache RTR series, Raider, and Radeon.
 
 Mopeds: Entry-level mobility solutions.
 
 Three-Wheelers: Commercial mobility segment.
 
 Electric Vehicles (EVs): iQube e-scooter, upcoming electric three-wheelers, and partnerships for last-mile electric deliveries.
 
 Revenue & Operations :
 The company’s revenue is broadly diversified across scooters, motorcycles, mopeds, and three-wheelers, with a growing contribution from the EV segment. Strategic tie-ups with Amazon India, BMW Motorrad, and Grupo Q expand its market reach and technology capabilities. Partnerships like the one with Zomato for deploying 10,000 EVs underline its commitment to green mobility.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

TVS Motor is scaling up its EV business with plans to produce 25,000 units per month and double its EV retail footprint in FY24. Strategic international expansions, such as into Central America and Nepal, along with partnerships with BMW Motorrad and Zomato, will aid global market penetration. Backed by significant investments in ICE and EV development, and a Rs 1,200 crore MoU for EV ecosystem expansion, TVS aims to drive sustainable growth through innovation, capacity expansion, and strategic alliances.

Name: VST Tillers Tractors Ltd
Overview:
VST Tillers Tractors Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in agricultural machinery, including power tillers, tractors, and allied equipment. Established in 1967 and headquartered in Bangalore, the company operates manufacturing facilities in Hosur, Maluru, and Mysuru.

The company focuses on delivering high-quality, efficient, and durable farm equipment, leveraging advanced technologies and a skilled workforce. VST Tillers Tractors is committed to innovation and operational excellence to meet the evolving demands of the agricultural sector and achieve sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
VST Tillers Tractors Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the agricultural machinery industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, VST Tillers Tractors is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Ashok Leyland Ltd
Overview:
Ashok Leyland is India’s second-largest manufacturer in the Medium & Heavy Commercial Vehicle (M&HCV) segment and is globally ranked among the top bus and truck producers. A flagship of the Hinduja Group, Ashok Leyland is renowned for its extensive product portfolio covering trucks, buses, LCVs, defense vehicles, and power solutions. Its strong brand trust, technological innovation, and wide service network make it a leader in India’s commercial vehicle space.
 
 Business Segments
  Commercial Vehicles (CVs):
 Core revenue driver (88% of total), including M&HCV trucks, buses, ICVs, LCVs, and newly launched CNG models for last-mile delivery and e-commerce needs.
 
 Foundry Division:
 Manufactures critical components such as cylinder blocks, heads, and tractor housings, supplying both in-house and third-party clients.
 
 Defense & Special Vehicles:
 Supplies bulletproof and fully built defense vehicles; delivered 1,125 units including kits in FY22.
 
 Financial Services:
 Contributes ~12% of revenue, supporting vehicle financing and fleet solutions.
 
 International Business:
 Operations in over 50 countries with export markets contributing about 11% of revenue.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ashok Leyland is actively pursuing a comprehensive growth strategy to enhance its market presence and technological capabilities. The company is investing significantly in the development and manufacturing of next-generation batteries in India, in partnership with China's CALB Group, to support the transition to electric mobility and renewable energy solutions. Additionally, Ashok Leyland is expanding its network in North India by establishing over 50 new outlets, aiming to increase its market share in this key region. The company is also focusing on cost optimization, targeting savings of over ₹2,000 crore, to improve profitability and financial efficiency.

Name: Menon Bearings Ltd
Overview:
Menon Bearings Ltd manufactures engine bearings, bushes, and thrust washers, catering to both automotive OEMs and replacement markets. The company is a trusted supplier to major automobile manufacturers in India.

Its product portfolio serves passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, and industrial applications. The company has also expanded into exports, gradually increasing its overseas revenue share.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
The company is positioning itself for robust growth, backed by rising automobile demand and global expansion. It has the potential to generate ₹55 crore annually from a new tractor component, half of which is already approved and slated for May 2025 production. With an established OEM base, global export momentum, aggressive capacity expansion, and diversification into EVs and new segments, Menon Bearings is well-poised for steady growth ahead.

Name: Grindwell Norton Ltd
Overview:
Grindwell Norton Limited, established in 1950, is a leading manufacturer specializing in Abrasives, Ceramics & Plastics, and Digital Services. As a part of the Saint-Gobain Group, the company serves a diverse range of industries, providing high-performance solutions that cater to both domestic and international markets.

With a strong focus on innovation and quality, Grindwell Norton has built a reputation for delivering value-added products and services. The company's commitment to sustainability and technological advancement positions it as a key player in its sector, continuously adapting to meet the evolving needs of its customers.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Grindwell Norton is focused on enhancing its product offerings and expanding its market presence. The company is investing in research and development to drive innovation and meet the evolving demands of its customers. Additionally, strategic initiatives are in place to improve operational efficiencies and sustainability practices. These efforts are expected to position the company for continued growth and success in the coming years.

Name: Carborundum Universal Ltd
Overview:
Carborundum Universal Ltd (CUMI), a flagship of the Murugappa Group, is a diversified materials science company engaged in manufacturing abrasives, ceramics, refractories, and electro-minerals. With a wide array of products and applications, the company caters to several industrial segments across domestic and international markets. Its robust portfolio and consistent innovation have made it a leader in several categories, including abrasives and silicon carbide production.

CUMI has a strong global footprint, supported by manufacturing units in India, Russia, Germany, South Africa, and Australia. The company has grown through organic expansion and acquisitions, such as RHODIUS Abrasives and Silicon Carbide Products LLC. Its strategy focuses on core business growth, M&A, and emerging sectors like clean energy, semiconductors, and electric mobility.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Carborundum Universal Ltd (CUMI) is focusing on expanding its product portfolio and enhancing its technological capabilities to cater to emerging industries.
