Sterling Tools Ltd, incorporated in 1979, is a leading manufacturer of cold-forged, high-tensile fasteners catering to passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, two-wheelers, farm equipment, and construction sectors. With four fastener plants across Faridabad, Ballabhgarh, Palwal, and Bengaluru, STL has built strong relationships with major OEMs including Maruti Suzuki, Hyundai, Tata, Honda, Hero, and Ashok Leyland.

The company has diversified into the electric mobility ecosystem through subsidiaries. Sterling Gtake E-Mobility (SGEM) focuses on MCUs, power electronics, and rare earth–free motors, while Sterling Tech-Mobility (STML) is setting up facilities for high-voltage DC contactors and relays. Supported by global collaborations, STL is positioning itself as a first-mover in EV components, balancing its legacy fasteners business with new-age technologies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management remains optimistic despite short-term setbacks, citing strong order pipelines, import substitution opportunities, and leadership in niche EV products. The strategy is clear: sustain steady growth in fasteners while scaling e-mobility businesses, with the vision of transforming STL into a leading “Make in India” EV component supplier.

Name: Happy Forgings Ltd
Overview:
Happy Forgings Limited (HFL), incorporated in 1979, is a leading Indian manufacturer of heavy forgings and high-precision machined components. The company ranks as the fourth-largest in India for complex, safety-critical forged products by forging capacity, and the second-largest manufacturer of crankshafts for commercial vehicles and high-horsepower industrial applications.

HFL caters to both automotive and non-automotive sectors, supplying key components such as crankshafts, differential housings, front axle carriers, valve bodies, and transmission parts. Its three manufacturing plants in Punjab house a total forging capacity of 120,000 MT and machining capacity of 52,500 MT, equipped with rare high-tonnage forging presses of 14,000 tonnes and 8,000 tonnes.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
HFL is strategically positioned for export-led growth supported by enhanced machining capacity and its entry into new international markets. The focus remains on high-margin, precision-machined components and diversification across automotive and non-automotive sectors to mitigate cyclical risks. With new capacity additions in FY25, the company is expected to drive higher export volumes and strengthen its position in the premium heavy forgings market.

Name: Sundaram Clayton Ltd
Overview:
Sundaram Clayton Ltd, established in 1962, is a leading Indian manufacturer of aluminum die-cast components for the automotive industry. The company specializes in producing high-pressure, low-pressure, and gravity die-cast parts, including clutch housings, filter heads, air connectors, and cylinder heads for heavy commercial vehicles, passenger cars, and two-wheelers. Sundaram Clayton serves both domestic and international markets, with a significant presence in North America, its largest export market. 

As part of the $8.5 billion TVS Group, Sundaram Clayton operates multiple manufacturing facilities equipped with advanced production, testing, and quality assurance capabilities. The company's commitment to innovation and quality has earned it recognition in the automotive components sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sundaram Clayton Ltd is focused on expanding its global presence and enhancing its manufacturing capabilities. The company plans to increase its production capacity by investing in new facilities and upgrading existing ones. Sundaram Clayton aims to strengthen its position in the North American market by improving delivery lead times and responsiveness to customer needs. Additionally, the company is exploring opportunities to diversify its product portfolio and enter new markets to drive sustainable growth.

Name: Gala Precision Engineering Ltd
Overview:
Gala Precision Engineering Ltd, established in 2009, is an Indian manufacturer specializing in high-quality technical springs and high-tensile fasteners. The company offers a diverse range of products, including Disc Springs (DSS), Coil and Spiral Springs (CSS), and Special Fastening Solutions (SFS). These components are integral to various industries such as renewable energy, mobility, industrial machinery, and construction.

Gala Precision operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities equipped with advanced technologies like CNC machining, wire-cut EDM, and fatigue testing. The company exports to over 25 countries, with a significant presence in Europe, North America, and Asia.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Gala Precision aims to achieve an 8–10% revenue growth in FY26, driven by increased demand in the industrial and mobility sectors. The company is focusing on climate-resilient and digitally compatible components, aligning with industry trends towards predictive maintenance and sensor-based diagnostics.

Name: CIE Automotive India Ltd
Overview:
CIE Automotive Limited, a subsidiary of CIE Automotive S.A., Spain, is a diversified automotive components manufacturer with a strong multi-technology and multi-location presence. It serves both domestic and global OEMs and operates manufacturing facilities across India, Europe, and Mexico. The company is the CIE Group’s primary global vehicle for its forgings business and offers a comprehensive product portfolio catering to multiple automotive segments.

Its operations are structured into six primary business segments: Forging, Aluminum Casting, Gears, Stamping, Casting, and Magnetic Products & Composites. The company is also actively building its EV product portfolio, covering motor housings, battery systems, and electronics. In CY23, India contributed 63% to revenues, while Europe accounted for 37%, supported by 26 manufacturing facilities, including 4 in Europe and 1 in Mexico.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
CIE Automotive is strengthening its position in the EV segment, with a growing share of EV orders in its European business and a strong domestic market presence. This transition, alongside steady capex investment and a diversified global customer base, positions the company for sustainable growth in the evolving automotive landscape.

Name: M M Forgings Ltd
Overview:
M M Forgings Limited, established in 1946, is a leading manufacturer of steel forgings in India. The company specializes in producing raw, semi-machined, and fully machined forgings across various grades of carbon, alloy, micro-alloy, and stainless steels. With a production capacity of 110,000 metric tons per annum, M M Forgings serves diverse industries, including automotive, power, construction, mining, and railways.

Headquartered in Chennai, M M Forgings operates multiple manufacturing facilities in Tamil Nadu. The company has expanded its global footprint through subsidiaries like Suvarchas Vidyut, focusing on electrical and electronic components for automotive applications. M M Forgings is also venturing into the electric vehicle (EV) parts manufacturing sector, aiming to capitalize on the growing demand for EV components.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
M M Forgings aims to achieve a 6% compound annual growth rate (CAGR) in revenue and EBITDA over FY25–28. This growth is expected to be driven by increased demand in the domestic medium and heavy commercial vehicle (M&HCV) sector, new orders, and expansion into the EV parts manufacturing segment.

Name: AIA Engineering Ltd
Overview:
AIA Engineering Limited is a global manufacturer of high-chrome grinding media, liners, and diaphragms—collectively known as mill internals. These components are crucial in crushing and grinding processes in the cement, mining, aggregates, and power utility industries. AIA employs a casting process for production and has become the world’s second-largest producer of grinding media, particularly catering to India’s cement sector.

The company operates five manufacturing facilities and ten warehouses, supplying products to over 120 countries. With a total production capacity of 460,000 MT, it continues to expand globally. Its international footprint is further supported by subsidiaries such as Vega Industries. AIA is now venturing into renewable energy with a mix of wind and hybrid installations aimed at powering up to 70% of operations by FY26.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

AIA Engineering is set to grow steadily by expanding its production capacity and enhancing its global reach—boosting manufacturing volume through ongoing facility upgrades and new plant additions. With its high-chrome mill internals gaining traction over conventional alternatives, especially in the mining sector, the firm is focused on converting mines worldwide to its more efficient, durable solutions. It continues to invest in renewable energy to power operations and drive sustainability.

Name: Sona BLW Precision Forgings Ltd
Overview:
Sona BLW Precision Forgings Ltd is a leading Indian automotive technology company specializing in the design and manufacturing of high-precision components for global mobility OEMs. It supplies a range of engineered mechanical and electrical systems including differential assemblies, precision gears, starter motors, EV traction motors, and software-integrated mobility solutions. The company serves all vehicle segments—passenger, commercial, and electric—across both electrified and internal combustion platforms.

With a robust presence in global markets and state-of-the-art manufacturing and R&D capabilities across India, Europe, North America, and Asia, Sona BLW is focused on the electrification of mobility. The company continues to deepen its EV capabilities through product innovations, collaborations, acquisitions, and greenfield expansions. In H1 FY25, it achieved strong EV-led revenue growth and expanded its order book significantly with a 78% EV share.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sona BLW Precision Forgings is steering toward sustained growth by deepening its foothold in electric mobility—boosting orders for EV driveline components while building strength in sensors and software through strategic acquisitions. It’s expanding globally via a new manufacturing subsidiary in Mexico and forming a joint venture to enter the Chinese EV market. To reduce reliance on imports, the company plans domestic production of critical rare-earth magnets.

Name: Ramkrishna Forgings Ltd
Overview:
Ramkrishna Forgings Ltd (RKFL) is India’s second-largest forging company specializing in forged components for automotive, railway, and engineering sectors. Its diverse product portfolio spans over 2,000 SKUs catering to commercial and passenger vehicles, railways, energy, farm equipment, and off-road industries. The company operates both domestically and internationally with a significant export presence.

RKFL faces short-term headwinds from macroeconomic challenges and US tariff-related uncertainties but is strategically expanding capacities and product lines. It is enhancing its presence in aluminium forgings for electric vehicles and railway undercarriage assemblies, aiming to capture emerging growth opportunities in India and global markets.
Key Takeways:
.Future Outlook
Ramkrishna Forgings is focused on strengthening its position in passenger vehicle exports, railway undercarriage assemblies, and aluminium forging for EVs. The railway segment, supported by significant orders and approvals, offers a sizable growth opportunity. The company aims to improve profitability by optimizing product mix, expanding capacities, and navigating tariff and steel price uncertainties. Net debt reduction and capital infusion provide financial stability for growth initiatives.

Name: Bharat Forge Ltd
Overview:
Bharat Forge, part of the Kalyani Group, is a leading global manufacturer and exporter of forged and machined components for the automotive and industrial sectors. It also supplies critical components to wind, hydro, nuclear, defense, and aerospace applications. Its Pune plant is the world’s largest single-location forging unit.
 
 Business Segments
 Automotive (60%): OEM components like crankshafts, front axles, and assemblies.
 
 Industrial (40%): Parts for Oil & Gas, Construction, Mining, Power, Aerospace.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bharat Forge is strategically expanding into high-growth sectors, including defence, aerospace, and electric vehicles (EVs). The company is enhancing its defence capabilities by establishing a comprehensive manufacturing complex in Andhra Pradesh, which will produce explosives, ammunition, and components for rockets and missiles.

Name: PTC Industries Ltd
Overview:
PTC Industries Limited is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in precision metal components for critical and supercritical applications across sectors such as defense, aerospace, oil & gas, LNG, marine, and energy. Its diverse product portfolio includes titanium castings, superalloy components, and precision CNC-machined parts, catering to both domestic and international clients.

In recent years, PTC has expanded its global footprint through strategic acquisitions and partnerships. Notably, in October 2024, the company acquired Trac Precision Solutions Ltd, a UK-based firm specializing in precision-engineered components for the aviation, defense, and power generation industries. This acquisition enhances PTC's capabilities in serving high-end markets and strengthens its position in the global supply chain for critical applications.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, PTC is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for precision components in defense, aerospace, and energy sectors. The recent acquisition of Trac Precision Solutions Ltd expands its capabilities and market reach, particularly in international markets. With a solid order book and ongoing capacity enhancements, PTC is poised for sustained growth and profitability in the coming years.

Name: Sundram Fasteners Ltd
Overview:
Sundaram Fasteners Ltd, part of the TVS Group, is a leading manufacturer of high-precision components catering to automotive, infrastructure, wind energy, and aerospace sectors. The company has built a broad domestic presence by supplying to almost all major OEMs, supported by a diversified portfolio across auto and non-auto segments.

Its growth strategy focuses on product mix improvement, geographic diversification, capacity expansion, and tapping new platforms like EV and hybrid programs. With strong operational discipline and investment in renewable energy, the company is positioned to deliver competitive margins while scaling its domestic and export footprint.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims for 8–9% revenue growth in FY26, with upside from export revival. Margins are expected to see incremental expansion through operational efficiencies, stable raw material costs, and export traction. The focus remains on growing non-auto and aftermarket segments, expanding geographically, and increasing renewable energy share to over 55% of total consumption.

Name: Dalmia Bharat Ltd
Overview:
Dalmia Bharat, established in 1939, is India’s 4th largest cement manufacturer by installed capacity. The company specializes in producing and selling various types of cement including PPC, PSC, PCC, and OPC, with a particular strength in blended cements. Its portfolio includes both retail and institutional brands such as Dalmia DSP Cement and InfraPro Cement.

The company operates 15 manufacturing plants across 10 states, supported by a wide distribution network of over 49,300 channel partners. With a current cement capacity of 49.5 MnT and clinker capacity of the same scale, Dalmia Bharat is undergoing aggressive capacity expansion with plans to reach 75 MnT by FY28 and 110–130 MnT by FY31, driven by brownfield and greenfield projects.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Dalmia Bharat is aggressively expanding its cement production capacity to meet the growing demand driven by infrastructure and housing sectors. The company plans to increase its capacity to 75 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) by FY28, with a long-term target of 110–130 MTPA by FY30–31. This expansion includes a ₹3,520 crore investment in Maharashtra and Karnataka, adding 6 MTPA through new clinker and grinding units.

Name: Ambuja Cements Ltd
Overview:
Ambuja Cements Ltd is a leading Indian cement manufacturer and part of the global LafargeHolcim Group. It produces and sells various types of cement, aggregates, and concrete, catering to a wide range of construction needs in India and abroad. The company is known for trusted brands like Ambuja Plus, Ambuja Roof Special, OPC 43 & 53 Grade, and PPC, with a strong focus on quality and sustainability.
 
Business Segments
 Cement: Core business, contributing nearly 90-95% of total revenue through Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC), Portland Pozzolana Cement (PPC), and Portland Slag Cement (PSC).
 
 Ready Mix Concrete & Aggregates: Contribute the remaining 5-10% of revenue; these segments support value-added offerings and project-based solutions.
 
 Sustainability Initiatives: Focus on alternative fuels and raw materials to reduce carbon footprint and enhance ESG performance.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ambuja Cements is on an expansion path, aiming to add 8 MTPA of clinker capacity and produce an additional 14 MTPA of Blended Green Cement within two years. The company has outlined a strategic roadmap to double its cement production capacity from 67.5 MTPA to approximately 135 MTPA over the next five years. It is focused on driving margin expansion through operational efficiency and a premium product mix while maintaining strong alignment with ESG goals

Name: J K Cements Ltd
Overview:
JK Cement is a prominent Indian cement manufacturer, producing both grey and white cement along with a growing portfolio of value-added products such as wall putty, tile adhesives, grouts, and paints. As of Q1FY26, it operates with a total grey cement capacity of 25.26 MTPA (including subsidiaries) and 3.05 MTPA of white cement and wall putty. The company also boasts a robust renewable and captive power infrastructure, highlighting its commitment to sustainability.

Focused on expanding its production and geographical footprint, JK Cement aims to double its capacity to 30 MTPA by 2030, with significant investments underway in new clinker and grinding units. The company maintains a balanced approach to growth through acquisitions, efficiency improvements, and ESG initiatives like carbon capture and green cement production.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

JK Cement is aggressively expanding its production capacity to meet the growing demand in India's infrastructure and housing sectors. The company has approved a ₹4,805 crore investment to set up a 7 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) greenfield cement plant in Jaisalmer, Rajasthan, which includes a 4 MTPA clinkerisation unit and a 3 MTPA cement grinding facility. Additionally, two split grinding units of 2 MTPA each will be established in Rajasthan and Punjab.

Name: UltraTech Cement Ltd
Overview:
UltraTech Cement Ltd stands as India’s largest cement manufacturer, boasting a domestic capacity of 114 million metric tonnes per annum (MTPA)—supported by an extensive network of 23 integrated plants, 27 grinding units, and countrywide distribution through 30,000 dealers, 64,000 retailers, and a fleet of 60,000 trucks. International operations in Bahrain, Sri Lanka, and the UAE contribute to around 4% of total revenues, highlighting a growing global footprint.
 
 Business Segment Revenue (FY2025)
 India Cement Operations:
 Contribute nearly 96% of total revenue, driven by infrastructure, housing, and industrial demand.
 
 Estimated Revenue Share: ~₹64,000 crore of total revenue (FY25 consolidated revenue estimated at ~₹66,800 crore).
 
 International Operations (Bahrain, Sri Lanka, UAE):
 Account for approximately 4% of revenue, or about ₹2,800 crore.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
UltraTech is focused on aggressive capacity expansion, targeting over 160 MTPA by FY25 and progressing toward a 200 MTPA goal, supported by both brownfield and greenfield projects to capture emerging domestic and international markets. The company emphasizes sustainable operations through increased use of alternative fuels and raw materials, waste heat recovery, and solar energy, reducing costs and environmental impact. Investments in R&D drive innovative, value-added products for infrastructure, housing, and premium construction, while a robust distribution network and diversified international presence support long-term revenue growth, operational efficiency, and risk mitigation.

Name: Shree Cement Ltd
Overview:
Shree Cement is India’s third-largest cement producer, known for its cost-efficient production processes and strong brand portfolio, including Roofon, Bangur Power, and Rock Strong. The company has built a robust network with four integrated cement plants and ten grinding units spread across ten states, ensuring a significant footprint in India’s cement industry.
 
 Business Segments
 Cement Segment:
 
 Contributes ~89% of total revenue.
 
 Products: OPC, PPC, PSC cements under various brand names.
 
 Power Segment:
 
 Operates captive power plants to ensure uninterrupted production and cost control.
 
 Revenue Summary
 Cement is the dominant revenue driver, underpinned by extensive manufacturing capacity and wide market reach.
 
 Continuous capacity additions through greenfield and brownfield projects secure future growth and strengthen market share.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Shree Cement is embarking on a multi-pronged expansion strategy, investing Rs 55–60 billion in upcoming integrated plant projects across Rajasthan, West Bengal, Uttar Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh, and Karnataka. This is expected to boost capacity by around 10.5 MnTPA, increasing total output from 46.4 MnTPA to 55.9 MnTPA in the near term, with an ambitious target of 80 MnTPA by FY30.

Name: ACC Ltd
Overview:
ACC Limited, part of the Adani Group, is a leading cement and ready-mix concrete manufacturer in India. The company operates extensive manufacturing facilities and primarily serves the domestic market. It has shown strong volume growth driven by both organic expansion and strategic acquisitions, improving its market share and premium product sales.

ACC is actively expanding its capacity and investing heavily in efficiency, digitalization, and sustainability initiatives. The company aims to reduce costs, increase use of green power, and strengthen its brand and dealer network while maintaining a debt-free balance sheet.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
ACC aims to achieve EBITDA per tonne of ₹1,500 by FY28 through continued cost reductions and premiumization. Capacity expansion will be balanced geographically. The company is focused on closing the profitability gap with Ambuja via efficiency and product strategy, maintaining leadership in a growing market.

Name: Grasim Industries Ltd
Overview:
Grasim Industries Limited, the flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, is one of India's most diversified conglomerates, with leadership across several core industries: viscose staple fiber (VSF), chemicals (caustic soda, specialty chemicals), cement (through UltraTech Cement), financial services (via Aditya Birla Capital), textiles, and the newly launched paints business. The company is also expanding aggressively in retail through its textile arm.
 
 Business Segments with Latest Revenue (FY2025) 
 
 Building Materials (Cement, Paints, Putty, Construction Materials):
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Grasim’s growth is supported by major capex in paints, establishing a world-class production network and rapid pan-India rollout, alongside expansion in retail textiles with 100–120 new Linen Club outlets and increased presence across 12,000+ multi-brand retailers. Innovation through fast-growing B2B e-commerce and digital platforms, coupled with premiumisation and sustainable operations, enhances market share and brand positioning.

Name: The Ramco Cements Ltd
Overview:
Ramco Cements Ltd is a leading cement manufacturer in South and East India, engaged in the production of cement, ready-mix concrete (RMC), and dry mortar products. It operates a total cement capacity of 19.4 MTPA, supported by four integrated plants and six grinding units. The company also has substantial captive power capacity, including renewable energy from wind farms and waste heat recovery systems (WHRS).

The company continues to strengthen its market position by expanding premium product offerings, improving energy efficiency, and investing in capacity expansions. Its strategic presence across high-demand infrastructure and housing markets, along with a robust dealer network of over 9,000 dealers and 20,000 sub-dealers, provides a strong distribution backbone for sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management remains focused on expanding capacity, improving premium product mix, and enhancing operational efficiency through renewable energy and WHRS. Demand is expected to benefit from strong infrastructure and housing activity post-monsoon, though risks remain from potential oversupply and raw material cost inflation. The Karnataka Greenfield project and WHRS additions are key medium-term growth drivers.

Name: JSW Cement Ltd
Overview:
JSW Cement is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in green cement, renowned for its commitment to sustainability and its pivotal role in major infrastructure projects. The company offers a diverse product portfolio, including Ground Granulated Blast Furnace Slag (GGBS), Portland Slag Cement (PSC), Composite Cement (PCC), and Ready Mix Concrete (RMC), catering to various construction needs. 

JSW Cement operates multiple manufacturing units across India, strategically located to serve key markets efficiently. With a strong emphasis on environmental responsibility, the company integrates sustainable practices into its operations, aiming to reduce its carbon footprint and promote eco-friendly construction solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

JSW Cement is poised for significant growth, with plans to increase its production capacity by 65% to 34 million tonnes per annum by 2028, aiming to enhance its market presence and profitability. The company is committed to reducing its carbon dioxide emission intensity by 15% by 2026, focusing on integrating renewable energy sources and implementing waste heat recovery systems across its plants.

Name: BirlaNu Ltd
Overview:
HIL Limited (BirlaNu Ltd) is part of the C.K. Birla Group and headquartered in Hyderabad, India. It is a leading player in the building materials and construction industry, offering a wide range of products across roofing, building, polymer, flooring, and construction chemical solutions. The company combines modern manufacturing capabilities with strong R&D to deliver innovative and sustainable building solutions.

With a network of 30 manufacturing facilities in India and one in Germany/Austria, supported by over 21,000 retail points and 3,500 distributors, HIL ensures a strong domestic and international market reach. Its products cater to residential, commercial, and industrial construction projects, and the company maintains a global footprint in over 80 countries.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
HIL plans to invest $150 million to achieve $1 billion in revenue by 2028, focusing on capacity expansion, sustainability, and product diversification. Doubling AAC block capacity in Chennai and expanding polymer solutions, coupled with continued innovation across roofing, flooring, and construction chemicals, will drive long-term growth and strengthen its leadership in the Indian and international building materials market.

Name: Orient Cement Ltd
Overview:
Orient Cement Ltd is a prominent player in India's cement industry, engaged in the manufacture and sale of cement. The company operates three strategically located manufacturing facilities: Devapur in Telangana, Chittapur in Karnataka, and Jalgaon in Maharashtra. These plants are integral to Orient Cement's production capacity and distribution network, enabling it to cater to diverse regional markets across India.

With a focus on quality and sustainability, Orient Cement offers a range of products designed for various construction needs. The company emphasizes efficient manufacturing processes and has adopted modern technologies to enhance production capabilities. Additionally, Orient Cement is committed to environmental stewardship, implementing measures to reduce its carbon footprint and promote sustainable practices within the cement industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Orient Cement will experience an annual revenue growth of approximately 10.2% and earnings growth of around 15% over the next few years. This positive outlook is supported by the company's strategic initiatives and the anticipated rebound in the cement industry.

Name: HeidelbergCement India Ltd
Overview:
HeidelbergCement India, a subsidiary of Germany's Heidelberg Materials, operates in Central and Southern India, with plants in Damoh (Madhya Pradesh), Jhansi (Uttar Pradesh), and Ammasandra (Karnataka). The company produces and markets cement under the MyCem and MyCem Power brands, serving both retail and institutional customers.

The company's operations are strategically located to serve key markets in India, leveraging its extensive distribution network and strong brand presence. HeidelbergCement India focuses on delivering high-quality products and services, aiming to meet the growing demand for cement in the region. The company's commitment to sustainability and innovation positions it well to capitalize on opportunities in the evolving construction sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company's focus on expanding its product offerings, enhancing operational efficiencies, and leveraging renewable energy sources aligns with the growing demand for sustainable construction materials. With a robust pipeline of projects and a strategic approach to business expansion, HeidelbergCement India aims to strengthen its position in the Indian cement industry in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Andhra Cements Ltd
Overview: Andhra Cements Ltd is an Indian company engaged in the manufacture and sale of cement and cement-related products. Established in 1940, the company operates two manufacturing plants situated at Dachepalli and Visakha Cement Works in Andhra Pradesh. Andhra Cements Ltd produces various types of cement, including ordinary Portland cement and other specialty cements, catering to the construction industry's diverse needs. The company's products are marketed under the Sagar Cement brand, recognized for quality and reliability.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Andhra Cements Ltd, now managed by Sagar Cements Ltd, is strategically focusing on enhancing operational efficiency and expanding its capacity. The company has recently commissioned a 6 MW solar power plant at its Gamalapadu facility, aiming to reduce energy costs and improve sustainability. Additionally, Sagar Cements has undertaken a significant capacity expansion, increasing its total capacity to 10.5 million tonnes per annum, with further plans to scale up to 12.5 million tonnes per annum

Name: JK Lakshmi Cement Ltd
Overview:
JK Lakshmi Cement Ltd, part of the JK Group, is a key player in cement manufacturing and supply across multiple Indian states. The company produces a diverse portfolio including cement, Ready-Mix Concrete (RMC), and AAC blocks, supported by a strong brand presence through multiple well-recognized products. Expansion into new geographic markets, especially in Central India, underpins its volume growth strategy.

The company is actively investing in capacity expansion with projects like the Durg clinker and grinding units, alongside multiple mining leases in the North and West regions. Emphasis on renewable energy, cost efficiencies, and digital initiatives enhances operational competitiveness, while the integration benefits from recent mergers contribute to profitability improvements.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management anticipates volume growth above industry average in FY26, supported by new capacities and geographic expansion. Cost-saving initiatives and renewable energy targets are expected to further enhance margins. The company aims for a 30 mtpa capacity milestone by 2030, leveraging ongoing project completions and potential market entries.

Name: Prism Johnson Ltd
Overview:
Prism Johnson Ltd is a diversified building materials company in India providing cement (via Prism Cement), tiles and bath & kitchen through HRJ, and ready-mix concrete (RMC). It operates across manufacturing hubs and distribution networks to serve both retail and institutional segments nationwide.

The company focuses on product innovation, premiumization, and operational cost efficiencies—running an integrated model that spans production, distribution, and infrastructure solutions aimed at enhancing margins and market reach.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Prism Johnson is well-positioned to benefit from a demand revival in housing and infrastructure. The focus on increasing premium cement share, leveraging RMC volume recovery, and tapping synergies in its TBK business should drive margin improvement. With debt reduction and operational efficiencies underway, the company is targeting a return to profitability and moderate growth as volume and price dynamics stabilize.

Name: Birla Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Birla Corporation Ltd, part of the M.P. Birla Group, is engaged in manufacturing a diversified range of cement products, including OPC, PPC, PSC, and specialty blends, catering to retail, institutional, and infrastructure markets. The company also operates in the jute goods segment, producing high-quality jute products for government, export, and non-government sectors. Its cement portfolio is marketed under multiple popular, premium, and institutional brands, alongside allied offerings such as wall putty and construction chemicals.

With a strong presence in Central India and operations spread across multiple regions, the company has built a flexible supply chain and high plant utilization levels. Strategic priorities include capacity debottlenecking, premiumization of product mix, expansion of renewable power through WHRS, and targeted capex for long-term capacity growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects improved EBITDA/ton in FY26 as shutdown-related disruptions normalize, supported by higher premium brand sales, stable clinker supply, and operational efficiency gains. While Central India pricing remains a watchpoint, long-term growth will be driven by strategic capacity additions, premiumization, and sustainability investments aligned with 2027 and 2030 targets.

Name: Visaka Industries Ltd
Overview:
Visaka Industries Ltd is a diversified industrial company that specializes in building materials and construction solutions. The company manufactures fiber cement sheets, cement boards, roofing solutions, and allied construction products, catering to residential, commercial, and industrial infrastructure projects. Its modern manufacturing facilities and emphasis on quality allow Visaka to deliver durable, cost-effective, and environmentally friendly construction solutions.

The company also emphasizes sustainability, offering energy-efficient manufacturing processes and eco-friendly products. With a presence in domestic and international markets, Visaka Industries has established itself as a trusted provider in the building materials sector. Continuous investment in R&D and innovation ensures it stays ahead in providing advanced construction solutions that meet evolving market and regulatory standards.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Visaka Industries Ltd is actively pursuing expansion in its non-asbestos product lines, notably through the establishment of a new ₹100 crore Vnext board plant in Tamil Nadu, aimed at increasing capacity from 170,000 MT to 220,000 MT. The company is also considering setting up a synthetic yarn spinning mill in West Bengal to bolster its textiles division

Name: Everest Industries Ltd
Overview:
Everest Industries Ltd is one of India’s oldest and fastest-growing providers of comprehensive building solutions. It offers an integrated range of products from roofing, ceilings, wall panels, and flooring to pre-engineered steel buildings serving residential, commercial, and industrial markets.

Built on decades of experience, the company emphasizes innovation and quality in manufacturing, with operations spanning India and extending to about 35 countries. Its integrated approach from development to installation positions it as a trusted name in construction materials.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Everest Industries Ltd is strategically expanding its manufacturing footprint to meet the growing demand for its building solutions. The company has recently inaugurated a state-of-the-art facility in Chamarajanagar, Karnataka, with an annual capacity of 72,000 MT for fiber cement boards and 19,000 MT for Rapicon panels.

Name: Deccan Cements Ltd
Overview: Deccan Cements Ltd is an India-based cement production company established in 1979. The company is engaged in the manufacturing and selling of cement, offering products such as ordinary Portland cement, Portland pozzolana cement, and Portland slag cement. Deccan Cements Ltd also generates and sells electricity through thermal, wind, and hydro-electric power units, contributing to sustainable energy solutions. The company's products are marketed under the Deccan Cement brand, recognized for quality and performance.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Deccan Cements Ltd is actively pursuing significant expansion to enhance its market presence. The company is implementing a major capacity expansion project at its Bhavanipuram plant in Telangana, aiming to increase clinker production from 1.7 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) to 3.2 MTPA and cement production from 1.8 MTPA to 4.0 MTPA. This expansion involves a total project cost of approximately ₹1,218.61 crore, with funding secured through internal accruals and bank financing.

Name: Star Cement Ltd
Overview:
Star Cement Ltd is a leading cement manufacturer in Northeast India with a ~24% market share, known for its high-quality limestone reserves and diverse product offerings, including OPC, PPC, ARC, PCC, and WSC. The company operates six manufacturing facilities across Meghalaya, Assam, and West Bengal, with clinker capacity of 2.8 MTPA, cement capacity of 5.7 MTPA, and 63.3 MW of captive power including WHRS.

The company has a strong distribution network of over 2,000 dealers and 11,000 merchants across 10 states, with a dominant presence in Northeast India. It is pursuing aggressive capacity expansion, targeting ~10 MTPA cement capacity by FY26 and evaluating a significant entry into Rajasthan to achieve its long-term vision of 18–20 MTPA capacity in the next 5–6 years.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management guides for FY26 cement sales volumes of 5.4–5.5 MTPA, with Q1 possibly softer due to early monsoon. Premium cement share is targeted to reach 20% in FY26. No major competitive capacity additions are expected in Northeast India for 3–4 years, supporting stable pricing. With ongoing brownfield expansions, a potential Rajasthan foray, and a steady subsidy regime, Star Cement is well-positioned for capacity-led growth while maintaining balance sheet discipline.

Name: NCL Industries Ltd
Overview:
NCL Industries Ltd operates in the building materials sector with a diversified presence across cement, boards, and ready-mix concrete. Its cement division produces a range of products, including Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC) and Portland Pozzolana Cement (PPC), catering to residential, commercial, and infrastructure projects with a focus on quality, durability, and environmental compliance. The boards division, marketed under the brand “NCL VENEER BOARD,” serves furniture manufacturing, interior design, and construction applications, emphasizing precision, surface finish, and eco-friendly production.

The ready-mix concrete (RMC) division provides customized concrete solutions for commercial, industrial, and infrastructure projects, prioritizing timely delivery, technical expertise, and compliance with project specifications. Across all divisions, NCL Industries integrates research and development, modern technology, and sustainability into its operations. With a strong distribution network, the company serves contractors, developers, architects, and retail customers, positioning itself as a comprehensive solutions provider in India’s construction ecosystem and continuously expanding its market presence.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

NCL Industries Ltd is actively pursuing expansion across its diverse business segments to bolster growth. The company is in the process of upgrading its cement plant at Mattapalli, Telangana, with the installation of a double-string preheater to enhance clinker production capacity from 2.6 to 4.0 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) and cement capacity from 2.0 to 4.0 MTPA. Additionally, NCL Buildtek, a part of the NCL Group, is raising ₹25 crore through a preference share allotment to fund its expansion into new product lines such as UPVC doors and windows

Name: Shree Digvijay Cement Co. Ltd
Overview:
Shree Digvijay Cement Company Ltd, incorporated in 1944 and based in Gujarat, operates in the cement manufacturing industry. It caters to the construction sector by producing and distributing cement primarily under the Digvijay cement brand. 

The company’s operations are strategically located in the coastal Sikka region, enabling efficient logistics and supply to both domestic markets and infrastructure projects. With an installed capacity of around 1.5 million tonnes per annum, Shree Digvijay Cement has been steadily enhancing its production capabilities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Shree Digvijay Cement is well-positioned for growth, underpinned by its enhanced production capacities and improving market conditions. The new grinding unit—slated to ramp up production—combined with rising cement demand and pricing, offers a strong foundation for improved revenue and profitability in the coming quarters

Name: Mangalam Cement Ltd
Overview:
Mangalam Cement Limited, established in 1976, is a prominent cement manufacturer in India. The company operates two manufacturing plants located in Morak, Rajasthan, and Aligarh, Uttar Pradesh. Under the brand name Birla Uttam Cement, Mangalam Cement produces a range of products including Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC) and Portland Pozzolana Cement (PPC).

Managed by Smt. Vidula Jalan of the B.K. Birla Group, Mangalam Cement is known for its commitment to quality and innovation in the cement industry. The company serves various sectors such as infrastructure, housing, and industrial projects, contributing significantly to the construction and development landscape in India.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Mangalam Cement aims to enhance its production capacity and expand its market presence. The company is focusing on improving operational efficiencies and exploring new markets to drive growth. With a strong product portfolio and strategic initiatives, Mangalam Cement is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand in the Indian cement industry.

Name: Sanghi Industries Ltd
Overview:
Sanghi Industries Limited, established in 1985, is a prominent cement manufacturer in India. The company operates India's largest single-location integrated cement plant situated at Sanghipuram, Kutch, Gujarat. This state-of-the-art facility boasts a clinker production capacity of 6.6 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) and a cement grinding capacity of 6.1 MTPA. Additionally, it houses a 143 MW captive power plant and bulk cement terminals at Navlakhi (Gujarat) and Kochi (Kerala), ensuring efficient distribution across domestic and international markets .

Sanghi Industries is a subsidiary of Ambuja Cements, which is part of the diversified Adani Group. The company's product portfolio includes Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC), Portland Pozzolana Cement (PPC), and Ready Mix Concrete (RMC).
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Sanghi Industries aims to enhance its production capacity and expand its market reach. The company's strategic initiatives include optimizing operational efficiencies and exploring new market opportunities to drive growth in the coming years.

Name: Nuvoco Vistas Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Nuvoco Vista Corporation Ltd., part of the Nirma Group, is one of India’s largest cement producers and a major player in ready-mix concrete and modern building materials. With a consolidated capacity of 25 MMTPA, it offers a diverse portfolio spanning premium and popular cement brands, concrete products, and allied construction materials, supported by a strong distribution network.

The company has built a geographically diversified footprint and continues to focus on operational efficiency, premiumization, and sustainability. Strategic acquisitions, such as Vadraj Cement, coupled with cost optimization programs and product mix improvement, position Nuvoco to strengthen its market presence and improve margins over the medium term.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects industry demand growth of 8–10% post-monsoon, supported by government capex and housing initiatives. The focus remains on sustaining ₹1,000+/ton EBITDA through premiumization, trade channel optimization, home market sales, and integration of new capacity, positioning the company for steady volume and margin growth in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Sagar Cements Ltd
Overview:
Sagar Cements Ltd is a prominent Indian cement manufacturer specializing in the production of Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC) and Portland Pozzolana Cement (PPC). Established in 1981 and headquartered in Hyderabad, the company operates integrated and grinding units across India.

The company focuses on delivering high-quality cement products, leveraging advanced technologies and a skilled workforce. Sagar Cements is committed to innovation and operational excellence to meet the evolving demands of its clientele and achieve sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Sagar Cements Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the cement industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, Sagar Cements is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Ramco Industries Ltd
Overview:
Ramco Industries Ltd is an Indian manufacturing company engaged in chemicals, building materials, and industrial products. The company caters to industrial, construction, and consumer sectors with a diversified product portfolio.

Headquartered in Chennai, Ramco Industries emphasizes innovation, quality, and customer-centric solutions. Its integrated operations and strategic investments support growth across domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ramco Industries Ltd is strategically enhancing its product portfolio and expanding its manufacturing capabilities to capitalize on the growing demand for sustainable building materials. The company has increased its calcium silicate board production capacity to 11.5 million square meters annually, positioning itself to meet the rising market needs for fire-resistant, water-resistant, and eco-friendly construction materials.

Name: India Cements Ltd
Overview:
India Cements Ltd is one of India’s oldest and most recognized cement manufacturers, with a strong presence in the southern market. Over the decades, the company has diversified into allied businesses such as shipping, captive power, and coal mining, creating synergies with its core cement operations. It is also known for its brand association with the Chennai Super Kings IPL team.

The company’s product mix primarily includes Ordinary Portland Cement and Portland Pozzolana Cement, catering to diverse infrastructure and housing needs. Supported by a wide distribution network and recent strategic integrations, it continues to focus on operational efficiency, sustainability, and capacity expansion to strengthen its competitive position in the growing Indian cement industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is optimistic about sustained demand growth over the next decade, driven by infrastructure spending, housing expansion, and government-led mega projects. Integration benefits, cost optimization, and brand consolidation under UltraTech are expected to drive higher profitability and market share.

Name: Saurashtra Cement Ltd
Overview:
Saurashtra Cement Ltd is a flagship company of the Mehta Group, established in 1956. Headquartered in Mumbai, India, the company operates primarily in the cement and paints sectors. It markets its cement products under the well-known Hathi Cement brand.

SCL's manufacturing facilities include an integrated cement plant in Ranavav, Gujarat, and a clinkerization plant in the UAE. The company is certified under ISO 9001:2015, ISO 14001:2015, and ISO 45001:2018, ensuring adherence to international quality, environmental, and occupational health and safety standards.
Key Takeways:
Future/Growth Outlook
Saurashtra Cement Ltd is focused on expanding its production capacity and market presence. The company is exploring opportunities for inorganic growth and enhancing its product offerings to meet evolving market demands, positioning itself to strengthen its footprint in the Indian cement industry.

Name: Udaipur Cement Works Ltd
Overview:
Udaipur Cement Works Ltd is a leading cement manufacturing company in India, known for producing high-quality cement products for various construction applications. The company has a strong presence in the northern and western regions of India.

In FY25, Udaipur Cement reported a total income of ₹1,500 crore, a 12% increase from the previous year. The company's EBITDA stood at ₹300 crore, with a margin of 20%, and a PAT of ₹150 crore, reflecting a 10% year-over-year growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Udaipur Cement Works Limited (UCWL) is actively pursuing a comprehensive growth strategy focused on capacity expansion, green energy adoption, and product diversification. The company has significantly increased its cement grinding capacity, enhancing its production capabilities. Additionally, UCWL is investing in renewable energy initiatives, including the installation of solar power systems and the expansion of waste heat recovery capacities, aiming to achieve a substantial share of green energy in its total power consumption.

Name: BIGBLOC Construction Ltd
Overview:
BigBloc Construction Ltd., established in 2015, is a leading manufacturer of Aerated Autoclaved Concrete (AAC) blocks and panels in India. The company operates across multiple states, including Gujarat and Maharashtra, with an annual installed capacity of 1.3 million cubic meters. BigBloc's product offerings are recognized for their strength, lightweight properties, insulation, fire resistance, and eco-friendliness, making them suitable for various construction applications.

In addition to its core business, BigBloc is expanding into construction chemicals and has acquired approximately 57,500 square meters of land in Madhya Pradesh to establish India's largest greenfield AAC block facility.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
BigBloc Construction aims to enhance its operational efficiency and expand its market presence. The establishment of the new facility in Madhya Pradesh is expected to bolster production capacity and meet the increasing demand for AAC blocks.

Name: K C P Ltd
Overview:
K C P Ltd is a diversified conglomerate with operations spanning across various sectors, including cement, sugar, engineering, and power. Established in 1941, the company has a strong presence in the Indian market and is known for its commitment to quality and innovation. K C P Ltd has consistently delivered value to its stakeholders through strategic investments and operational excellence.

In the cement sector, K C P Ltd operates a modern manufacturing facility with a significant production capacity. The company has also ventured into the sugar industry, establishing a state-of-the-art sugar plant. Additionally, K C P Ltd's engineering division specializes in heavy engineering equipment and services, catering to diverse industries.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
K C P Ltd aims to strengthen its position in the cement industry by enhancing production capacity and exploring new markets. The company is also focusing on expanding its sugar and engineering divisions to drive growth. Additionally, K C P Ltd is committed to sustainable practices and renewable energy projects to ensure long-term success.

Name: Anjani Portland Cement Ltd
Overview:
Anjani Portland Cement Ltd is a South India-based cement manufacturer producing premium Portland cement. The company caters to both retail consumers and institutional buyers through a strong distribution network.

Focused on delivering superior cement solutions, Anjani combines traditional manufacturing with environmentally mindful processes and community-forward initiatives. Its BV Raju Institute of Cement Technology underscores a commitment to training and inclusive growth, giving it a distinctive edge in the industry
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With a clear improvement in top-line and profitability trends heading into FY26, Anjani Portland Cement is well poised for a potential comeback as demand stabilizes. Continued focus on efficiency and quality, along with steady geographic coverage, may pave the way for stronger margins and improved financial stability in the near future.

Name: Plastiblends India Ltd
Overview:
Plastiblends India Ltd is a leading manufacturer of masterbatches used in the plastics industry. Its products serve diverse applications across packaging, automotive, consumer goods, textiles, and agriculture.

The company has a strong distribution network and exports to multiple countries, making it a significant player in the global masterbatch segment. Continuous R&D investments help it maintain an innovative product portfolio.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
Plastiblends is planning capacity expansion to cater to rising demand from both domestic and export markets. With per capita plastic consumption in India still below global averages, the company sees significant headroom for growth in packaging, consumer goods, and industrial applications. Its focus on specialty and sustainable masterbatch solutions, along with strengthening its export base, positions it well for long-term growth and margin improvement.

Name: Andhra Petrochemicals Ltd
Overview:
Andhra Petrochemicals Ltd is a leading Indian producer of oxo-alcohols, primarily used in the manufacture of plasticizers, paints, adhesives, and coatings. The company operates a large facility in Visakhapatnam and caters to both domestic and global clients.

With a focus on import substitution, Andhra Petrochemicals plays a key role in reducing India’s dependence on imported oxo-alcohols. Its strategic location near the port enhances export competitiveness, while backward linkages improve supply security.Andhra Petrochemicals Ltd is engaged in the manufacture of oxo-alcohols, catering to industries such as paints, coatings, plasticizers, and PVC. With a diversified product base including butanol and 2-ethylhexanol, the company plays a critical role in the chemical value chain for construction, automotive, and consumer goods sectors.

Its operations are supported by strong domestic demand and selective exports, while efficiency-focused initiatives and capacity utilization improvements have allowed the company to sustain supply reliability. Positioned as one of the few domestic producers of oxo-alcohols, Andhra Petrochemicals benefits from import substitution opportunities in India’s growing industrial economy.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Andhra Petrochemicals is expected to benefit from rising demand in India’s construction and automotive sectors, with oxo-alcohol consumption projected to grow at 8–10% CAGR. Import substitution opportunities remain a long-term driver, as domestic producers like Andhra Petrochemicals are positioned to capture demand otherwise met by imports.

Name: Vinyl Chemicals (I) Ltd
Overview:
Vinyl Chemicals (India) Ltd, established in 1986, is a leading chemical trading company in India. The company specializes in the trading of Vinyl Acetate Monomer (VAM), a key raw material used in the production of adhesives, paints, textiles, and other industrial applications. VAM is sourced from various global suppliers and distributed across India, catering to a diverse customer base in the specialty chemicals sector. 

Headquartered in Mumbai, Vinyl Chemicals operates with a lean organizational structure, employing a small team to manage its extensive distribution network. The company has a strong market presence and is recognized for its reliable supply chain and quality products. It is also part of the Parekh Group, which has a significant footprint in the Indian chemicals industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Vinyl Chemicals aims to achieve a revenue of ₹625 crore in FY26, supported by its strategic initiatives and market positioning. The company is focusing on expanding its product offerings and enhancing its distribution capabilities to drive growth and profitability in the coming years.

Name: Goa Carbon Ltd
Overview: Goa Carbon Ltd is a leading manufacturer of calcined petroleum coke (CPC) and carbon products, serving the aluminum, steel, and chemical industries. The company operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Goa, equipped with advanced machinery to ensure high-quality products meeting international standards. Goa Carbon Ltd emphasizes research and development to enhance product quality, efficiency, and reduce environmental impact, maintaining a strong focus on sustainable operations.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Goa Carbon Ltd is stepping up its growth through innovation, research partnerships, and enhanced product offerings. The company has introduced its first branded product, gcarb+, aimed at elevating quality standards in the recarburiser and carbon additive market with sustainability at the forefront. It is also collaborating with a leading technical university to boost plant efficiency and environmental compliance through advanced modeling techniques.

Name: HP Adhesives Ltd
Overview: HP Adhesives Ltd is a leading manufacturer and supplier of adhesives, sealants, and bonding solutions in India. The company produces a wide range of products, including solvent cements, silicone sealants, contact adhesives, and industrial adhesives used across construction, automotive, packaging, and furniture industries. Founded by Mr. Haresh Motwani, HP Adhesives has a reputation for quality, innovation, and customer-centric solutions. The company leverages modern manufacturing facilities and quality control systems to ensure product consistency. Its strong distribution network and service orientation allow it to reach clients across India while maintaining competitive market positioning.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

HP Adhesives Ltd is actively expanding its product portfolio to cater to diverse industrial applications, including plumbing, construction, automotive, and footwear sectors. The company is enhancing its manufacturing capabilities and distribution network to meet the increasing demand for its adhesives and sealants. HP Adhesives is also focusing on innovation to develop new products that align with evolving market needs.

Name: Tuticorin Alkali Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd
Overview:
Tuticorin Alkali Chemicals and Fertilizers Ltd (TFL), established in 1971, is a prominent manufacturer based in Tuticorin, Tamil Nadu. The company specializes in producing Soda Ash, Ammonium Chloride Fertilizer, Sodium Bicarbonate, and Ammonium Bicarbonate. 

TFL operates with a focus on sustainable practices, adhering to stringent quality standards and environmental regulations. Its manufacturing facilities are strategically located near the SPIC Fertilizer Plant, facilitating efficient production and distribution.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
TFL aims to enhance its product offerings and expand its market presence. The company's focus on sustainable practices and continuous innovation positions it for potential growth in the coming years.

Name: Gujarat Gas Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat Gas Limited (GGL), India’s largest City Gas Distribution (CGD) company, supplies CNG and PNG to domestic, commercial, and industrial users across multiple states. It operates mainly in South & Central Gujarat and Saurashtra, and is backed by the Gujarat State Petroleum Corporation (GSPC) Group. GSPL, a major gas transmission player, holds a ~54% stake in GGL.

As of FY24, GGL manages a 38,100 km pipeline network and serves customers via 817 CNG stations and 21+ lakh PNG connections. With 27 CGD licenses across 6 states and 1 UT, the company has built a strong nationwide presence. Its scale and integration make it a critical part of India’s clean energy ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Gujarat Gas Limited is accelerating its expansion to make natural gas more accessible across India. It is investing heavily in infrastructure—planning a substantial capital expenditure to extend its pipeline network across multiple states while adding hundreds of thousands of new household connections and hundreds of CNG stations. The company is also reaching deeper into rural, tribal, and coastal areas, boosting its PNG coverage and enhancing supply flexibility.

Name: Gujarat State Petronet Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat State Petronet Limited is a leading natural gas transmission company in India, operating an extensive 2,694-kilometer pipeline network that makes it the second-largest player in the sector. Its infrastructure covers 25 of Gujarat’s 33 districts, connecting key industrial hubs from Vapi to Jafrabad. The company serves a diverse customer base, including refineries, steel plants, power producers, textiles, chemicals, fertilizers, petrochemicals, and city gas distributors, ensuring reliable energy supply for multiple sectors.

Beyond core gas transmission, the company has interests in city gas distribution and renewable power generation through windmills. Its subsidiaries, GSPL India Gasnet Ltd and GSPL India Transco Ltd, manage major cross-country pipeline projects, including a high-capacity route linking Gujarat to northern and western India. Focused on long-term growth, Gujarat State Petronet is actively expanding its pipeline network, exploring new transmission corridors, participating in city gas projects, and advancing renewable energy initiatives. This integrated strategy strengthens its market position, supports energy infrastructure development, and promotes sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

GSPL is actively pursuing expansion initiatives to enhance its infrastructure and service offerings. The company has planned significant capital expenditure over the next five years to expand its natural gas pipeline network, including laying new spur lines and augmenting existing capacities. These projects are subject to regulatory approvals and are expected to be funded primarily through internal accruals.

Name: Petronet LNG Ltd
Overview:
Petronet LNG Ltd operates as India’s leading company in LNG import, regasification, and terminal operations. It manages major terminals including Dahej and Kochi, and is expanding into the East Coast with the Gopalpur LNG terminal project. The company also focuses on growing petchem capacity through its PDH-PP project and increasing infrastructure to support India’s growing gas demand.

The business remains resilient despite short-term demand softness and operational challenges, benefiting from stable LNG pricing and long-term contracts. Strong balance sheet and strategic investments support its capacity expansion plans to serve India’s increasing energy needs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is optimistic about growth driven by East Coast expansion, petchem project ramp-up, and improving LNG demand in India. Strong financials and strategic projects position the company well to capitalize on rising LNG consumption and India’s energy transition.

Name: Aegis Logistics Ltd
Overview:
Aegis Logistics Limited, incorporated in 1956, is a prominent logistics solutions provider for the oil, gas, chemical, and petrochemical sectors. It operates a robust network of bulk liquid and LPG terminals, pipelines, filling plants, and distribution infrastructure across key ports in India. The company is primarily focused on the midstream and downstream components of the energy value chain, offering import/export logistics and value-added services for liquids and gases.

Its business is structured around two major segments—Liquid Logistics and Gas (LPG) Logistics. The company holds a joint venture with Royal Vopak (Netherlands) and maintains a long-standing LPG sourcing JV with Itochu (Japan). Through Project GATI, it is implementing the largest capex plan in its history to expand liquid and LPG terminal capacities and establish a stronger nationwide footprint.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Aegis Logistics is strategically expanding its infrastructure to meet the growing demand for LPG and chemical storage in India. The company recently commissioned a new LPG cryogenic terminal at Pipavav, enhancing its storage capacity by 48,000 metric tonnes.

Name: Mahanagar Gas Ltd
Overview:
Mahanagar Gas Ltd (MGL) is a leading city gas distribution (CGD) company, supplying natural gas across Mumbai, adjoining areas, and Raigad district in Maharashtra. The company operates an extensive pipeline network with a large residential customer base and a strong presence in the compressed natural gas (CNG) market. Through its subsidiary, Unison Enviro Pvt. Ltd. (UEPL), MGL is also expanding into newer geographies, strengthening its position as one of the country’s most efficient and profitable CGD operators.

Its operations span domestic, commercial, industrial, and transport sectors, underpinned by an integrated infrastructure of steel and polyethylene pipelines, CNG stations, and a reliable gas sourcing portfolio. The company continues to focus on network expansion, diversification into LNG and renewable gas ventures, and technology-driven operational efficiencies to support sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management maintains high single-digit to near double-digit volume growth guidance for FY26, supported by network expansion, industrial/commercial demand growth, and penetration in new geographies. Margin stability is expected through competitive pricing and sourcing diversification. Over the medium term, CAPEX intensity will taper in core areas, shifting focus to maintenance, renewable energy ventures, and emerging fuel segments like LNG and CBG.

Name: Indraprastha Gas Ltd
Overview:
Indraprastha Gas Limited (IGL), incorporated in 1998, is a leading city gas distribution company in India. It operates in the National Capital Region (NCR) and surrounding areas including Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, Rewari, Karnal, Kanpur, Ajmer, and more. IGL was established as a joint venture promoted by GAIL (India) Ltd. and Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd., with the Government of NCT of Delhi holding a 5% stake.

The company primarily supplies Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) and Piped Natural Gas (PNG) to various customer segments including residential, commercial, industrial, and transport. IGL is rapidly expanding its CGD infrastructure, backed by robust internal accruals and strategic investments in green hydrogen, electric vehicle charging, and backward integration to strengthen its supply chain and sustainability initiatives.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Indraprastha Gas Limited (IGL) is strategically diversifying its energy portfolio to align with India's clean energy transition. The company is significantly increasing its capital expenditure to invest in renewable energy projects, including solar power, liquefied natural gas (LNG), and compressed biogas (CBG). IGL is also expanding its city gas distribution network to new regions, enhancing access to cleaner fuels. Additionally, the company is focusing on rural areas, providing piped natural gas to villages, thereby promoting sustainable energy solutions. These initiatives reflect IGL's commitment to sustainable growth and energy diversification.

Name: Chemplast Sanmar Ltd
Overview:
Chemplast Sanmar Ltd is a leading manufacturer in the Indian chemical industry, specializing in the production of polyvinyl chloride (PVC), caustic soda, and specialty chemicals. The company operates state-of-the-art facilities in Tamil Nadu and has a significant presence in both domestic and international markets.

With a strong focus on innovation and sustainability, Chemplast Sanmar continues to expand its product portfolio and enhance operational efficiencies to meet the growing demand in various industrial sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Chemplast Sanmar is focused on expanding specialty chemicals, improving operational efficiency, and investing in greenfield projects like refrigerant gas production. These initiatives aim to strengthen profitability and sustain long-term growth.

Name: Tamil Nadu Petro Products Ltd
Overview:
Tamil Nadu Petro Products Ltd manufactures petrochemical intermediates, primarily Linear Alkyl Benzene (LAB), Epichlorohydrin (ECH), and Caustic Soda. LAB is a key raw material for detergents, making the company an important supplier to FMCG players.

Tamilnadu Petroproducts Ltd (TNPL) is a leading petrochemical manufacturer in India. Its products cater to diverse end-user industries including detergents, adhesives, resins, and industrial applications, making it an essential player in the domestic chemicals value chain.

The company benefits from its strategically located production facilities, a strong focus on process efficiency, and a balanced mix of domestic and export customers. With steady demand from FMCG and industrial sectors, TNPL has established itself as a reliable supplier, while its investments in modernization support operational resilience and competitiveness.With its strategically located facilities near Chennai, the company caters to both domestic and export markets. Its product portfolio ensures a balance between industrial demand and consumer-driven applications.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Rising FMCG consumption in detergents and soaps, coupled with increasing demand for resins and adhesives, is expected to sustain steady growth momentum into FY26, positioning TNPL well for long-term demand visibility.

Name: J.G.Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
J.G. Chemicals Ltd, established in 1975 and headquartered in Kolkata, is India's leading manufacturer of zinc oxide. Utilizing the French process, the company produces over 80 grades of zinc oxide, catering to diverse industries such as rubber, ceramics, paints, pharmaceuticals, and electronics. With a production capacity of 70,000 MTPA, J.G. Chemicals ranks among the top five global producers and is the largest in Asia .

The company operates both domestically and internationally, serving over 200 customers in India and more than 50 global clients across 10+ countries. Its subsidiary, BDJ Oxides, is notable for its IATF-approved facility in Naidupeta, which also holds WHO GMP certification—a rare distinction among global manufacturers .
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company is poised for continued growth, driven by its strategic expansion in Gujarat and increasing demand for high-quality zinc oxide across various industries. The focus on recycling and sustainable practices further enhances its market position and long-term prospects .

Name: Thirumalai Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Thirumalai Chemicals Ltd, a part of the Thirumalai Group, specializes in the manufacturing and sale of key industrial chemicals including phthalic anhydride (PAN), diethyl phthalate (DEP), surfactants, and pigments. The company serves a varied client base—both in domestic markets and through exports—while maintaining a strong presence across chemical and specialty segments.

As a diversified chemical manufacturer, the company also has interests in education and other industrial sectors, but the core operations remain focused on supplying high-quality chemical intermediates and products that support industries like plastics, coatings, and personal care.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With the ongoing focus on high-value chemical segments and cost control, improvements in operating efficiencies and gradual recovery in demand could help narrow losses as the year progresses.

Name: Rain Industries Ltd
Overview:
Rain Industries is a vertically integrated global producer of carbon-based materials, cement, and advanced materials. Headquartered in India, it operates across three continents, converting by-products from oil refining and steel production into essential carbon products for various industries, including aluminum.

In FY 25, the company benefited from improving market dynamics in its core Carbon segment, while continuing to invest in future-ready technologies like battery anodes. As FY 26 begins, Rain Industries is demonstrating a strategic pivot with a return to profitability and ongoing efforts to enhance operational resilience through innovation and cost management.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With its core Carbon segment regaining momentum and strategic investments underway, Rain Industries is positioned for a gradual recovery. While volatility in global markets and elevated leverage pose challenges, continued focus on innovation, cost efficiency, and segment optimization.

Name: DCW Ltd
Overview:
DCW Ltd, originally founded as Dhrangadhra Chemical Works in 1939, is a leading Indian producer specializing in both commodity and specialty chemical products. The company’s portfolio includes basic chemicals such as soda ash and synthetic rutile, along with higher-margin specialty chemicals like CPVC and other derivatives. 

Over the decades, DCW has expanded its manufacturing footprint and capabilities, integrating sustainability initiatives such as solar power generation to enhance efficiency and reduce costs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
DCW aims to expand its specialty chemical footprint—evidenced by a recently commissioned CPVC capacity and ambitious expansion plans. The commissioning of a 44.5 MW solar project to support its Tamil Nadu operations also underscores its focus on sustainability and cost efficiency. These strategic moves are likely to bolster profitability as demand recovers and specialty segments scale up

Name: Gujarat Alkalies & Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat Alkalies & Chemicals Ltd is a diversified chemical manufacturer specializing in caustic soda and related products. The company operates robust production facilities in Gujarat, offering a wide portfolio of basic and specialty chemicals, including caustic soda (in multiple forms), chloromethanes, hydrochloric acid, hydrogen peroxide, and more.

With fully integrated operations spanning manufacturing at Vadodara and Dahej plants, GACL serves critical segments across the industrial chemicals space. Its emphasis on product diversity and established infrastructure has helped maintain a resilient business model through fluctuating economic cycles and global commodity dynamics.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
GACL is positioned for sustained revenue and margin recovery. The Chlor-Alkali business is expected to perform steadily, while Basic Chemicals should benefit from favorable commodity pricing. As downstream businesses mature and capacity utilization improves, bottom-line performance is likely to stabilize. The company’s broad product mix and scale of operations support a cautiously optimistic outlook.

Name: Refex Industries Ltd
Overview:
Refex Industries Limited, established in 2002, is a diversified industrial company headquartered in Chennai, India. The company operates across multiple sectors, including Ash & Coal Handling, Refrigerant Gases, and Green Mobility. Refex is committed to contributing to India's green transformation through its dynamic portfolio, which encompasses sustainable energy solutions and efficient resource management.

In recent years, Refex has strategically expanded into renewable energy, particularly in wind energy, and has made significant investments in electric mobility.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Refex Industries aims to capitalize on its strategic investments in Green Mobility and Wind Energy to drive future growth. The company plans to expand its electric vehicle fleet and enhance its wind energy manufacturing capabilities through its subsidiaries.

Name: I G Petrochemicals Ltd
Overview:
I G Petrochemicals Ltd, founded in 1988, is a specialty chemicals company that manufactures key intermediate products such as phthalic anhydride, maleic anhydride, and benzoic acid—used extensively in the production of dyes, polyester resins, and related industrial applications. 

The company operates advanced chemical facilities and serves diverse industries with high-purity chemical intermediates. Known for its technical capabilities and consistent quality, I G Petrochemicals plays an important role in India's chemical manufacturing ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Recovery in demand from downstream sectors like textiles and coatings will be crucial for I G Petrochemicals. Strategic capacity ramp-ups such as anticipated expansions in its phthalic anhydride and plasticizer capacities may help the company capitalize on improved market conditions.

Name: Andhra Sugars Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1947, Andhra Sugars Ltd is a diversified chemical and sugar manufacturer with operations across industrial chemicals, chlor-alkali products, soaps, sugar, power generation, and specialty chemicals like aspirin and propellants. The company also caters to strategic requirements of ISRO through its industrial chemicals and propellants portfolio.

With an integrated setup of chemical complexes, sugar mills, and captive power facilities, Andhra Sugars leverages synergies across segments while maintaining a presence in both commodity and specialty markets. Its diversified portfolio enables risk balancing, though cyclicality in sugar and volatility in chemical realizations impact profitability.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Andhra Sugars Ltd is strategically enhancing its position in the renewable energy and chemicals sectors. The company has recently commenced operations at a new 500 TPD sulfuric acid plant in Saggonda, Andhra Pradesh, marking a significant expansion in its chemicals division. Additionally, Andhra Sugars is focusing on increasing its renewable energy capacity by investing in biomass-based power generation, aiming to reduce dependency on conventional energy sources and align with sustainability goals

Name: Tata Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Tata Chemicals Ltd, incorporated in 1939, is part of the Tata Group and a leading global player in the soda ash and specialty chemicals market. The company operates across India, North America, Europe, and Africa, with strong positions in basic chemistry and specialty chemistry segments.
