It is the third-largest soda ash producer globally, with a significant share of low-cost natural soda ash capacity, and a diversified portfolio that includes sodium bicarbonate, salt, cement, and specialty silica.

The company also has a strong presence in agri-sciences through subsidiary Rallis India, as well as in nutritional sciences and material sciences, supplying to sectors such as glass, detergents, food and nutrition, pharmaceuticals, and rubber. Its manufacturing footprint spans multiple geographies, enabling it to serve diverse global markets efficiently.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Tata Chemicals expects stable pricing in a narrow range amid global oversupply, with performance gains driven by internal levers like throughput, cost control, and selective project execution. The U.K. business is expected to break even at PAT level by year-end, and Kenya’s new capacity should aid margins. Management remains cautious on large new capex until market clarity improves, but continues to monitor potential supply rationalization globally, which could improve the demand-supply balance.

Name: GHCL Ltd
Overview:
GHCL Limited is a leading Indian chemical company specializing in the production of soda ash, a key raw material used in glass manufacturing, detergents, and other industrial applications. The company operates one of the largest single-location soda ash manufacturing facilities in India, ensuring significant economies of scale and operational efficiency. GHCL's soda ash business has been growing at a rate faster than India's GDP, driven by robust domestic demand and strategic capacity expansions. 

In addition to its core soda ash operations, GHCL is diversifying its product portfolio by venturing into the production of bromine and vacuum salt. These initiatives are aimed at catering to large FMCG customers and are expected to contribute meaningfully to the company's revenue streams starting in the second half of FY26.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
GHCL is poised for growth with its strategic initiatives aimed at expanding production capacity and diversifying its product portfolio. The commissioning of bromine and vacuum salt plants in the second half of FY26 is expected to contribute approximately ₹200 crore to annual revenue. Additionally, the greenfield soda ash project in Gujarat, with an estimated capacity of 5.5 lakh MT per annum, is planned to commence operations by 2028, further strengthening GHCL's market position.

Name: Whirlpool of India Ltd
Overview:
Whirlpool of India Ltd, a subsidiary of Whirlpool Corporation (USA), is one of India’s leading manufacturers and marketers of home appliances. Its portfolio spans refrigerators, washing machines, air conditioners, microwave ovens, built-in products, and small appliances. The parent company is a global kitchen and laundry appliances leader with a brand portfolio including Whirlpool, KitchenAid, Maytag, and others, operating 55 manufacturing and research centers worldwide.

The company has three manufacturing facilities located in Faridabad, Pune, and Puducherry, supported by significant R&D and capital investments. In FY24, Whirlpool of India reported a diversified revenue mix, with refrigerators contributing ~33%, washing machines ~25%, air conditioners ~26%, and other products ~16%. The acquisition of an 87% stake in Elica India has strengthened its cooking appliances presence, delivering strong revenue and profitability growth in that segment.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Whirlpool aims to win through product leadership, execution excellence, and a resilient supply chain while expanding its direct-to-consumer business. The company will continue driving premiumization and market share gains, leveraging strong seasonal cycles, new product launches, and partnerships. Cost productivity initiatives and a negative working capital position are expected to support profitability, while Elica’s performance and premium cooking portfolio will remain growth catalysts.

Name: Amara Raja Energy & Mobility Ltd
Overview:
Amara Raja Batteries Limited (ARBL), the flagship company of the Amara Raja Group, is one of India’s largest manufacturers of lead-acid batteries for automotive and industrial use. It holds a dominant position across key battery categories, with well-established brands such as Amaron and PowerZone. The company is a technology leader and preferred OEM supplier to top automotive brands like Maruti Suzuki, Tata Motors, Hyundai, and Mahindra, and to critical industrial sectors like telecom, UPS, and railways. Its products are exported to over 50 countries globally.

The company is also building strong capabilities in the New Energy Business (NEB), venturing into lithium-ion cell and battery pack manufacturing. It has developed India’s first 21700 cylindrical lithium cell and serves clients like Piaggio and Mahindra. To support this transition, ARBL is executing large-scale capex plans including a Giga Factory and a new lead recycling plant. The company also operates a robust distribution network domestically with 100,000+ points of sale and has an expanding international footprint.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Elgi Equipments is actively expanding its global presence, focusing on key markets such as North America, Europe, and Australia. The company aims to enhance its product portfolio by introducing advanced technologies, including intelligent, energy-efficient air compressors and vacuum solutions, to meet the evolving needs of various industries. Elgi is also investing in manufacturing capabilities to support the growing demand for high-capacity compressors in sectors like automotive, metalworking, and woodworking.

Name: Symphony Ltd
Overview:
Symphony Limited, founded in 1988 and headquartered in Ahmedabad, is a global leader in evaporative air cooling, catering to residential, commercial, and industrial segments. The company has established its dominance in the domestic market while expanding its footprint across international geographies.

With a product portfolio focused largely on air coolers, Symphony has complemented its core offerings with adjacent categories and innovative designs. Its business model is asset-light and capital-efficient, supported by strong brand recognition, a wide distribution network, and growing export opportunities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management remains cautiously optimistic, focusing on category diversification, export-led growth, and maintaining profitability despite near-term weather-related headwinds. Emphasis will be on expanding counter-seasonal categories, enhancing export penetration, and optimizing channel inventory for a stronger performance in the next summer season.

Name: Exide Industries Ltd
Overview:
Exide Industries Ltd is a leading manufacturer of storage batteries and allied products in India, serving diverse sectors including mobility aftermarket, solar, industrial UPS, and telecom. The company is investing heavily in cutting-edge manufacturing technologies such as punched grid lines and continuous casting to improve cost efficiencies and product quality.

It continues to realign product offerings, expand distribution channels, and innovate in technology to sustain growth across core and emerging segments. The company remains focused on operational excellence and leveraging government incentives to capitalize on the evolving energy storage landscape.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Exide targets aggressive growth in solar, aftermarket 2W batteries, and inverter segments, supported by new product launches and network expansion. Margin expansion is expected as raw material prices stabilize and new manufacturing technologies mature. Lithium-ion business ramp-up will be closely monitored, with profitability clarity post scale-up.

Name: HBL Engineering Ltd
Overview:
HBL Power Systems Ltd, now trading as HBL Engineering, is a prominent Indian engineering company specializing in advanced battery solutions, e-mobility products, and industrial electronics. Its expertise spans industrial nickel batteries, PLT lead batteries, and advanced electronic systems such as the Kavach train collision avoidance system, serving critical sectors like telecom, railways, defense, and renewable energy through both OEM manufacturing and integrated solution delivery.

The company continues to leverage its market leadership in niche battery segments and electronics to capitalize on infrastructure modernization and digital transformation trends. With a balanced focus on defense, railway safety systems, and renewable energy storage solutions, HBL is positioned to expand its footprint in high-growth, mission-critical domains.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Leveraging its battery dominance and expanding electronics portfolio, including safety systems, HBL is well-positioned for growth driven by infrastructure, telecom, and rail modernization. With PLI scheme eligibility and robust order inflows in signaling systems, it should deliver continued revenue expansion through FY26–FY27, though margins may moderate modestly due to competitive and operational dynamics.

Name: LG Electronics India Ltd
Overview:
LG Electronics India Limited, established in 1997, is a leading manufacturer and distributor of home appliances and consumer electronics in India. The company offers a wide range of products, including refrigerators, washing machines, air conditioners, televisions, and kitchen appliances, catering to the diverse needs of Indian households. With a strong presence across the country, LG Electronics India has built a reputation for delivering innovative and high-quality products that enhance the living experience of its customers.

The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Noida, Pune, and Sri City, ensuring the production of world-class products that meet international standards. LG Electronics India is committed to sustainability and energy efficiency, incorporating advanced technologies in its products to promote environmental conservation. Through continuous research and development, the company strives to introduce cutting-edge solutions that align with the evolving preferences of Indian consumers.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

LG Electronics India aims to expand its footprint in Tier-II and Tier-III cities, focusing on value-conscious customers without compromising on quality. The company plans to deepen its manufacturing capabilities and enhance export growth by leveraging India's software expertise for developing advanced technologies such as semiconductors, robotics, and artificial intelligence.

Name: Eureka Forbes Ltd
Overview:
Eureka Forbes Limited, owned by Advent International, operates in the health and hygiene segment, offering water purifiers, vacuum cleaners, air purifiers, and home security systems. Its flagship brand, Aquaguard, leads the water purifier market, while Forbes-branded vacuum cleaners dominate the cleaning segment. The company serves over 20 million customers across 35 countries, backed by 21,000+ dealers and 8,000+ trained technicians delivering nationwide service coverage.

FY25 marked the second full year of its transformation program, delivering six consecutive quarters of double-digit growth. This performance was powered by over 30 new product launches, premiumization strategies, and expansion in IoT-enabled solutions. Growth was broad-based across water, cleaning, and air categories, with innovation and brand-led marketing reinforcing leadership in underpenetrated markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management targets further revenue acceleration and margin improvement in FY26, led by service revival, premium product penetration, and robotics-led vacuum cleaner growth. With strong cash reserves, structural tailwinds in low-penetration categories, and leadership in health and hygiene solutions, the company aims to sustain double-digit growth while deepening IoT and premium portfolio offerings.

Name: TTK Prestige Ltd
Overview:
TTK Group, through its flagship brand TTK Prestige, is India’s largest kitchenware company known primarily for its strong presence in the pressure cooker segment and a diversified portfolio including cookware, cookers, and small domestic appliances. The company is focused on premiumization, expanding its product portfolio with new SKUs and leveraging its Prestige Exclusive Stores to deepen market penetration, especially in metro and mid-tier towns.

The company is investing strategically in capacity, talent, and innovation while balancing near-term margin pressures due to these futuristic spends. Its export business, though impacted by global tariff uncertainties, remains a long-term growth focus, particularly in cookware exports.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management views the current phase as a transition with a focus on long-term investments in people, technology, and market presence. Margin improvement is expected as strategic spends mature and sales growth accelerates. While cautious about global uncertainties and input cost pressures, TTK remains optimistic about growth driven by premiumization, expanded distribution, and product innovation over the medium term.

Name: Butterfly Gandhimathi Appliances Ltd
Overview:
Butterfly Gandhimathi Appliances Ltd, incorporated in 1986, is a leading manufacturer of domestic kitchen and electrical appliances under the “Butterfly” brand. The company is now part of Crompton Greaves Consumer Electricals Ltd, which holds ~75% stake and provides managerial and operational support. Its offerings span LPG stoves, mixer grinders, wet grinders, pressure cookers, chimneys, flasks, and a range of home appliances such as coolers, irons, and fans.

With a brand presence of over four decades, Butterfly enjoys strong consumer recall, particularly in South India, where it is a top-3 player in mixers, cookers, and gas stoves. The company has built a distribution strength of 29,000+ retailers and 750 distributors, alongside leadership on e-commerce platforms, ranking #1 in Flipkart and Amazon in categories like mixer grinders, wet grinders, and gas stoves. About 80% of revenues come from in-house manufacturing, supported by a 25-acre integrated R&D and production campus.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management has positioned the combined entity to unlock significant growth potential by scaling up innovation in kitchen and home appliances, leveraging complementary product portfolios, and enhancing distribution across India. Butterfly’s asset-light trading model in certain categories, along with Crompton’s financial strength, is expected to drive long-term value creation for shareholders while keeping ROCE and free cash flow generation strong.

Name: Hawkins Cookers Ltd
Overview:
Hawkins Cookers Ltd, founded in 1959, is one of India’s foremost manufacturers of premium pressure cookers and cookware under brands like Hawkins and Futura. It operates multiple manufacturing facilities and supplies cookware across domestic and international markets.

Known for its durable and safety-oriented kitchenware, the company has built strong brand recall and a loyal customer base. It distributes through a wide network of retail and modern trade channels, offering both traditional and innovative product lines.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Hawkins is poised to maintain its growth trajectory by reinforcing brand loyalty, expanding product ranges, and optimizing distribution. With continued demand for safe, durable cookware and measured entry into kitchen-related innovations, the company aims to sustain margin resilience and steady earnings growth in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Carysil Ltd
Overview:
Carysil Ltd., established in 1987 and headquartered in Bhavnagar, Gujarat, is a leading manufacturer and exporter of premium kitchen solutions. The company specializes in composite quartz and stainless steel kitchen sinks, bath products, tiles, kitchen appliances, and accessories. With a strong presence in both domestic and international markets, Carysil has become one of Asia's largest producers of composite quartz granite kitchen sinks.

Carysil operates with a focus on innovation, quality, and sustainability, offering a wide range of products designed to meet the evolving needs of modern kitchens.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Carysil is poised for significant expansion, focusing on scaling its manufacturing capabilities and broadening its product portfolio. The company plans to invest ₹500 crore to double its sink production capacity to 2 million units annually, enhance stainless steel sink output to 2.5 lakh units, and boost faucet and appliance manufacturing to 50,000 units each per year. Additionally, Carysil is strengthening its international presence, notably by increasing its business with IKEA and exploring opportunities in the UK market following the recent Free Trade Agreement

Name: Eveready Industries India Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1934, Eveready Industries India Ltd is a leading manufacturer of dry cell batteries, flashlights, lighting solutions, and home appliances. The company operates under the Eveready brand, which has been a household name in India for decades. 

Eveready Industries India Ltd has a rich history of providing reliable and quality products. The company remains committed to sustainability and technological advancements, ensuring that its products align with contemporary standards and consumer expectations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Eveready aims to expand its product portfolio and enhance distribution channels to drive growth. The company is exploring new product categories and innovations to meet changing consumer demands and strengthen its market position.

Name: IFB Industries Ltd
Overview:
IFB Industries Limited, originally known as Indian Fine Blanks Limited, began operations in 1974 in collaboration with Heinrich Schmid AG of Switzerland. The company operates in two major divisions – home appliances and engineering – offering a wide range of consumer durables like washing machines, air conditioners, refrigerators, and microwaves, along with automotive and precision engineering components.

Over the years, IFB has established itself as a trusted consumer brand in India while also maintaining a strong engineering footprint. The company continues to focus on strengthening its product portfolio, expanding distribution reach, and driving cost optimization initiatives to support profitability and long-term growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

IFB Industries is strategically expanding its operations to achieve significant growth across both its consumer durables and engineering divisions. The company plans to invest ₹200–₹220 crore to enhance production capabilities in appliances, air conditioning, and automotive chain businesses. In the consumer durables sector, IFB aims to increase sales of large-capacity washing machines and refrigerators, targeting monthly sales of 60,000 units.

Name: Bajaj Electricals Ltd
Overview:
Bajaj Electricals Limited is a leading player in the Fast Moving Electrical Goods (FMEG) sector, operating primarily through its Consumer Products and Lighting Solutions segments. The company offers a wide range of home and kitchen appliances, fans, lighting products, and premium cookware under well-known brands like Morphy Richards and Nirlep. It holds a strong market position across categories, with a growing focus on premiumization, operational efficiencies, and distribution expansion.

The company also engages in project execution (EPC) in lighting and power distribution, alongside growing export operations. Strategic initiatives emphasize brand investment, channel diversification, manufacturing expansion, and innovation, aiming to boost growth and profitability in a competitive market.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management targets EBIT margin improvement in Consumer Products to around 6% aiming for double-digit margins within three years. Growth in Consumer Lighting is expected to continue, with Professional Lighting execution picking up in the second half of FY26. Increased brand spends and continued premiumization underpin a positive outlook for sustained margin and profitability growth.

Name: Stove Kraft Ltd
Overview:
StoveKraft Ltd, incorporated in 1999 by Rajendra Gandhi, is a leading Indian kitchenware manufacturer offering a range of kitchen solutions. It markets products under its own brands—Pigeon (value segment) and Gilma (semi-premium)—and serves the premium segment as the exclusive partner of BLACK + DECKER in India. 

The product portfolio includes cookware, cooking appliances, and select home utilities like LED bulbs and oximeters, reflecting a broader lifestyle approach. The company’s operations are anchored in its abilities to design, manufacture, and distribute high-quality domestic kitchen products across India.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
StoveKraft is optimistic about driving growth through the remainder of FY26. Management expects revenue to grow in the high teens, supported by consistent demand and margin improvement, particularly as greater share of manufacturing shifts to in-house production, enhancing cost competitiveness.

Name: Elin Electronics Ltd
Overview:
Elin Electronics Ltd, founded in 1969 and based in New Delhi, is a one-stop electronics manufacturing services (EMS) provider offering end-to-end product design, development, and manufacturing. It produces an extensive range of electronic components and finished goods—motors, lighting (LED), fans, appliances, small kitchen equipment, diagnostic cartridges, and automotive parts—serving OEM and ODM clients across domestic and international markets.
The company integrates capabilities in plastic molding, injection, die casting, tool/mold design, motors, and EMS, providing seamless solutions from conceptual design to final product assembly. Its diverse product portfolio caters to industries ranging from consumer appliances to healthcare instruments.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Elin Electronics Ltd is strategically expanding its product portfolio to include advanced composite molds and components, catering to diverse industries beyond its traditional auto sector. The company is investing in state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and adopting cutting-edge technologies to enhance production capabilities and meet the growing demand for high-quality composite products.

Name: Focus Lighting & Fixtures Ltd
Overview:
Focus Lighting & Fixtures Limited, established in 2005, specializes in the design, manufacture, and trading of innovative LED lighting solutions and fixtures. The company is committed to delivering high-quality, energy-efficient lighting products that cater to diverse applications, including retail, home, infrastructure, and railway sectors. 

With a strong emphasis on research and development, Focus Lighting & Fixtures aims to set new standards in lighting technology and aesthetics. Headquartered in Mumbai, the company operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Sanand, Gujarat. Focus Lighting & Fixtures is listed on the National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) under the ticker symbol "FOCUS."
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Focus Lighting & Fixtures Ltd is strategically expanding its presence in the Indian lighting industry by focusing on innovative technologies and market diversification. The company has recently ventured into new segments such as home lighting, infrastructure lighting, and railways, with an emphasis on integrating Internet of Things (IoT) capabilities into its products.

Name: Orient Electric Ltd
Overview:
Orient Electric Limited, a part of the CK Birla Group, is a leading consumer electrical brand in India. The company offers a diverse portfolio encompassing fans, lighting solutions, home appliances, and switchgear products. With a legacy of over six decades, Orient Electric has established itself as a trusted name in the Indian electrical industry.

Operating manufacturing facilities in Faridabad, Noida, Hyderabad, and Kolkata, Orient Electric has a strong presence across India. The company emphasizes research and development to drive continuous innovation in its product offerings. Orient Electric's products are designed to enhance the lifestyle of consumers, combining functionality with aesthetic appeal.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Orient Electric aims to continue its growth trajectory by focusing on product innovation, expanding its market presence, and enhancing operational efficiencies. The company's strategic initiatives are expected to drive sustained revenue growth and profitability in the coming fiscal years.

Name: Foods & Inns Ltd
Overview: Foods & Inns Ltd is an Indian-based food processing company specializing in the manufacturing and marketing of a diverse range of processed tropical fruit and vegetable products. These include pulps, purees, spices, spray-dried powders, frozen foods, pectin, and ready-to-eat (RTE) and ready-to-cook (RTC) products. The company operates under brands such as Greentop, Kusum, and Madhu, catering to both domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Foods & Inns Ltd is gearing up to strengthen its position as a leading processor and exporter of fruit and vegetable products. The company plans to broaden its product range through full-capacity spray drying operations, expanding frozen food lines with new production lines, and introducing innovative packaging like Tetra Recart. It is also boosting value-added and branded offerings and turning processing waste into pectin to enhance sustainability and meet diverse customer needs.

Name: KSE Ltd
Overview:
KSE Ltd, also known as Kerala Solvent Extractions Ltd, is one of India’s prominent cattle feed manufacturers. The company specializes in producing compounded cattle feed, coconut oil, and related by-products. With a strong presence in southern India, particularly Kerala, it has created a loyal customer base through consistent product quality and robust distribution.

Over time, KSE has expanded its product portfolio to serve the needs of dairy farmers and households, providing both industrial-grade cattle feed and consumer-focused edible oils. The company’s long-standing presence in the industry has allowed it to maintain trust while steadily upgrading its technology to meet rising demand. Its focus on quality raw material sourcing and production efficiency ensures a strong position in a competitive market.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking ahead, KSE is expected to benefit from strong rural demand, higher per-capita dairy consumption, and potential expansion into newer markets. Efforts to modernize plants and improve feed formulations will support long-term growth while maintaining competitiveness against peers.

Name: Annapurna Swadisht Ltd
Overview:
Annapurna Swadisht Ltd is an emerging FMCG company based in Kolkata, India, specializing in manufacturing a wide range of affordable, ready-to-eat packaged snacks and food products. Established in 2016, the company has rapidly expanded its footprint in the eastern Indian markets, including West Bengal, Bihar, Odisha, Jharkhand, and Assam. 

Annapurna Swadisht's product portfolio encompasses over 38 SKUs across six product lines, featuring popular brands such as Jackpot, Chatpata Moon, Finger, Rambo, Balloon, Dhamaka, Phoochka, Jungle, Kurchure, Malai Pop, Imli Pop, Kaju Candy, Coffico, Gram Gathiya, Diet Chidwa, and Gram Masala Muri.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Annapurna Swadisht Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and strengthening its distribution network. The company plans to further penetrate untapped markets and enhance brand visibility through targeted marketing campaigns.

Name: Pyramid Technoplast Ltd
Overview:
Pyramid Technoplast Ltd is engaged in manufacturing polymer-based molded products, particularly intermediate bulk containers (IBCs), drums, and packaging solutions. Its products cater to industries such as chemicals, pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and food processing, where safe and efficient bulk packaging is critical.

The company’s focus on quality and compliance with global packaging standards has enabled it to build a strong client base in both domestic and export markets. As demand for durable industrial packaging grows, Pyramid Technoplast is positioned as a reliable supplier.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company plans to scale up capacity and enhance export focus. With industrial packaging demand expanding globally, Pyramid Technoplast has strong growth opportunities.

Name: Mamata Machinery Ltd
Overview:
Mamata Machinery Ltd is a packaging machinery manufacturer specializing in flexible packaging equipment, bag-making machines, and extrusion systems. The company caters to packaging converters, FMCG players, and industrial clients seeking modern and cost-efficient packaging solutions. With a focus on quality engineering, automation, and innovation, Mamata has earned a strong reputation in both domestic and international markets.The company caters to a diverse client base spanning FMCG, pharmaceuticals, food, and industrial sectors, where demand for high-speed, automated packaging solutions is growing rapidly.

Over the years, Mamata has strengthened its reputation for innovation, offering cost-effective and efficient machines that meet international quality standards. Its exports to multiple global markets provide strong diversification beyond domestic sales

Its wide product portfolio spans blown film extrusion, printing and converting equipment, and pouch-making systems, making it a one-stop solution provider in the flexible packaging industry. Leveraging strong export linkages, the company has built a global footprint across North America, Latin America, Africa, and Asia, reinforcing its position as a globally competitive player in packaging machinery.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Mamata Machinery expects demand to remain strong in FY26, backed by sustained growth in packaged food and e-commerce sectors. Investments in automation and digital integration of machines are expected to further strengthen its competitive positioning globally.

Name: Arrow Greentech Ltd
Overview:
Arrow Greentech Ltd specializes in water-soluble and biodegradable packaging films, catering to agrochemicals, consumer products, and specialty segments. With its patented eco-packaging technologies, the company has established itself as a leading innovator in the sustainable packaging space.

The business is export-oriented, with international customers accounting for a significant portion of revenues. Domestically, Arrow Greentech is benefiting from growing regulatory push toward eco-friendly packaging and rising demand for sustainable solutions from FMCG and agrochemical industries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Arrow Greentech is expected to grow topline at 12–14% CAGR in FY26, driven by regulatory tailwinds for sustainable packaging and increasing adoption by FMCG and agrochemical firms. Exports are projected to maintain ~60% share, while domestic sales are set to grow faster with rising eco-compliance norms. EBITDA margin expansion toward 16% levels is expected as higher-margin biodegradable variants gain scale.

Name: Ester Industries Ltd
Overview:
Ester Industries Ltd, established in 1985, is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in polyester films and specialty polymers. The company operates through two primary business segments: Polyester Films and Specialty Polymers. Its Polyester Films division focuses on producing BOPET films used in flexible packaging and other industrial applications. 

The Specialty Polymers segment offers a range of products, including thermoplastic polyester compounds, catering to industries such as automotive, electrical, electronics, and consumer durables. Ester Industries is committed to sustainability and innovation. The company has been expanding its product offerings, particularly in the recycled PET (rPET) segment, aligning with global trends towards eco-friendly materials.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ester Industries Ltd is actively enhancing its manufacturing capabilities to meet the increasing demand for polyester films and specialty polymers. The company is expanding its rPET (recycled PET) production capacity in Hyderabad, with plans to commence commercial production at a new 20,000 TPA facility.

Name: Cosmo First Ltd
Overview:
Cosmo Films is a leading global manufacturer of specialty films used in packaging, lamination, and labeling—including BOPP, CPP, and BOPET films. The company operates multiple manufacturing facilities across India and serves customers worldwide in food, beverages, personal care, pharmaceuticals, and industrial sectors, emphasizing innovation, capacity expansion, and sustainability in film solutions.

Over recent years, Cosmo has made strategic investments to enhance its production capabilities—including new BOPP and CPP lines, and its window film ("Sunshield") business. These additions are set to strengthen its cost competitiveness and widen its specialty film portfolio.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The ramp-up of the new BOPP line and other specialty investments should significantly boost both revenue and profitability. Coupled with growing traction for Sunshield window films and strong performance from specialty chemicals, Cosmo is well-positioned to drive robust growth and higher margins through FY26.

Name: Mold-Tek Packaging Ltd
Overview:
Mold-Tek Packaging is a Hyderabad-based manufacturer specializing in injection-molded plastic containers across sectors such as lubricants, paints, food, FMCG, and pharmaceuticals. With a strong heritage in high-quality packaging, the company leverages its expertise in in-mould labeling (IML) and lightweighting to serve premium clients.

It operates multiple plants including those in Panipat, Cheyyar, Satara, and Telangana, with a focus on modern production techniques and capacity expansion to meet evolving industry demands.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Expansion plans include ramped-up pharma capacity and high-IML production. With pharma segment targeting ₹30–35 crore in FY26 (and ₹50–60 crore in FY27), coupled with robust volume gains in paints, FMCG, and food packaging, the company is poised for sustained growth and improving margins toward FY27.

Name: R M Drip & Sprinklers Systems Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1996, R M Drip & Sprinklers Systems Ltd is a prominent Indian company specializing in the design and manufacture of micro-irrigation systems. The company offers a comprehensive range of products, including drip irrigation systems, sprinkler irrigation systems, HDPE pipes, and various filtration solutions. These products are crafted in-house at their facility in Sinnar, near Nashik, ensuring high-quality standards and innovation.

R M Drip & Sprinklers Systems Ltd is committed to supporting sustainable agricultural practices by providing efficient irrigation solutions that conserve water and enhance crop yields. Their products are tailored to meet the diverse needs of farmers across India, addressing challenges such as water scarcity and inconsistent rainfall.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

R M Drip & Sprinklers Systems Ltd is focused on expanding its presence in both domestic and international markets. The company aims to enhance its product offerings by integrating advanced technologies such as automation and artificial intelligence into its irrigation systems. Additionally, R M Drip is exploring opportunities to diversify its product range and enter new markets, thereby broadening its customer base and revenue streams.

Name: All Time Plastics Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1971, All Time Plastics Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in plastic houseware products. The company offers a wide range of items, including food storage containers, kitchen tools, cleaning accessories, and bathroom essentials, catering to both domestic and international markets. All Time Plastics operates on a business-to-business (B2B) model, producing consumerware for clients to market under their own brand names, as well as offering products under its own "All Time" brand. 

The company exports its products to over 25 countries, leveraging its extensive experience and expertise in the plastic manufacturing industry. All Time Plastics maintains a strong commitment to quality and innovation, ensuring that its products meet the highest standards and cater to the evolving needs of consumers worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

All Time Plastics Ltd is focused on expanding its market presence by enhancing its product offerings and increasing its export reach. The company aims to strengthen its position in the global market by leveraging its manufacturing capabilities and commitment to quality. Investments in research and development are central to All Time Plastics' strategy to innovate and meet the diverse needs of consumers.

Name: Huhtamaki India Ltd
Overview:
Huhtamaki India Ltd is a leading player in the flexible packaging industry, specializing in sustainable packaging solutions for the food, beverage, and consumer goods sectors. The company operates multiple manufacturing facilities across India, serving both domestic and international markets.

With a strong emphasis on innovation and sustainability, Huhtamaki India is committed to providing high-quality packaging solutions that meet the evolving needs of its customers while adhering to environmental standards.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Huhtamaki India aims to strengthen its market position by expanding its product offerings and enhancing operational efficiencies. The company is also exploring opportunities for mergers and acquisitions to further bolster its growth prospects in the competitive packaging industry.

Name: Nahar Polyfilms Ltd
Overview:
Nahar Poly Films Ltd is a specialist manufacturer of flexible packaging films, primarily producing transparent and metalized packaging materials such as BOPP films. These films find broad application across food, pharmaceutical, and industrial segments, catering to the growing demand for durable and high-quality packaging in India and export markets. The company's product range underscores its commitment to serving evolving packaging needs through innovation and consistency.

With well-established manufacturing capabilities, Nahar Poly Films focuses on delivering technical precision and fulfillment excellence. Its operations are structured to support both domestic consumption and international markets, enhancing its position in the packaging industry’s value chain.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Nahar Poly Films is positioned for sustained growth in FY26. Continued stability in raw material prices and healthy order flows could help the company further enhance margins and reinforce its leadership in flexible packaging.

Name: Everest Kanto Cylinder Ltd
Overview:
Everest Kanto Cylinder Ltd (EKC) is a specialist manufacturer of high-pressure seamless gas cylinders and related equipment designed for storing and transporting natural gas, liquids, and compressed air. The company leverages advanced metallurgy and precision engineering to deliver products essential for energy, industrial, and gas handling sectors. 

With a production base in India and an expanding global footprint, EKC focuses on quality, compliance, and engineering reliability to serve both domestic and international clients.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Everest Kanto's trajectory hinges on balancing volume growth with cost management and margin recovery. Continued demand from industrial and energy sectors is expected to underpin its growth, provided input costs remain stable. Operational improvements and strategic client expansions could help restore profitability levels seen in prior periods.

Name: Paras Defence and Space Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Paras Defence and Space Technologies Ltd (PDSL) is an Indian private-sector company specializing in the design, development, manufacturing, and testing of a diverse range of defence and space engineering products and solutions. Established in 1990, the company operates across four major segments: Defence & Space Optics, Defence Electronics, Heavy Engineering, and Electromagnetic Pulse (EMP) Protection Solutions. 

The company is recognized for its expertise in providing critical technologies and components for defence and space applications. Paras Defence's product offerings include advanced optical systems, electronic warfare solutions, heavy engineering components, and EMP protection systems.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Paras Defence aims to leverage its technological expertise and strong order book to drive growth in FY26. The company is well-positioned to benefit from the Indian government's increased focus on indigenous defence production and capital expenditure in the sector. With a commitment to innovation and quality, Paras Defence is poised to contribute significantly to India's defence capabilities in the coming fiscal year.

Name: Avantel Ltd
Overview:
Avantel Limited designs, develops, and maintains communication products and systems primarily for the defence and aerospace sectors. Its offerings include wireless and satellite communication equipment, radar systems, and network management software solutions tailored to high-security applications.

Serving critical industries such as defence, the company supports national initiatives in advanced technological capability. By partnering with key public agencies and integrating into mission-critical platforms, Avantel plays a vital role in strengthening domestic aerospace and defence infrastructure.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Avantel secured a significant purchase order worth ₹17.7 crore from Bharat Electronics Ltd in FY25, aimed at supplying high-frequency software-defined radios (SDRs). This contract reinforces its growth pipeline in the defence electronics space and supports its long-term positioning within Make in India and Atmanirbhar Bharat frameworks.

Name: Krishna Defence & Allied Industries Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1997, Krishna Defence & Allied Industries Ltd (KDAIL) is a diversified Indian company specializing in manufacturing equipment for the defence, security, dairy, and mega kitchen sectors. The company operates two state-of-the-art manufacturing plants located in Kalol and Halol, Gujarat, each dedicated to specific verticals: Kalol focuses on dairy and kitchen equipment, while Halol caters to defence and homeland security products. 

KDAIL is committed to indigenous manufacturing, having entered into licensing agreements with the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) to produce specialized defence application products for the Indian Armed Forces.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Krishna Defence & Allied Industries Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. KDAIL's strategic focus includes venturing into advanced technologies such as N-Type Tunnel Oxide Passivated Contact (TOPCon) and Heterojunction Technology (HJT) to enhance module efficiency and cater to high-irradiance regions.

Name: DCX Systems Ltd
Overview:
DCX Systems Ltd specializes in defense and aerospace manufacturing, offering end-to-end solutions such as cable and wire harness assemblies, PCB assembly (through its subsidiary), and system integration. It serves diverse markets including domestic and international clients in Israel, the US, Korea, and India.

The company stands out for its strategic collaborations in “Make in India” programs and advanced product offerings like airborne and maritime radar systems. Strong partnerships and its own manufacturing capabilities underscore its integrated defense electronics position.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Despite a muted FY25, DCX is building momentum through strategic license grants and high-security product line expansion. Q1 FY26’s revenue and profit recovery, coupled with a strong Y-on-Y order book, suggest improving execution. Its JV strategies and defense credentials position it well for ramping up manufacturing in defense electronics and radar systems.

Name: Unimech Aerospace and Manufacturing Ltd
Overview:
Unimech Aerospace and Manufacturing Limited (UAML), incorporated in 2016, is a global high-precision engineering solutions company specializing in the manufacturing of complex products for aerospace, defense, power generation, and semiconductors. It serves as a critical partner to leading global OEMs such as Airbus, Boeing, Dassault Aviation, Rolls Royce, and GE, delivering aero tooling, precision components, and mechanical assemblies used across production, maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) activities. The company has steadily expanded its portfolio to include over 4,700 SKUs across 35 customers, with strong customer stickiness due to qualification complexity and high trust.

With two ISO-certified facilities in Bangalore and a capacity utilization nearing 95% in FY24, UAML has demonstrated execution strength in build-to-print and build-to-specification offerings, covering machining, fabrication, testing, and final assembly. Its export-oriented business model, where over 90% of revenues come from international markets, reflects deep global integration. Backed by IPO funds and sectoral tailwinds in aerospace, defense, and nuclear, Unimech is strategically aligning itself with India’s push for domestic manufacturing leadership while continuing to tap global demand opportunities.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management has reiterated FY26 revenue growth guidance of 35–40%, with H2 expected to drive the bulk of growth from precision ramp-up, nuclear tender wins, and aerospace inflows. EBITDA margin is targeted at 30–32% despite tariff headwinds, with cost optimization, energy savings, and supply chain renegotiations expected to provide cushion. . With nuclear tenders, precision components expansion, and indigenous jet engine projects, Unimech is well positioned to scale its business, though tariff impacts, working capital stretch, and integration of future M&A remain key watchpoints.

Name: Jaykay Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
Jaykay Enterprises Ltd, established in 1961 and headquartered in Kanpur, India, is a prominent player in the field of advanced manufacturing technologies. As part of the esteemed JK Organisation, the company specializes in additive manufacturing, prototyping, 3D printing, and precision engineering. With over six decades of experience, Jaykay Enterprises has developed a strong reputation for delivering high-precision components and solutions across various sectors, including defense, aerospace, space technology, and medical technology. 

The company's expertise spans multiple areas, including powder metallurgy, large-scale digital manufacturing, reverse engineering, and plant modeling, enabling it to meet the complex requirements of its clients. Jaykay Enterprises operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Bengaluru and Hyderabad, equipped with cutting-edge infrastructure to undertake large-scale and complex projects.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jaykay Enterprises Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to expand its operations in emerging sectors, including space technology and medical technology, to diversify its portfolio and align with evolving market demands. Additionally, Jaykay Enterprises aims to strengthen its position in the defense and aerospace industries by enhancing its capabilities in additive manufacturing and precision engineering.

Name: Centum Electronics Ltd
Overview:
Centum Electronics Ltd, founded in 1993 and headquartered in Bangalore, is a leading Electronics System Design and Manufacturing (ESDM) company in India. The company specializes in providing end-to-end solutions, including design, casting, fabrication, assembly, and testing, for mission-critical products across various sectors such as Defence, Space, Aerospace, Industrial, Transportation, and Medical.

With a global presence, Centum exports its products to over 45 countries and is recognized as an industry leader in India. The company's commitment to innovation and quality has established it as a trusted partner for high-tech electronic systems and subsystems.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Centum Electronics is focusing on expanding its product offerings and enhancing its manufacturing capabilities. The company is investing in new facilities and technologies to strengthen its position in the global market. With a robust order book and strategic initiatives in place, Centum Electronics aims to achieve sustainable growth in the coming fiscal years.

Name: Rossell Techsys Ltd
Overview:
Rossell Techsys Ltd is an Indian aerospace and defense company specializing in high-reliability engineering and manufacturing services. Established in 2011 as the aerospace and defense arm of Rossell India, the company was demerged and listed separately in 2020. Headquartered in Bengaluru, Rossell Techsys provides a range of services, including Build to Print (BTP) and Build to Specification (BTS) solutions, focusing on electrical wiring and interconnect systems, electronic systems, and systems integration. The company serves global OEMs such as Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Honeywell, and General Electric.

Rossell Techsys operates state-of-the-art facilities in India, including a Center of Excellence in Bengaluru dedicated to manufacturing wire harnesses for platforms like the Boeing T-7A Red Hawk. The company has a strong export focus, with nearly two-thirds of its revenue derived from U.S. clients
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Rossell Techsys Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company aims to more than double its contribution to defense aircraft production, targeting a 15% share of an aircraft platform's value within the next three to five years, up from the current 7%. To support this growth, Rossell Techsys plans to expand its workforce to approximately 1,000 employees by the end of the year, up from over 850 currently, and anticipates annual headcount growth of 30% to 35% going forward .

Name: Bharat Electronics Ltd
Overview:
Bharat Electronics Ltd is a leading Indian state-owned defense and aerospace electronics company, established in 1954. It operates nine major factories and several regional offices across India, serving primarily the Indian defense forces but with a growing non-defense business. BEL spends 7% of its turnover on research and development, among the highest for Indian defense PSUs, and maintains a reputation for innovation and strong in-house engineering capabilities.
 
 Business Segment Revenue (FY25)
 * Defence Segment: Accounts for ~92–93% of total revenue in FY2025 (₹21,180 crore out of ₹23,024 crore).
 
 * Non-Defence Segment:
 
 Contributes 7–8% of revenue (₹1,844 crore).
 
 Exports:
 
 Export sales grew 14% in FY2025 to about US$106 million.
 
 Company targeting further expansion in overseas defence and civilian markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
BEL is well-positioned for sustained growth, driven by rising domestic defense spending and a strong pipeline of large-value orders from the armed forces for radars, communication systems, and integrated defense solutions. Strategic collaborations with HAL and industry partners for IRST systems and semiconductor fabs enhance its technological capabilities and diversification.

Name: Great Eastern Shipping Company Ltd
Overview:
The Great Eastern Shipping Company Ltd (GE Shipping) is India’s largest private sector shipping company, with operations spanning crude oil, petroleum products, gas, dry bulk commodities, and offshore oilfield services through its wholly owned subsidiary Greatship India Ltd. The company manages a diversified fleet of tankers, bulk carriers, LPG carriers, offshore supply vessels, and mobile offshore drilling rigs, servicing both domestic and international markets.

Founded in 1948, the company has built a strong position in the maritime and offshore energy services sector, with a business model balancing spot and short-term charters. GE Shipping focuses on operational efficiency, prudent capital allocation, and strategic fleet renewal rather than aggressive expansion, supported by a healthy cash position and disciplined cost management.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
GE Shipping’s strategy centers on preserving financial strength, maintaining spot exposure to capture market upside, and executing disciplined fleet renewal. The offshore segment is expected to stabilize as rigs secure new contracts, while shipping markets may see moderated but steady rates. Strong cash reserves position the company to act opportunistically in asset purchases when market valuations are favorable, without compromising balance sheet strength.

Name: Aequs Ltd
Overview:
Aequs Limited is a vertically integrated precision engineering and contract manufacturing company that focuses on supplying highly complex components and assemblies to leading global original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), especially in the aerospace sector. It operates large advanced manufacturing ecosystems capable of machining, forging, surface treatment and final assembly of critical aerospace parts used in aircraft structures, engines, landing systems and interiors. 

Headquartered in Bengaluru, Aequs has a global footprint with facilities in India, the United States and France, positioning it close to major OEMs such as Airbus, Boeing, Safran and Spirit AeroSystems. Its long-standing customer relationships and expansive product portfolio make it a key Tier-1 supplier in highly regulated global supply chains.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Aequs plans to grow by expanding its aerospace manufacturing capacity and moving into higher-value, more complex aircraft components to capture deeper content in global OEM supply chains. It is also working on broadening its presence in adjacent precision segments such as consumer electronics and engineered consumer products to diversify revenue sources. The company aims to use technological upgrades and process improvements to increase efficiency and strengthen its integrated SEZ ecosystem.

Name: Jyoti CNC Automation Ltd
Overview:
Jyoti CNC Automation Ltd., founded in 1989 and headquartered in Rajkot, Gujarat, is a major manufacturer of metal-cutting CNC machines with a strong focus on the aerospace and defence sectors. It holds a 10–12% share of the Indian CNC machine market and is considered a leading player in 5-axis CNC technologies. The company operates across 60+ countries with over 1.35 lakh machines installed globally and has established itself as a vertically integrated manufacturer with facilities in India and France.

The company provides over 200 variants across 44 product verticals, catering to diverse industries such as aerospace, electronics, automotive, general engineering, EMS, and more. With three manufacturing facilities—two in India and one in Strasbourg, France—Jyoti CNC has an annual capacity of over 6,000 machines in India and 121 machines in France. It has a strong domestic and international distribution network supported by 29 sales and service centers in India and 11 distributors globally.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jyoti CNC Automation is strategically expanding its manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand for advanced CNC machinery. The company has completed an enhancement of its production capacity by 6,000 machines per annum and is set to increase this by an additional 10,000 machines annually over the next two fiscal years. This expansion supports its focus on high-growth sectors such as aerospace, defense, automotive, and general engineering.

Name: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd
Overview:
Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL) is a leading Indian shipyard known for building warships, submarines, and commercial vessels. It has a rich legacy of constructing advanced naval vessels and holds Navratna status. MDL serves primarily the Indian Ministry of Defence but is also growing its commercial and export orders.

The company continues to expand its capacity and capabilities, especially in submarine construction, and plans significant capital expenditure to double its capacity. It maintains a strong order book and is focused on executing large naval projects while exploring export and commercial diversification.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders is embarking on an ambitious expansion to enhance its shipbuilding and repair capabilities. The company is investing between $4.66 billion and $5.8 billion to construct two new drydocks and upgrade existing infrastructure at its Mumbai facilities. Additionally, MDL is acquiring a 51% stake in Colombo Dockyard PLC, Sri Lanka's largest shipyard, to bolster its regional presence and diversify revenue streams.

Name: Azad Engineering Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1983, Azad Engineering Ltd is a precision manufacturing firm specializing in highly engineered components for sectors such as aerospace, defense, energy, and oil & gas. The company supplies critical systems like turbines and industrial components to OEMs, leveraging advanced engineering and machining capabilities.

Its end markets include complex industrial technologies and defense platforms, underpinned by partnerships with leading global organizations and strategic contracts for products such as gas turbine engines and aviation components.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With a robust order book backed by defense and energy contracts, Azad Engineering is well-positioned for sustained growth. Its strong Q1 performance demonstrates resilience and execution strength, while strategic contracts and emerging sector tailwinds suggest continued upward momentum in revenues and margins.

Name: Bharat Dynamics Ltd
Overview:
Bharat Dynamics Ltd (BDL) is a Government of India enterprise under the Ministry of Defence, engaged in the manufacturing of guided missiles, underwater weapons, countermeasure systems, and allied defense equipment. It also offers lifecycle support, refurbishment, and upgrade services for older weapon systems. BDL plays a vital role in India’s defence ecosystem, serving the Indian Armed Forces and select international clients through both indigenous capabilities and joint development programs with foreign OEMs.

The company operates three primary manufacturing units in Hyderabad, Bhanur, and Visakhapatnam, with additional facilities under development. BDL continues to diversify into next-gen defense technologies, drone-based systems, and strategic components like warheads and missile engines, while working toward higher levels of localization and R&D intensity.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL) is poised for significant growth, underpinned by a robust order book exceeding ₹22,700 crore and a strategic focus on expanding its manufacturing capabilities. The company is establishing new production facilities in Ibrahimpatnam (Telangana), Amravati (Maharashtra), and Jhansi (Uttar Pradesh), with a capital expenditure exceeding ₹600 crore, to enhance its capacity to meet the increasing demand for advanced defence systems.

Name: Data Patterns (India) Ltd
Overview:
Data Patterns (India) Limited is a fully integrated defence and aerospace electronics solutions provider with over three decades of expertise, specializing in in-house design, prototype testing, qualification, production, and manufacturing of high-reliability electronic systems. The company’s domain expertise spans radars, electronic warfare systems, communication platforms, avionics, satellites, and automated test equipment, serving major Indian defence agencies like MoD, DRDO, DPSUs, and international clients. It operates from advanced manufacturing facilities in Chennai, featuring 200,000 sq. ft. of built-up area, EMS assembly lines, satellite integration clean rooms, and radar/EW vehicle integration capabilities.

In FY25, Data Patterns achieved a revenue of ₹7,084 million, maintaining strong profitability with an EBITDA margin of 38.8%, ROE of 16%, and ROCE of 18%. The company’s order book stood at ₹8,140 million as of June 30, 2025, with significant contributions from radar and avionics segments. Strategic investments in R&D and capacity expansion, coupled with a focus on building complete systems from in-house IP, position the company for sustained growth, supported by the “Make in India” initiative and rising domestic and export demand.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Data Patterns (India) Limited is poised for significant growth, with projections indicating a 20–25% annual increase in both revenue and earnings per share. The company is focusing on expanding its product offerings, including radar systems, electronic warfare suites, avionics, and communication systems, to cater to the evolving needs of the defense and aerospace sectors. Strategic initiatives involve deepening customer engagements, scaling high-value product platforms, and unlocking operating leverage across its facilities.

Name: Cochin Shipyard Ltd
Overview:
Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL), incorporated in 1972, is a premier shipbuilding and ship repair company in India. It has delivered diverse vessels ranging from bulk carriers to technologically advanced offshore support vessels and warships. CSL has a strong export record, having supplied over 45 vessels globally. It has significantly expanded its capabilities to include green and hybrid vessels aligned with sustainability goals.

CSL operates two major verticals: Shipbuilding and Ship Repair. It has robust infrastructure capable of handling vessels up to 1.25 lakh DWT, with facilities spread across Kochi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Andaman & Nicobar, Udupi, and Howrah. Recent capacity augmentations, including the International Ship Repair Facility (ISRF) and New Dry Dock, position the company well for high-value projects. Its order book exceeds ₹22,500 Cr, offering strong visibility into future revenues.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL) is actively pursuing expansion to solidify its position as a global leader in shipbuilding and maritime services. The company is focusing on constructing larger, technologically advanced vessels and enhancing its ship repair capabilities to meet the growing demands of the defense and commercial sectors. CSL has secured significant contracts, including the construction of hybrid service operation vessels for offshore wind projects and a Master Ship Repair Agreement with the U.S. Navy, indicating a strong international presence.

Name: Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers Ltd
Overview:
Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers Ltd (GRSE) is a leading Indian shipbuilding company under the Ministry of Defence, primarily serving the Indian Navy and Coast Guard. It is the first Indian shipyard to export warships and has delivered over 100 warships. GRSE's portfolio includes a mix of defence and export projects, covering frigates, survey vessels, offshore patrol vessels, research ships, and ferries.

The company maintains a substantial and diversified order book spanning multiple large-scale defence programs, positioning it well for continued growth. Capacity expansions and strategic collaborations aim to meet increasing demand from both defence and commercial shipbuilding sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The outlook remains positive with FY26 expected to be a peak revenue year, supported by deliveries of key projects including P-17 Alpha frigates, survey vessels, and shallow watercraft. New mega projects’ revenue recognition will begin 2+ years post-award. Capacity expansion, strong order inflows, and technology upgrades underpin growth plans, while management remains confident about meeting delivery timelines and sustaining margins amid a growing defence shipbuilding market.

Name: Hindustan Aeronautics Ltd
Overview:
Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) is a pivotal defense PSU in India, specializing in the design, manufacture, and servicing of aircraft, helicopters, avionics, and related systems. The majority of HAL's business comes from supplying the Indian Ministry of Defense, reinforced by a strong order book exceeding ₹1.8 lakh crore as of March 2025. Exports remain a minor contributor, with HAL's focus squarely on India's military needs.
 
 Latest Revenue and Business Segmentation
 Total Revenue (FY 2024-25):
 Approx. ₹30,981 crore from core operations.
 
 Revenue Segmentation (estimates based on available breakdowns):
 
 Aircraft and Aero-structures: Largest contributor, over ₹14,234 crore.
 
 Helicopters and related services: Significant share, estimated between 20–30%.
 
 Engines, Avionics, Accessories, and MRO Services: Balance, including overhaul and repair, technical services, and spares.
 
 Export Revenue: Remains below 5% of total revenue.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
HAL’s growth is driven by strong government support and a steady stream of defense contracts, including fighter aircraft, helicopters, and upgrades. Expansion of Tejas Mk1A production from 8 to 24 aircraft annually ensures timely delivery and fulfillment of IAF requirements. Strategic collaborations with global OEMs, such as Safran and ZeroAvia, support next-generation product development, including helicopter engines and hydrogen powertrains. Continued investment in R&D and eco-friendly technologies positions HAL at the forefront of sustainable and advanced aviation.

Name: Tarsons Products Ltd
Overview:
Tarsons Products Ltd (TPL) is an Indian labware company specializing in designing, developing, manufacturing, and marketing laboratory consumables and equipment. Established in 1983, TPL serves various sectors, including research organizations, academic institutions, pharmaceutical companies, contract research organizations, diagnostic companies, and hospitals. 

The company offers a diverse product portfolio encompassing consumables, reusables, and benchtop equipment, catering to the needs of laboratories across India and internationally.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Tarsons Products Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing its market presence. The company's focus on quality, innovation, and strategic acquisitions positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the global labware market. With a strong foundation and commitment to excellence, TPL is poised for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: GPT Healthcare Ltd
Overview:
GPT Healthcare Ltd, incorporated in 1989, operates a network of multi-specialty hospitals in Eastern India under the ILS Hospitals brand. The company manages 5 hospitals with 719 beds, supported by 91 full-time consultants and over 570 visiting consultants. Its model is designed to serve both Tier I and Tier II cities with a strong focus on accessibility, affordability, and quality of care.

The company’s hospitals include ILS Salt Lake, ILS Dum Dum, ILS Howrah, ILS Agartala, and the newly commissioned ILS Raipur facility. Together, they provide advanced services such as bariatric surgery, robotic procedures, renal transplants, oncology, and interventional neurology. GPT Healthcare is expanding through an asset-light rental model, targeting 1,000 beds in the next 2–2.5 years with new projects like the Jamshedpur hospital.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

GPT Healthcare Ltd is on a robust growth trajectory, aiming to expand its hospital network to 1,000 beds within the next two to three years. The company has recently inaugurated a 158-bed quaternary care hospital in Raipur, Chhattisgarh, marking a significant milestone in its expansion plans. Additionally, GPT Healthcare is focusing on enhancing its service offerings and operational efficiency to support this growth.

Name: Laxmi Dental Ltd
Overview:
Laxmi Dental Ltd is a healthcare and dental technology company focused on manufacturing and supplying dental products, implants, and oral care solutions. It serves dentists, clinics, hospitals, and distributors with a wide range of products that include dental consumables, restorative solutions, and equipment. With a strong emphasis on quality manufacturing and regulatory compliance, the company caters to both domestic and international dental markets.
Laxmi Dental Ltd is a healthcare company specializing in the production of dental products and materials, catering to clinics, hospitals, and laboratories. Its offerings span prosthetics, restorative materials, and dental equipment.

By focusing on innovation and affordability, the company supports both dentists and patients in India and select international markets. Its emphasis on quality has built a strong reputation among practitioners. 

The company combines innovation in dental sciences with advanced production facilities to deliver reliable and affordable solutions to dental practitioners. Its product suite spans endodontics, prosthetics, and orthodontics, supported by a distribution network that enhances accessibility. The increasing awareness of oral healthcare in India and the rising adoption of cosmetic dentistry are driving greater demand for Laxmi Dental’s offerings.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
The outlook remains strong as oral healthcare in India shifts from reactive treatment to preventive and cosmetic care. Laxmi Dental plans to scale exports, deepen institutional partnerships, and expand in tier-2 and tier-3 cities, where dental awareness is growing. With rising healthcare expenditure and an expanding middle-class consumer base, the company is well placed to capture long-term demand in the dental technology space, while maintaining competitive pricing and regulatory compliance.

Name: Suraksha Diagnostic Ltd
Overview:
Suraksha Diagnostic Ltd is a leading integrated diagnostics provider based in Kolkata, offering pathology, radiology, and advanced medical testing services. Established in 1992, it operates across Eastern India with a wide network of diagnostic centers. Its service portfolio spans cardiology, neurology, gastroenterology, and other specialties, making it a comprehensive healthcare provider.

The company has established itself as a trusted brand in diagnostics through quality services and affordability. By integrating preventive healthcare with consultations and testing, it serves a large and growing patient base. Recent capital infusion has enabled the company to pursue further expansion and technological upgradation.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Suraksha Diagnostics is actively expanding its footprint across eastern India, focusing on enhancing access to advanced diagnostic services in tier 2 and tier 3 cities. The company plans to invest ₹200 crore to establish over 20 advanced imaging centres, collaborating with United Imaging to provide state-of-the-art equipment and services. Additionally, Suraksha has acquired a 63% stake in Fetomat Wellness, a fetal and maternal medicine centre, to diversify its service offerings

Name: Max India Ltd
Overview:
Max India is the holding company of the Max Group’s senior care business, Antara Senior Care. Established to address the growing needs of India's aging population, Antara offers a comprehensive range of services, including senior living communities, assisted care, and home healthcare solutions. 

The company aims to provide holistic care that enhances the quality of life for seniors, ensuring they live with dignity and comfort. Antara operates across various locations in India, with facilities in cities like Dehradun, Bengaluru, Gurugram, Noida, and Chennai.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Antara's future outlook for FY26 is promising, with strategic initiatives aimed at expanding its service offerings and geographic presence. The company's focus on enhancing operational efficiency and leveraging digital platforms is expected to drive growth.

Name: Fischer Medical Ventures Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1993, Fischer Chemic Ltd is a trading company specializing in chemicals and machinery. The company operates in various sectors, including pharmaceuticals, food additives, cosmetics, and laboratory instruments, serving a diverse clientele across multiple industries. Fischer Chemic Ltd has established itself as a certified supplier of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) and intermediates, as well as other essential chemical products. 

With a strong presence in the Indian market, Fischer Chemic Ltd continues to focus on expanding its product offerings and enhancing its distribution network. The company aims to meet the growing demand for high-quality chemicals and machinery by leveraging its industry expertise and strategic partnerships. Fischer Chemic Ltd's commitment to quality and customer satisfaction positions it well for sustained growth in the competitive market landscape.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Fischer Chemic Ltd is actively pursuing expansion opportunities to strengthen its position in the Indian market. The company plans to diversify its product portfolio by introducing new chemical and machinery offerings to cater to emerging industry needs. Additionally, Fischer Chemic Ltd aims to enhance its distribution channels, ensuring wider reach and improved customer service.

Name: Artemis Medicare Services Ltd
Overview:
Artemis Medicare Services Ltd, established in 2004 by the promoters of the Apollo Tyres Group, is a prominent healthcare provider in India. The company operates a 541-bed multi-specialty tertiary care hospital in Gurgaon, offering a wide array of medical services. AMSL is recognized for its expertise in specialties such as orthopaedics, oncology, cardiovascular care, neurosciences, and bariatric & minimally invasive surgery. 

In addition to its flagship hospital, AMSL has diversified its healthcare services through various models, including Artemis Daffodil, Artemis Lite, Artemis Cardiac Care, and Artemis Solace. These initiatives aim to expand the company's reach and provide specialized care across different patient needs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Artemis Medicare Services Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its service offerings and enhancing operational efficiencies. The company plans to leverage its diversified healthcare models to cater to a broader patient base.

Name: Indraprastha Medical Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Indraprastha Medical Corporation Ltd is a prominent healthcare provider in India, operating a network of hospitals and diagnostic centers. The company offers a wide range of medical services, including inpatient and outpatient care, diagnostic services, and specialized treatments.

In FY25, Indraprastha Medical Corporation reported significant growth in its financial performance, driven by increased patient volumes and operational efficiencies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Indraprastha Medical Corporation is planning to enhance its healthcare reach by establishing over ten satellite clinics around Delhi, designed as mini-hospitals with emergency backup services to extend post-treatment care. Alongside this, the hospital is undertaking a phased expansion of its main facility, aiming to add around 300 new beds by investing internal accruals and mobilizing debt, unlocking significant additional capacity on its under-utilized campus.

Name: Krsnaa Diagnostics Ltd
Overview:
Krsnaa Diagnostics Ltd is a leading diagnostic services provider in India, offering a wide range of services, including radiology, pathology, and tele-radiology. The company operates an extensive network of diagnostic centers across the country.

Krsnaa Diagnostics focuses on providing affordable and accessible diagnostic services, leveraging technology to enhance service delivery and operational efficiency.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook 
Management guided for revenue growth ahead of industry averages of 15–16%, With retail and PPP ramp-ups, growth is expected to accelerate in coming quarters. The Rajasthan project offers medium-term visibility, while new opportunities such as tie-ups with Apulki Oncology Hospitals bring 30-year revenue streams in urban markets at better-than-PPP pricing. Additional PPP tenders are in the pipeline, though details remain undisclosed.

Name: Shalby Ltd
Overview:
Shalby Ltd is a prominent healthcare provider in India, specializing in multi-specialty hospitals and orthopedic implant manufacturing. Established in 1994, the company operates a network of hospitals across India, offering a range of medical services with a focus on joint replacement surgeries. Shalby Ltd also manufactures orthopedic implants through its subsidiary, Shalby MedTech, catering to both domestic and international markets.

The company is committed to delivering high-quality healthcare services and products, leveraging advanced medical technologies and a skilled workforce. Shalby Ltd continues to expand its footprint in the healthcare sector, aiming to enhance patient outcomes and achieve sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Shalby Ltd aims to expand its hospital network and enhance its implant manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet evolving demands in the healthcare industry. With steady financial performance and strategic initiatives, Shalby Ltd is positioned for sustainable growth in the coming years.

Name: Gaudium IVF and Women Health Ltd
Overview:
Gaudium IVF and Women Health Limited is a healthcare service provider focused on fertility treatments and women’s health solutions. The company specializes in in vitro fertilization (IVF) and offers a range of assisted reproductive services to couples facing infertility issues. Its services include fertility consultations, diagnostic testing, IVF procedures, and other advanced reproductive treatments aimed at improving success rates for patients.

The company operates fertility clinics equipped with specialized medical infrastructure and experienced professionals to deliver personalized treatment plans. It focuses on patient-centric care by combining medical expertise with advanced technology in reproductive healthcare.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its network of fertility clinics across key cities to capture the growing demand for infertility treatments. It aims to invest in advanced reproductive technologies and improve treatment success rates through continuous innovation and research. Increasing awareness about fertility solutions and rising lifestyle-related infertility issues are expected to drive demand for its services.

Name: Gujarat Kidney & Super Speciality Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat Kidney and Super Speciality Limited is a regional multispeciality healthcare provider that operates a chain of hospitals and in-hospital pharmacies across several cities in Gujarat, India. The company focuses on delivering integrated secondary and tertiary medical care, offering services such as general surgery, internal medicine, orthopaedics, obstetrics and gynaecology, cardiology, nephrology and other super-speciality treatments through its network of facilities. 

The company’s hospital portfolio includes multiple facilities in cities like Vadodara, Godhra, Bharuch, Borsad and Anand, with a growing bed capacity and medical staff to serve a wide spectrum of healthcare needs. By providing both routine and advanced medical treatments in underserved and emerging healthcare markets in central Gujarat, it aims to make quality healthcare more accessible.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
