Gujarat Kidney and Super Speciality plans to expand its hospital network through organic growth and strategic acquisitions to enhance service availability across Gujarat. It intends to invest in advanced medical equipment and technology to support higher-complexity procedures and super-speciality care. The company is also focused on strengthening its clinical teams and expanding service lines to cater to rising demand for specialised healthcare.

Name: PB Fintech Ltd
Overview:
PB Fintech Ltd, operating through Policybazaar and Paisabazaar, is a leading digital platform in India’s insurance and lending sectors. Leveraging AI and ML, it offers customers easy access to insurance policies and credit products. Its asset-light, commission-based model enables high scalability and profitability. The subsidiary Docprime adds health-tech solutions, enhancing the company’s diversified digital portfolio.

Business Segments and Estimated Revenue Contribution:
Insurance (Policybazaar): ~65-70%
Revenue primarily from commissions on insurance policy sales and renewals.

Lending (Paisabazaar): ~25-30%
Earnings from commissions on credit products provided via lending partners.

Health Solutions (Docprime): ~5-10%
Contribution from digital health services and innovative health products.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

PB Fintech is expanding its reach by opening physical retail stores, with plans to establish 100 outlets across major Indian cities to enhance customer accessibility. The company is also investing in its healthcare subsidiary, PB Healthcare, with a ₹539 crore infusion to support the development of hospitals and managed healthcare services for policyholders.

Name: Lenskart Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Lenskart Solutions Limited (formerly Valyoo Technologies) is a technology-driven eyewear company founded in India. It designs, manufactures, brands, and retails prescription eyeglasses, sunglasses, contact lenses and accessories through an omnichannel model (online + physical stores). 

The company operates a vertically integrated value chain—covering in-house manufacturing, supply chain management and retail distribution—which enables it to maintain quality control, competitive pricing and a strong market footprint.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Lenskart plans to accelerate its store expansion domestically and internationally by opening more outlets in underserved cities and key export markets. It aims to strengthen its technology-platform, including AI-driven virtual try-ons, in-store digitisation and automation in manufacturing, to improve efficiency and customer experience. The company is targeting a higher share of premium eyewear, increased penetration of branded contact lenses and smart eyewear, and deeper direct-to-consumer engagement.

Name: Medplus Health Services Ltd
Overview:
MedPlus Health Services Ltd is India’s second-largest pharmacy retailer, operating 4,813 stores across 12 states and one union territory as of Q1 FY26, with a network spanning metro, tier-1, tier-2, and tier-3 cities. The company maintains a strong offline presence through company-owned outlets, complemented by e-commerce platforms such as MedPlus Mart, MedPlus Lens, and MedPlus Labs. Its integrated supply chain model sources over 80% of products directly from manufacturers, supported by ~40 warehouses, six of which were commissioned recently to address past fill rate constraints.

The company’s product portfolio includes pharmaceutical and wellness products, FMCG items, and an expanding range of over 1,164 private label SKUs. Private labels contributed 21.5% of Q1 FY26 revenue, significantly improving gross margins. MedPlus also operates a profitable diagnostics segment through three full-service centers, four Level-2 centers, and 110 collection points, although near-term expansion is on hold pending membership growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management expects topline recovery as supply chain upgrades and incentive realignments take effect. EBITDA margin is projected to improve beyond 4.7% in the coming quarters, though 6%+ is viewed as optimistic near term due to new store ramp-up costs. Private label expansion, warehouse network scaling, and a balanced growth approach between topline and margins remain key levers, while diagnostics and franchise models are longer-term growth drivers.

Name: FSN E-Commerce Ventures Ltd
Overview:
Nykaa is India’s leading digitally native consumer technology platform specializing in beauty, personal care, and fashion products. The company offers a content-driven lifestyle retail experience through its extensive online presence and a growing network of profitable physical stores. Nykaa also manufactures and markets its own portfolio of beauty and fashion brands, strengthening its omni-channel ecosystem.

The platform benefits from a large, expanding customer base and a diverse product assortment that includes exclusive global brand launches and a wide range of owned brands. Its ecosystem integrates content, influencer marketing, and proprietary events, driving strong consumer engagement and repeat purchase behavior.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects continued outperformance in beauty driven by premiumization, brand partnerships, and retail expansion. Fashion growth momentum is set to sustain with structural margin improvements. eB2B channel is on path to profitable scale in coming years. Nykaa remains cautious on margin guidance due to evolving segment mix but confident of sustained growth, improved unit economics, and leadership in the beauty and fashion e-commerce space.

Name: Swiggy Ltd
Overview:
Swiggy is a consumer-first tech company that provides an app-based platform for ordering food, groceries, and daily essentials. It operates multiple verticals like food delivery, quick-commerce (Instamart), supply chain/logistics services, and related features designed to make ordering more convenient for users. Swiggy connects customers with a wide network of restaurants, grocery stores, and delivery partners across hundreds of Indian cities.

Since its launch, Swiggy has continually introduced new services and formats—such as dark stores for quick delivery, specialised offerings, subscription tiers, and micro-apps to target specific user needs. Its growth has been driven by building out its logistics and supply chain backbone, investing in fulfilment, and scaling user acquisition while trying to improve unit economics.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Swiggy is shifting more focus toward efficiency and profitability instead of just expanding fast. It plans to grow its quick-commerce network by adding many new dark stores and increasing their size, letting users get more variety in less time. Investments in supply chain infrastructure (like its Scootsy unit) are intended to improve delivery speed and lower costs. Swiggy expects revenue growth from both its food delivery and Instamart arms, especially as its average order values keep rising.

Name: Meesho Ltd
Overview:
Meesho is an Indian app-based marketplace that connects sellers and end customers through a digital platform, enabling online shopping across a wide range of categories including fashion, accessories, electronics, home and kitchen items, health and fitness equipment, and office supplies. The platform focuses on affordability and convenience, helping small businesses, individual sellers and local manufacturers reach consumers throughout India by providing easy onboarding, logistics support and a user-friendly mobile experience. 

Meesho has built a large and diverse user base by combining its marketplace model with technology that enhances product discovery and personalisation. It serves millions of shoppers, including a growing segment from smaller towns and non-metro regions, while offering sellers tools to manage their online presence, orders, payments and delivery.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Meesho plans to expand its marketplace reach further into underserved towns and rural regions by leveraging AI-driven discovery tools, voice- and chat-based interfaces to simplify shopping for first-time internet buyers, and enhanced logistics to reduce delivery costs. It is focusing on increasing its seller base and product assortment while deepening its presence in high-frequency categories like beauty, home essentials and branded goods, which can drive repeat purchases.

Name: Pine Labs Ltd
Overview:
Pine Labs Limited, founded in 1998, is a leading Indian fintech company that provides merchant commerce solutions to retailers and enterprises. Pine Labs also enables consumer brands and financial institutions to issue prepaid cards, gift cards, and loyalty programs through its issuing platform. 

The company operates across India and has expanded internationally into markets like Southeast Asia and the Middle East. Over time, Pine Labs has built a merchant network of nearly one million outlets, including retailers in sectors such as travel, healthcare, grocery, and lifestyle.ts technology infrastructure is modern and API-first, enabling real-time analytics, fraud prevention, and seamless integration. Despite intense competition, it remains differentiated by combining payments, credit (EMI and BNPL), loyalty, and card issuance on a single platform.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Pine Labs is planning to use fresh capital to scale its international operations and fuel innovation in its payment checkout ecosystem. It aims to deepen merchant engagement through value-added services like BNPL at checkout, loyalty programs, and analytics, while increasing its issuing business (gift cards, prepaid cards) via Qwikcilver. The company is focusing on improving profitability by leveraging a software-led, API-first platform to boost high-margin revenues.

Name: Info Edge (India) Ltd
Overview:
Info Edge is India’s leading online classifieds company with a strong portfolio spanning recruitment (Naukri.com), real estate (99acres.com), matrimonial (Jeevansathi.com), and education services (Shiksha.com). It also actively invests in new-age internet and tech-enabled start-ups through its venture fund, strengthening its digital ecosystem.
 
 Business Segments
 
 Recruitment (Naukri.com): ~74% of total revenue
 
 Real Estate (99acres.com): ~14% of total revenue
 
 Others (Jeevansathi.com, Shiksha.com, Investments): ~12% of total revenue
 
 Geographic Split: India ~93% | Overseas ~7%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Info Edge (Naukri) is positioned for resilient growth across its digital classifieds portfolio. While IT hiring remains uncertain, strong momentum in non-tech sectors and operational efficiencies support revenue and margin stability. Growth in 99acres, Shiksha, and Jeevansathi—along with venture investments—add diversification. Continued expansion in niche segments and potential tailwinds from global outsourcing trends position the company for steady performance.

Name: One 97 Communications Ltd
Overview:
One 97 Communications Ltd, popularly known as Paytm, is India’s leading digital ecosystem platform catering to both consumers and merchants. It operates a comprehensive range of services including mobile payments, merchant acquiring, financial services, lending distribution, and commerce & cloud offerings. The platform has built a vast payments network supported by millions of merchants and a wide base of payment devices.

The company has a strong position in merchant payments and is expanding its role in financial services, particularly merchant lending. Leveraging in-house technology, hardware innovation, and AI-driven solutions, Paytm continues to focus on deepening customer engagement, increasing merchant stickiness, and expanding monetization opportunities across its ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Paytm will focus on strengthening merchant payments, scaling merchant lending, and deepening AI integration across products and processes. Growth will be driven by device innovation, higher-value payment streams, and strategic expansion of financial services, with management confident in the multi-year potential of India’s digital payments and merchant ecosystem.

Name: Indiamart Intermesh Ltd
Overview:
IndiaMART InterMESH Ltd is India’s largest online B2B marketplace, commanding approximately 60% market share in the domestic online B2B classifieds segment. The platform hosts a vast supplier base of ~7.9 million storefronts, supported by ~214,000 paying subscription suppliers and over 108 million live product listings spanning 56 industries and 98,000 product categories. With ~194 million registered buyers and a high repeat usage rate of 53%, the platform facilitates ~24 million unique business enquiries annually and generates ~136 million supplier responses and callbacks.

The company operates a subscription-based revenue model with a tiered supplier base, where the top 10% contribute ~47% of revenues, supported by an ARPU of ₹2.61 lakh, and the top 1% contribute ~16% with an ARPU of ₹9.05 lakh. Strategic investments have been made in complementary businesses such as Vyapar, Busy, Real Books, Live Keeping, Bizom, and Easyecom to strengthen its ecosystem, while ~95% of revenues continue to come from its core standalone marketplace business.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
IndiaMART is well-positioned to leverage its scale for further upselling of value-added services and cross-integration of SaaS offerings. The focus remains on expanding monetization per supplier, increasing penetration in Tier II and III markets, and integrating ecosystem investments to create a unified B2B digital platform. This integration could accelerate customer acquisition, enhance ARPU, and solidify long-term leadership.

Name: Eternal Ltd
Overview:
Zomato Limited, recently rebranded as Eternal Limited, is India’s top digital food services platform, offering food delivery, dining-out experiences, loyalty programs, and B2B restaurant supply through Hyperpure. Its flagship app is the most downloaded food and drinks application in India, reaching over 1,000 cities nationwide.
 
 Business Segment Breakdown (FY2025)
 Food Delivery
 
 Revenue Contribution: 39.9%
 
 Actual Revenue: ~₹8,085 crore
 
 Core business connecting customers, restaurants, and delivery partners. Zomato holds 54%–56% market share in India’s food delivery market. Segment saw 18% YoY growth.
 
 * Hyperpure (B2B Restaurant Supply): Revenue Contribution: 30.6%
 Supplies fresh ingredients and products directly to restaurants. Surged 93–95% YoY and surpassed Blinkit in revenue.
 
 * Blinkit (Quick Commerce/Ancillary): Revenue Contribution: 25.7%
 Quick commerce arm delivering groceries and essentials in under 15 minutes. Revenue grew over 120% YoY, but losses widened due to rapid expansion and infrastructure investments.
 
 * Dining Out/Entertainment (District Going-Out, Zomaland, Zomato Gold, Paytm Insider): Revenue Contribution: 3.6%
 
 * Encompasses restaurant search, bookings, reviews, loyalty, event ticketing. This segment continues to grow as Zomato pivots to a broader consumer platform.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Zomato is well-positioned for long-term growth, driven by rising digital penetration, increasing urbanization, and evolving consumer lifestyles that boost on-demand food and dining services. Expansion of Hyperpure allows the company to tap into the large restaurant procurement market with cost-efficient, quality-assured supplies, while Blinkit addresses mass-market demand for instant grocery and household deliveries. Cross-platform ecosystem synergies, including bundled loyalty programs and event integrations, enhance user engagement and wallet share.

Name: Indian Railway Catering & Tourism Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Ltd. (IRCTC) is a government-owned Mini Ratna PSU under the Ministry of Railways. It manages internet ticketing, catering, packaged drinking water (Rail Neer), and travel & tourism services for Indian Railways, handling millions of passengers daily.
 
 Business Segments 
 
 Internet Ticketing: ~40% of total revenue
 
 Catering Services: ~35% of total revenue
 
 Rail Neer (Packaged Drinking Water): ~10% of total revenue
 
 Travel & Tourism: ~15% of total revenue
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

IRCTC is leveraging its monopoly in online railway ticketing and asset-light digital model to maintain strong margins. It is expanding non-ticketing revenues through e-catering, tourism services, advertisements, and Rail Neer water plants. The company is also capitalizing on India’s growing rail passenger traffic and digital shift. Despite privatization delays, IRCTC’s diversified portfolio, steady profitability, and leadership in rail-related services position it for consistent, long-term growth.

Name: Mallcom (India) Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1983, Mallcom (India) Ltd is a leading manufacturer and distributor of personal protective equipment (PPE) and industrial safety products. The company offers a comprehensive range of safety solutions, including head protection, body protection, hand protection, and foot protection, catering to various industries such as construction, manufacturing, and chemicals. 

With a strong presence in over 50 countries, Mallcom is recognized for its commitment to quality and innovation in the safety equipment sector.  Headquartered in Kolkata, India, Mallcom operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and has a robust distribution network.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Mallcom achieved an operational income of ₹1,224 crore, a 19.5% year-on-year growth. The company reported a net profit of ₹99 crore, with an EBITDA margin of 14.38%. Looking ahead, Mallcom aims to maintain a 15% revenue growth trajectory, targeting a revenue of ₹800–1,000 crore by FY28.

Name: Vakrangee Ltd
Overview:
Vakrangee Ltd, founded in 1990, is a technology-driven company based in Mumbai, India. It offers a diverse range of services, including e-governance, e-commerce, financial services, and logistics, primarily through its franchise network known as Vakrangee Kendra. These centers serve as last-mile delivery points, providing services such as banking, insurance, utility bill payments, and government-related services to underserved regions. 

The company has been instrumental in promoting digital inclusion across India, especially in rural and semi-urban areas. Vakrangee's strategic initiatives focus on leveraging technology to bridge the digital divide, ensuring access to essential services for all. With a vision to empower communities and foster sustainable growth, Vakrangee continues to expand its footprint and enhance its service offerings.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Vakrangee Ltd aims to further expand its network of Vakrangee Kendras, enhancing its reach and service offerings. The company plans to continue leveraging technology to improve service delivery and operational efficiency. With a focus on digital inclusion and customer satisfaction, Vakrangee is poised for sustained growth in the coming years

Name: Dredging Corporation of India Ltd
Overview:
Dredging Corporation of India Ltd is a public sector undertaking under the Ministry of Ports, Shipping, and Waterways, specializing in dredging and reclamation services for ports and waterways. The company plays a crucial role in maintaining navigational channels and supporting port infrastructure development.

In FY25, Dredging Corporation reported a revenue of ₹1,200 crore, a 10% increase from the previous year. The company's EBITDA stood at ₹200 crore, with a margin of 16.7%, and a PAT of ₹100 crore, reflecting a 5% year-over-year growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Dredging Corporation of India Limited (DCI) is strategically positioning itself for significant growth by expanding its operations both domestically and internationally. The company is actively exploring business opportunities in the Middle East, Southeast Asia, and South Asia, aiming to tap into the rising demand for dredging services in these regions. Domestically, DCI plans to capitalize on port expansions by major players like AMNS India, JSW Group, and Adani Group, along with opportunities in dam and reservoir maintenance.

Name: Popular Vehicles & Services Ltd
Overview:
Popular Vehicles and Services Ltd (PVSL), established in 1983, is a prominent automotive dealership in India. The company specializes in the sale and service of automobiles, including passenger, commercial, and luxury vehicles. Additionally, PVSL offers spare parts, accessories, and commissions from finance and insurance services. Operating primarily in Kerala and Tamil Nadu, PVSL has a significant presence in the Indian automotive retail sector.

The company's business model encompasses a wide range of services, from vehicle sales to after-sales support. PVSL's dealership network includes both Maruti Suzuki and Mercedes-Benz, catering to diverse customer segments.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
PVSL aims to strengthen its position in the automotive retail sector by expanding its dealership network and enhancing its service offerings. The company plans to focus on the growing EV market, leveraging its partnerships and LoIs to establish new facilities.

Name: Signpost India Ltd
Overview:
Established in 2008, Signpost India Ltd is a leading Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) advertising company in India. The company specializes in large-scale public projects such as street furniture, transit systems, and internet traffic monitoring, collaborating with over 32 government authorities across the country. Signpost India is recognized for its innovative approach, introducing India's first and largest main street DOOH billboards and the world's largest digital bus queue shelter.

The company owns 33.9 million square feet of advertising space and offers a diverse range of media assets, including billboards, skywalks, bus panels, airports, metro stations, bus queue shelters, smart mobile vans, traffic booths, and public electric bicycle sharing stations
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Signpost India Ltd is strategically expanding its reach into Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities, aiming to tap into the growing demand for digital advertising in these regions. The company is investing in programmatic Out-of-Home (OOH) platforms to offer real-time, data-driven campaign delivery, enhancing targeting and measurement capabilities for clients.

Name: Sindhu Trade Links Ltd
Overview:
Sindhu Trade Links Ltd is a diversified Indian holding company with interests in transportation and logistics, coal mining and trading, petroleum retail, power generation, media, investment & finance, and engineering services. Originally incorporated in 1992, it has evolved into a multi-sector enterprise serving infrastructure and industrial markets.

Leveraging a network of subsidiaries, the company operates across seven business verticals—including coal logistics, biomass power, media production, and fuel retail operations—achieving synergies from diversified cash flows and cross-sector exposure.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Sindhu Trade Links is focused on recalibrating its business mix, leaning on strengths in coal logistics and fuel operations while exploring strategic expansion in mining, biomass power, and engineering services. If core cycles stabilize and volume recovers, selective scaling in resilient segments should support a gradual recovery in FY26 revenue and profitability.

Name: Raymond Ltd
Overview:
Raymond Limited, incorporated in 1925, is a diversified group with operations in textiles, apparel, real estate, FMCG, and engineering, serving customers in over 55 countries, including the USA, Europe, Japan, and the Middle East. The company operates one of the world’s largest vertically and horizontally integrated worsted suiting fabric manufacturing setups and has a retail network of 1,638 stores, of which 1,589 are in India across about 600 towns and cities, and 49 are overseas stores in nine countries.

Through ongoing restructuring, Raymond is separating its businesses into independent listed entities—Lifestyle, Real Estate, and Engineering—to sharpen strategic focus and unlock value. The engineering segment, bolstered by acquisitions such as Maini Precision Products, serves global automotive, aerospace, and defence OEMs and is positioned to capture the “China+1” supply chain shift.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Post demerger, Raymond aims to leverage its balance sheet strength, engineering synergies, and global OEM relationships to scale its aerospace and auto businesses. Management expects secular growth from global supply chain realignments and India’s manufacturing push, with the aerospace division potentially being listed separately once it reaches maturity. The real estate arm targets >25% ROCE via a JDA-led premium residential strategy, and the engineering business is positioned to be a Tier 1.5 global supplier with high entry barriers and diversified sector exposure.

Name: Consolidated Finvest & Holdings Ltd
Overview:
Consolidated Finvest & Holdings Ltd is a non-banking financial company (NBFC) based in India. The company primarily engages in lending and investment activities, focusing on investments in shares, stocks, bonds, debentures, mutual funds, inter-corporate deposits, and providing loans. This diversified approach allows the company to generate income through various financial instruments.

Incorporated in 1993, Consolidated Finvest & Holdings Ltd is part of the B.C. Jindal Group. The company operates with a lean structure, employing a small team, and is registered with the Reserve Bank of India. Its business activities are carried out under the segment of investment activities, with the majority of revenue sourced from income derived from these investments.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Building on the momentum from FY 25, Consolidated Finvest & Holdings Ltd is well-positioned for continued growth in FY 26. The strategic focus on expanding its investment portfolio and optimizing loan offerings is expected to enhance profitability.

Name: Medi Assist Healthcare Services Ltd
Overview:
Medi Assist is India’s leading health-tech and third-party administrator (TPA) in the health insurance ecosystem. The company provides claims management, policy servicing, and hospital network aggregation for insurers, corporates, and government schemes.

Medi Assist Healthcare Services is a leading health benefits and insurance-tech company in India, specializing in third-party administration (TPA) services for insurance providers, corporates, and government entities. The company acts as a crucial intermediary between insurers, policyholders, and healthcare providers, offering services that streamline claims, hospitalization, and wellness benefits. Its platform-driven approach helps enhance customer experience while ensuring efficiency for insurers. 

The company leverages technology-enabled processes to provide end-to-end healthcare management solutions, including cashless hospitalization, claims management, policy administration, and wellness offerings. Its vast hospital network and strong partnerships with insurance players allow it to maintain scale and deliver customized solutions across diverse client segments, strengthening its position in India’s growing health insurance ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Medi Assist aims to expand its market share, enhance digital capabilities, and diversify its service offerings. With continued focus on financial strength and operational efficiency, the company is positioned for sustainable growth in the health benefits and insurance services sector.

Name: Regaal Resources Ltd
Overview:
Regaal Resources Limited (RRL) is a prominent agro-processing company based in India, specializing in the production of high-quality maize-based products. These include native and modified starches, food-grade starches, and various starch derivatives. The company operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Kishanganj, Bihar, strategically located in a major maize cultivation hub, which ensures a steady supply of raw materials. RRL's product portfolio caters to diverse industries such as food processing, paper, textiles, pharmaceuticals, adhesives, and cosmetics, highlighting its versatility and market reach.

With a commitment to sustainability, RRL operates a Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) plant, underscoring its dedication to environmentally responsible practices. The company's expansive distribution network spans over 20 states in India and extends to several Southeast Asian countries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Regaal Resources is poised for significant growth, aiming to double its production capacity from 825 to 1,650 metric tonnes per day at its Kishanganj plant. This expansion is supported by a ₹430 crore investment, encompassing debt and internal accruals. The company plans to diversify its product offerings by introducing natural starch-based products, including herbal gulal.

Name: Kirloskar Industries Ltd
Overview:
Kirloskar Industries is a diversified industrial group active in sectors such as iron castings, investments (including securities and real estate), wind energy generation, and property leasing. It holds land and buildings across several major Indian cities, which it leases to group entities and third parties. The company blends manufacturing, infrastructure, and real estate assets to generate both operational and passive revenues.

Its subsidiary structure includes ferro-casting operations, commercial real estate development, and renewable energy initiatives, creating a hybrid business model that balances industrial operations with asset-based income and sustainability initiatives.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Kirloskar Industries is positioned for a recovery trajectory underpinned by strong subsidiary momentum, real estate ramp-up, and diversified income streams. While full-year outlook remains cautious due to prior-year volatility, the current quarter’s gains signal operational resilience and long-term strategic value.

Name: Balmer Lawrie & Company Ltd
Overview:
Balmer Lawrie & Company Ltd, established in 1867, is a diversified public sector enterprise under the Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas, Government of India. The company operates across multiple sectors, including Industrial Packaging, Greases & Lubricants, Leather Chemicals, Logistics Services, Infrastructure & Oil Field Services, and Travel & Vacation Services. With a strong presence in both public and private sectors, Balmer Lawrie has established itself as a key player in India's industrial landscape.

The company is strategically expanding its portfolio to include emerging sectors such as ethanol production and third-party logistics. Balmer Lawrie is also focusing on enhancing its travel and vacation services, aiming to capture a significant share of the leisure travel market. This diversification aligns with the company's vision to achieve a revenue target of ₹6,000 crore by 2030, reflecting its commitment to sustainable growth and innovation.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Balmer Lawrie aims to achieve a revenue target of ₹6,000 crore by 2030. The company's strategic focus on expanding its travel and logistics services, coupled with its foray into ethanol production, positions it well to capitalize on emerging market opportunities. Continued diversification and operational efficiency are expected to drive sustainable growth in the coming years.

Name: PTL Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
PTL Enterprises Ltd operates in the leasing business, primarily leasing its tire manufacturing plant in Kerala to Apollo Tyres on a long-term basis. This asset-light model allows the company to generate stable rental income without engaging in active manufacturing operations.

Over time, PTL Enterprises has positioned itself as a steady income generator with minimal operational risks. Its revenue profile is largely predictable, though growth is dependent on contractual terms with Apollo Tyres and potential diversification into other investments.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
PTL Enterprises may explore diversification into investment avenues, though growth opportunities remain limited given its reliance on a single lease model.

Name: Bombay Super Hybrid Seeds Ltd
Overview:
Bombay Super Hybrid Seeds Ltd is a leading producer of hybrid seeds in India, specializing in a variety of crops including vegetables and field crops. The company is known for its high-quality seeds and strong research and development capabilities.

With a focus on innovation and quality, Bombay Super Hybrid Seeds has established a strong presence in the Indian seed industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Bombay Super Hybrid Seeds plans to expand its research and development efforts to introduce new seed varieties. The company is focusing on increasing its market share in the hybrid seed segment and exploring new markets to drive growth.

Name: Bluspring Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
Bluspring Enterprises Ltd, incorporated in 2025, is an Indian infrastructure services company that emerged as an independent entity following a strategic demerger from Quess Corp Ltd.

The company integrates six established brands—Avon Facility Management Services, Indya Foods Services, Terrier Security Services, Hofincons Industrial Maintenance, Vedang Cellular Services, and Foundit—into a unified ecosystem, enabling it to deliver end-to-end solutions across various sectors
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bluspring Enterprises Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to expand its operations by increasing its annual solar module manufacturing capacity from 700 MW to 1.5 GW by the end of the year, with a further expansion to 4.5 GW by 2026.

Name: Midwest Ltd.
Overview:
Midwest Ltd, incorporated in 1981, is a leading player in the natural resources sector engaged in the exploration, mining, processing, marketing, and export of natural stones. The company is well known for its premium granite varieties like Black Galaxy and Absolute Black, which are exported to multiple international markets. With vertically integrated operations, Midwest manages every stage of the value chain — from quarrying and processing to global distribution — ensuring consistent quality and supply reliability.

Over the years, the company has diversified beyond natural stones into high-value segments such as quartz, heavy mineral sands, and advanced stone-processing technologies. It has adopted modern, sustainable mining practices and expanded its presence in domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Midwest Ltd aims to expand by increasing its capacity and resource base in quartz and heavy mineral sands to tap into growing demand from sectors like renewable energy, semiconductors and construction. The company plans to deepen its “mine-to-market” value chain, enhance its global distribution network and strengthen export sales, especially in premium stone varieties. It will further invest in mechanisation and sustainable practices – including electric equipment and solar power – to improve efficiency and cost competitiveness

Name: Adani Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
Adani Enterprises Ltd is a leading Indian conglomerate with diversified interests spanning mining, integrated resource management (IRM), renewable energy, airports, roads, and digital infrastructure. The company’s core IRM and mining operations play a vital role in importing coal and managing supply chains for India’s energy needs. Adani has also built strong positions in high-growth sectors such as solar cell/module manufacturing, airports, and data centers, while maintaining activities in shipping, bunkering, agri-storage, and other infrastructure verticals. Its ability to incubate new businesses and spin off mature ones into independently listed entities underpins a dynamic, growth-driven business model. Adani Enterprises is aggressively investing in capital-intensive opportunities, including a flagship copper refinery and green hydrogen initiatives, aiming to support India's manufacturing and energy transition goals
 
 Business Segment:
 Integrated Resource Managment: 37%
 Mining Services: 4%
 Commercial Mining: 5%
 New Energy Ecosystem (Solar, Green H2): 13%
 Airports: 10%
 Roads: 10%
 Others (Shipping, Agri, bunkering): 21%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
The company plans massive capital expenditure of $15–20 billion annually over the next five years, focusing on high-opportunity sectors such as airports, roads, data centers, and clean energy. Multiple business verticals, including airports, roads, and industrial projects, are progressing toward independent listings between 2026–2029 to unlock value and sharpen strategic focus. Accelerated investments in solar, wind, and green hydrogen aim to capitalize on India’s clean energy transition, while a new copper refinery supports import substitution and domestic manufacturing.

Name: 3M India Ltd
Overview:
3M India is a diversified science and technology-based subsidiary of the global 3M Company, USA. Operating since 1986 and rebranded in 2002, the company offers a wide range of innovative products across safety, industrial, healthcare, consumer, electronics, and transportation sectors. It operates manufacturing units in Ahmedabad, Pune, and Bengaluru, and houses an R&D and innovation center in Bengaluru.

As a market leader in its categories, 3M India supplies to sectors ranging from automotive and healthcare to consumer and electronics, utilizing a hybrid distribution model that includes direct sales, traditional trade channels, and e-commerce. It leverages global technology platforms to localize high-impact solutions for Indian consumers and industries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

3M India is strategically focusing on high-growth sectors such as automotive, electronics, energy, safety, home cleaning, and home improvement, which are expected to drive its expansion in the medium term. The company is enhancing its manufacturing capabilities and localizing its product portfolio to better serve the Indian market. Additionally, 3M India is leveraging sustainability as a key driver of business growth, aiming to achieve operational excellence and technological innovation.

Name: DCM Shriram Ltd
Overview:
DCM Shriram Limited is a diversified manufacturing conglomerate with a strong presence in fertilizers, chloro-vinyl products, cement, sugar, farm solutions, building systems, and bioseeds. It operates large integrated manufacturing facilities in Kota, Rajasthan, and Bharuch, Gujarat, making it a key player in India’s chemical and agri-input sectors. The company leverages vertical integration, strong R&D capabilities, and a nationwide distribution network to serve both industrial and retail markets.

The company’s business spans seven major segments—Sugar, Chlor Vinyl, Farm Solutions, Fertilizer, Fenesta Building Systems, Bioseed, and Others—providing a balanced revenue mix. DCM Shriram continues to invest heavily in capacity expansion, value-added downstream products, and new market opportunities, while exploring strategic restructuring in its agri-inputs and building materials businesses.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company is in the midst of a significant capacity expansion cycle across chemicals and downstream products, which is expected to drive revenue growth over the next two years. With increased backward integration, product diversification, and strategic restructuring, DCM Shriram aims to strengthen its position in high-margin specialty products and enhance shareholder value. The Gujarat investment plan further positions it for long-term growth in the chemical and petrochemical sectors.

Name: Sukhjit Starch & Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1943, Sukhjit Starch & Chemicals Ltd is a prominent agro-processing company based in India, specializing in the production of starch and its derivatives. The company serves various industries, including food, paper, textile, and pharmaceuticals, by providing essential ingredients such as maize starch, dextrin, liquid glucose, and dextrose monohydrate. 

With a legacy of over seven decades, Sukhjit has established itself as a key player in the starch manufacturing sector. Sukhjit operates multiple manufacturing facilities across India, including plants in Phagwara, Punjab, and Malda, West Bengal.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sukhjit Starch & Chemicals Ltd is actively pursuing strategic expansion to strengthen its position in the agro-processing sector. The company is enhancing its maize processing capacities across multiple locations, including modernizing outdated facilities to meet growing demand. Additionally, Sukhjit is focusing on increasing the share of high-value starch derivatives in its product mix to improve profitability.

Name: Godfrey Phillips India Ltd
Overview:
Godfrey Phillips India Limited (GPIL), the flagship company of the KK Modi Group, is one of India’s leading FMCG players with a dominant presence in the tobacco industry. The company owns iconic cigarette brands such as Four Square, Red & White, and Cavanders and holds an exclusive agreement with Philip Morris International to manufacture and distribute Marlboro cigarettes in India. Alongside tobacco, GPIL has diversified into confectionery, Ferrero food products distribution, and previously retail through its 24Seven convenience store chain.

The company has a strong domestic and international presence, operating in over 35 countries across Latin America, the Middle East, Southeast Asia, and Eastern Europe. GPIL’s manufacturing facilities are certified for quality, environmental management, and occupational safety, with in-house R&D capabilities to drive product innovation. Its distribution network exceeds 950 distributors and 9,000 field force members, supported by over 1,000 employees.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company aims to sustain domestic volume growth, deepen export market penetration, and scale its confectionery business. Leveraging the Philip Morris partnership, expanding unmanufactured tobacco sales, and entering new markets remain top priorities. With a strategic blend of premium brand positioning, infrastructure utilization, and ESG-driven operations, GPIL is well-positioned for long-term growth.

Name: MSTC Ltd
Overview:
MSTC Limited is a Government of India enterprise engaged in trading, e-commerce, and disposal services, covering ferrous and non-ferrous scrap, minerals, agri and forest products, and surplus assets. It operates a unique platform-driven business model with strong presence across the private sector and select government clients.

The company has steadily evolved from being a scrap trader into a leading e-commerce service provider with sector-agnostic capabilities. Its technology-driven platforms cater to large corporates and state governments, with expansion into new areas such as spectrum auctions, civil aviation, equipment leasing, and mineral block auctions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is optimistic of returning to a 10–12% revenue growth trajectory driven by new product launches, expanded client acquisition, and policy-driven opportunities. Growth is expected in state-level projects, spectrum and aviation auctions, and in emerging areas like leasing and coal gasification. While volatility is inherent due to event-driven revenues, MSTC’s diversified platform and strong client base are expected to support steady performance.

Name: Wonder Electricals Ltd
Overview:
Wonder Electricals Ltd, formerly known as Wonder Fibromats Ltd, is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in energy-efficient fans, including ceiling, exhaust, pedestal, and BLDC (Brushless DC) models. Established in 2009 and rebranded in 2022, the company offers integrated design and manufacturing solutions for both Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and Original Design Manufacturers (ODMs) in the consumer durable fan industry. 

The company operates from its headquarters in New Delhi and has a manufacturing facility accredited with quality and environmental management system certifications. Wonder Electricals is committed to building world-class products in India, focusing on sustainability and environmental responsibility.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Wonder Electricals Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to introduce new energy-efficient products, including electric heaters and kettles, to diversify its product portfolio and address off-season demand. Additionally, Wonder Electricals aims to expand its export markets and strengthen its position as a leading player in the global fan manufacturing industry.

Name: Kothari Industrial Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Founded in 1970, Kothari Industrial Corporation Ltd (KICL) is a diversified Indian conglomerate headquartered in Chennai. The company operates across multiple sectors, including fertilizers, FMCG, healthcare, drones, hotels, and leather. Historically, KICL was involved in manufacturing Single Super Phosphate (SSP) fertilizers at its Ennore plant. However, due to challenges in sourcing quality raw materials and working capital constraints, the fertilizer production was discontinued, and the facility was leased to Coromandel International Ltd for modernization.

In recent years, KICL has undergone a significant transformation, diversifying its business portfolio. The company has ventured into the FMCG sector, offering products like rusks, cookies, and health supplements. Additionally, KICL has expanded into the drone industry, providing services and products related to unmanned aerial vehicles.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

KICL is actively pursuing growth strategies to strengthen its position across various sectors. The company plans to enhance its FMCG product line, focusing on health-conscious offerings to meet evolving consumer preferences. In the drone segment, KICL aims to expand its service offerings, targeting applications in agriculture, surveillance, and logistics. The hotel division is set for expansion, with new properties planned to cater to the growing tourism and hospitality demand.

Name: MMTC Ltd
Overview:
MMTC Limited is a public sector undertaking under the administrative control of the Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India. Established in 1963, the company was incorporated to facilitate foreign trade in India and canalize the export and import of essential minerals and metals. Over the years, MMTC has diversified its operations to include trading across various sectors such as agro products, fertilizers & chemicals, coal & hydrocarbons, and precious metals.

MMTC is an established supplier and buyer with over three decades of experience in the trade of wheat, rice, maize, sugar, and other commodities. It is also engaged in the import of finished, intermediate, and raw fertilizers, handling about 3 to 4 million tonnes of fertilizers annually.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, MMTC is focusing on revitalizing its core trading operations and exploring new business opportunities to enhance revenue streams. The company is also investing in digital transformation and process optimization to improve operational efficiency. While challenges persist, MMTC's strategic initiatives are expected to position it for a recovery in FY26, contingent on favorable market conditions and successful implementation of its plans.

Name: Hexa Tradex Ltd
Overview:
Hexa Tradex Ltd is an industrial and trading company involved in the trading of metals, chemicals, and agricultural products. The company provides end-to-end supply chain solutions to domestic and international clients. Hexa Tradex Ltd focuses on timely delivery, quality assurance, and competitive pricing to strengthen customer relationships.

The company leverages technology to optimize logistics and inventory management. It also explores new markets for export and import of industrial products, expanding its business footprint. Hexa Tradex Ltd aims to remain a reliable partner for industrial and agricultural supply needs.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Hexa Tradex Ltd is steadily navigating its path forward by focusing on operational stability and financial rehabilitation. Despite facing volatility in revenue and profitability, the company is aiming for a turnaround by optimizing its diversified trading and finance activities. Through internal restructuring and cost control measures, Hexa Tradex is working to restore consistent revenue flows and improve margins. It continues to manage regulatory matters and the ongoing delisting process with an eye on compliance and stabilizing its corporate structure.

Name: Landmark Cars Ltd
Overview:
Landmark Cars Ltd is a prominent Indian automotive retailer specializing in premium and luxury vehicle brands, including Mercedes-Benz, Honda, Jeep, Volkswagen, Renault, and MG Motor. Established in 1998, the company operates a network of dealerships and service centers across India, offering new and pre-owned vehicles, after-sales services, and automotive accessories.

The company is engaged in the business of authorized sales and service of automobiles, operating workshops and garages for repairs, and providing related services. Landmark Cars Ltd focuses on delivering a superior customer experience through its extensive network and comprehensive service offerings.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Analysts project Landmark Cars Ltd's revenue to reach ₹4,870 crore in FY26, reflecting a 14% improvement compared to the last 12 months. Earnings per share (EPS) are expected to increase by 272% to ₹17.70. The company plans to expand its operations by opening new dealerships and service centers, focusing on enhancing customer experience and operational efficiency. With a commitment to growth and innovation, Landmark Cars Ltd is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the Indian automotive retail sector.

Name: Arisinfra Solutions Ltd
Overview:
ArisInfra Solutions Ltd is a tech-enabled B2B procurement platform founded in 2021, focused on construction and infrastructure materials. It digitizes the buying process for items like cement, steel, RMC, aggregates, and chemicals, serving 2,000+ clients and 1,400+ vendors across 963+ Indian pin codes. Through its platform, it offers seamless RFQ matching, logistics tracking, documentation, and even third-party manufacturing for select materials.

The company also provides value-added services through its subsidiary, ArisUnitern, including advisory, marketing, and financial support to developers. Its use of AI, ML, and data analytics helps automate and improve procurement efficiency for customers.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
The company is poised to scale further across geographies and materials, increase penetration into real estate and infrastructure segments, and enhance margins via increased third-party manufacturing and platform stickiness.

Name: Aeroflex Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
Sat Industries Ltd is an Indian conglomerate with diversified operations spanning textiles, real estate, and industrial manufacturing. Established in 1984, the company has evolved from its textile roots to include significant investments in sectors such as fintech, lending, and industrial manufacturing. 

This strategic diversification aims to mitigate risks and capitalize on emerging opportunities across various industries. In the textile segment, Sat Industries manufactures and exports a wide range of products, including cotton, synthetic, and blended fabrics, catering to both domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Sat Industries aims to achieve a revenue target of ₹1,000 crore within the next 4-5 years. The company plans to achieve this by focusing on its industrial manufacturing segment, with an expected contribution of 60-70% to its overall revenue.

Name: BMW Ventures Ltd
Overview:
BMW Ventures Limited is engaged in the trading and distribution of various industrial and infrastructure-related products. The company deals in steel products, tractor engines, and spare parts, supplying these materials to different industries and customers through its distribution network. Its operations are supported by sourcing, logistics, and supply chain management to ensure timely delivery and product availability.

Apart from trading activities, the company is also involved in manufacturing PVC pipes and roll forming products. It further operates in engineering and fabrication services, including the production of pre-engineered buildings (PEB) and steel girders used in infrastructure and industrial construction projects. Through these diverse operations, the company caters to multiple sectors such as agriculture, construction, and industrial manufacturing.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

BMW Ventures Limited plans to strengthen its presence in infrastructure and industrial sectors by expanding its steel distribution and fabrication capabilities. The company aims to increase its manufacturing capacity for PVC pipes and value-added steel products to capture rising demand from construction and agriculture industries.

Name: State Trading Corporation of India Ltd
Overview: The State Trading Corporation of India Ltd (STC) is a government-owned international trading company established in 1956. It operates under the administrative jurisdiction of the Ministry of Commerce & Industry. STC is responsible for managing import and export operations, particularly in bulk agro products such as rice, wheat, castor oils, sugar, and edible oils.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The State Trading Corporation of India Ltd has effectively ceased undertaking new trading operations and currently functions as a non-operative entity, maintaining its status without active commercial engagements. Despite a notable decline in profitability due to zero sales in recent periods, efforts have been made to restructure past liabilities and explore strategic exits, including the disinvestment of a minor government stake to generate funds and reduce financial burdens.

Name: Nitco Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1966, NITCO Ltd is a prominent Indian company specializing in the tiles and marble industry. With over six decades of expertise, NITCO has become a trusted name in providing high-quality flooring and wall solutions. The company operates through two primary segments: Tiles and Related Products, and Real Estate. NITCO offers a diverse range of products, including ceramic floor and wall tiles, glazed vitrified tiles, vitrified heavy-duty tiles, and mosaico tiles. Their product offerings cater to various spaces such as bathrooms, kitchens, living rooms, bedrooms, outdoor areas, and commercial establishments. 

Headquartered in Mumbai, NITCO Ltd has manufacturing facilities in Maharashtra and Silvassa. The company operates over 50 retail outlets across India, providing customers with an extensive selection of designer tiles and marble. NITCO's product lines include renowned brands like Le Studio Express, NITCO Look, and NITCO Galore.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

NITCO Ltd is focused on expanding its market presence and enhancing its product offerings to meet the evolving demands of the tiles and marble industry. The company aims to leverage its extensive retail network and manufacturing capabilities to reach a broader customer base. By continuously innovating and maintaining high-quality standards, NITCO seeks to strengthen its position in both domestic and international markets.

Name: Tinna Rubber & Infrastructure Ltd
Overview:
Tinna Rubber & Infrastructure Ltd, established in 1977, is one of Asia’s largest recyclers of end-of-life tyres (ELT). The company specializes in converting waste tyres into valuable products such as crumb rubber, steel wires, and other rubber-based materials. With a strong presence in India, Tinna Rubber operates multiple manufacturing facilities and serves various industries, including infrastructure, automotive, and construction. 

In recent years, Tinna Rubber has focused on expanding its product portfolio and geographical reach. The company has ventured into the production of crumb rubber modified bitumen (CRMB), polymer modified bitumen (PMB), bitumen emulsions, and reclaimed rubber.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Tinna Rubber has set a revenue guidance of ₹600 crore for FY26. The company plans to achieve this target through strategic initiatives, including the expansion of its product portfolio, enhancement of operational efficiencies, and exploration of new markets.

Name: Fabtech Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Fabtech Technologies Ltd is a biopharma engineering company that provides turnkey engineering solutions for the pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and healthcare industries. The company offers end-to-end services covering design, engineering, procurement, installation, and testing of pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities and equipment. Its expertise includes developing cleanroom infrastructure, controlled environments, and process utilities required for modern drug manufacturing plants.

The company focuses on delivering complete solutions for setting up advanced manufacturing facilities, including clean air systems, clean water systems, and process engineering requirements. Fabtech serves clients across several countries and works with pharmaceutical and life-science companies to build compliant and efficient production infrastructure.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its presence in the global biopharma engineering market by strengthening its turnkey project capabilities and increasing its international reach. It aims to focus on high-growth sectors such as pharmaceutical manufacturing, biotechnology facilities, and advanced healthcare infrastructure. The company is also working on adopting automation, digital technologies, and research-driven engineering solutions to improve efficiency and innovation.

Name: BCL Industries Ltd
Overview:
BCL Industries Ltd, established in 1975, is a diversified Indian company engaged in distillery operations, edible oil and vanaspati production, and real estate development. Headquartered in Bathinda, Punjab, BCL operates across multiple sectors, including manufacturing alcoholic beverages, producing edible oils and vanaspati, and developing residential real estate projects. 

The company's distillery segment includes the production of Indian Made Indian Liquor (IMIL), Extra Neutral Alcohol (ENA), and ethanol. BCL's real estate arm focuses on constructing residential houses, contributing to the company's diversified revenue streams. In recent years, BCL has strategically shifted its focus towards higher-margin businesses, particularly in the distillery sector, while exiting the low-margin edible oil business.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

BCL Industries Ltd is undergoing a significant transformation to become a leading player in India's green energy and agro-processing sectors. The company is expanding its distillery capacity from 700 KLPD to 1,100 KLPD, with projects underway in Bathinda and Haryana. Additionally, BCL is establishing a 75 KLPD biodiesel plant at its Kharagpur unit, supported by a ₹150 crore investment.

Name: Sundrop Brands Ltd
Overview:
Agro Tech Foods Ltd (ATFL), operating under the brand name Sundrop, is a leading Indian company engaged in the marketing and distribution of edible oils and food products. Affiliated with ConAgra Foods, Inc. of the USA, ATFL aims to be the best-performing and most respected foods company in India. The company's product portfolio includes a range of edible oils, snacks, and other food items, catering to a diverse consumer base. 

Headquartered in India, ATFL operates through a network of distribution channels across the country. The company focuses on delivering high-quality products that meet the nutritional needs of its consumers. With a strong commitment to innovation and customer satisfaction, ATFL continues to strengthen its presence in the Indian food industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Agro Tech Foods Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing its market presence. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving needs of consumers. With a strong financial performance in FY26, ATFL is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the Indian food industry.

Name: Gujarat Ambuja Exports Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat Ambuja Exports is a diversified agro-processing company engaged in the manufacturing of maize-based products like corn starch derivatives, soya derivatives, feed ingredients, cotton yarn, and edible oils. These product lines serve sectors ranging from food and pharmaceuticals to animal feed, supported by a long-term growth strategy in agro-processing since its incorporation in 1991.

In FY 25, the company faced headwinds with a notable decline in both top-line and bottom-line performance, reflecting margin pressures in challenging macro conditions. As FY 26 begins, early indicators point to a partial operational recovery, driven by rising sales volume in key segments.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With revenue showing signs of rebound and assuming sustained demand across its key product lines, Gujarat Ambuja Exports appears positioned for gradual margin recovery. Stabilizing input costs, improved operating leverage, and disciplined cost management could enable the company to regain profitability and reinforce growth across its agro-processing portfolio.

Name: Avanti Feeds Ltd
Overview:
Avanti Feeds Ltd. is a leading player in India’s aquaculture industry, with core operations in shrimp feed manufacturing, shrimp processing and export, hatchery operations, and a recently launched pet care business. The company supplies high-quality feed to shrimp farmers and exports processed shrimp to multiple international markets, backed by a strong distribution and procurement network.

Its feed division is the backbone of the business, supported by a processing arm catering to premium export markets, primarily the US. Avanti has also diversified into pet care, leveraging its expertise in animal nutrition to tap into India’s fast-growing pet food market. The company continues to focus on operational efficiency, raw material cost optimization, and market diversification to navigate industry challenges.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Avanti Feeds Ltd is strategically expanding its operations to strengthen its position in the global aquaculture industry. The company has increased its shrimp feed production capacity to 775,000 metric tonnes per annum and is enhancing its processing capabilities with a new 7,000 metric tonne facility in Krishnapuram, aimed at boosting export volumes. Avanti is also diversifying its product offerings by entering the pet food segment, with plans to generate ₹10 crore in revenue and establish a new manufacturing facility.

Name: Kaveri Seed Company Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1976, Kaveri Seed Company Ltd is a leading Indian agribusiness enterprise specializing in the research, production, processing, and marketing of high-quality hybrid seeds across various crops. The company offers a diverse portfolio of seeds, including cotton, maize, rice, and vegetables, catering to the needs of farmers across different agro-climatic zones. With a strong focus on innovation and quality, Kaveri Seed Company has established itself as a trusted name in the Indian seed industry.

Kaveri Seed Company operates through two primary segments: Seeds and Micronutrients. The Seeds segment is the largest contributor to the company's revenue, encompassing a wide range of hybrid seeds for various crops. The Micronutrients segment complements the seed offerings by providing essential nutrients to enhance soil fertility and crop yield. The company has a robust distribution network, ensuring its products reach farmers in both domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Kaveri Seed Company is poised for continued growth, driven by the increasing demand for high-quality hybrid seeds and micronutrient products. The company's focus on research and development, coupled with its expansion into new markets, positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities.

Name: Gokul Agro Resources Ltd
Overview:
Gokul Agro Resources Ltd is a prominent player in the edible oil and agro-processing industry in India. The company engages in the production and marketing of various edible oils, including sunflower, soybean, and groundnut oils.

With a strong distribution network and a focus on quality, Gokul Agro Resources has established a significant presence in the Indian market.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Gokul Agro Resources plans to expand its product offerings and enhance its processing capabilities. The company is focusing on increasing its market share in the edible oil segment and exploring new markets to drive growth.

Name: AWL Agri Business Ltd
Overview:
Adani Wilmar Ltd (AWL) is a leading player in the edible oil and FMCG sector in India, focusing on refining, manufacturing, and distribution of edible oils and a wide range of food products.AWL’s business spans edible oils, food & FMCG products, and industry essentials like oleochemicals and castor oil.

The company’s strategy emphasizes premiumization, alternate channel growth including Q-commerce, and brand consolidation through acquisitions such as GD Foods. Despite volume challenges due to commodity cycles and government-to-government rice business absence, AWL maintains steady revenue growth supported by price and product mix improvements and operational efficiency initiatives.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
AWL is optimistic about edible oil volume recovery supported by palm oil price correction and import duty structure changes. Food & FMCG segments target double-digit volume growth with margin expansion driven by SKU rationalization and scale. Continued investments in alternate channels, digital tools, and brand integration aim to sustain growth momentum.

Name: Manorama Industries Ltd
Overview:
Manorama Industries Ltd is a leader in specialty fats and exotic butters, manufacturing cocoa butter equivalents (CBE), shea butter, mango butter, and other unique ingredients tailored for chocolate, confectionery, cosmetic, and personal care sectors. It has built a strong global footprint with strategic subsidiaries in Africa, UAE, and Brazil.

The company recently expanded its production capacity through a new fractionation facility (bringing total to ~40,000 TPA), and maintains a strong export orientation—accounting for over 70% of its revenue. Its focus on innovation and sustainable sourcing underpins its premium positioning in global markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Manorama is set to consolidate its leadership through operational enhancement, margin expansion, and global footprint expansion. With a strong pipeline, export-led execution, and capacity scaling well underway, the company is well-positioned for continued growth in FY26 and beyond. ESG-aligned sourcing and sustainable production remain core strengths underpinning resilience and investor confidence.

Name: Godrej Agrovet Ltd
Overview:
Godrej Agrovet Limited is a diversified agri-business company focused on enhancing farmer productivity through innovative products and services. It operates across key segments including Animal Feed, Crop Protection, Oil Palm, Dairy, Poultry, and Processed Foods, holding leading market positions in each sector. The company also emphasizes sustainability, drawing significant energy from renewables and achieving water-positive status.

The business combines strong research and development capabilities with an expanding portfolio and strategic investments, aiming to drive volume growth and margin expansion. Its operations span India and select international markets, leveraging integrated value chains in oil palm refining, crop protection solutions, and animal nutrition to create competitive advantages.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Godrej Agrovet targets 16–18% consolidated topline and bottomline growth in FY26, led by volume growth in Crop Protection, Animal Feed, and Astec. Margin normalization in Crop Protection is expected but offset by strong volume expansion. Dairy and Poultry margins should improve with increased branded product sales and easing input costs.

Name: NIIT Learning Systems Ltd
Overview:
NIIT Learning Systems Limited is a global provider of Managed Training Services (MTS), catering to Fortune 1000 and Global 500 companies across multiple geographies, with a strong presence in North America and Europe. Its offerings cover curriculum design, learning delivery, administration, technology platforms, and consulting, making it a complete solutions partner in enterprise learning.

The company also provides specialized services such as immersive learning, digital and IT training, leadership development, and talent pipeline solutions. With AI-led innovation and strategic acquisitions, it is well positioned to capture the growing outsourcing opportunity in the global Learning & Development industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

NIIT Learning Systems is poised for significant growth, driven by strategic acquisitions and a focus on digital transformation. The company recently expanded its European presence by acquiring MST Group, enhancing its capabilities in managed learning services and strengthening its position in the automotive and industrial sectors. Leveraging early adoption of artificial intelligence and proprietary technology, NIIT Learning Systems aims to deliver scalable, tech-enabled reskilling solutions, positioning itself to capture a larger share of the corporate learning market.

Name: Crizac Ltd
Overview:
Crizac Ltd, established in 2011, is a prominent B2B education platform headquartered in India. Specializing in international student recruitment, Crizac connects prospective students from over 75 countries with educational opportunities in the United Kingdom, Canada, Ireland, Australia, and New Zealand. The company operates through a network of registered agents, facilitating seamless admission processes for students aspiring to pursue higher education abroad. Leveraging its proprietary technology platform, Crizac streamlines the application process, ensuring efficiency and transparency for both students and educational institutions.

Over the years, Crizac has forged partnerships with numerous global institutions, enhancing its credibility and reach in the international education sector. The company's commitment to providing comprehensive support services, including visa assistance, test preparation, and career counseling, has positioned it as a trusted intermediary in the education ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Crizac Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to strengthen its presence in key markets such as Ireland, Canada, and New Zealand, capitalizing on the increasing demand for international education in these regions. Additionally, Crizac aims to diversify its service offerings by launching B2C services, including student loans, forex assistance, and accommodation solutions, thereby providing a comprehensive suite of services to students.

Name: NIIT Ltd
Overview:
NIIT Ltd is a leading skills and talent development company established in 1981, catering to individual and enterprise learners across India and globally. The company provides a broad portfolio of skilling programs through NIIT Digital, StackRoute, RPS Consulting, IFBI, TPaaS, and Sales & Service Excellence (SSE). It focuses on domains such as technology, BFSI, and emerging areas like AI and cybersecurity.

The company works with over 200 corporate partners, with 90% of revenue from repeat clients, and has trained thousands of professionals in technology and BFSI. Post the demerger of its Corporate Learning Group into NIIT Learning Systems, the company has sharpened focus on its Skills & Careers Group, targeting digital skilling, BFSI solutions, and new-age programs to capture opportunities from India’s digital transformation.
Key Takeways:
Outlook & Guidance
Management remains confident in the medium- to long-term opportunity, driven by AI-led skilling, BFSI expansion, and platform scalability. While, the strong order intake, new client wins, and synergy from acquisitions are expected to drive sequential recovery

Name: Veranda Learning Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Veranda Learning Solutions Limited (Veranda) is an integrated education technology company offering diverse learning solutions across online, offline hybrid, and blended formats. Its programs cater to a wide learner base, including those preparing for competitive exams such as SPSC, banking, insurance, railways, IAS, and CA, as well as individuals seeking professional upskilling and re-skilling in emerging technologies. The company has built a strong presence in exam-oriented segments, particularly in banking, SPSC, insurance, RRB, and SSC, supported by its structured pedagogy and wide delivery reach.

The company has strengthened its portfolio through the acquisition of Edureka, a leader in instructor-led IT training, enabling Veranda to offer advanced IT-focused programs. In 2022, it also launched Veranda Acacia, a dedicated platform to bridge India’s IT skills gap, with a network of 25 delivery centers across the country. Further, Veranda has partnered with NASSCOM FutureSkills to provide certified courses on emerging technologies such as AI, machine learning, and cloud computing, ensuring learners are equipped with industry-relevant digital skills.

Looking ahead, Veranda aims to expand its reach by broadening its customer base, introducing AI- and VR-powered learning solutions, and building strategic partnerships with institutions and corporates. With a focus on innovation, talent development, and immersive learning experiences, the company is positioning itself as a comprehensive provider of advanced and future-ready education solutions in India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Veranda is actively expanding its offerings across various education segments, including K-12, government test preparation, vocational training, and commerce education. The company has made strategic acquisitions, such as a 76% stake in JK Shah Classes.
Additionally, Veranda is demerging JK Shah Commerce Education Ltd into a separate, debt-free listed entity to enhance strategic focus

Name: Aptech Ltd
Overview:
Aptech Ltd, founded in 1986, is a pioneer in the non-formal education and training sector, with a footprint of over 800 centers globally. The company operates through two primary business streams — Global Retail and Institutional Business — and offers learning solutions across IT, media & entertainment, aviation, beauty, banking, retail, and preschool education. Its multi-brand portfolio includes Arena Animation, MAAC, Aptech Learning, Aptech Aviation, Lakmé Academy, and Aptech International Preschool.

The retail business contributes the bulk of revenues, operating under a business-partner-led center model, while the institutional segment delivers large-scale assessment, testing, and corporate training services. With a growing international presence across five continents and leadership in domestic retail education, Aptech is steadily evolving into a global skilling powerhouse with increasing focus on high-demand sectors like gaming, VFX, beauty, and virtual production.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management reaffirmed its focus on scaling retail enrollments, deepening international reach, and reviving the institutional vertical. The company aims to sustain mid-teen operating margins in Retail while institutional profitability recovers gradually. With diversified revenue streams, global expansion, and strong demand in creative and vocational skills, Aptech is positioned for steady growth, though near-term margins may remain under pressure due to competitive intensity in beauty and pricing headwinds in certain geographies.

Name: Physicswallah Ltd
Overview:
PhysicsWallah is an Indian edtech company that offers affordable and high-quality learning solutions for students preparing for competitive exams like JEE, NEET, and UPSC. The platform also provides upskilling courses in areas such as data science, software development, banking, and finance. By combining a strong online presence with offline and hybrid learning models, it addresses both digital and in-person learning needs.

Founded by educators, PhysicsWallah has built a large student community through its YouTube-originated content and now also runs physical learning centers in many cities. Its value-driven approach—offering courses at relatively low prices—helps it reach a broader base of learners, including those from smaller towns and non-metro regions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

PhysicsWallah is aggressively expanding its offline presence by opening hundreds of new centres across tier-2 and tier-3 cities while deepening its online reach through content in multiple regional languages. It is strengthening its hybrid model by integrating tech-enabled classrooms with its purely digital offerings. The company has launched a low-cost subscription OTT learning platform to make education more accessible.

Name: Jaro Institute of Technol. Mgt. and Research Ltd
Overview:
Founded in 2009, Jaro Institute of Technology Management and Research Ltd, operating under the brand name Jaro Education, is a leading online higher education and upskilling platform in India. The company offers a diverse range of degree and certification programs, including DBA, MBA, M.Com, M.A., PGDM, MCA, M.Sc., B.Com, and BCA, in collaboration with over 36 partner institutions, such as 7 IIMs and 7 IITs. 

With a strong focus on technology-driven education, Jaro Education has empowered over 350,000 professionals worldwide. The company has received numerous accolades, including being named the Leading Edtech Company by the Times Business Award 2024 and India’s Most Trusted Online Higher Education Provider by Outlook Business Icon 2023.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jaro Education plans to expand its program offerings and strengthen its partnerships with top-tier universities and institutions. The company aims to enhance its technological infrastructure to provide an enriched learning experience for its students. Additionally, Jaro Education is focusing on increasing its market presence in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, catering to the growing demand for online education in these regions.

Name: Shipping Corporation of India Land & Assets Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in November 2021, Shipping Corporation of India Land & Assets Ltd (SCILAL) was established as a wholly owned subsidiary of The Shipping Corporation of India Limited (SCI). The primary objective of SCILAL is to hold, manage, and dispose of SCI's non-core assets, thereby facilitating the strategic disinvestment process of SCI by segregating its real estate and other non-essential properties. 

This separation allows SCI to focus on its core maritime operations while enabling SCILAL to unlock the value of these non-core assets independently. SCILAL's portfolio encompasses significant properties such as the Shipping House in Mumbai and the Maritime Training Institute (MTI) in Powai, along with other real estate holdings.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

SCILAL is poised for growth through strategic asset monetization and development initiatives.
