John Cockerill India Ltd, established in 1986, specializes in the design, manufacture, and commissioning of advanced industrial systems, including cold rolling mills, processing lines, chemical equipment, and industrial furnaces. As a subsidiary of the Belgium-based John Cockerill Group, the company serves both ferrous and non-ferrous industries globally. Headquartered in Mumbai, Maharashtra, John Cockerill India operates two manufacturing facilities located in Taloja and Hedavali, Maharashtra.

The company offers a comprehensive range of products and services, encompassing the design and installation of cold rolling mill complexes, galvanizing lines, color coating lines, tension leveling lines, skin pass mills, acid regeneration plants, wet flux lines, and pickling lines. These offerings cater to a diverse clientele across various regions, including Asia, Africa, the Middle East, Europe, North America, and South America.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
John Cockerill India aims to leverage its expertise in industrial systems to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the global market. The company's focus on innovation and quality is expected to drive growth in the coming years.

Name: Giriraj Civil Developers Ltd
Overview:
Giriraj Civil Developers Ltd, established in 2005, is a prominent player in the Indian civil construction sector. The company specializes in executing infrastructure projects for government, semi-government, and private organizations. 

With a strong track record, Giriraj Civil Developers has undertaken various contract works, contributing significantly to the development of the nation's infrastructure.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Giriraj Civil Developers aims to continue its growth trajectory by leveraging its established expertise in executing large-scale infrastructure projects. The company is poised to capitalize on increasing infrastructure development initiatives across India, positioning itself for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Singer India Ltd
Overview:
Singer India Ltd is a household appliances and sewing machines company, with a legacy brand that enjoys strong consumer recognition in India. The company offers a wide range of sewing machines, domestic appliances, and kitchen products through retail stores, distributors, and online platforms.

Singer continues to modernize its product portfolio, introducing technologically advanced and design-oriented products to cater to changing consumer preferences. Its strong brand equity and distribution reach remain its key strengths.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Despite the challenges faced in the first quarter, Singer India remains optimistic about its future prospects. The company is focusing on expanding its appliances segment and enhancing its product offerings to drive revenue growth.

Name: Taneja Aerospace & Aviation Ltd
Overview:
Taneja Aerospace & Aviation Ltd (TAAL) is an Indian aerospace and aviation company specializing in the manufacture and sale of non-military aircraft, including light transport and trainer aircraft. 

The company has diversified its operations into various segments of the aviation industry through its three divisions: Aerostructures, Airfield & Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul (MRO), and Aviation & Charter Business. Headquartered in Bangalore, TAAL serves both domestic and international markets, offering a range of services from aircraft manufacturing to airfield operations and maintenance.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Taneja Aerospace & Aviation Ltd (TAAL) is actively pursuing expansion through the establishment of a new aircraft manufacturing facility in Andhra Pradesh, focusing on producing 12-seater private jets and launching an aviation software division under its TallTech brand. This strategic move aims to position TAAL as a significant player in India's aviation sector, aligning with the government's initiative to develop the state as a hub for aerospace innovation.

Name: Disa India Ltd
Overview:
Disa India Ltd, founded in 1984, is a leading manufacturer of foundry equipment and machinery. Headquartered in Bangalore, the company specializes in supplying complete foundry systems, including molding machines, sand mixers, and surface preparation machines. Its products cater to a wide spectrum of industries such as automotive, construction, and energy, providing innovative solutions for improved manufacturing processes.

The company is known for its emphasis on quality, technological advancement, and customer-focused solutions. Disa India Ltd has a strong domestic presence and is expanding internationally, offering turnkey solutions and value-added services. Its commitment to innovation and reliable execution has earned it a strong reputation in the industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company plans to expand its product range and enhance technological capabilities to meet evolving industry demands. Automation and digitalization are key priorities for improved productivity. International market expansion through strategic partnerships remains a focus for sustained growth.

Name: Hercules Hoists Ltd
Overview:
Hercules Hoists Ltd is a long-established Indian manufacturer specializing in lifting and handling equipment, including hoists, cranes, and related machinery. With decades of experience since its incorporation in 1962, the company has built a reputation for engineering reliability and quality within industrial sectors across India.

Headquartered in Bangalore, Hercules Hoists caters to a broad client base from manufacturing plants and construction firms to infrastructure projects offering tailored lifting solutions that meet rigorous industry standards and operational demands.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Hercules Hoists is positioned for steady growth, supported by its strong engineering capabilities, established product portfolio, and dependable order sources. Management's outlook remains optimistic that improving demand and cost controls will translate into continued margin improvement and profitability through the rest of FY26.

Name: DE Nora India Ltd
Overview:
DE Nora India Ltd, established in 1989 (initially as Titanor Components), is a key Indian subsidiary of the Italian multinational Industrie De Nora. Headquartered in Goa, the company supplies advanced electrode technologies, including anodes, cathodes, and electrochlorination systems, serving the chlor-alkali, water treatment, and surface finishing industries. Leveraging global R&D from its parent company, DE Nora India has become a centre of excellence in cathodic protection and brine chlorination.
With a reputation built on innovation, the company offers energy-saving and environmental protection technologies, aligning with the global transition to cleaner industrial processes. It operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and offers services including electrode recoating and system repair.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

De Nora India Ltd is strategically positioning itself to capitalize on the expanding green hydrogen and sustainable energy sectors. The company is enhancing its capabilities in water electrolysis, a key technology for producing green hydrogen with zero CO₂ emissions, by advancing the development of efficient and low-power consumption electrodes.

Name: Forbes & Company Ltd
Overview: Forbes & Company Ltd, established in 1767, is one of India's oldest companies, involved in engineering, shipping, and logistics. The company operates through various subsidiaries, including Forbes Engineering, Forbes Real Estate, and Forbes Technosys. Forbes & Company Ltd offers products and services in robotics, industrial automation, precision cutting tools, springs, ATM and banking kiosks, and real estate development.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Forbes & Company Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to bolster its growth trajectory. The company is in the process of acquiring full ownership of Forbes Macsa Private Limited by purchasing shares from Macsa ID, ending their joint venture. This move is expected to enhance Forbes & Company's capabilities in the business automation sector, particularly in transaction management services and networks.

Name: Kilburn Engineering Ltd
Overview:
Kilburn Engineering Ltd is a specialist engineering firm that designs, manufactures, and installs customized equipment and systems for industries with critical performance requirements, including chemicals, steel, nuclear power, petrochemicals, soda ash, and food processing. 

With a strong engineering foundation and diversified sectoral focus, the company provides integrated and precision-engineered solutions across both domestic and international markets. Kilburn’s approach emphasizes high technical quality and sector-focused customization, making it a reliable partner for process-critical applications across heavy industries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Kilburn Engineering Ltd is poised for significant growth, driven by strategic expansions and a robust order book. The company is enhancing its manufacturing capabilities through a brownfield expansion at its Saravali facility, aiming to increase production capacity by 25%. Additionally, Kilburn has secured new contracts totaling ₹30.81 crore, bringing its order backlog to ₹387.63 crore, indicating strong demand across sectors such as chemicals, steel, nuclear power, petrochemicals, and food processing

Name: Mazda Ltd
Overview:
Mazda Ltd operates in the engineering sector, manufacturing vacuum systems, evaporators, crystallizers, and pollution control equipment. The company also has a food products division, producing condiments and culinary products under established brand names. This dual business model gives Mazda a unique balance between industrial engineering and consumer food products.

With a strong client base in industries such as chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and food processing, Mazda has carved a niche in custom-engineered solutions. Its focus on precision, innovation, and sustainability in engineering, along with brand visibility in foods, enhances its diversified profile.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Mazda is expected to focus on expanding its engineering exports while scaling up its food brand distribution. This dual strategy provides resilience and potential for long-term growth across multiple industries.

Name: International Conveyors Ltd
Overview:
International Conveyors Ltd manufactures and supplies PVC conveyor belts that are widely used in underground mining and material handling applications. The company is one of India’s leading exporters of specialized conveyor belts, with significant presence in the US, Australia, and other international markets.

Its long-standing relationships with global mining companies and focus on quality standards give it a competitive edge. The company also invests in technology upgrades to improve efficiency and expand its product portfolio.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook 
With increasing mechanization in mining and industrial sectors, demand for conveyor systems is expected to rise. The company is also exploring opportunities in non-mining sectors to diversify its revenue base.

Name: Deepak Builders & Engineers India Ltd
Overview:
Deepak Builders & Engineers India Ltd is an integrated engineering and construction company specializing in the execution and construction of administrative and institutional buildings, hospitals and medical colleges, industrial buildings, and historical monuments. The company focuses on assisting and scaling large-scale projects in both the government and private sectors, contributing significantly to infrastructure development.

Established in 2017, Deepak Builders has rapidly expanded its footprint in the construction industry. The company's commitment to quality, timely delivery, and adherence to safety standards has earned it a reputation as a reliable partner for complex construction projects.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Deepak Builders & Engineers India Ltd is poised for continued growth. The steady increase in revenue and net profit indicates effective project execution and cost management. If this positive trend continues, the company is well-positioned to achieve sustainable growth and enhance shareholder value in the coming quarters.

Name: Suratwwala Business Group Ltd
Overview:
Suratwwala Business Group Ltd is a prominent real estate development company based in Pune, India. Established in 2008, the company has rapidly expanded its footprint in the real estate sector, focusing on residential, commercial, and IT park developments. Suratwwala is renowned for delivering high-quality projects that cater to the evolving needs of urban infrastructure.

The company's portfolio includes a diverse range of projects, such as Suratwala’s Sweet 16, Suratwala Mark Plazzo A & B, Gajanan Heritage, Car Mall, and Brahma Chaitanya. Suratwwala's commitment to excellence is evident in its approach to construction, design, and customer satisfaction. The company also emphasizes sustainability and innovation in its projects, aiming to create spaces that are both functional and environmentally friendly.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Suratwwala Business Group Ltd plans to expand its project portfolio by venturing into new geographic markets and exploring mixed-use developments. The company is also focusing on adopting sustainable construction practices and integrating smart technologies into its projects. These initiatives are expected to drive long-term growth and enhance shareholder value.

Name: Artson Ltd
Overview:
Artson Engineering Ltd is a subsidiary of Tata Projects Ltd, engaged in providing engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) services. The company specializes in industrial projects such as oil & gas infrastructure, tanks, terminals, and structural works.

Its expertise lies in executing turnkey solutions for process industries, with a strong reputation for quality and safety standards. The company continues to leverage Tata Group synergies to secure projects and build long-term client relationships.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
With India’s industrial capex cycle picking up, Artson is strategically positioned to capitalize on opportunities in oil, gas, and allied industries. The 77% order book expansion and robust execution capability underpin strong growth prospects. Management’s ability to convert its healthy pipeline into high-margin contracts will be critical for sustaining profitability momentum.

Name: Affle 3i Ltd
Overview:
Affle (India) Limited is a global technology company specializing in mobile advertising and consumer intelligence solutions, enabling marketers to acquire, engage, and convert consumers across connected devices. Incorporated in 1994, its proprietary Consumer Intelligence Platform transforms ads into personalized recommendations, leveraging AI, fraud detection systems, and contextual targeting to enhance ROI for advertisers. The platform serves both new user acquisition and re-engagement needs, along with online-to-offline conversions, and operates across 130+ countries with a reach of over 3.4 billion devices.

The company’s primary revenue model is cost-per-converted-user (CPCU), contributing 99% of its earnings in H1 FY25, with the remainder from non-CPCU revenues. Affle owns multiple demand-side platforms, including Appnext, Jampp, Mediasmart, RevX, Newton, and MDSP, integrating these into a unified AI-driven tech stack. The company focuses on high-quality revenue, direct advertiser relationships (~75% of revenue), and selective inorganic growth to complement its long-term “Affle 3i Vision” of achieving 10x scale over a decade.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management reaffirmed its FY26 guidance of 20%+ organic revenue growth with ~23% EBITDA margin, underpinned by AI-led innovation, expansion in high-growth verticals, and deeper penetration in both developed and emerging markets. The medium-term target remains 10x growth over a decade through sustained organic growth and selective M&A, focusing on companies with 20%+ EBITDA potential and scalable toplines.

Name: Media Matrix Worldwide Ltd
Overview:
Media Matrix Worldwide Ltd (MMWL) is a B2B technology and services company specializing in Value Added Services (VAS) for mobile and digital ecosystems. It provides mobile content delivery, digital marketing solutions, and IT services to telecom operators and corporate clients.

Incorporated in 1985, MMWL also distributes products across mobility, audio, consumer electronics, and IT segments through subsidiaries. Its focus on innovation, customer-centric solutions, and building strong industry partnerships positions it as a key player in the Indian digital services space.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
MMWL plans to expand digital and mobile service offerings, integrating emerging technologies to enhance solutions. Market expansion, strategic partnerships, and customer-centric innovations will drive long-term growth. Focus on operational efficiency and diversification supports scalability.

Name: Dish TV India Ltd
Overview:
Dish TV India Ltd is a leading direct-to-home (DTH) service provider in India, offering satellite television services to millions of subscribers. The company provides a wide range of channels and services, catering to diverse viewer preferences. Dish TV operates under the Essel Group and is headquartered in Noida, Uttar Pradesh.

In addition to traditional DTH services, Dish TV has ventured into digital platforms, aiming to adapt to changing consumer viewing habits. The company focuses on delivering high-quality content and enhancing customer experience through technological innovations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Dish TV aims to strengthen its position in the competitive DTH market by focusing on content diversification and enhancing customer experience. The company plans to leverage its digital platforms to offer a broader range of services and attract a wider audience. Strategic initiatives and technological advancements are expected to drive growth and profitability in the coming years.

Name: Balaji Telefilms Ltd
Overview:
Balaji Telefilms Ltd is one of India’s leading entertainment content houses, engaged in producing television serials, digital content, and films. The company has built strong brand equity in television, supplying content to top broadcasters, while also expanding into digital through its OTT platform, ALTBalaji.

With a wide content library and strong relationships across TV channels and streaming platforms, Balaji has diversified revenue streams. However, profitability in the OTT segment remains challenged due to high content costs and rising competition, making efficient monetization critical for sustaining growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Balaji’s growth prospects lie in scaling its digital streaming footprint while preserving its leadership in TV serial production. Effective cost control, better monetization of ALTBalaji, and strategic alliances will be key drivers for sustained performance.

Name: Basilic Fly Studio Ltd
Overview: Basilic Fly Studio Ltd is a visual effects (VFX) studio headquartered in Chennai, India, with subsidiaries operating in Canada and the UK. The company specializes in providing high-quality VFX, CGI, and animation services for films, television, and other media. Basilic Fly Studio has a team of over 500 skilled professionals and has worked on various international projects.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Basilic Fly Studio Ltd is strengthening its global VFX footprint through strategic investments in top-tier creative studios and advanced production technologies. By acquiring a majority stake in a renowned London-Paris studio, the company is enhancing its creative reach and technical capabilities while boosting its international presence. This expansion is supported by one of the firm’s strongest-ever sales pipelines, featuring major projects from global streaming giants, signaling robust demand for its services.

Name: T.V. Today Network Ltd
Overview:
T.V. Today Network Limited (TVTN), part of the India Today Group, operates as a leading television broadcaster and digital media company. Its portfolio includes prominent Hindi and English news channels such as Aaj Tak, Aaj Tak HD, Good News Today, and India Today Television. The company has built a strong presence across broadcasting, digital platforms, and international markets, offering news and current affairs content in multiple languages.

The company has also diversified into digital-first video content under the Tak brand, spanning more than 30 channels across 10 content genres and 5 languages. Alongside, platforms like The Lallantop have emerged as leading digital-native news sources. With operations extending to markets such as the USA, UK, UAE, Qatar, Canada, South Africa, Maldives, and Seychelles, TVTN continues to expand its footprint both domestically and internationally.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
The company outlined a future-ready strategy with digital at its core. With rising digital ad spend in India and sustained consumption of news content, TVTN expects accelerated growth in online revenue streams. The management highlighted its strong brand equity, diversified platforms, and robust audience reach as competitive advantages. Going forward, TVTN will focus on strengthening its digital portfolio, expanding global reach, and improving operating efficiency, while divesting non-core assets like radio.

Name: Delta Corp Ltd
Overview:
Delta Corp Ltd is India’s only listed player in the casino gaming and integrated hospitality space, operating under its Deltin brand across Goa, Sikkim, and Daman. The company also manages offshore and land-based casinos, resorts, and digital gaming businesses.

Known for its stronghold in gaming and hospitality, Delta Corp has expanded into online gaming platforms and strategically leveraged asset monetization to streamline operations and unlock shareholder value.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Moving forward, Delta Corp is focusing on strengthening gaming and hospitality operations, exploring organic and inorganic growth levers. The company is also navigating regulatory and demand headwinds in offline gaming. Sustainable growth hinges on operational revamp, digital gaming scale-up, and leverage from monetized assets to fund expansion and profitability in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Zee Media Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Zee Media Corporation Ltd is one of India’s prominent news broadcasting companies, operating multiple national and regional news channels. Its portfolio includes popular brands such as Zee News, Zee Business, and regional language news channels.

The company caters to diverse audiences across Hindi, English, and regional segments, monetizing content through advertising revenues and digital platforms. With the rise of online consumption, Zee Media has invested heavily in digital-first news formats.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
The company is expected to benefit from rising digital advertising spends and regional viewership expansion. However, competitive intensity in news broadcasting may continue to pressure margins.

Name: Entertainment Network (India) Ltd
Overview:
Entertainment Network India Ltd (ENIL) is a leading Indian media and entertainment company and a subsidiary of the Times Group. It primarily operates under the brand Radio Mirchi, one of India’s top FM radio networks, offering music, talk shows, and entertainment content in multiple languages. With a strong presence across major cities, ENIL has built a significant and loyal listener base, driven by innovative programming and engaging on-air personalities.

Beyond radio broadcasting, ENIL has diversified into events and activations, organizing concerts, music festivals, and other live entertainment experiences. The company also provides integrated marketing solutions for advertisers, leveraging its radio and digital platforms to reach a broad audience. Recognized for its quality content and industry contributions, ENIL has received numerous awards, solidifying its position as a key player in the Indian media and entertainment landscape.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Entertainment Network (India) Ltd (ENIL), the operator of Radio Mirchi, is strategically diversifying its revenue streams to capitalize on the evolving media landscape. In the first quarter of FY26, the company reported a 3% year-on-year increase in consolidated revenue, reaching ₹117 crore, driven by strong performances in its events, solutions, and digital segments.

Name: Wonderla Holidays Ltd
Overview:
Wonderla Holidays Ltd operates a chain of amusement parks across India, currently with four parks situated in Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Kochi, and Bhubaneswar, complemented by a resort adjacent to its Bengaluru location. The company delivers leisure and entertainment experiences for families and tourists, positioning itself as one of the country’s premier theme park operators.

Its business model is built on integrating engaging attractions with hospitality services to extend visitor experience, while leveraging location-based brand recognition for sustained footfall and revenue generation across its verticals.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Wonderla appears strongly positioned for growth, backed by rising footfalls, expanding park capacity, and improving operational efficiency. Higher costs remain a concern, but increased spend per visitor and strong execution could support margin recovery and steady performance going forward.

Name: GTPL Hathway Ltd
Overview:
GTPL Hathway Ltd is a leading Indian cable television and broadband service provider. The company delivers high-speed internet, digital cable TV, and value-added services to residential and commercial customers across multiple states.

Headquartered in Ahmedabad, GTPL Hathway focuses on technology-driven solutions, customer experience, and network expansion. Its scalable infrastructure, wide coverage, and diversified service portfolio make it a prominent player in India’s digital services and cable industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
GTPL Hathway plans to expand its broadband and digital TV services, strengthen network infrastructure, and increase penetration in underserved markets. Focus on new technology adoption, digital content offerings, and strategic alliances will enhance growth prospects.

Name: Hathway Cable & Datacom Ltd
Overview:
Hathway Cable & Datacom Ltd is a leading provider of broadband internet and cable television services in India. The company operates an extensive network infrastructure, delivering high-speed internet and digital TV services to a wide customer base across various regions.

With a commitment to quality and customer satisfaction, Hathway continues to expand its service offerings and enhance its network capabilities to meet the growing demand for digital connectivity in the country.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Hathway plans to expand its FTTH broadband footprint, introduce new value-added services, and optimize operational efficiency to improve margins. The company aims to strengthen its market position and capitalize on the growing demand for digital connectivity across India.

Name: OnMobile Global Ltd
Overview:
OnMobile Global Ltd, founded in 2000 and headquartered in Bengaluru, is a global player in mobile entertainment and digital content services. The company operates across mobile gaming, music, video, and digital engagement platforms, serving telecom operators, enterprises, and direct-to-consumer markets in over 50 countries. It leverages cloud, AI, and personalization technologies to deliver engaging consumer experiences, while generating recurring revenue streams through subscriptions and partnerships.

In recent years, OnMobile has shifted its focus towards mobile gaming, e-sports, and gamified digital communities to align with evolving consumer behavior. The company’s in-house platforms, such as ONMO (cloud gaming) and Challenges Arena (e-sports competitions), reflect its pivot toward high-growth digital entertainment categories.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

OnMobile Global Ltd is strategically enhancing its position in the mobile entertainment sector by expanding its gaming platform offerings. The company aims to increase its gaming subscription revenue to $2 million per month, leveraging its existing base of over 12 million active gamers and strong telecom partnerships. Additionally, OnMobile has introduced Buzzmo, a next-generation enterprise engagement platform, to diversify its service offerings and cater to a broader market

Name: Den Networks Ltd
Overview:
Den Networks Ltd is a leading mass media and entertainment company in India, providing visual entertainment through cable TV, over-the-top (OTT) platforms, and broadband services. Established in 2007, the company has a significant presence in the Indian market, offering a wide range of entertainment options to its customers. 

The company has curated media content from various broadcasters across a wide range of genres, ensuring a diverse and comprehensive viewing experience for its customers. Den Networks continues to innovate and expand its services to meet the evolving entertainment needs of its audience.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Den Networks aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its service offerings and enhancing customer experience. The company's focus on innovation and strategic partnerships positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the entertainment industry. With a strong subscriber base and a diverse range of services,

Name: Imagicaaworld Entertainment Ltd
Overview:
Imagicaaworld Entertainment Ltd is India's premier global-scale theme leisure and entertainment destination, located in Khopoli, Maharashtra. Spanning over 110 acres, its flagship Imagicaa Park offers an all-weather family entertainment experience, featuring a theme park, water park, snow park, and the Novotel Imagicaa Khopoli hotel. The park is renowned for its immersive attractions, including thrilling rides, live shows, and themed dining options, catering to visitors of all ages.

The company has also ventured into real estate development, planning to utilize its surplus land for township projects, thereby diversifying its revenue streams and enhancing its long-term growth prospects.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Imagicaaworld Entertainment Ltd is poised for continued growth, driven by its diversified offerings and strategic initiatives. The company's expansion into real estate development and its focus on enhancing visitor experiences position it well to capitalize on the growing demand for leisure and entertainment in India.

Name: Brightcom Group Ltd
Overview:
Brightcom Group Ltd, formerly known as Lycos Internet Ltd, provides digital marketing and technology solutions to businesses, agencies, and online publishers around the world. The company delivers services such as programmatic advertising, media monetization, and digital ad-tech platforms, helping clients optimize their digital presence and campaigns.

Its operations have grown to span multiple geographies, supported by technological capabilities in artificial intelligence, analytics, and software services. Brightcom is positioning itself as a tech-driven media solutions provider, aiming to expand from traditional digital marketing into adjacent technology domains.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Brightcom announced the launch of a new Brightcom Defence division, focusing on AI-powered aerospace and autonomous defence technologies. Alongside, the group is being restructured into four divisions—Advertising, Software Services, Defence, and Next-Gen Technologies—to sharpen strategic focus and scalability across digital and emerging tech domains.

Name: New Delhi Television Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1988, NDTV Ltd is a leading Indian news broadcaster and digital media company, part of the Adani Group through AMG Media Networks Ltd. It operates multiple TV channels, including NDTV 24x7 (English), NDTV India (Hindi), NDTV Profit (Business), and regional channels in MP-CG, Rajasthan, and Marathi, supported by strong digital platforms and global availability.

The company has built a significant presence across 65+ countries, reaching over 500 million viewers globally. Alongside television broadcasting, NDTV runs digital properties such as ndtv.com, ndtv.in, ndtvprofit.com, and regional portals, supported by its subsidiaries NDTV Convergence Ltd and NDTV Worldwide Ltd.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management emphasized leveraging NDTV’s digital dominance, growing regional presence, and international reach to expand both audience and advertiser engagement. With the backing of the Adani Group, NDTV aims to invest in technology, analytics, and premium content to capture market share in an increasingly digital-driven news ecosystem.

Name: Concord Enviro Systems Ltd
Overview:
Concord Enviro Systems Ltd is a leading water and wastewater management company, offering turnkey solutions for industrial and municipal clients. Its expertise includes effluent treatment, recycling, zero liquid discharge (ZLD), and sewage treatment.

By focusing on technology-driven, sustainable solutions, Concord has established a strong presence in water-intensive industries such as textiles, pharmaceuticals, and chemicals. Its projects support both environmental compliance and circular economy goals.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Concord Enviro Systems Ltd is poised for significant expansion in the water and wastewater treatment sector. The company is actively pursuing greenfield and brownfield projects, including a new manufacturing facility in Vasai and international ventures in the UAE, aimed at enhancing its production capabilities and market reach. Additionally, Concord Enviro is exploring opportunities in emerging technologies such as green hydrogen, carbon capture, and desalination, aligning with global sustainability trends.

Name: Praj Industries Ltd
Overview:
Praj Industries Ltd. is a leading global biotechnology and engineering company focused on sustainable solutions in bioenergy, water purification, process equipment, breweries, and wastewater treatment. The company operates with a diversified product portfolio spanning BioEnergy, Critical Process Equipment, Brewery & Beverages, and Pharma & Healthcare Systems, serving domestic and international markets. It emphasizes environment-friendly technologies and farm-to-fuel innovations.

Despite near-term challenges including execution delays and tariff uncertainties, Praj continues to build new capacities like the GenX facility and expand in promising segments such as Compressed Biogas (CBG) and international markets. Its strategic focus on innovation, service offerings, and diversification aims to drive sustained growth and margin improvement.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management targets sustainable double-digit EBITDA margins, driven by services scaling, internationalization, and commercialization of co-product technologies. Despite near-term execution and liquidity challenges, Praj remains confident in multi-pronged growth strategies across bioenergy, engineering, and new verticals like bioplastics and SAF, positioning the company for long-term resilience and value creation.

Name: Denta Water & Infra Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Established in 2016, Denta Water & Infra Solutions Ltd operates primarily in the water infrastructure domain, focusing on water management, irrigation systems, and operations & maintenance of water-related projects. The company undertakes projects including water treatment plants, irrigation schemes, pipeline distribution networks, and dam or bridge-cum-barrage structures.

With operational capabilities rooted in infrastructure execution and water sector solutions, the company supports both rural and urban water needs through engineering-led project delivery and maintenance services.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Buoyed by a sizable order book and renewed execution momentum, the company is well-positioned for growth in FY 26. If Q1 trends sustain, both revenue and profit could see double-digit percentage gains for the full year, reinforcing its comeback trajectory.

Name: TruAlt Bioenergy Ltd
Overview:
TruAlt Bioenergy Ltd, established in 2021 and headquartered in Bengaluru, is one of India's largest biofuels producers, specializing in the production of ethanol and other renewable energy solutions. The company operates as a prominent and diversified player in the Indian biofuels industry, focusing on crafting sustainable energy solutions. 

TruAlt Bioenergy is strategically positioned as a leading producer of biofuels in India, primarily in the production of ethanol. The company holds the distinction of being the largest ethanol producer in India based on installed capacity, with the largest market share in terms of ethanol production capacity
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

TruAlt Bioenergy is focusing on expanding its production capacity and diversifying its product offerings to meet the growing demand for renewable energy solutions. The company plans to venture into the production of second-generation (2G) ethanol, sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), and green hydrogen, aligning with the government's push towards cleaner energy sources.

Name: EMS Ltd
Overview:
EMS Limited, established in 2006 and headquartered in Delhi, is a multi-disciplinary EPC (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction) company specializing in turnkey solutions for water and wastewater management. 

The company offers comprehensive services, including engineering design, construction, and installation of water supply systems, sewage treatment plants (STPs), and domestic waste treatment facilities. EMS has a robust presence across India, executing projects in both urban and rural areas.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
EMS is poised for continued growth, supported by a robust order book exceeding ₹2,200 crore. The company's strategic focus on large, bundled EPC + O&M projects positions it well to capitalize on government initiatives like AMRUT 2.0 and the Swachh Bharat Mission. With its comprehensive capabilities and strong market presence, EMS aims to maintain a consistent growth trajectory in the coming years.

Name: Enviro Infra Engineers Ltd
Overview:
Enviro Infra Engineers Ltd focuses on environmental infrastructure projects, with specialization in sewage treatment plants, water treatment, and waste management. Since 2009, it has executed projects for urban bodies, industrial clusters, and state governments across India. Its integrated model spans engineering, procurement, construction, and maintenance.

The company has built a reputation as a reliable EPC player in water and sanitation. Its solutions address India’s critical urbanization and water resource challenges, enabling sustainable growth in cities and industrial hubs. A growing order book underscores strong demand for its services.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With government-led spending on sanitation and water management expected to rise, the company is well placed to capture new contracts and expand its recurring operations and maintenance revenue base.

Name: Va Tech Wabag Ltd
Overview:
VA Tech Wabag Ltd is a prominent multinational player in the water treatment sector, focusing on the design, supply, construction, and operational management of drinking water, wastewater, industrial water, and desalination plants. The company delivers comprehensive solutions across municipal and industrial domains through both EPC and O&M models.

With a global presence, VA Tech Wabag serves clients across India, the Middle East, Africa, and beyond, playing a crucial role in addressing water scarcity, environmental sustainability, and infrastructure development in emerging and developed markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
VA Tech Wabag is expected to maintain growth momentum in FY26 on the back of a robust order book, balanced domestic and international exposure, and increasing demand for water management solutions. Strong execution, backed by policy support and global opportunities, is likely to sustain both revenue growth and margin expansion.

Name: Antony Waste Handling Cell Ltd
Overview:
Antony Waste Handling Cell Ltd (AWHCL) is among India’s top five municipal waste management players with over two decades of experience. The company provides end-to-end MSW solutions including collection & transportation (C&T), processing, disposal, and waste-to-energy projects. Its flagship project is the integrated waste management facility at Kanjurmarg, Mumbai, one of Asia’s largest, handling over 5,800 TPD.

The company has diversified operations across 9 states, with a fleet of 2,295 vehicles and expertise in C&T, mechanical sweeping, construction & demolition recycling, and bio-mining. AWHCL has also entered the Waste-to-Energy (WTE) space with its Pimpri project and is expanding into resource recovery and circular economy solutions, positioning itself as a key beneficiary of India’s growing privatization in waste management.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management reaffirmed its 25% revenue CAGR target over 4–5 years, driven by WTE expansion, processing, and bio-mining projects. In the short term, 8–10% revenue growth is assured from existing contracts, with upside expected from upcoming tenders. Only one small C&T contract (3% of revenue) is due to expire in Dec 2025, with bids already placed for renewal.
The company remains cautious on non-MSW diversification (auto scrapping, tyre recycling), preferring to focus on profitable core areas.

Name: Ion Exchange (India) Ltd
Overview:
Ion Exchange (India) Ltd is a pioneering public-listed company in water and environmental management solutions, offering comprehensive services including industrial water treatment, zero-liquid discharge systems, packaged drinking water, and specialty chemical applications. Headquartered in Mumbai, the company operates manufacturing facilities in India, Portugal, UAE, Indonesia, Bangladesh, and Saudi Arabia, and boasts a global execution footprint, having delivered over 100,000 projects to date.

Its core strength lies in delivering integrated engineering, chemical, and consumer product solutions across industrial, institutional, municipal, and residential sectors. The company is structured across three business verticals—as of FY25, Engineering contributed 61%, Chemicals 29%, and Consumer Products 10% of consolidated revenues—underpinned by long-term contracts and a diversified client base across domestic and international geographies.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Ion Exchange is well-positioned for sustained growth in FY26, with a strong order pipeline in water infrastructure projects, rising demand for zero-liquid discharge and industrial wastewater solutions, and international expansion opportunities. Premiumization in consumer products and higher-margin chemical sales are expected to further support profitability, even as large engineering projects drive topline momentum.

Name: Ganesha Ecosphere Ltd
Overview:
Ganesha Ecosphere Ltd is India’s leading PET waste recycling company, specializing in manufacturing recycled polyester staple fiber (RPSF), spun yarn, and dyed texturized yarn. Its operations are based on sustainable textile solutions, converting post-consumer PET into high-quality fibers for both domestic and global markets.

The company supports a circular economy model through advanced recycling facilities and eco-sensitive manufacturing. Its product mix addresses growing environmental compliance needs and has positioned it as a prominent name in the sustainable textile value chain.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ganesha Ecosphere is gearing up for strong growth by investing heavily in expanding its recycling capacity and focusing on higher-margin products. The company is increasing its rPET granule capacity significantly—adding up to 90,000 tons annually—which will enable it to triple its current production and raise the share of value-added products like rPET chips and filament yarn to around 60–65% of its sales.

Name: Gravita India Ltd
Overview:
Gravita India Limited is one of India’s largest lead producers with a diversified presence across lead, aluminium, plastic recycling, and turnkey projects. The company has built deep expertise in recycling used batteries, cable scrap, aluminium scrap, plastic scrap, and other materials, serving both domestic and global markets.

With operations spanning multiple countries and a strong sourcing network, Gravita leverages economies of scale, value-added product innovation, and strategic expansions to enhance profitability. The business is supported by a clear vision to diversify beyond lead while maintaining leadership in sustainable recycling solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Gravita India Ltd is poised for significant growth, driven by strategic diversification and global expansion. The company plans to reduce its reliance on lead recycling from 88% to 70% by increasing its aluminium recycling capacity to 70,000–80,000 tonnes, aiming for a 35–40% annual growth in this segment. Additionally, Gravita is entering high-potential markets such as rubber and lithium-ion battery recycling, with pilot projects underway in India and Africa.

Name: KFin Technologies Ltd
Overview:
KFin Technologies Limited is a leading technology-driven financial services platform providing comprehensive solutions to both investors and issuers in the capital markets ecosystem. It is the largest registrar and transfer agent (RTA) in India with offerings spanning mutual funds, alternatives, pensions, and corporate issuer services, supported by strong technology capabilities and digital platforms. The company also operates internationally, serving markets like Malaysia, the Philippines, Hong Kong, Singapore, and GIFT City.

The business is diversified across domestic mutual fund services, issuer solutions, international operations, and alternative investment services, with strong traction in value-added solutions. Its recent strategic focus includes scaling international business, expanding product innovation such as KRA, IRIS, and wealth platforms, and strengthening market share in both mutual fund and corporate RTA segments. Management remains bullish on industry growth tailwinds, client additions, and innovation-led expansion.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management remains bullish on growth across all verticals, supported by strong client wins, product innovation, and favorable industry trends. With cost discipline, international expansion, and platform upgrades underway, KFin Tech expects sustained double-digit revenue growth and margin stability in FY26.

Name: Central Depository Services (India) Ltd
Overview:
Central Depository Services Limited (CDSL) is a Market Infrastructure Institution (MII) operating within the Indian capital markets ecosystem. It facilitates the holding and transacting of securities in electronic form and enables settlement of trades executed on stock exchanges. Established in 1999, it has steadily expanded its services to include depository operations, KYC records management, and insurance/commodity repositories.

CDSL is one of the two depositories in India and the only listed one in Asia-Pacific. It operates through a vast network of Depository Participants (DPs), offering services across equities, debentures, mutual funds, government securities, and corporate bonds. Over the years, the company has introduced innovations like eVoting, eDIS, and direct security pay-outs, making it a digital-first enabler in the Indian capital markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Central Depository Services (India) Ltd (CDSL) is poised for sustained growth, driven by India's expanding retail investor base and increasing financialization. The company is enhancing its digital infrastructure, integrating services like e-KYC, e-signatures, and e-voting to streamline investor onboarding and engagement. Additionally, CDSL is capitalizing on regulatory mandates for the dematerialization of unlisted securities and insurance policies, broadening its service offerings and revenue streams.

Name: Protean eGov Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Protean eGov Technologies Limited (formerly NSDL e-Governance Infrastructure Ltd.), incorporated in December 1995, develops large-scale, citizen-centric digital public infrastructure and e-governance solutions. Its core offerings span digital identity, tax services (PAN, TIN), pension (CRA for NPS & APY), consent-based data sharing platforms, cloud services, and open digital ecosystems—serving central and state governments across sectors like finance, welfare, and commerce 

In FY25, Protean maintained its focus on population-scale solutions and expanded its digital footprint through international deployments and innovative modules such as “DPI-in-a-Box.” The company leveraged strategic wins in regulatory registries, digital tax, and pension infrastructure, while exploring SaaS and fintech extensions for broader digital inclusion
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Protean eGov is positioned for steady growth momentum and a healthy, net-cash balance sheet—that enables continued investment in DPI, SaaS extensions, and international expansion. However, the loss of the PAN 2.0 contract poses strategic risks, potentially affecting revenue contribution and free cash generation.

Name: Computer Age Management Services Ltd
Overview:
Computer Age Management Services Limited (CAMS) is India’s largest registrar and transfer agent (RTA) for mutual funds, offering end-to-end investor services, distributor services, and AMC services. With a dominant 68% market share in mutual fund average assets under management (AAUM), CAMS serves 26 of the 50 fund houses in India, including 10 of the top 15. The company provides technology-enabled solutions for account creation, transaction processing, and redemptions, along with a growing portfolio of non-mutual fund services such as Alternative Investment Funds (AIF), Portfolio Management Services (PMS), insurance repositories, payment aggregation, account aggregation, KYC registration, and National Pension Scheme (NPS) record-keeping.

CAMS has built a strong presence across India with over 280 service centers in 25 states and 5 union territories, supported by 8 call centers and multiple digital platforms like myCAMS, MFCentral, CAMServ, and WealthServ. It continues to expand in emerging financial hubs such as GIFT City and is investing in AI, data analytics, and digital transformation capabilities through strategic acquisitions and joint ventures. Its diversified offerings, strong operational infrastructure, and technology-first approach position it as a critical enabler in India’s asset management and financial services ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
CAMS targets increasing its non-mutual fund revenue share to 20% within 2–3 years by scaling AIF/PMS, insurance repository, payments, and account aggregation services. Expansion in GIFT City, integration of AI/data analytics capabilities through acquisitions, and continued leadership in MF RTAs are expected to sustain its growth momentum. The company is well positioned to leverage India’s rising financialization of savings and digital adoption trends for long-term growth.

Name: CARE Ratings Ltd
Overview:
CARE Ratings Ltd (now part of the CareEdge Group) is one of India’s leading credit rating agencies, offering services across credit ratings, analytics, advisory, research, and ESG assessments. Founded in 1993 and headquartered in Mumbai, the company serves a broad spectrum of sectors including financial institutions, corporates, structured finance, and infrastructure.

In addition to domestic rating capabilities, CARE has a growing global presence via subsidiaries in Africa, Nepal, Mauritius, and an IFSC-based global operations desk. The firm is steadily expanding its non-rating businesses—like analytics and ESG—to complement its core rating services.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
With a well-diversified portfolio, improving margins, and strong investor confidence, CARE is well-poised for sustained growth through FY26. Key levers include continued penetration in bond ratings, scale-up of non-rating services like ESG and analytics, and strengthened international operations—all underpinned by resilient financials and strategic expansion efforts.

Name: Multi Commodity Exchange of India Ltd
Overview:
Multi Commodity Exchange of India Ltd. (MCX), incorporated in 2002 and operational since November 2003, is India’s first listed, national-level, electronic commodity derivatives exchange. Operating under SEBI regulation, MCX provides a robust platform for fair price discovery and price risk management, catering to the Indian commodity market ecosystem. The exchange offers futures and options contracts across precious metals, energy, base metals, and select agricultural commodities, with near-complete dominance in non-agri segments.

In Q1 FY26, MCX maintained its market leadership with a 100% share in precious metals, base metals, and index futures, along with 99.97% in energy. The exchange recorded an Average Daily Turnover (ADT) of ₹40,547 Cr in futures and ₹2,70,228 Cr in options. Operationally, it has a wide network of 552 members, 32,417 authorized participants, and 3.47 Cr unique client codes across 687 cities/towns, positioning it as the central player in India’s commodity derivatives space.
Key Takeways:
Growth / Future Outlook
MCX is poised for sustained growth through new product launches like electricity derivatives, coal trading, and sectoral indices, alongside rising institutional participation. Its near-monopoly and expanding product suite are expected to support double-digit growth ahead.

Name: NSDL
Overview:
Established in 1996, National Securities Depository Ltd (NSDL) is India's first and largest central securities depository, headquartered in Mumbai. It plays a pivotal role in the Indian financial market by providing electronic infrastructure for the dematerialization of securities and facilitating the electronic settlement of trades. 

NSDL offers services to investors, stock brokers, custodians, and issuer companies through a network of Depository Participants and digital platforms.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

NSDL is poised for continued growth, driven by the expanding retail investor base and the increasing adoption of digital financial services in India. The company aims to enhance its technological infrastructure by integrating advanced technologies such as blockchain and artificial intelligence to streamline and secure depository services.

Name: ICRA Ltd
Overview:
ICRA Limited, established in 1991, is an independent credit rating agency based in India, offering a range of services including credit ratings, research, risk analytics, and consulting. The company serves various sectors such as banking, financial services, insurance, infrastructure, and corporate entities, providing insights that aid in investment decisions and risk management.

In addition to its core rating services, ICRA has expanded its offerings to include research and analytics, catering to the growing demand for data-driven insights in the financial sector. The company's strategic initiatives focus on enhancing its capabilities in these areas to maintain its competitive edge and support the evolving needs of its clients.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
ICRA is focusing on expanding its analytics capabilities and regulatory compliance solutions to meet the growing demands of the financial sector. The acquisition of Fintellix is expected to bolster these efforts, positioning ICRA for sustained growth and increased market share in the evolving financial services landscape.

Name: CRISIL Ltd
Overview:
Crisil Ltd, established in 1987, is India’s first credit rating agency and has evolved into a globally diversified analytical company. Headquartered in India and majority-owned by S&P Global Inc., Crisil provides ratings, research, risk, and policy advisory services. It has a significant global presence with over 4,600 employees across 12+ countries including the US, UK, Poland, China, and the UAE.

Its operations are structured across four core businesses: Crisil Ratings, Crisil Intelligence, Crisil Coalition Greenwich, and Crisil Integral IQ. Crisil's domain expertise and analytical rigour enable clients across government and private sectors to make high-stakes decisions confidently. In H1 FY25, the company posted robust financial performance with income from operations growing 7.9% YoY and PAT rising 15.1% YoY.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

CRISIL Ltd is strategically expanding its footprint in India's burgeoning infrastructure sector, with a focus on renewable energy, airports, and state finances. The company projects a 50–60% annual increase in capital expenditure at small private airports over the next three years, driven by rising terminal utilization and the need for capacity expansion. Additionally, CRISIL anticipates a 7–9% revenue growth for 18 major Indian states in fiscal year 2026, supported by robust GST collections and increased devolutions from the central government

Name: Colgate-Palmolive (India) Ltd
Overview:
Colgate-Palmolive India Ltd. is India’s market leader in oral care, with iconic brands across toothpaste, toothbrushes, and toothpowder segments. It continues to strengthen its position through innovation, premiumization, and brand trust, while expanding into face care and exports to broaden its portfolio.
 
 Business Segments 
 
 Oral Care (Toothpaste, Toothbrushes, Toothpowder): ~85% of total revenue
 
 Personal Care (Palmolive range, face care, soaps, etc.): ~10% of total revenue
 
 Exports & Other: ~5% of total revenue
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Colgate-Palmolive India is focused on premiumization, sustainability, and category expansion beyond oral care into personal care and skincare. New product launches and a growing export base support revenue diversification. Sustainability goals—Net Zero carbon by 2040 and 100% recyclable packaging by 2025—further align the brand with evolving consumer expectations. These initiatives, along with its dominant market share, ensure long-term stability and growth.

Name: Patanjali Foods Ltd
Overview:
Ruchi Soya Industries Limited is primarily engaged in the processing of oilseeds and refining of edible oils, with an expanding footprint in the food, FMCG, and home & personal care (HPC) segments. The company has a strong distribution network across India, serving both urban and rural markets through a mix of modern trade, e-commerce, and traditional retail channels.

In recent years, the company has been diversifying its portfolio beyond edible oils into value-added and higher-margin segments such as biscuits, ghee, honey, ethnic foods, nutraceuticals, and personal care products. With strategic brand-building, aggressive distribution expansion, and integration of HPC operations, Ruchi Soya aims to become a more diversified FMCG player while sustaining leadership in its core edible oil business.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ruchi Soya Industries is focusing on expanding its edible oil production capacity to meet the growing domestic demand. The company is enhancing its backward integration by developing oil palm plantations in the northeastern states of India, aiming to reduce import dependence. Additionally, Ruchi Soya is diversifying its product portfolio by venturing into the nutraceutical and wellness segments, offering plant-based and vegetarian products.

Name: Bikaji Foods International Ltd
Overview:
Bikaji Foods International Limited is a prominent FMCG player in India, specializing in ethnic snacks, sweets, papads, western snacks, and emerging categories like frozen foods and bakery products. The company has built its brand on traditional Indian flavors while continuously innovating to cater to evolving consumer preferences in both domestic and international markets. It enjoys strong leadership in several core states and has expanded its footprint to 23 states and 4 union territories.

Leveraging its established distribution network, the company has made significant inroads into export markets, including a strategic joint venture in Nepal to overcome import duty barriers. With a focus on premiumization, operational efficiency, and market expansion, Bikaji Foods is positioning itself to capture a larger share of the rapidly growing organized snacks sector in India and abroad.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bikaji Foods International is actively expanding its footprint both domestically and internationally. The company has entered a 50:50 joint venture with Nepal's Chaudhary Group to establish a manufacturing facility in Nepal, aiming to enhance its presence in the South Asian market'. Domestically, Bikaji is focusing on increasing its retail presence, with plans to expand its store count and enhance distribution channels. The company is also investing in modernizing its production facilities to improve efficiency and meet growing demand.

Name: Procter & Gamble Hygiene and Health Care Ltd
Overview:
Procter & Gamble Hygiene and Health Care Limited (PGHH) is a leading FMCG company in India, specializing in feminine hygiene and personal healthcare products. With iconic brands like Whisper, Vicks, and Old Spice, it holds leadership positions in its core categories and enjoys strong brand equity across urban and rural markets. 

Over decades, PGHH has maintained a track record of consistent growth, driven by a focus on product superiority, consumer education, and market penetration. Its business model is built on a balanced mix of premium and affordable offerings, strong distribution reach, and productivity-led margin improvements, enabling it to navigate industry challenges while capturing long-term growth opportunities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to grow ahead of industry averages by deepening category penetration, premiumizing offerings, and expanding reach. The focus remains on consistent, sustained growth through innovation, consumer engagement, and operational excellence, with rural recovery expected to complement steady urban demand over time.

Name: Hatsun Agro Product Ltd
Overview:
Hatsun Agro Product Limited is a leading private-sector dairy company in India with a strong presence in milk, milk products, and ice cream segments. Established over five decades ago, the company has built an extensive procurement and distribution network, sourcing milk directly from farmers and delivering products to urban and rural markets. Its portfolio includes well-known brands in fresh dairy, curd, ice cream, and other value-added products.

The company leverages its brand equity, quality standards, and rural outreach to sustain market leadership. It continues to invest in expanding capacity, cold chain infrastructure, and product innovation. With a diversified product basket and a strong retail presence, Hatsun Agro focuses on both volume growth and premium product segments to enhance profitability and market share.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to drive growth through product diversification, expanding rural penetration, and enhancing brand positioning in premium categories. With continuous capacity expansion, technology upgrades, and strong demand fundamentals in the dairy sector, the company expects sustained growth momentum in FY26, supported by both volume increases and value-added product expansion.

Name: CCL Products (India) Ltd
Overview:
CCL Products (India) Ltd is a global coffee manufacturer specializing in instant and freeze-dried coffee. It operates across India, Vietnam, and Switzerland, supplying both B2B (bulk, private labels) and branded retail segments, with a growing footprint in specialty and value-added products.

The company is pursuing strategic diversification, expanding branded revenues under “Continental Coffee,” ramping up value-added production capacity (notably its Vietnam freeze-dried unit), and managing inventory and cost pressures tightly through forward procurement and operational excellence.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management reiterated guidance for FY26 with 10–20% volume growth and 15–20% EBITDA growth, supported by expanded Vietnam capacity (77,000 MT), strong export order book (50–60% volumes already booked), and higher-margin branded sales scaling.

Name: ITC Ltd
Overview:
ITC Limited is a diversified Indian conglomerate with leading positions in FMCG, Cigarettes, Hotels, Agri Business, and Paperboards & Specialty Papers. Traditionally India’s cigarette market leader, ITC has successfully expanded its presence into packaged foods, personal care, and lifestyle products under well-known brands like Aashirvaad, Sunfeast, Bingo!, Fiama, and Classmate. It also runs India’s second-largest hotel chain (ITC Hotels) and is the largest Indian exporter of agricultural products.
 
 Business Segments
 FMCG – Cigarettes — India’s dominant cigarette player with unmatched market share.
 
 FMCG – Others — Packaged foods, personal care, lifestyle retail, and new FMCG brands.
 
 Hotels — Operates 108 hotels across 70+ cities, second-largest hotel chain in India.
 
 Agri Business — Largest exporter of agri-products like wheat, rice, leaf tobacco.
 
 Paperboards & Specialty Papers — Leader in premium packaging and eco-friendly paper products.
 
 Revenue Mix 
 FMCG – Cigarettes: 40% of total revenue
 
 FMCG – Others: 25%
 
 Agri Business: 20%
 
 Paperboards & Packaging: 15%
 
 India: 78% of revenue
 
 Exports: 22%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
ITC is positioned to capitalize on India’s growing FMCG market, expected to surpass ₹21 trillion by 2030, through portfolio expansion and premiumization strategies. The company is focusing on high-growth nutrition, organic, and convenience food segments via own brands and strategic acquisitions. Export-led growth in agri and paper, supported by investments and acquisitions like Century Pulp and Paper, strengthens its global presence. Tech-enabled, agile supply chains will drive rural and e-commerce expansion, while the planned demerger of the Hotels business aims to unlock focused growth and shareholder value.

Name: Gillette India Ltd
Overview:
Gillette India Limited, incorporated under the Companies Act 1956, is a public company engaged in the manufacturing and sale of packaged fast-moving consumer goods. The company primarily operates in the grooming and oral care segments, offering a portfolio of premium products across razors, blades, shaving gels, creams, and electric grooming solutions. Its products are distributed through various retail channels including department stores, drug stores, grocery chains, and mass merchandisers.

With flagship brands like Gillette, Venus, Braun, and Oral-B, the company enjoys a dominant position in the Indian blades and razors category. It operates two manufacturing facilities located in Bhiwadi (Rajasthan) and Baddi (Himachal Pradesh), and exports to 13 countries. Gillette India is a subsidiary of Procter & Gamble, USA, which holds a 75% stake through its global subsidiaries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Gillette India is strategically focusing on expanding its grooming segment, which now constitutes over 80% of its revenue. The company is introducing innovative products like Gillette Guard and premium Mach 3 razors to cater to the growing demand for self-care among Indian consumers. To enhance accessibility, Gillette is employing a distribution strategy that includes Guard Starter Kits, bundled with razors, blades, and gel, sold through traditional retail channels and mobile units, effectively reaching rural markets.

Name: Nestle India Ltd
Overview:
Nestlé India stands out as a top FMCG company, operating across a range of categories including milk and nutrition, beverages, prepared dishes and cooking aids (notably Maggi), and chocolates and confectionery (Kit Kat, Munch, etc.). The company benefits from strong brand leadership, a diversified product basket, local R&D expertise, and robust investments in capacity and innovation.
 
 Business Segment Revenue FY2025
 Milk Products & Nutrition:
 Accounts for approximately 47% of total revenue, which translates to around ₹9,436 crore. This segment includes dairy products, infant nutrition, and related items.
 
 Prepared Dishes & Cooking Aids (Maggi, etc.):
 Contributes roughly 31% or ₹6,824 crore. Led by Maggi noodles and related culinary products, this is a robust growth and market leadership area.
 
 Chocolates & Confectionery:
 Comprises about 17% of revenue, equal to ₹3,413 crore, driven by brands like Kit Kat, Munch, and Milkybar.
 
 Beverages (Nescafé, etc.):
 Makes up close to 10% of revenue, around ₹2,008 crore, featuring instant coffee and ready-to-drink products.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Nestlé India is focused on expanding manufacturing capacity through new facilities in Odisha and Gujarat, enhancing output across noodles, chocolates, coffee, and dairy. The company continues to innovate its product portfolio with region-specific offerings, health and nutrition-focused products, and new launches in snacking, beverages, and pet care. Accelerated rural and semi-urban distribution, combined with direct-to-consumer digital channels, strengthens consumer engagement.

Name: Godrej Consumer Products Ltd
Overview:
Godrej Consumer Products Limited (GCPL) is a major FMCG player focused on the household and personal care sector across fast-growing emerging markets. It leads in household insecticides and hair care products in India, Indonesia, and Sub-Saharan Africa, with notable presence in soaps, air fresheners, and detergents. GCPL combines strong market share with innovation, direct rural reach, and a commitment to sustainability and diversity.
 
Business Segments ):
 
 India: ~55–60% — Leadership in household insecticides, hair color, soaps, detergents, and air fresheners.
 
 Africa, USA & Middle East: ~25–30% — Strong hair care and extensions portfolio, especially in Sub-Saharan Africa and US retail channels.
 
 Indonesia: ~15–20% — Dominates household insecticides and air fresheners, with growing presence in health & beauty channels.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Godrej Consumer Products’ growth is driven by its rural expansion strategy in India, targeting deeper penetration into underserved villages, alongside distribution channel growth in Indonesia’s pharma and beauty segments. The US Hair Extensions business is set to expand through new retail partnerships, while the new ₹515 crore manufacturing unit in Tamil Nadu enhances capacity and supports long-term scalability.

Name: Jyothy Labs Ltd
Overview:
Founded in 1983, Jyothy Labs Limited (JLL) is a prominent player in India’s FMCG sector, operating across four major categories — fabric care, dishwashing, personal care, and household insecticides. Its flagship brands include Ujala, Henko, Exo, Pril, Margo, and Maxo, many of which hold leadership positions in their respective categories. With 23 manufacturing plants, 9,900+ channel partners, and a reach of 3.6 million outlets (1.3 million direct), JLL has built a strong nationwide presence supported by sustained advertising, celebrity endorsements, and product innovation.

The company achieved ₹2,847 crore in revenue in FY25, with fabric care contributing the largest share. Its business is geographically diversified across urban (60%) and rural (40%) India, and also enjoys a healthy export presence. The portfolio expansion during FY25 included new launches like Ujala Young & Fresh, Maxo Anti-Mosquito Racquet, Maxo Aerosol, and Jovia beauty soap, backed by strong ATL and digital campaigns.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
JLL expects subdued urban demand in H1 FY26 due to macro pressures but foresees gradual improvement supported by fiscal measures like income tax relief. It aims to maintain margin resilience through cost efficiency and selective price hikes. Innovation remains a key focus, with plans to scale recent launches and strengthen high-potential categories such as liquid detergents, liquid vaporizers, and premium personal care. Competitive intensity is expected to remain high, prompting sustained investment in brand equity and differentiated product offerings.

Name: Marico Ltd
Overview:
Marico Limited is a leading FMCG company with strong brands across hair care, edible oils, healthy foods, skin care, and male grooming. It has a robust presence in India and key international markets like Bangladesh, SEA, and the Middle East. The company is driving growth through premiumization, digital-first brands, and distribution expansion.
 
 Business Segments
 
 Domestic Business: ~71% of total revenue
 
 Coconut Oils: ~40%
 
 Refined Oils: ~25%
 
 Value-Added Hair Oils: ~21%
 
 Personal Care (Male grooming, skincare, fabric care): ~5%
 
 Modern Trade & E-commerce: ~23% of domestic share (14% + 9%)
 
 International Business: ~23% of total revenue
 
 Bangladesh: ~51% of international share
 
 South East Asia: ~23%
 
 Middle East: ~13%
 
 South Africa: ~7%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Marico is navigating short-term margin pressure through operational efficiency while investing in high-growth areas like premium foods, male grooming, and digital-first brands. It’s also expanding distribution in underpenetrated rural markets via Sales 3.0 and e-commerce. Strong international presence and a 4D strategy (Diversification, Distribution, Digital, Diversity) prepare the company to sustain growth amid shifting consumer trends and macroeconomic fluctuations.

Name: Hindustan Unilever Ltd
Overview:
Hindustan Unilever Limited (HUL) is India’s largest Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) company, with market leadership in about 90% of its product categories. Its diverse portfolio covers Home Care, Beauty & Personal Care, and Foods & Refreshments, with household brands trusted by 90% of Indian households. HUL’s massive reach spans over 80 lakh+ retail outlets, making its products deeply embedded in Indian daily life.
 
 Business Segments
 Beauty & Personal Care (42%) — Soaps, skin care, hair care, and oral care under brands like Dove, Lifebuoy, and Sunsilk.
 
 Home Care (29%) — Fabric wash, household cleaning with brands like Surf Excel, Vim, and Domex.
 
 Foods & Refreshments (29%) — Beverages, health food drinks, ice creams, sauces, and tea brands like Lipton, Bru, Horlicks, and Knorr.
 
 Revenue Mix
 Beauty & Personal Care: 42% of revenue
 
 Home Care: 29% of revenue
 
 Foods & Refreshments: 29% of revenue
 
 India: 96% of total revenue
 
 International: 4%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
HUL is focusing on expanding its presence in health, nutrition, and wellness to capture high-growth consumer trends. Strategic investments in digitalization, e-commerce, and analytics-driven marketing are enhancing channel innovation and customer engagement. The company aims to deepen rural penetration and reach the ‘Next Billion’ consumers through mobile-first campaigns, vernacular product initiatives, and new employment models. Acquisitions and partnerships, such as the addition of Minimalist, are aligning its portfolio with evolving consumer demands. Continued investment in agile supply chains, green energy, and responsible sourcing supports resilience and reinforces HUL’s ESG leadership.

Name: Dabur India Ltd
Overview:
Dabur India is a leading FMCG company with a strong presence in natural healthcare, personal care, and food products. It holds dominant market shares in health supplements (Chyawanprash), oral care, hair oils, and fruit juices (Real, Active), and has built a trusted brand presence both in India and across global markets.
 
 Business Segments
 Home & Personal Care: Includes hair oils, skincare, oral care, and hygiene products.
 
 Healthcare: Ayurvedic and natural health supplements, OTC products, and digestives.
 
 Food & Beverages: Fruit juices (Real, Active), health drinks, and culinary products.
 
 Revenue Summary
 Revenue is diversified across three main verticals: Home & Personal Care, Healthcare, and Food & Beverages. The international business contributes over 26% of total turnover with strong footprints in Asia, Africa, America, Europe, and the Middle East. The company’s wide rural and urban distribution network (over 3.2 million retail outlets) and focus on contract as well as in-house manufacturing help maintain scale and flexibility.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Dabur is focusing on expanding into high-growth categories like fruit drinks, baby care, and health foods, while also deepening rural distribution to tap into rural demand recovery. With a strong global footprint and a trusted brand portfolio, the company is leveraging digital and e-commerce platforms to extend its reach.

Name: L T Foods Ltd
Overview:
LT Foods Ltd is a leading Indian-origin FMCG company specializing in specialty rice, particularly Basmati (notably Daawat® and Royal®), along with organic staples, RTH/RTC meals, and ready-to-eat offerings. Operating via a farm-to-fork model, it maintains global manufacturing and distribution across India, the U.S., and Europe.

With a robust presence in more than 80 countries, LT Foods combines deep agricultural sourcing with consumer brand strength, targeting millennials through organic and clean-label products like Ecolife®, while continuing innovation and premiumization across food verticals.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
LT Foods is targeting further premiumization, channel expansion (e-commerce, modern trade), and new NPD launches, aiming to raise EBITDA margins above 14% over the next 4 years. Conservative financials, growing international share, and innovations in global staple and organic segments position the company for sustainable volume and margin-led growth through FY26 and beyond.

Name: Tata Consumer Products Ltd
Overview:
TCPL is a leading player in India’s food & beverages sector, with strong domestic and international presence. World’s second-largest tea company, with leadership in core markets. FY20 acquisition of Tata Chemicals’ consumer products business added iconic brands like Tata Salt and Tata Sampann, strengthening its foods portfolio.
 
 Segmental Revenue Split:
 India Beverages (37%): Key brands – Tata Tea, Tata Coffee Grand, Tata Gluco+, Himalayan.
 
 India Foods (27%): Market leader with Tata Salt, Tata Sampann (pulses, spices), Soulfull, and Yumside.
 
 US Coffee (11%): Eight O'clock brand.
 
 International Tea (15%): Tetley, teapigs, Good Earth – strong presence in UK, Canada, Europe.
 
 Tata Coffee (10%): Integrated coffee operations including plantations and extraction.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Tata Consumer Products is driving growth through innovation and premiumization across its core categories of salt, tea, and beverages, while aggressively expanding its staples portfolio under Tata Sampann. Its joint venture with Starbucks is gaining traction, with rapid store expansion and improved operational efficiency contributing to profitability.

Name: Bombay Burmah Trading Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1863, Bombay Burmah Trading Corporation Limited (BBTCL) is a flagship company of the Wadia Group. Originally engaged in the teak business, the company diversified into tea plantations in 1913. Today, BBTCL operates across a wide range of sectors, including plantations, food processing, healthcare, auto components, and real estate. This diversification allows the company to leverage synergies across its various business units, enhancing its market presence and operational efficiency.
