BBTCL's operations are primarily divided into seven business segments: Plantations (Tea), Auto Electric Components, Investments, Healthcare, Horticulture, Food (Bakery and Dairy Products), and Others. The company generates the majority of its revenue from the Food (Bakery and Dairy Products) segment, followed by Plantations and Auto Electric Components. This diversified portfolio enables BBTCL to mitigate risks associated with market fluctuations in any single sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
BBTCL is well-positioned to capitalize on growth opportunities across its diversified business segments. The company's strategic focus on expanding its presence in the Food (Bakery and Dairy Products) and Healthcare sectors, coupled with a strong order book, is expected to drive revenue growth in FY26.

Name: Britannia Industries Ltd
Overview:
Britannia Industries, with over 100 years of legacy, is one of India’s leading food companies known for iconic brands like Good Day, Tiger, NutriChoice, and Marie Gold. Its wide product range includes biscuits, bread, cakes, rusks, and dairy items. Britannia has a strong PAN India presence, reaching over 50% of Indian households through more than 5 million retail outlets, and exports to 80 countries with manufacturing units in India, UAE, Oman, and Nepal.
 
Business Segments (Revenue Mix)
 
 Biscuits: ~90% of total revenue — core segment driving sales and leadership in India’s organized biscuit market.
 
 Bread: ~3–4% — significant player with over 1 lakh tons of annual volume, Rs 450 crore in value.
 
 Cakes, Rusks, Dairy & Others: ~5–6% — includes cakes, dairy products, and value-added bakery items.
 
 Exports: ~5.5% of total revenue — sales to 80 countries, strong market share in GCC countries.
Key Takeways:
Key Takeaways:
 
 Britannia is the undisputed leader in India’s biscuit segment with deep rural penetration and dominant brands.
 
 Strong distribution footprint covers 5 million+ retail outlets, reaching half of Indian households.
 
 Growing international footprint with plants in UAE, Oman, and a new facility in Nepal to boost exports.
 
 Rural expansion and direct distribution gains have helped outperform the market in volume growth.
 
 Faces challenges from input cost pressures due to sustained food inflation and wheat price volatility.
 
 Increasing competition in certain segments may lead to pricing strategy recalibration.
 
 Growth Outlook:
 
 Focus on differentiated products like Atta Pizza and Cheese Garlic Bread to meet evolving tastes.
 
 Continued investment in brand-building and direct distribution to deepen rural reach.
 
 Expansion into new related categories and adjacencies to drive incremental growth.
 
 Cost efficiency programs and pricing actions to manage raw material inflation risks.
 
 Sustainability initiatives to align with changing consumer preferences and global ESG norms.
 
 Strengthening international operations with localized production to tap emerging export markets.

Name: Emami Ltd
Overview:
Emami Limited is a leading FMCG company engaged in the manufacturing and marketing of personal care and healthcare products with a portfolio of well-known household brands. The company operates in categories such as skin care, hair care, pain management, healthcare remedies, and male grooming, catering to a wide consumer base in both domestic and international markets.

Its strength lies in brand equity, a robust distribution network, and continuous innovation across core categories. Emami combines traditional Ayurvedic formulations with modern consumer trends to maintain relevance. The company has a growing omnichannel presence and focuses on expanding its portfolio to address evolving consumer needs, particularly in male grooming, pain management, and value-added personal care products.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to drive growth through brand relaunches, portfolio diversification, and deeper omnichannel penetration. Expansion in high-growth categories like male grooming, healthcare, and pain management, along with new market entries internationally, is expected to support sustainable growth in FY26 while maintaining strong margins.

Name: VST Industries Ltd
Overview:
VST Industries is a leading Indian tobacco company, known particularly for its cigarette and tobacco product portfolio. It holds a strong market presence across key regions such as West Bengal, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Bihar, and Uttar Pradesh, including a popular brand among the top industry titles. The company benefits from integrated manufacturing operations and a legacy of quality and distribution.

Its operational model is focused on efficient production and strong brand positioning within a regulated consumer product environment, helping drive customer loyalty and steady engagement in its target markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
VST Industries is positioned to sustain profits by leveraging its brand strength, cost discipline, and stable cash flow. However, sales growth could hinge on regulatory landscape and volume retention. Continued operational efficiency and prudent cost control will remain key to driving profitability in the near term.

Name: Zydus Wellness Ltd
Overview:
Zydus Wellness Ltd is an integrated consumer products company operating across the entire value chain from product development to marketing and distribution in the health and wellness space. The company manages a diverse portfolio of well-established brands in food, nutrition, and personal care, catering to both urban and rural consumers.

Its core segments include Food & Nutrition with brands like Glucon-D, Complan, Sugar Free, and Nutralite, and Personal Care with brands such as Everyuth and Nycil. Through innovation, acquisitions, and expansion into new categories, the company aims to capture a larger share of the growing health-conscious consumer market.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is confident of sustaining structural double-digit growth (ex-seasonal brands) driven by innovation, distribution expansion, and category leadership. While seasonal and nutrition drink categories face temporary headwinds, portfolio diversification, strong execution, and premiumisation initiatives are expected to deliver sustained growth and margin expansion over the medium term.

Name: Honasa Consumer Ltd
Overview:
Honasa stands as India’s leading digitally-first beauty and personal care company, anchored by its flagship brand, Mamaearth, along with other niche consumer-focused brands. The company has implemented a strategic direct-distribution model and prioritized high-impact product categories to reignite brand momentum and streamline operations.

Despite a recent restructuring phase, Honasa has begun to see early operational improvements—manifested in healthier margins, improved product mix, and stronger distribution reach—laying the foundation for a return to sustained growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook 
Honasa anticipates a return to double-digit full-year revenue growth in FY26, driven by its distribution scale-up, revival of core categories, and momentum from its younger brands. Analyst forecasts suggest ~14% revenue growth and significant improvement in profitability.

Name: Chamanlal Setia Exports Ltd
Overview:
Chaman Lal Setia Exports Limited (CLSE) was incorporated in Amritsar, Punjab, in 1994 by Mr. Chaman Lal Setia and his sons. It is one of India’s oldest rice millers and exporters, specializing in Basmati rice of all varieties. The company is engaged in milling and processing Basmati rice and has been involved in export operations since 1982, recognized as an export house by the Ministry of Commerce in 1989, and presently classified as a ‘Star Export House’.

CLSE offers a diverse range of products, including flagship Basmati brands Maharani, Mithas, and Begum, non-Basmati brands like Green World Aromatic Rice, and innovative products such as Maharani Diabetics Rice, Basmati Rice Plus, and organic Brown Basmati Rice. The company is India’s largest private label exporter, managing over 300 private label brands, reflecting its strong global footprint and brand presence.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook & Expansion Plans
CLSE plans to add three new packing units in Karnal, doubling or tripling current packing capacity. The company targets increasing branded sales to 30% of revenues in the next 2–3 years, expanding into modern trade channels like JioMart and Amazon, and entering new international markets such as Indonesia and the Philippines. It is also pursuing regulatory approvals from the US FDA and UK FDA.

Name: Sanstar Ltd
Overview:
Sanstar Ltd is a prominent player in the specialty chemicals sector, primarily focusing on bio-polymers derived from renewable sources. The company's product portfolio includes corn starch derivatives, which find applications in various industries such as food, pharmaceuticals, and textiles. Sanstar's commitment to sustainability and innovation has positioned it as a key supplier in the bio-based chemicals market.

In FY25, Sanstar reported a decline in revenue, with total income from operations at ₹953.42 crore, down from ₹1,067.27 crore in the previous fiscal year. Despite the revenue dip, the company continued to invest in research and development to enhance product offerings and maintain its competitive edge in the industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Sanstar plans to enhance its research and development efforts to innovate and diversify its product offerings. The company aims to strengthen its market position by focusing on sustainability and exploring new applications for its bio-based products.

Name: Apex Frozen Foods Ltd
Overview:
Apex Frozen Foods Ltd is a fully integrated aquaculture company engaged in the processing and export of shrimps. The company operates hatcheries, farms, processing facilities, and exports primarily to the US and European markets.

Its vertically integrated operations allow control over quality and cost, providing competitive advantage in the global seafood market. Apex continues to strengthen its export base while balancing risks from regulatory and environmental factors.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
The company is expanding its processing capacity by an additional 20,000 MTPA, which will enhance its overall scale in value-added products. With a strategic focus on diversifying its export base and scaling premium shrimp offerings, Apex aims to strengthen margins and reduce geographic concentration. Rising seafood consumption globally, especially in the US and Europe, positions the company for long-term sustainable growth.

Name: Gopal Snacks Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1999, Gopal Snacks is a fast-moving consumer goods company specializing in a wide range of snack products ranging from traditional ethnic snacks like namkeen and gathiya to Western-style offerings like wafers and extruded snack pellets. The company operates through a network of distributors and manufacturing facilities to serve diverse demographic markets with its flagship Gopal brand.

The business is also characterized by its focus on growing packaged snack consumption across regional markets, leveraging brand innovation, expanded reach, and product diversification to drive its market positioning.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Gopal Snacks is positioned for recovery and continued growth. With the Gondal plant ramped up, an expanding distributor network, and wafer-driven margin improvements, it stands to benefit from demand revival and operational efficiencies. Its focus on premium, large-pack products and expansion into new markets should help navigate inflationary pressures and drive long-term performance.

Name: Tasty Bite Eatables Ltd
Overview:
Tasty Bite Eatables Ltd is an Indian-American food company specializing in ready-to-eat meals, noodles, and rice. Established in 1995 and headquartered in Pune, India, the company operates a manufacturing facility in Bhandgaon, India, and is a subsidiary of Mars, Incorporated.

The company focuses on delivering high-quality, shelf-stable food products with a shelf life of up to 18 months. Tasty Bite's products are available in every state in the U.S. and are sold in most American grocery chains.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Tasty Bite Eatables Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the ready-to-eat food industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, Tasty Bite is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: HMA Agro Industries Ltd
Overview:
HMA Agro Industries Limited, established in 2008, is a prominent Indian food trade organization specializing in the processing and export of frozen buffalo meat. The company offers a diverse range of products, including frozen fresh buffalo meat, prepared and frozen natural products, vegetables, and cereals, catering to both domestic and international markets. 

Headquartered in India, HMA Agro operates state-of-the-art processing facilities that adhere to international food safety and quality standards. The company's strategic focus on expanding its product portfolio and enhancing operational efficiencies has positioned it as a key player in the agro-food sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

HMA Agro Industries is focused on becoming a global leader by diversifying its product range beyond its core meat exports and expanding its processing footprint through strategic partnerships and advanced technologies. The company is leveraging facility agreements and modern automated plants to boost capacity and operational efficiency. Its product portfolio now includes not only frozen buffalo meat but also seafood, basmati rice, pet food, fruits, vegetables, and leather, helping it tap into new revenue streams.

Name: AVT Natural Products Ltd
Overview:
AVT Natural Products Limited, part of the A.V. Thomas Group, is a specialized manufacturer of plant-based extracts and natural ingredient solutions catering to global food, beverage, animal nutrition, and nutraceutical industries. The company operates across diverse product segments such as marigold oleoresins, spice extracts, and tea, backed by a strong R&D orientation and integrated processing facilities in Kerala and Karnataka.

The business has evolved beyond commodity supply into solution-based ingredient offerings. With long-term agreements with major global customers, the company enjoys steady demand visibility, while simultaneously diversifying into new verticals like derma-cosmetics, nutraceuticals, and crop inputs. Its geographic presence is well-spread, with strong exposure to the Americas, alongside growing opportunities in Asia and other emerging regions.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking ahead, AVT aims to strengthen its marigold cultivation base, expand spice and tea footprints, and commercialize new product verticals. The management’s focus remains on diversifying revenue streams, improving process efficiencies, and sustaining margins despite competitive and climatic challenges. With long-term strategic customer partnerships and product innovations, the company is positioning itself for sustainable growth across global food, nutrition, and wellness markets.

Name: Hindustan Foods Ltd
Overview:
Hindustan Foods Limited (HFL) is a leading contract manufacturer in India, specializing in FMCG products across food, beverages, home care, personal care, and OTC pharma categories. The company also manufactures leather shoes and accessories, catering to both domestic and multinational clients.

Over the years, HFL has transformed into a diversified manufacturing powerhouse with a unique blend of dedicated and shared manufacturing models. With strong client partnerships, multi-category presence, and ongoing capacity expansions, the company has built a resilient business insulated from demand and inflationary volatilities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
HFL is optimistic about achieving  targets, with strong bets on ice creams, footwear, beverages, and OTC pharma. While seasonal volatility and global trade/tariff uncertainties remain potential headwinds, the company’s diversified portfolio, multi-category expertise, and client stickiness are expected to drive sustainable growth and profitability.

Name: Amir Chand Jagdish Kumar (Exports) Ltd
Overview:
Amir Chand Jagdish Kumar (Exports) Limited is engaged in the processing and export of basmati rice along with other FMCG food products. The company sources paddy, processes it into high-quality rice, and supplies it to domestic as well as international markets. Its product portfolio mainly includes different varieties of basmati rice known for their aroma, grain length, and premium quality, catering to both retail and bulk buyers.

The company operates processing units equipped with cleaning, milling, grading, and packaging facilities to ensure quality standards. It exports its products to multiple countries and also caters to domestic demand through distribution networks. By focusing on quality control, sourcing efficiency, and branding, the company serves a wide range of customers including wholesalers, retailers, and institutional buyers.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its export presence by entering new international markets and strengthening relationships with existing buyers. It aims to enhance its product portfolio by introducing value-added and branded rice products to improve margins. Increasing global demand for premium basmati rice is expected to support business growth.

Name: Prataap Snacks Ltd
Overview:
Prataap Snacks Ltd is an Indian food company engaged in manufacturing and marketing branded snacks, including potato chips, namkeens, and other savory products. It serves both retail and institutional segments across India, leveraging an extensive distribution network.

In FY25, Prataap Snacks expanded distribution, launched new product variants, and enhanced brand visibility through marketing campaigns. Operational efficiency, cost optimization, and product innovation contributed to maintaining profit margins.

The company focuses on R&D for new flavors and healthy snack alternatives, while strengthening supply chain and logistics capabilities. Prataap Snacks is well-positioned to capitalize on growing demand in both urban and rural markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Prataap Snacks Ltd is actively pursuing growth through strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing its market presence and operational efficiency. The company is focusing on expanding its product portfolio by introducing new variants in both sweet and salty snack categories. Additionally, Prataap Snacks is working on optimizing its distribution channels and implementing data-driven sales strategies to improve reach and customer engagement.

Name: Parag Milk Foods Ltd
Overview:
Parag Milk Foods Ltd, established in 1992 by Mr. Devendra Shah, is a leading dairy company in India. The company specializes in producing and promoting 100% fresh cow’s milk and a diverse range of milk products under international brand names. With a commitment to quality and innovation, Parag Milk Foods has built a strong presence in the dairy industry.

The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Manchar and Palamaner, ensuring operational efficiencies and stringent quality control measures.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Parag Milk Foods aims to achieve a revenue target of ₹10,000 crore within the next five years. The company plans to expand its distribution network, invest in brand development, and introduce new product categories to drive growth.

Name: SKM Egg Products Export (India) Ltd
Overview:
SKM Egg Products Export (India) Ltd is one of the largest producers and exporters of egg-based value-added products in India. Its portfolio includes whole egg powder, yolk powder, albumen powder, and liquid egg products.

The company primarily caters to the international food industry, supplying bakery, confectionery, and nutraceutical companies. Its export-focused business gives it strong exposure to global markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
With global protein demand rising and increasing health-conscious consumption trends, SKM Egg Products is well placed to benefit from sustained export growth and premium product expansion. The company’s investments in value-added proteins and new applications are likely to drive consistent revenue momentum into FY26 and beyond, while domestic opportunities provide an additional buffer for long-term growth.

Name: Venkys (India) Ltd
Overview:
Venky’s (India) Ltd is a poultry-focused firm primarily engaged in breeding, processing, and selling poultry products. It supports India’s poultry ecosystem through segments like broiler farming, SPF eggs, and value-added poultry goods. The company also diversifies through oilseed processing to produce edible oil and de-oiled cake for poultry feed.

A prominent player in the Indian poultry space, Venky’s integrates animal health, poultry feed, and edible oil production, bolstering both upstream and downstream operations within the food and farming sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company continues to pivot toward improving efficiency and exploring new product avenues. Key highlights include 10% year-on-year growth in poultry segment revenue—mirrored by expansion in SPF egg capacity—and animal health segment growth of over 20%. Venky’s is also venturing into ready-to-cook spices, indicating a strategic move to diversify revenue sources

Name: ADF Foods Ltd
Overview:
ADF Foods Ltd specializes in producing and exporting traditional Indian food products such as pickles, chutneys, pastes, sauces, ready-to-eat meals, as well as frozen and canned foods. Its products reach households across Europe, the US, Australia, and the Gulf, supported by dedicated branches in the UK and USA that distribute FMCG items in those markets.

By combining India’s rich culinary heritage with global distribution capabilities, ADF Foods bridges cultural food experiences to overseas consumers through branded and high-quality packaged offerings.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

ADF Foods is strategically expanding its global presence by securing listings in major international retail chains such as Costco and Tesco, which are expected to drive export growth and support higher margins through automation and operating leverage. The company is focusing on brand-building and health-oriented products to capture higher-margin segments and benefit from industry consolidation and organized retail trends.

Name: Ganesh Consumer Products Ltd
Overview:
Ganesh Consumer Products Ltd is an FMCG company based in Kolkata, incorporated in 2000, with a strong foothold in Eastern India. It is known for its “Ganesh” brand and offers staples like whole wheat flour (atta), maida, sooji, dalia, and gram-based products like besan and sattu. Over time it has expanded into related product lines including spices, ethnic snacks, instant food mixes and specialty flours, operating through a network of seven manufacturing plants in states like West Bengal, Uttar Pradesh and Telangana. 

The company sells via B2C channels (traditional retail and modern trade), plus some B2B, supported by a large distribution network of super stockists, distributors and retailers spread across its core states. Its revenues have shown steady growth, while margins are modest but improving, and the balance sheet has gradually strengthened.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ganesh Consumer Products is aiming to deepen its presence in its core Eastern India markets while gradually moving into new geographies to widen its footprint. It plans to keep adding products (new SKUs) especially in categories adjacent to its staples business (like ethnic snacks, blended spices, etc.), to leverage its brand and distribution strength. Investment in capacity expansion (including a new roasted gram flour unit), and better supply chain and procurement efficiencies are expected to help improve margins.

Name: Gandhar Oil Refinery (India) Ltd
Overview:
Gandhar Oil Refinery (India) Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in the production of white oils, lubricants, and specialty oils under the brand name "Divyol." Established in 1992 and headquartered in Mumbai, the company caters to diverse industries, including pharmaceuticals, healthcare, automotive, and industrial applications.

The company operates multiple manufacturing facilities and has a significant presence in both domestic and international markets. Gandhar Oil Refinery is committed to innovation, quality, and sustainability in its operations to meet the evolving needs of its customers.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Gandhar Oil Refinery aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the specialty oils and lubricants industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, Gandhar Oil Refinery is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: GRM Overseas Ltd
Overview:
GRM Overseas Ltd is engaged in the manufacturing and export of textiles, including polyester yarn, cotton yarn, and blended fabrics. The company caters to domestic and international markets, focusing on high-quality textile products. GRM Overseas Ltd leverages advanced manufacturing techniques and quality control measures to maintain product consistency and meet international standards.

The company exports to countries across Europe, the Middle East, and Asia. It invests in technology, skilled workforce, and infrastructure to enhance production efficiency. GRM Overseas Ltd continues to strengthen its presence in the textile industry while expanding its global market footprint.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

GRM Overseas Ltd is advancing its evolution from a traditional basmati rice exporter into a dynamic consumer staples and new-age investment vehicle. The company is broadening its domestic footprint under its "10X" brand by adding wheat flour, besan, spices, mustard oil, and ready-to-cook products, especially in fast-growing tier II and III markets. Internationally, it is strengthening its export strategy through partnerships—such as exclusive distribution deals in Georgia and Morocco—as well as securing government contracts like the major Omani order

Name: Heritage Foods Ltd
Overview:
Heritage Foods Ltd is a prominent Indian dairy and agri-business company, founded in 1992, offering a diverse portfolio that includes milk, curd, paneer, value-added dairy products, feed, and renewable energy. The company is deeply rooted in southern India with an expansive network spanning multiple states.

Built on ethical farmer partnerships and omni-channel distribution—from traditional retail to Q-commerce—Heritage combines a strong supply chain, robust brand presence, and category leadership in products like curd and paneer.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Heritage remains confident. Its upcoming green-field ice cream facility, heightened omni-channel reach, and strong brand loyalty form a foundation for recovery. As demand rebounds, especially in VAP categories, and supply chain enhancements commence, the company appears well-positioned to continue its FY25 momentum into the rest of FY26.

Name: Vadilal Industries Ltd
Overview:
Vadilal Industries Ltd, founded in 1907 and headquartered in Ahmedabad, is one of India’s most iconic ice cream and frozen foods manufacturers. Its portfolio includes an extensive range of ice creams, frozen desserts, packaged frozen foods, and dairy-based products, with both domestic and export distribution.

The company operates major manufacturing units in Gujarat and Uttar Pradesh and reaches consumers through a vast network of distributors, retail partners, and export channels. Its long-standing legacy and strong brand presence make it a prominent player in the food processing sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Vadilal is navigating through cyclical demand in frozen treats with renewed vigor via its strong brand, expanding manufacturing capacities, and revitalized channel strategy. As it transitions through early FY26, key priorities include sustaining distribution momentum, bolstering exports, and managing cost structures to regain full-year growth trajectory and margin stability.

Name: Mukka Proteins Ltd
Overview:
Mukka Proteins Ltd, established in 2003, is a prominent manufacturer and exporter specializing in fish and insect-based protein products. The company produces fish meal, fish oil, fish soluble paste, insect meal, and insect oil, which are essential ingredients in aqua feed, poultry feed, and pet food. 

With a strong presence in both domestic and international markets, Mukka Proteins caters to the growing demand for sustainable and high-quality protein sources in the animal nutrition industry. The company's commitment to quality and innovation has positioned it as a key player in the protein manufacturing sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Despite the challenges faced in the early part of FY26, Mukka Proteins Ltd remains optimistic about its long-term prospects. The company's strategic initiatives, including product diversification and market expansion, are expected to drive growth in the coming quarters.

Name: Krishival Foods Ltd
Overview:
Krishival Foods Ltd is an agro-processing company specializing in cashews, dried fruits, and nuts. The company sources raw materials domestically and internationally, processes them at its facilities, and markets them under the “Krishival” brand and private labels. With rising demand for packaged and healthy foods, the company has established itself as a niche player in the value-added nuts and dry fruits space.

The company’s integrated operations, from sourcing to processing and branding, provide control over quality and cost efficiency. Its products are sold across retail chains, e-commerce platforms, and export channels.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company is focusing on scaling its branded product line and expanding distribution across India and exports. Health-conscious consumer trends support strong long-term demand.

Name: Zuari Agro Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Zuari Agro Chemicals Ltd is one of India’s established fertilizer producers, engaged in manufacturing and marketing a wide range of agri-inputs including urea, DAP, NPK complexes, and specialty crop nutrients. The company’s portfolio caters to diverse crop requirements, making it a key enabler of farm productivity and food security. With an integrated approach covering manufacturing, trading, and distribution, it enjoys a strong footprint in the Indian agri-input ecosystem.

The company operates modern production facilities with an emphasis on efficiency, quality, and cost competitiveness, supported by robust supply chain management. Its brand enjoys strong farmer loyalty across southern and western India. With agricultural reforms, rising food demand, and government subsidy support, Zuari Agro Chemicals is positioned to sustain growth while expanding into value-added and eco-friendly products.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Rising demand for high-yield crops, increasing government focus on fertilizer availability, and farmer adoption of advanced agro-inputs provide a strong growth runway. Expansion in specialty nutrients and integrated farm solutions is expected to drive value-accretive growth in FY26 and beyond.

Name: National Fertilizer Ltd
Overview:
National Fertilizers Limited (NFL) is a prominent public sector enterprise in India, primarily engaged in the production and marketing of fertilizers and agro-products. Established in 1974, NFL operates under the administrative control of the Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India. The company has a diverse portfolio that includes Neem Coated Urea, Bio-Fertilizers (both solid and liquid), and various allied industrial products. Additionally, NFL is involved in trading activities encompassing imported and domestic fertilizers, compost, seeds, and other agro products.

NFL's manufacturing facilities are strategically located across India, including gas-based ammonia-urea plants in Nangal and Bathinda (Punjab), Panipat (Haryana), and two at Vijaipur (Madhya Pradesh).
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
NFL aims to expand production, modernize facilities, diversify its fertilizer and agro-product portfolio, and strengthen distribution networks. Government support for sustainable agriculture provides growth opportunities, positioning NFL for long-term expansion.

Name: Madhya Bharat Agro Products Ltd
Overview:
Madhya Bharat Agro Products Ltd is an Indian agribusiness company engaged in processing, manufacturing, and marketing of edible oils, oilseeds, and related products. The company sources raw materials from domestic farmers and caters to retail, institutional, and industrial segments.

Headquartered in Madhya Pradesh, Madhya Bharat Agro emphasizes quality, supply chain efficiency, and sustainable agricultural practices. It operates integrated processing facilities to ensure consistent product standards and competitive pricing.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Madhya Bharat Agro Products Ltd (MBAPL), part of the Ostwal Group, is strategically expanding its operations to enhance production capacity and market reach. The company is focusing on backward integration by increasing its phosphoric and sulphuric acid production capacities, with plans to relocate a phosphoric acid plant to Nardana, Maharashtra, and expand its sulphuric acid plant in Sagar, Madhya Pradesh.

Name: Krishana Phoschem Ltd
Overview:
The company operates manufacturing facilities in Surat and Mumbai, while its subsidiaries and marketing arms span the US, Hong Kong, UAE, and several other geographies. Asian Star has long-standing supply arrangements with global mining giants like De Beers, Alrosa, and Arctic Canadian, ensuring consistent rough diamond sourcing. Through strong distribution channels across Asia, Europe, and the US, the company has positioned itself among the prominent Indian exporters of polished diamonds and jewellery.Krishana Phoschem Ltd (KPL), part of the Ostwal Group, operates as one of India’s largest private fertilizer and chemical manufacturers with a fully integrated process for converting low-grade rock phosphate into high-grade inputs for complex fertilizers. The company markets products under the Annadata (SSP) and Bharat (NPK/DAP complex) brands, enjoying strong presence in central India with a wide distribution network.

KPL’s facilities at Meghnagar, Madhya Pradesh, and other locations have capacities across NPK/DAP, SSP, phosphoric acid, and sulphuric acid, supported by backward integration into rock phosphate. With a pan-India distribution of ~2,500 wholesalers and 30,000+ retailers, the company is well-positioned to cater to India’s structurally deficit phosphatic fertilizer market.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
The Meghnagar expansion is a key growth lever, expected to deliver 20–25% incremental growth post-commissioning. Management remains confident of sustaining profitability through innovation, debottlenecking, and disciplined raw material procurement, despite global volatility.

Name: Mangalore Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd
Overview:
Mangalore Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd is a leading manufacturer of chemical fertilizers in India. The company produces a range of fertilizers, including nitrogenous and phosphatic fertilizers, under the brand name "Mangala."

With a manufacturing facility located in Panambur, Karnataka, Mangalore Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd serves the agricultural sector by providing quality fertilizers that enhance crop yield and soil health.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company plans to expand its product offerings and enhance manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand for fertilizers in the agricultural sector. Mangalore Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd is also focusing on sustainable practices to promote environmental conservation.

Name: Khaitan Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd
Overview:
Khaitan Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd is engaged in the manufacturing of single super phosphate (SSP) fertilizers, sulphuric acid, and other chemicals. It caters to the agriculture sector and allied industries, with a significant presence in central India.

The company benefits from a diversified product portfolio catering to both industrial and agricultural segments, ensuring consistent revenue streams.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Khaitan Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd (KCFL) is strategically enhancing its position as India's largest Single Super Phosphate (SSP) producer by expanding production capacities and focusing on sustainable growth. The company has an installed SSP capacity of 1.13 million tonnes per annum and a sulfuric acid capacity of 270,600 tonnes per annum, complemented by renewable energy initiatives such as a 1,250 kW windmill in Maharashtra.

Name: Chambal Fertilisers & Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Chambal Fertilisers & Chemicals Ltd (CFCL) is a leading fertiliser manufacturer in India, primarily producing urea from its three high-capacity plants located in Kota, Rajasthan. The company also markets a diversified range of agri-inputs, including Di-Ammonium Phosphate (DAP), Muriate of Potash (MOP), NPK fertilisers, speciality plant nutrients, crop protection chemicals, and seeds. Through its joint venture IMACID in Morocco, CFCL is engaged in the production of phosphoric acid, securing a critical input for phosphatic fertilisers.

In recent years, CFCL has been expanding into high-value segments such as crop protection chemicals (CPC), speciality nutrients (SN), and biologicals, with a growing focus on sustainable agriculture. The company has also entered the seeds business, launched farmer education initiatives, and invested in large-scale projects like its ₹1,645 crore Technical Ammonium Nitrate (TAN) plant, expected to be completed by October 2025. Supported by a vast dealer network, strong marketing programs, and consistent product innovation, CFCL is positioning itself as a diversified agri-solutions provider.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Chambal Fertilisers & Chemicals Ltd is strategically enhancing its production capabilities to meet the growing demand for fertilisers and agrochemicals. The company is investing ₹1,645 crore in expanding its Technical Ammonium Nitrate (TAN) production capacity to 0.24 million metric tonnes per annum, with commissioning expected over the next 34 months.

Name: Paradeep Phosphates Ltd
Overview:
Paradeep Phosphates Limited, incorporated in 1981, is India’s second-largest private-sector phosphatic fertilizer manufacturer, offering a diverse portfolio of complex fertilizers, including DAP, multiple NPK grades (NPK-10, NPK-12, NP-20), Zypmite, phospho-gypsum, and hydrofluorosilicic acid. Its integrated operations span manufacturing, trading, and a vast distribution network across India.

The company successfully leveraged its integrated supply chain and strong market positioning to deliver industry-leading performance in FY 25. In FY 26, it is pursuing ambitious capacity expansion, aiming to substantially scale its production footprint and market share through strategic investments and consolidation efforts.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With favorable monsoon conditions, government support, and growing demand for phosphatic fertilizers, Paradeep Phosphates is well positioned for sustained growth. Analysts highlight backward integration into phosphoric acid, energy-efficiency initiatives, and higher-margin NPK products as key drivers for future earnings. JM Financial projects strong EBITDA and EPS growth through FY 28.

Name: Coromandel International Ltd
Overview:
Coromandel International Ltd is one of India's foremost agri-solutions companies, offering a comprehensive range of products across the agricultural value chain. Its core focus lies in fertilizers, crop protection, specialty nutrients, bio-pesticides, and emerging technologies like nano fertilizers and drones. The company commands a significant market share in the phosphatic fertilizer segment, particularly in the southern states of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, the largest markets for complex fertilizers in India.

Coromandel has diversified its operations into two key segments: Crop Nutrition (89%) and Crop Protection (11%), with domestic sales contributing to 95% of its revenue. It operates 760 retail outlets, serves over 3 million farmers, and exports to 80+ countries. With an expanding manufacturing base and strong backward integration efforts, the company continues to invest in future-ready infrastructure, new product development, and strategic acquisitions like Dhaksha Unmanned Systems to broaden its agritech footprint.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Coromandel International is expanding its agricultural solutions through a mix of capacity building, innovation, and strategic acquisitions. The company has doubled its sulphur fertilizer capacity at its Visakhapatnam unit, boosting supply reliability for farmers and reinforcing its specialty nutrients presenc. At its Kakinada site, it's ramping up backward integration with a phosphoric and sulphuric acid complex, alongside a new NPK granulation unit, setting the stage for stronger fertilizer outpu. It's also building innovation by partnering with IFDC to develop next-gen, eco-friendly fertilizers.

Name: Southern Petrochemicals Industries Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Southern Petrochemicals Industries Corporation Ltd, incorporated in 1944, is a well-established Indian company in the fertilizer industry. The company specializes in manufacturing and selling urea and nitrogenous chemical fertilizers, serving agricultural and industrial markets. SPIC is recognized for its long-standing experience and operational presence within India’s agrochemical sector.

The company’s core business is supported by a robust manufacturing infrastructure and distribution network, enabling consistent delivery of fertilizer products to its client base across diverse regions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
SPIC is expected to maintain consistent performance supported by steady agricultural demand and supportive government policies. The ability to sustain growth in FY26 hinges on pricing stability, production efficiency, and favorable policy environment—factors that position the company well for continued profitability in upcoming quarters.

Name: Fertilizers & Chemicals Travancore Ltd
Overview:
Fertilisers & Chemicals Travancore Ltd (FACT), established in 1943, is a central public sector enterprise headquartered in Kochi, Kerala. It operates under the administrative control of the Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India. FACT is engaged in manufacturing a diverse range of fertilizers, including complex fertilizers like FACTAMFOS, straight fertilizers such as ammonium sulfate, and specialty products like caprolactam.

The company operates two major production units: the Udyogamandal Complex and the Cochin Division. These facilities have a combined capacity to produce over 4.8 lakh tons per annum of complex fertilizers, along with significant quantities of sulfuric acid and phosphoric acid. Additionally, FACT's consultancy arm, FACT Engineering and Design Organisation (FEDO), provides engineering services in various sectors, including chemicals, petrochemicals, and pharmaceuticals.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, FACT aims to leverage its established infrastructure and product portfolio to capitalize on the growing demand for fertilizers and chemicals in India. The company's strategic focus includes enhancing production capacities, expanding its product range, and exploring new markets to drive sustainable growth.

Name: Madras Fertilizers Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1966, Madras Fertilizers Limited (MFL) is engaged in the manufacture and marketing of fertilizers and agro-inputs under its flagship brand ‘Vijay’. Its core portfolio includes Urea, NPK complex fertilizers, bio-fertilizers, neem-based products, and organic manures. The company also trades in eco-friendly agrochemicals and city compost, catering to both retail and institutional farmers across India.

MFL operates with a strong manufacturing base, with installed capacities of 3,46,500 MT of Ammonia, 4,86,750 MT of Urea, 2,80,000 MT of NPK fertilizers, and 400 MT of biofertilizers annually. Its operations are supported by subsidy income from the government, which forms the bulk of revenues, alongside direct product sales. The company is also pursuing energy efficiency and diversification initiatives, including a gas-based power project and expansion into new agri-input segments.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Outlook
Management emphasized product diversification as a future growth driver, with two new projects under technical and financial feasibility studies in advanced agri-inputs and specialty fertilizers. The company expects to maintain steady volumes in FY26, supported by higher farm demand, stable gas supplies, and government subsidy backing.

Name: Rashtriya Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd
Overview:
Rashtriya Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd. (RCF) is a public sector undertaking with 75% ownership by the Government of India. The company manufactures fertilizers and a broad range of industrial chemicals at its Thal and Trombay units. Its fertilizer portfolio includes urea, NPK, biofertilizers, and micronutrients under well-known brands like Ujwala, Suphala, Biola, and Microla, while its industrial division caters to sectors with chemicals such as nitric acid, ammonia, sulphuric acid, methylamines, and others.

The company has a strong market presence supported by over 5,800 dealers, with Maharashtra contributing more than half of revenues, followed by Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Telangana. In August 2023, RCF became the first PSU in the fertilizer sector to achieve Navratna CPSE status, underscoring its financial strength and strategic importance. Expansion initiatives, including new NPK capacity, nano urea production, and a coal-based fertilizer JV at Talcher, highlight its forward-focused growth strategy.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Outlook
RCF’s FY25 momentum reflects a pivot toward industrial chemicals and efficient trading while fertilizers faced margin pressure. Ongoing projects such as the nano urea plant at Trombay (₹238 crore), the new NPK plant at Thal (₹914.58 crore, subject to viability), and participation in the Talcher fertilizer complex (₹17,080 crore project) will expand capacity and improve diversification. As the first Navratna PSU in the fertilizer sector, RCF is strategically positioned to capture demand growth from both agriculture and industrial users, though fertilizer margin volatility remains a key monitorable.

Name: Gujarat State Fertilizers & Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1962, Gujarat State Fertilizers & Chemicals Ltd (GSFC) is a Government of Gujarat-promoted public sector enterprise engaged in manufacturing fertilizers and industrial products. The company’s fertilizer portfolio comprises Urea, Ammonium Sulphate, DAP, APS, NPK, and other traded products, while its industrial product offerings include Caprolactam, Nylon-6, Melamine, Methanol, and related intermediates.

GSFC is a market leader in the value chain of Caprolactam, Nylon-6 polymer, and its compounds, catering to automotive, consumer durables, and other industrial applications. With four manufacturing units across Vadodara, Surat, and Jamnagar, the company is strategically positioned to serve both domestic and export markets. It is also executing large-scale expansion projects, including green energy initiatives, to enhance capacity and efficiency.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management expects fertilizer volumes to remain stable or improve in FY26, driven by improved Sikka plant utilization and favorable monsoon conditions. DAP production is expected to run at full capacity with government loss-compensation support. Industrial products performance will hinge on Caprolactam market recovery, with HX crystal and ammonia trading expected to sustain margins. GSFC plans to leverage its strong balance sheet and aggressive capex program to expand capacity, optimize product mix, and align with government policy support, positioning itself for steady growth in both fertilizers and industrial segments.

Name: Kingfa Science & Technology (India) Ltd
Overview:
Kingfa Science & Technology (India) Limited is a subsidiary of Kingfa Science & Technology Co., Ltd., based in China. Established in 1983, the company specializes in manufacturing high-quality modified plastic compounds. These compounds cater to various industries, including automotive, consumer products, and personal protective equipment (PPE) such as masks and gloves.

The company operates with a strong emphasis on innovation and sustainability, producing materials that meet international standards. With a significant presence in the Indian market, Kingfa India plays a crucial role in the supply chain of several key sectors, contributing to the advancement of technology and safety standards in the country.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Kingfa India aims to further strengthen its position in the market by focusing on innovation and expanding its product range. The company plans to invest in research and development to enhance the performance and sustainability of its products.

Name: AGI Greenpac Ltd
Overview:
AGI Greenpac Ltd, established in 1960, is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in container glass bottles, PET bottles, and security caps and closures. The company is recognized as one of the top players in the organized glass packaging industry, holding a significant market share. With a focus on quality and innovation, AGI Greenpac caters to various sectors, including food and beverages, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics.

In recent years, AGI Greenpac has been actively investing in expanding its production capabilities and enhancing operational efficiencies. Strategic initiatives include capacity expansions and technological advancements aimed at improving product quality and reducing environmental impact. The company's commitment to sustainability and customer satisfaction positions it well for continued growth and leadership in the packaging industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
AGI Greenpac's strategic initiatives, including capacity expansions and technological advancements, are expected to drive continued growth and enhance its market position. The company's focus on sustainability and customer satisfaction positions it well for sustained success in the packaging industry.

Name: XPRO India Ltd
Overview:
XPRO India Ltd is a diversified, multi-locational company specializing in polymer processing, with a strong presence in the manufacturing of BOPP films, coextruded plastic sheets, and thermoformed components. Established in 1984, the company operates across multiple locations in India, catering to various industries including consumer electronics, automotive, and packaging.

With a focus on innovation and quality, XPRO India Ltd has developed a robust product portfolio and established long-term relationships with leading Indian and multinational companies, positioning itself as a key player in the polymer processing industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company aims to strengthen its market position by expanding product offerings, enhancing manufacturing capabilities, and focusing on sustainable growth. XPRO India Ltd is positioned to capitalize on increasing demand from key industries while maintaining quality and innovation standards.

Name: Jai Corp Ltd
Overview:
Jai Corp Ltd, established in 1985, is a diversified conglomerate engaged in manufacturing steel, plastic processing, and spinning yarn. The company also offers investment advisory services and is involved in the development of land and buildings. Jai Corp operates across multiple sectors, leveraging its manufacturing capabilities and strategic investments to drive growth.

The company has a significant presence in the Indian market and is expanding its footprint internationally. Jai Corp's diversified business model allows it to capitalize on opportunities across various industries, contributing to its resilience and long-term sustainability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Jai Corp aims to strengthen its position in the manufacturing sector by expanding production capacities and exploring new markets. The company plans to enhance its investment advisory services and capitalize on opportunities in real estate development. With a focus on innovation and strategic growth, Jai Corp is poised for continued success in the coming years.

Name: Uflex Ltd
Overview:
Uflex Ltd. is India's largest integrated flexible packaging company, offering a comprehensive range of packaging solutions across various sectors, including food, pharmaceuticals, and personal care. The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in India and internationally, with a strong focus on innovation and sustainability.

With a global presence and a diverse product portfolio, Uflex Ltd. has established itself as a leader in the flexible packaging industry, catering to the evolving needs of its customers worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Uflex Ltd. plans to enhance its production capacities and invest in technological advancements to meet the increasing demand for flexible packaging solutions. The company aims to capitalize on the growing trends in e-commerce, sustainable packaging, and automation to drive future growth. With a focus on operational excellence and customer satisfaction, Uflex Ltd. is well-positioned to maintain its leadership in the global flexible packaging industry.

Name: Supreme Industries Ltd
Overview:
Supreme Industries Limited is a leading Indian plastic products manufacturer with a wide portfolio spanning plastic piping systems, packaging, industrial components, furniture, and more. With 25 manufacturing units across 12 states and a distribution network of over 3,500 partners, the company serves multiple customer segments. Its strategic focus on high-margin Value Added Products (VAP) and expanding exports underpins its strong market position and growth ambition.
 
 Business Segments
 Plastic Piping Systems & Industrial Components: Major revenue driver
 
 Packaging Products: Significant share
 
 Furniture & Other Products: Complementary share
 
 Value Added Products (VAP): ~38% of total turnover
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Supreme Industries is set for consistent long-term growth through capacity expansion, export development, and a strong focus on high-margin value-added products. With 3 greenfield projects underway and increased capacity aiming for 1 million tonnes annually, the company is reinforcing its leadership in India’s plastic products market. Its capex strategy, pan-India network, and potential new market entries (e.g., Northeast India) position it for scalable, profitable growth across domestic and global markets.

Name: Polyplex Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Polyplex Corporation is a well-established manufacturer of BOPP, Blown PP/PE, and CPP films used in flexible packaging and industrial applications such as tapes, labels, and release liners. With over three decades of experience, the company operates through multiple manufacturing facilities across India and several countries, serving both domestic and global markets.

Its business is centered around reliable supply chains and extensive global reach, delivering essential films to packaging, electrical, and industrial sectors. The film products are critical for a variety of end uses, including lightweight packaging and specialty industrial materials.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Polyplex is gearing up for strong growth by significantly increasing its specialty film production and bolstering global capacity. The company is setting up a large, new BOPET film line in India—with capacity to boost output by over 52,000 tonnes annually—to meet growing demand in packaging and industrial segments. Overseas, it's expanded its U.S. operations with a major thin-film line and resin plant upgrade, making it the largest BOPET producer in that market.

Name: Garware Hi Tech Films Ltd
Overview:
Garware Hi-Tech Films Limited (formerly Garware Polyester Ltd), established in 1957, is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in polyester films. The company operates a vertically integrated "chip-to-film" manufacturing process, producing a wide range of specialty films, including solar control films, paint protection films, and high-end BOPET films for various industrial applications.

Garware Hi-Tech Films is recognized as the sole producer of solar control window films in India and holds a unique position globally with its backward integration capabilities. This integration allows the company to produce the raw materials and components needed for its films, ensuring quality control and cost efficiency.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Garware Hi-Tech Films is poised for continued growth, driven by its strategic focus on innovation and expansion into high-margin specialty films. The company's investment in research and development, coupled with its unique backward integration capabilities, positions it well to capitalize on emerging trends in the specialty films industry.

Name: Time Technoplast Ltd
Overview:
Time Technoplast Limited is a multinational manufacturing conglomerate engaged in technology-driven polymer and composite products. The company operates in diverse end-markets, including industrial packaging, mobility solutions, energy storage, and alternative fuels. Its offerings are spread across polymer-based and composite products, serving both domestic and international customers.

With operations in multiple countries, Time Technoplast has positioned itself as a strong player in industrial packaging and composite cylinders, while expanding into emerging areas like hydrogen solutions, energy storage, and sustainability-driven recycling initiatives. The company emphasizes innovation, capacity expansion, and green initiatives to strengthen its long-term competitiveness.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company expects sustained double-digit growth, with composites as the primary growth driver supported by capacity expansion and new product development. Management is targeting debt-free status within 18 months, even without a QIP. With a robust order pipeline, global footprint, sustainability initiatives, and strong balance sheet, Time Technoplast is well positioned for long-term growth and margin expansion.

Name: Shaily Engineering Plastics Ltd
Overview:
Shaily Engineering Plastics Ltd is engaged in the manufacture and sale of injection-moulded precision plastic components and sub-assemblies for Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs). The company serves sectors such as healthcare, consumer goods, beauty and personal care, lighting, appliances, and automotive, delivering high-quality, precision-engineered solutions.

Shaily Engineering Plastics Ltd also provides secondary operations, including vacuum metalizing, hot stamping, and ultrasonic welding. With manufacturing facilities in Savli and Halol, Vadodara, Gujarat, the company offers end-to-end solutions that combine functionality and aesthetic appeal for its clients.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company is driving future growth by enhancing its manufacturing footprint and scaling operations across its Gujarat facilities. It is investing in modernized automation technologies and production systems—including advanced injection molding and secondary processes—to boost precision and efficiency. By expanding capacity and integrating capabilities like vacuum metallizing, hot stamping, and ultrasonic welding, the company aims to cater more effectively to OEM demand.

Name: Jain Irrigation Systems Ltd
Overview:
Jain Irrigation Systems Limited (JISL) is a leading Indian multinational company specializing in integrated solutions for agriculture, piping, and infrastructure. The company manufactures a diverse range of products, including micro irrigation systems, PVC and HDPE pipes, agro-processed foods, renewable energy solutions, and tissue culture plants.

In recent years, JISL has focused on enhancing its core businesses, particularly in piping, hi-tech agriculture, and international markets. The company has also been actively involved in securing significant orders, such as the supply of solar pumps under government schemes, to bolster its renewable energy segment. Despite facing challenges like early monsoons affecting demand in certain segments, JISL continues to demonstrate resilience and adaptability in its operations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
JISL is expected to maintain growth momentum across key segments, including piping, solar solutions, and international markets. Improved margins and cash flow management position the company to capitalize on opportunities in agriculture and infrastructure.

Name: Jindal Poly Films Ltd
Overview:
Jindal Poly Films Limited, established in 1974, is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in biaxially oriented polyethylene terephthalate (BOPET) and biaxially oriented polypropylene (BOPP) films. 

As part of the B.C. Jindal Group, the company has expanded its product portfolio to include metalized films, coated films, and polypropylene-based nonwoven fabrics. These products serve various industries, including packaging, textiles, and electronics, both domestically and internationally.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Jindal Poly Films is focusing on expanding its production capacity to meet growing demand. The planned ₹250 crore investment in a new BOPP line is expected to enhance production capabilities and improve profitability. Additionally, the company's efforts to streamline operations and reduce costs are anticipated to positively impact margins in the coming quarters.

Name: Nilkamal Ltd
Overview:
Nilkamal Ltd, established in 1981, is a leading Indian manufacturer renowned for its extensive range of plastic products, including molded furniture, crates, and containers. The company has expanded its portfolio to offer complete home and office furniture solutions, encompassing various materials and upholstered products, under the brand Nilkamal Homes.

The company is recognized as the world's largest manufacturer of molded furniture and Asia's largest processor of plastic molded products.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Nilkamal Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its presence in both the B2B and retail segments. The company plans to enhance its product offerings and strengthen its distribution network to cater to the evolving needs of its customers.

Name: TCPL Packaging Ltd
Overview:
TCPL Packaging Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer of flexible packaging solutions, catering to FMCG, food, pharmaceutical, and industrial sectors. Headquartered in Mumbai, the company specializes in laminates, films, and customized packaging solutions.

The company emphasizes quality, technological innovation, and operational efficiency. Its strong domestic and international client base, coupled with robust supply chain, supports consistent growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

TCPL Packaging Ltd is focusing on expanding its manufacturing capabilities and enhancing its product offerings to capitalize on the growing demand for sustainable packaging solutions. The company has recently inaugurated a new packaging plant in Vengal, Chennai, as part of its strategy to strengthen its presence in South India and support long-term growth.

Name: EPL Ltd
Overview:
EPL Limited, formerly Essel Propack, is the world’s largest specialty packaging company with a global footprint in laminated plastic tubes serving beauty, cosmetics, pharma, food, oral, and home care industries. Backed by Blackstone since 2019, the company has built scale across multiple regions, leveraging innovation and operational efficiency.

With a diversified portfolio and presence in high-growth categories, EPL is focused on premiumization, sustainability, and global expansion. The company is also actively pursuing inorganic opportunities in beauty and cosmetics, while maintaining strong financial discipline and balance sheet strength.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management remains confident of sustaining double-digit revenue growth, further margin expansion, and superior ROCE. With beauty and cosmetics as the key growth driver, upcoming capacity in Thailand, and strong sustainability positioning, EPL is well-placed to deliver consistent growth and create long-term stakeholder value.

Name: GFL Ltd
Overview: GFL Ltd, also known as Gujarat Fluorochemicals Ltd, is an industrial refrigerant manufacturer in India and a leading producer of fluoropolymers and fluorospecialties. The company is part of the INOXGFL Group and has been in operation since 1987. GFL Ltd manufactures products such as polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), refrigerants, and specialty chemicals. The company has two manufacturing facilities in Gujarat, one for refrigerants and another for PTFE production. GFL Ltd serves various industries, including automotive, chemicals, and electronics, providing high-performance materials for diverse applications.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

GFL Ltd, a diversified holding company, is strategically enhancing its investment portfolio and expanding its distribution of investment products. The company holds significant stakes in key associates, including PVR INOX Limited, India's largest multiplex chain, and INOX Infrastructure Limited, which focuses on real estate and property development. Additionally, GFL Ltd is actively involved in the distribution of investment products, such as mutual funds, catering to the growing demand for financial products in India

Name: Gujarat Fluorochemicals Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat Fluorochemicals Ltd (GFL), formerly part of GFL Ltd and demerged into a standalone entity in 2018, is a flagship company of the INOX GFL Group. It is one of the top five global producers of fluoropolymers and also a leading manufacturer of fluorospecialities, refrigerants, and bulk chemicals. The company exports to key international markets, including Europe, the Americas, and Asia.

Its diversified portfolio spans segments such as PTFE-based fluoropolymers, agro-pharma focused fluorospecialities, HCFC-based refrigerants, and industrial bulk chemicals. With vertically integrated facilities in Dahej, Ranjitnagar, and Jolva (India), and a mining unit in Morocco, GFL supports global demand across strategic industries like semiconductors, pharma, automotive, and clean energy.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Gujarat Fluorochemicals is gearing up to power its future through major investments in new-age, high-value segments. Key plans include ramping up fluoropolymer capacities by multiples such as FKM, PVDF, and PFA to tap growing demand from industries like EV batteries, solar, and green hydrogen. The company has allocated a hefty capex totaling ₹1,600 crore, with a significant share directed towards its EV arm, while expanding hydrofluoric acid and refrigeration-grade R-32 capacity

Name: Navin Fluorine International Ltd
Overview:
Navin Fluorine International Ltd, part of the Padmanabh Mafatlal Group, is among India’s leading specialty fluorochemical manufacturers with over five decades of expertise. The company operates across three primary segments—High Performance Products (HPP), Specialty Chemicals, and Contract Development & Manufacturing Organisation (CDMO)—serving industries such as pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, refrigeration, and advanced materials. Its footprint spans both domestic and global markets, with a significant share of revenues coming from exports.

The company maintains an integrated business model, backed by state-of-the-art manufacturing in Surat, Dewas, and Dahej, along with strong in-house R&D capabilities. In FY25, it focused on scaling high-value product lines, backward integration, and capacity expansions in R32 refrigerants, high-end fluorine intermediates, and advanced materials, positioning itself for sustained long-term growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
The company anticipates continued double-digit growth in FY26, supported by full-scale contributions from new capacities, multi-year contracts in HPP and agrochemicals, and strong demand in Specialty Chemicals and CDMO. With robust order visibility and strategic global partnerships, NFIL is positioned to expand market share and sustain profitability improvement.

Name: Ellenbarrie Industrial Gases Ltd
Overview:
Ellenbarrie Industrial Gases Ltd (EIGL) is one of India's oldest and largest 100% Indian-owned industrial gas companies, with over 50 years of experience. The company manufactures a wide range of gases, including oxygen, nitrogen, argon, helium, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, nitrous oxide, and acetylene, catering to diverse industries such as shipbuilding, steel manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, welding, fabrication, and more.

The company has a strong presence in eastern and southern India, leading in states like West Bengal, Andhra Pradesh, and Telangana in terms of installed manufacturing capacity. EIGL is recognized for its robust distribution network, including a large fleet of cryogenic transport tankers and cylinders.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ellenbarrie Industrial Gases Ltd is focused on expanding its production capacity to meet the growing demand for industrial gases in India. The company plans to increase its capacity to 4,630 tons per day by 2027, with new plants in East and North India. This expansion strategy is supported by a strong in-house project engineering team and a multi-vendor procurement approach.

Name: Linde India Ltd
Overview:
Linde India Limited, a subsidiary of BOC Group UK, manufactures and supplies industrial and medical gases. The company operates mainly through two segments: Gas and Related Products and Healthcare. Its portfolio serves large industries like steel, glass, chemicals, fabrication, and hospitals with pipeline, liquefied, and compressed gases, plus advanced medical gas systems.
 
 Business Segments
 Gas & Related Products: Bulk and cylinder gases, supply to industrial clients via pipelines, tankers, and cylinders.
 
 Healthcare: Medical gases like oxygen, nitrous oxide; medical gas systems for hospitals.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Linde India is ramping up its future-ready strategy by expanding capacity and entering new industrial ecosystems: it’s in advanced talks to set up a plant in Dholera to serve the growing semiconductor sector; it has acquired additional air separation units that will boost its on-site production capacity by 37%, adding around 3,600 tonnes per day; and it has deployed a 250-tonne-per-day unit in Ludhiana to widen its distribution footprint—all measures that reinforce its intent to meet rising demand, diversify its infrastructure, and strengthen supply resilience across key industry verticals. Important figures include the 3,600 TPD capacity gain and the 250 TPD Ludhiana expansion.

Name: SRF Ltd
Overview:
SRF Limited is a diversified, multi-business company with a strong presence across chemicals, packaging films, technical textiles, and specialty coated and laminated fabrics. Its Chemicals Business, which includes fluorochemicals and specialty chemicals for agro and pharma industries, is a key growth driver. SRF also supplies flexible packaging solutions for FMCG and industrial sectors through its Packaging Films Business and serves the automotive and industrial sectors with technical textiles like tyre cord fabrics and industrial yarns. By focusing on innovation and expanding into emerging areas such as specialty fluoropolymers and clean technologies, SRF positions itself as a versatile player catering to multiple high-growth industries.
 
 Business Segments 
 
 Chemicals Business (Fluorochemicals & Specialty Chemicals): ~45–50% of total revenue
 
 Packaging Films: ~35–40%
 
 Technical Textiles: ~10–15%
 
 Other Businesses (Coated & Laminated Fabrics, Specialty Polymers): ~5%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

SRF is well-placed for long-term growth through its diversified operations in specialty chemicals, fluorochemicals, technical textiles, and packaging films. Ongoing investments in battery chemicals, PFAS-free materials, and solar-related products reflect a proactive pivot toward high-growth, sustainable markets. While short-term operational challenges persist in certain product lines, SRF’s strong R&D pipeline, export focus, and leadership across industrial segments make it a durable, innovation-led growth story.

Name: Tanfac Industries Ltd
Overview:
Tanfac Industries Ltd is an Indian chemical manufacturing company specializing in the production of specialty chemicals and fluorochemicals. The company serves various industries, including automotive, electronics, and pharmaceuticals.

In FY25, Tanfac Industries reported record financial results, driven by the successful commissioning of its new HF expansion plant and increased demand for its products.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Tanfac Industries plans to expand its product offerings and enhance its manufacturing capabilities. The company aims to increase its market share in the specialty chemicals industry and improve profitability through operational efficiencies.

Name: Chemfab Alkalis Ltd
Overview: Chemfab Alkalis Ltd is a leading manufacturer of industrial chemicals and specialty chemicals in India. The company produces caustic soda, chlorine, and allied chemicals catering to industries such as textiles, pharmaceuticals, paper, water treatment, and aluminum. Chemfab Alkalis Ltd emphasizes environmentally responsible manufacturing practices and maintains advanced facilities to ensure high-quality, consistent chemical products.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Chemfab Alkalis Ltd is actively expanding its production capabilities to meet the growing demand for its products. The company is increasing its Oriented PVC (OPVC) pipe production capacity at its Sri City facility from 20 tonnes per day to 65 tonnes per day, with plans to add three more production lines in the coming financial year. Additionally, Chemfab Alkalis has enhanced its caustic soda production capacity from 45,600 tonnes per annum to 56,575 tonnes per annum, reflecting its commitment to scaling operations.

Name: Epigral Ltd
Overview:
Epigral Limited (formerly Meghmani Finechem), incorporated in 2007, is a leading integrated Indian chemical manufacturer based at its Dahej complex, which is backward- and forward-integrated and automated. It pioneered epichlorohydrin production in India and hosts the largest CPVC resin capacity in the country. Its portfolio also includes caustic soda, caustic potash, chloromethanes, hydrogen peroxide, chlorine, and hydrogen.

Leveraging its integrated operations and high-capacity facilities, Epigral delivered record-breaking performance in FY 25 and is actively scaling up specialty and derivative chemical production. Its focus on downstream high-value products and efficiency gains continues to define its growth trajectory as it enters FY 26.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Epigral is confident of sustaining a 35–40% CAGR over the next two years, propelled by its growing derivatives & specialty business, high-value downstream product ramp-ups, and capacity additions. Margin guidance remains steady at around 25%, while energy cost savings and continued capital discipline are set to underpin future profitability.

Name: Stallion India Fluorochemicals Ltd
Overview:
Stallion India Fluorochemicals Ltd operates in the chemicals sector, focusing on fluorochemicals used across refrigerants, pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and specialty materials. The company plays a role in critical applications requiring high-performance chemical solutions.

Stallion India Fluorochemicals Ltd (SIFL) is a forward-integrated specialist in the processing, blending, and distribution of refrigerant and industrial gases—serving sectors such as electronics, air-conditioning, pharma, automotive, semiconductors, and firefighting. Operating multiple plants across Maharashtra, Rajasthan, and Haryana, the company provides niche, blended gas solutions (HCs, HFCs, HFOs), differentiating itself from base molecule producers. With over three decades of technical expertise, a robust distribution network, and recent strategic capacity expansions into semiconductor and specialty gases, Stallion is well-positioned to benefit from rising demand in high-growth applications. Its integrated operations and technology focus allow it to meet both domestic and global demand for fluorochemicals, an industry with high entry barriers.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
With its unique technical capabilities, expanding production capacity, and leadership in gas blending, Stallion is well-placed to capture growth in high-margin industrial applications. Its focus on diversification into semiconductor and specialty gas segments positions it for strong demand tailwinds. A robust distribution network and industry-specific offerings further reinforce the company’s long-term growth trajectory.

Name: Relaxo Footwears Ltd
Overview:
Relaxo Footwears Limited is India’s largest non-leather footwear manufacturer, operating in the value segment across multiple categories such as slippers, sports shoes, and sandals. With brands like Relaxo, Sparx, Flite, and Bahamas, the company holds a dominant position in the affordable footwear market and has expanded its footprint across both urban and rural India through a vast distribution network and retail presence.

The company maintains a strong manufacturing infrastructure with nine facilities producing ~30 crore pairs annually. It continues to modernize its supply chain and retail systems with initiatives like mobile outreach, lean manufacturing, and a revamped digital strategy. Relaxo is aiming to grow its EBO footprint, expand exports, and drive double-digit revenue growth in FY25, despite challenges such as rising labor costs.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Relaxo Footwears is strategically expanding its manufacturing capabilities and product offerings to meet the growing demand for affordable and quality footwear. The company is constructing a new plant in Tamil Nadu to increase production capacity and enhance distribution efficiency. Additionally, Relaxo has launched a new line of eco-friendly footwear made from recycled materials, aligning with sustainability trends and catering to environmentally conscious consumers

Name: Sreeleathers Ltd
Overview:
Sreeleathers Ltd is engaged in the retail and distribution of leather and footwear products. The company operates through a network of exclusive showrooms and franchise outlets, offering affordable and durable footwear to mass-market consumers.

With its strong brand presence in eastern India, Sreeleathers continues to expand its retail footprint while maintaining a focus on affordability and volume-driven growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Sreeleathers plans to strengthen its national presence by entering newer markets in central and western India. The company is investing in expanding both physical outlets and online sales channels to capture evolving consumer demand.
