Narayana Hrudayalaya Ltd is a leading healthcare provider in India, offering affordable and high-quality medical services through a network of multispecialty and super-specialty hospitals, clinics, and heart centers. The company operates 40 healthcare facilities, including two in the Cayman Islands, with a total of 5,563 operational beds. Its expertise spans cardiac sciences, oncology, medicine and GI sciences, renal sciences, neurosciences, orthopedics, and other specialties.

The hospital chain has a strong presence across India, with significant clusters in Bangalore, Kolkata, Southern Peripheral regions, and emerging markets such as Guwahati, Raipur, and Mysore. It also maintains an international footprint through its Cayman Islands operations. The company serves a diverse patient base through domestic walk-ins, insured patients, government healthcare schemes, and international clientele.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management remains focused on scaling both domestic and international operations while maintaining strong profitability. Expansion into flagship clusters, adoption of advanced medical technologies, and strengthening digital health platforms are expected to drive growth. The company’s greenfield and brownfield projects, along with its sustainability commitments, position it for long-term operational efficiency and market leadership in affordable healthcare.

Name: Max Healthcare Institute Ltd
Overview:
Max Healthcare Institute Ltd. is India’s second-largest hospital chain, providing secondary and tertiary healthcare services across multiple specialities like oncology, cardiac sciences, neurosciences, orthopaedics, and advanced surgeries. The company also operates diagnostics under Max Lab and home healthcare under Max@Home, strengthening its integrated healthcare model.
 
 Business Segments 
 
 Hospital Services (In-patient & Out-patient): ~85% of total revenue
 
 Diagnostics (Max Lab): ~8% of total revenue
 
 Home Healthcare (Max@Home) & Other Services: ~7% of total revenue
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Max Healthcare is on a robust expansion path with plans to add 2,600+ beds in high-demand metro regions through greenfield and brownfield projects. It’s investing in cutting-edge technologies, building its diagnostics business, and focusing on asset-light models for faster scale.

Name: Rainbow Childrens Medicare Ltd
Overview:
Rainbow Children’s Medicare Limited (RCML) is India’s largest pediatric hospital chain, operating 19 hospitals and 5 outpatient clinics across 6 cities with a total capacity of 1,935 beds. It specializes in pediatric care under the “Rainbow Children’s Hospital” brand and women’s healthcare under “BirthRight by Rainbow.” The company offers comprehensive services including newborn and pediatric intensive care, multi-specialty and quaternary pediatric care, obstetrics, gynecology, and fertility treatment.

RCML follows a hub-and-spoke model with regional spokes located within 200–250 km of city hubs, enabling high-quality pediatric and women’s healthcare access in Tier-II cities while linking them to advanced care at hub hospitals. With several NABH- and JCI-accredited facilities, the company combines infrastructure quality with a focus on specialty depth, critical care capabilities, and asset-light expansion in key growth markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
RCML is positioned to drive volume growth through capacity expansion and specialty care penetration. The asset-light expansion model, combined with its hub-and-spoke network and high-acuity service mix, is expected to sustain revenue growth while maintaining margins. The IVF vertical, expanded to 11 hospitals, is poised to enhance revenue diversification. The company’s strategy remains focused on building regional dominance in pediatric and women’s healthcare, leveraging its balance sheet strength for self-funded growth without debt reliance.

Name: Poly Medicure Ltd
Overview:
Poly Medicure Limited is a leading Indian medical devices manufacturer and exporter, specializing in plastic medical disposables across 12 specialties such as infusion therapy, oncology, renal care, and critical care. The company operates with a diversified global presence, serving customers in over 125 countries through a robust network of distributors and sales associates. It maintains a backward-integrated manufacturing setup with 12 operational plants across four countries and an annual capacity of over 1.8 billion devices.

The company continues to strengthen its innovation-led growth, supported by a global patent portfolio of 334 patents and targeted R&D investments. With a zero net-debt position, strong liquidity of ₹1,248.6 Cr as of Q1 FY26, and ongoing capacity expansion in key therapeutic areas, Poly Medicure is positioned for long-term sustainable growth in both domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company aims to grow its market share from 5-6% to 10% in the next 3-4 years by expanding its direct global presence, strengthening its portfolio in cardiology, critical care, oncology, and renal care, and leveraging AI and IoT for manufacturing efficiency. With strong liquidity, expanding manufacturing capabilities, and deepening domestic penetration, Poly Medicure targets sustainable double-digit growth while maintaining EBITDA margins in the 25-27% range for FY26.

Name: Dr Lal Pathlabs Ltd
Overview:
Dr. Lal PathLabs Limited (DLPL) is one of India’s foremost consumer healthcare brands in the diagnostic services sector. The company offers a comprehensive catalogue of over 3,000 pathology and radiology tests, backed by a nationwide network of 298 clinical labs, 6,607 patient service centers (PSCs), and 12,365 pick-up points (PUPs). With 75+ years of legacy, it has served over 131 million patients in the last five years, reinforcing its dominance across diagnostics ranging from preventive screenings to specialized genomics and molecular biology tests.

The company’s operational model is centralized, scalable, and technology-enabled, allowing it to efficiently expand into Tier 3 and Tier 4 cities. DLPL has achieved significant milestones, including being the first in South Asia to offer Amyloid Typing via mass spectrometry and launching “Reco.ai,” a recommendation engine for test suggestions. The firm also runs advanced testing divisions under brands like Genevolve and L-CoRD. Its international footprint extends to Nepal, Bangladesh, and multiple countries across Africa and the Middle East through sample referrals.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Dr. Lal PathLabs is strategically expanding its presence in India's Tier 3 and Tier 4 cities, aiming to enhance accessibility to quality diagnostic services in underserved regions. The company plans to add more laboratories and patient service centers to its network, reinforcing its commitment to reaching a broader patient base. Additionally, Dr. Lal PathLabs is focusing on diversifying its service offerings, including exploring opportunities in radiology, to cater to the evolving healthcare needs of the population.

Name: Dr Agarwals Health Care Ltd
Overview:
Dr Agarwal’s Health Care Ltd, widely recognized as Dr Agarwal’s Eye Hospital, is a leading integrated eye-care provider in India with a legacy spanning over six decades. The company has built a strong reputation for delivering high-quality ophthalmic services, ranging from advanced surgical procedures like cataract, LASIK, and corneal transplants to diagnostics, consultations, and optical solutions. It complements its medical services with an ecosystem that includes in-house pharmacies and optical retail outlets, ensuring a comprehensive patient experience under one umbrella.

The group operates one of the most expansive eye-care networks in India, with nearly 200 centers spread across multiple states, while also maintaining a growing presence in Africa with facilities across nine countries. Its business model is based on a scalable hub-and-spoke approach, where central hubs handle complex surgeries and advanced treatments, while smaller satellite centers extend reach into underserved markets. This structure not only optimizes operational efficiency but also allows rapid expansion into Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company is expected to deepen its footprint by entering new cities, expanding its African presence, and leveraging technology such as tele-ophthalmology for remote consultations. With its strong reputation, scalable infrastructure, and rising demand for specialized eye care, Dr Agarwal’s is well positioned to maintain leadership in the evolving healthcare ecosystem.

Name: Healthcare Global Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
Healthcare Global Enterprises Ltd. (HCG) is a leading provider of specialized healthcare services in India, focusing primarily on oncology and fertility treatments. Established in 2005, the company operates a network of cancer care centers across the country, offering comprehensive services that include diagnostics, treatment, and patient care. HCG is recognized for its commitment to clinical excellence and innovative treatment modalities.

In addition to its core oncology services, HCG has expanded its offerings to include fertility treatments, making it a prominent player in the fertility healthcare sector. The company's integrated approach combines advanced medical technologies with a patient-centric care model, aiming to provide accessible and high-quality healthcare services to a diverse patient base.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
HCG is focusing on expanding its footprint in underserved markets and enhancing its service offerings through technological advancements. The company aims to improve its profitability by optimizing operational efficiencies and leveraging its established brand in oncology and fertility services. The future outlook remains positive, with expectations of sustained growth driven by strategic expansions and a commitment to clinical excellence.

Name: Thyrocare Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Thyrocare Technologies Ltd is a leading independent diagnostic services provider in India, specializing in high-throughput pathology and preventive health testing. It operates one of the largest centralized automated laboratories using a franchise- and partner-led network to reach consumers across India and internationally.

Founded in 1996, the company leverages technology-driven efficiency, affordable testing options, and expansive coverage—serving millions of patients annually through both diagnostic testing and imaging services.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Thyrocare is primed for continued growth through its tech-driven, asset-light franchise model and strong margin profile. Expansion of its lab network, increased test volumes, and cost-conscious operations position it well for steady growth in FY26, bolstered by healthy demand for preventive healthcare solutions.

Name: Cupid Ltd
Overview:
Cupid Limited, founded in 1993, stands as a foremost manufacturer of male and female condoms, personal lubricants, and IVD (In Vitro Diagnostic) kits in India. The company also offers a broader portfolio of personal care and FMCG products, reflecting its leadership position in safe and hygiene-focused consumer goods.

Leveraging a robust manufacturing foundation and diverse product range, the company caters to both domestic and international markets. Its operations span B2B exports and B2C channels, supported by strategic certifications and an expanding retail reach.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Cupid Limited is setting the stage for solid future growth through a mix of smart expansion and stronger market access. The company is boosting production capacity by increasing it by 1.5 times after acquiring new land near Mumbai—so it can make far more condoms in-house. To make buying easier, Cupid has launched a large new warehouse in Mumbai and is getting its products into twice as many stores in India. Online shopping is also a big focus, with its full product lineup now available on platforms like Amazon, Flipkart, and 1mg

Name: Kovai Medical Center & Hospital Ltd
Overview:
Kovai Medical Center & Hospital Ltd. is a Coimbatore-based healthcare provider offering advanced hospital and diagnostic services. The company further diversified into education in 2019 with the launch of its medical college, strengthening its presence in the healthcare ecosystem.

The business continues to deliver consistent operational performance, with hospitals being its primary growth driver while education provides an additional long-term revenue stream. Together, these segments create a balanced model of service delivery and future expansion potential.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Building on its robust performance and diversified model, Kovai Medical is well-positioned for continued growth. The blend of hospital operations, diagnostics, and education creates a resilient business structure. With strategic expansions on the horizon and an integrated talent-development model, Kovai Medical is poised to strengthen its leadership in regional healthcare delivery and sustain its upward trajectory.

Name: Nephrocare Health Services Ltd
Overview:
Nephrocare Health Services Ltd is a healthcare company that provides comprehensive dialysis care through an extensive network of clinics across India and select international markets. The company’s services include diagnosis, haemodialysis, home and mobile dialysis, wellness programmes and in-house pharmacy support, catering to patients with chronic kidney disease and renal disorders. 

In addition to its domestic footprint, Nephrocare has expanded internationally with clinics in countries such as the Philippines, Uzbekistan and Nepal, and runs what is described as one of the world’s largest dialysis centres. Its presence across a wide geographical area helps it serve a diverse patient base while partnering with major hospitals to offer integrated kidney care.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Nephrocare Health Services aims to grow by opening more dialysis clinics in underserved regions to meet rising demand for renal care and increase accessibility across broader geographies. The company plans to expand its service offerings by enhancing home, mobile and wellness-oriented care options while maintaining high-quality clinical standards. It also intends to strengthen partnerships with hospitals and healthcare providers to integrate in-house dialysis services.

Name: Jupiter Life Line Hospitals Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 2007, Jupiter Life Line Hospitals Limited is a multi-specialty tertiary and quaternary healthcare provider operating in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR) and the western region of India. The company manages three hub-model hospitals in Thane, Pune, and Indore, with a combined operational capacity of 1,061 census beds and 1,275 doctors. Each facility operates independently, equipped with comprehensive specialties including cardiology, oncology, orthopaedics, neurology, nephrology, and organ transplants, catering to both inpatient and outpatient needs.

The group is actively expanding its capacity through greenfield projects in Dombivli, Pune II (Bibwewadi), and Mira-Bhayandar, expected to add around 1,440 beds and nearly double total capacity to 2,500 beds. FY25 saw incremental capacity additions with new beds in Pune and Indore, alongside ICU expansion. Backed by its IPO proceeds of ₹869 crores, including ₹542 crores in fresh issuance, the company is funding growth, debt repayment, and corporate purposes while positioning itself for a stronger regional presence.
Key Takeways:
Strategic and Financial Positioning
The company’s hub-only model ensures each hospital operates as a self-sufficient unit, enabling high specialization and independence in operations. IPO proceeds are being deployed towards debt repayment and funding expansion. The growth strategy focuses on deepening market presence in MMR and western India, with significant capacity addition set to drive scale, patient inflows, and revenue growth in the coming years.

Name: Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences Ltd
Overview:
Krishna Medical Institutions Ltd. (KIMS), founded in 1973, is among the largest corporate healthcare groups in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, offering a wide spectrum of primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary care services. With over 40 specialties and super-specialties, KIMS operates a network of hospitals strategically located across Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 cities, with a strong regional dominance in South India. The company emphasizes affordability while maintaining advanced clinical capabilities, ensuring it caters to both urban and semi-urban patient segments.

The group has a robust hospital portfolio of 4,859 total beds (4,342 operational) spread across Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Kerala. It is aggressively expanding, with 2,000–2,500 incremental beds expected over the next two years, effectively doubling capacity. KIMS has also adopted a cluster-based model, driving high occupancy and profitability in its home markets while making strategic forays into new geographies like Bengaluru, Thane, and Anantapur.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
KIMS aims to double its bed capacity within two years, leveraging its strong regional presence to enter high-growth urban markets while maintaining profitability through cluster synergies. The mix of high ARPOB specialties and strategic location choices is expected to drive sustained revenue and EBITDA growth in the medium term.

Name: Yatharth Hospital & Trauma Care Services Ltd
Overview:
Yatharth Hospital & Trauma Care Services Ltd is a prominent healthcare provider in the National Capital Region (NCR) of India. The company operates multi-specialty hospitals offering a wide range of medical services, including trauma care, critical care, cardiology, neurology, and orthopedics.

With a commitment to providing high-quality healthcare, Yatharth Hospital has expanded its presence across NCR, aiming to meet the growing demand for medical services in the region. The company's focus on patient care and technological advancements has established it as a trusted name in the healthcare industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company aims to expand its bed capacity to around 3,000 by FY28, add new specialties, and enhance operational efficiency. Strategic initiatives focus on market expansion and sustained profitability, positioning Yatharth Hospital for long-term growth in the competitive healthcare sector.

Name: Metropolis Healthcare Ltd
Overview:
Metropolis Healthcare Limited is one of India’s largest diagnostic companies, ranked second nationally and the leading player in Western and Southern India. The company operates a wide network of diagnostic laboratories and collection centers across India and has a growing international footprint spanning South Asia, Africa, and the Middle East. Its business model is predominantly asset-light, with the majority of its service network operated under a low-capex, lease-based format.

In FY25, Metropolis executed an aggressive “string of pearls” inorganic growth strategy, acquiring Core Diagnostics (oncogenomics leader), Scientific Pathology (Agra), and Dr. Ahujas’ Pathology & Imaging Center (Dehradun). These acquisitions are expected to lift North India’s revenue contribution from 8% to ~14–15% in FY26, while reinforcing its B2C focus and market penetration in underrepresented geographies. Alongside acquisitions, the company added 29 labs and over 400 collection centers during the year, expanding its reach to 750 towns nationwide.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
For FY26, management guides for ~12% organic revenue growth (7% volume, 5% realization) and an additional 13–14% from acquisitions, totaling ~25–26% reported growth. Organic EBITDA margins are expected to improve by ~100 bps to ~25.4%, supported by cost rationalization, automation, and maturing of new labs. Capex is pegged at ₹50–55 cr, with a focus on collection center expansion rather than large lab rollouts. Integration of recent acquisitions, continued B2C mix improvement, and genomics expansion are key levers for sustaining growth and margin gains.

Name: Vijaya Diagnostic Centre Ltd
Overview:
Vijaya Diagnostics Centre Limited is a leading integrated diagnostic services provider in Southern India, offering a wide range of services including nuclear medicine, radiology, laboratory testing, preventive health check-ups, and other medical services. The company operates through a network of diagnostic centers and reference laboratories, delivering high-quality, reliable, and timely results to its patients.

Its operations are built on a hub-and-spoke model that enables scalability, cost efficiency, and service consistency across locations. With a strong focus on the B2C segment, Vijaya Diagnostics emphasizes brand trust, quality assurance, and customer-centric service, positioning itself as a preferred choice in both core and new geographies.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects 15% revenue growth in FY26, supported by ~13% volume growth. The focus remains on expanding the network, scaling the B2C model, leveraging advanced radiology for margin enhancement, and exploring acquisitions. Structural industry tailwinds such as growing health awareness, preference for branded diagnostics, and wellness uptake are expected to support long-term growth.

Name: Dr Agarwals Health Care Ltd
Overview:
Dr. Agarwal’s Health Care Ltd, established in 2010, is a leading provider of eye care services in India. The company offers a comprehensive range of services, including cataract and refractive surgeries, consultations, diagnoses, and non-surgical treatments. Additionally, they provide optical products, contact lenses, accessories, and pharmaceutical items related to eye care. Dr. Agarwal’s Health Care operates a network of eye hospitals across the country, delivering advanced medical care to a diverse patient base.

The company has been at the forefront of innovation in the ophthalmology sector, implementing cutting-edge technologies and treatment methodologies. With a strong commitment to quality and patient satisfaction, Dr. Agarwal’s Health Care has established itself as a trusted name in eye care, continuously expanding its reach to serve communities nationwide.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Dr. Agarwal’s Health Care is focused on expanding its network of eye hospitals to enhance accessibility to quality eye care services. The company plans to increase its center count significantly in the coming years, aiming to broaden its presence in both urban and rural areas. Investments in advanced medical technologies and infrastructure are expected to improve patient outcomes and operational efficiency.

Name: Entero Healthcare Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Entero Healthcare Solutions Ltd., established in 2018, is a prominent healthcare supply chain solutions specialist in India. The company operates as a B2B distributor, offering demand fulfillment and generation services to manufacturers. Its extensive network encompasses over 25,500 manufacturers and supplies to healthcare institutions and retail pharmacies.

Since its inception, Entero has completed over 45 acquisitions, integrated more than 100 warehouses, and reached a customer base of over 86,000 pharmacies and 3,500 hospitals. Entero continues to expand its geographic footprint and product segments, delivering better buying experiences to customers through technology-led solutions and efficient infrastructure .
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Entero is on track to deliver a 30% revenue growth including both organic and inorganic growth. The company aims to surpass a 4% full-year EBITDA margin in FY26, targeting over 30% annual growth . Entero's focus on consolidating the fragmented healthcare supply chain through organic expansion and disciplined mergers and acquisitions positions it for continued growth and market leadership.

Name: Park Medi World Ltd
Overview:
Park Medi World Limited is a private healthcare services company that operates a network of multi-super-speciality hospitals under the “Park” brand across several states in North India, including Haryana, Delhi, Punjab and Rajasthan. 

The company’s hospitals are accredited by the National Accreditation Board for Hospitals & Healthcare Providers (NABH) and offer a wide range of medical specialities such as internal medicine, cardiology, neurology, gastroenterology, orthopaedics and oncology, supported by trained doctors, nurses and clinical staff. Using a cluster-based approach, Park Medi World has grown its footprint by establishing and acquiring hospitals to provide accessible and high-quality care, focusing on both general and specialised treatments.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Park Medi World plans to expand its hospital network further by opening new facilities and enhancing existing ones to reach more patients in underserved and high-growth regions across North India. It aims to invest in modern medical equipment, advanced treatment capabilities and specialised healthcare services to attract a broader patient base. The company is focusing on improving operational efficiencies, strengthening its clinical teams, and increasing bed capacity to support higher patient throughput.

Name: TBO Tek Ltd
Overview:
TBO Tek Ltd, incorporated in 2006, is part of the TBO Group and operates as a global travel distribution platform, primarily serving the B2B segment. Through its flagship portal Travelboutiqueonline.com, it aggregates and distributes travel inventory sourced from airlines, hotels, and other suppliers to travel agents worldwide. The company is an IATA-registered ticketing agency and generates the bulk of its revenues from airline and hotel reservations.

The company services over 20,000 travel agents, with tie-ups with 7 lakh+ hotels across more than 47 countries, and has over 45,000 annual transacting buyers. Its operational footprint includes India, Dubai, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Qatar, Brazil, Egypt, China, and the USA. TBO Tek has also expanded its service offerings through platforms like TBO Academy, TBO+, Paxes, and Travel Partner Solutions, catering to niche travel needs, corporate bookings, and technology-enabled solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
With increasing overseas acquisitions, a robust domestic base, and expanding tech-enabled travel solutions, TBO Tek aims to deepen penetration in emerging markets and enhance cross-border B2B travel trade. The integration of its recent acquisitions is expected to accelerate revenue diversification and improve margins through scale benefits.

Name: India Tourism Development Corporation Ltd
Overview:
India Tourism Development Corporation (ITDC), a Public Sector Undertaking established in 1966 under the Ministry of Tourism, India, operates across hospitality, travel, and tourism services. Its diversified portfolio includes hotels and restaurants, transport services, duty-free retail, event management, travel and ticketing, publicity, consultancy, and tourism education.

Leveraging its integrated offerings and strategic alignment with national tourism initiatives, ITDC has advanced its service quality, infrastructure, and experience-driven products. In FY 25, the company combined stable legacy operations with expansion into event tourism and customized hospitality solutions, setting the stage for continued growth into FY 26.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

India Tourism Development Corporation (ITDC), a public sector enterprise under the Ministry of Tourism, is actively pursuing expansion across its diverse business verticals. The corporation is enhancing its hospitality offerings by renovating and upgrading properties such as The Ashok and Hotel Samrat, aiming to provide world-class amenities and services. ITDC is also focusing on diversifying its tourism services, including travel and tour operations, event management, and duty-free retail, to cater to the evolving needs of domestic and international tourists.

Name: EIH Ltd
Overview:
EIH Limited, the flagship company of the Oberoi Group, is a pioneer in the Indian luxury hospitality space. It operates high-end hotels and cruisers under the "The Oberoi", "Trident", and "Maidens" brands, with a total of 3,708 domestic keys and 497 international keys. The company also engages in flight catering, airport F&B, project management, and corporate air charters, positioning itself as a multi-vertical hospitality player.

EIH continues to pursue an asset-light strategy, signing management contracts and joint ventures to expand without over-leveraging its balance sheet. The company reported zero debt as of FY25 and maintained a cash surplus of ₹711 Cr. An ambitious expansion pipeline of 21 new properties (1,473 keys) is underway, planned through 2029, spanning India, Europe, Middle East, and Southeast Asia.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

EIH Limited, the flagship company of The Oberoi Group, is strategically expanding its luxury hospitality footprint both domestically and internationally. The company has announced the addition of four new hotel projects in India, including The Oberoi, Gir (a 20-key wildlife retreat near Gir National Park), The Oberoi, Hyderabad (a 220-key luxury hotel), Trident, Nandi Hills (a 150-key hotel in Bengaluru), and Trident, Fort Aguada (a 170-key property in Goa).

Name: Ventive Hospitality Ltd
Overview:
Ventive Hospitality Ltd operates in the hospitality sector, offering a portfolio of hotels, resorts, and allied services catering to leisure and business travelers. The company focuses on mid-scale and premium categories, blending affordability with quality guest experiences.

With India’s rising travel demand and growth of domestic tourism, Ventive has positioned itself to capture opportunities in urban centers as well as emerging tourist hubs. Its emphasis on customer-centric operations strengthens brand loyalty and occupancy rates.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ventive Hospitality is poised for significant expansion, focusing on enhancing its luxury hospitality offerings and strategic developments. The company has plans to develop a new hotel in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, aiming to tap into the growing tourism and business travel markets in the region. Ventive's commitment to excellence is reflected in its partnerships with globally recognized hospitality brands, ensuring high standards and consistent service quality across its properties.

Name: Le Travenues Technology Ltd
Overview:
Le Travenues Technology Ltd, incorporated in 2007, is India’s second-largest technology-driven online travel agency, operating brands such as ixigo, ConfirmTkt, and AbhiBus. The company provides comprehensive travel planning, booking, and management solutions for trains, flights, buses, and hotels, backed by proprietary algorithms and AI-led platforms.

With a deep presence in Tier 2/3 cities and a growing user base, the company focuses on organic, product-led growth and cross-platform synergies. Its tech-first model emphasizes AI integration, operational efficiency, and customer experience, enabling sustainable market share gains across travel segments.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects faster-than-market growth across all segments, supported by underpenetrated categories, AI-led efficiency, and deep Tier 2/3 market reach. While margins may be reinvested into new verticals, the company aims to sustain strong contribution margins and leverage operational efficiency for long-term profitability.

Name: Rategain Travel Technologies Ltd
Overview:
RateGain Travel Technologies Ltd is a leading global distribution technology provider and India’s largest Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) player in the travel and hospitality industry. The company operates across multiple verticals, including hotels, airlines, online travel agencies, car rentals, cruise lines, and vacation rentals, offering integrated products to enhance guest acquisition, engagement, and retention.

Its business spans the full commercial value chain—sales, marketing, distribution, and revenue management—through three main segments: Data-as-a-Service (DaaS), Distribution, and Marketing Technology (MarTech). The company’s strong client base includes top global OTAs, hotel chains, airlines, and car rental companies, underpinned by high customer retention and consistent contract wins.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
RateGain is focused on scaling its integrated AI-powered platform to capture a larger share of the $500–700 billion hotel booking industry. Strategic priorities include expanding the transaction business, strengthening presence in high-growth geographies, and pursuing disciplined M&A. The medium-term goal is to achieve double-digit organic growth by FY27, supported by sustained innovation and operational leverage.

Name: ITC Hotels Ltd
Overview:
ITC Hotels Ltd is a standalone hospitality company that was carved out from its parent conglomerate to focus purely on running and managing hotel properties. It operates a multi-brand portfolio under names such as ITC Hotels, WelcomHotel, Fortune, WelcomHeritage, Mementos, and Storii, offering luxury, upscale, and heritage stays across many locations. The business model combines ownership of some high-end properties with a larger share of properties under management and franchise agreements, allowing it to scale more efficiently and flexibly.

The company emphasizes high standards of service, iconic culinary experiences, and strong brand value, while also aligning with sustainable practices in hotel operations. After its demerger, it has become more asset-light, meaning it is focussing more on managing or franchising hotels rather than owning all of them, to reduce capital intensity and improve returns.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

ITC Hotels plans to rapidly increase its hotel and room inventory, targeting over 220 properties with more than 20,000 rooms by 2030, largely through management and franchise contracts to keep capital costs lower. It is pushing an asset-right strategy, partnering with property owners for managed operations instead of owning all real estate itself. A sizeable portion of its growth will come from brownfield projects (upgrading or converting existing hotels) rather than building entirely new ones.

Name: Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd
Overview:
Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd is India’s largest mid-priced and the third largest overall hotel chain, operating across upscale, upper-midscale, midscale, and economy segments. Its portfolio spans multiple brands, including Aurika, Lemon Tree, and Red Fox, offering differentiated hospitality experiences with a strong value-for-money proposition.

The company has expanded its footprint to over 50 locations in India and select international markets like Bhutan, Nepal, and Dubai. With a mix of owned, managed, and franchised properties, it is strategically pursuing an asset-light growth model supported by brand strength, operational efficiency, and targeted renovations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Lemon Tree is targeting EBITDA margins above 50% post-renovation, with potential to approach 55% by FY28. Growth will be driven by aggressive expansion into Tier II/III cities, retail demand growth through loyalty and direct channels, and asset-light managed portfolio scaling. The company is well-positioned to benefit from India’s hospitality demand upcycle and rising domestic travel spend.

Name: Schloss Bangalore Ltd
Overview:
Schloss Bangalore Ltd—owner and operator of The Leela luxury hotel chain—is a premium hospitality player managing iconic destinations across India. With a portfolio of 12 luxury properties and plans for a signature 250-room addition in Mumbai’s BKC, the company is known for world-class service, upscale experiences, and strategic city-center and resort locations. Backed by Brookfield, Schloss combines high-end branding with institutional strength.

Its operations have seen revenue and room metrics rebound sharply post-pandemic, reflecting robust demand in both corporate travel and upscale tourism segments.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
The upcoming BKC project signals long-term expansion and stronger presence in Mumbai’s luxury market. Continued focus on service innovation and destination expansion could further elevate its position in the premium hospitality sector.

Name: Indian Hotels Co Ltd
Overview:
The Indian Hotels Company Ltd (IHCL), part of the Tata Group, is India’s largest hospitality company with a strong portfolio of 180+ hotels (21,000+ rooms) across 12 countries and 100+ cities. It operates iconic brands like Taj, Vivanta, SeleQtions, and Ginger, catering to luxury, upscale, and midscale segments, alongside new ventures in gourmet delivery, homestays, and airline catering.
 
 Business Segments
 
 Rooms, Food & Beverage, Banquets: ~87% of total revenue
 
 Management Fees, Membership Fees, Shop Rentals: ~13% of total revenue
 
 Geographic Split: India ~81% | Overseas ~19%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Indian Hotels Company Ltd. (IHCL) is executing an aggressive growth strategy with 62 hotels (~8,000 rooms) in the pipeline and a target of 300 hotels and 500 homestays by 2025. Its balanced asset model, backed by the Tata Group, supports scalability and returns. Diversification into gourmet delivery (Qmin), airline catering, and international expansion reinforces earnings resilience. With premium positioning via the Taj brand and expanding midscale offerings, IHCL is on track for strong growth and market leadership.

Name: Chalet Hotels Ltd
Overview:
Chalet Hotels Limited, part of the K Raheja Corp group, operates in hospitality, commercial leasing, and real estate development. The company’s hospitality portfolio comprises 11 operational hotels with 3,351 keys as of July 2025, spread across key cities including Mumbai, Hyderabad, Pune, Bengaluru, NCR, Uttarakhand, and Khandala. It operates under renowned global brands such as Marriott, Westin, Sheraton, Novotel, and Taj (upcoming), serving both business and leisure segments. Its commercial portfolio covers ~2.4 million sq. ft. of leasable space with a diversified annuity income stream, complemented by a residential project in Bengaluru.

In FY25, hospitality contributed 87% of revenue, with commercial leasing and residential projects contributing 11% and 2%, respectively. The company has embarked on significant growth initiatives, including ~1,200 rooms and 0.9 mn sq. ft. of commercial space under construction or planning, alongside leisure expansions like The Dukes Retreat and resort projects in Goa. Strategic asset ramp-ups, coupled with operational efficiency measures and sustainability initiatives, position Chalet Hotels for continued performance improvement.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Chalet Hotels has a robust pipeline with ~1,200 rooms and 0.9 mn sq. ft. of commercial space under development, including high-profile projects like Taj at Delhi International Airport, CIGNUS Powai Tower II, Varca Goa Resort, and Hyatt Regency Navi Mumbai. Leisure expansion, strategic entry into North Goa and Trivandrum, and premium business assets in key metros are expected to enhance ADR and RevPAR. Sustainability targets, including 100% renewable energy by 2030 and net-zero emissions by 2040, reinforce long-term positioning.

Name: HLV Ltd
Overview:
HLV Ltd, formerly known as Hotel Leelaventure Ltd, is a prominent player in India's luxury hospitality sector. Established in 1981, the company owns and operates The Leela Mumbai, a renowned resort-style business hotel. The Leela Mumbai is part of a strategic alliance with the Global Hotel Alliance, offering the Leela DISCOVERY program to its customers.

In recent years, HLV Ltd has undergone significant restructuring, including a reshuffling of roles and realignment of operations. These changes were driven by Brookfield's acquisition of the company's assets, aiming to achieve greater operational efficiencies.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
HLV Ltd is focused on revitalizing its operations and improving profitability. The company plans to enhance guest experiences, optimize operational efficiencies, and explore new market opportunities. By leveraging its strategic alliances and brand equity, HLV Ltd aims to achieve sustainable growth and strengthen its position in the luxury hospitality industry.

Name: Brigade Hotel Ventures Ltd
Overview: Brigade Hotel Ventures Limited (BHVL) is a hospitality company that owns and develops hotels in key Indian cities, with most of its presence in South India. Its portfolio includes several operating hotels across cities like Bengaluru, Chennai, Kochi, Mysuru, and GIFT City, with over 1,600 rooms, managed under international hotel brands. The properties span multiple hotel segments such as upscale, upper-midscale, and luxury, offering amenities like fine dining, meeting and event spaces, fitness and recreational services.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Brigade Hotel Ventures plans to expand aggressively by adding multiple new hotels across cities such as Chennai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Kerala, aiming to increase its total room inventory significantly. It has entered into several management agreements with global hotel brands to run these new properties, including tie-ups under Marriott, Hyatt, and others. The company also intends to use funds from its capital raising activities to reduce debt, acquire land for future projects, and support corporate growth.

Name: Apeejay Surrendra Park Hotels Ltd
Overview:
Apeejay Surrendra Park Hotels Ltd operates in the hospitality sector, offering premium boutique hotel experiences along with yacht hiring services. The company manages upscale properties under its renowned brands such as The Park Hotels and Zone Connect by The Park, and has a growing footprint in leisure and heritage tourism markets.

In its latest fiscal year, the company’s performance was buoyed by strong occupancy rates, expansion into Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, and strategic acquisitions that strengthened its brand portfolio. Operational efforts focused on maintaining premium positioning while scaling through disciplined brand management.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company is targeting double-digit revenue growth—driven by new hotel openings, room inventory expansion, and its heritage café network—while sustaining its premium positioning. With an upscale brand ecosystem, robust occupancy, and strategic expansion into emerging leisure destinations, ASPHL is poised to deliver continued growth and value in the upcoming period.

Name: Royal Orchid Hotels Ltd
Overview:
Royal Orchid Hotels Ltd, established in 1986, is a prominent hospitality company in India, operating a diverse portfolio of hotels and resorts across various categories and geographies. The company offers a range of accommodations, including five-star, four-star, economy hotels, resorts, and service apartments, catering to both business and leisure travelers.

With a strong presence in India, Sri Lanka, and Nepal, Royal Orchid Hotels has become a significant player in the hospitality industry.  The company's business model focuses on an asset-light approach, with a significant portion of its properties operated under management contracts and franchises.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Royal Orchid Hotels aims to expand its portfolio to 345 hotels and over 20,000 keys by 2030, demonstrating its commitment to growth and market leadership. The company's strategic initiatives and focus on emerging markets position it well for sustained growth in the coming years

Name: Oriental Hotels Ltd
Overview:
Oriental Hotels Ltd, an associate company of The Indian Hotels Company Limited (IHCL), operates a portfolio of seven hotels across southern India. These properties are managed under renowned IHCL brands such as Taj, Vivanta, and Gateway, contributing to the company’s strong presence in the hospitality sector.

The company’s business model is anchored in its strategic alliance with IHCL, leveraging the latter's operational expertise and brand recognition. This partnership enhances Oriental Hotels' ability to deliver exceptional guest experiences and maintain operational efficiency across its properties.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Oriental Hotels Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by capitalizing on the strong demand in the hospitality sector and leveraging its strategic partnership with IHCL. The company is poised to maintain its focus on operational excellence and guest satisfaction to drive sustained growth in the coming quarters.

Name: Thomas Cook (India) Ltd
Overview:
Thomas Cook India is a leading integrated travel and travel-related services company offering a wide range of services including foreign exchange, corporate and leisure travel, MICE, visa and passport services, and digital solutions. With a strong global presence spanning over 25 countries, the company leverages a diverse brand portfolio to cater to evolving customer needs.

Ongoing technology adoption and customer-centric programs support operational efficiency and growth, while strategic initiatives aim to capture emerging market opportunities and drive margin improvement.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Thomas Cook expects growth to outpace the overall industry driven by its diversified portfolio, technological investments, and premium service offerings. The leisure and travel business anticipates stronger performance in the second half of the year, with digital transformation and operational excellence key to sustaining margin improvements. The company maintains an asset-light approach with controlled capital expenditure and focus on cash flow generation.

Name: TajGVK Hotels & Resorts Ltd
Overview:
TajGVK Hotels & Resorts Limited is a joint venture between the Hyderabad-based GVK Group and Indian Hotels Company Limited (IHCL). Established in 1995, the company specializes in owning, operating, and managing hotels, palaces, and resorts under the renowned "TAJ" brand. Its portfolio includes five hotels: Taj Krishna and Taj Deccan in Hyderabad, Taj Club House in Chennai, Taj Chandigarh, and Vivanta Begumpet in Hyderabad. Additionally, through its joint venture, Greenwoods Palaces and Resorts Private Limited, TajGVK co-owns the Taj Santacruz in Mumbai.

The company's operations are centered around providing luxury hospitality services, catering to both business and leisure travelers. With a strong presence in key metropolitan cities, TajGVK aims to deliver exceptional guest experiences through its premium properties.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
TajGVK Hotels & Resorts Limited is poised for continued growth, leveraging its strong brand presence and strategic locations. The company's focus on enhancing guest experiences and operational efficiencies is expected to drive future profitability. With the hospitality industry showing signs of recovery and increasing demand for luxury accommodations, TajGVK is well-positioned to capitalize on these trends in the upcoming quarters.

Name: Mahindra Holidays & Resorts India Ltd
Overview:
Mahindra Holidays & Resorts India Ltd. (MHRIL), part of the Mahindra Group’s Leisure and Hospitality segment, is the largest vacation ownership company outside the US and ranks sixth globally. Established in 1996, it operates primarily under its flagship brand ‘Club Mahindra’, offering family holidays through a membership-based model. The company has built a strong brand presence, known for full-service resorts and curated travel experiences.

Its network spans across India and select international markets, supported by a mix of owned, leased, and associate properties. MHRIL focuses on delivering quality holiday experiences, driving customer engagement through curated activities, high service standards, and resort-based experiences. Strategic expansion, digital transformation, and capital-light growth remain at the core of its long-term plan.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
MHRIL plans to focus on profitability and ROCE rather than aggressive member additions in the near term, as it completes its business model transition. Room addition momentum, experience-led offerings, and operational efficiency are expected to support growth. The international business is projected to recover from macro headwinds by H2FY26, potentially boosting consolidated profitability.

Name: Juniper Hotels Ltd
Overview:
Juniper Hotels Limited is a luxury hotel development and ownership company with a portfolio of premium properties primarily under the Hyatt brand across key Indian cities. Founded in 1985, the company operates eight hotel assets comprising over 2,100 keys, including serviced apartments, strategically located in metropolitan and emerging leisure destinations. It focuses on the luxury segment, leveraging brand affiliations and premium service offerings to capture high-value leisure and business travel markets.

Juniper emphasizes asset upgrades and renovations to maintain competitive positioning and enhance revenue per available room (RevPAR) and average room rates (ARR).
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Juniper Hotels is poised for robust growth driven by strategic expansions, asset enhancements, and premium positioning in luxury markets. The focus on operational efficiencies and ramping up of new assets is expected to support margin expansion and sustained revenue growth. Management anticipates continued demand recovery and premium pricing power, with disciplined capital deployment fueling long-term value creation.

Name: Ecos (India) Mobility & Hospitality Ltd
Overview:
Ecos (India) Mobility & Hospitality Ltd. is a prominent provider of chauffeur-driven mobility and employee transportation services in India. Established in 1996, the company operates in over 100 cities across India and 30+ countries globally, catering primarily to corporate clients with scalable transportation solutions.

The company operates two main business segments: Chauffeur-Driven Rentals (CCR) and Employee Transportation Services (ETS). Ecos has been expanding its client base and service offerings, aiming to strengthen its position as a leader in the mobility and hospitality sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Ecos aims to continue expanding its footprint both domestically and internationally. The company plans to enhance its service offerings and operational efficiencies to meet the growing demand for corporate transportation solutions. With a focus on client retention and acquisition, Ecos is well-positioned to capitalize on the increasing trend of outsourcing transportation services by large corporations.

Name: Easy Trip Planners Ltd
Overview:
Easy Trip Planners Ltd (EaseMyTrip) is an Indian online travel agency founded in 2008, offering services across flight, train, bus, hotel bookings, and holiday packages. The company operates both through digital platforms and a strong agent network, with growing international presence.

Over time, EaseMyTrip has diversified beyond travel booking—venturing into electric mobility (via Easy Green Mobility and YoloBus) and potentially hospitality—while pursuing growth across non-air segments to reduce reliance on air travel revenues.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

EaseMyTrip is embarking on a comprehensive expansion strategy to diversify its offerings and enhance its market presence. The company is actively investing in various sectors, including wellness, mobility, insurance, and customized travel solutions, to create a seamless end-to-end experience for its customers. Through strategic equity partnerships, EaseMyTrip aims to back scalable startups, allowing founders to retain operational control while benefiting from the company's extensive ecosystem.

Name: Samhi Hotels Ltd
Overview:
SAMHI Hotels Ltd operates as a prominent branded hotel ownership and asset-management platform in India, partnering long-term with global operators such as Marriott, IHG, and Hyatt. It owns and manages a diversified portfolio of hospitality assets across major urban centers, delivering premium lodging solutions.

In its latest fiscal year, SAMHI demonstrated strong operational performance with expanding hotel inventory, improving asset performance, and a strategic institutional partnership. These developments drove enhanced revenue generation and solidified its financial discipline and scalability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With its strengthened balance sheet, institutional investment backing, strong demand environment, and a well-capitalized expansion plan, SAMHI is well-positioned for continued growth. The company aims to solidify margin gains, expand its upscale inventory, and deliver sustained shareholder value as it executes on its FY26 roadmap.

Name: Kamat Hotels (India) Ltd
Overview:
Kamat Hotels (India) Ltd (KHIL), established in 1986, is a prominent hospitality company in India. It operates a diverse portfolio of hotels and resorts under various brands, catering to different market segments. The company's flagship brand, 'The Orchid,' is recognized as Asia's first Ecotel-certified five-star hotel chain, emphasizing eco-friendly practices. 

KHIL's operations span across multiple states in India, including Maharashtra, Goa, and Odisha, offering a range of accommodations from luxury hotels to mid-premium and heritage properties. Beyond its core hospitality services, KHIL is involved in hotel consultancy and setup services, leveraging its extensive experience in the industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
KHIL aims to achieve a revenue of ₹400 crore in FY26, supported by the planned expansion and robust business from existing properties. The company is also focusing on improving operational efficiencies and enhancing guest experiences to drive growth.

Name: EIH Associated Hotels Ltd
Overview:
EIH Associated Hotels Ltd is a prominent hospitality company in India, operating luxury hotels under the Oberoi and Trident brands. Established in 1981, the company manages properties in key cities and tourist destinations across the country.

With a strong presence in the luxury segment, EIH Associated Hotels Ltd is known for its exceptional service standards and world-class amenities, catering to both domestic and international guests.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
EIH Associated Hotels plans to expand its portfolio of luxury properties while continuing renovations and sustainability measures. The focus on enhancing guest experiences and operational efficiency positions the company for steady growth in domestic and international hospitality segments.

Name: Speciality Restaurants Ltd
Overview: Speciality Restaurants Ltd is a prominent Indian restaurant chain and food service company operating multiple brands across casual dining, fine dining, and QSR formats. The company manages well-known brands such as Mainland China, Oh! Calcutta, Sigree, Haka, and others, offering diverse cuisines including Chinese, Indian, and continental. Speciality Restaurants emphasizes quality, customer experience, and consistent service standards across its outlets. With a network spanning metro and Tier-2 cities, the company has established a strong brand presence in India’s organized food service sector. Speciality Restaurants continues to innovate with menu offerings, loyalty programs, and technology-driven operations such as online ordering and delivery partnerships.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Speciality Restaurants is positioned to accelerate recovery as dining demand strengthens post-pandemic. If quarter-on-quarter momentum continues, improved revenues and managed operating costs could help restore profitability and drive better returns in FY26.

Name: Bajaj Housing Finance Ltd
Overview:
Bajaj Housing Finance Ltd is a non-deposit taking Housing Finance Company established in 2015 and regulated by the NHB. Part of the Bajaj Group, it has been offering mortgage products since FY18, specializing in home loans, loans against property (LAP), lease rental discounting, and developer finance. As of June 2024, the company served over 323,000 active customers, with home loans accounting for ~83% of its loan portfolio across a diversified operating network of 215 branches in 174 locations across 20 states and 3 union territories 
 Asset Type
 Groww
 .
 
Business Segments (with % of Total Loan Portfolio):
 
 Home Loans (~57.5%): Core business, primarily to salaried and self-employed customers, serving mass-affluent borrowers .
 
 Loans Against Property (LAP) (~10%): Focused on self‑occupied residential collateral, lower risk profile; salaried professionals account for ~29% of LAP AUM .
 
 Developer Finance & Lease Rental Discounting (~19–20%): Financing for developers and rental assets to HNI/commercial clients; supports commercial-real-estate cash flows .
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Bajaj Housing Finance is poised for sustained growth, driven by continued expansion in disbursements and robust AUM momentum, which reached ₹1,20,420 crore by June 2025. Rising retail traction in home loans, supported by diverse products and omnichannel sourcing through branches, digital platforms, and developer partnerships, underpins future asset growth. Strong credit ratings enable low-cost funding, allowing competitive pricing and efficient capital deployment.

Name: Rail Vikas Nigam Ltd
Overview:
Rail Vikas Nigam Ltd (RVNL) is a key public sector enterprise under the Ministry of Railways, responsible for implementing a wide range of rail infrastructure projects across India. Its core mandate includes executing projects like new railway lines, doubling existing tracks, gauge conversions, railway electrification, metro projects, major bridges, workshops, and related infrastructure. RVNL plays a vital role in expanding and modernizing India’s rail network, ensuring enhanced connectivity, capacity, and operational efficiency.
 
 Business Segments & Revenue Contribution
 RVNL primarily operates as a project executing agency for the Ministry of Railways. Its revenue is majorly derived from:
 
 Railway Infrastructure Projects: ~100% of revenue is earned from turnkey project execution — covering new lines, doubling, electrification, metro development, bridges, and related works.
 
 RVNL’s earnings are based on project management fees, execution contracts, and cost-plus margins linked directly to the value of projects implemented for Indian Railways and urban transport bodies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Strong pipeline of new orders, targeting ₹75,000–₹1,00,000 crore in fresh orders, supported by India’s rising rail infra capex. Exploring international opportunities to diversify beyond domestic projects.

Name: GIC Housing Finance Ltd
Overview:
GIC Housing Finance Ltd, established in 1989 and originally named GIC Grih Vitta Limited, offers housing loans to individuals and entities constructing residential units. The company places a strong emphasis on ethical and customer-friendly lending, with a mission to build trust while creating value for shareholders.

Promoted by General Insurance Corporation of India and its erstwhile subsidiaries, the company operates across India through a widespread network of offices and sales associates. It also collaborates with real estate developers to facilitate financing for homebuyers.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
GIC Housing Finance appears focused on strengthening its lending portfolio while navigating recent earnings variability—like the Q1 dip in income—to build resilience and financial stability in FY 26 and beyond.

Name: LIC Housing Finance Ltd
Overview:
LIC Housing Finance Ltd (LICHFL), a subsidiary of Life Insurance Corporation of India, is a leading housing finance company engaged in home loans, loan against property, project financing, lease rental discounting, and commercial property loans. Established in 1989, it has built a robust portfolio backed by a wide distribution network and strategic support from LIC’s agent ecosystem.

As of FY25, LICHFL has surpassed ₹3 lakh crore in loan portfolio and ₹5,000 crore in annual profit. The firm has steadily strengthened its focus on digital transformation, operational efficiency, and asset quality, reflected in its growing disbursements and improved asset health. It has also expanded its physical presence to 307 area offices and a representative office in Dubai.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

LIC Housing Finance is strategically shifting its focus from individual home loans, which currently constitute 85% of its portfolio, to higher-yielding loan segments such as loan against property (LAP), lease rental discounting (LRD), and developer finance. This move aims to enhance net interest margins (NIM) amid a softer interest rate environment. The company plans to reduce the share of individual home loans to 70% within two years.

Name: PTC India Financial Services Ltd
Overview:
PTC India Financial Services Ltd is a non-banking financial company (NBFC) registered with the Reserve Bank of India and holds the status of an Infrastructure Finance Company. It provides equity and debt financing solutions tailored to the energy value chain, supporting companies across generation, transmission, and related infrastructure.

By specializing in financing within the energy sector, PFS plays a crucial role in enabling infrastructure development, bridging funding gaps for projects that drive power sector growth and sustainability across India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

PTC India Financial Services Limited (PFS) is strategically enhancing its position in the infrastructure finance sector. The company is focusing on expanding its renewable energy portfolio, including wind, solar, and geothermal power projects, to align with India's energy transition goals. PFS is also exploring opportunities in emerging sectors such as electric mobility and water treatment. To support its growth initiatives, the company is working on improving its asset quality and capital structure.

Name: Housing & Urban Development Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Housing & Urban Development Corporation Ltd (HUDCO) is a premier government-owned financial institution focused on financing housing and urban infrastructure projects across India. The company has rapidly scaled its sanctioning and disbursement operations, supporting a diverse portfolio with a strong emphasis on affordable housing and urban infrastructure. 

The company maintains a best-in-class asset quality with stringent risk management practices, while cautiously foraying into private sector and PPP projects. HUDCO has been proactive in liability management, lowering its cost of funds and diversifying borrowing sources.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expresses confidence in growth opportunities driven by government initiatives such as PMAY 2.0 and Urban Challenge Fund. The company aims to achieve a loan book of INR 1.5 lakh crore in FY26 with a cautious approach, while long-term targets remain flexible to India’s economic trajectory. Investments in digital transformation, hiring, and process improvements position HUDCO well to capitalize on upcoming urban infrastructure financing demand.

Name: Repco Home Finance Ltd
Overview:
Repco Home Finance Ltd (RHFL), established in 2000 and headquartered in Chennai, is a prominent housing finance company in India. Registered with the National Housing Bank (NHB), RHFL offers a range of home loan products to individuals and developers, focusing on affordable housing. The company has a strong presence across southern and western India, with an expanding footprint in other regions.

RHFL's product portfolio includes home loans, plot loans, and loans for construction and renovation. The company caters to a diverse customer base, including salaried and self-employed individuals, with a focus on providing financial solutions to underserved segments. Through its customer-centric approach and efficient service delivery, RHFL aims to be a preferred choice for home financing needs.
Key Takeways:
.Future Outlook
RHFL aims to continue growth by expanding product offerings and geographic reach. The company plans to enhance digital capabilities for better customer experience and operational efficiency. With a strong capital base and focus on asset quality, RHFL is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for housing finance in India.

Name: REC Ltd
Overview:
REC Ltd is a Central Public Sector Undertaking under India’s Ministry of Power and a key financier for the country’s power sector. It provides financial assistance across the entire power value chain — from generation and transmission to distribution — and plays a pivotal role in rural electrification and government-led power schemes. REC raises low-cost funds through tax-free and capital gain bonds, ensuring affordable finance to drive India’s power infrastructure development.
 
Business Segments (with % of Total Loan Book):
 
 Conventional Power Projects (Generation, Transmission, Distribution): ~70% — Loans for thermal power plants, transmission lines, and grid strengthening.
 
 Renewable Energy & Green Projects: ~30% (targeted share by FY2030) — Financing solar, wind, and emerging areas like large-scale hydro and grid integration for clean energy.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
REC aims to double its loan book to over ₹10 lakh crore by 2030, leveraging India’s expanding power sector. The company will prioritize renewable energy financing, allocating ₹3 lakh crore to support the nation’s 500 GW renewable capacity target and facilitate annual clean energy additions of around 50,000 MW.

Name: PNB Housing Finance Ltd
Overview:
PNB Housing Finance Limited (PNBHF), promoted by Punjab National Bank, is among India’s top housing finance companies, offering a broad range of retail and non-housing loans. Its portfolio includes home loans, loans against property, non-resident property loans, and loans for commercial spaces. The company has a strong deposit base, positioning itself as the largest player by deposits in the HFC segment.

Over recent years, PNBHF has strategically shifted its focus towards retail lending, significantly reducing its corporate loan book to de-risk operations. Supported by a wide network of branches and channel partners, the company has maintained consistent growth in its prime and emerging markets loan segments while improving asset quality and profitability.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

PNB Housing Finance is intensifying its focus on high-yielding segments, particularly affordable and emerging housing markets, aiming to increase their share to 50% of its loan portfolio by 2027. The company plans to expand its branch network by 50–60 annually, targeting a total of 500 branches, with a significant portion dedicated to affordable housing.

Name: India Shelter Finance Corporation Ltd
Overview:
India Shelter Finance Corporation Limited, incorporated in 1998, operates in the housing finance sector with a focus on first-time home buyers and loans against property. The company’s core strategy revolves around catering to self-employed and underserved customers, especially in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities. Its offerings are secured against residential property, ensuring a balanced risk profile.

Over the years, the company has steadily scaled operations with a growing branch network and improved technology-led processes for loan origination and credit assessment. With prudent underwriting standards, a resilient liability profile, and strong rural demand tailwinds, it is positioned as a niche player in the affordable housing finance space.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

India Shelter Finance Corporation is strategically expanding its operations by increasing its branch network to 266 locations and enhancing its digital infrastructure to support scalable growth. The company is focusing on affordable housing solutions, offering competitive interest rates ranging from 12% to 20% to meet the diverse needs of borrowers. With a robust asset under management (AUM) growth of 35% year-on-year, India Shelter Finance is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for housing finance in India.

Name: IFCI Ltd
Overview:
IFCI Ltd, formerly the Industrial Finance Corporation of India and owned by the Government of India, operates as a non-banking financial company focused on delivering long-term infrastructure and industrial financing across sectors such as airports, roads, power, real estate, and manufacturing. Over its decades of operation, it has backed landmark projects from ports and airports to highways and transmission networks and also serves as a nodal agency for various socio-financial schemes.

The company provides a diversified suite of financial services including project finance, corporate lending (such as working capital, lease rental discounting, and promoter funding), and syndication & advisory services aimed at reengineering corporate finances and facilitating strategic client solutions
Key Takeways:
Strategic and Structural Developments
IFCI has emerged as a turnaround theme among BSE 500 stocks, signaling regained investor confidence. Meanwhile, structural changes are under way as the government considers transitioning IFCI’s role from a lender to an advisory-focused entity

Name: Aadhar Housing Finance Ltd
Overview:
Aadhar Housing Finance Ltd. is one of India’s largest housing finance companies focused exclusively on the low-income segment, offering mortgage loans primarily under ₹15 lakh, with an average ticket size of ₹10 lakh. It caters predominantly to economically weaker sections and low-to-middle-income households, maintaining an average loan-to-value ratio of 59% as of 9MFY25. The company’s loan book is well-diversified geographically across 21 states and union territories, with notable presence in Uttar Pradesh, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, and Rajasthan.

Its operations are supported by a network of 557 branches, including 109 sales offices, reaching over 11,000 pin codes. Aadhar’s product suite includes home purchase, construction, improvement, extension, and commercial property loans. The company adopts a prudent borrowing strategy focused solely on long-term funding, with borrowings diversified across banks, the National Housing Bank, and NCDs. Backed by Blackstone Group, Aadhar Housing Finance leverages advanced digital tools, AI-driven underwriting, and predictive analytics to enhance loan processing efficiency, improve asset quality, and strengthen customer retention.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Aadhar Housing Finance aims to leverage its extensive network and technology-driven processes to deepen penetration in existing geographies and expand in under-served markets. It plans to focus on asset growth, maintain strong asset quality, and enhance operational efficiency, while capitalising on rising demand in the affordable housing finance sector supported by government incentives and urbanisation trends.

Name: Can Fin Homes Ltd
Overview:
Can Fin Homes Ltd is a deposit-taking housing finance company focused on providing smaller-ticket housing loans primarily to salaried and self-employed non-professional borrowers. The company operates across India through a network of branches, concentrating on retail housing finance while maintaining a cautious approach toward portfolio quality and regional expansion. Its offerings cover housing loans, commercial real estate, and non-housing loans, with a strategic emphasis on increasing direct sourcing and expanding presence in key markets.

The company’s business growth is driven by increasing disbursements, expansion in loan ticket sizes, and a steady decline in dependence on direct selling agents. Can Fin Homes is also undergoing significant technology upgrades and branch network expansions to support future growth, alongside focused efforts on asset quality, cost management, and regional recovery in key states.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management projects AUM growth of 12–13% in FY26, accelerating to around 15% in FY27, driven by branch and sales team expansions, direct sourcing improvements, and recovery in lagging regions. Cost control and technology investments will support margin and operational improvements. Dividend payout is expected to remain steady, while no immediate plans for co-lending or portfolio buyouts exist until IT systems stabilize.

Name: Aptus Value Housing Finance India Ltd
Overview:
Aptus Value Housing Finance India Ltd is a retail-focused housing finance company specializing in serving self-employed, low- and middle-income families, primarily in semi-urban and rural areas of South India. Its primary offering includes housing loans for first-time buyers, small business loans against residential property, quasi home loans, and insurance-linked lending products. The company differentiates itself by avoiding builder loans or commercial real estate exposure, ensuring a focused and low-risk portfolio.

It maintains complete in-house control over its lending operations, from origination and underwriting to legal assessment and collections, enabling operational efficiency, better credit control, and faster turnaround times. As of FY25, Aptus had an AUM of ₹18,865 Cr, serviced through 300 branches spread across Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Karnataka, Odisha, and Maharashtra, with the majority of its business concentrated in Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
Management targets an AUM of ₹25,000 Cr by FY28, aiming for sustained 25–30% annual growth in disbursements and loan book. FY25 saw expansion into Maharashtra and Odisha with 10 new branches, with plans to add 35–40 branches annually. The company intends to maintain its South India focus while gradually deepening its presence in new states.

Name: Tourism Finance Corporation of India Ltd
Overview:
Tourism Finance Corporation of India Ltd (TFCIL) is a specialized financial institution dedicated to supporting the tourism ecosystem. The company provides tailored financial assistance to projects such as hotels, resorts, restaurants, multiplexes, amusement parks, and related infrastructure. 

Leveraging its expertise, TFCIL fosters growth in the travel and leisure sectors by offering both project financing and working capital solutions. TFCIL’s operations are driven by deep sector knowledge and disciplined underwriting, which position it as a niche lender within India’s broader financial services landscape.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
TFCIL is optimistic about its growth trajectory. With a healthy capital adequacy ratio (over 60%) and a gearing ratio below 1, the company is well-positioned to scale further. Management is pursuing fresh capital infusion, anticipates rating upgrades, and has proposed a stock split to enhance liquidity.

Name: Sammaan Capital Ltd
Overview:
Sammaan Capital Ltd (formerly Indiabulls Housing Finance) is an NHB-regulated housing finance company focused on retail mortgages—primarily affordable home loans and loans against property (LAP)—with selective exposure to corporate mortgages such as lease rental discounting and residential construction finance. The franchise uses an asset-light model built on co-lending, direct assignments and securitization/PTC to scale while managing ALM and capital efficiency.

The company is in the midst of a multi-year pivot from a legacy, wholesale-skewed book to a retail-heavy “growth AUM” engine. Its strategy emphasizes strengthened capital, tighter asset quality, and partnership-led origination, supported by a dedicated affordable-housing subsidiary (Sammaan Finserve) and an emerging AIF platform to broaden funding channels and fee income.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management’s FY27 trajectory remains intact: ₹1,00,000 cr+ consolidated AUM, disbursals of ~₹35,000 cr annually, consolidated ROA ~3.2% (trending from ~3%), incremental ROE in high-teens, and NPA ≤1.2%. The mix is expected to tactically balance ~40% co-lending / ~40% direct assignment / ~20% PTC by FY27, with continued legacy run-down to single-digit share of AUM, disciplined cost-to-income execution, and scaling of the AIF platform to ~₹15,000 cr AUM.

Name: Indian Railway Finance Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) is the dedicated financing arm of Indian Railways, operating under the Ministry of Railways. Its core business involves raising capital from domestic and international markets to finance and lease rolling stock and related railway assets to Indian Railways.
 
 Business Segments and Latest Revenue Contribution
 Lease Income:
 Approximately 75% of IRFC’s revenue comes from lease rentals charged to Indian Railways for rolling stock assets like locomotives, passenger coaches, and freight wagons. This segment generates steady and predictable cash flows.
 
 Interest Income:
 The remaining 25% of revenue is derived from interest on funds lent or invested by IRFC in various financial instruments and infrastructure projects connected to Indian Railways.
 
 Asset Financing Focus:
 IRFC finances about 67% of Indian Railways’ capital outlay, making it a backbone in railway infrastructure funding.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
IRFC’s growth is anchored in expanding its leasing portfolio to support Indian Railways’ modernization and capacity expansion under initiatives like the National Rail Plan 2030. The company will continue to access diversified domestic and international funding sources, including bonds, loans, and multilateral financing, ensuring competitive and sustainable capital.

Name: AAVAS Financiers Ltd
Overview:
Aavas Financiers Ltd is a retail housing finance company focusing on affordable housing loans for low- and middle-income self-employed customers in semi-urban and rural India. The company offers home loans, loans against property, and MSME loans, aiming to serve underserved segments with tailored financial products.

With steady growth in assets under management and enhanced technology platforms, Aavas has strengthened its operational efficiency and customer reach. Its strategy emphasizes risk-adjusted pricing, distribution expansion, and digital partnerships to scale sustainably.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company targets sustained spreads above 5%, with further reductions in operating expenses as technology investments mature. Asset quality is expected to improve, with GNPA forecasted to return below 1% shortly. Management remains confident of achieving risk-adjusted profitable growth while maintaining a cautious stance on underwriting.

Name: Home First Finance Company India Ltd
Overview:
Home First Finance Company India Ltd is an affordable housing finance institution catering primarily to the low- and middle-income segments through standardized housing, top-up, renovation, and mortgage loans. With a digital-first approach, it blends robust underwriting with expansive reach via over 150 branches and a growing network of customer touchpoints.

Driven by technological integration in lending and a focus on under-served customers, Home First has rapidly scaled its AUM and distribution footprint while maintaining strong asset quality and investor confidence.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Home First Finance Company India is strategically expanding its footprint by increasing its presence in Tier-3 markets and enhancing its digital asset base. The company has set ambitious targets, aiming for a 15% Return on Equity (ROE) within the next 5–6 quarters and a Return on Assets (ROA) approaching 4%, while maintaining an operating expense-to-assets ratio between 2.60% and 2.70%. Additionally, Home First Finance plans to raise ₹1,250 crore through a Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP) to support its growth initiatives

Name: Power Finance Corporation Ltd
Overview:
