Power Finance Corporation Limited (PFC) is a systemically important non-deposit taking NBFC registered with the RBI that plays a vital role in financing India’s power and infrastructure sectors. With “Maharatna” status, it provides fund-based loans like project finance, lease financing, and debt refinancing, along with non-fund-based products such as guarantees and letters of comfort. PFC is a key financial backbone for India’s conventional and renewable energy projects.
 
 Business Segment:
 Project finance and term loans for the power sector make up the largest portion of total revenue, contributing about 85–90%. Corporate finance, working capital lending, and other asset management services contribute about 10–15%, covering diversified sectors like renewable energy, coal, oil & gas, and other infrastructure segments.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
PFC is set for robust growth by significantly expanding its renewable energy portfolio from 10% to 27% by FY2030 and focusing on emerging sectors like green hydrogen and ammonia financing. The company plans to disburse approximately ₹3 lakh crore toward clean energy projects over the next seven years, leveraging its strong position as India’s leading power sector financier.

Name: Tech Mahindra Ltd
Overview:
Tech Mahindra Ltd, part of the Mahindra Group, is a leading global IT services and consulting company providing a wide range of technology solutions — from IT-enabled services and application development to infrastructure, engineering, data analytics, cybersecurity, and consulting. With operations in over 90 countries and a workforce exceeding 150,000, it serves diverse industries and many Fortune 500 clients, helping them drive digital transformation through next-generation technologies like 5G, Blockchain, AI, and Quantum Computing.
 
Business Segments (Revenue Mix)
 
 Communications: 40% of total revenue — core focus on telecom and network services.
 
 Manufacturing: 16% — serving automotive, industrial, and discrete manufacturing clients.
 
 Banking, Financial Services & Insurance (BFSI): 16% — includes IT solutions for banks, insurers, and financial institutions.
 
 Technology, Media & Entertainment: 9% — includes media houses, streaming, and tech product companies.
 
 Retail, Transport & Logistics: 8% — retail chains, e-commerce, logistics, and supply chain management.
 
 Others: 11% — covers smaller verticals including healthcare and new focus areas.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
The company is positioned for robust growth through its “4+1” strategy across five verticals, with healthcare and Hi-Tech as key focus areas. Strategic investments in innovation hubs, platform-led business models, and products/platforms expansion aim to drive non-linear revenue growth and higher margins. Sustainability and ESG integration are being embedded into offerings to meet evolving client expectations. Large-scale hiring, coupled with M&A initiatives, will enhance capabilities, access new geographies, and strengthen next-generation technology offerings.

Name: Infosys Ltd
Overview:
Infosys Ltd is a leading global IT services and consulting company and India’s second-largest IT firm after TCS. The company enables Fortune 500 clients worldwide to drive digital transformation through consulting, next-gen technologies, and outsourcing services. Infosys is recognized for its strong focus on cloud, AI, analytics, blockchain, IoT, and automation solutions.
 
 Business Segments
 Digital Services (57% of revenue): Cloud, digital experience, AI/automation, data analytics, IoT, cybersecurity, and digital consulting.
 
 Core Services (43% of revenue): Application development & maintenance, infrastructure management, traditional IT outsourcing, and legacy system modernization.
 
 Revenue Mix
 By Service: Digital Services — 57%, Core Services — 43%
 
 By Geography: North America — 62%, Europe — 25%, Rest of World (ROW) — 10%
 India — 3%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Infosys is focused on expanding its digital and generative AI service offerings while capitalizing on a strong pipeline of large, multi-year deals across regions and industries. The company continues to strengthen global partnerships and forge innovative alliances to enhance its service capabilities. Ongoing investments in talent development, reskilling, and proprietary technology platforms position Infosys to drive sustainable growth and maintain its leadership in the global IT services sector.

Name: Tata Consultancy Services Ltd
Overview:
Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), the flagship of the Tata Group, stands as one of the world’s leading IT services, consulting, and business solutions providers. With a workforce of over 600,000 professionals in 55 countries, TCS partners with industry leaders globally to deliver a broad suite of technology and digital transformation services.
 
 Business Segment Revenue Share (%): 
 Banking, Financial Services & Insurance (BFSI) : 32.0%
 Consumer Business : 15%
 Regional Markets : 13.4%
 Technology & Services : 8.4%
Others : 31.2%

 Services Portfolio
 TCS delivers a wide range of services, including: IT Consulting, Application Development and Maintenance, Cloud & Platform Services, Digital Transformation, Business Process Outsourcing, Engineering, IoT & Industrial Services , Cybersecurity
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
TCS is targeting USD 50 billion in revenues by 2030, supported by a new organizational structure that adds two business groups to enhance customer alignment and journey management. The company’s strategic roadmap emphasizes new customer acquisition, market incubation, sustainable growth, and digital transformation as key growth drivers. Continued focus on innovation and transformation enables TCS to help clients grow while capturing emerging digital opportunities globally, reinforcing its position as a leading IT services provider.

Name: L&T Technology Services Ltd
Overview:
LTTS is a global engineering services company offering solutions in engineering, research & development, and digitalization across industries like transportation, industrial products, telecom, medical devices, and plant engineering. Serving a large client base including Fortune 500 companies, LTTS provides end-to-end digital engineering advisory and product development services, with a presence in over 25 countries.

The company’s revenue is split mainly across three segments—Mobility, Sustainability, and Technology—with each now crossing $400 million in annualized revenue. Despite some challenges in the Automotive vertical and seasonal softness in Smart World projects, LTTS maintains a strong order pipeline and steady growth, backed by investments in AI, new design centers, and strategic acquisitions like Intelliswift.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

LTTS is strategically enhancing its capabilities in digital engineering and embedded software to meet the growing demand for software-defined vehicles (SDVs) and smart manufacturing solutions. The company has been selected as a strategic engineering supplier by Volvo Cars, marking a significant expansion of their long-standing relationship. Additionally, LTTS is focusing on expanding its presence in key industries while strengthening its technological capabilities.

Name: Coforge Ltd
Overview:
Coforge Limited is a global IT services and solutions provider with a focus on digital transformation across industries. The company delivers services in areas such as product engineering, intelligent automation, data integration, AI/ML, and business process re-engineering. It operates across 21 countries, supported by 26 delivery centers. A majority of its business comes from North America and Europe, with a strong offshore presence making up over half of its revenues.
The company added 47 new clients, and its repeat business stood at an impressive 94%. It maintained one of the lowest attrition rates in the industry at 10.9%.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Coforge is intensifying its focus on digital transformation, aiming to become an AI-first, digital-first organization. The company is enhancing its global presence through strategic acquisitions and expanding its service offerings in emerging markets. Coforge is also investing in innovation ecosystems within its centers and accelerating product and platform transformation to create measurable business value.

Name: HCL Technologies Ltd
Overview:
HCL Technologies Ltd is a leading global IT services and consulting company with a strong presence in 46 countries, offering a comprehensive range of software-led IT solutions, remote infrastructure management, engineering and R&D services, and business process outsourcing. The company serves diverse industries, including financial services, manufacturing, telecom, media, retail, life sciences, healthcare, oil & gas, travel, and logistics, leveraging its extensive global delivery network and offshore capabilities. 
 Software-led IT services
 Remote infrastructure management
 Engineering & R&D
 Business Process Outsourcing (BPO)
 
 HCL serves diverse sectors: Financial Services, Manufacturing, Telecom, Media, Retail, Life Sciences, Healthcare, Oil & Gas, Travel & Logistics.
 
 Revenue Mix
 IT & Business Services: 72%
 
 Engineering & R&D Services: 16%
 
 Products & Platforms: 12%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
HCL Technologies is set to drive sustained growth through accelerated investments in AI, cloud, engineering services, and proprietary digital platforms. For FY26, the company projects 2–5% constant currency revenue growth and EBIT margins of 18–19%, with digital services contributing an increasing share of new revenue. Strategic investments, including the OSAT semiconductor plant in Gujarat, position HCL to capitalize on global tech demand, while large-scale deals across core and emerging verticals further strengthen its growth pipeline.

Name: Mphasis Ltd
Overview:
Mphasis is a global IT solutions provider specializing in cloud and cognitive services that enable enterprise digital transformation worldwide. The company has been aggressively leveraging AI-led technologies, significantly enhancing its deal pipeline and delivering next-generation platforms across industries such as BFSI, insurance, and TMT. 

Geographically, Mphasis derives most of its revenue from the US and RoW markets, with recent declines in EMEA due to specific customer ramp-downs. The company continues to expand its wallet share within key verticals and accounts, driving strong client additions and deepening relationships with large clients.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management anticipates FY26 growth at approximately twice the industry rate, driven by strong deal conversions and ramp-ups. Despite cautious optimism due to macro uncertainties, execution visibility is improving with most prior risks largely mitigated. Continued AI adoption, client wallet share gains, and platform-based delivery models are expected to sustain momentum and margin stability.

Name: Oracle Financial Services Software Ltd
Overview:
Oracle Financial Services Software Ltd (OFSS), a subsidiary of Oracle Global (Mauritius) Limited and part of the global Oracle Corporation group, is a leading provider of financial software products and services for the global financial services industry. With a robust presence in over 150 countries, OFSS delivers mission-critical solutions that help banks and financial institutions manage operations, mitigate risks, enhance compliance, and drive customer engagement.
 
 Business Segments
 Products: Contributes ~90% of total revenue. Includes flagship solutions like FLEXCUBE (core banking), along with solutions for Anti-Money Laundering (AML), Financial Crime Compliance, Risk & Finance, Revenue Management, Billing, and Insurance.
 
 Services: Contributes ~10% of revenue. Encompasses implementation, consulting, and support services, as well as BPO operations through its step-down subsidiary Oracle (OFSS) BPO Services Ltd.
 
 Geographic Mix: Revenue diversified across the USA, Europe, Asia Pacific, Middle East, Africa, and India, reducing regional concentration risk.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Oracle Financial Services Software Ltd is focusing on expanding its cloud-based financial solutions, aiming to enhance automation and efficiency in financial processes. The company is integrating advanced artificial intelligence (AI) features into its software suite, such as predictive analytics and anomaly detection, to support real-time decision-making and improve forecasting accuracy. Additionally, Oracle is investing in global infrastructure to support its cloud services, ensuring scalability and compliance with regional data regulations. These strategic initiatives are designed to help financial institutions streamline operations, reduce costs, and adapt to the evolving digital landscape.

Name: Persistent Systems Ltd
Overview:
Persistent Systems is a global technology services company specializing in software engineering, platform development, and digital transformation. It offers end-to-end capabilities in product design, cloud, data, AI, and enterprise integration, leveraging proprietary frameworks and platforms to accelerate delivery. Strategic alliances with technology leaders like Salesforce and AWS strengthen its market positioning.

The company serves clients across BFSI, Healthcare & Life Sciences, and Software/Hi-Tech industries, with operations spanning North America, Europe, India, and other regions. Persistent’s model focuses on deepening client relationships, expanding digital and AI-driven solutions, and maintaining consistent, profitable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management continues to target a USD 2 billion annual revenue run rate by FY27, implying a near 20% CAGR, supported by organic growth and selective acquisitions. BFSI is expected to remain the leading growth driver in FY26, followed by Hi-Tech and Healthcare. While macro uncertainty is elongating decision cycles, Persistent aims to sustain profitable growth through a strong deal pipeline, AI-led differentiation, and expansion in high-potential geographies like Europe.

Name: Wipro Ltd
Overview:
Wipro Ltd is a leading global IT services provider and the fourth-largest Indian player in the global IT industry. The company is organized into two main business lines:
 
 iDEAS: Encompasses domain and consulting, application and data engineering, R&D, and Wipro Digital.
 
 iCORE: Includes cloud and infrastructure services, digital operations and platform, and cybersecurity and risk services.
 
 Business Segments & Latest Revenue (FY2025)
 IT Services:
 Makes up 97% of total revenue, contributing approximately $10.1 billion (₹871 billion) for FY2025.
 
 IT Products:
 Accounts for about 1% of revenue, or $105 million (₹8.9 billion).
 
 ISRE (India State Run Enterprises):
 Contributes around 2% of revenue, or $210 million (₹17.8 billion).
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Wipro’s growth is driven by its strategic focus on AI and digital transformation, backed by a $1 billion investment in emerging technologies, platform development, and large-scale upskilling initiatives. Expansion in cloud services, infrastructure, and cybersecurity positions the company to meet rising global enterprise demand. Diversified sector exposure across banking and insurance, consumer, technology, and manufacturing ensures broad opportunity capture, while operational excellence, record large deal wins, and disciplined cost management support margin improvement.

Name: TVS Electronics Ltd
Overview:
TVS Electronics Ltd, part of the TVS Group, is engaged in providing electronic products and services, with a key focus on IT peripherals, automation solutions, and after-sales support. The company manufactures and markets products such as printers, keyboards, and barcode scanners, and provides managed services to enterprise customers.

Its service division, which includes installation, warranty management, and multi-brand after-sales support, has emerged as a key growth vertical, complementing its hardware business. This combination of products and services enables TVS Electronics to deliver value across the entire customer lifecycle.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
TVS Electronics is expected to expand its services footprint across more cities and focus on enterprise clients for long-term contracts. Digital transformation and retail automation provide strong opportunities for growth in the medium term.

Name: Creative Newtech Ltd
Overview:
Creative Newtech Ltd, established in 1992 and headquartered in Mumbai, is a prominent distributor specializing in Information Technology (IT), Imaging, Lifestyle, and Security products. T

he company operates through a diversified portfolio, representing global brands and offering a wide range of consumer and enterprise solutions. Creative Newtech has expanded its footprint across India and internationally, establishing a strong presence in the distribution sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Creative Newtech is poised for continued growth, supported by its diversified product portfolio and strategic partnerships. The expansion of licensing agreements and the development of digital platforms like 'Ckart' are expected to drive future revenue streams.

Name: Orient Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Orient Technologies Ltd is an information technology solutions provider based in India. The company specializes in delivering IT infrastructure solutions, including data center services, virtualization, and cloud computing.

With over 25 years of experience, Orient Technologies Ltd has established itself as a trusted partner for businesses seeking reliable and scalable IT solutions. The company serves clients across various industries, including pharmaceuticals, healthcare, and BFSI.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Orient Technologies Ltd is experiencing robust growth, driven by strategic investments in cloud infrastructure and digital transformation services. The company has secured a ₹29.86 crore contract with Protean eGov Technologies Ltd for a five-year AWS infrastructure setup and support, enhancing its enterprise cloud capabilities.

Name: NINtec Systems Ltd
Overview: NINtec Systems Ltd, headquartered in Ahmedabad, India, is a forward-thinking global technology company specializing in software development and solutions. Established in 2015, the company offers a comprehensive range of IT services, including application development and maintenance, offshore software product development, legacy system development and software migration, and search engine optimization. NINtec Systems Ltd serves clients across various industries, leveraging cutting-edge technologies to deliver innovative solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

NINtec Systems Ltd is strategically positioning itself for future growth by expanding its service offerings and embracing emerging technologies. The company is actively investing in areas such as blockchain development, Internet of Things (IoT) solutions, process automation, and cognitive artificial intelligence (AI), aiming to enhance operational efficiency and deliver innovative solutions to clients

Name: Ivalue Infosolutions Ltd
Overview:
iValue Infosolutions Limited is an enterprise-technology solutions provider that helps organizations secure, manage, and transform their digital infrastructure. It offers a wide range of services such as cybersecurity, hybrid-cloud solutions, data-centre infrastructure, application lifecycle management, and managed services. The company works through Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and a large network of system integrators to reach corporate customers across India and several other countries in the SAARC and Southeast Asia regions. 

iValue Infosolutions began in 2008 as a private limited company and later converted into a public limited company. Over time, it has expanded its offerings to include more value-added services and built up strength in areas like managed security operations, data & application protection, compliance, and digital asset lifecycle management.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

iValue is focusing on strengthening its presence both domestically and in nearby international markets by leveraging its OEM partnerships and system integrator network to serve more enterprise customers. It plans to expand its managed and professional services, especially in cybersecurity, hybrid cloud, and data management, in response to rising demand for secure, scalable IT environments. The company is also investing in technical talent upskilling, certification, and internal training to handle complex technology projects and reduce delivery time.

Name: Allied Digital Services Ltd
Overview:
Allied Digital Services Ltd is a global provider of IT solutions and managed services, specializing in digital transformation, cybersecurity, cloud migration, and smart city solutions. The company serves enterprise clients and government agencies, offering end-to-end IT consulting, implementation, and infrastructure management.

With a strong reputation for executing complex projects across geographies, Allied Digital has built capabilities in IoT integration, AI-driven solutions, and next-gen enterprise automation. Its hybrid delivery model ensures a diversified revenue stream spanning recurring managed services and large-scale technology deployments.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
The outlook remains positive with strong demand for digital transformation, smart infrastructure, and cybersecurity solutions. Allied Digital plans to deepen its recurring managed services revenue while scaling up government and enterprise projects. With increasing reliance on secure and digitally enabled IT environments, the company is expected to sustain long-term growth, leveraging innovation and a strong execution track record.

Name: Aurum Proptech Ltd
Overview: Aurum PropTech Ltd, headquartered in Navi Mumbai, is a leading PropTech company in India, revolutionizing the real estate industry through an integrated technology ecosystem. The company offers proprietary software solutions, IT consulting, and application development services tailored to the real estate sector. Aurum's platform streamlines property transactions, enhances customer experiences, and boosts efficiencies across the real estate value chain. By combining deep technological expertise with comprehensive real estate domain knowledge, Aurum is pioneering the future of real estate in India.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With a strengthened ecosystem post-PropTiger acquisition, solid rental business growth, and improved margins, Aurum PropTech appears well-positioned for sustained momentum in FY26. As scale builds across its platforms and operating leverage improves, the company is on a trajectory to narrow losses and ultimately achieve profitability.

Name: Ksolves India Ltd
Overview:
Ksolves India Ltd, incorporated in 2014, is a CMMi Level 3 software development company specializing in advanced digital technologies. Its service offerings include AI/ML, Big Data, Salesforce, Odoo, DevOps, and enterprise-grade digital transformation solutions. The firm has delivered over 150 projects across industries such as BFSI, telecom, education, healthcare, and technology.

The company also develops proprietary products, including Salesforce plug-ins, Odoo ERP applications, and Data Flow Manager (DFM), its flagship Big Data solution. With a global presence spanning 30+ countries and offices in the USA, UAE, and India, Ksolves derives ~59% of revenues from North America and ~22% from India, reflecting a diversified geographic base.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management expects stronger growth as branding investments, leadership hires, and product adoption gain traction. Focus remains on scaling DFM, expanding Salesforce/AI verticals, and converting larger enterprise deals despite longer sales cycles. Order book continues to expand, though conversion timelines have stretched. Long term, Ksolves is targeting margin recovery, product-led revenue growth, and deeper penetration in the U.S. and enterprise markets.

Name: Quick Heal Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Quick Heal Technologies Ltd, founded in 1995 and headquartered in Pune, India, is a leading cybersecurity software company. The company specializes in developing security solutions for consumers, small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), enterprises, and government entities. Its product portfolio includes offerings such as Quick Heal Total Security and Seqrite enterprise solutions, which address endpoint protection, mobile security, zero-trust network access, and data privacy management.

Quick Heal has a significant domestic presence and has expanded internationally with subsidiaries in Japan, Africa, the United States, and the Middle East. The company emphasizes technological advancement and regulatory compliance to meet evolving cyber threats globally.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Quick Heal is focusing on expanding its mobile security segment, which has shown rapid growth. The company aims to enhance its product offerings and increase adoption among mobile users to drive future revenue growth.

Name: Subex Ltd
Overview:
Subex Ltd, established in 1992, is a global provider of Operations and Business Support Systems (OSS/BSS) to communication service providers (CSPs) worldwide. The company offers a range of solutions, including fraud management, revenue assurance, network analytics, and digital transformation services. Subex's products are designed to help CSPs optimize their operations, reduce costs, and enhance customer experience.

Headquartered in Bengaluru, India, Subex has a global presence with offices in North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. The company serves over 100 CSPs across more than 90 countries, making it one of the leading players in the telecom software industry. Subex's commitment to innovation and customer-centric solutions has positioned it as a trusted partner for CSPs seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Subex aims to leverage its AI-driven solutions to capitalize on the growing demand for digital transformation in the telecom industry. The company is focusing on expanding its customer base and enhancing its product offerings to address the evolving needs of communication service providers.

Name: Xchanging Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Xchanging Solutions Ltd is an IT services and business process outsourcing (BPO) company headquartered in India. The company provides technology solutions and services to various industries, including insurance, financial services, and healthcare.

In 2016, Xchanging Solutions Ltd was acquired by DXC Technology, a global IT services company. Despite the acquisition, Xchanging continues to operate under its brand, delivering specialized services to its clients.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Xchanging Solutions Ltd (XSL) is experiencing a positive growth trajectory, driven by strategic initiatives and operational enhancements. In the first quarter of FY 2025–26, the company reported a 2.5% quarter-on-quarter increase in consolidated revenues and an 11.2% rise in net profit, reflecting improved operational efficiency and market demand. The earnings per share (EPS) for the quarter stood at ₹1.20, indicating strong profitability.

Name: Genesys International Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Genesys International Corporation Ltd provides geospatial and mapping solutions across sectors like urban planning, infrastructure development, and disaster management. The company leverages advanced technologies and GIS expertise to deliver accurate and actionable spatial insights.

Serving government and private clients, Genesys has established a strong reputation in precision mapping and geographic data services. Its solutions are integral to planning, monitoring, and resource management activities.

The company focuses on innovation and service diversification to strengthen its market position, targeting domestic and international projects that require high-quality geospatial analytics.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Genesys International Corporation Ltd is strategically expanding its geospatial capabilities to meet the growing demand for advanced mapping solutions. The company is investing ₹270 crore over the next three years to enhance its 2D (WoNoBo) and 3D (Oyster) platform infrastructure, focusing on data centers, LiDAR scanners, SaaS platform development, and network upgrades. These investments aim to strengthen its position in the geospatial industry and support the development of AI-powered navigation and connected car solutions.

Name: Expleo Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Expleo Solutions Ltd is a global engineering, technology, and consulting service provider that partners with leading organizations to guide them through their business transformation. The company offers a range of services, including software validation and verification, digital business support, and consulting services.

With over 50 years of experience, Expleo Solutions Ltd has established itself as a trusted partner for organizations seeking to achieve operational excellence and future-proof their businesses. The company's expertise spans various industries, including banking, financial services, insurance, and manufacturing, enabling it to deliver tailored solutions that meet the unique needs of each client.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Expleo Solutions Ltd plans to expand its service offerings by incorporating emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and blockchain into its solutions. The company is also focusing on strengthening its presence in international markets to capitalize on global growth opportunities. These initiatives are expected to drive sustained growth and enhance shareholder value.

Name: Dynacons Systems & Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Dynacons Systems & Solutions Ltd is a well-established player in the Indian IT infrastructure services domain. The company specializes in system integration, data centre development, networking, cloud services, and managed infrastructure solutions. 

Through strategic partnerships with global technology vendors, it serves a broad spectrum of clients—including government bodies, PSUs, BFSI institutions, and corporates—offering end-to-end IT infrastructure deployment and maintenance. With a focus on turnkey delivery and managed services, Dynacons helps organizations build, manage, and modernize their IT infrastructure.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Dynacons is well-positioned for future growth driven by its strong order pipeline and expertise in critical IT infrastructure services. With strategic partnerships and a focus on enterprise and public sector clients, the company is expected to capitalize on rising demand for cloud, data center modernization, and managed services in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Digitide Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Digitide Solutions Ltd, incorporated in 2025 and headquartered in Bengaluru, India, is a leading provider of AI-driven digital transformation and business process solutions. The company offers a comprehensive suite of services, including AI-led digital solutions, data intelligence, and business process services. Digitide's technology and AI services encompass cloud migrations, infrastructure managed services, cybersecurity, digital assurance, digital engineering, ERP services, Generative AI (GenAI) solutions, and enterprise modernization. 

Its business process services include banking back-office operations, customer experience management, collections, and employee experience management. The company's products and platforms include AI-Driven Insurance Data Hub, Pulse.ai, Integrated Insurance Platform, SmartHR, SmartPay, Digital BPaaS, and Sequre.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Digitide Solutions Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to expand its offerings in AI-driven digital solutions and business process services to cater to the growing demand across various industries. Digitide aims to strengthen its position in the insurtech sector by enhancing its platforms and services, providing innovative solutions to insurance companies.

Name: Innovana Thinklabs Ltd
Overview:
Innovana Thinklabs Limited is an Indian technology company specializing in software development, IT consulting, and digital transformation services. The company provides a broad suite of solutions including custom applications, web and mobile app development, and software testing, tailored to meet diverse client requirements. With expertise in artificial intelligence, machine learning, data analytics, and cloud computing, Innovana supports businesses in adopting next-generation digital solutions that enhance efficiency and customer experience.

Beyond development, Innovana offers strategic IT consulting services, covering IT strategy, system integration, infrastructure optimization, and project management. Its portfolio further extends to cybersecurity, ERP implementation, and business intelligence solutions, enabling organizations to build secure, scalable, and intelligent IT systems. Backed by a team of skilled professionals, the company emphasizes customer-centricity, continuous support, and long-term client partnerships to deliver high-quality outcomes.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Innovana Thinklabs Ltd is strategically enhancing its software development capabilities and expanding its product offerings to drive future growth. The company is focusing on developing innovative solutions to meet the evolving needs of its customers. Additionally, Innovana Thinklabs is exploring new market opportunities to diversify its revenue streams and strengthen its market position. These initiatives are aimed at fostering long-term growth and delivering value to stakeholders.

Name: Onward Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Onward Technologies Ltd, established in 1991 and headquartered in Mumbai, is a prominent software outsourcing company specializing in Digital and Engineering Research & Development (ER&D) services. The company caters to global Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) across various industries, including Transportation & Mobility, Industrial Equipment & Heavy Machinery, and Healthcare & Lifesciences. 

With a strong presence in North America and Europe, Onward Technologies operates from 13 offices across six countries, supported by state-of-the-art offshore delivery centers in India.  Onward Technologies offers a comprehensive suite of services encompassing Mechanical Engineering, Embedded & Electronics Engineering, and Digital Engineering.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Onward Technologies is poised to capitalize on growing market opportunities in its key verticals. The company's strategic initiatives and focus on high-margin ER&D services position it well for sustained growth and value creation for its stakeholders.

Name: Kellton Tech Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Kellton Tech Solutions Ltd. provides digital transformation and technology services to businesses across industries, including banking and financial services, healthcare, manufacturing, retail, and e-commerce. Its offerings include digital transformation, enterprise solutions, cloud computing, data analytics, IoT, AI, and machine learning, helping organizations modernize IT infrastructure, optimize operations, and drive growth.

The company delivers end-to-end software development services, including custom applications, mobile and web development, and software testing and maintenance, alongside consulting and advisory services to align technology strategies with business objectives. With a global presence and focus on innovation, quality, and timely delivery, Kellton Tech empowers businesses to enhance competitiveness and achieve strategic goals in the digital era.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Kellton Tech Solutions Ltd is strategically enhancing its global footprint and service offerings to accelerate growth. The company has implemented the 'OneKellton' initiative, integrating its global operations to provide clients with a comprehensive suite of digital transformation solutions. This restructuring aims to streamline services and improve client engagement across its markets in the US, Europe, India, and Asia-Pacific.

Name: Excelsoft Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Excelsoft Technologies Ltd is a global vertical-SaaS company focused on the learning and assessment space. Its platforms include tools for online examinations, remote proctoring, digital textbooks and learning-management, which allow educational institutions, certification bodies and corporates to deliver assessments, manage learning and track learner success. 

The firm serves clients globally across many countries supporting millions of learners with scalable cloud-based systems.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Excelsoft aims to expand its reach by onboarding more global clients and increasing usage of its AI-powered assessment and proctoring products, leveraging demand for remote testing and digital learning. It plans to invest in infrastructure, upgrade its IT systems and strengthen platform capabilities to support large-scale assessments and learning solutions.

Name: Tracxn Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Tracxn Technologies Ltd, established in 2012, is a subscription-based platform that offers curated data on startups and private companies. Its primary product, the Tracxn Platform, serves as a comprehensive tool for deal sourcing, market intelligence, and competitive benchmarking. 

Headquartered in India, Tracxn has expanded its reach globally, with customers spanning over 50 countries. The company's revenue model is primarily subscription-based, providing recurring revenue streams.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Tracxn Technologies Ltd aims to capitalize on the increasing demand for data-driven insights in the startup ecosystem. The company plans to invest in enhancing its platform's capabilities, expanding its customer base, and exploring new market opportunities.

Name: Sahana Systems Ltd
Overview:
Sahana System Ltd, founded in 2012 and headquartered in Ahmedabad, is a leading Indian IT services company specializing in digital transformation solutions. The company offers a comprehensive range of services, including web and mobile application development, artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) solutions, blockchain technology, Internet of Things (IoT) integration, and cybersecurity. 

Sahana System Ltd caters to various industries such as banking, healthcare, education, defense, and retail, providing tailored solutions to meet the unique needs of each sector. In addition to its core IT services, Sahana System Ltd is engaged in the trading of computer hardware, including franking machines, computers, LEDs, and laptops.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sahana System Ltd is focused on expanding its presence in the IT services and hardware trading sectors by leveraging emerging technologies such as AI, ML, IoT, and blockchain. The company aims to enhance its service offerings to meet the evolving needs of its clients across various industries. Additionally, Sahana System Ltd plans to strengthen its position in the EV industry by establishing a network of EV charging stations.

Name: Hexaware Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Hexaware Technologies Ltd is a global IT services company that delivers digital transformation solutions powered by artificial intelligence, automation, data analytics, and cloud technologies. The company operates across multiple industries, including financial services, healthcare, travel, manufacturing, and professional services, positioning itself as a strategic digital partner for enterprises worldwide.

With a strong workforce and delivery centers in multiple geographies, Hexaware has achieved consistent revenue growth, crossing multi-billion-dollar revenues and sustaining strong profitability metrics. Its proprietary platforms, combined with domain expertise, enable it to compete effectively with larger IT players while maintaining an edge in agility and client-focused solutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company is likely to focus on deepening penetration in existing industries while expanding into newer digital domains. Investments in AI-driven solutions, increased global partnerships, and sector-focused offerings position Hexaware for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Intellect Design Arena Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 2011, Intellect Design Arena Limited is a global financial technology company providing banking, insurance, and capital market solutions. Its portfolio covers consumer banking, central banking, transaction banking, risk and treasury management, and insurance, delivered through a microservices-based, API-led, cloud-native architecture.

The company operates across multiple geographies with strong market presence in India, UK, Canada, and now the US. Its platform-led approach, anchored by eMACH.ai and Purple Fabric, focuses on recurring license-linked revenues, advanced AI integration, and large-scale digital transformation programs for global financial institutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to sustain ~20% annual revenue growth with profit growth outpacing revenues. AI-led solutions, deeper penetration in regulated markets, and expansion in North America are expected to drive medium-term growth towards a ₹4,000 Cr revenue aspiration within three years.

Name: Sagility India Ltd
Overview:
Sagility India Limited, incorporated in 2021 (formerly Berkmeer India Pvt. Ltd), is a technology-driven service provider focused on healthcare solutions for U.S.-based payers and providers. The company delivers end-to-end, technology-enabled services in areas such as administration, claims processing, revenue cycle management, and analytics. 

Built on automation and deep domain expertise, Sagility’s offerings aim to streamline healthcare operations and improve efficiency. Sapient infrastructure supports a global footprint—operating across multiple countries with practice centers that serve long-term and new clients. Its service model is geared toward creating scalable, digital-first solutions tailored for U.S. healthcare systems
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Moving forward, Sagility is well-positioned for sustained momentum, propelled by integration synergies, expanding service reach, and operational efficiencies. If its current trajectory continues, the company is poised to deliver stronger profitability and wider healthcare transformation impact through FY26 and beyond.

Name: Capillary Technologies India Ltd
Overview:
Capillary Technologies India Ltd is a software-as-a-service (SaaS) company that specialises in customer loyalty and engagement solutions for large brands and enterprises. It provides a cloud-native platform suite — including tools like Loyalty+, Engage+, Insights+, Rewards+ and a Customer Data Platform (CDP) — that helps companies run loyalty programmes, analyse customer data, and deliver personalized engagement across digital and physical channels. 

Founded originally in Bengaluru and now with a global presence spanning many countries, Capillary serves hundreds of brands across retail, FMCG, hospitality, fuel retail and other sectors. Its platform supports thousands of customer touchpoints and processes large volumes of transactions and data, enabling its clients to build long-term consumer relationships, drive repeat purchases, and manage omnichannel customer engagement effectively.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Capillary Technologies aims to expand both organically and inorganically by acquiring complementary firms and integrating them into its global loyalty-management ecosystem. It plans to deepen its geographical reach — especially in North America, Europe, and emerging Asian markets — while adding new enterprise clients across sectors such as retail, healthcare, travel, and e-commerce.

Name: LTIMindtree Ltd
Overview:
Larsen & Toubro Infotech Ltd (LTI) is a leading global IT services and consulting firm offering end-to-end technology solutions, including application development, enterprise platforms, infrastructure management, digital and cloud services, and industry-focused solutions. With the merger of Mindtree, the combined entity — LTIMindtree — has strengthened its market position, client reach, and capabilities to deliver large-scale digital transformation for global clients.
 
Business Segments ):
 
 North America (~66% of total revenue): Largest geography, driven by Banking & Financial Services, Insurance, Hi-Tech, and Manufacturing clients.
 
 Europe, India, APAC & RoW (~34% combined): Contribute remaining revenue across sectors like Retail, Media, Consumer Goods, Energy & Utilities, Pharma, and more.
 
 Sector mix: Key verticals include Banking & Financial Services (~30–35%), Insurance (~15–20%), Manufacturing & Hi-Tech (~15–20%), with the rest split across Retail, Media, Pharma, Consumer Goods, and Energy.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
The merger with Mindtree positions the combined entity as one of India’s largest IT services players, with significant potential for $1 billion in revenue uplift and 200–300 basis points margin expansion over four to five years. Growth will be driven by high-demand digital services, including cloud, AI, IoT, cybersecurity, and analytics, supported by a robust deal pipeline targeting large, integrated contracts. Strategic acquisitions and global expansion initiatives enhance niche capabilities and innovation, while the LTIM One GTM strategy enables cross-selling and operational synergies. The company is well-placed to capitalize on rising global IT outsourcing and digital transformation spending.

Name: Sonata Software Ltd
Overview:
Sonata Software Ltd is a global IT services and solutions provider with a strong footprint across the USA, Europe, Middle East, Australia, and India. Leveraging its proprietary Platformation™ framework, the company delivers modernization engineering, digital transformation, and AI-driven automation across core verticals including Healthcare & Life Sciences, BFSI, Retail/Manufacturing, and TMT.

Its service portfolio spans modernization services, digital engineering, cloud and infrastructure, data services, customer experience modernization, enterprise solutions, and AI-led automation. Sonata maintains deep partnerships with technology majors such as Microsoft, AWS, and Google, and has set an ambitious target of reaching $1.5 billion in revenue by FY27, aiming for international EBITDA margins in the low 20s.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Sonata aims to position itself as a differentiated modernization engineering leader, combining AI-first capabilities with proprietary IP to capture large strategic deals. The company targets significant growth in BFSI and HLS—projecting $250 million combined revenue from these verticals in the next 3–5 years—while addressing softness in Retail/Manufacturing through diversification and large deal wins. The AI pipeline and deep cloud partnerships are expected to drive higher-margin engagements, supporting its goal to reach $1.5 billion in revenue by FY27 with improved profitability.

Name: Tata Elxsi Ltd
Overview:
Tata Elxsi is a global company that provides design and technology services mainly for Automotive, Media, Communication, and Healthcare sectors. They handle everything from research and product design to software development and testing. The biggest part of their business comes from Transportation, which makes up more than half of their revenue.

Tata Elxsi is investing in new technologies like artificial intelligence and is growing its aerospace and defense services and expanding its presence worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Transportation is expected to drive growth  with media recovering from Q2 and healthcare remaining volatile but improving in the second half. Margins are likely to improve gradually with revenue recovery and operational focus on cost and efficiency.

Name: Tata Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Tata Technologies Limited, incorporated in 1994, is a global engineering services company specializing in product development and digital solutions for automotive, aerospace, and industrial sectors. The company partners with leading OEMs and Tier-1 suppliers to deliver design, engineering, and technology-driven manufacturing solutions, leveraging its deep domain expertise.

Its operations span multiple geographies, with a strong offshore delivery base in India, complemented by nearshore and onsite capabilities. Tata Technologies focuses on innovation-led growth, integrating advanced digital platforms, AI-driven solutions, and sustainability initiatives to strengthen its position as a preferred partner for global manufacturers.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Tata Technologies is strategically enhancing its capabilities in digital engineering and embedded software to meet the growing demand for software-defined vehicles (SDVs) and smart manufacturing solutions. The company has been selected as a strategic engineering supplier by Volvo Cars, marking a significant expansion of their long-standing relationship. Additionally, Tata Technologies is focusing on expanding its presence in key industries while strengthening its technological capabilities.

Name: Newgen Software Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Newgen Software is an Indian IT product company specializing in enterprise digital transformation solutions, particularly in automation, content management, and customer engagement platforms. The company’s low-code automation suite enables businesses to digitize complex processes across industries like BFSI, government, insurance, and healthcare.

With global operations across more than 70 countries, Newgen has been strengthening its positioning in the digital transformation wave. Its long-term investments in low-code/no-code platforms, AI-driven document processing, and workflow automation have created strong stickiness with large enterprise clients.
Key Takeways:
.Growth and Future Outlook
Management refrained from issuing formal guidance but indicated that historically the company has delivered 18–20% growth. Given current headwinds, growth trajectory is expected to be more volatile, with near-term pressures but a strong longer-term opportunity pipeline. Margins are likely to remain around 20% if topline growth stabilizes, supported by productivity gains from AI-led automation and steady R&D investments (9% of revenues).

Name: Birlasoft Ltd
Overview:
Birlasoft, part of the multibillion-dollar CK Birla Group, is a global technology and IT services company specializing in Cloud, AI, Data, and enterprise solutions. The firm serves key verticals including Banking, Financial Services & Insurance (BFSI), Manufacturing, Life Sciences & Services (LSS), and Energy & Utilities (E&U). Leveraging a workforce of over 11,800 professionals, Birlasoft focuses on delivering large-scale digital transformation programs while embedding next-generation AI capabilities like Agentic AI and Generative AI into client engagements.

In Q1 FY26, the company navigated a challenging demand environment marked by macroeconomic headwinds but achieved sequential growth in BFSI, LSS, and E&U. However, Manufacturing, its largest vertical, saw a decline due to project closures and ramp-downs. The company continues to maintain a robust balance sheet with cash and cash equivalents reaching $266.6 million, and remains focused on operational efficiency, cashflow generation, and selective capital allocation.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management expects deal momentum to improve as client decision-making accelerates in the coming quarters. The company is prioritizing AI-led transformation, operational efficiency, and strategic capital allocation to enhance margins and growth. The healthy cash position provides flexibility for investments in high-return initiatives, while expansion in BFSI, LSS, and E&U, along with scaling Digital & Data services, remains central to its growth strategy.

Name: Cyient Ltd
Overview:
Cyient Limited, founded in 1991 and headquartered in Hyderabad, is a global technology solutions company specializing in engineering, manufacturing, data analytics, and network operations. The company operates through four primary segments: Digital, Engineering & Technology (DET), Design Led Manufacturing (DLM), Cyient Semiconductors, and Others. Its DET segment delivers intelligent engineering solutions across transportation, connectivity, sustainability, and new growth areas, serving over 300 global clients, including 30% of the world’s top innovators.

With operations spanning the Americas, EMEA, and Asia-Pacific, Cyient has built a balanced portfolio across industries such as aerospace, rail, automotive, utilities, energy, healthcare, mining, and semiconductors. In FY25, the company completed the carve-out of its semiconductor business to target high-growth opportunities in ASIC/ASSP technologies and AI-led innovation. Cyient combines domain expertise, digital capabilities, and manufacturing strengths to address critical infrastructure, industrial transformation, and emerging technology demands.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management expects a stronger FY26 driven by DET recovery, continued scaling of DLM, and growth acceleration in Cyient Semiconductors. The company is targeting high-value deals, expanding its innovation ecosystem through partnerships (e.g., AICTE, MIPS), and leveraging leadership hires to strengthen domain expertise and operational execution.

Name: Firstsource Solutions Ltd
Overview:
BLS International, a part of the BLS Group, is a global leader in visa application outsourcing and related citizen services, operating in 62 countries with 2,325 offices and serving 36 government clients. The company primarily generates revenue from visa outsourcing, complemented by e-governance and business correspondent (BC) services. It has built a strong international presence, with the majority of revenue coming from overseas operations, supported by an asset-light, capital-light model that ensures high cash generation and financial flexibility.

In recent years, BLS has expanded its portfolio through strategic acquisitions such as iDATA, Citizenship Invest, and Aadifidelis, enhancing both geographic reach and service offerings. The transition to a self-managed model has improved margins by giving the company direct operational control. Its strong tender pipeline, focus on technology-driven services, and growth in digital offerings position BLS well to capture opportunities in the growing visa processing and e-governance sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
BLS aims to sustain Visa & Consular segment margins above 30% and grow at least 15–20% annually through a mix of organic expansion, strategic acquisitions, and increased value-added services. With only three qualified global players in its industry, significant market share gains, higher per-application revenues, and digital service growth are anticipated to drive strong medium-to-long-term performance.

Name: Happiest Minds Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Happiest Minds Technologies Ltd, incorporated in 2011, is a next-generation IT solutions and services company focusing on digital transformation, artificial intelligence, automation, cloud, analytics, and product engineering. The company operates across six verticals, with BFSI, Healthcare, and Hi-Tech forming key growth pillars.

Its strong track record of consistent revenue and profit growth, supported by strategic acquisitions and investments in emerging technologies like GenAI and automation, positions it well in a competitive global IT landscape. The business model emphasizes high-margin digital services, recurring customer relationships, and a diversified geographic presence.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to sustain double-digit growth and industry-leading margins by leveraging its verticalization strategy, expanding GenAI and automation offerings, and deepening customer relationships. The company is on track toward its $1 billion revenue target for FY31, supported by organic growth and selective acquisitions.

Name: BLS International Services Ltd
Overview:
BLS International is a global leader in visa application outsourcing, passport, consular, and citizen services, with operations spanning 62 countries and partnerships with 36 governments. Operating through over 2,300 offices worldwide, the company runs an asset-light and capital-light model, ensuring strong cash generation and efficient cost structures. Its revenue mix is dominated by the Visa & Consular segment, complemented by a growing digital services arm.

Through strategic acquisitions, including iDATA, Citizenship Invest, and Aadifidelis, BLS has strengthened both its global footprint and service portfolio. The shift from a partner-led to a self-managed operational model has structurally improved margins, enhanced cost control, and positioned the company to capitalize on rising global travel demand and expanding e-governance opportunities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company targets sustained 15–20% annual growth, underpinned by organic expansion, high-margin acquisitions, and increased value-added services. With a structurally improved margin profile, strong cash reserves, and a large tender pipeline, BLS is well-placed to capture market share and grow both its visa outsourcing and digital services businesses globally.

Name: Zensar Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Zensar Technologies, part of the RPG Group, is a leading digital solutions and technology services provider headquartered in Pune, India, with global operations across the USA, Europe, Africa, and Asia. It operates mainly through two segments — Digital and Application Services, and Digital Foundation Services — catering to industries like hi-tech, manufacturing, consumer services, BFSI, and healthcare. The company leverages automation, AI, and cloud-based solutions to deliver application modernization, infrastructure management, and enterprise-grade digital transformation.

The business is supported by a diversified client base with strong recurring engagements, including multiple multi-million-dollar accounts. Strategic initiatives in FY25 and FY26 focus on expanding high-margin digital offerings, strengthening presence in core geographies, and driving operational efficiency. The company maintains a healthy cash position and continues to invest in capability-building to address evolving client needs.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Zensar Technologies is actively enhancing its digital capabilities and expanding its global presence. The company is focusing on strengthening its Application Management and Infrastructure Management services, aiming to deliver innovative solutions across key industry verticals such as HiTech & Manufacturing, Consumer Services, and Banking, Financial Services & Insurance. Zensar is also investing in advanced technologies and partnerships to drive growth and operational efficiency. The company's commitment to innovation and excellence positions it for sustained growth and leadership in the digital solutions and technology services industry.

Name: Redington Ltd
Overview:
Redington Limited, established in 1993, is a leading IT, mobility, and technology solutions distributor with operations spanning 40 markets and presence in 32 countries. The company’s portfolio includes end-point solutions, enterprise technology, mobility products, cloud services, renewable energy offerings, logistics, and IT support services. Its deep vendor network covers ~450 global brands, including Apple, HP, Lenovo, Dell EMC, and Samsung, with top vendors contributing a significant portion of revenues.

In FY25, the company delivered robust performance, achieving $11.8 billion in revenue with strong ROE of 17% and ROCE of 21%. Its vertical mix remains well diversified — ESG (36.6%), TSG (30%), MSG (34.3%), and CSG (4.1%) — and geographically balanced between SISA (50.1%) and ROW (49.9%). Redington continues to maintain market leadership (#1 or #2) across all its served geographies, supported by 200+ warehouses and advanced distribution centers.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Redington aims to become a top-five global IT distributor, focusing on emerging markets with localized growth strategies and leadership positioning. The business is expected to benefit from trends in hybrid cloud adoption, AI integration, cybersecurity demand, and sustainable technology solutions.

Name: C.E. Info Systems Ltd
Overview:
C E Info Systems Limited (MapmyIndia) is India’s leading digital maps and geospatial technology provider, specializing in advanced digital maps, location-based IoT, and platform-based software solutions. Since its inception, the company has focused on pioneering digital mapping and offering high-value products for automotive, government, and enterprise customers, supporting India’s growing digital ecosystem.

The company’s revenue growth is driven by its Map-led business, which includes high-definition maps and digital twin technology, alongside emerging IoT ventures and strategic joint ventures focused on Southeast Asia. MapmyIndia continues to deepen its penetration in automotive and e-commerce sectors while expanding government and international engagements.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management remains confident in achieving the INR 1,000 crore revenue target by FY28, driven by sustained growth in Map-led products, IoT business turnaround, JV scaling, and expanding government and enterprise engagements. The company aims to leverage its product differentiation, technology leadership, and strategic partnerships to consolidate its market leadership and deliver sustainable profitability.

Name: Seshaasai Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Seshaasai Technologies Ltd, established in 1993 and headquartered in Mumbai, is a technology-driven solutions provider specializing in the banking, financial services, and insurance (BFSI) sectors. The company offers a comprehensive suite of services, including payment solutions, communication and fulfillment services, and Internet of Things (IoT) solutions. 

In the payments domain, Seshaasai manufactures various instruments such as debit and credit cards, prepaid cards, and wearable payment devices. Their communication services encompass the secure printing and distribution of documents like insurance policies and utility bills. The IoT division focuses on providing radio frequency identification (RFID) tags and related solutions for industries including retail, logistics, and manufacturing.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Seshaasai Technologies is focusing on expanding its Internet of Things (IoT) segment, aiming to increase its contribution to total revenue. The company is capitalizing on the growing demand for connected devices and RFID solutions across various industries. Additionally, Seshaasai plans to enhance its presence in underserved markets, leveraging its established infrastructure and client relationships.

Name: KPIT Technologies Ltd
Overview:
KPIT is a global technology company focused on embedded software and AI-driven digital solutions for next-generation mobility, including autonomous, clean, smart, and connected vehicles. It operates across key geographies with a strong global delivery footprint and specializes in transforming mobility through platform-led, AI-infused offerings. KPIT serves major OEMs primarily in passenger cars, commercial vehicles, and off-highway segments, with growing engagement in emerging markets such as India and China.

It maintains robust growth in revenues and profitability, supported by strong deal wins in powertrain and connected vehicle domains, while strategically investing in new geographies and technology areas like hydrogen and sodium-ion batteries.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects a flat first half of FY26 with growth picking up in the second half as macro headwinds ease. Europe is anticipated to be the primary growth driver, while India and China will gain significant revenue share within two years. The company is cautiously optimistic about maintaining momentum and increasing platform-driven business in the evolving mobility landscape.

Name: eClerx Services Ltd
Overview:
eClerx Services Ltd is a leading provider of business process management, automation, analytics, and AI-led solutions for Fortune 2000 companies across financial services, communications, retail, media, manufacturing, and technology sectors. 

The company has built a strong global delivery network, expanding into new geographies like Peru and Egypt to support client growth and diversification. With a focus on embedding GenAI and Agentic AI into proprietary platforms, eClerx is driving innovation in compliance, market intelligence, and omnichannel operations. Strategic M&A remains a lever for capability and client base expansion, while disciplined capital allocation supports shareholder value creation.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management remains cautiously optimistic for FY26, expecting sequential revenue growth in Q2 and stronger performance in H2. Expansion into new geographies, growth in analytics and automation, and continued investment in AI capabilities are expected to drive margins and topline growth, despite macroeconomic uncertainties and longer deal conversion cycles.

Name: eMudhra Ltd
Overview:
eMudhra is a licensed certifying authority in India operating under the IT Act and accredited by the Controller of Certifying Authorities. The company issues digital signature certificates and provides enterprise-grade cybersecurity, paperless transformation, and digital trust services across industries.

With a growing global footprint supported by acquisitions and partnerships, eMudhra is expanding in North America, Europe, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific. Its integrated offerings across identity, authentication, and trust services make it a key player in the digital security and compliance ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is optimistic about sustained cybersecurity demand globally. With increasing deal sizes, strong order book growth, new acquisitions, and geographic expansion, eMudhra expects to deliver robust growth in FY26 while strengthening its position in digital trust and enterprise cybersecurity solutions.

Name: RPSG Ventures Ltd
Overview:
RPSG Ventures Ltd is an investment holding company under the RP-Sanjiv Goenka Group, with interests spanning various sectors including FMCG, retail, and infrastructure. The company focuses on strategic investments and partnerships to drive growth and value creation across its portfolio. RPSG Ventures aims to leverage its diversified holdings to capitalize on emerging market opportunities.

In FY25, RPSG Ventures reported a consolidated net profit of ₹83.09 crore, marking a significant turnaround after three consecutive quarters of losses. This improvement was driven by strategic investments and operational efficiencies across its subsidiaries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

RPSG Ventures Ltd is undergoing a strategic transformation to position itself as a dynamic conglomerate with a focus on sustainable growth, efficiency, and innovation. The company is actively expanding its presence in diverse sectors, including IT services, business process management, FMCG, Ayurveda formulations, real estate, sports, and restaurants. Under the leadership of Shashwat Goenka, RPSG Ventures is committed to fostering resilience and value creation across its subsidiaries, aiming to build a brand that embodies sustainability and progress.

Name: R Systems International Ltd
Overview:
R Systems International Ltd is a global technology and digital transformation services provider, specializing in artificial intelligence, analytics, and knowledge services. The company partners with clients across various industries to drive digital transformation through a comprehensive suite of services, including product engineering, cloud enablement, quality assurance testing, and digital platforms and solutions.

With a strong presence in over 40 countries, R Systems leverages its expertise to deliver innovative solutions that address the evolving needs of its clients. The company's commitment to excellence and customer satisfaction has positioned it as a trusted partner in the digital transformation journey.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
R Systems is poised for continued growth, driven by its expanding service offerings and global presence. The company's focus on innovation and customer-centric solutions positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the digital transformation space. With a commitment to excellence and strategic investments, R Systems aims to sustain its upward trajectory and deliver value to its stakeholders.

Name: Aurionpro Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Aurionpro Solutions Ltd is a technology products and solutions provider with a strong presence in banking, financial services, transit, infrastructure, and government sectors. The company leverages its expertise in enterprise software, AI-driven platforms, and integrated hardware solutions to deliver high-value projects across global markets.

Its diversified portfolio spans transaction banking, lending platforms, transit automation, and data center design, supported by proprietary intellectual property and in-house manufacturing capabilities. Aurionpro’s strategy focuses on innovation, market expansion, and deep integration of AI across its offerings to strengthen competitiveness and client value.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Aurionpro Solutions Ltd is strategically expanding its global footprint and enhancing its technological capabilities to drive future growth. The company is focusing on increasing its presence in key international markets, including Europe, Africa, and North Asia, through targeted investments in sales and distribution channels.

Name: Latent View Analytics Ltd
Overview:
Latent View Analytics Ltd is a leading provider of analytics and data-driven solutions catering to blue-chip clients across technology, BFSI, CPG & retail, and industrial sectors. The company delivers advanced predictive analytics, consulting, data engineering, and digital transformation services, leveraging deep domain expertise and technology partnerships. It has maintained consistent revenue growth through expanding client engagements and strategic innovation.

The business benefits from a diversified client base, strong presence in key verticals, and growing partnerships, notably with Databricks. Latent View is also building capabilities in GenAI and agentic AI, positioning itself at the forefront of emerging technology trends. The company targets sustained high double-digit revenue growth with robust margins, supported by operational efficiency and expanding solution portfolios.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is confident of exceeding the historical 18-19% USD revenue growth, aiming to reach over 20% in FY26 and targeting $200 million revenue by FY28 (~26% CAGR). Growth drivers include strong BFSI demand, emerging CPG/retail opportunities, expanded GenAI adoption, and international expansion. Margin improvement and steady operating performance support a positive outlook.

Name: Sasken Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1989, Sasken Technologies Ltd. is a leading global provider of product engineering and digital transformation services. Headquartered in Bangalore, India, the company offers end-to-end R&D solutions across various industries, including semiconductors, automotive, industrials, consumer electronics, enterprise devices, satellite communications, and transportation. 

Sasken's expertise spans concept-to-market and chip-to-cognition services, catering to the evolving technological needs of its clients worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sasken Technologies is strategically enhancing its capabilities to lead in the evolving digital transformation landscape. The company is focusing on expanding its expertise in 5G, AI, IoT, and V2X technologies to support industries such as automotive, semiconductor, and telecommunications. Through its acquisition of Borqs Technologies, Sasken aims to strengthen its position in connected device development, enabling end-to-end solutions from concept to market.

Name: InfoBeans Technologies Ltd
Overview:
InfoBeans Technologies Ltd is an Indian IT services company specializing in software development and digital transformation solutions. Established in 2000, the company offers services such as enterprise mobility, cloud computing, and AI-led engineering to clients worldwide. 

InfoBeans collaborates with global technology platforms like Salesforce, ServiceNow, and Microsoft to deliver tailored solutions that enhance operational efficiency and competitiveness for large enterprises. The company is publicly listed and has demonstrated a strong operational and financial profile.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
InfoBeans Technologies Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its client base and enhancing its service offerings. The company's strong financial performance in Q1 FY26 positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the IT services sector.

Name: Saksoft Ltd
Overview:
Saksoft Ltd, established in 1999 by Autar Krishna and his son Aditya Krishna, specializes in providing business intelligence and information management solutions. The company primarily serves mid-tier companies based in the USA and UK, offering services that include data analytics, digital transformation, and AI-driven solutions. Saksoft's expertise lies in delivering tailored solutions that enhance business decision-making and operational efficiency.

With a focus on innovation and customer-centric services, Saksoft has established a strong presence in the IT services industry. The company's commitment to leveraging emerging technologies positions it as a reliable partner for businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital landscape. Saksoft's strategic initiatives aim to drive growth and deliver value to its clients in an ever-evolving market.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Saksoft Ltd is strategically investing in AI-driven product engineering and domain-based accelerators to enhance its value proposition. The company's focus on expanding its customer base and increasing revenue from existing clients positions it for sustained growth. With a strong emphasis on innovation and operational efficiency, Saksoft aims to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the IT services sector.

Name: Datamatics Global Services Ltd
Overview:
Datamatics Global Services Ltd is a digital solutions provider delivering services in digital technologies, operations, and experiences—spanning AI-first automation, BPM, and engineering solutions. The company serves clients across industries such as fintech, logistics, and enterprise applications.

With a global presence and in-house solutions like TruBot, TruCap, and TruAI, Datamatics differentiates through proprietary platforms and hybrid digital service models. Its diversified geographic reach includes markets like the US, Europe, and India.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company is targeting further margin expansion of 50–150 basis points in FY26 through rigorous cost control and business efficiencies. With sustained focus on strategic accounts, deeper penetration in Western markets, and growing adoption of its AI-driven platforms, Datamatics is well-positioned to drive continued growth and margin improvement through the year.

Name: Mastek Ltd
Overview:
Mastek Ltd is a global digital engineering and enterprise cloud services firm that helps organizations with AI-first, cloud-native, and transformation solutions. Built through strategic acquisitions and deep partnerships with Oracle, Salesforce, AWS, Microsoft, and others, it operates across digital engineering, automation, and AI.

With a presence in over 40 countries and a workforce exceeding 5,000 professionals, Mastek serves sectors such as healthcare, government, education, BFSI, and manufacturing, delivering platform modernization and managed services. Its AI-driven ADOPT.AI platform and Engineering CoE reinforce its positioning as a transformation partner for enterprise-grade clients.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With its growing AI-led deals, ADOPT.AI platform, and robust order pipeline, Mastek is well placed for sustainable growth in FY26. UK and healthcare verticals will be the primary growth engines, while US and AMEA performance needs stabilization to unlock full potential.

Name: Nucleus Software Exports Ltd
Overview:
Nucleus Software Exports Ltd is a fintech software provider specializing in digital lending and transaction banking platforms. Its flagship offerings—FinnOne Neo® for lending and FinnAxia® for corporate and transaction banking—cater to banks and financial institutions globally, enabling seamless digital transformations.

With over three decades of domain expertise, the company supports more than 200 financial institutions across 50+ countries. Its scalable, API-driven architecture processes millions of daily transactions while serving diverse segments such as retail, corporate, automotive, Islamic finance, and financial inclusion.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
As FY26 begins, the company has started with a confident Q1, with consolidated revenue near ₹217–235 crore and PAT around ₹35 crore, affirming continued demand for its digital platforms. Strategic emphasis remains on AI-first enhancements, cloud adoption, and global expansion to drive next-phase scale and margin growth.

Name: Magellanic Cloud Ltd
Overview:
Magellanic Cloud Ltd is a digital technology firm delivering solutions across IT services, e-surveillance, AI, and drone systems. The company focuses on enterprise-grade digital transformation and monitoring platforms for sectors like surveillance, fintech, and defense.

Strategically positioned, Magellanic Cloud integrates cutting-edge technology into high-growth verticals, combining software innovation with domain-specific engineering to serve diverse institutional clients across geographies.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Magellanic Cloud is well-poised for FY26 growth with a growing Total Contract Value (~₹100 crore) across IT, drone, and surveillance verticals. Expansion into FinTech analytics and drone-enabled offerings should fuel margin-rich growth. Strong topline visibility, continued innovation, and platform expansion position the company for sustained growth in enterprise transformations.

Name: Ramco Systems Ltd
Overview:
Ramco Systems Ltd, incorporated in 1997 and part of the Ramco Group, is a cloud-first enterprise software company. It develops and delivers multi-tenant ERP, Global Payroll, Aviation MRO, Enterprise Asset Management, and Logistics solutions, serving asset- and service-centric industries worldwide. The company’s offerings are deployed on both cloud and on-premise platforms, enabling flexibility and global reach.

With a footprint in 35+ countries and over 20 lakh users, Ramco has emerged as a key ERP player across sectors such as aviation, logistics, manufacturing, cement, infrastructure, defense, and staffing services. It is also a recognized solutions provider for aviation MRO and defense asset management. Partnerships with leading global firms like Microsoft, Honeywell, AWS, Deloitte, and KPMG have reinforced its market positioning.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Ramco aims to deepen its footprint in aviation, defense, and logistics verticals, while continuing to scale its Global Payroll platform in developed markets.
