With a strong presence across urban and semi-urban regions, Niva Bupa has been expanding rapidly to address India’s underpenetrated health insurance market.

Over the years, Niva Bupa has built a wide hospital network and robust digital ecosystem to simplify policy issuance, claims, and renewals. It has strategically positioned itself as a customer-first insurer, focusing on affordable coverage, quick settlement, and value-added services like health check-ups and wellness apps. Its positioning in India’s fast-growing healthcare ecosystem continues to strengthen.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Niva Bupa is poised to capitalize on India’s low insurance penetration, targeting deeper penetration in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities. Strategic focus remains on digital distribution, bancassurance tie-ups, and innovative wellness-linked insurance products, which should sustain strong growth momentum in FY26.

Name: New India Assurance Company Ltd
Overview:
New India Assurance Company Ltd, promoted by the Government of India with an ~86% stake, is the largest non-life insurance provider in India. Established in 1919 by Sir Dorabji Tata and nationalised in 1973, it later became an autonomous entity when GIC transitioned into a reinsurance company in 1999. The company operates with a diversified portfolio across fire, marine, health, motor, and miscellaneous segments.

Its domestic presence is extensive, comprising 1,668 offices including regional, large, medium, and small business centers along with corporate broker and auto hub offices. Internationally, it maintains operations in 25 countries through branches, agencies, subsidiaries, and associates, representing 9% of its AUM. The company also operates an IFSC office in GIFT City and maintains a significant global insurance presence including participation at Lloyd’s of London.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

New India Assurance is steering toward stronger and more profitable growth by introducing fresh insurance solutions tailored for retail customers and MSMEs, alongside exploring innovative offerings like parametric insurance for better risk coverage. It plans to broaden market reach by tapping into underserved segments, aiming to boost its footprint and market share. The company is also enhancing digital adoption and efficiency to cut costs and simplify operations.

Name: SBI Life Insurance Company Ltd
Overview:
SBI Life Insurance is one of India’s leading life insurance and annuity providers, operating as a joint venture between State Bank of India and BNP Paribas Cardif. It offers a broad product range covering individual and group plans, pensions, annuities, ULIPs, and more. With 825 offices, 24,000 SBI branches, over 170,000 agents, and multiple partners like brokers and corporate agents, the company has a vast, diversified distribution network supported by strong digital channels.
 
 Business Segments ):
 
 Individual Business (~83–85% of total revenue): Includes participating & non-participating life insurance, unit-linked insurance plans (ULIPs), pension, and annuity products sold directly to individuals.
 
 Group Business (~15–17% of total revenue): Includes group insurance products like gratuity, leave encashment, superannuation, group immediate annuity, and other employer-related life insurance solutions for corporates and institutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
SBI Life Insurance’s growth is supported by expanding distribution through new partnerships and digital channels, alongside leveraging its strong bancassurance network across 24,000 SBI branches. Continuous product innovation addresses diverse customer needs, including rising demand for protection, retirement, and annuity products in India.

Name: HDFC Life Insurance Company Ltd
Overview:
HDFC Life Insurance is a leading private sector life insurer in India, offering a wide range of individual and group life insurance products, including protection, savings, pension, and annuity plans. Established as a JV between HDFC Ltd. and Standard Life Aberdeen, the company holds a strong position in the life insurance space and ranks consistently among the top three private life insurers. Its extensive reach is enabled through 270+ partnerships with banks, small finance banks, MFIs, and other financial institutions, alongside its strong tie-up with HDFC Bank.
 
Business Segments 
 
 Individual Protection Plans & Pure Term: ~7–10% of new business premium — focused on life cover for individuals (e.g., Click 2 Protect 3D Plus).
 
 Savings & Participating Plans: ~40–50% — includes traditional endowment, savings, and guaranteed products.
 
 Non-Participating & Annuity Products: ~25–30% — includes guaranteed income and pension/annuity solutions.
 
 Group Business (Credit Protect & Group Term): ~20–25% — driven by partnerships with banks and MFIs to cross-sell credit protection.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
HDFC Life is well-positioned for long-term growth, supported by low insurance penetration in India, rising incomes, and increasing awareness of financial security. The company aims to expand its high-margin protection and annuity segments while leveraging cross-sell opportunities through its extensive bancassurance and microfinance partnerships. Efficiency gains and scale benefits from the Exide Life merger will enhance cost optimization, and continued product innovation, along with tech-driven customer onboarding, will improve reach and conversion.

Name: Go Digit General Insurance Ltd
Overview:
Go Digit General Insurance Ltd, incorporated in 2016, is a digital full-stack non-life insurance provider offering a wide range of products, including motor, health, property, marine, travel, and liability insurance. With a strong technology backbone, the company enables customers to customize insurance solutions while maintaining a seamless experience across distribution and claim processing.

The company is backed by Fairfax Financial (holding ~33.3%) and Mr. Kamesh Goyal with Oben Ventures (~40.3%). It has transitioned to profitability post-FY22 by optimizing underwriting practices and enhancing investment income. Digit has rapidly scaled its operations with over ₹20,000 crore in AUM and significant growth in both top and bottom lines, supported by its extensive digital partner network and API-led policy issuance.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Go Digit General Insurance is poised for robust expansion, leveraging its digital-first approach to cater to India's growing insurance demand. The company has experienced significant growth, with its customer base reaching 71 million and a 14% increase in gross written premiums.  In response to evolving market dynamics, Go Digit is enhancing its product offerings and exploring new distribution channels to further penetrate the market.

Name: Star Health & Allied Insurance Company Ltd
Overview:
Star Health & Allied Insurance Ltd, incorporated in 2006, is India’s first and largest Standalone Health Insurance (SAHI) company. It has established itself as the market leader in retail health insurance with a strong pan-India presence. The company offers specialized health insurance policies including disease-specific and demographic-specific offerings, catering to a wide consumer base.

Its business model is deeply rooted in a diversified distribution strategy with a strong focus on agency networks, bancassurance, and digital partnerships. With an expansive presence of over 789,000 agents and more than 914 branches as of Q1 FY26, Star Health stands out for its scale, customer focus, and retail-centric approach. The company is backed by prominent investors like WestBridge Capital and the late Rakesh Jhunjhunwala’s family.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Star Health & Allied Insurance Ltd is intensifying its focus on the retail health insurance sector, aiming to significantly increase its premium income from ₹17,500 crore in FY25 to ₹30,000 crore within the next three years. This ambitious growth strategy includes enhancing its retail business while limiting exposure to large corporate group insurance segments. The company is also working to improve profitability by reducing its combined ratio from over 100% to 97%.

Name: Aegis Vopak Terminals Ltd
Overview:
Aegis Vopak Terminals Ltd is India’s largest independent tank storage provider for LPG and a wide range of liquid products. A joint venture between Aegis Logistics and Royal Vopak, it operates around 18–20 strategically located terminals across major Indian ports, offering a combined capacity of approximately 1.5 million cubic meters (liquids) and over 70,000 metric tons (LPG). Its terminals are equipped with advanced connectivity—jetty access, pipeline, rail, and road linkages—delivering efficient, high-volume logistics services.

Firmly rooted in safety, sustainability, and operational expansion, AVTL plays a critical role in India’s energy and chemical logistics infrastructure, with continuous investments in new capacity and terminal enhancements.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Planned capacity expansions and deeper integration with global supply chains could drive double-digit growth. Its JV with Royal Vopak ensures access to international best practices, enhancing competitiveness.

Name: JSW Infrastructure Ltd
Overview:
JSW Infrastructure Limited, part of the JSW Group, is a leading integrated maritime services provider engaged in cargo handling, storage solutions, and logistics operations. It is the second-largest commercial port operator in India by capacity and also operates overseas terminals, with a diversified portfolio across commodities and geographies.

The company’s growth strategy focuses on capacity expansion, strengthening third-party cargo share, and enhancing its integrated logistics network. With a balanced mix of organic developments and strategic acquisitions, JSW Infrastructure aims to leverage captive cargo as well as high-potential third-party opportunities to drive sustainable long-term growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to sustain double-digit volume growth in FY26, with stronger H2 performance due to seasonal factors. Capacity expansion, privatization bids, and integration of recent acquisitions are expected to boost throughput and revenue. With assured cargo agreements, low leverage, and a robust capex pipeline, JSW Infrastructure is positioned for steady margin expansion and long-term growth in both port and logistics segments.

Name: Blue Dart Express Ltd
Overview:
Blue Dart Express Limited, incorporated in 1988, is a leading courier and integrated air express package distribution company in South Asia. The company offers time-definite and day-definite delivery solutions across domestic and international markets, catering to industry-specific needs such as life sciences, healthcare, and e-commerce logistics. With an asset-light model, it operates through a strong air and ground network, supported by advanced technology, to ensure service reliability and operational efficiency.

Blue Dart operates six Boeing 757-200 and two Boeing 737-800 aircraft, covering over 56,400 locations with 12,725+ employees. The company has subsidiaries Blue Dart Aviation Ltd. and Concorde Air Logistics Ltd., and benefits from the global network of DHL Express, which holds a 75% stake. Leveraging its leadership in the B2B air express segment, Blue Dart continues to expand its e-commerce reach, invest in automation, and pioneer innovative delivery methods, including drone-based services, to enhance customer experience and operational sustainability.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Blue Dart aims to maintain its leadership in air and ground express segments by expanding coverage to 98% of India’s GDP, enhancing air fleet capabilities, and adopting next-generation logistics technologies. With supportive government policies, e-commerce growth, and infrastructure upgrades, the company is well-positioned for sustainable volume and revenue growth while maintaining profitability and shareholder returns.

Name: Adani Ports & Special Economic Zone Ltd
Overview:
Adani Ports & SEZ is India’s largest commercial port operator, managing a network of 15 ports and terminals and commanding about 25% of India’s cargo movement. The company is expanding into integrated logistics solutions—rail, multimodal parks, warehouses, cold storage, and more—aiming to be the world’s largest private port operator and India’s top integrated transport utility by 2030.
 
 Business Segments & Latest Revenue (FY2025, )
 Ports & Terminals:
 The cornerstone segment, contributing approximately 75% of consolidated revenue, or around ₹23,310 crore. This includes cargo handling, container throughput, marine services, and port-based operations.
 
 Logistics (Rail, Warehousing, Hinterland Connectivity):
 Around 12% of revenue, approximately ₹3,730 crore. The company has ramped up integrated logistics parks, rail, and warehousing services across key hinterland locations.
 
 Special Economic Zones (SEZ) & Industrial Land Leasing:
 Contributes 8% to total revenues, or about ₹2,490 crore, through SEZ development and industrial zone leasing.
 
 Other/Ancillary (O&M, Services):
 Remaining 5% (~₹1,550 crore) covers operations & maintenance for third-party terminals, marine services for non-Adani ports, and smaller business lines.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
APSEZ is focused on aggressive network expansion, including new terminals, international acquisitions, and capacity ramp-ups at key ports like Mundra and Vizhinjam, driving long-term cargo growth. The company is advancing its integrated logistics strategy with investments in rail, multimodal parks, warehousing, and inland logistics to offer end-to-end supply chain solutions. Sustainability remains a core priority, aiming for global carbon neutrality by 2025 and net zero emissions by 2040 through electrification, onsite renewable generation, and mangrove afforestation.

Name: Interglobe Aviation Ltd
Overview:
IndiGo is India's largest passenger airline and the world's sixth-largest carrier by passenger numbers, known for affordable fares, on-time performance, and an extensive network spanning both domestic and international destinations.
 
 Key Business Segments and Operational Scale
 Domestic Operations:
 Operates across 93 domestic destinations in India, with deep connectivity into non-metro and Tier-2/Tier-3 cities. Over 2,200 daily flights are operated, making IndiGo the leader in India's aviation market.
 
 International Expansion:
 Flies to 39 international destinations (including recent additions—Jakarta, Nairobi, Almaty, Baku, Tbilisi, Tashkent, among others), serving Southeast Asia, Central Asia, Middle East, and Africa. The international network continues to expand rapidly via both direct flights and codeshare partnerships.
 
 Fleet Size and Orders:
 As of June/July 2025, IndiGo operates a fleet of 411–437 aircraft (the largest in India), consisting of Airbus A320s (ceo and neo), A321neos, ATR 72-600s, and Boeing 777/787s on wet-lease for international/long-haul.
 
 Current Fleet: ~437 aircraft.
 
 On Order: 946 aircraft including narrowbody (A320neo, A321neo, A321XLR) and widebody (60 A350-900s plus options), giving IndiGo the largest firm-order book globally for single-aisle jets
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
IndiGo is poised for sustained growth, driven by rapid fleet expansion—including entry into wide-body, long-haul segments—and deepening its domestic and international network across Asia, Africa, the Middle East, and Europe. Strategic codeshare partnerships with global carriers unlock new premium and long-haul routes while minimizing upfront capital outlay.

Name: Shadowfax Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Shadowfax Technologies Ltd is an Indian logistics solutions provider that focuses on last-mile delivery and supply chain services for e-commerce, food delivery and quick commerce companies. The company offers a wide range of services including parcel delivery, hyperlocal logistics, reverse logistics and line-haul operations, enabling businesses to efficiently move goods from warehouses to end customers. Its asset-light model leverages a large network of delivery partners to ensure flexible and scalable operations across urban and semi-urban regions.

The company has built a strong technology-driven platform that uses data analytics, route optimisation and real-time tracking to improve delivery efficiency and reduce turnaround time. Shadowfax serves major e-commerce platforms, direct-to-consumer brands and enterprises, helping them manage logistics seamlessly.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Shadowfax Technologies aims to expand its logistics network by increasing its delivery partner base and strengthening its presence in tier-2 and tier-3 cities. The company is focusing on enhancing its technology platform with better automation, route optimisation and data analytics to improve efficiency and reduce costs. It also plans to diversify its service offerings across segments like quick commerce, same-day delivery and B2B logistics solutions.

Name: Delhivery Ltd
Overview:
Delhivery Limited is India's largest and fastest-growing fully integrated logistics service provider. It offers a comprehensive range of supply chain solutions, including express parcel delivery, part truckload (PTL) and truckload (TL) freight services, cross-border logistics, warehousing, and supply chain software. The company was founded with a vision to enable reliable, scalable, and technology-driven logistics operations across India and globally.

The company operates on an asset-light model, partnering with third-party fleet operators and delivery agents while leveraging automation and AI-led data intelligence platforms. As of Q1 FY26, Delhivery maintained operations in over 220 countries and served 18,857 pin codes across India through 119 gateways, 45 automated sort centers, and a delivery team exceeding 65,000 personnel. The company recently acquired Ecom Express and is undergoing a strategic rationalization of assets and services.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Delhivery is strategically expanding its logistics network and service offerings to capitalize on the growing demand for efficient supply chain solutions. The company is enhancing its infrastructure by establishing new hubs and expanding existing ones, such as the state-of-the-art facility in Lucknow, to improve operational efficiency and capacity. Additionally, Delhivery is investing in technology and automation to streamline operations and reduce costs, aiming to achieve sustainable profitability.

Name: Container Corporation Of India Ltd
Overview:
Container Corporation of India Ltd (CONCOR) is a leading PSU logistics player specializing in multimodal transport and containerized cargo movement in India. It operates a robust network of rail-linked Inland Container Depots (ICDs) and Container Freight Stations (CFS), which serve as critical nodes connecting hinterland cargo to ports. The company plays a pivotal role in India’s EXIM (export-import) and domestic logistics ecosystem, leveraging rail, road, and warehousing solutions.
 
 Business Segments
 EXIM Operations:
 Major revenue driver, focused on international containerized cargo movement, customs bonded warehousing, and efficient rail-port connectivity.
 
 Domestic Operations:
 Provides inland container transport, warehousing, LCL (Less than Container Load) consolidation, cold chain logistics, and last-mile delivery for domestic trade.
 
 Value-Added Services:
 Includes container repair, storage, CFS services, bonded warehousing, air cargo clearance, and subsidiary operations like Fresh & Healthy Enterprises Limited (FHEL) for agri-logistics.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Container Corporation of India (CONCOR) is actively expanding its capabilities to strengthen its position as a comprehensive logistics provider. The company is enhancing its multimodal infrastructure by investing in new terminals, cold chain facilities, and expanding warehousing capacity. Additionally, CONCOR is exploring the introduction of IceBattery® technology, which offers a sustainable solution for cold chain logistics by reducing energy consumption and CO₂ emissions. The company is also venturing into port operations, as evidenced by a recent Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with BPIPL to operate a terminal in Gujarat, aiming to cater to the logistics needs of Central Gujarat and the Dholera Industrial Belt.

Name: Zinka Logistics Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Zinka Logistics, popularly known as BlackBuck, is India’s largest digital freight and trucking network, transforming the logistics sector through its technology-driven marketplace. Established in 2015, the company connects truck operators with shippers while also offering ancillary services such as tolling, fuel management, and fleet tracking solutions. Its platform enhances efficiency by digitizing transactions and improving utilization rates across India’s vast trucking ecosystem.

The company has successfully scaled operations to reach nearly a third of India’s truck operators, making it one of the most widely adopted logistics platforms in the country. Beyond freight matching, it has diversified into financial services, including vehicle financing and lending for used trucks. By integrating payments, telematics, and marketplace services, BlackBuck has become a one-stop ecosystem for the trucking community.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With India’s logistics sector undergoing rapid digital transformation and infrastructure expansion, BlackBuck is well-positioned to capture rising demand and consolidate its leadership in digital freight.

Name: Allcargo Terminals Ltd
Overview:
Allcargo Terminals Limited (ATL), incorporated in 2019, is India’s largest Container Freight Station (CFS) and Inland Container Depot (ICD) operator, and part of the Allcargo Group. The company provides containerized cargo handling, warehousing, customs clearance, bonded storage, and specialized cargo solutions across major ports such as JNPT, Mundra, Chennai, and Kolkata.

With seven CFS facilities and one ICD at Dadri, ATL has a total installed capacity of 8.3 lakh TEUs and operates at high utilization levels of 85–90%. Its market share in non-DPD addressable volumes at JNPT, India’s largest port, is around 15%. The company is actively scaling capacity, leveraging digital tools like myCFS 2.0, and aligning with large infrastructure projects such as the Dedicated Freight Corridor (DFC) and the Haryana Orbital Rail Corridor to strengthen multimodal connectivity.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Allcargo Terminals Ltd (ATL) is poised for significant expansion in India's logistics sector. The company plans to increase its container handling capacity by 65%, aiming to reach 1 million TEUs by FY28. This growth will be achieved through the expansion of existing facilities and the development of new Container Freight Stations (CFS) and Inland Container Depots (ICD). Currently, ATL operates seven facilities across five hubs, handling approximately 80% of India's EXIM trade. The company is also raising ₹38.28 crore through the issuance of fully convertible warrants to fund these expansion plans.

Name: Gujarat Pipavav Port Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat Pipavav Port Limited (GPPL) is India’s first private sector port strategically located on the southwest coast of Gujarat. It operates under a concession agreement to manage APM Terminals' facilities until 2028, handling a diversified cargo mix including containers, dry bulk, liquid bulk, and Roll-on/Roll-off (RoRo). The port benefits from its location on key international maritime trade routes connecting India with major global markets.

The company’s business is marked by strong growth in RoRo and liquid bulk segments, while container volumes face challenges due to geopolitical disruptions and market conditions. GPPL continues to focus on operational efficiency and capacity expansions like the upcoming liquid jetty, with a steady emphasis on maintaining robust margins and prudent capital allocation.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management projects robust RoRo volume growth of around 40% for FY26 and moderate liquid bulk growth of 5-7%. Container volumes may see a cautious 3-5% increase but remain uncertain due to external factors. EBITDA margins are expected to hold steady in the 59-60% range. Capital spending will continue conservatively without debt raising, balancing expansion and shareholder returns.

Name: Glottis Ltd
Overview:
Glottis Limited is a logistics solutions company that provides integrated transportation and supply chain services across multiple modes of transport. The company offers logistics services through ocean freight, air freight, and road transportation, enabling the movement of goods both domestically and internationally. Its services include freight forwarding, cargo handling, customs clearance, and end-to-end logistics coordination for businesses across various industries.

The company focuses on providing reliable and efficient logistics solutions by managing the entire transportation process from origin to destination. Glottis Limited works with shipping lines, airlines, and transport partners to ensure timely delivery of goods and smooth cargo movement.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its logistics network and strengthen its multimodal transportation capabilities to support the rising demand for global trade and supply chain services. It aims to enhance its presence in international freight forwarding while increasing its footprint in domestic road transportation. The company is also focusing on adopting digital technologies and logistics management systems to improve operational efficiency and real-time cargo tracking.

Name: GKW Ltd
Overview:
GKW Ltd is a long-established company primarily focused on warehousing and investment & treasury operations. Its warehousing arm leases covered and open space to industries such as e-commerce, retail, agriculture, and manufacturing from its logistics hub in Howrah, West Bengal. The company also derives income from its investment portfolio, including interests, dividends, and mark-to-market gains from financial instruments.

Operating an integrated business model, GKW balances its steady rental income with returns from a diversified investment strategy. This blend of real asset backing (warehousing) and financial assets (treasury services) positions the company to navigate economic cycles with resilience and flexibility.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
GKW’s growth trajectory hinges on its ongoing capacity expansion in warehousing and prudent allocation of its investment portfolio. If global markets stabilize and leasing demand sustains, the company is well-positioned to build on this momentum and potentially deliver consistent profitability throughout FY26.

Name: Dreamfolks Services Ltd
Overview:
DreamFolks Services Ltd, established in 2013 and headquartered in Gurugram, is India's largest airport service aggregator. The company offers a technology-driven platform that enhances the airport experience for passengers by providing access to lounges, food and beverage services, spa treatments, and other travel-related amenities. With a presence in over 121 countries, DreamFolks has become a key player in the global travel and lifestyle services industry. 

Initially focused on lounge access, DreamFolks has diversified its offerings to include a wide range of lifestyle services, catering to the evolving needs of travelers. The company's strategic partnerships with various service providers and its expansion into new markets have positioned it for continued growth and success in the competitive travel services sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
DreamFolks aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its service offerings and enhancing its technology platform. The company plans to onboard additional enterprise clients and explore new markets to drive revenue growth. With a focus on innovation and customer satisfaction, DreamFolks is well-positioned to capitalize on the increasing demand for premium travel services.

Name: Shreeji Shipping Global Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1995, Shreeji Shipping Global Limited is a prominent Indian shipping and logistics company specializing in the handling and transportation of dry bulk cargo, including coal, grain, cement, and ores. The company operates a fleet of over 80 vessels, comprising barges, mini bulk carriers, tugboats, and floating cranes, and possesses more than 370 units of earthmoving equipment such as excavators, payloaders, tippers, and trailers. 

Headquartered in Jamnagar, Gujarat, Shreeji Shipping Global Limited provides integrated services across more than 20 ports and jetties in India, with a focus on non-major ports along the western coast, including Kandla, Navlakhi, Magdalla, Bhavnagar, Bedi, and Dharmatar.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Shreeji Shipping Global Limited is poised for significant growth, with plans to expand its fleet by acquiring Supramax dry bulk carriers through the secondary market, utilizing a substantial portion of its Initial Public Offering (IPO) proceeds for this purpose. The company aims to enhance its operational capacity and service offerings to meet the increasing demand in the dry bulk cargo sector.

Name: SpiceJet Ltd
Overview:
SpiceJet Ltd is a prominent Indian airline primarily engaged in providing air transport services for passengers and cargo. Established in 2005, the airline has grown to become one of the leading low-cost carriers in the country, offering both domestic and international flight services. SpiceJet operates a fleet of aircraft, including Boeing 737 and Q400 models, catering to a wide range of travel needs.

The airline's business model focuses on cost efficiency, operational flexibility, and customer satisfaction. Over the years, SpiceJet has expanded its network, introduced various service offerings, and implemented strategic initiatives to strengthen its market position. The company's commitment to innovation and quality service continues to drive its growth in the competitive aviation industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
SpiceJet aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its fleet and network. The airline's focus on operational efficiency and customer satisfaction is expected to bolster its market position. With strategic initiatives in place, SpiceJet is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the aviation sector.

Name: Allcargo Logistics Ltd
Overview:
Allcargo Logistics, incorporated in 1993, is a leading integrated logistics solutions provider with a diversified service portfolio covering multimodal transport operations, container freight stations (CFS), inland container depots (ICD), contract logistics, and project & engineering services. The company operates both domestically and internationally, with its core business anchored in global supply chain operations.

Through its international arm, ECU Worldwide, Allcargo has built strong capabilities in LCL, FCL, and air freight forwarding, complemented by its domestic express and contract logistics segments. With an asset-light approach, a focus on technology upgrades, and strategic operational enhancements, the company aims to strengthen its position across key logistics verticals and geographies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Allcargo Logistics is strategically enhancing its infrastructure and service offerings to capitalize on the expanding logistics and supply chain sectors. The company is actively increasing its warehousing capacity, with plans to add 3 million square feet over the next 2–3 years. Additionally, Allcargo is expanding its terminal operations at major ports, including acquiring land in Mundra and Chennai. The company is also embracing digital transformation by integrating with the Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) and implementing green energy practices.

Name: Gateway Distriparks Ltd
Overview:
Gateway Distriparks Ltd. (GDL) is a leading integrated inter-modal logistics service provider in India, specializing in containerized cargo transportation and warehousing solutions. The company operates a network of Inland Container Depots (ICDs) and Container Freight Stations (CFSs) strategically located across the country. Additionally, GDL offers value-added services such as bonded warehousing, rail and road transportation, and first & last mile connectivity to support the EXIM industry.

GDL expanded its service offerings by acquiring Snowman Logistics, a prominent cold chain logistics provider. This acquisition has enabled GDL to diversify its portfolio and strengthen its position in the logistics sector. The company is also focusing on enhancing its digital capabilities and expanding its infrastructure to meet the growing demand for efficient logistics solutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
GDL aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its infrastructure and service offerings. The company plans to increase its market share in the cold chain logistics sector through the expansion of Snowman Logistics. Additionally, GDL is focusing on enhancing its digital capabilities to improve operational efficiency and customer service. With ongoing strategic initiatives and a strong market presence, GDL is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for integrated logistics solutions in India.

Name: TVS Supply Chain Solutions Ltd
Overview:
TVS Supply Chain Solutions Ltd is a leading integrated supply chain and logistics service provider in India, operating across two core businesses: Integrated Supply Chain Solutions (ISCS) and Network Solutions (NS). It delivers end-to-end logistics, warehousing, and fulfillment services across automotive, retail, FMCG, and industrial sectors via global operations.

With a strong execution platform and presence in India, UK, Europe, and North America, the company offers end-to-end solutions—including final-mile delivery—positioning itself as a full-service supply chain partner for multinational clients.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

TVS Supply Chain Solutions is strategically enhancing its global presence by expanding into new markets and strengthening its technological capabilities. The company is investing in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and predictive analytics to optimize supply chain processes and improve efficiency. Through initiatives like 'Project One,' TVS SCS aims to integrate operations across regions and business verticals, enabling cohesive and agile supply chain management.

Name: FlySBS Aviation Ltd
Overview:
Established in 2020, FlySBS Aviation Ltd is a prominent private jet charter company based in India, specializing in providing bespoke air travel solutions. The company offers a range of services, including short-term chartering programs, subscription-based models, and single-time charters, catering to the diverse needs of its clientele. 

FlySBS Aviation operates a fleet comprising ultra-luxury, large luxury, super luxury, and high-speed jets, ensuring a tailored and comfortable flying experience for its passengers. The company is committed to delivering exceptional service, prioritizing privacy, safety, and convenience for its customers.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

FlySBS Aviation Ltd is poised for significant growth, with plans to expand its fleet and service offerings to meet the increasing demand for private air travel. The company aims to enhance its market presence by introducing innovative programs and services that cater to the evolving needs of its clientele. FlySBS Aviation is committed to maintaining high standards of safety and luxury, ensuring a seamless and exceptional flying experience for its passengers.

Name: Ritco Logistics Ltd
Overview:
Ritco Logistics Ltd is a fast-growing integrated logistics and supply chain solutions provider in India. The company specializes in offering road transportation, warehousing, and value-added services tailored to a wide range of industries including FMCG, automotive, pharmaceuticals, and e-commerce. With a robust fleet and a pan-India presence, Ritco has built a reputation for efficiency, reliability, and cost-effective services.

The company’s strength lies in its ability to combine traditional logistics expertise with technology-driven solutions, including GPS-enabled fleet management and real-time tracking. By focusing on both B2B and B2C segments, Ritco has positioned itself to capture opportunities across the growing logistics landscape in India. Its asset-light model and collaboration with key clients make it scalable and flexible in handling diverse logistics needs.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The logistics sector in India is witnessing structural tailwinds due to GST-led consolidation, rising e-commerce penetration, and government focus on infrastructure. Ritco is well-positioned to benefit from these trends, with plans to expand its fleet, strengthen warehousing footprint, and enhance digital logistics solutions. This strategic direction underpins a positive medium-term Growth Outlook.

Name: Snowman Logistics Ltd
Overview:
Snowman Logistics Ltd, incorporated in 1993, is a leading integrated temperature-controlled logistics service provider in India. The company specializes in offering end-to-end cold chain solutions, including warehousing, transportation, and consignment agency services, catering to industries such as pharmaceuticals, quick-service restaurants (QSR), and e-commerce. 

With a robust infrastructure comprising strategically located warehouses and a fleet of temperature-controlled vehicles, Snowman Logistics ensures the safe and efficient movement of temperature-sensitive goods across the country.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Snowman Logistics aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its network and enhancing its service offerings. The company's strategic initiatives, including the addition of new facilities and the adoption of advanced technologies, are expected to drive operational efficiencies and meet the increasing demand for cold chain logistics services.

Name: Transport Corporation of India Ltd
Overview:
Transport Corporation of India (TCI) is a diversified logistics and supply chain solutions provider, offering freight transport, end-to-end supply chain management, and coastal shipping services. The company operates across multiple industries, including automotive, retail, FMCG, hi-tech, healthcare, and chemicals, delivering customized multimodal solutions to its clients. Its network spans road, rail, and sea, supported by warehousing and value-added services.

TCI leverages an integrated business model to provide both domestic and cross-border logistics solutions. With strong positions in its core segments and ongoing investments in technology, infrastructure, and strategic partnerships, the company aims to enhance efficiency, expand capacity, and diversify its service portfolio to address evolving industry demands.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Transport Corporation of India (TCI) is strategically enhancing its capabilities to meet the growing demand for integrated logistics solutions. The company is focusing on expanding its multimodal transportation network, integrating road, rail, and sea services to provide efficient and cost-effective solutions. TCI is investing in digital transformation initiatives, including the development of advanced tracking systems and automation technologies, to improve operational efficiency and customer experience.

Name: Allcargo Gati Ltd
Overview:
Allcargo Gati Ltd (AGL), formerly known as Gati Limited, is a leading logistics company in India, specializing in express distribution and supply chain solutions. The company offers integrated services across surface, air, and rail logistics, catering to diverse sectors including e-commerce, retail, and industrial clients. AGL's operations are managed through its subsidiary, Gati Express and Supply Chain Pvt. Ltd. (GESCPL), which oversees surface express, air express, and supply chain management services.

As part of Allcargo Logistics Ltd., AGL benefits from a robust infrastructure and extensive network, positioning itself as one of India's largest road transport companies. The company's commitment to operational excellence and customer satisfaction has enabled it to maintain a strong presence in the competitive logistics industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
AGL aims to leverage its operational strengths to deliver profitable and sustainable growth. The company is focusing on network optimization and cost efficiency to enhance its service offerings. With the upcoming festive season nearing, AGL expects increased traction across its B2B express services, positioning itself for continued growth in FY 26.

Name: VRL Logistics Ltd
Overview:
VRL Logistics Limited is a prominent Indian logistics and transportation company, primarily engaged in domestic goods transportation. The company operates an extensive network across India, providing services that include parcel and courier delivery, passenger bus operations, air chartering, and renewable energy generation through windmills. Founded in 1976, VRL has established itself as a key player in the logistics sector, offering a diverse range of services to meet the varying needs of its clientele.

With a strategic focus on efficiency and customer satisfaction, VRL Logistics continues to expand its operations and services. The company's commitment to innovation and quality service has enabled it to maintain a strong presence in the competitive logistics industry. Through continuous improvement and adaptation to market demands, VRL Logistics aims to sustain its growth trajectory and reinforce its position as a leading logistics provider in India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
VRL Logistics is gearing up for strong growth by expanding its branch network and modernizing its fleet to enhance reach and efficiency. The company is adding hundreds of branches and investing in new vehicles to optimize routes and improve turn-around times, while also upgrading infrastructure in key cities to support this expansion. These efforts are underpinned by improved pricing power and margin enhancement via freight rate adjustments and better load utilization across its owned fleet.

Name: TCI Express Ltd
Overview:
TCI Express Limited is an express cargo logistics company operating its own multimodal distribution network across India, including surface, air (domestic and international), rail, and e-commerce delivery services. It specializes in time-definite and value-added solutions, serving sectors such as automotive, consumer goods, pharmaceuticals, and engineering.

In the most recent financial year, TCI Express focused on expanding its branch and sorting-center footprint, automating operations, and enhancing multimodal logistics capabilities. This strategic emphasis reinforced its leadership in express distribution and strengthened service reliability and reach.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management’s outlook for FY26 includes projected revenue growth of 10–12%, buoyed by volume resurgence and selective pricing measures. Volume growth guidance stands at 7–8%, supported by strategic branch expansion (targeting 80 new branches) and improving network utilization.

Name: Mahindra Logistics Ltd
Overview:
Mahindra Logistics Ltd. is a leading integrated logistics and supply chain solutions provider in India, offering services across third-party logistics (3PL), express delivery, freight forwarding, and warehousing. The company caters to diverse sectors including automotive, e-commerce, pharmaceuticals, and consumer goods.

With a pan-India presence and over 20 million square feet of warehousing capacity, Mahindra Logistics leverages technology-driven solutions and robust infrastructure to provide end-to-end logistics services, enhancing operational efficiency and customer experience.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
For FY26, Mahindra Logistics aims to achieve revenue of ₹10,000 crore by expanding operations, enhancing technology adoption, and entering new markets. Strategic initiatives include digital transformation, sustainability practices, and talent development. The company plans to strengthen its pan-India presence and optimize high-margin services, positioning itself for sustainable growth and improved profitability in the coming fiscal year.

Name: Western Carriers (India) Ltd
Overview:
Western Carriers (India) Ltd (WCIL), incorporated in 1972, is a multi-modal, rail-focused, asset-light 4PL logistics company offering end-to-end supply chain management solutions. The company provides integrated logistics services including freight forwarding, air and sea cargo handling, trucking, warehousing, custom house agency, and project logistics. Its scalable, asset-light model is supported by leased infrastructure and advanced digital tools like GPS tracking, enabling seamless operations across India.

With operations spread across 23 states, WCIL manages a wide network of 50+ branches, 16 leased warehouses, and 55+ rake handling points. Its asset base includes 500+ GPS-enabled trucks, 850+ shipping containers, and rail handling capacity through mini and jumbo rakes. The company serves marquee clients such as Hindalco, HUL, Tata, Vedanta, and Cipla, and derives revenue from sectors including metals, FMCG, pharmaceuticals, oil and gas, and utilities.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking ahead, WCIL is strategically diversifying beyond metals into FMCG, agro-chemicals, food products, MSMEs, and industrial aggregates. With major infrastructure investments like the new 31-acre ICD/MMCT at Devaliya (Ahmedabad) and specialized heavy-duty containers for steel coils under a 3-year order exceeding ₹1,100 crore, the company is well-positioned for growth.

Name: Navkar Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Navkar Corporation Limited operates in India’s logistics and cargo transit sector, providing end-to-end solutions through Container Freight Stations (CFS), Private Freight Terminals (PFT), Inland Container Depots (ICD), and Multimodal Logistics Parks. 

The company offers comprehensive services ranging from handling imports and exports to warehousing, railway terminal operations, and value-added services like barcoding and shrink-wrapping catering to a wide spectrum of logistics needs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Navkar Corporation is poised to capitalize on its improving operational performance and growing cargo volumes. Management expects seasonal tailwinds and a robust second half to help meet annual growth targets. With a lean cost structure and expanding throughput, Navkar is positioned for scaled revenue growth through FY26 and beyond.

Name: Sundaram Finance Ltd
Overview:
Sundaram Finance Ltd is a well-established, deposit-taking NBFC in India, offering a broad suite of retail financial services, including vehicle finance, home loans, mutual funds, general insurance, and financial product distribution. The company began with commercial vehicle financing and has steadily diversified its offerings through strategic subsidiaries.

Its business model emphasizes prudent underwriting, a focus on secured lending (especially to SME and rural customers), and robust capital adequacy. Supported by a wide branch network and a conservative risk culture, Sundaram Finance continues to deliver consistent growth and resilience in its core financing operations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Sundaram Finance is well-positioned to sustain growth across FY26–FY27, leveraging strong demand in commercial vehicle financing and asset management segments. With resilient asset quality, efficient operations, and diversified income streams, the company is expected to continue delivering steady credit growth and profitability, maintaining its medium-term guidance.

Name: L&T Finance Ltd
Overview:
L&T Finance Ltd. is a prominent NBFC offering diversified financial products across rural, urban, and retail segments, with a strong emphasis on retailisation and technology-driven credit underwriting. The company maintains a robust consolidated loan book and leverages fintech partnerships to expand digital origination and enhance customer acquisition.

Focused on steady growth and risk management, L&T Finance balances a diverse portfolio including rural business finance, farmer finance, two-wheeler and personal loans, home loans, SME finance, and gold loans. The company continuously innovates with AI/ML initiatives to improve underwriting and portfolio management, while maintaining strong asset quality and capital position.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects accelerated disbursement growth through FY26, driven by digitization, risk-calibrated lending, and strong retail traction. RoA is guided to reach about 2.5% by FY26-end and 2.8% in FY27. Strategic initiatives continue to focus on expanding retail penetration, improving asset quality, and building a technology-led risk-first organization aligned with long-term growth targets.

Name: CreditAccess Grameen Ltd
Overview:
CreditAccess Grameen Limited is a leading NBFC-MFI registered with the RBI, primarily focused on providing microfinance services to women through the Joint Liability Group model. The company caters to underbanked rural and semi-urban segments, offering small-ticket loans to support income-generating activities and improve livelihoods.

Alongside its core microfinance business, CreditAccess Grameen leverages its distribution network to offer additional retail finance products, including unsecured and secured business loans and affordable housing loans. Its strategy combines deep rural reach, prudent risk management, and progressive customer graduation to strengthen portfolio quality and drive sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to achieve sustained growth through ~200 branch additions in FY26, a higher share of retail finance (targeting 12–15% by FY28), and continued improvement in asset quality. With expected moderation in credit costs, enhanced operational efficiency, and steady customer acquisition, profitability is projected to improve significantly in H2 FY26.

Name: Tata Capital Ltd
Overview:
Tata Capital is a diversified non-banking financial company (NBFC) under the Tata Group, offering a broad suite of financial services including retail loans, SME finance, commercial lending, wealth management and investment banking. 

It operates through a large branch network across many states in India, serving individuals, businesses, and institutional clients, with many of its customers onboarded and serviced via digital channels. The company has built scale via both secured and unsecured lending products, and leverages data, analytics, and technology for underwriting, collections, and customer engagement.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Tata Capital aims to double its loan book over the next few years while bringing credit costs down to below 1%, focusing particularly on growing SME and retail lending. It plans to increase exposure to high-margin segments such as two-wheeler loans, affordable housing, equipment finance, and micro-home loans. The IPO proceeds are expected to strengthen its capital base (Tier I), reduce leverage, and support expansion without needing frequent external capital.

Name: HDB Financial Services Ltd
Overview:
HDB Financial Services Ltd is a large retail-focused non-banking finance company that provides various loan products under three major verticals: enterprise lending (for small and medium businesses), asset finance (like vehicles and equipment), and consumer finance (for personal and retail needs). It also provides business process outsourcing (BPO) services. The company aims to serve both people with established credit histories and those from under-banked or semi-urban/rural areas who may be newer to formal credit.

Its operations combine physical branches with digital tools to reach a wide customer base across many states. It emphasizes risk management, diversified product offerings, and leverage from being associated with a well-known financial group, seeking to balance growth with stability in its financial services.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Going forward, HDB Financial looks set to expand significantly by raising capital via its IPO, which will strengthen its ability to grow its loan book across enterprise, asset, and consumer finance. It plans to deepen penetration into under-served markets (including smaller towns), embrace more technology (AI, analytics) to improve credit underwriting and cost efficiency, and maintain diversification so no single product dominates its lending.'

Name: Mahindra & Mahindra Financial Services Ltd
Overview:
Mahindra & Mahindra Financial Services Ltd (MMFSL), incorporated in 1991, is one of India’s leading non-banking finance companies (NBFCs) and a part of the Mahindra Group. It primarily provides loans for auto and utility vehicles, tractors, pre-owned vehicles, commercial vehicles, construction equipment, and SME financing. With a widespread rural and semi-urban presence, the company is focused on delivering financial services to “emerging India.”

MMFSL has steadily grown its asset base, product offerings, and customer footprint. The company is diversifying into fee-based income segments such as insurance distribution and leasing, while continuing to improve operational efficiency, credit quality, and profitability. As of Q1 FY26, MMFSL manages an AUM of ₹1.22 lakh crore and remains adequately capitalized with a Tier I capital of 17.9%.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Mahindra & Mahindra Financial Services (MMFSL) is strategically enhancing its asset quality and expanding its rural and semi-urban presence. The company is focusing on high-quality asset growth, leveraging technology and data analytics to strengthen controls and improve its risk profile. MMFSL aims to be a leader in the banking system by prioritizing asset quality over volume. Additionally, MMFSL is diversifying its offerings by expanding into mortgage loans, including housing finance, top-up loans, and home improvement loans.

Name: Five-Star Business Finance Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1984 and headquartered in Chennai, Five Star Business Finance Ltd is a Non-Banking Financial Company – Non-Deposit taking Systemically Important (NBFC-ND-SI). The company focuses on providing secured business loans to micro-entrepreneurs and self-employed individuals. These customers typically operate in the informal economy and possess collateral, such as land or buildings, to secure their loans.

The firm primarily serves customers across urban, semi-urban, and rural areas in South India. It targets borrowers with household monthly cash flows between ₹25,000 to ₹40,000. The company employs a conservative underwriting approach with family co-applicants and structured installment-to-income ratios to mitigate credit risk.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Five-Star Business Finance is focused on expanding its presence in South India by increasing its loan book at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 25% over the next three years. The company aims to achieve an asset under management (AUM) CAGR of approximately 27% from FY25 to FY27, supported by strong disbursement growth and a focus on secured micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSME) lending.

Name: Aye Finance Ltd
Overview:
Aye Finance Limited is a non-banking financial company (NBFC) focused on providing credit solutions to micro and small businesses that typically have limited access to formal banking channels. The company offers a range of secured and unsecured loans, including working capital finance, mortgage-backed loans, property loans, and hypothecation-based lending. Its primary target customers are micro-scale MSMEs operating in segments such as manufacturing, trading, and services, where access to timely funding is critical for business operations.

The company uses a data-driven underwriting approach along with on-ground assessment to evaluate borrowers who may lack formal financial records. It has built a strong distribution network across multiple regions to reach underserved businesses and provide customized loan products.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its lending portfolio by increasing its reach among underserved MSMEs across new and existing geographies. It aims to strengthen its technology-driven underwriting models to improve credit assessment and reduce risk while enabling faster loan disbursals. The company is also focusing on diversifying its loan products to cater to different business needs and sectors.

Name: Fusion Finance Ltd
Overview:
Fusion Finance Limited, established in 2010, is a registered Non-Banking Financial Company – Microfinance Institution (NBFC-MFI) operating in India. The company primarily provides microfinance services to underserved women entrepreneurs in rural and semi-urban areas, utilizing a Joint Liability Group lending model inspired by the Grameen Bank approach. Fusion Finance aims to empower economically and socially disadvantaged women by offering financial products tailored to their needs, thereby fostering financial inclusion and entrepreneurship. 

Over the years, Fusion Finance has expanded its operations across India, establishing a significant presence with numerous branches and a growing active borrower base. The company's product offerings include income-generating loans, top-up loans, emergency loans, and MSME loans, catering to the diverse financial requirements of its clientele.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook

The company is focused on improving asset quality and operational efficiency, expanding its borrower base, and diversifying loans to achieve sustainable profitability while leveraging a strong capital and liquidity position.

Name: Capri Global Capital Ltd
Overview:
Capri Global Capital Limited is a diversified Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC) engaged in MSME lending, affordable housing finance, construction finance, car loan distribution, gold loans, and indirect lending. The company also has a presence in sports through ownership of the UP Warriorz women’s cricket team in the Women’s Premier League (WPL).

Over the years, the company has steadily expanded into multiple lending verticals, leveraging its pan-India presence and strong partnerships with leading banks. It operates with a multi-segment approach, supported by healthy capitalization levels and a focus on underserved customer segments across semi-urban and rural markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth / Future Outlook
Capri Global targets an AUM of ₹30,000 crore by FY28 with mid-teen RoE, driven by deeper penetration in gold loans, scale-up in housing finance, expansion into insurance distribution, and steady growth in MSME lending. Strategic geographic expansion and cross-selling are expected to be key enablers of this growth.

Name: Muthoot Microfin Ltd
Overview:
Muthoot Microfin Limited is a microfinance institution catering primarily to women entrepreneurs in rural India, offering small-ticket loans aimed at fostering financial inclusion and livelihood enhancement. As part of the Muthoot Pappachan Group, it emphasizes disciplined operations and long-term sustainability through responsible lending.

The company supports underserved segments with products tailored to their needs, combining geographic expansion, borrower-first engagement, and strong liquidity to drive its mission of empowering rural women across multiple Indian states.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company aims to strengthen growth by expanding into newer geographies like Assam, improving digital collections which now form 23%, and balancing risk through diversification. Strong capital buffers and liquidity position it well for sustainable growth in FY26.

Name: Indostar Capital Finance Ltd
Overview:
IndoStar Capital Finance Ltd. is a non-banking financial company (NBFC) registered with the Reserve Bank of India, focusing on providing financing solutions for used and new commercial vehicles, as well as loans to small business owners. The company is professionally managed and institutionally owned, with Brookfield and Everstone as co-promoters.

With a market capitalization of approximately ₹3,727 crore, IndoStar Capital Finance Ltd. is listed on the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) under the ticker symbol 532767.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
IndoStar Capital Finance Ltd. plans to enhance its focus on retail lending, particularly in the vehicle finance and micro loans against property segments. The company aims to capitalize on the growing demand for financing solutions in these areas and expects to achieve sustainable growth in the coming fiscal year.

Name: MAS Financial Services Ltd
Overview:
MAS Financial Services Limited is a non-deposit-taking Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC) registered with the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). Established in 1995, the company specializes in providing retail financing products tailored to the needs of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs), as well as individual borrowers. Its product portfolio includes MSME loans, home loans, two-wheeler loans, used car loans, and commercial vehicle loans. The company operates primarily in tier 2 and tier 3 cities, focusing on underserved markets to promote financial inclusion.

MAS Financial Services has developed a robust distribution network through a combination of direct branches and a wide-reaching channel partner model. This approach enables the company to efficiently serve a diverse customer base across various geographies. By leveraging technology and data analytics, MAS Financial Services aims to enhance credit underwriting processes and improve customer experience, positioning itself as a key player in India's retail financing sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
MAS Financial Services aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and deepening its presence in underserved markets. The company plans to leverage technology to enhance credit assessment processes and improve customer engagement. With a strong capital base and a commitment to financial inclusion,

Name: Northern ARC Capital Ltd
Overview:
Northern Arc Capital Ltd is a diversified non-banking financial company (NBFC) in India, specializing in providing structured debt solutions to underserved individuals and businesses. The company offers a range of services, including lending, placements, and fund investments, leveraging technology to enhance financial inclusion.

With a strong presence across 691 districts in 28 states and 7 Union Territories, Northern Arc has facilitated financing of over ₹2.2 trillion since its inception, impacting the lives of over 124 million people.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Northern Arc plans to expand direct lending to underserved segments, grow fee-based income through technology-driven solutions, maintain low NPAs, and further strengthen capital adequacy. These strategic initiatives aim to support sustainable growth and reinforce the company’s leadership in structured financial solutions.

Name: SBFC Finance Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 2008, SBFC Finance Limited is a systemically important NBFC (NBFC-ND-SI) specializing in secured MSME loans and loans against gold. The company operates with a strong focus on Tier II and Tier III cities, catering to small business owners with limited access to formal income documentation but good credit profiles. As of H1 FY25, SBFC had operations in 155 cities across 16 states and 2 union territories through 192 branches, expanding to 215 branches by June 2025.

SBFC’s asset base is well-diversified geographically, with a significant presence in southern and northern India. It has built a high-yield portfolio primarily consisting of secured MSME loans, supported by a growing gold loan book. The company maintains full in-house loan origination, strong collection infrastructure, and strategic partnerships like co-origination with ICICI Bank, ensuring controlled growth and sound underwriting practices.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management maintains a 5–7% QoQ AUM growth target for FY26, with product mix stability and gold loans at ~15% of the portfolio. The company expects ROE to reach 15% over the long term, with ROA moderating as leverage increases. Opex is projected to decline as new branches mature, and cost of borrowings is likely to ease with favorable rate transmission. Credit costs are expected to rise modestly by 15–20 bps in FY26 to fortify balance sheet resilience. SBFC’s focus remains on profitable growth, disciplined risk management, and maintaining operational leverage advantages while navigating macro headwinds in the small-ticket MSME space.

Name: Fedbank Financial Services Ltd
Overview:
Fedbank Financial Services Limited (Fedfina), a subsidiary of Federal Bank, is a retail-focused non-banking finance company specializing in gold loans, loan against property (LAP), and mortgage financing. With an emphasis on secured lending, the company has transitioned from unsecured business loans to a portfolio dominated by gold and mortgage-backed loans, thereby improving its risk profile.

The company leverages its strong parentage, low cost of borrowing, and efficient branch-led model to expand across India, particularly in North and West regions. Strategic co-location of gold and LAP businesses, coupled with prudent cost management and robust credit discipline, underpins its growth trajectory.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management’s strategy centers on expanding gold loans and LAP as dual growth engines, with 25%+ gold loan growth targeted and stable growth in mortgages. The company expects profitability to strengthen as cost synergies from branch rationalization, improved collections, and higher operating leverage flow through. Over the medium term, Fedfina targets ROA above 2% and ROE of 15%+, backed by disciplined secured lending and geographical expansion.

Name: Paisalo Digital Ltd
Overview:
Paisalo Digital Ltd is a Delhi-headquartered, Agra-based NBFC specializing in small to medium business and personal lending. As a non-deposit-taking finance company, it focuses on expanding financial inclusion across underserved markets through a blend of direct lending and co-lending partnerships.

The company actively leverages omnichannel distribution models, combining physical touchpoints with digital offerings to serve a growing customer base efficiently, while maintaining focus on asset quality and technological deployment.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Paisalo on a solid footing, driven by robust topline and margin performance, prudent asset quality, and strong balance sheet with diversified funding. Continued traction in AUM growth, combined with disciplined cost control and deeper market expansion, sets the stage for sustained growth ahead.

Name: Edelweiss Financial Services Ltd
Overview:
Edelweiss Financial Services Ltd is a diversified financial services group offering offerings across asset management (mutual funds, alternatives), insurance (life and general), credit (NBFC), wealth advisory, and resolutions (asset reconstruction). It serves retail, institutional, and corporate clients with an integrated platform, spanning asset-light and capital-intensive businesses.

The firm’s growth is driven by strong asset management momentum (both traditional and alternatives), expanded insurance footprint, improving underwriting performance, and disciplined credit and ARC operations—all orchestrated to maintain stable earnings and robust capital buffers.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With all businesses well capitalized and improving synergy, Edelweiss is positioned for sustained growth across FY26. Equity and alternative asset management are expected to continue delivering higher margins. Insurance businesses are stabilizing, ARC and advisory will benefit from macro normalization, and NBFC lending remains disciplined. Together, they promise confident earnings momentum ahead.

Name: Sri Adhikari Brothers Television Network Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1994, Sri Adhikari Brothers Television Network Ltd (SABTNL) is a prominent Indian media company specializing in content production and syndication. The company offers a diverse range of services, including the creation of television content, syndication to various broadcasters, aggregators, and satellite networks. SABTNL has a significant presence in the media industry, contributing to the development and distribution of television content across multiple platforms.

The company has been actively exploring opportunities in the digital media landscape. Recognizing the growing demand for digital content, SABTNL plans to venture into the creation of various digital media intellectual properties (IP), such as short and long-format content, music albums, audiobooks, podcasts, and internet broadcasting.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

SABTNL is focusing on expanding its digital media presence by developing a diverse portfolio of digital content, including short and long-format videos, music albums, audiobooks, podcasts, and internet broadcasting. The company aims to leverage the increasing consumption of digital content, especially in rural areas, to enhance its reach and engagement.

Name: Mufin Green Finance Ltd
Overview:
Mufin Green Finance Ltd is a non-deposit taking non-banking financial company (NBFC-ND) based in India. The company specializes in providing financial services, focusing on electric vehicle (EV) loans for income generation purposes. Mufin Green Finance aims to contribute to the electric vehicle ecosystem in India by offering accessible financing options for EVs.

The company operates as a provider of consumer and small and medium-sized enterprise (SME) loans, catering to the growing demand for electric vehicles in the country. Mufin Green Finance is committed to promoting sustainable transportation solutions by facilitating the adoption of electric vehicles through its financial products.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
The company is aggressively targeting new EV categories such as buses, LCVs, and electric four-wheelers. Management aims to leverage its strong market positioning in EV financing to sustain 60%+ revenue CAGR achieved over the past three years. With industry tailwinds from government incentives and rising EV adoption, Mufin Green Finance is expected to scale further, while its ability to manage asset quality and capital adequacy will be critical for sustaining profitability.

Name: Laxmi India Finance Ltd
Overview:
Laxmi India Finance Limited, incorporated in 1996, operates as a Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC) providing a range of financial services to individuals and small businesses. The company primarily focuses on lending activities, offering financial solutions such as business loans, vehicle loans, and other credit products designed to meet the funding needs of customers who may have limited access to traditional banking services.

The company aims to support financial inclusion by providing quick and flexible financing options, particularly in semi-urban and rural regions. Through its branch network and customer-focused approach, Laxmi India Finance works to build long-term relationships with borrowers while maintaining disciplined risk management and responsible lending practices.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Laxmi India Finance Limited plans to expand its lending portfolio by increasing its presence in underserved and high-growth markets. The company aims to strengthen its branch network and digital capabilities to improve customer access to financial services. It is also focusing on diversifying its loan products and enhancing credit assessment systems to manage risk efficiently.

Name: The Investment Trust of India Ltd
CSL Finance Ltd
Overview:
CSL Finance provides a range of financial products and services, including personal loans, business loans, loan against property, loan against securities, working capital finance, and equipment financing. The company focuses on simplifying the loan application and approval process, offering flexible repayment options, streamlined documentation, and accessible financial solutions for individuals, businesses, and corporate clients.

In addition to lending, CSL Finance offers investment advisory and wealth management services, assisting clients with portfolio diversification, financial planning, and informed decision-making. The company emphasizes transparency, ethical practices, customer satisfaction, and adherence to regulatory guidelines to protect client interests and ensure secure financial services.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

CSL Finance Ltd is actively expanding its operations with a strategic focus on increasing its branch network by 50% in the current fiscal year. The company is enhancing its digital infrastructure to streamline loan processing and improve customer reach. Emphasizing wholesale lending, CSL Finance aims to increase its share in this segment to 69%, up from 66%, by focusing on larger ticket-size loans.

Name: Capital India Finance Ltd
Overview:
Capital India Finance Ltd (CIFL), established in 1994, is a non-deposit-taking, mid-sized Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC) registered with the Reserve Bank of India. Headquartered in Mumbai, CIFL offers a diverse range of financial services, including lending, foreign exchange (forex) services, and fintech solutions. 

The company primarily focuses on providing customized financial products to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), mid-corporates, and retail customers across India.  CIFL operates through two main business segments: Lending and Forex Services. The Lending segment includes products such as loan against property, equipment finance, supply chain finance, and home loans, catering to the financing needs of SMEs and retail customers.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
CIFL aims to leverage its diversified business model and strategic initiatives to drive growth in FY26. The company plans to expand its lending portfolio, enhance its digital finance offerings through fintech innovations, and strengthen its presence in the forex services segment.

Name: Arman Financial Services Ltd
Overview:
Arman Financial Services Ltd is a non-banking financial company (NBFC) in India, primarily engaged in providing financial solutions to corporate and retail clients. The company specializes in loans, lease financing, and investment advisory services, catering to diverse sectors such as manufacturing, infrastructure, and small businesses.

Headquartered in Mumbai, Arman Financial Services has built a reputation for personalized client services, robust risk management, and prudent lending practices. Its focus on customer-centric solutions, technology adoption, and sustainable growth strategies has strengthened its position in the NBFC segment.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company plans to expand its lending portfolio and enhance digital platforms for better customer engagement. Focus on risk management, market expansion, and strategic partnerships will support sustainable growth. Efforts to diversify product offerings and enter new segments are expected to strengthen its market position.

Name: Satin Creditcare Network Ltd
Overview:
Satin Creditcare Network Limited (SCNL), established in 1990 and headquartered in Gurugram, India, is a leading non-banking financial company (NBFC) specializing in microfinance services. The company operates in 29 states and union territories, with a network of 1,599 branches, providing financial inclusion to underserved households.

The company's mission is to empower economically disadvantaged communities by providing access to affordable financial services. SCNL's operations are supported by a strong capital base, with a capital adequacy ratio of 26% as of June 2025, and a diversified borrowing profile.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, SCNL aims to strengthen its position in the microfinance sector by enhancing its product offerings and expanding its customer base. The company plans to leverage technology to improve operational efficiency and customer service.

Name: Spandana Sphoorty Financial Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 2003, Spandana Sphoorty Financial is one of India’s leading microfinance institutions, catering primarily to semi-urban and rural households. The company provides small-ticket, unsecured loans to low-income customers through joint liability group lending and individual loan products.

Its portfolio spans microfinance, loan against property, nano enterprise loans, and individual loans, supported by strong geographic presence across India. With focus on financial inclusion, Spandana leverages both traditional and technology-driven models to expand reach and improve collection efficiency.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects industry normalization and projects ~20% AUM growth, with stronger disbursements in H2. Focus will remain on quality lending, portfolio diversification, and improving loan officer productivity. Profitability recovery is anticipated from Q3 FY26, with margins benefiting from tighter controls and stable credit costs. Despite regulatory and operational challenges, Spandana aims to leverage its strong capital base, liquidity buffer, and customer pool to return to sustainable growth.

Name: Ugro Capital Ltd
Overview:
U GRO Capital Limited is a technology-driven non-banking financial company (NBFC) focusing on small business lending across India. The company employs a data-centric and technology-enabled approach to provide customized loan solutions to small businesses operating in select sectors.

By leveraging its proprietary platform, U GRO Capital aims to address the capital needs of underserved small businesses, thereby fostering growth and contributing to the development of the Indian economy.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, U GRO Capital plans to expand its operations by increasing its branch network and enhancing its technology platform. The company aims to further diversify its loan offerings and strengthen its presence in the small business lending sector, contributing to the financial inclusion of underserved businesses across India.

Name: Muthoot Capital Services Ltd
Overview:
This company operates as a deposit-taking non-banking financial company (NBFC) within the reputable Muthoot Group. Beginning its journey in 1998 with two-wheeler financing, it has since widened its reach to include used car financing, consumer durables, and small-ticket business loans. The promoters bring over three decades of lending experience, lending strong institutional know-how to its operations.

With deep roots in South India and a trusted brand image across India, the NBFC leverages the Muthoot Group’s legacy in financial services. It actively taps into diverse lending markets while building on the parent group’s strong reputation for reliability and service.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Given the strong lending growth and profitability in the group’s flagship operations, this deposit-taking NBFC is well-positioned to ride the same wave. If it maintains prudent asset quality and tailors its product stack effectively, it could see a meaningful lift—potentially aiming for double-digit growth in AUM in FY26, aligned with the 42% growth seen in flagship lending.

Name: Sandur Manganese & Iron Ores Ltd
Overview:
The Sandur Manganese & Iron Ores Limited (SMIORE), incorporated in 1954, is the flagship company of the Karnataka-based Sandur Group and is the third-largest manganese ore miner in India.
