The company operates in the Hosapete-Ballari region of Karnataka, engaging in the mining of low-phosphorus manganese and iron ore, along with manufacturing ferroalloys and coke. It possesses two large mining leases valid until 2033, with significant reserves of 17 million tonnes of manganese ore and 110 million tonnes of iron ore.

Its diversified operations span three major segments: Mining, Ferroalloys, and Coke & Energy. The Mining segment is the largest revenue contributor, while Ferroalloys benefit from self-generated energy through waste heat recovery and renewable sources. Coke production supports both ferroalloys manufacturing and external sales. The company has expanded production capacities in both manganese and iron ore, positioning itself for higher profitability, aided by exports following the removal of iron ore export restrictions from Karnataka.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
SMIORE is targeting further expansions in mining capacity in compliance with Supreme Court guidelines, alongside enhancing ferroalloy production through renewable energy integration. The export market is expected to be a major growth driver, supplemented by value-added products and efficient energy use. These expansions position the company to sustain high profitability and strengthen its market share in both domestic and global arenas.

Name: Owais Metal and Mineral Processing Ltd
Overview:
Founded in 1979, Oswal Agro Mills Ltd is an Indian holding company primarily engaged in real estate development, trading of goods, and investment activities. The company operates through three main segments: Trading, Real Estate, and Investment. In the Trading segment, Oswal Agro Mills Ltd deals in commodities and goods. 

The Real Estate segment involves the development and trading of real estate properties, while the Investment segment focuses on lending surplus funds as interest-bearing inter-corporate deposits and investing in equity instruments and other securities.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Oswal Agro Mills Ltd is focusing on expanding its real estate portfolio by exploring new development projects and enhancing its trading operations to capitalize on market opportunities. The company aims to optimize its investment strategies by diversifying its holdings and exploring new avenues for growth.

Name: Orissa Minerals Development Company Ltd
Overview:
Orissa Minerals Development Company Limited (OMDC), established in 1918, is a public sector undertaking under the administrative control of the Department of Steel and Mines, Government of Odisha. The company is a subsidiary of Rashtriya Ispat Nigam Limited and is primarily engaged in the mining and production of iron ore and manganese ore.

Consequently, the company has been funding its expenses primarily through interest earnings. Despite these challenges, OMDC continues to maintain its status as a significant entity in the Indian mining sector, with its shares listed on the National Stock Exchange (NSE), Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE), and Calcutta Stock Exchange (CSE).
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The future outlook for OMDC hinges on the resolution of its regulatory and clearance issues, which would allow the resumption of mining operations. The company has been actively seeking to address these challenges to revitalize its core business.

Name: Coal India Ltd
Overview:
Coal India Limited (CIL) is the world's largest coal-producing company and the dominant coal supplier in India, accounting for around 80% of the country’s total coal production. Its operations are vital for India’s energy and industrial sectors, especially power generation and steel manufacturing.
 
 Business Segments and  Revenue Contribution (Estimates)
 Coal Mining and Production:
 The primary business, contributing over 90% of revenue, derived from coal mining across 318 mines in 84 mining areas covering 8 provinces in India. CIL’s annual coal production capacity is supported by ongoing expansion projects with an emphasis on increasing thermal and coking coal output.
 
 Power Sector Coal Dispatch:
 The power sector accounts for 82% of coal despatch, emphasizing CIL’s critical role in electricity generation across India.
 
 Steel and Other Industries:
 Steel, cement, fertilizers, brick kilns, and other industries consume the remaining coal output.
 
 Coal Washeries Operations:
 CIL operates 13 coal washeries with a combined operational capacity of 24.94 million tonnes per year to improve coal quality and meet consumer needs.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Coal India plans a massive ₹91,000 crore capex by FY26 focused on ramping up coal production, advancing green energy initiatives, and modernizing operations. Expansion into coal gasification and leveraging the developer-cum-operator model will enhance capacity and efficiency, while aggressive solar projects—including 3,000 MW by FY24—and ventures into solar wafer manufacturing and aluminum diversify revenue streams and support decarbonization. Automation and eco-friendly coal handling improve operational efficiency and environmental performance.

Name: MOIL Ltd
Overview:
MOIL Limited is a Government of India Enterprise and the country’s largest manganese ore producer, commanding a 53% market share. With over 120 years of history, the company operates 10 mines—7 underground and 3 open-cast—in Maharashtra and Madhya Pradesh, producing multiple grades of manganese ore catering to ferromanganese, silico manganese, hot metal, battery, and chemical industries. It also operates the only Electrolytic Manganese Dioxide (EMD) plant in India, supplying to pharmaceuticals, batteries, and chemicals sectors. MOIL has a diversified power generation portfolio consisting of wind and solar plants, partly for captive use and partly for sale.

The company reported its highest-ever manganese ore production and sales in FY24, with 1.756 million tons produced and 1.536 million tons sold. It achieved a turnover of ₹1,449.42 crores in FY24, marking an 8% year-on-year increase. In Q1 FY25, MOIL recorded its best-ever quarterly revenue of ₹492 crores, a 30% increase year-on-year, driven by improved realization and volume growth. The company’s expansion plans include increasing manganese ore production to 3.5 million tons by 2030, supported by Rs. 2,400 crores planned investment and ramped-up exploration targeting 100,000 meters in FY25.
Key Takeways:
Market Demand and Future Outlook
Driven by India’s ambition to increase steel production capacity to 300 million tons by 2030, MOIL expects growing manganese ore demand, aiming to increase its market share from about 20%-21% currently to 32% by 2030. The company targets production of 3.5 million tons by 2030, supported by environmental clearances and planned investments totaling Rs. 2,400 crores by 2030. Management is optimistic about overcoming regulatory and market challenges.

Name: Maithan Alloys Ltd
Overview:
Maithan Alloys Ltd is a prominent Indian ferroalloys producer, specializing in the manufacture of ferro manganese, silico manganese, and ferro silicon—key raw materials for steel, stainless steel, and alloy industries. Established in the mid-1980s, the company operates multiple modern smelting and power facilities to serve both domestic and export markets.

Leveraging integrated operations—including mining, smelting, and captive power—Maithan ensures cost-competitive supply, high reliability, and quality consistency. This setup underpins its strong presence across steelmaking supply chains in India and beyond.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
With consolidation of operations and a leaner corporate structure post-merger, Maithan Alloys is well-positioned to benefit from scaling demand as the metals cycle stabilizes. Improved execution, capacity utilization, and strategic focus on high-demand alloys should support sustained growth and margin resilience into FY26.

Name: NMDC Ltd
Overview:
NMDC is India’s largest iron ore producer, accounting for about 18% of domestic production. Its core operations span iron ore mining, diamond mining, sponge iron production, wind power generation, and a growing footprint in coal mining and steel production.
 
 Business Segments 
 
 Iron Ore: ~90% of total revenue
 
 Sponge Iron, Diamonds, Wind Power, Other Minerals: ~5%
 
 New Businesses (Coal, Steel, Strategic Minerals): ~5% and growing
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

NMDC is targeting a transformational growth phase, aiming to scale iron ore production capacity from ~40 MMT to 100 MMT by FY30, while also expanding into coal mining, gold, and strategic minerals to diversify revenue. The commissioning of its 3 MTPA steel plant at Nagarnar and allied infrastructure like pellet and beneficiation plants, alongside slurry pipelines, marks a push toward vertical integration.

Name: Ashapura Minechem Ltd
Overview:
Ashapura Minechem Ltd is a leading Indian multi-mineral solutions provider engaged in the mining, manufacturing, and trading of various minerals and their derivative products. Established in 1982 and headquartered in Mumbai, the company serves diverse industries including ceramics, refractories, foundries, and agriculture.

The company operates globally with a presence in over 50 countries, offering a wide range of products such as bauxite, bentonite, and other industrial minerals. Ashapura Minechem is committed to sustainable mining practices and operational excellence to meet the evolving demands of its clientele.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Ashapura Minechem Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the mining and minerals industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, Ashapura Minechem is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Vedanta Ltd
Overview:
Vedanta Ltd is a diversified Indian natural resources conglomerate operating in metals, mining, oil & gas, power generation, steel, port operations, and advanced glass substrate manufacturing. It has a multi-continent footprint, but India remains its core market, accounting for 65% of total revenues (with the rest from Malaysia, China, UAE, and other regions).
 
 Business Segments with Revenue Breakdown (FY2025)
 Aluminum Business:
 Contributes the largest share—39% of total revenues, driven by ramp-up in domestic smelting and alumina refining capacities. Ongoing capex will see aluminum capacity rise to 3 million tonnes per annum (MTPA), and alumina refining to 6 MTPA.
 
 Zinc, Lead & Silver:
 Accounts for 25% of revenues, including strong performance from Hindustan Zinc and international mining assets.
 
 Copper Business:
 Generates 12% of revenues, with fully integrated operations in mining, refining, and value-added products.
 
 Oil & Gas:
 Forms 9% of revenues, where Vedanta is one of India's largest private sector crude oil producers, with robust expansion capex planned.
 
 Iron Ore Segment:
 Comprises 5% of revenues, including domestic and export iron ore shipments.
 
 Power Segment:
 Provides 4% of group revenues from commercial and captive power plants.
 
 Other Businesses (including Nickel, Cobalt, Glass Substrate, Port Operations, Steel, etc.):
 Makes up 6%, now including India's only nickel and cobalt production after the recent Goa plant acquisition.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Vedanta is poised for robust growth through aggressive capacity expansion in aluminum, alumina, and oil & gas, supported by 100% captive coal integration. Strategic entry into high-tech sectors via semiconductor and display fabs positions the company to benefit from India’s import substitution push and fast-growing technology markets.

Name: KIOCL Ltd
Overview:
KIOCL Limited, a Miniratna under the Ministry of Steel, Government of India, is an export-oriented enterprise specializing in iron ore mining, filtration technology, and the production of high-quality iron-oxide pellets. It operates a 3.5 MTPA pellet plant at Mangalore, Karnataka, which is the second-largest merchant pellet plant in India. The company has a strategic role in India’s steel value chain, being the fourth-largest pellet exporter from the country.

Apart from pellet manufacturing, KIOCL is also engaged in mineral exploration projects, having executed and ongoing projects across iron ore, manganese, limestone, and other minerals. The company is diversifying into value-added steel products and backward integration projects, including a coke oven plant, to enhance operational viability.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Going forward, KIOCL is targeting greater domestic market penetration, expansion into value-added steel products through joint ventures, and enhanced cost efficiency via backward integration. The commissioning of the Devadari iron ore mine and coke oven plant will be pivotal in reducing input cost volatility and improving margins, while expanded customer reach should stabilize revenues amid global demand swings.

Name: 20 Microns Ltd
Overview:
20 Microns Ltd is a leading manufacturer of industrial minerals and specialty chemicals, supplying products such as micronized minerals, additives, fillers, and performance materials. Its solutions are used across paints, plastics, rubber, paper, ceramics, adhesives, and coatings, making it integral to multiple downstream industries.

The company has built a strong reputation for innovation and R&D in minerals, offering customized solutions that enhance material performance and cost efficiency. Its wide product range and focus on value-added specialty chemicals have positioned it as a leading player in the industrial minerals sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
20 Microns aims to expand its specialty chemicals portfolio and increase exports, tapping into global demand for advanced material solutions. With rising industrial activity in India, the company is poised for consistent growth in the coming years.

Name: Gujarat Mineral Development Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat Mineral Development Corporation (GMDC) operates primarily in the mining and power sectors, extracting a diverse range of minerals such as lignite, bauxite, fluorspar, manganese, and others across Gujarat. Its power division runs a lignite-based thermal power plant, currently undergoing an overhaul to improve performance and financial returns. 

GMDC’s growth strategy centers on ramping up lignite volumes from existing and upcoming mines while aggressively pursuing coal mining in Odisha as the next major growth driver. It aims to scale revenues significantly by 2030 through Project Shikhar, which integrates lignite, coal, critical minerals like copper and rare earths, and power assets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
GMDC remains confident in achieving its ambitious revenue target of Rs 14,500 crore by 2030 through a diversified portfolio spanning lignite, coal, copper, and critical minerals. While acknowledging project timing risks due to land and regulatory factors, the company is bullish on Odisha coal prospects and lignite volume recovery.

Name: Central Mine Planning & Design Institute Ltd
Overview:
Central Mine Planning & Design Institute Limited (CMPDI) is a consultancy and engineering services company that provides end-to-end solutions in the coal and mineral sector. The company offers services across the entire value chain, including exploration, geological studies, mine planning, design, and project implementation support. It plays a key role in helping mining companies assess resources, plan efficient extraction, and develop mining infrastructure.

The company provides technical expertise in areas such as drilling, environmental management, mine safety, and advanced surveying. It serves coal companies, mineral developers, and government organizations by delivering data-driven insights and engineering solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its consultancy services beyond coal into diversified minerals and allied sectors to capture new opportunities. It aims to strengthen its capabilities in advanced exploration technologies, digital mine planning, and environmental solutions. Increasing focus on sustainable mining and regulatory compliance is expected to drive demand for its services.

Name: Bharat Coking Coal Ltd
Overview:
Bharat Coking Coal Limited (BCCL), incorporated in 1972, is a public sector company engaged in the mining and production of coking coal, non-coking coal and washed coal. It operates primarily in the Jharia coalfields of Jharkhand, which are known for high-quality coking coal used in steel manufacturing. The company plays a crucial role in supplying raw material to the steel industry, making it an important part of India’s core industrial sector.

BCCL functions under Coal India Limited and operates numerous underground and open-cast mines along with coal washeries to improve coal quality. Its operations include extraction, processing and supply of coal to various industries such as power, steel and cement. The company also focuses on safety, environmental management and efficient mining practices to sustain long-term operations.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

BCCL aims to increase coal production by expanding its mining capacity and modernising existing mines with advanced technologies. The company is focusing on improving productivity through mechanisation, digitisation and better mine planning. It also plans to enhance coal washing capacity to supply higher-quality coal to steel and industrial customers.

Name: Global Surfaces Ltd
Overview: Global Surfaces Ltd is a leading manufacturer of engineered quartz and natural stone products in India. With over 20 years of experience, the company has established itself as a trusted name in the industry. Under the leadership of Managing Director Mr. Mayank Shah, Global Surfaces has expanded its operations to multiple countries, including the United States and the United Arab Emirates. The company's state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities produce a wide range of products, including slabs, tiles, and countertops, catering to both residential and commercial applications.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
After a challenging FY25 marked by substantial losses, the modest recovery in Q1 FY26 revenue suggests a cautious improvement in demand or operational execution. If this trend continues, the company could gradually shift towards profitability—though specific forecasted numbers are not yet available.

Name: Radiant Cash Management Services Ltd
Overview:
Radiant Cash Management Services Limited provides retail cash management solutions to banks, financial institutions, and organized retail and e-commerce companies. Its services include cash and cheque pickup and delivery, cash processing, overnight vaulting, ATM services, and management of electronic financial transactions. The company operates across 13,044 pin codes through 55,513 touchpoints in over 5,388 locations, supported by a fleet of 694 armored vans and a team of 8,780 personnel.

Serving a diverse clientele that includes major banks, e-commerce companies, retail chains, NBFCs, insurance firms, railways, and petroleum outlets, Radiant Cash Management Services has a strong presence in tier-2 and tier-3 cities, contributing 67% of its revenue. The company leverages its nationwide network and efficient operations to address rising currency circulation, organized retail expansion, COD demand, and increasing security requirements, demonstrating adaptability in a dynamic financial and retail environment.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Radiant Cash Management Services Ltd is strategically expanding its footprint in the cash logistics sector by enhancing its retail presence and digital capabilities. The company aims to increase its retail outlets from 800 to 25,000 across metro and tier I cities over the next five years, focusing on small retail outlets such as restaurants, groceries, pharmacies, salons, and auto service stations.

Name: Cholamandalam Investment & Finance Company Ltd
Overview:
Cholamandalam Investment & Finance Company Ltd (Chola) is a leading NBFC under the Murugappa Group, known for vehicle finance, home loans, SME loans, and other retail lending.The company is undergoing a major digital transformation to become a data-driven financial services player, focusing on automation, digital workflows, and API integrations.
 
Key Segments & Initiatives
 Launched three new businesses in FY22:
 
 CSEL – Personal loans for salaried borrowers.
 
 SBPL – Loans for self-employed professionals.
 
 SME Product Suite – Loans for micro and small businesses, supply chain finance, and capex needs.
 
 Introduced Chola Connect (HRMS suite) to boost internal process efficiency.
 
 Core strength remains vehicle finance, benefiting from rising demand tied to infrastructure spending and fleet upgrades.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Cholamandalam Finance is leveraging digital transformation and technology partnerships to widen its reach, improve customer experience, and enhance margins. The company is seeing strong traction in its newer segments like consumer and SME finance, while its core vehicle finance business continues to benefit from structural tailwinds such as increased infrastructure investment and replacement demand

Name: Muthoot Finance Ltd
Overview:
Muthoot Finance Ltd is India’s leading gold loan company with an extensive branch network of over 4,400 branches nationwide. Primarily engaged in providing loans secured against gold jewellery, Muthoot also offers diversified financial services such as personal loans, housing finance, vehicle finance, money transfer, insurance, foreign exchange, and wealth management through its group companies.
 
Muthoot Finance is India’s largest gold loan NBFC, providing secured financing against household gold holdings. It caters to retail customers by offering quick liquidity solutions at affordable rates while maintaining strong risk controls through fully secured lending practices.

With an extensive branch network across India, particularly in southern states, Muthoot has also diversified into other lending products such as housing finance, microfinance, and vehicle loans. However, gold loans remain the backbone of its portfolio and profitability.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Muthoot Finance is fueling its future expansion by tapping international capital through bond issuances to broaden its funding and support ambitious lending goals, while anticipating continued strong demand for gold-backed loans—especially as easier norms for small-ticket gold lending make borrowing more accessible—alongside a strategic push into diversified, non-gold lending to steadily grow its overall loan portfolio.

Name: Manappuram Finance Ltd
Overview:
Manappuram Finance Ltd. is a leading Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC-ND) specializing in secured lending, particularly gold loans, alongside diversified offerings including microfinance, vehicle & equipment finance, MSME loans, and affordable housing finance. The company is the second-largest gold loan NBFC in India, with a strong nationwide branch network comprising 4,044 gold loan branches and 1,311 non-gold branches.

The company serves over 25 million active customers in its gold loan business and over 4 million borrowers in microfinance through its subsidiary Asirvad Microfinance Ltd. Its operations are increasingly technology-driven, with a dominant share of gold loans sourced online, underscoring its focus on digital transformation and operational scalability.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Manappuram Finance is strategically enhancing its growth trajectory by expanding its secured lending portfolio, particularly in gold loans, which constitute approximately 75% of its revenue. The company is also focusing on diversifying its offerings through secured non-gold segments such as affordable housing and MSME lending.

Name: Bajaj Finance Ltd
Overview:
Bajaj Finance Ltd. (BFL) is one of India’s largest and fastest-growing non-banking financial companies (NBFCs), offering a wide suite of lending and digital financial services. It serves retail, SME, and commercial customers through an extensive network covering both urban and rural India. BFL operates under trusted brands like Bajaj Finserv, Bajaj Housing Finance, and Bajaj Allianz Insurance, and extends its reach digitally through Bajaj Finserv MARKETS and Bajaj Finserv EMI Store.
 
 Revenue / AUM Mix (Approximate)
 Contribution by business segments (FY23 AUM):
 
 Consumer Lending: 32% — Consumer durable loans, lifestyle financing, EMI cards.
 
 Mortgages: 31% — Home loans, loan against property.
 
 SME Lending: 13% — Working capital, business loans, professional loans.
 
 Commercial Lending: 8% — Loan against securities, vendor financing, lease rental discounting.
 
 Rural Lending: 9% — Gold loans, two-wheeler and three-wheeler loans, personal loans in rural areas.
 
 Others: 7% — Co-branded credit cards, new digital products.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Bajaj Finserv aims to expand its market share across credit and retail lending while growing its customer base to over 100 million in the mid-term and 200 million by FY29. The company targets an AUM of ₹4 trillion by FY25, driven by new product launches in microfinance, auto, tractor, and commercial finance to reach under-penetrated segments. Continued digital leadership through AI, advanced analytics, and green digital initiatives will enhance customer onboarding, servicing, and security. Geographic expansion into high-potential and underserved regions, coupled with a focus on solar and EV financing, positions Bajaj Finserv for sustainable growth, further supported by regulatory advantages improving profitability and liquidity.

Name: PNB Gilts Ltd
Overview:
PNB Gilts Ltd is a subsidiary of Punjab National Bank, engaged in the business of trading in government securities and providing investment solutions. The company operates in the financial services sector.

PNB Gilts focuses on government securities trading, offering investment products to institutional and retail investors. The company leverages its parent bank's network to expand its reach.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

PNB Gilts Ltd is strategically expanding its presence in the fixed income market by focusing on government securities, treasury bills, state development loans, corporate bonds, and various money market instruments. The company is enhancing its underwriting capabilities and trading activities to support the government's borrowing program and meet the growing demand for fixed income products.

Name: Shriram Finance Ltd
Overview:
Shriram Transport Finance Company Ltd, part of the SHRIRAM Group, is a leading NBFC specializing in commercial vehicle financing, primarily focusing on trucks ranging from used to new vehicles. With an extensive network of over 1,834 branches, including rural centers, and strong partnerships with private financiers, the company serves a wide customer base across India.
 
 Business Segments
 Commercial Vehicle Financing: Loans for commercial goods vehicles, passenger commercial vehicles, tractors, farm equipment, and construction equipment.
 
 Business Loans: Financing for customers, dealers, private financiers, and the commercial vehicle ecosystem.
 
 Deposit Services: Fixed deposits, recurring deposits, and related services.
 
 Working Capital Finance: Loans for vehicle insurance, tyre finance, tax finance, toll finance, repair/top-up loans, fuel finance, and challan discounting.
 
 Life Insurance: Products such as Shriram Life Cashback Term Plan and Shriram Life Smart Protection Plan.
 
 Government Schemes: Participation in initiatives like the Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme supporting MSMEs.
 
 Revenue Mix
 Commercial Vehicle Loans: Core revenue driver
 
 Business Loans & Working Capital Finance: Supporting segments
 
 Deposit Services: Supplementary income source
 
 Life Insurance: Emerging segment
 
 Government Scheme Participation: Contributes to loan portfolio quality and outreach
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Shriram Finance leverages its expansive branch and partner network to capture demand in the growing used commercial vehicle sector. With a flexible portfolio—including insurance and working capital products—and participation in government MSME credit schemes, it is poised to sustain growth and strengthen customer relationships across cycles.

Name: Poonawalla Fincorp Ltd
Overview:
Poonawalla Fincorp Ltd, formerly known as Magma Fincorp, is a non-deposit-taking NBFC registered with the RBI. It operates in the consumer and MSME lending space, with a focus on personal loans, business loans, LAP, and pre-owned car finance. Backed by the Cyrus Poonawalla Group, the company is undergoing a significant digital transformation to scale up its operations.

The company has adopted a lean, tech-first model post-acquisition by the Poonawalla Group, drastically reducing its physical branch presence while increasing digital loan origination from 10% to 81%. With a rapidly growing AUM, product diversification, and new digital offerings, Poonawalla Fincorp is positioning itself as a scalable and efficient retail lender targeting strong long-term growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Poonawalla Fincorp is rapidly scaling up its lending operations through the launch of diverse finance products—including gold loans, shopkeeper loans, education financing, and consumer durable lending—and rolling these out aggressively across hundreds of new branches to deepen its reach. To fuel this expansion, the promoter is leading a sizeable capital infusion that strengthens the company’s financial base and supports sustained growth.

Name: SBI Cards & Payment Services Ltd
Overview:
SBI Cards and Payment Services Limited is a leading credit card issuer in India and a subsidiary of the State Bank of India, giving it strong brand credibility and distribution reach. The company has consistently outpaced the overall Indian credit card market in new cards issued and spends, driven by its diversified card portfolio, strong co-brand partnerships, and strategic digital transformation initiatives. By leveraging parent bank support, extensive partnerships, and technology-led solutions, SBI Cards aims to maintain its leadership position and capture India’s growing credit card opportunity.
 
 Business Segments 
 
 Interest Income & Fees on Revolving Credit: ~50–55% of revenue
 
 Interchange Fees & Transaction Revenue: ~35–40%
 
 Other Income (Late Fees, Annual Fees, Co-brand Revenue): ~5–10%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

SBI Cards is positioned to capitalize on the underpenetrated credit card market in India, with the backing of the SBI network and a diverse product portfolio targeting mass, affluent, and premium segments. Its digital-first strategy, strong brand equity, and premiumization efforts (e.g., AURUM) support scalable growth. As digital payments and credit penetration expand, SBI Cards’ unique position as the only listed standalone credit card company offers investors focused exposure to this secular trend, though continued emphasis on credit quality and cybersecurity is vital to sustaining profitability.

Name: Manaksia Ltd
Overview:
Manaksia Coated Metals & Industries Ltd, established in 1984, is a prominent player in the Indian steel industry, specializing in the manufacturing and trading of coated steel products. The company offers a diverse range of products, including pre-painted galvanized steel and plain galvanized steel, catering to various industrial applications. 

The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities equipped with advanced technology to ensure the production of superior products. Manaksia's commitment to quality and innovation has enabled it to maintain a competitive edge in the industry. The company continues to focus on expanding its product portfolio and enhancing its production capabilities to meet the growing demand in the market.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Manaksia aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing production capabilities. The ongoing investment in technology upgrades and renewable energy initiatives is expected to improve operational efficiency and reduce costs.

Name: IRM Energy Ltd
Overview:
IRM Energy Ltd is an Indian energy company engaged in the generation and distribution of renewable and conventional power. Headquartered in India, the company focuses on power generation projects including solar, hydro, and thermal power.

IRM Energy Ltd emphasizes sustainable energy solutions, operational efficiency, and technological adoption to meet the growing energy demands of industrial and commercial clients. Its diversified energy portfolio positions it to address emerging energy needs and policy incentives.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

IRM Energy Ltd is poised for significant expansion in the natural gas distribution sector. The company is actively enhancing its infrastructure by increasing the number of Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) stations and expanding its pipeline network across various regions. IRM Energy is also diversifying its energy portfolio by integrating Compressed Bio-Gas (CBG) into its City Gas Distribution (CGD) network, aligning with India's sustainability goals.

Name: Reliance Industrial Infrastructure Ltd
Overview:
Reliance Industrial Infrastructure Ltd (RIIL), a part of the Reliance Group, is primarily engaged in the business of setting up and operating industrial infrastructure. The company's main activities include providing services for the transportation of petroleum products, natural gas, and raw water through pipelines, as well as other infrastructure support services. RIIL operates mainly in the Mumbai and the Rasayani regions of Maharashtra, Surat, and Jamnagar belts of Gujarat.

Incorporated in September 1988, RIIL commenced its commercial operations in March 1992. The company has been instrumental in establishing and managing critical infrastructure projects, contributing to the industrial development of the regions it serves.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Reliance Industrial Infrastructure Limited (RIIL), a key player in India's infrastructure sector, is strategically expanding its operations to capitalize on the growing demand for industrial infrastructure. The company is actively involved in setting up and operating industrial infrastructure projects across various regions in India, including Mumbai, Rasayani, Surat, and Jamnagar, thereby strengthening its presence in critical industrial hubs.

Name: Confidence Petroleum India Ltd
Overview:
Confidence Petroleum India Ltd specializes in the manufacturing of LPG cylinders and supplies auto LPG through a nationwide network of bottling plants and dispensing stations. The company also engages in LPG bottling for industrial and commercial customers and distributes packed LPG cylinders for household use.

By combining cylinder manufacturing with deep-channel LPG distribution across retail, industrial, and commercial segments, Confidence Petroleum serves a wide range of energy needs throughout India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Confidence Petroleum India Ltd (CPIL) is strategically enhancing its infrastructure to meet the growing demand for clean energy solutions. The company plans to expand its LPG bottling plants from 68 to 100 and increase its Auto LPG Dispensing Stations by 500, alongside adding 200 CNG outlets within the next three years. Additionally, CPIL is focusing on establishing small-scale LPG import terminals at key locations to streamline logistics and support its pan-India operations.

Name: Deep Industries Ltd
Overview:
Deep Industries Ltd is a diversified industrial company focused on oilfield services, infrastructure, and energy solutions. Headquartered in Jaipur, the company operates primarily in exploration, drilling, and natural resource management services, catering to oil, gas, and industrial sectors.

With a history spanning over three decades, Deep Industries has established advanced technical capabilities and a skilled workforce to deliver high-quality solutions. Its commitment to safety, efficiency, and sustainable practices has earned it long-term relationships with domestic and international clients.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Deep Industries aims to expand its energy and infrastructure services, invest in technology, and pursue international contracts. Focus on sustainability, efficiency, and diversified service offerings will support long-term growth. Strategic partnerships and capacity expansion are key priorities.

Name: Dolphin Offshore Enterprises (India) Ltd
Overview:
Dolphin Offshore Enterprises (India) Ltd, established in 1979, is a prominent Indian company specializing in providing comprehensive underwater services to the offshore oil and gas industry. The company offers a diverse range of services, including air, mixed gas, and saturation diving, underwater engineering, fabrication, installation, and maintenance of offshore platforms and drilling rigs.

Additionally, Dolphin Offshore Enterprises provides marine logistics, vessel operations, and turnkey Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) solutions. Headquartered in Mumbai, Dolphin Offshore Enterprises operates both domestically and internationally, with a presence in regions such as Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and Africa
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Dolphin Offshore Enterprises (India) Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to expand its operations in emerging markets, including Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and Africa, to capitalize on the growing demand for offshore services in these regions.

Name: Jindal Drilling & Industries Ltd
Overview:
Jindal Drilling & Industries Ltd is an Indian company engaged in the business of drilling and exploration services for the oil and gas industry. The company provides offshore and onshore drilling services to major oil and gas companies.

In FY25, Jindal Drilling & Industries reported significant growth in its financial performance, driven by increased demand for drilling services and operational efficiencies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jindal Drilling & Industries is accelerating its growth by expanding its fleet—most notably through the addition of a jack-up rig acquired via internal funds—enhancing its scale and efficiency in offshore drilling operations. It is also strengthening its financial base by maintaining a strong cash position, reducing debt, and securing long-term contracts that support steady earnings visibility.

Name: Asian Energy Services Ltd
Overview: Asian Energy Services Ltd (AESL) is an Indian company providing comprehensive services in the oil and gas sector, including geophysical data acquisition, field development, and production facility construction. Established in 1993, AESL has been instrumental in supporting the energy industry's infrastructure development.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

AESL's diversified offerings across the energy spectrum ensure strategic positioning to drive sustainable growth. However, the recent board-approved merger with Oilmax Energy has led to a 13% decline in share value, reflecting market uncertainties. Despite this, AESL's strong project pipeline and strategic initiatives position it well for long-term growth in the energy sector.

Name: Hindustan Oil Exploration Company Ltd
Overview:
Hindustan Oil Exploration Company Ltd is engaged in the exploration, development, and production of crude oil and natural gas across both onshore and offshore blocks in India. As the country's first private exploration and production company, HOEC operates in key regions including Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Assam, and Arunachal Pradesh.

Focusing on upstream energy operations, the company plays a significant role in India’s domestic hydrocarbon production, contributing to energy security. Its diversified geographic presence helps mitigate risks associated with any single region’s disruptions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
HOEC’s earnings call highlighted strategic plans including drilling 40 development wells and 3 exploration wells in the Kharsang block—4 of which are already producing. Additionally, production improvement in the Dirok field and a focused capital expenditure program (supported by a ₹250 crore debt raise) are intended to boost future output.

Name: Prabha Energy Ltd
Overview:
Established in 2009, Prabha Energy Ltd is an Indian oil and gas exploration and production company headquartered in Ahmedabad, Gujarat. The company specializes in both conventional and unconventional hydrocarbon resources, with a primary focus on Coal Bed Methane (CBM) projects. 

Prabha Energy has secured interests in key onshore CBM blocks, including the North Karanpura Block (with a 25% stake) and the Jharia Block (with 100% participation), both located in coal-rich regions of Jharkhand and West Bengal. These assets position the company strategically in India's energy landscape. The company's mission is to develop cost-effective, dependable, and environmentally friendly solutions in both conventional and unconventional energy sectors.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Prabha Energy Ltd is strategically expanding its presence in the Indian energy sector, focusing on increasing its CBM gas production to align with the government's target of 5.0 MMSCMD by 2027–28. The company is actively developing its existing CBM blocks and exploring new opportunities to enhance production capacity. Additionally, Prabha Energy is committed to adopting advanced technologies and sustainable practices to improve operational efficiency and minimize environmental impact.

Name: Deepak Nitrite Ltd
Overview:
Deepak Nitrite Ltd, established in 1970, is a prominent Indian chemical manufacturing company headquartered in Vadodara, Gujarat. Initially focused on producing sodium nitrite and sodium nitrate, the company has diversified its product portfolio to include a wide range of chemicals such as agrochemicals, dyes, pigments, pharmaceutical intermediates, and specialty chemicals. .

Over the years, Deepak Nitrite has grown through strategic acquisitions and expansions. In 1984, the company acquired Sahyadri Dyestuffs & Chemicals from Mafatlal Industries. Further consolidating its position in the chemical industry, Deepak Nitrite established Deepak Phenolics Ltd in 2015 to manufacture phenol and acetone, essential components in various industrial applications.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Deepak Nitrite Ltd is actively investing in expanding its chemical manufacturing capabilities to meet growing domestic and international demand. The company has approved a ₹3,500 crore investment through its subsidiary, Deepak Chem Tech Ltd, to establish a new manufacturing complex for phenol and other solvents, aiming to reduce India's reliance on imports and strengthen its supply chain. Additionally, Deepak Nitrite is setting up a multi-purpose manufacturing facility for specialty fluorochemicals, with an investment of approximately ₹220 crore.

Name: Solar Industries India Ltd
Overview:
Solar Industries India Ltd is a leading manufacturer of bulk and packaged explosives and initiating systems, primarily serving mining, infrastructure, and construction sectors. The company has strategically diversified into defense manufacturing, producing propellants for missiles and rockets, warheads, and related systems — making it one of the few Indian private players in this space.
 
 Business Segments
 Industrial Explosives: Bulk and packaged explosives for mining, infrastructure, construction, and institutional clients.
 
 Defense: Propellants for missiles and rockets, warheads, and specialized defense-grade explosives.
 
 Initiating Systems: Detonators, detonating cords, and accessories supporting blasting operations.
 
 Revenue Mix 
 Industrial Explosives: ~76% — Core revenue driver.
 
 Defense: ~14% — Fast-growing, supported by successful indigenization and technology transfers.
 
 Initiating Systems: ~10% — Supporting explosives business and contributing to operational integration.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Solar Industries is rapidly scaling its defense and international business, investing around ₹500 crore annually to expand manufacturing capacity and diversify beyond Coal India. Its successful Pinaka rocket test and involvement in high-tech defense systems (UAVs, counter-drone, BrahMos propellants) position it as a key private player in India’s defense modernization.

Name: Fine Organic Industries Ltd
Overview:
Fine Organic Industries is a leading Indian manufacturer of oleochemical-based niche additives with a global footprint across 80+ countries. Its diversified product portfolio includes polymer additives, food additives, feed nutrition, cosmetics & pharma additives, and coatings & specialty additives. The company serves 5,200+ end-users, including marquee clients like Coca-Cola, Britannia, Asian Paints, and Pidilite, without dependency on any single customer for more than 5% of revenue.

With manufacturing spread across seven sites in Maharashtra and strong R&D capabilities, Fine Organic has established itself as one of the six largest global players in polymer additives and a leading global player in specialty food emulsifiers. The company is expanding its manufacturing base with a new SEZ facility near Jawaharlal Nehru Port to further boost export capacity and global presence.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
For FY26, the company targets high single-digit revenue growth driven by rising demand for polymer and food additives, capacity ramp-up from the new SEZ plant, and deeper penetration into developed markets. Margin stability is expected despite raw material volatility, aided by cost pass-through mechanisms and premium product mix.

Name: Privi Speciality Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Privi Speciality Chemicals Ltd, established in 1985, is a leading Indian manufacturer, supplier, and exporter of aroma and fragrance chemicals. The company specializes in producing a wide range of aroma chemicals used in various applications, including soaps, detergents, shampoos, and other fine fragrances. Privi's commitment to quality and innovation has positioned it as a trusted partner in the global fragrance industry. 

With a strong emphasis on research and development, Privi Speciality Chemicals Ltd continually enhances its product offerings to meet the evolving needs of its customers. The company's products are integral to the fragrance and flavor industry, contributing to the sensory appeal of numerous consumer goods. Privi's dedication to excellence and customer satisfaction underscores its reputation as a leader in the aroma chemicals sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Privi Speciality Chemicals Ltd anticipates a 20% revenue growth, driven by continued demand for its aroma and fragrance chemicals and expansion into new markets. The company's focus on innovation, sustainability, and operational excellence positions it well for sustained growth and leadership in the global aroma chemicals sector.

Name: Himadri Speciality Chemical Ltd
Overview:
Aster DM Healthcare Limited is one of India’s leading integrated private healthcare providers, with a strong focus on delivering services across the primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary care spectrum. Post the April 2024 demerger of its GCC business, the listed entity now comprises the India operations, which span 19 hospitals and 13 clinics across Maharashtra, Karnataka, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Telangana. The company has strengthened its positioning in South India, ranking as the largest in Kerala by bed capacity, second-largest in Andhra Pradesh, and third-largest in Karnataka among listed players.

Aster’s India operations are supported by a network of over 19,500 healthcare professionals, including 2,649 doctors and 6,235 nurses. The company’s strategic focus is on niche specialties such as cardiac sciences, neurosciences, oncology, gastroenterology, orthopaedics, and nephrology, which together contribute nearly 60% of revenues. Its asset-light O&M model enables faster scale-up in tier-II and tier-III cities with minimal capex outlay, complementing its brownfield and greenfield expansions. With an ARPOB of ₹44,200 and occupancy of 67% as of Q1 FY25, Aster is driving operational efficiencies while expanding capacity toward its FY27 goal of 6,500+ beds.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Himadri aims to strengthen its global leadership in advanced carbon materials and specialty chemicals, scale battery material production to tap the EV and energy storage markets, and deepen integration in downstream value chains. With fully funded expansion plans and a diversified industrial base, it is positioned for sustained high-growth in both domestic and export markets.

Name: PCBL Chemical Ltd
Overview:
The company, part of the RP-Sanjiv Goenka Group, is a leading manufacturer of Carbon Black, specialty chemicals, and also generates captive power with surplus sales. It operates across segments including Tyre-grade Carbon Black, Performance Chemicals, Specialty Carbon Black, and Aquapharm Chemicals. With a strong export focus, nearly 41% of Carbon Black volumes are exported, mainly to US and Europe. The company is actively expanding capacity via brownfield and greenfield projects, targeting a million-ton capacity by FY28.

Its product strategy emphasizes innovation and diversification, including developing new grades for plastics, pigments, inks, and emerging high-performance applications such as battery chemicals and energy storage. Aquapharm chemicals and power generation are complementary segments contributing to revenue and margin stability, supported by ongoing debottlenecking and new capacity additions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is cautiously optimistic amid global macroeconomic headwinds and policy uncertainties. Focus remains on capacity ramp-up, product innovation, operating leverage, and cross-segment synergies to enhance margins and returns. The company targets >1 million tons capacity by FY28 and anticipates margin improvement in Aquapharm as operating leverage kicks in over upcoming quarters.

Name: Jubilant Ingrevia Ltd
Overview:
Jubilant Ingrevia is a global life sciences and specialty chemicals company that delivers integrated and innovative solutions to the pharmaceutical, nutrition, agrochemical, consumer, and industrial sectors. With a legacy spanning over four decades in the chemicals space, the company has established strong leadership in pyridine and its derivatives, vitamin B3, and acetic anhydride. Its portfolio is well diversified across Specialty Chemicals, Nutrition & Health Solutions, and Chemical Intermediates, serving marquee customers including 15 of the top 20 global pharma companies and 7 of the top 10 global agrochemical companies.

The company operates five large-scale manufacturing facilities in India and maintains a global footprint across North America, Europe, Asia, and Africa. Its business mix demonstrates strength in specialty chemicals, which now drive the bulk of profitability. With strong in-house R&D capabilities, more than 150 scientists, and 49 new products slated for launch over the next four years, Jubilant is strategically positioned to benefit from global structural shifts such as the China Plus One trend and regulatory interventions in key nutrition markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management reiterated confidence in sustaining growth momentum, particularly in Specialty Chemicals and Nutrition, which are expected to contribute 65–70% of EBITDA even as Acetyls recover gradually. The European Choline Chloride opportunity is poised to drive strong export-led growth in Nutrition, while upcoming CDMO contracts in Agro and Pharma should provide a step-up in revenues from FY26 onward. Long-term revenue potential from ongoing capex is pegged at ₹6,500 crore once 70–80% capacity utilization is achieved by FY27.

Name: Anupam Rasayan India Ltd
Overview:
Anupam Rasayan India Ltd is a specialty chemicals manufacturer with a strong presence in custom synthesis and manufacturing (CSM) for global and domestic markets. The company operates across life science specialty chemicals—covering agrochemicals, personal care, and pharmaceuticals—and other specialty chemicals, including performance materials like pigments, dyes, and polymer additives. It serves 75 clients, including 31 multinational corporations, with a top-10 customer contribution of 71% to FY25 revenues.

In FY25, Life Science Specialty Chemicals contributed 86.7% of revenues, with Pharma accounting for 21.9% of total sales and delivering 92% YoY growth. Performance Materials accounted for 13.3% of revenues, growing 18% YoY. The company operates six manufacturing facilities in Gujarat with ~27,000 MT capacity at 70–75% utilization, supported by two DSIR-recognized R&D centers. A completed ₹670 crore capex program added three new multi-purpose plants, positioning Anupam for higher volumes in FY26.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Anupam Rasayan is positioned for a sharp growth rebound, with revenue acceleration from new product commercialization, geographical diversification, and the battery chemicals foray. Margins are expected to remain in the 26–28% range despite a higher pharma share, supported by strong polymer margins. The combination of vertical integration, long-duration contracts, and a robust R&D pipeline provides high revenue visibility and a sustainable competitive moat in specialty chemicals.

Name: Aether Industries Ltd
Overview:
Aether Industries Limited, incorporated in 2013, is a specialty chemicals manufacturer catering to global and domestic markets. The company produces unique products where it holds a leadership position in India and supplies to sectors such as pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, oil & gas, material science, and renewables.

Its business model spans large-scale manufacturing, contract/exclusive manufacturing, and contract research and manufacturing services. With strong R&D capabilities, multi-purpose manufacturing facilities, and a growing client base, Aether continues to expand its footprint in both domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to capitalize on the “golden age” for Indian specialty chemicals by ramping up key contracts, commissioning new sites, and diversifying revenue streams across four equal pillars—pharma/agro, oil & gas, material science, and sustainability. With strong client engagement, stable pricing, and 12–18 advanced-stage CEM projects in the pipeline, the company expects sustained revenue growth and margin stability above 30% EBITDA.

Name: Atul Ltd
Overview:
Atul Ltd., a part of the Lalbhai Group, is a diversified Indian chemical company engaged in manufacturing over 900 products and 400 formulations, catering to around 30 industries across 82 countries. Its operations are structured under two main segments — Life Science Chemicals and Performance & Other Chemicals — with a strong market presence in crop protection, pharmaceuticals, polymers, colors, aromatics, and specialty chemicals. The company serves a vast clientele of ~4,000 customers through a network of ~2,000 distributors and ~38,000 retailers, backed by eight manufacturing sites in India and a global marketing footprint through subsidiaries and joint ventures.

Over the years, Atul has consistently expanded its capacities and product portfolio, investing heavily in new plants, debottlenecking initiatives, and technology upgrades. In FY25, it commissioned its liquid epoxy resin plant, launched multiple new products in crop protection and pharmaceuticals, and achieved highest-ever sales in several sub-segments. Strategic capex, focused on downstream value-added products and process efficiency improvements, is aimed at unlocking significant unrealised sales potential across business lines.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Atul aims to become one of the top three textile chemical players in India by FY29, with a focus on expanding market share in technical textiles, high-performance pigments, and sustainable solutions. It plans to commercialise multiple new specialty products, leverage new plant capacities, and strengthen its global footprint in polymers, colors, and crop protection.

Name: Aarti Industries Ltd
Overview:
Aarti Industries Ltd is a leading Indian company in the specialty chemicals and pharmaceuticals sectors. It is recognized for:

Its benzene-based specialty chemical products

Its pharmaceutical APIs and intermediates

The company has a strong global footprint, with 44% of its revenue coming from exports, highlighting its international competitiveness.

A strategic demerger has led to the formation of Aarti PharmLabs Ltd, which now handles the pharma segment, allowing Aarti Industries to sharpen its focus on specialty chemicals.

Business Segments and Revenue Contribution
Segment-wise Revenue Contribution
Specialty Chemicals: 84%

Pharmaceuticals: 16%

Geographical Revenue Contribution
Domestic Sales: 56% (by implication)

Exports: 44%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Aarti Industries is actively expanding its production capabilities to meet the increasing demand for specialty chemicals. The company is progressing with the commissioning of its Zone IV facility in Jhagadia, Gujarat, which is expected to produce high-value products for agrochemicals and pharmaceuticals. Additionally, Aarti Industries is scaling up its Methyl Methacrylate (MMA) and Nitrotoluene capacities to enhance its product offerings.

Name: Clean Science & Technology Ltd
Overview:
Clean Science and Technology Ltd, incorporated in 2003, is a global leader in manufacturing functionally critical specialty chemicals, with a strong focus on Performance Chemicals, Pharmaceutical & Agro Intermediates, and FMCG Chemicals. Its products hold top global rankings in several categories, serving diverse industries like polymers, food, pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, and agrochemicals. The company operates four manufacturing facilities with 17 plants in Maharashtra, boasting a total capacity of 54,500 MTPA, and supplies to over 500 customers across 35 countries.

The company has been expanding through strategic capex, especially in high-value products such as Hindered Amine Light Stabilisers (HALS), and new performance chemical units. With strong R&D capabilities and continuous product innovation, Clean Science aims to enhance market penetration, diversify geographically, and increase its addressable market by over USD 1.7 billion in the coming quarters through commercialization of new product lines.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Clean Science and Technology Ltd is actively expanding its global footprint, with a presence in over 30 countries. The company is investing approximately ₹300 crore in the first phase of its expansion, focusing on increasing production capacities for key specialty chemicals such as MEHQ, BHA, and AP

Name: Sumitomo Chemical India Ltd
Overview:
Sumitomo Chemical India Ltd. (SCIL) is a prominent agrochemical company with a strong balance of technical and formulation products, supported by backward integration for key molecules. It serves the agrochemicals, animal nutrition, and environmental health segments, leveraging proprietary innovations from its Japanese parent, Sumitomo Chemical Co. Ltd., and biological solutions from Valent Biosciences LLC.

Post its merger with Excel Crop Care Ltd., SCIL enhanced its generics portfolio, strengthened its marketing reach, and moved up in the Indian crop protection industry rankings. The company maintains a robust presence in both domestic and export markets, with operations extending into Africa, Asia, Europe, and the Americas, along with a network of over 15,000 distributors in India.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
SCIL is set to strengthen its specialty share via patented launches in FY26, while maintaining a dominant position in generics. With targeted capacity expansions, domestic manufacturing of SCC molecules, and a growing export base, the company aims for sustainable growth driven by innovation and global demand for high-value agrochemicals.

Name: Vinati Organics Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1989, Vinati Organics Limited is a leading specialty chemicals manufacturer with a diversified portfolio spanning over 30 products, including specialty aromatics, monomers, butyl phenols, antioxidants, and other specialty intermediates. The company is the world’s largest producer of Iso Butyl Benzene (IBB) and 2-Acrylamido-2-Methylpropane Sulphonic Acid (ATBS), holding a 60–65% global market share in ATBS. Its products find applications across pharmaceuticals, oil recovery, water treatment, polymer additives, personal care, and industrial sectors. Operations are anchored by two manufacturing facilities in Maharashtra (Mahad and Lote) and supported by an established global client base across 40+ countries.

Vinati’s strategic focus rests on capacity expansions, backward and forward integrations, and product diversification via its wholly owned subsidiary, Veeral Organics Pvt Ltd (VOPL). FY25 was marked by strong revenue growth, robust performance in ATBS, butyl phenols, and antioxidants, and significant capital investments aimed at scaling production capacity and broadening the product portfolio. The company also continues to invest in renewable energy, with 32.5 MW of solar capacity installed, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable operations.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management targets butyl phenols and antioxidants revenue of ₹800–850 Cr in FY26, with AO capacity utilization climbing from ~50% to ~90% over two years. ATBS demand remains robust, supported by oil recovery and water treatment applications, while VOPL’s new product portfolio is set to contribute ~₹100 Cr in FY26.

Name: GOCL Corporation Ltd
Overview:
GOCL Corporation Ltd is a prominent Indian chemical company engaged in manufacturing bio-diesel, edible oils, and chemical intermediates. Headquartered in Hyderabad, the company serves sectors including food processing, industrial chemicals, and energy.

With over three decades of experience, GOCL Corporation focuses on innovation, quality, and sustainable production practices. Its integrated manufacturing facilities, strong supply chain, and focus on research and development ensure consistent product quality and competitiveness in domestic and global markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company aims to expand bio-diesel and chemical production, invest in R&D, and enhance export capabilities. Focus on sustainability, technology adoption, and market expansion will drive long-term growth. Strategic collaborations are expected to strengthen market presence.

Name: Jayant Agro Organics Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1992, Jayant Agro-Organics Ltd is a leading global company specializing in castor-based specialty chemicals. The company manufactures and trades castor oil and its derivatives, such as oleochemicals, which find applications across various industries including agriculture, cosmetics, electronics, lubricants, and pharmaceuticals.

The company is committed to sustainable practices and innovation, ensuring that its products meet the highest standards of quality and performance. With a global presence, Jayant Agro-Organics continues to expand its reach and strengthen its position in the castor-based specialty chemicals industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Jayant Agro-Organics Ltd is well-positioned for continued growth. The strategic focus on enhancing research capabilities and maintaining stringent quality controls is expected to drive innovation and meet the evolving demands of the global market.

Name: Indo Amines Ltd
Overview:
Indo Amines specializes in the production and distribution of specialty chemicals, particularly amines and their derivatives, serving industries such as agrochemicals, pharmaceuticals, water treatment, and personal care. The company’s product portfolio includes aliphatic, aromatic, and specialty amines, produced at state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities with stringent quality control standards.

With a strong domestic and international customer base, Indo Amines supplies to over 500 clients globally and continuously develops customized solutions through its dedicated R&D team. The company emphasizes sustainable practices, including eco-friendly manufacturing processes and waste management, reinforcing its commitment to environmental responsibility.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Indo Amines Ltd is actively pursuing expansion to enhance its production capacity and product offerings. The company has acquired industrial land and a factory building in Mahad, Maharashtra, to establish a new manufacturing facility, signaling a strategic move to bolster its operations. Additionally, Indo Amines is expanding its product line to include various ketonic products, with plans to invest up to ₹75 crore in this initiative

Name: Camlin Fine Sciences Ltd
Overview:
Camlin Fine Sciences Ltd is a prominent player in the specialty chemicals sector, focusing on the production of aroma chemicals, antioxidants, and nutraceutical ingredients. The company operates globally, with a significant presence in North America, Europe, and Asia, catering to diverse industries such as food & beverages, animal nutrition, and biodiesel. Camlin Fine Sciences is committed to innovation and sustainability, continuously enhancing its product offerings and expanding its market reach.

With a robust manufacturing infrastructure and a strong emphasis on research and development, Camlin Fine Sciences strives to deliver high-quality products that meet the evolving needs of its customers.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Camlin Fine Sciences Ltd (CFS) is strategically expanding its global footprint and product offerings to capitalize on the growing demand for specialty chemicals. The company has reported a 15% year-on-year growth in its core business revenues for FY25, indicating strong performance in its key segments. CFS is also focusing on innovation and sustainability. The company is developing a new product in the oleochemicals category, with laboratory-scale work completed and a pilot plant under construction, targeting commercial implementation

Name: Balaji Amines Ltd
Overview:
Balaji Amines Limited is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in methylamines, ethylamines, derivatives of specialty chemicals, and pharma excipients. It is among the largest producers of aliphatic amines in the country and the sole domestic supplier for certain specialty products. The company’s products cater to diverse end-user industries, including pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and other specialty sectors, with a strong focus on import substitution.

The company operates multiple manufacturing facilities, with ongoing expansions to diversify its product range and enhance capacity. Its portfolio includes both core amines and downstream derivatives, supported by in-house R&D capabilities and a disciplined funding strategy using internal accruals. Strategic initiatives include capacity expansion in high-entry-barrier products, technology upgradation, and a move toward value-added specialty chemicals to strengthen market leadership.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The next two years will see commercialization of multiple new products, ramp-up of high-value specialty chemical capacities, and stronger subsidiary contribution. Focus remains on import substitution, moving up the value chain, and maintaining healthy margins through operational efficiency and market diversification.

Name: Laxmi Organic Industries Ltd
Overview:
Laxmi Organic Industries Limited is a specialty chemicals manufacturer with a diverse portfolio catering to pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, packaging, coatings, and industrial sectors. The company operates through two key segments — Essentials, comprising large-volume commodity chemicals like ethyl acetate and acetic anhydride, and Specialties, which includes high-value diketene derivatives and fluorospecialties.

With operations spanning over 30 countries and a strong domestic market presence, the company leverages cost leadership, product innovation, and strategic capacity expansions to enhance competitiveness. Its ongoing capex projects are aimed at boosting production capacity, improving margins, and positioning it for long-term growth in high-value specialty markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Laxmi Organic Industries Ltd is strategically positioning itself for robust growth by focusing on expanding its specialty chemicals segment, particularly in fluorine-based intermediates and diketene derivatives. The company is investing ₹1,100 crore over three years to enhance its capabilities in essentials and specialties, aiming to move higher up the value chain and enter new chemical spaces.

Name: Archean Chemical Industries Ltd
Overview:
Archean Chemical Industries Limited is India’s leading specialty marine chemical manufacturer engaged in the production of bromine, industrial salt, and sulphate of potash (SOP). The company operates an integrated facility in Hajipir, Gujarat, supported by captive brine reserves and proximity to Jakhau Jetty and Mundra Port, enabling cost efficiencies and global export reach.

The company is the largest Indian producer and exporter of bromine and industrial salt, with a dominant position in long-term supply contracts across multiple geographies. Its products cater to pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, flame retardants, petrochemicals, water treatment, fertilizers, and specialty chemicals. It is also one of the few global producers of SOP from natural sea brine.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Archean Chemical Industries Ltd is strategically expanding its specialty chemical portfolio, focusing on bromine derivatives, industrial salt, and sulfate of potash. The company is investing in capacity enhancements and product diversification to meet the growing global demand.

Name: Bodal Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Bodal Chemicals Limited specializes in the production and export of specialty chemicals, including dyes, dye intermediates, and pigments, serving industries such as textiles, leather, paper, and cosmetics. The company offers a wide-ranging product portfolio encompassing reactive dyes, acid dyes, solvent dyes, and other specialty chemicals, addressing diverse customer requirements. With a strong emphasis on research and development, Bodal Chemicals continuously innovates to meet global quality standards while implementing eco-friendly manufacturing practices to minimize environmental impact.

The company maintains a strong focus on customer satisfaction, quality control, and ethical business practices, positioning itself as a trusted partner in the global chemical industry. Bodal Chemicals leverages technology and strategic collaborations to enhance operational efficiency and ensure timely delivery of high-quality products across domestic and international markets.

These initiatives support Bodal Chemicals’ growth, sustainability, and strengthened presence in the specialty chemicals sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bodal Chemicals Ltd is actively pursuing strategic expansions to bolster its position in the specialty chemicals sector. The company has initiated the construction of a 5 MW lignite-based captive power plant at its Dudhvada unit in Gujarat, aiming to enhance energy efficiency and support its dye and dye intermediates manufacturing capacity expansion from 2,200 to 6,000 metric tons per month. Additionally, Bodal is diversifying its product portfolio by investing in the production of specialty benzene derivatives and expanding its sulfuric acid and derivatives capacity at the Saykha GIDC in Bharuch, Gujarat.

Name: Rossari Biotech Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 2003, Rossari Biotech is one of India’s premier specialty chemical manufacturers, focusing on product segments such as home, personal care and performance chemicals (HPPC), textile specialty chemicals, and animal health & nutrition (AHN). The company distinguishes itself through tailored solutions across consumer-focused and industrial chemistries.

Its operational strength is anchored by two R&D facilities one located at its Silvassa manufacturing campus and another at Dahej  supporting innovation and technological capabilities across its product lines.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With capacity expansions already underway including ₹95 crore of capex at Rossari and ₹97 crore for its subsidiaries the company is set to commission new assets by Q4 FY26, enhancing supply reliability and scale. Strategies focused on innovation, export growth, and strengthening emerging verticals suggest Rossari is well-positioned to sustain momentum and deliver profitable growth ahead

Name: Chemcon Speciality Chemicals Ltd
Overview: Chemcon Speciality Chemicals Ltd is a leading manufacturer of specialty chemicals, including silanes, pharmaceutical intermediates, oilfield chemicals, and agrochemical intermediates. Established over three decades ago, the company serves clients both in India and globally, emphasizing high-quality products and compliance with international standards. Chemcon’s operations focus on R&D, process innovation, and technology-driven production to deliver differentiated solutions. Its products are used in pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, paints, coatings, and oil and gas applications. The company is recognized for reliable supply, consistent quality, and sustainable manufacturing practices.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With its recent acquisition and focus on specialized chemical segments, Chemcon is positioned for continued growth in FY26. If global demand for pharmaceutical and oilfield intermediates remains resilient, the company is likely to sustain its upward earnings trajectory and further cement its specialty niche.

Name: Oriental Aromatics Ltd
Overview:
Oriental Aromatics Ltd. is a leading manufacturer of fine chemicals, specializing in camphor, perfumery and specialty aroma chemicals, fragrances, and flavors. As one of only ten fully integrated flavor, fragrance, and aroma chemical manufacturers globally, the company has positioned itself as a comprehensive solution provider for the fragrance and flavor industry. Its custom-designed fragrances find applications across a wide range of products, including soaps, shampoos, detergents, incense sticks, candles, and fine perfumes, while its flavors serve industries such as ice creams, bakeries, confectioneries, chocolates, chewing gums, and beverages. This diverse application base has enabled Oriental Aromatics to serve both domestic and international markets effectively.

The company has embarked on strategic capacity expansion, including the commissioning of a Hydrogenation plant at Vadodara for specialty aroma chemicals and the Mahad Capex project, aimed at strengthening its aroma chemical capabilities. With a total investment outlay of ₹350 crore, these initiatives highlight its commitment to scaling up production and reinforcing its leadership in specialty aroma chemicals. Additionally, the company focuses on high-value, low-volume products and adopts green chemistry practices to drive sustainable operations.

Oriental Aromatics’ long-term growth strategy emphasizes innovation-led product development, reengineering existing offerings, and expanding global reach. By continuously investing in R&D, capacity enhancement, and sustainability practices, the company aims to consolidate its market position while contributing to environmentally responsible growth in the fragrance and aroma chemical industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Oriental Aromatics Ltd is poised for a strategic growth phase, underpinned by significant capacity expansion and operational enhancements. The company has recently commissioned a brownfield project in Vadodara and is nearing completion of a greenfield facility in Mahad, Maharashtra, both aimed at bolstering its specialty aroma chemicals and camphor production capabilities.

Name: Sudarshan Chemical Industries Ltd
Overview:
Sudarshan Chemical Industries Ltd., established in 1952, is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in organic and inorganic pigments, effect pigments, and industrial chemicals. The company caters to diverse industries, including coatings, plastics, printing, cosmetics, and special applications, offering a wide range of color solutions. With a strong commitment to innovation and sustainability, Sudarshan has developed a robust portfolio of brands such as Sudasol, Sumica, and Sudajet.

Operating globally, Sudarshan maintains state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in India and has expanded its footprint internationally. The company's focus on research and development has enabled it to deliver high-quality products that meet international standards. Through strategic investments and acquisitions, Sudarshan continues to strengthen its position in the global market, aiming for sustainable growth and enhanced shareholder value.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Sudarshan Chemical Industries aims to leverage its expanded capabilities and global presence to drive growth. The company is focused on enhancing its product offerings, expanding into new markets, and achieving sustainable growth through innovation and strategic initiatives.

Name: Vidhi Specialty Food Ingredients Ltd
Overview:
Vidhi Specialty Food Ingredients Limited is a leading manufacturer of synthetic and natural food-grade colors, catering to a diverse range of industries including food & beverage, confectionery, pharmaceuticals, pet foods, cosmetics, inkjet inks, personal care, and home care. Established in 1994, the company has grown to become Asia's second-largest food color manufacturer. With a global presence, Vidhi's products are distributed and consumed in over 80 countries across all six continents.

The company's manufacturing facilities are spread over an area of 176,000 square feet, located in Dhatav Village of Raigad District in Maharashtra, India. Vidhi is ISO 9001:2008, ISO 22000:2018, GMP, HACCP, and FSSC 22000 certified, with its products also carrying HALAL and Kosher certifications.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Vidhi Specialty Food Ingredients aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing its service capabilities. The company's focus on integrating AI solutions and surveillance products is expected to drive future growth. Vidhi's commitment to operational excellence and strategic partnerships positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the ICT distribution sector.

Name: Tatva Chintan Pharma Chem Ltd
Overview:
Tatva Chintan Pharma Chem Limited, established in 1996, is a leading manufacturer specializing in a diverse portfolio of specialty chemicals. The company offers products such as Structure Directing Agents (SDAs), Phase Transfer Catalysts (PTCs), electrolyte salts for batteries, and intermediates for pharmaceuticals and agrochemicals. These products cater to various industries, including energy storage, semiconductors, and agriculture.

With a strong emphasis on research and development, Tatva Chintan has established a significant presence in both domestic and international markets. The company is committed to innovation and sustainability, aiming to meet the evolving needs of its global clientele. Through strategic initiatives and a focus on quality, Tatva Chintan continues to strengthen its position in the specialty chemicals sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
