Tatva Chintan aims to achieve over 25% revenue growth and a 20% EBITDA margin for FY26. The company is focusing on expanding its presence in the semiconductor sector and enhancing its product offerings in the electrolyte salts segment. Management remains cautiously optimistic, acknowledging global uncertainties while pursuing strategic growth initiatives.

Name: Alkyl Amines Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1979, Alkyl Amines is a leading Indian manufacturer of aliphatic amines and related specialty chemicals, serving diverse industries such as pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, dyes, rubber chemicals, surfactants, and specialty chemicals. The company’s product portfolio includes base amines, derivatives, and specialty chemicals, primarily catering to domestic markets with a growing export presence.

The company operates in a competitive environment marked by pricing pressures and oversupply, particularly in base amines. Despite these challenges, Alkyl Amines continues to invest in capacity expansion and new product development, with a focus on specialty chemicals and higher value-added products to maintain margins and market positioning.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company targets double-digit volume growth of 10–15% for FY26, with continued pricing pressure in base amines expected to persist. Anti-dumping duties on acetonitrile could help stabilize pricing and margins. Alkyl Amines aims to leverage capacity expansions, new specialty products, and growing pharma demand to drive sustainable growth over the next two years.

Name: Galaxy Surfactants Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1986, Galaxy Surfactants Ltd is India’s largest manufacturer of oleochemical-based surfactants and specialty care products catering to the home and personal care industries. With over 215 product grades, the company serves leading global, regional, and local FMCG brands in hair care, skin care, oral care, cosmetics, and home cleaning. Its clientele includes top multinationals such as Unilever, Reckitt Benckiser, P&G, and L’Oréal.

Galaxy operates seven manufacturing facilities across India, Egypt, and the USA, with a combined capacity exceeding 440,000 MTPA. Its revenue mix in 9MFY25 was 60% performance surfactants and 40% specialty care products, with geographical exposure of 40% India, 24% AMET, and 36% RoW. The company is pursuing capacity expansion at Jhagadia (India) and Suez (Egypt) while building a mini-plant at Tarapur to support specialty manufacturing.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management is confident of delivering double-digit volume growth in RoW (Americas/Europe) and 8–10% in India over the medium term. Specialty share is set to rise steadily, with leave-on products and beauty & wellness expected to be key contributors to incremental EBITDA. Despite near-term headwinds from inflation, geopolitical risks, and competitive threats from China, Galaxy remains focused on disciplined execution, market diversification, and margin expansion through premiumization and innovation leadership.

Name: Neogen Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Neogen Chemicals Ltd, founded in 1991 and headquartered in Thane, India, is a prominent manufacturer specializing in bromine-based and lithium-based specialty chemicals. The company produces a diverse range of products, including organo-bromine compounds, Grignard reagents, advanced intermediates, and lithium salts, catering to industries such as pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and engineering. Neogen's commitment to quality and innovation has established it as a trusted partner in the specialty chemicals sector.

The company has acquired land in Gujarat to set up a state-of-the-art facility for manufacturing lithium-ion battery electrolytes and additives, aiming to meet the growing demand from the electric vehicle and energy storage sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Neogen Chemicals Ltd is poised for growth, driven by its strategic expansion into battery materials through Neogen Ionics Ltd. The company projects revenues of ₹300 crore to ₹500 crore from its electrolytes business in FY26, indicating significant potential in this new vertical.

Name: Yasho Industries Ltd
Overview:
Yasho Industries Ltd is a specialty chemicals manufacturer, specializing in the production of surfactants, emulsifiers, and other chemical intermediates. The company's products serve various industries including agriculture, textiles, and personal care. Yasho Industries is committed to innovation and sustainability, focusing on developing eco-friendly solutions for its customers.

In FY25, Yasho Industries reported a consolidated net profit of ₹6.11 crore, with revenue volumes increasing by 18% and value by 13% compared to the previous year. The company's performance was bolstered by strong demand across its product segments and efficient operational management.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Yasho Industries Ltd is strategically expanding its global footprint and product portfolio to drive sustainable growth. The company has secured a 15-year global supply agreement for lubricant additives, expected to generate approximately ₹150 crore in annual revenue starting from FY27. To support this, Yasho is investing ₹100 crore in FY26, with ₹30 crore already allocated, to enhance its manufacturing capabilities.

Name: Premier Explosives Ltd
Overview:
Premier Explosives Limited is an Indian manufacturer specializing in high-energy materials such as bulk explosives, detonators, and solid propellants. The company caters to various sectors, including defense, space, mining, and infrastructure. It also provides operation and maintenance services for solid propellant plants at ISRO's Sriharikota Centre and the DRDO's Solid Fuel Complex at Jagdalpur.

With a strong presence in the defense and space sectors, Premier Explosives has established itself as a key player in the Indian industrial explosives industry. The company's commitment to quality and innovation has enabled it to secure significant contracts and expand its operations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Premier Explosives aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and increasing its presence in the defense and space sectors. The company is focusing on enhancing its manufacturing capabilities and exploring new markets to drive revenue growth.

Name: Valiant Organics Ltd
Overview: Valiant Organics Ltd is a specialty chemicals manufacturer focused on producing high-value intermediates for agrochemical and pharmaceutical industries. Armed with advanced processing capabilities, the company develops customized ingredients that meet diverse client specifications, enabling deep integration in complex chemical supply chains. With an integrated manufacturing footprint, Valiant Organics serves both domestic and export markets, supporting its position as a niche supplier in its sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Valiant Organics is well-positioned for robust growth, supported by its strategic investments in niche intermediates. With a healthy sales recovery and a return to profitability, the company appears on track to continue building value in FY26—especially if global demand for precision specialty inputs remains strong and operational efficiencies are maintained.

Name: Fairchem Organics Ltd
Overview:
Fairchem Organics Ltd is an India-based company engaged in the manufacture of specialty chemicals, such as oleo chemicals and intermediate nutraceuticals. The company produces distilled fatty acids, methyl, nutraceuticals, and other related chemicals. Fairchem Organics Ltd was incorporated on March 27, 2019, and operates a manufacturing plant at Village Chekhala, Ta. Sanand, in Ahmedabad, Gujarat.

The company's products find application in various industries, including nutraceuticals, paints, printing inks, detergents, adhesives, and more. Fairchem Organics Ltd aims to lead the Indian specialty chemicals industry by procuring and refining waste from vegetable oil refineries into valuable components.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Fairchem Organics Ltd is actively pursuing growth through strategic initiatives aimed at expanding its product portfolio and market presence. The company is developing a new product in the oleochemicals category, with laboratory-scale work completed and a pilot plant under construction, targeting commercial implementation. Additionally, Fairchem is enhancing its position in the high-value cosmetics segment by expanding its isostearic acid product line, which has already seen shipments to Europe

Name: IDBI Bank Ltd
Overview:
IDBI Bank is a leading Indian commercial bank with a wide branch network and a strategic mix of retail and corporate banking. It has a growing international footprint and is focused on digital transformation through partnerships with fintech firms to offer innovative financial products.
 
 Business Segments
Retail Banking:   Savings, personal loans, education loans, and digital banking services.
 
Corporate Banking:  Term loans, working capital finance, and project financing.
 
Overseas Operations:  DIFC Dubai branch and IFSC unit at GIFT City, expanding international reach.
 
Revenue & Metrics Summary
CASA ratio: 55.65% indicating strong low-cost deposits.
 
GNPA reduced to 1.25%, NIM at 4.02%, CAR at 19.57% — all showing robust asset quality, profitability, and capital strength.
 
Focused on maintaining a corporate to retail mix of 40:60 while targeting 10-12% business growth and sustaining NIM > 3.25%.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

IDBI Bank plans to deepen its reach by growing its customer base in rural and semi-urban areas through a digital-first strategy complemented by fintech partnerships. It aims to augment fee-based income from diversified sources, while ensuring prudent risk management and strong capital adequacy for sustainable expansion.

Name: Punjab National Bank
Overview:
Punjab National Bank (PNB) is one of India’s largest public sector banks, operating an extensive domestic network of over 10,900 branches and 14,000 ATMs, with strong dominance in North India. The bank strengthened its scale and reach through its mergers with Oriental Bank of Commerce and United Bank of India. PNB also has a modest international footprint spanning six countries.
 
Business Segments :
 
 Domestic Banking (~97.4% of total business): Core operations include retail, corporate, and SME banking, with significant loan exposure to sectors like energy, telecom, agriculture, and MSMEs.
 
 International Banking (~2.6% of total business): Overseas branches, subsidiaries, and JVs contribute a small share, focusing on trade finance and NRI banking.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Punjab National Bank’s growth is expected to benefit from India’s government-driven capital expenditure, which will boost credit demand across sectors. Loan growth is likely to be supported by economic momentum and seasonal trends, while strong risk management and diversified lending practices help maintain stable asset quality. Healthy CASA ratio and net interest margins underpin profitability, with focused efforts on managing NPAs in stressed sectors like energy and telecom.

Name: Union Bank of India
Overview:
Union Bank of India is a large PSU bank offering comprehensive banking, insurance, mutual funds, merchant banking, and wealth management services. The bank’s scale significantly expanded post its merger with Andhra Bank and Corporation Bank, making it one of India’s largest networks with nearly 9,600 branches and 13,000 ATMs.
 
Key Business Segments & Revenue Drivers
 Corporate & Infrastructure Lending: ~16% of total domestic advances to infrastructure; significant exposure to sectors like NBFCs/HFCs (11%), basic metals, food processing, textiles, construction, and engineering (combined ~26.5%).
 
 Retail, Agriculture & MSME (RAM): Key growth driver with rising credit demand.
 
 Government & Merchant Banking: Steady fee-based income streams.
 
 Minor International Business: Overseas contributes ~2% to total business.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Union Bank is focusing on sustaining asset quality by reducing fresh slippages and enhancing recoveries, while maintaining a stable lending mix with growing emphasis on RAM (Retail, Agriculture, MSME) segments. The bank is strategically aligned with government initiatives to boost rural and MSME lending, and is well-positioned to capitalize on the uptick in infrastructure spending and capital-intensive sectors as the Indian economy recovers

Name: Punjab & Sind Bank
Overview:
Punjab & Sind Bank operates primarily in deposits, loans, advances, and remittances, with a strategic focus on growing its Retail, Agriculture, and MSME (RAM) segments. The bank has demonstrated steady business growth driven by advances and deposits, supported by digital lending initiatives and branch network expansion. Its portfolio mix and co-lending partnerships reflect a push towards higher-yielding assets and enhanced digital customer experience.

The bank continues to balance margin pressures amid rate cuts by optimizing its deposit costs and repricing advances, aiming to sustain profitability with a disciplined approach towards growth and risk management.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Punjab & Sind Bank targets 15-16% credit growth and 8-10% deposit growth in FY26, alongside further RAM portfolio expansion. Margin guidance is under review with repo rate cuts expected. Co-lending scale-up and branch network expansion remain strategic growth pillars. Asset quality and profitability focus continue amid competitive and macro challenges.

Name: Bank of India
Overview:
Bank of India (BOI) is a major public sector bank with a long-standing presence in India’s banking sector. The bank provides a wide range of banking and financial services, serving retail, corporate, MSME, and international customers through an extensive domestic network and growing overseas operations.
 
  Business Segments
 Retail Banking:
 Includes deposits, personal loans, housing finance, and digital banking products aimed at individuals and SMEs.
 
 Wholesale & Corporate Banking:
 Provides a range of working capital, project finance, and structured credit solutions to corporates, mid-corporates, and large institutions.
 
 Treasury Operations:
 Engages in investments, forex, and money market operations to manage liquidity, hedging, and risk.
 
 International Banking:
 Operates branches and subsidiaries abroad, supporting cross-border trade, NRI services, and international treasury activities.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bank of India is focusing on expanding its loan portfolio, particularly in retail and priority sectors, to drive growth. The bank is enhancing its digital capabilities by investing ₹2,000 crore in IT upgrades, aiming to improve cybersecurity and streamline operations. Initiatives like the Star Samriddhi Home Loan Scheme and Project Nextech are designed to capture new customer segments and enhance service delivery. Additionally, the bank is exploring open banking and wealth management services to diversify its offerings. With a commitment to innovation and customer-centric solutions, Bank of India is positioning itself for sustained growth in the evolving banking landscape.

Name: Indian Overseas Bank
Overview:
Indian Overseas Bank (IOB) is a public sector bank founded in 1937 and nationalized in 1969. With a network of over 3,200 branches and 3,100 ATMs, it primarily serves rural and semi-urban markets across India. IOB also maintains a modest overseas presence through branches, remittance centers, and a JV subsidiary abroad, though international business contributes just ~2% of total deposits. The bank has an 18% stake in Universal Sompo General Insurance, broadening its exposure beyond traditional banking.
 
Business Segments 
 Retail Banking (including CASA deposits & retail loans): Core focus area, key driver for low-cost deposits and fee income.
 
 Corporate Banking: Significant exposure but with challenges — ~40% of corporate loans are NPAs; only 44% of corporate book is rated A or higher.
 
 MSME & Agri: Growing focus to diversify credit book towards safer, granular lending.
 
 International Banking: Small share (~2% of deposits); limited overseas operations.
 
 Insurance: 18% stake in Universal Sompo General Insurance Ltd.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
IOB aims to drive growth by focusing on retail and MSME lending to reduce dependence on riskier corporate loans, supported by stronger credit appraisal and recovery processes to enhance asset quality. Expansion of CASA deposits and continued digital transformation initiatives are expected to improve cost efficiency and customer reach. The bank plans to grow its domestic branch network while selectively scaling international operations, complemented by strategic partnerships and alliances to diversify offerings and strengthen profitability.

Name: Bank of Baroda
Overview:
Bank of Baroda (BOB) is India’s third-largest public sector bank after its amalgamation with Vijaya Bank and Dena Bank in FY19. With the Government of India holding a ~64% stake, BOB offers a comprehensive suite of services including retail banking, corporate banking, SME and rural banking, international banking, NRI banking, and treasury operations. The bank maintains a strong domestic and international network, supporting its vision to serve diverse customer segments across India and key global markets.
 
Business Segments 
 
 Retail & Personal Banking: ~45% of total advances — includes home loans, personal loans, education loans, auto loans, and deposits.
 
 Corporate & Wholesale Banking: ~40% of total advances — provides working capital, project finance, trade finance, and treasury services.
 
 International Banking: ~15% of total business — operations in 17 countries with 94 overseas branches/offices, plus JVs and associate banks.
 
 SME & Rural Banking: Spread across rural and semi-urban India (35% of branches); key driver of priority sector lending and financial inclusion.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
BOB is positioned for steady growth through healthy expansion of its loan book, particularly in unsecured retail and SME segments, supported by margin improvement from corporate loan repricing and better asset yields. A strong rural presence and focus on financial inclusion enable the bank to tap underpenetrated markets, while digital initiatives and branch network optimization enhance efficiency and customer reach.

Name: Indian Bank
Overview:
Indian Bank is a mid-sized public sector bank in India with a long operational history and a diversified portfolio of banking services. It serves customers across Treasury, Corporate/Wholesale Banking, Retail Banking, and Other Banking Operations. Following its merger with Allahabad Bank in 2020, Indian Bank has significantly expanded its scale and branch network. It also maintains a modest international presence through branches in Singapore, Colombo, and Jaffna. The bank focuses on retail, SME, agri-business, and NRI services and continues to strengthen its digital and value-added offerings under branded initiatives like IOB-Udaan and IOB-Wealth.
 
 Business Segments 
 
 Retail Banking: ~50% of total business
 
 Corporate/Wholesale Banking: ~40%
 
 Treasury & Other Banking Operations: ~10%
 
 International Operations: ~2% of total business
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Indian Bank is on a path of steady performance improvement post-merger with Allahabad Bank, supported by focus on the RAM (Retail, Agriculture, MSME) sectors and improving asset quality. With enhanced digital offerings, targeted customer products, and better cost control, it aims to boost return ratios and credit growth. While challenges around the restructured book and macroeconomic risks persist, the bank's disciplined risk management and diversification strategy provide a solid foundation for sustainable and profitable growth in the evolving Indian banking landscape.

Name: Bank of Maharashtra
Overview:
Bank of Maharashtra is a public sector bank offering treasury, corporate, retail, and other banking services. It is focused on steady growth through aggressive branch expansion, strong risk management, and improving customer experience using digital tools. The bank maintains a high CASA ratio and disciplined cost control.

The bank’s key growth drivers include gold loans, agri, and MSME segments, supported by new technology initiatives like a revamped mobile app and an international banking unit. Management is confident about meeting growth and profitability targets despite some market challenges.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bank of Maharashtra is focused on expanding its retail banking operations, aiming for a 17% credit growth by enhancing its loan portfolio. The bank is also increasing its branch network, planning to open 1,000 new branches over the next five years, with 321 branches targeted in the next 18 months, to improve accessibility and customer service. Additionally, the bank is enhancing its digital banking services, including enabling UPI transactions for NRIs in 12 countries, to improve customer accessibility and convenience.

Name: City Union Bank Ltd
Overview:
City Union Bank Limited (CUB) is one of India’s oldest private sector banks, with a strong presence in South India and a primary focus on MSME lending, retail banking, and wholesale trade. Headquartered in Tamil Nadu, the bank has built a niche in secured lending products, including jewel loans, loan against property, and home loans, while maintaining a conservative approach toward unsecured credit. Its diversified operations span treasury, corporate banking, retail banking, and other banking services.

The bank’s strategy emphasizes stable growth, strong asset quality, and prudent cost management, supported by an expanding branch network and deep customer relationships. Leveraging its expertise in MSME financing,
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
CUB targets sustaining mid-teen credit growth in FY26 while maintaining stable margins and asset quality. Focus areas include scaling secured retail products, expanding in rural and semi-urban markets, and leveraging margin levers such as higher gold loan penetration and improved CD ratio. Management remains optimistic, citing strong demand, robust asset quality metrics, and no visible signs of stress in the current portfolio.

Name: State Bank of India
Overview:
State Bank of India (SBI) — India’s oldest and largest public-sector bank — plays a pivotal role in the nation’s banking and financial ecosystem. A Fortune 500 company, SBI serves over 45 crore customers, including retail, corporate, SME, and NRI segments. Its robust physical and digital presence makes it a backbone of India’s financial inclusion and banking outreach.
 
 Business Segments
 Retail Banking: 42.54% Savings & current accounts, home loans, personal loans, auto loans, credit/debit cards, and wealth services.
 
 Corporate Banking: 33.25% Lending, trade finance, working capital, and specialized services for corporates & institutions.
 
 International Banking: Offshore branches, NRI services, and global remittance products — operating in 32 countries.
 
 Digital Banking: Platforms like Yono, SBI-Easy Ride, digital document execution, and various mobile/internet banking services.
 
 Government & Priority Initiatives: Leading contributor to financial inclusion, PMJDY, rooftop solar project financing, and EV infrastructure support.

Retail Personal Advances: Increased by 12.56% YoY, reaching ₹15.39 lakh crore, fueled by robust demand in home loans and personal lending. 

Small and Medium Enterprises (SME): Expanded by 19.1% YoY, highlighting the bank's support for the MSME sector. 

Agricultural Loans: Grew by 12.67% YoY, reflecting SBI's commitment to rural development. 

Corporate Advances: Registered a more modest growth of 5.7% YoY, indicating a cautious approach in corporate lending.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
SBI plans to continue investing in digital banking platforms and green financing initiatives to enhance customer experience and sustainability. The bank is expanding into new domestic and international markets while strengthening partnerships with organizations to promote financial literacy, inclusion, and eco-friendly banking practices. Leveraging its unmatched scale, strong financial metrics, and focus on innovation, SBI aims to sustain growth and maintain its leadership position in India’s evolving banking sector.

Name: Canara Bank
Overview:
Canara Bank, founded in 1906, is one of India’s largest and oldest public sector banks. With a vast domestic and international presence, the bank plays a crucial role in providing banking and financial services across urban, semi-urban, and rural India. The merger with Syndicate Bank significantly expanded its scale and outreach.
 
 Business Segments
 Retail Banking: Loans, deposits, savings accounts, and digital banking services for individuals.
 
 Corporate Banking: Lending and treasury services for large and mid-sized businesses.
 
 Agricultural & Rural Banking: Strong focus on rural financing and priority sector lending.
 
 International Operations: Subsidiaries and branches in UK, USA, UAE, South Africa, Tanzania, and joint venture bank in Russia.
 
 Other Services: Insurance, wealth management, merchant banking, and partnerships.
 
 Revenue & Metrics Summary
 A significant share of Canara Bank’s business comes from its extensive domestic branch network — with 60% of branches in rural and semi-urban areas. The bank’s CASA ratio stands at ~34%, supporting low-cost funding. Net performing assets are at a comfortable 1.96%, with a healthy NIM of 2.93% and a strong CAR of 16.72%, indicating stable profitability and capital strength.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Canara Bank is pursuing balanced growth by strengthening its retail and rural loan book, capitalizing on its extensive network and post-merger scale. The bank is focusing on mobilizing CASA deposits to maintain low-cost funding, while simultaneously leveraging digital banking platforms to improve customer experience.

Name: Central Bank of India
Overview:
Central Bank of India is a leading public sector commercial bank with diversified operations across treasury, corporate/wholesale banking, retail banking, and other banking services. Its offerings span government and corporate securities trading, money market operations, foreign exchange, corporate lending, retail banking, and MSME financing.

The bank has been strategically shifting its loan book toward retail, agriculture, and MSME segments while maintaining a disciplined approach to corporate lending. Strong capital adequacy, robust asset quality, and a growing low-cost CASA base underpin its profitability focus.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
For FY26, the bank targets sustained credit growth led by RAM, NIM above 3%, and net NPA below 0.5%. Continued digital expansion, CASA growth, treasury gains, and tax regime benefits are expected to further improve profitability. The disciplined approach to risk, focus on bottom-line, and strong capital base position the bank for steady and sustainable growth.

Name: UCO Bank
Overview:
UCO Bank is an established Indian public sector bank offering a comprehensive suite of banking and financial services. With a robust network of 3,100 branches and 2,300 ATMs nationwide — particularly strong in the eastern region — the bank serves diverse customer segments, including retail, corporate, and international banking clients.
 
  Business Segments
 Retail Banking:
 Offers deposits, personal loans, housing loans, NRI services, internet banking, and value-added digital solutions.
 
 Corporate Banking:
 Provides banking services to sectors like infrastructure, NBFCs, basic metals, construction, food processing, textiles, engineering, and more.
 
 Treasury & Overseas:
 Overseas business contributes ~5% of total business. Also includes authorized branches for direct tax collection and a dedicated NRI corner.
 
 Bancassurance:
 Partners with LIC and Future Generali India Insurance to distribute life and general insurance products; exploring new tie-ups to strengthen this revenue stream.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

UCO Bank is focusing on sustainable growth by emphasizing its Retail, Agriculture, and MSME (RAM) segments, which collectively constitute over 60% of its loan portfolio. The bank aims to achieve credit growth between 12-14% by selectively pursuing corporate lending opportunities with favorable pricing and yields. To enhance profitability, UCO Bank is implementing digital transformation initiatives, such as Project Parivartan, to improve customer experience and operational efficiency. Additionally, the bank plans to raise capital in tranches to meet regulatory requirements and support its growth strategies. By focusing on these areas, UCO Bank aims to strengthen its position in the banking sector and deliver value to its stakeholders.

Name: Dodla Dairy Ltd
Overview:
Dodla Dairy Limited, incorporated in 1995 and headquartered in Telangana, is an integrated dairy company engaged in the procurement, processing, and sale of milk and dairy-based value-added products. The company operates through a strong procurement network spread across five states, with products available in eleven states under popular brands such as Dodla, Dodla Dairy, and KC+.

Its product portfolio covers fresh milk, curd, buttermilk, lassi, paneer, ghee, flavored milk, sweets, and ice cream. Beyond India, it has established operations in African markets under the Dairy Top and Dodla brands. With a strong procurement base, expanding footprint, and increasing contribution from value-added products, Dodla positions itself as a consumer-focused dairy brand with both domestic and international reach.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Dodla Dairy is strategically expanding its operations to achieve significant growth across both its consumer durables and engineering divisions. The company plans to invest ₹200–₹220 crore to enhance production capabilities in appliances, air conditioning, and automotive chain businesses. In the consumer durables sector, IFB aims to increase sales of large-capacity washing machines and refrigerators, targeting monthly sales of 60,000 units. The company is also focusing on cost-reduction initiatives to improve profit margins.

Name: Orkla India Ltd
Overview:
Orkla India Limited, incorporated in 1996 (originally as a different entity before subsequent reorganisations), is a multi-category Indian food company that offers a wide range of packaged food products spanning breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks, beverages and desserts.

It operates under well-known brands such as MTR and Eastern, focusing especially on packaged spices, convenience meals and ready-to-eat food items, while also reaching export markets. Orkla India is a subsidiary of the Norwegian investment company Orkla ASA and leverages global brand-building expertise along with strong regional distribution in South India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Orkla India is targeting growth by reinforcing its leadership in core categories such as spices and spice blends while also accelerating its presence in ready-to-eat, breakfast and dessert segments with new product innovation and premiumisation. It plans to expand exports selectively across its network of over 40 countries to tap Indian diaspora and global demand for Indian flavours.

Name: Jeena Sikho Lifecare Ltd
Overview:
Jeena Sikho Lifecare is a provider of Ayurvedic healthcare systems in India, operating a range of wellness centers and clinics under its prominent brand. It focuses on holistic treatments rooted in traditional practices, aiming to promote stress-free and healthy living.

Over the past decade, it has developed both service (wellness treatments) and product (Ayurvedic medicines) businesses, expanding its presence through clinics, hospitals, and manufacturing capabilities. It also emphasizes quality accreditation and strategic infrastructure growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The growth roadmap includes global expansion plans into regions such as Dubai, Vietnam, Mauritius, London, the U.S., Australia, and New Zealand. It is also increasing hospital bed capacity and entering the OTC medicines market with clinical trials completed for key products—positioning itself for further international and product growth

Name: KRBL Ltd
Overview:
KRBL Limited is a globally recognized player in the basmati rice industry, with end-to-end control over the value chain from seed development to branded marketing under titles like India Gate. Its operations also include a growing captive energy footprint comprising wind, solar, and biomass, which supports its agricultural processes and contributes excess power to local distribution.

The company's integrated approach, including contract farming and extensive distribution networks, positions it as a leader in the premium rice segment both domestically and internationally. Their sustained focus on operational efficiency and brand expansion continues to underpin their performance in a demanding commodities market.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
KRBL is well-positioned for continued growth anchored in its premium rice offerings and global distribution strength. Export acceleration, broader domestic market outreach, and operational automation should help sustain margins and top-line expansion. While raw material volatility and policy shifts will need close monitoring, strategic execution and brand strength form a solid foundation for future performance.

Name: Bajaj Consumer Care Ltd
Overview:
Bajaj Consumer Care Ltd is a prominent Indian personal care company, best known for its flagship brand Bajaj Almond Drops Hair Oil, along with coconut oil and other hair care products. With decades of legacy, it enjoys strong brand equity and wide retail presence across urban and rural India.

Part of the Bajaj Group, the company has expanded into skincare and niche personal care categories, while also pursuing international growth and strategic acquisitions to broaden its portfolio and reach.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Early indicators show strategic optimism. With stabilized consumer trends, marketing focus on core brands, channel revamps, and deeper international penetration, the company is positioned to regain growth traction and margin stability. Integration of Vishal Personal Care is expected to strengthen product depth and geographic reach.

Name: Mrs Bectors Food Specialities Ltd
Overview:
Mrs Bectors Food Specialities Ltd is a leading Indian FMCG company known for its biscuit and bakery brands—Cremica and English Oven—catering to both premium retail and institutional segments. The company combines strong brand equity with robust manufacturing and distribution capabilities to serve varied consumer needs.

With a diversified portfolio across biscuits, bakery, and dairy offerings, and growing export presence, Mrs Bectors continues scaling through innovation and geographic expansion while steering toward higher operational efficiency.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The outlook is optimistic: capacity additions by FY26 will aid volume growth; cost pressures are easing alongside duty reductions; and margin recovery is projected with a targeted EBITDA margin of ~14% from Q2 onward. Growth will be fueled by innovation, premiumization, and expansion in east and west India, supported by exports to MENA and Southeast Asia.

Name: Sirca Paints India Ltd
Overview:
Sirca Paints India Ltd, established in 2006, is a prominent player in India's decorative coatings industry. The company specializes in manufacturing, selling, and exporting wood coatings and other decorative paints under the 'Sirca' brand. Sirca offers a diverse product range, including polyurethane (PU) coatings, stains, acrylic PU, polyester, wall paints, and UV products. 

Its product categories encompass interior and exterior paints, wall primers, finishes/effects, and wall putty. The company operates manufacturing units in India and imports products from Sirca S.p.A., Italy, to meet domestic demand. Sirca's commitment to quality and innovation has positioned it as a trusted brand in the Indian paint industry
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Sirca Paints India Ltd aims to capitalize on its strong market position and expanding product portfolio to drive sustained growth. The acquisition of Wembley Group's business undertaking is expected to contribute to increased market share and enhanced product offerings.

Name: Kansai Nerolac Paints Ltd
Overview:
Kansai Nerolac Paints, a subsidiary of Kansai Paints Co. Ltd, Japan, is a leading manufacturer in the paint and coatings industry, holding a dominant position in industrial coatings and a strong presence in decorative paints. The company leverages its parent’s global technology expertise to enhance product quality, innovation, and operational efficiency. It caters to diverse customer segments across automotive, powder coatings, general industrial, protective, and decorative categories.

The company operates through two main segments Decorative and Industrial coatings serving both institutional and retail markets in India and internationally. Its business model focuses on product innovation, distribution network expansion, operational efficiency, and customer-centric solutions. With a wide product range and strong partnerships with leading OEMs, Kansai Nerolac continues to expand market share while maintaining sustainable and profitable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to sustain double-digit growth in FY26, driven by strong decorative paint sales, industrial segment recovery, and continuous operational improvements. Focus will remain on innovation, rural penetration, and cost efficiency to strengthen margins while capitalizing on favorable macroeconomic conditions.

Name: Asian Paints Ltd
Overview:
Asian Paints is the largest paint manufacturer in India and one of the top global players, operating in 15 countries with 26 manufacturing facilities. The company has a robust product portfolio, including decorative and industrial paints, home décor, furnishings, lighting, and furniture. It commands a dominant position in the Indian paints market, serving both domestic and international customers
 
 Business Segment: 
 Decorative Business (~84% in Q1 FY25 vs 88% in FY22): The company offers interior and exterior wall finishes, waterproofing, wood finishes, enamels, adhesives, and more. It also includes a home decor business that offers modular kitchens and wardrobes, bath fittings and sanitaryware, and decorative lighting
 
 International Business (7% in Q1 FY25 vs 10% in FY22): [4] The company operates in 14 countries spanning across 4 regions globally. It serves 60+ countries
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Asian Paints is set to strengthen its market leadership through planned investments of ₹6,750 crore over three years, aimed at expanding manufacturing capacity—including a new eco-friendly plant in Madhya Pradesh—enhancing backward integration in chemicals, and pursuing strategic acquisitions. The company is diversifying into home décor, lighting, and furnishings, targeting 8–10% of revenue from these segments by FY26, while continuing international expansion across 15 countries. Innovation and sustainability remain key priorities, with eco-friendly products and advanced manufacturing technologies supporting long-term growth, operational efficiency, and resilience against competitive pressures.

Name: Indigo Paints Ltd
Overview:
Indigo Paints Ltd, incorporated in 2000, is a leading player in India’s decorative paints industry, recognized for pioneering several category-creator products such as metallic emulsions and tile coat emulsions. The company holds dominant market share in certain differentiated products, enabling strong brand positioning and pricing power in select niches.

Its diverse product portfolio includes emulsions, enamels, wood coatings, primers, distempers, cement paints, and putties. Indigo maintains a strong distribution network with a growing dealer base and tinting machine installations, while also investing in capacity expansion and marketing to strengthen its geographic presence across India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Indigo Paints is actively pursuing a multi-faceted growth strategy to enhance its market presence and operational efficiency. The company is expanding its footprint in Tier 3 and 4 cities, aiming to leverage first-mover advantages and pricing power in these underserved markets. Additionally, Indigo Paints is enhancing its distribution network and increasing dealer throughput to bolster sales and brand visibility

Name: Berger Paints India Ltd
Overview:
Berger Paints India Ltd is one of India’s largest and most trusted paint companies, with a robust domestic and global presence. Holding the second-largest market share in India, Berger ranks fourth in Asia and seventh globally among decorative paint companies. The company’s legacy, widespread manufacturing footprint, and consistent focus on innovation and brand building have helped it become a household name across urban and rural markets.
 
 Business Segments
 Decorative Paints (80–85% of Revenue)
 This is Berger Paints’ core revenue driver, covering a wide spectrum from economy to premium and luxury interior and exterior paints. It includes products for households, commercial buildings, and institutions.
 
 Industrial Coatings
 
 Protective Coatings: Berger is a market leader in high-performance coatings for infrastructure, refineries, and heavy industries.
 
 Automotive & General Industrial: Coatings for OEMs and industrial components.
 
 Powder Coatings: Catering to furniture, appliances, and other sectors.
 
 International Markets (8% of Revenue)
 Berger Paints has operations in eight countries, including plants in Nepal, Poland, and Russia, providing geographic diversification.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Berger Paints India is focused on expanding its presence in both urban and rural markets, aiming to double its distributor network from 50,000 to 100,000 by 2030. The company plans to enhance its retail footprint in southern, western, and central regions, including Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, and Rajasthan. To support this growth, Berger is investing in new manufacturing facilities in Panagarh, West Bengal, and Odisha, which will produce paints, coatings, construction chemicals, and intermediates

Name: Shalimar Paints Ltd
Overview:
Shalimar Paints Ltd, established in 1902, is among India’s oldest paint companies with operations spanning decorative paints, industrial coatings, and allied services. The company manufactures products for both retail consumers and institutional clients, catering to surfaces like walls, metals, pipelines, and marine structures.

The firm operates manufacturing units at Nashik, Howrah, Sikandrabad, and Chennai, backed by certifications in quality, environment, and safety standards. With investments planned towards capacity expansion and R&D, Shalimar is focusing on strengthening its decorative paints portfolio while retaining its industrial coatings expertise.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
Shalimar Paints aims to double down on decorative growth, supported by a broader dealer network and premium offerings. Management remains confident of sustained gross margin improvement, while operating margins are targeted to turn positive over the medium term as scale builds. With the planned capacity expansion to 185 mn litres p.a., the company is positioning itself to capture higher demand, though near-term profitability may stay constrained until scale benefits kick in.

Name: Akzo Nobel India Ltd
Overview:
Akzo Nobel India Ltd, incorporated in 1954, is engaged in the manufacturing, trading, and sale of paints, coatings, and related products, while also offering R&D services to its group companies. It operates across diverse verticals including decorative paints, automotive and specialty coatings, marine and protective coatings, industrial coatings, and construction-related solutions.

The company’s product portfolio spans decorative brands like Dulux, professional offerings for builders and institutions, and specialized industrial brands such as Interpon, Sikkens, and Levasil. With manufacturing units located across Mohali, Thane, Gwalior, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad, the company has a strong domestic presence. Recently, Akzo Nobel India entered into a transition phase following the JSW acquisition, under which the Dulux brand and associated intellectual property remain with the Indian entity, while powder coatings have been divested.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management expects a rebound growth driven by seasonal demand recovery. For FY26 overall, revenue growth is targeted in high single digits, following a weak start in Q1. Margins are expected to remain stable despite inflationary pressures from raw material duties. Integration with JSW, new product launches, dealer network expansion, and foray into construction chemicals will be key growth drivers. With Dulux’s strong brand equity and renewed channel confidence post-acquisition, the company aims to consolidate market share and improve incremental growth momentum.

Name: West Coast Paper Mills Ltd
Overview:
West Coast Paper Mills Ltd is among India’s most established and sizable producers of paper, catering to printing, writing, and packaging needs across diverse industries. Founded in 1955, the company operates from its mill in Dandeli, Karnataka, producing high-quality paper that supports the publishing, stationery, notebook, and packaging sectors through its broad product portfolio.

In its most recently completed fiscal year, the company’s performance was challenged by market pressure and rising input costs. Despite these hurdles, operations continued with a focus on cost control and sustaining production quality, while keeping an eye on strategic recovery in demand and margin stabilization.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company faces ongoing challenges due to high material costs and weak demand. A turnaround will depend on improving cost efficiencies, stabilizing raw-material pricing, and reviving offtake in core segments. While no explicit FY26 guidance has been issued, recovery in margins and incremental demand revival are critical to reestablish profitability.

Name: Pakka Ltd
Overview:
Pakka Limited, formerly known as Yash Pakka Limited, is a pioneer in the sustainable packaging industry in India. Established in 1981, the company specializes in manufacturing and trading specialty papers and molded (tableware) products. With a strong commitment to environmental sustainability, Pakka has positioned itself as a leader in providing eco-friendly packaging solutions.

The company's manufacturing facility is located in Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh, strategically situated in one of India's largest wheat-producing regions, facilitating the easy procurement of wheat straw, a primary raw material for paper production.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Pakka Limited is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing global demand for sustainable packaging solutions. The company's strategic investments, coupled with its strong product portfolio and international presence, are expected to drive revenue growth and enhance shareholder value.

Name: Emami Paper Mills Ltd
Overview:
Emami Paper Mills Ltd, established in 1981, is a prominent player in the Indian paper industry, specializing in the production of writing and printing paper, newsprint, and packaging boards. The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities, ensuring high-quality standards in its product offerings.

With a strong commitment to sustainability, Emami Paper Mills Ltd integrates eco-friendly practices in its production processes. The company has a significant presence in both domestic and international markets, catering to a diverse clientele across various sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Emami Paper Mills Ltd aims to further strengthen its market position by focusing on innovation and sustainability. The company's strategic initiatives are expected to drive continued growth and enhance shareholder value in the coming years.

Name: Tamil Nadu Newsprint & Papers Ltd
Overview:
Tamil Nadu Newsprint & Papers Ltd (TNPL) is an integrated pulp and paper manufacturing company headquartered in Chennai. The company produces newsprint, printing and writing paper, and specialty paper products for domestic and international markets.

TNPL emphasizes sustainable practices, including utilizing bagasse and other renewable raw materials for production. Its modern facilities, strong R&D, and focus on quality control allow it to serve diversified customer segments efficiently.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company strengthened its pulp and paper production capabilities while optimizing energy consumption and raw material utilization. Investments in modern machinery enhanced operational efficiency and product quality. Strategic focus on high-margin specialty paper and eco-friendly solutions improved market competitiveness and customer satisfaction.

Name: Satia Industries Ltd
Overview:
Satia Industries Limited (SIL) is a fully integrated wood and agro-based paper manufacturer that manages the end-to-end process from raw material sourcing to final paper production. Its products are widely used in printing applications, including books, directories, envelopes, diaries, calendars, computer stationery, and notebooks. The company offers a diverse product mix such as Super Snow White Paper, Map-Litho Paper, Coloured Paper, and Ledger Paper, and is now expanding into value-added products like copier paper, surface-sized Maplitho, cup stock, wedding base, and carry bag paper through its Plant Machinery 4 expansion.

The company’s revenue mix reflects its core reliance on paper manufacturing, with the paper segment contributing around 84% of revenues, while the electricity and steam segment accounts for ~15%. SIL has achieved self-sufficiency in energy needs with a 41.95 MW cogeneration power plant, ensuring lower costs and operational efficiency. This in-house energy generation capability not only supports its large-scale operations but also reduces dependence on external power supply.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Satia Industries Ltd is actively pursuing expansion and modernization initiatives to strengthen its position in the paper manufacturing industry. The company plans to enhance its production capacity by modernizing its Paper Machine 3 (PM-3), aiming to increase output from 600 to 700 tons per day, representing a 16.6% growth. Additionally, Satia Industries is implementing energy-efficient measures, including the installation of a new soda recovery boiler, to optimize energy consumption and reduce costs

Name: JK Paper Ltd
Overview:
JK Paper Limited stands as a leading name in India’s paper industry, offering a diversified product range including packaging paper, copier and office paper, writing and printing paper, and related paperboard solutions. Its fully integrated operations span from raw material sourcing and manufacturing to branding and distribution, underpinning its market leadership and operational resilience.

Facing challenges in raw material costs and import-induced pricing pressure, the company remains committed to enhancing plantation capabilities, operational efficiencies, and product diversification to sustain its competitive edge and profitability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, the company prioritizes self-sufficiency in raw materials, plantation-led cost control, and expansion in packaging conversion capabilities. These strategic levers, coupled with improving demand patterns and operational execution, should aid margin recovery and support sustainable growth in FY 26.

Name: Seshasayee Paper & Boards Ltd
Overview:
Seshasayee Paper & Boards Ltd (SPB) is a leading manufacturer of printing and writing paper in India. Established in 1960, the company operates an integrated pulp, paper, and paperboard mill at Pallipalayam, Erode, Tamil Nadu. SPB is part of the SPB-ESVIN Group and has a strong presence in the Indian paper industry.

The company's product portfolio includes a range of paper products under brands such as Sprint, SPD, Color Sprint, and Success. SPB's commitment to quality and sustainability is reflected in its manufacturing processes, which adhere to stringent environmental standards. The company serves a diverse clientele, including educational institutions, publishers, and commercial establishments.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Seshasayee Paper & Boards Ltd plans to expand its product portfolio by introducing new paper grades and enhancing existing offerings. The company is also focusing on increasing its production capacity to meet growing market demand. Additionally, SPB aims to strengthen its sustainability initiatives by adopting eco-friendly practices in its operations.

Name: Orient Paper & Industries Ltd
Overview:
Orient Paper & Industries Ltd, founded in the mid-20th century, is one of India’s established manufacturers of writing, printing, and specialty papers. It has long been recognized for its commitment to responsible and high-quality paper production, embracing sustainable practices such as shifting entirely to renewable energy and pioneering digital transformation across its operations.

The company focuses on innovation in its product offerings, including virgin tissue papers and solutions aimed at plastic substitution. It combines technology-driven manufacturing—with AI-enabled, closed-loop production—and an emphasis on environmental stewardship like water positivity and carbon-negative ambitions, reflecting a harmony between industrial leadership and sustainability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Orient Paper is well-positioned to benefit from growing demand for sustainable paper products. With its strong Q1 FY26 performance and green manufacturing focus, the company is poised to build on its momentum and potentially deliver continued financial improvement and market leadership in sustainability-aligned paper segments.

Name: N R Agarwal Industries Ltd
Overview:
N R Agarwal Industries Ltd, incorporated in 1993 and based in Mumbai, specializes in paper and packaging solutions with an emphasis on recycled paper-based products. The company manufactures duplex boards, newsprint, writing & printing paper, and kraft paper, largely from recovered paper as raw material, demonstrating its commitment to sustainability. Its products serve the FMCG, publishing, education, and packaging industries across India and select overseas markets.

With a strong recycling-led business model, the company contributes to reducing carbon footprint and conserving natural resources. Its integrated operations, modernized manufacturing facilities, and strategic focus on cost efficiency allow N R Agarwal to maintain competitiveness in India’s fast-growing paper and packaging sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

N. R. Agarwal Industries Ltd (NRAIL) is undergoing a significant transformation to solidify its position as a leading manufacturer of recycled paper and duplex boards in India. The company is investing ₹1,200 crore to establish a new Multilayer Board plant (Unit VI) with a capacity of 1,000 tonnes per day (TPD), aiming to enhance production efficiency and meet growing market demand.

Name: Andhra Paper Ltd
Overview:
Andhra Paper Ltd is one of India's leading integrated paper and pulp manufacturers, with a rich legacy in the paper industry. The company operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Kadiyam, East Godavari, Andhra Pradesh, producing a wide range of paper products. Known for its commitment to quality and sustainability, Andhra Paper has consistently been at the forefront of innovation in the paper industry.

In recent years, the company has undertaken significant capital investments to expand its production capacity. Notably, Andhra Paper approved the installation of a cutting-edge tissue paper machine with a daily production capacity of 100 tonnes. This strategic move aims to cater to the growing demand for tissue products in both domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company is focusing on expanding its product range and enhancing production capacity to drive growth. The installation of the new tissue paper machine is expected to contribute significantly to revenue generation. Additionally, Andhra Paper is committed to sustainable practices, which will bolster its position in the market.

Name: Kuantum Papers Ltd
Overview:
Kuantum Papers Ltd, incorporated in 1980, is a leading agro-based paper manufacturer in India, producing a diverse range of wood-free writing, printing, and specialty paper. Its portfolio spans products such as maplitho, creamwove, copier papers, thermal paper, parchment paper, and innovative eco-friendly solutions like base paper for cups and straws. The company follows an order-based production model, ensuring low inventories and efficient working capital management.

The company operates a fully integrated manufacturing facility in Hoshiarpur, Punjab, spread across 259 acres, with a chemical recovery plant, a 38 MW co-gen power plant, and effluent treatment facilities. It has a total production capacity of 450 TPD, with access to raw materials like agro residues and locally procured wood, supported by a social farm forestry program and strong sustainability initiatives including EUDR compliance.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
Kuantum Papers is positioned for medium-term growth with its capacity scale-up to 2,50,000 MTPA by FY26, a strategic shift toward specialty and coated papers, and strengthened cost efficiency from modernization. Management expects EBITDA margins to return to 22%+ levels post-expansion, with volumes and realizations improving from FY26 onwards. While near-term risks from imports and raw material prices persist, the company’s sustainability credentials, niche product focus, and digital transformation provide a strong foundation for future profitability.

Name: Agarwal Industrial Corporation Ltd
Overview: Agarwal Industrial Corporation Ltd (AICL) is a prominent Indian company specializing in the manufacturing and trading of petrochemicals, particularly bitumen and bituminous products. Established in 1984, AICL has expanded its operations from transportation services to encompass manufacturing, logistics, liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) distribution, and power generation through windmills. The company's integrated approach positions it as a significant player in the infrastructure and energy sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
AICL plans to diversify its portfolio by entering polymer-modified bitumen, emulsions, and eco-friendly road construction materials, aiming to capture emerging green infrastructure demand. On the energy side, it intends to scale its renewable capacity from 20 MW to 50 MW by FY27, further strengthening its sustainability profile. Additionally, the company targets international revenue share to rise to 15% within 3 years, tapping new markets in Asia and Africa.

Name: Panama Petrochem Ltd
Overview:
Panama Petrochem Ltd is an Indian chemical manufacturing company specializing in petrochemical products, polymers, and specialty chemicals. Headquartered in Ahmedabad, the company supplies products to industries including packaging, construction, and industrial applications.

Panama Petrochem emphasizes research and development, quality assurance, and sustainable manufacturing. Its modern facilities and extensive distribution network enable it to serve both domestic and international markets efficiently.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Panama Petrochem plans to expand production capacity, diversify chemical offerings, and explore export markets. Sustainable manufacturing, technological upgrades, and innovation will support long-term growth. Strategic partnerships and market expansion remain key priorities.

Name: Styrenix Performance Materials Ltd
Overview:
Styrenix Performance Materials Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer of engineering thermoplastics, including ABS (Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene), SAN (Styrene Acrylonitrile), and PS (Polystyrene). Established in 2016, the company operates manufacturing facilities in Gujarat and a research and development center in Mumbai.

The company caters to diverse industries such as automotive, electronics, consumer goods, and packaging. Styrenix focuses on delivering high-quality materials, leveraging advanced manufacturing technologies and a skilled workforce, with an emphasis on innovation and sustainability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Styrenix aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company focuses on innovation and sustainability to meet the evolving demands of the engineering thermoplastics industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, Styrenix is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Supreme Petrochem Ltd
Overview:
Supreme Petrochem Ltd (SPL) is a market leader in India’s Polystyrene (PS) and Expanded Polystyrene (EPS) industry with a 50% domestic market share and is the sole domestic producer of XPS insulation boards. The company manufactures a wide range of styrenics and allied products, including PS, EPS, masterbatches, compounds of styrenics, and XPS boards, catering to diverse industries like packaging, construction, and consumer goods.

SPL operates two manufacturing facilities at Amdoshi, Maharashtra, and Chennai, Tamil Nadu, with a combined installed capacity of over 4.48 lakh MT for polymers and 72,000 CBM for XPS boards. With an extensive domestic footprint and exports to 100+ countries, the company continues to strengthen its leadership through capacity expansion, renewable energy integration, and high-value product development.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
SPL targets higher sales volumes driven by ABS plant commissioning, expanded EPS and masterbatch capacities, and rising demand from packaging and construction sectors. Revenue growth will be supported by diversification into high-margin ABS and insulation products, with margins expected to improve on better product mix and operational efficiencies.

Name: Dhunseri Ventures Ltd
Overview:
Dhunseri Ventures Ltd, established in 1916, is a diversified Indian company engaged in treasury operations and trading activities. The company has a rich history and has evolved to focus on financial investments and trading as its core business operations. Dhunseri Ventures Ltd operates as a holding company with investments in various sectors, including plastics, tea, and packaging.

The company aims to generate value for its shareholders through strategic investments and efficient management of its portfolio. In recent years, Dhunseri Ventures Ltd has been focusing on optimizing its operations and enhancing shareholder value. The company has undertaken initiatives to streamline its business activities and improve financial performance.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Dhunseri Ventures Ltd aims to achieve a revenue of ₹1,400 crore by FY27, driven by the completion of its brownfield expansion at Panagarh, West Bengal. The company is also progressing on a ₹1,240 crore greenfield packaging film project in Jammu's Kathua district, with expectations to reach ₹2,000 crore in revenue by FY28

Name: Bhansali Engineering Polymers Ltd
Overview:
Bhansali Engineering Polymers Ltd specializes in manufacturing and selling a range of engineering plastics—such as ABS, AES, ASA, SAN resins, and their alloys. These materials are essential inputs for industries like automotive, home appliances, electronics, healthcare, and kitchenware, helping the company serve a broad set of high-demand markets.

Through its product offerings, the company acts as a key supplier in India’s value chain for modern consumer and industrial goods. Its focus on versatile resin products positions it well to support innovation and quality across multiple manufacturing sectors.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bhansali Engineering Polymers Ltd (BEPL) is significantly expanding its production capacities to meet the growing demand for high-quality engineering thermoplastics. The company plans to increase its ABS (Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene) production capacity from 75,000 tonnes per annum (TPA) to 200,000 TPA across its facilities in Abu Road (Rajasthan) and Satnoor (Madhya Pradesh).

Name: Kothari Petrochemicals Ltd
Overview:
Kothari Petrochemicals Ltd, part of the HC Kothari Group, is India’s largest manufacturer of polyisobutylene (PIB), a versatile polymer used in lubricants, adhesives, sealants, and fuel additives. Its manufacturing plant in Tamil Nadu serves both domestic and international clients.

As a key supplier to global chemical companies, Kothari Petrochemicals maintains a strong export presence. Its focus on consistent product quality, cost efficiency, and niche specialization gives it a competitive edge in the petrochemicals sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
With PIB demand in India expected to grow at 7–8% CAGR, Kothari Petrochemicals is well-positioned to benefit from both domestic substitution and export markets. FY26 topline is projected to expand by 8–10%, driven by higher-value PIB grades and steady end-user consumption in FMCG and automotive sectors. Margins are expected to stabilize as raw material sourcing efficiencies improve.

Name: Manali Petrochemicals Ltd
Overview:
Manali Petrochemicals Ltd (MPL), established in 1986, is a leading petrochemical company in India. The company specializes in manufacturing and supplying innovative polyurethane raw materials and blended systems for various industries.

MPL's product portfolio includes propylene oxide, propylene glycol, and polyols, which find applications in appliances, automotive, furniture, footwear, paints, coatings, pharmaceuticals, and food & fragrances.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Manali Petrochemicals Ltd aims to expand its product offerings and enhance manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand in domestic and international markets. The company is also focusing on sustainability and technological advancements to stay ahead in the industry.

Name: Savita Oil Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Savita Oil Technologies Limited is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in high-quality petroleum-based specialty products. Established in 1961, the company has built a strong reputation for producing transformer oils, white oils, and other specialty lubricants that serve a wide array of industrial applications. Savita Oil Technologies operates with a commitment to innovation and quality, catering to both domestic and international markets.

The company has a robust manufacturing infrastructure and a dedicated research and development division, ensuring the continuous enhancement of product offerings. With a focus on sustainability and customer satisfaction, Savita Oil Technologies aims to maintain its leadership position in the specialty petroleum products sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Savita Oil Technologies is poised for continued growth, driven by its strong product portfolio and commitment to quality. The company aims to expand its market presence and enhance operational efficiencies to sustain its upward trajectory. With a focus on innovation and customer satisfaction, Savita Oil Technologies is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the specialty petroleum products sector.

Name: Akums Drugs & Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
Akums Drugs and Pharmaceuticals Limited is a contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) specializing in a broad spectrum of pharmaceutical dosage forms including tablets, injectables, liquids, and specialty formulations. Since its listing in FY25, Akums has emphasized long-term value creation while operating with a cost-plus business model, passing API and packaging costs largely to customers.

The company’s diversified portfolio spans domestic branded formulations, international branded generics, APIs, and trade generics. Akums is expanding manufacturing capacities and investing heavily in R&D to enhance its product offerings and enter regulated global markets, while securing large-scale global contracts that will underpin future revenue streams.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Akums expects high single-digit volume growth in CDMO for FY26, with revenue growth contingent on API price stabilization. The API business is forecast to halve losses but profitability is unlikely before FY27. Domestic formulations aim for double-digit growth, outpacing market trends. Capex and R&D investments support capacity ramp-up and product innovation, positioning Akums for accelerated growth in regulated global markets and sustained shareholder value creation.

Name: Supriya Lifescience Ltd
Overview:
Supriya Lifescience Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), with a strong presence in the global pharmaceutical market. Established in 1984, the company offers a diverse portfolio of products, including APIs for analgesics, anaesthetics, anti-asthmatics, and anti-hypertensives.

The company operates primarily in regulated markets, with a significant export business constituting a substantial portion of its revenue. Supriya Lifescience Ltd has been expanding its client base and product offerings, aiming to strengthen its position as a leader in the global pharmaceutical sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Supriya Lifescience Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its presence in regulated markets and enhancing its product portfolio. The company is focusing on research and development to introduce new APIs and strengthen its position in the global pharmaceutical industry. With a commitment to quality and innovation, Supriya Lifescience Ltd is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the pharmaceutical sector.

Name: Shilpa Medicare Ltd
Overview:
Shilpa Medicare Limited, established in 1987, is a prominent Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in the development and manufacturing of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), formulations, Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) services, and biologics. The company operates a diversified portfolio, focusing on oncology, non-oncology, and complex generics, catering to both domestic and international markets.

With a robust pipeline of New Chemical Entities (NCEs) and biosimilars, Shilpa Medicare is actively expanding its global footprint. The company's strategic initiatives include enhancing its presence in regulated markets, advancing its biologics segment, and leveraging its CDMO capabilities to serve global biotech clients. These efforts are aimed at driving sustainable growth and establishing Shilpa Medicare as a key player in the global pharmaceutical industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Shilpa Medicare aims to capitalize on its robust product pipeline and expanding global presence. The company plans to enhance its CDMO services, focusing on complex APIs and biologics, to meet the growing demands of international clients. With ongoing investments in R&D and strategic partnerships, Shilpa Medicare is poised for sustained growth and increased market share in the global pharmaceutical industry.

Name: Windlas Biotech Ltd
Overview:
Windlas Biotech Ltd is a prominent Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in contract development and manufacturing (CDMO) services. The company offers a comprehensive range of services, including product discovery, development, and commercial manufacturing of generic and complex generic pharmaceutical products.

Windlas Biotech operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Dehradun, Uttarakhand, adhering to Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) standards. In addition to its CDMO services, Windlas Biotech markets its own branded generic products in the domestic market and exports to various international markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Windlas Biotech aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing its manufacturing capabilities. The company plans to leverage its strong R&D and regulatory expertise to develop and manufacture complex generic products, catering to both domestic and international markets.

Name: Granules India Ltd
Overview:
Granules India Ltd operates across three key pharmaceutical segments: Finished Dosages, Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (API), and Pharmaceutical Formulation Intermediates (PFI). The company has a strong presence in the US market, which accounts for the majority of its revenue, and is focused on expanding its product portfolio in CNS, ADHD, and oncology. Recent capacity additions at the Genome Valley facility and acquisition of Senn Chemicals mark its strategic entry into peptide therapeutics, positioning it for long-term growth in advanced pharma segments.

Despite near-term challenges related to the Gagillapur facility remediation, Granules continues to drive growth through new product launches, geographic diversification, and vertical integration. Its increasing emphasis on higher-margin formulations and R&D productivity underpins a shift away from commoditized APIs. The company is also strengthening its European footprint and expects significant ramp-up in capacity utilization in coming years.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects remediation-related expenses to taper off by mid-FY26 and anticipates outperforming FY25 performance despite near-term headwinds. Ramp-up at Genome Valley and integration of peptide capabilities are key growth levers. The strategic focus remains on high-margin formulations, innovative therapies, and geographic diversification to sustain long-term growth.

Name: Laurus Labs Ltd
Overview:
Founded in 2005, Laurus Labs is a research-driven pharmaceutical and biotechnology company with global leadership in select Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) such as anti-retroviral, oncology, cardiovascular, and gastro therapeutics. The company also offers integrated Contract Development and Manufacturing Organisation (CDMO) services to global innovators, covering the entire value chain from clinical phase drug development to commercial manufacturing.

With more than 6,500 employees, including over 1,000 scientists, Laurus operates across 11+ facilities approved by leading global regulatory agencies. Its diversified portfolio spans APIs, formulations, biotechnology, and large molecule capabilities, positioning it strongly to serve both generic and innovator markets worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to accelerate the shift towards CDMO, potentially making it the largest revenue contributor in the medium term. Margins are expected to remain strong as the business mix tilts towards high-value projects. With robust project visibility, capacity additions, and strategic partnerships, Laurus is confident of delivering healthy growth and profitability in FY26, while using generics and ARVs as a stable foundation for funding innovation-led expansion.

Name: Zota Health Care Ltd
Overview:
Zota Health Care Ltd is a pharmaceutical company specializing in the manufacture and marketing of branded generics and therapeutic formulations. Established in the late 1990s, it serves the domestic market with a focus on chronic and lifestyle disease management.

The company operates modern manufacturing facilities and emphasizes research-driven product development. Zota Health Care Ltd caters to multiple therapeutic segments, including cardiology, gastroenterology, and dermatology, positioning itself as a trusted healthcare partner.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Zota Health Care Ltd is actively expanding its footprint in both domestic and international markets. The company has significantly increased its retail presence, with the Davaindia pharmacy chain growing to 1,530 operational stores as of March 2025. This expansion is complemented by the launch of a B2C online portal and mobile app, aiming to enhance accessibility and customer engagement

Name: SMS Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
SMS Pharmaceuticals Ltd is a diversified and integrated pharmaceutical company based in India, specializing in the manufacturing of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) and intermediates. Established in 1990, the company has developed a strong presence in the global pharmaceutical market, catering to both generic and innovator firms. 

SMS Pharmaceuticals operates several state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in and around Hyderabad, India, which are compliant with international standards such as USFDA, EUGMP, TGA, and Japan PMDA .
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
SMS Pharmaceuticals aims to achieve a revenue growth of 20% in FY26, supported by its diversified product portfolio and expanding market reach. The company plans to enhance its manufacturing capabilities and invest in research and development to introduce new products and improve existing ones.

Name: Ajanta Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Ajanta Pharma is a specialty pharmaceutical company primarily engaged in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of branded generic formulations. It operates across multiple geographies, including India, Asia, Africa, and the US, with a strong presence in chronic therapies such as cardiology, ophthalmology, dermatology, and pain management. Around 74% of its revenue in FY25 came from the branded generics segment, highlighting the company's clear strategic focus on specialized and high-margin markets.

The company operates in over 30 countries and maintains a strong leadership position in many sub-therapeutic categories. It has developed a reputation for being first-to-market, with nearly 50% of its 500+ products in India falling under this category. Ajanta has seven state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and a robust R&D engine with over 850 scientists supporting its product pipeline.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ajanta Pharma is poised for sustained growth, driven by strategic investments in capacity expansion and a focus on high-demand therapeutic areas. The company plans to establish a new liquid formulation facility at its Pithampur site in Madhya Pradesh to meet increasing global demand. Ajanta Pharma's India business is projected to grow at a 12% compound annual growth rate (CAGR) over the next four years, fueled by new product launches and strong volumes

Name: ERIS Lifesciences Ltd
Overview:
Eris Lifesciences Ltd is a leading player in the Indian branded formulations market, specializing in high-margin chronic therapies such as diabetes, cardiovascular, and neurology. Over the years, it has strengthened its position through organic growth, strategic acquisitions, and a strong domestic presence, making it the youngest among the top 20 companies in the Indian pharmaceutical market.

The company’s portfolio is further enhanced by its strong foothold in the insulin and GLP-1 space, supported by robust in-house manufacturing capabilities. With focused investments in speciality segments, integration of acquired assets, and expansion into newer therapeutic areas, Eris is well-positioned for sustained growth in the domestic and emerging export markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
