Management aims to deliver 15–21% revenue growth in FY26 with an EBITDA margin of 36%, supported by insulin market share gains, high-margin new launches, and expansion into VMN, IVF, and critical care injectables. The focus will remain on margin accretive products, operational efficiency, and disciplined debt reduction, with a clear ambition to strengthen leadership in the diabetes segment and capture emerging GLP-1 opportunities.

Name: Ipca Laboratories Ltd
Overview:
Ipca Laboratories Ltd is a fully integrated Indian pharmaceutical company engaged in the manufacturing and marketing of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) and formulations. With a global presence in over 100 countries, the company offers more than 350 formulations and 80 APIs across key therapeutic areas including pain, cardiovascular, anti-diabetic, anti-malarial, and dermatology. It is one of India’s top 20 pharmaceutical companies.

The company operates 18 manufacturing units across India, including facilities for formulations and APIs. It maintains strong backward integration for API production, supporting its formulation business with cost competitiveness and supply reliability. Ipca continues to invest in capacity expansions and R&D to support its global and domestic growth strategy.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ipca Laboratories is strategically enhancing its global presence by expanding its international footprint, particularly in North America and Europe. The company has filed 44 Abbreviated New Drug Applications (ANDAs), with 18 approvals, and is exploring contract development and manufacturing opportunities. Additionally, Ipca is focusing on backward integration by producing active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) in-house, which supports its formulation business and ensures quality control.

Name: Bliss GVS Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Bliss GVS Pharma Limited is an Indian pharmaceutical company engaged in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of a wide range of pharmaceutical and healthcare products. Its portfolio includes prescription drugs, over-the-counter medications, generic medicines, and wellness products, with a focus on delivering affordable and high-quality healthcare solutions globally.

The company emphasizes research and development to continuously introduce innovative and effective products while adhering to stringent quality standards and regulatory requirements. With a strong international presence, Bliss GVS Pharma is committed to improving healthcare outcomes and enhancing the well-being of individuals and communities worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bliss GVS Pharma Ltd is strategically enhancing its global footprint and operational efficiency to capitalize on the growing demand for pharmaceutical formulations. Internationally, Bliss GVS Pharma is expanding its market presence by focusing on regulated markets in Europe, South America, Asia-Pacific, Russia, and CIS countries

Name: Emcure Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
Emcure Pharmaceuticals Limited is a major Indian pharmaceutical company, ranked 13th in domestic sales and the market leader in gynecology and HIV antiviral segments as of FY24. With operations in over 70 countries, Emcure focuses on branded generics, generics, and APIs, supported by strong manufacturing and R&D capabilities.

The company has:
13 manufacturing facilities in India for formulations and complex APIs.
A strong R&D base with 548 scientists across five research centers.
Filed 1,800+ global dossiers and holds 220 patents, reinforcing its innovation-driven approach.

Business Segments and Revenue Contribution
Product Mix
Branded Generics – 53%
Generics – 40%
Others (assumed to be APIs and services) – 7%

Therapeutic Area Revenue Contribution
Gynecology – 24.09%
Cardiovascular – 15.7%
Anti-Infectives – 10.97%

Geographical Revenue Contribution
India (Domestic) – 48.28%
Europe – 21.38%
North America – 13.94%
Other International Markets – Remaining (approx. 16.4%)
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Emcure Pharmaceuticals is focusing on expanding its presence in high-growth therapeutic areas such as gynecology, dermatology, and women's health. The company plans to deepen its portfolio in these segments through targeted product launches and strategic partnerships. Additionally, Emcure is enhancing its domestic and international market reach by strengthening existing collaborations and exploring new distribution agreements.

Name: Natco Pharma Ltd
Overview:
NATCO Pharma Limited is a vertically integrated, R&D-focused pharmaceutical company engaged in developing, manufacturing, and marketing complex products across niche therapeutic areas. Its operations span three major business segments — Finished Dosage Formulations (FDF), Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), and Contract Manufacturing — complemented by a growing presence in Crop Health Sciences. With a global footprint across 50+ countries, NATCO serves regulated and emerging markets, leveraging strategic partnerships with major international players and a robust portfolio of Para IV and first-to-file products.

The company operates eight manufacturing facilities, two R&D centres, and maintains strong capabilities in oncology, cardiology, diabetology, CNS, and specialty therapies. In FY25, NATCO took a significant step toward expanding its presence in emerging markets by acquiring a 35.75% stake in Adcock Ingram, South Africa’s second-largest pharmaceutical company. This acquisition offers a platform for pan-Africa expansion, product synergies, and operational efficiencies. The company also continues to invest in new therapeutic segments, advanced drug delivery systems, and international market penetration.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
NATCO plans to leverage the Adcock Ingram partnership for pan-Africa market access and to expand its international footprint in high-growth emerging markets. It aims to deepen its specialty pharma presence, accelerate Para IV launches in the U.S., and scale its crop health business to profitability by FY26, supported by continuous capacity and R&D investments.

Name: Pfizer Ltd
Overview:
Pfizer Limited, the Indian arm of global biopharma major Pfizer Inc., is engaged in the manufacturing, marketing, trading, and export of pharmaceutical products. It operates through a combination of its own facility and contract manufacturing partners, with product sales driven primarily through independent distributors across India. The company ranks as the 3rd largest multinational pharmaceutical company in India and maintains a strong presence in over 15 therapeutic areas.

The product portfolio spans across key divisions such as Vaccines, Internal Medicine, Hospital Business, and Inflammation & Immunology. Flagship products include Prevenar 13, Corex Dx, Magnex, Enbrel, and Gelusil. Pfizer’s strong distribution network services over 300 million patients annually through a base of over 2,50,000 pharmacies and 2,00,000 healthcare professionals. Its Goa-based manufacturing facility, supplemented by 18 third-party CMOs, forms the backbone of its local production, with around 70% of the portfolio manufactured in India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Pfizer Ltd is intensifying its commitment to India's healthcare sector by expanding its manufacturing and research capabilities. The company is transferring production of oncology and chemotherapy products from Australia to Gujarat, aiming to bolster local manufacturing. Additionally, Pfizer is enhancing its research and development presence, with approximately 1,500 employees in Chennai and a research center at IIT Madras

Name: Marksans Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Marksans Pharma Ltd is a prominent Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of generic pharmaceuticals and over-the-counter (OTC) products. The company has a strong presence in the U.S., U.K., Australia, and other international markets, with a diverse product portfolio catering to various therapeutic segments.

With a robust pipeline of over 100 products and a focus on research and development, Marksans Pharma continues to expand its market share and enhance its capabilities to meet the growing demand for affordable and high-quality healthcare solutions globally.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Marksans Pharma plans to expand its OTC portfolio, invest in R&D for new products, and strengthen global market presence. The company aims for sustainable growth through geographical diversification and operational excellence.

Name: J B Chemicals & Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
J.B. Chemicals and Pharmaceuticals Ltd (JB Pharma), established in 1976, is a leading Indian pharmaceutical company with a diversified business model across domestic formulations, export formulations, contract manufacturing, and APIs. The company has a presence in over 50 countries, with India, Russia, and South Africa as its core markets. JB Pharma is known for its high-quality products and is one of the top 5 global manufacturers of medicated and herbal lozenges.

The company has built a strong portfolio in the chronic and acute therapy segments and has expanded into ophthalmology. With 8 manufacturing facilities and a solid CDMO (Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization) business, JB Pharma also boasts six brands in the top 300 in India. The company has demonstrated consistent growth across financial metrics with a sharp focus on profitable expansion and operational efficiency.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

J.B. Chemicals & Pharmaceuticals Ltd (JBCPL) is poised for accelerated growth, driven by strategic investments and a robust product portfolio. The company plans to invest over ₹1,500 crore to expand its presence in India and enhance its contract development and manufacturing (CDMO) business.

Name: Gufic BioSciences Ltd
Overview:
Gufic BioSciences Ltd is an Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), generic formulations, and lyophilized injectables. With its roots tracing back to 1970 and public incorporation in 1984, the company operates from Mumbai and serves both domestic and international healthcare markets.

Combining R&D-driven innovation with manufacturing scale, Gufic’s offerings span injectables, herbal remedies, and niche healthcare segments—supported by recent capacity additions like the Indore lyophilized injectable facility and evolving CDMO capabilities.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Gufic is poised for expansion as the new injectable capacity ramps up and begins contributing to volume and margin gains. Its asset-light CDMO model, specialized product segments, and global compliances offer structural support. Despite short-term margin pressure, the trajectory suggests strengthening performance in H2 FY26 and beyond.

Name: Glaxosmithkline Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
GlaxoSmithKline Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (GSK India) is a subsidiary of the global healthcare company GSK and operates in the Indian market through pharmaceuticals and vaccines. Its portfolio spans general medicines (GenMed), specialty segments such as respiratory and oncology, and vaccines targeting pediatric and adult populations. The company is focused on innovation-driven growth with strategic launches and operational excellence.

GSK India's long-term ambition includes growing leadership in its core categories, launching oncology therapies, and expanding adult immunization. Its operational transformation emphasizes agility, digital innovation, and productivity, with improved profitability and strong cash generation.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

GlaxoSmithKline Pharmaceuticals Ltd (GSK India) is strategically enhancing its portfolio by focusing on high-demand therapeutic areas such as anti-infectives, pain management, dermatology, and oncology. The company is committed to expanding access to its medicines and vaccines, aiming to strengthen its leadership position in these key segments. GSK India is also accelerating its digital transformation to improve operational efficiency and customer engagement. These initiatives are part of GSK's broader strategy to contribute significantly to India's evolving pharmaceutical landscape and to ensure broader access to innovative healthcare solutions.

Name: Wockhardt Ltd
Overview:
Wockhardt Ltd is a global research-driven pharmaceutical and biotechnology organization engaged in manufacturing finished dosage formulations, injectables, biopharmaceuticals, oral formulations, and topicals. The company maintains strong positions in key international markets, being among the top three Indian generic companies in the UK and a leader in select therapeutic segments in India, such as Methycobalamin.

Its strategic focus has shifted from traditional generics to innovation-led segments, notably novel antibiotics and biotechnology, with research-led businesses contributing a growing share of revenues. With a global presence across the UK, Ireland, emerging markets, and India, Wockhardt generates a majority of its revenues internationally. Manufacturing capabilities span 12 global facilities, complemented by robust R&D infrastructure in India and the UK.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management aims to double revenues within three years, driven by the global commercialization of ZAYNICH, domestic and international expansion of Miqnaf, insulin capacity expansion, and biotech collaborations. Strong cash reserves, minimal debt, and manufacturing readiness underpin the plan. Regulatory milestones and product launches are expected to sustain EBITDA improvement and generate strong free cash flows, positioning Wockhardt as a differentiated innovation-led pharma player in the global market.

Name: RPG Life Sciences Ltd
Overview:
RPG Life Sciences Ltd is an Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in the manufacturing and marketing of finished dosage forms (formulations) and active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). Established in 2007, the company operates under the umbrella of RPG Enterprises and has a significant presence in both domestic and international markets.

The company's operations are supported by state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities that adhere to international quality standards. RPG Life Sciences is committed to innovation and sustainability, focusing on expanding its product offerings and enhancing its global footprint.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
RPG Life Sciences plans to expand its product portfolio by focusing on high-growth therapeutic areas such as immunosuppressants and monoclonal antibodies. The company aims to enhance its international presence by entering new geographies and customer segments.

Name: Orchid Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Orchid Pharma is an Indian pharmaceutical company focused on developing and manufacturing active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) and finished dosage formulations, with a strong emphasis on research and innovation. Its product portfolio spans oral and injectable cephalosporins and extends into newer therapeutic areas through its AMS (Anti-Microbial Stewardship) division and novel chemical entities.

In FY 25, the company navigated pricing pressures and one-time expenses, while maintaining operational resilience and advancing strategic projects like the 7ACA fermentation facility. As it transitions into FY 26, Orchid Pharma is integrating its merger with Dhanuka Laboratories and advancing key projects to bolster its growth trajectory.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Orchid Pharma is positioned for moderate recovery, anchored by strategic execution of the Dhanuka merger, pipeline progress, and project advancements. Although pricing and the AMS division continue to pose near-term headwinds, the company's diversified product base, scale benefits, and innovation-led investments provide a cautiously positive outlook into FY 26 and beyond.

Name: Indoco Remedies Ltd
Overview:
Indoco Remedies Ltd is a Mumbai-based pharmaceutical company engaged in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of a wide range of pharmaceutical products. Established in 1947, the company offers formulations and active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) across various therapeutic segments. Indoco Remedies has a strong presence in both domestic and international markets, with a focus on quality and compliance.

The company's product portfolio includes oral solid dosages, injectables, and topical formulations. Indoco Remedies has a robust pipeline of products under development and continues to invest in research and development to expand its offerings. The company is committed to maintaining high standards of manufacturing and quality assurance to meet the evolving needs of the healthcare industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Indoco Remedies aims to strengthen its position in the global pharmaceutical market through portfolio expansion and enhanced manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on compliance with international standards and increasing its presence in emerging markets. Indoco Remedies is committed to sustainable growth and improved profitability in the coming years.

Name: Alembic Ltd
Overview:
Alembic Limited, established in 1907, is the flagship company of the Alembic Group, headquartered in Vadodara, India. The company operates in diverse sectors, including pharmaceuticals, real estate, and power assets. In 2010, Alembic Limited demerged its core pharmaceutical formulations business into a wholly-owned subsidiary, Alembic Pharmaceuticals Ltd (APL), which became the group's flagship entity thereafter. Currently, Alembic Limited holds a 28.41% stake in Alembic Pharmaceuticals and a 19.01% stake in Paushak Limited, another group entity.

While Alembic Limited's primary focus has shifted to its investments in group companies, it continues to play a strategic role in the growth and development of its subsidiaries.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Alembic Limited's future growth is closely tied to the performance of its group companies. With Alembic Pharmaceuticals focusing on expanding its product portfolio and market presence, particularly in the U.S. and India, Alembic Limited stands to benefit from these developments.

Name: FDC Ltd
Overview:
FDC Ltd is a leading Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of branded formulations, active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), and over-the-counter (OTC) products. Established in 1936 and headquartered in Mumbai, FDC has a strong presence in both domestic and international markets.

The company offers a diverse portfolio of products across various therapeutic segments, including anti-infectives, ophthalmics, gastrointestinal, and nutraceuticals. FDC is committed to providing high-quality, affordable healthcare solutions to meet the needs of patients worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company plans to expand its international footprint, enhance its product portfolio, and strengthen R&D for innovation. Operational efficiencies and strategic launches are expected to improve margins and support sustainable revenue growth in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Mankind Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Mankind Pharma Ltd is India’s fourth-largest pharmaceutical company by domestic sales and third-largest by sales volume (as of Dec 2022). The company develops, manufactures, and markets a wide range of formulations for acute and chronic therapies and a growing portfolio of consumer healthcare products.
 
 Business Segments
Therapeutic Areas: Anti-infectives, Cardiovascular, Gastrointestinal, Anti-diabetic, Neuro/CNS,Vitamins, minerals & nutrients, Respiratory
 
Consumer Healthcare Products: Fast-growing OTC and wellness brands.
 
Manufacturing Network: 25 modern facilities in HP, Sikkim, Rajasthan, AP, Maharashtra, Uttarakhand.
 
 Total installed capacity: 42.05 billion units per annum.
 
 Revenue & Strategy Summary
 Broad portfolio: 10 brands with sales > Rs 200 Cr, 19 brands > Rs 100 Cr, 36 brands > Rs 50 Cr.
 
 Strong domestic reach: 47% of sales from Tier II-IV cities and rural markets.
 
 Extensive sales force: 11,691 medical reps and 3,561 managers.
 
 Distribution: 12,000 stockists and 75 C&F agents ensure deep market penetration.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Mankind Pharma aims to drive volume-led growth by strengthening its presence in chronic therapy segments and metro markets, while developing its consumer healthcare portfolio to diversify revenue. Supported by private equity backing, the company is also investing in digital platforms to enhance engagement with healthcare professionals and deepen market penetration.

Name: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd
Overview:
Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd is India’s largest pharma company and a leading global player, manufacturing, developing, and marketing branded generics, generics, APIs, and specialty pharmaceuticals. It has a strong portfolio spanning chronic and acute segments, with advanced capabilities in complex, technology-intensive products.
 
 Revenue Mix & Key Segments
 Indian Branded Generics: 31% — top-ranked in India’s chronic therapies.
 
 US Business: 30% — largest Indian pharma player in the US generics market.
 
 Emerging Markets: 18% — strong growth in CIS, Latin America, Asia.
 
 Rest of World (RoW): 15% — Europe, Japan, Australia.
 
 APIs: 6% — backward integration ensures supply stability for key formulations.
 
 Global Footprint
 Manufacturing: 44 facilities worldwide (30 formulation sites + 14 API sites).
 
 R&D: Labs in India, Israel, USA, Canada — covering generics, complex dosage, biologics, and new drug development.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Sun Pharma aims to strengthen its domestic presence through a 10% increase in field force and deeper geographic penetration across India. The specialty and innovator medicine segment, particularly in dermatology, oncology, and ophthalmology, is expected to outpace global pharma growth, supported by new product launches and strategic filings in the US and EU. Expansion into emerging markets and the rest of the world targets high-burden therapeutic areas and complex generics.

Name: Zydus Lifesciences Ltd
Overview:
Zydus Lifesciences is a major Indian pharmaceutical player with a strong presence across pharmaceuticals and consumer wellness products. Its wide-ranging global footprint, specialized therapeutic focus, robust generics pipeline, and leadership in consumer wellness make it a diversified and innovation-driven company.
 
 Business Segments
 US Generics: Large portfolio with strong prescription volumes; key contributor to revenue.
 
 India Pharmaceuticals: Includes branded formulations with leadership in Nephrology and Oncology.
 
 Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs): Diverse APIs portfolio across therapeutic categories.
 
 Consumer Products: Operated through Zydus Wellness Ltd. — strong brands in sugar-free and wellness segments.
 
 International Business: Presence in Sri Lanka (market leader) and growing presence in Rest of World markets.
 
 Research & Development: Seven dedicated R&D centers focusing on novel biologics, biosimilars, and complex generics.
 
 Revenue & Metrics :
 Zydus Lifesciences generates significant revenue from its US generics and India pharma businesses, with growing traction in APIs and consumer wellness via Zydus Wellness. The company’s robust global footprint and high R&D investments support a healthy pipeline of new product launches and market leadership in select therapeutic areas.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Zydus Life is poised for strong growth driven by high-value launches in the US like g-Revlimid and upcoming biosimilars, along with expansion in branded formulations and consumer wellness in India. The company plans 2–3 biosimilar launches over the next two years and is diversifying revenue streams by strengthening ROW markets.

Name: Alkem Laboratories Ltd
Overview:
Alkem Laboratories is a leading Indian pharmaceutical company with a strong market position in both domestic and international markets. It is widely recognized for its leadership in the acute therapy space, especially in anti-infectives, GI, vitamins & nutrients, pain management, and neurology. The company’s high-quality manufacturing facilities are approved by top global regulatory bodies, including the US FDA, reinforcing its compliance and global reach.
 
 Business Segments
 Domestic Formulations:
 Contributes ~82% of revenue from its top five therapeutic areas, with a dominant position in acute care and a growing presence in chronic therapies.
 
 International Business:
 Focuses mainly on the US generics market, alongside a growing footprint in other regulated and emerging markets. Includes both organic growth and strategic acquisitions.
 
 Research & Development (R&D):
 Significant investments in R&D support development of generics, complex formulations, biosimilars, APIs, and nutraceuticals. The company holds a robust pipeline with 160+ ANDA filings, demonstrating its commitment to innovation.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Alkem Laboratories is strategically enhancing its global presence by expanding into high-growth international markets. The company is focusing on developing novel drug formulations, biologics, and biosimilars, particularly in areas like oncology, diabetes, cardiovascular diseases, and central nervous system disorders. Through its subsidiary, Enzene Biosciences, Alkem is investing in advanced manufacturing facilities, including a $70 million plant in the United States, to strengthen its biopharmaceutical capabilities.

Name: Aurobindo Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Aurobindo Pharma is one of India’s leading pharmaceutical companies with a strong foothold in the global generics market. The company has a significant presence in both India and the United States, where it is the largest generics player by prescriptions dispensed. With operations in over 150 countries, Aurobindo Pharma has established itself as a trusted global healthcare provider.
 
 Business Segments
 Formulations: Major contributor to revenue, supplying finished dosage forms across multiple therapeutic areas.
 
 APIs (Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients): Manufactures and exports APIs to global clients, supporting its integrated supply chain.
 
 Therapeutic Areas: Covers key areas such as antibiotics, antiretrovirals, CVS, CNS, gastroenterology, biosimilars, and complex injectables.
 
 Production Footprint: Strategically expanding manufacturing capacities under government incentives like the PLI scheme to reduce import dependence and boost domestic capabilities.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Aurobindo Pharma is actively enhancing its global presence through strategic acquisitions and expanding its product portfolio. The company is in advanced discussions to acquire Zentiva, a leading European generic drugmaker, which would significantly bolster its footprint in the European market. Concurrently, Aurobindo is set to acquire Lannett Company, a U.S.-based generics manufacturer, to strengthen its position in the U.S. market. These acquisitions are expected to diversify its offerings, particularly in high-value therapeutic areas like ADHD treatments.

Name: Torrent Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
Torrent Pharmaceuticals Ltd is a leading Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in the research, development, manufacturing, and marketing of generic formulations. As the flagship entity of the diversified Torrent Group, it offers a broad range of dosage forms and focuses on therapeutic areas like cardiovascular, CNS, vitamins & nutrients, gastrointestinal, and anti-diabetes treatments. The company operates globally, with a strong presence in regulated markets such as the USA and Europe, and emerging markets like Brazil.
 
 Business Segments
 Generic Formulations: Oral solids, liquids, topicals, suppositories, human insulin, and synthetic APIs across multiple therapeutic categories.
 
 Global Markets: Operations in over 40 countries, including regulated and emerging markets.
 
 Diversified Group Interests: Besides pharmaceuticals, the Torrent Group is active in power generation and city gas distribution.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Torrent Pharmaceuticals is enhancing its reach with 300 new medical representatives in India and entering the trade generic space for acute therapies. International growth is expected from new branded launches in Brazil, a rebound in the German tender market, and USFDA clearances pending for export expansion. R&D-driven product innovation across global markets underpins its growth plans.

Name: Dr Reddys Laboratories Ltd
Overview:
Dr. Reddy’s is a leading Indian pharmaceutical company with a strong global presence. It operates across generics, APIs, pharmaceutical services, and proprietary products, strategically focusing on niche, limited-competition areas, injectables, and biosimilars to drive sustainable growth.
 
 Business Segments
 Global Generics: Core business contributing ~83% of total revenue; focuses on limited-competition generics, injectables, and biosimilars.
 
 Pharmaceutical Services & Active Ingredients (PSAI): Among the world’s largest API manufacturers; supports own generics and collaborates with global formulators.
 
 Custom Pharmaceutical Services (CPS): Provides development and manufacturing services to innovator pharma companies.
 
 Proprietary Products & Others: Develops differentiated formulations addressing unmet needs with enhanced efficacy and usability.
 
 Aurigene Pharmaceutical Services (Subsidiary): Expanding CDMO capabilities for biotherapeutics (proteins, antibodies, viral vectors).
 
 Revenue & Metrics :
 The Global Generics business forms the bulk of revenue, anchored by therapeutic areas like nervous system, gastrointestinal, and anti-infectives. PSAI strengthens the generics cost advantage and enables collaboration with global partners. New investments in biotherapeutics and differentiated proprietary products strengthen long-term growth visibility.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories is targeting differentiated growth by aiming for 25% of its new launches to be first-to-market by FY27. The company is enhancing its large molecule capabilities with expanded fill-finish capacity and investing in CDMO operations through Aurigene. With a strong focus on biosimilars, injectables, and proprietary products, along with cost efficiencies from backward-integrated APIs, Dr. Reddy’s is well-positioned to grow in both mature and emerging pharmaceutical markets.

Name: Abbott India Ltd
Overview:
Abbott India Ltd is one of India’s leading multinational pharmaceutical companies and a subsidiary of Abbott Laboratories, the US-based global healthcare giant with over 130 years of legacy. Abbott India has built a strong presence in India’s pharmaceutical landscape through its wide portfolio of over 125 branded products, trusted by healthcare professionals and millions of patients across the country.
 
 The company leverages the parent company’s global expertise in healthcare and R&D while focusing on India-specific needs with localized product development, marketing, and distribution.
 
 Business Segments
 Therapeutic Areas:
 Abbott India operates across key segments such as Women’s Health, Gastroenterology, Central Nervous System (CNS), Metabolics, Vaccines, and Consumer Health, meeting a wide range of healthcare needs.
 
 Product Forms:
 Revenue primarily comes from injectables, followed by tablets, liquids, capsules, and other dosage forms.
 
 OTC & Consumer Health:
 Abbott India places strategic emphasis on the over-the-counter (OTC) segment with popular everyday health products in antacids, laxatives, analgesics, and preventive care.
 
 Distribution:
 A robust network of 8,600+ stockists and 60 lakh retailers ensures wide product availability across India, including expansion into modern trade pharmacy chains and e-pharmacies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Abbott India is actively pursuing a comprehensive growth strategy to strengthen its position in the Indian healthcare market. The company is focusing on expanding its portfolio by launching new products, particularly in chronic therapies such as diabetes and thyroid treatments, where it has a strong presence. Additionally, Abbott India is enhancing its distribution network to improve access to its products across the country. The company is also investing in research and development to introduce innovative healthcare solutions that cater to the evolving needs of the Indian population.

Name: Cipla Ltd
Overview:
Cipla is a leading Indian pharma company with a strong domestic base and a significant presence in international markets, especially in South Africa and other emerging economies. Operates with a vast portfolio of 1,500+ products across multiple therapeutic areas.
 
Key Business Segments & Revenue Drivers
 Core Focus: Respiratory segment — target to make it 25–30% of revenue.
 
 Consumer Health: Cold & cough brands add an additional 10% revenue.
 
 Expansion Areas: Antidiabetics & Oncology — strengthened through strategic tie-ups with global pharma majors.
 
 APIs: Over 75 active pharmaceutical ingredients in pipeline for regulated markets.
 
 Geography: Growth drivers include India, US, and South Africa markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Cipla is reinforcing its leadership in the lung/respiratory segment while expanding its global footprint, particularly in high-margin niche areas like the US generic and specialty drug market. The company’s strong pipeline across multiple therapies, along with its shift toward private markets in India, supports sustainable profitability.

Name: Lupin Ltd
Overview:
Lupin Limited is a global pharmaceutical company with a strong presence in both the US and Indian markets. It is a leader in therapeutic segments like Anti-TB and Cephalosporins and continues to diversify into areas such as CNS, diabetes, and anti-infectives. The company is known for its robust focus on research and development, strategic partnerships, and commitment to launching complex generics, inhalation products, injectables, and biosimilars to expand its global footprint and deliver better healthcare outcomes.
 
 Business Segments 
 
 US Generics: ~38–40% of total revenue
 
 India Formulations: ~33–35% of total revenue
 
 EMEA & APAC Markets: ~10–12% of total revenue
 
 APIs & Others: ~10–12% of total revenue
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Lupin is strategically focused on enhancing profitability through complex generics, biosimilars, and differentiated product launches in key markets like the US and India. Its expansion into China and active investment in R&D for niche products support global growth ambitions. The company is also expanding in domestic branded generics, particularly in high-growth segments like CNS and diabetes.

Name: Glenmark Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
Glenmark Pharmaceuticals Ltd is a global research-led pharmaceutical company with operations in over 80 countries. It is involved in the generics, specialty, and OTC segments, and has established itself with a strong presence in dermatology, respiratory, and oncology therapies. The company operates 11 manufacturing sites globally and employs over 15,000 people.

Glenmark continues to expand its global footprint, with branded markets now contributing over 60% of its revenue. It maintains a leadership position in several key therapeutic areas and regions, including being the second-largest player in dermatology in India and a top-tier respiratory player in various emerging markets. The company has made major strides in innovation through its biotech subsidiary IGI, advancing a pipeline of novel oncology therapies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Glenmark Pharmaceuticals is strategically transitioning towards a branded, innovation-driven model, aiming for 70% of its portfolio to consist of branded products by 2030. This shift is underscored by a significant $700 million licensing agreement with AbbVie for its investigational cancer therapy, ISB 2001, highlighting Glenmark's commitment to oncology innovation.

Name: Divis Laboratories Ltd
Overview:
Divis Laboratories is a leading Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in the manufacture of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), intermediates, and nutraceutical ingredients. Known for its strong export focus, robust backward integration, and process innovation, the company serves global pharma majors with a diverse portfolio of generic APIs and custom synthesis solutions.
 
 Business Segments
 Generic APIs: Core portfolio supplying bulk drugs for multiple therapeutic areas.
 
 Custom Synthesis: Contract manufacturing & custom synthesis for large pharma innovators.
 
 Nutraceuticals: Supplies high-purity nutraceutical ingredients for global customers.
 
 Research & Process Development: Three dedicated R&D centers drive process efficiencies and new molecule development.
 
 Revenue Mix 
 Exports contribute ~85% of total revenue, with Europe as the largest market (47%), followed by America (23%), India (15%), Asia (11%), and the rest of the world (4%). The company’s integrated operations and strong global reach ensure resilient sales across regulated and emerging markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Divi’s Laboratories is poised for long-term growth through strategic investments in high-potential areas such as contrast media and off-patent molecules. The company’s strong backward integration, process innovation using advanced chemistries, and leadership in high-volume APIs ensure cost competitiveness and operational efficiency.

Name: Syncom Formulations (India) Ltd
Overview:
Syncom Formulations (India) Ltd is a pharmaceutical company focused on the development, manufacturing, and marketing of generic formulations and active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). Established in 1981, the company caters to domestic and international markets.

The company operates GMP-compliant facilities and has a diversified product portfolio in therapeutic areas such as anti-infectives, cardiovascular, and dermatology. Syncom emphasizes regulatory compliance and innovation to develop cost-effective pharmaceutical solutions for global markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Syncom Formulations (India) Ltd is actively pursuing expansion and modernization to strengthen its position in the global pharmaceutical market. The company is enhancing its manufacturing capabilities by increasing injectable capacity from 200 lakh to 300 lakh units per annum and modernizing its tablet department to meet growing demand.

Name: Themis Medicare Ltd
Overview:
Themis Medicare Ltd is an Indian pharmaceutical company engaged in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of generic formulations, APIs, and specialty drugs. The company serves domestic and international markets, focusing on therapeutic segments including cardiovascular, anti-infectives, and oncology.

Headquartered in Mumbai, Themis Medicare emphasizes R&D, regulatory compliance, and quality manufacturing. Its vertically integrated operations enable consistent supply, cost efficiency, and product innovation.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Themis Medicare Ltd is actively pursuing growth through strategic initiatives across its formulation and API segments. The company is expanding its hospital-focused portfolio, with plans to launch 13 new products in critical care and intensive care divisions, including anti-infectives, cardiology, diabetes, and neurology. In the formulation segment, Themis is enhancing its product basket and increasing productivity, aiming for significant growth in the coming quarters.

Name: Beta Drugs Ltd
Overview:
Beta Drugs Ltd is an Indian pharmaceutical company engaged in manufacturing formulations and active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). Established in 1983, it caters to domestic markets and exports to regulated markets globally.

The company focuses on chronic therapies, generic drugs, and over-the-counter formulations. Beta Drugs Ltd emphasizes research, quality control, and regulatory compliance across its manufacturing operations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Beta Drugs is aggressively expanding its global presence with over 350 international product registrations filed, compared to just 53 earlier, positioning it for accelerated export-led growth. The company is targeting entry into the US market within 2–2.5 years, with a strong pipeline in biosimilars and hormonal therapies. It is also investing in recombinant proteins and backward integration into key starting materials (KSMs).

Name: Jagsonpal Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview: Jagsonpal Pharmaceuticals Ltd is a Delhi-based pharmaceutical company founded in 1978, specializing in the development and manufacturing of pharmaceutical products and active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). The company offers a diverse range of products, including tablets, softgels, injections, capsules, and syrups. Jagsonpal Pharmaceuticals Ltd focuses on therapeutic segments such as gynaecology and orthopaedics, providing medications like Divatrone, Endoreg, Cystelia-M, Fibristone, Yuliprist, Deereg, and Lycored Softgel in the gynaecology segment, and JP Tone Up, Metadec 50 Injection, Metadec 25 Injection, Indocap SR, Indocap, and Colla-2 in the orthopaedics segment. The company's commitment to quality and healthcare excellence has established it as a trusted name in the pharmaceutical industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jagsonpal Pharmaceuticals Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to bolster its growth trajectory. The company has recently appointed a new Chief Operating Officer to drive operational excellence and efficiency. In line with its expansion strategy, Jagsonpal has acquired the India and Bhutan businesses of Yash Pharma, thereby enhancing its portfolio with 33 dermatology and pediatrics brands, which complements its existing strengths in gynecology and orthopaedics.

Name: Remus Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
Remus Pharmaceuticals Ltd, established in 2015, is an Indian pharmaceutical company engaged in the trading and export of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) and Finished Formulations. The company also offers technical consultancy services, assisting distributors in preparing reports for product registrations across various countries.

Remus Pharmaceuticals specializes in a diverse range of pharmaceutical products, including capsules, creams, eye drops, gels, infusions, inhalers, injections, nasal solutions, ointments, oral gels, oral solutions, oral suspensions, sachets, suspensions, and tablets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Remus Pharmaceuticals Ltd is strategically expanding its global footprint, with a presence in over 40 countries. The company is focusing on niche and critical care formulations, enhancing its direct-to-consumer (D2C) channels through pharmacy networks in various markets. Additionally, Remus is strengthening its distribution channels and optimizing operations to sustain long-term growth. Through these initiatives, the company aims to deliver consistent value to stakeholders and capitalize on emerging opportunities in the pharmaceutical sector.

Name: Sakar Healthcare Ltd
Overview:
Sakar Healthcare Ltd is a pharmaceutical formulations company engaged in manufacturing tablets, capsules, liquid orals, dry syrups, and injectables. Its facilities are WHO-GMP certified and cater to both domestic and international markets.

The company has established a strong presence in regulated and semi-regulated export markets, supplying to multiple therapeutic segments. Sakar’s emphasis on injectables and niche formulations has enhanced its growth trajectory.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Sakar Healthcare is positioned to benefit from rising global demand for cost-effective injectables. With injectables expected to sustain 15%+ growth and exports contributing the majority of sales, the company targets double-digit revenue growth into FY26. New therapeutic launches and geographic expansion will further support momentum.

Name: Zim Laboratories Ltd
Overview:
Zim Laboratories Ltd operates in the pharmaceutical sector, specializing in oral dosage forms and innovative drug delivery systems. The company focuses on novel formulations such as orally disintegrating strips (ODS) and dispersible tablets, catering to both regulated and semi-regulated markets. Its strength lies in R&D-driven product innovation and export-focused strategy.

The company has a strong export presence across Asia, Africa, and emerging markets, alongside a growing presence in regulated geographies. With healthcare access and affordable formulations gaining priority, Zim’s emphasis on technology-driven generics provides it with a competitive advantage.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Zim aims to expand its ODS portfolio and increase penetration in regulated markets. Strong R&D capabilities and global demand for affordable generics position it well for future growth.

Name: Kopran Ltd
Overview:
Kopran Limited is an Indian pharmaceutical company engaged in the manufacturing and marketing of a wide range of pharmaceutical products, including prescription drugs, over-the-counter medicines, active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), and veterinary products. The company operates in both domestic and international markets, serving patients and healthcare professionals across multiple therapeutic areas such as cardiovascular, neurological, gastrointestinal, and respiratory conditions.

With a strong focus on research and development, Kopran invests in discovering new drug molecules and formulations through its in-house research facilities. Its manufacturing processes adhere to stringent quality control and Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) standards, ensuring the safety, efficacy, and reliability of its products. The company also offers contract manufacturing and research services, collaborating with other pharmaceutical firms to expand its offerings and support innovation in the healthcare sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Kopran Ltd is strategically enhancing its global presence by expanding its manufacturing capabilities and diversifying its product portfolio. The company is focusing on the development of novel drug delivery systems and complex formulations across various therapeutic segments, including cardiovascular, anti-diabetic, and CNS drugs. Kopran is also working on opportunities for contractual drug development and manufacturing, aiming to enhance value addition and competitiveness in global markets.

Name: NGL Fine Chem Ltd
Overview:
NGL Fine-Chem Ltd operates as a specialized pharmaceuticals manufacturer and exporter, focusing on APIs and intermediates for both human and veterinary health applications. Its core strength lies in multi-step organic synthesis, custom manufacturing, and process development—capabilities bolstered by in-house R&D, pilot plant facilities, and a strong focus on delivering cost-effective, high-quality chemical solutions to global clients.

The company leverages advanced capabilities in handling complex and hazardous reactions such as nitration, halogenation, and hydrogenation, enabling it to serve niche segments across pharmaceutical value chains with precision and flexibility.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
NGL Fine-Chem is well-positioned for growth driven by its robust process development capabilities and niche product expertise. With global demand for specialized APIs and intermediates projected to increase, the company’s innovation-led manufacturing model and disciplined execution sets it up for strong volume and margin growth in FY26 and beyond.

Name: TTK Healthcare Ltd
Overview:
TTK Healthcare Ltd is a diversified healthcare company in India, with interests in medical devices, diagnostics, and consumer healthcare products. The company is known for its strong brand presence and commitment to quality and innovation.

In FY25, TTK Healthcare reported a revenue of ₹1,000 crore, a 5% increase from the previous year. The company's EBITDA stood at ₹150 crore, with a margin of 15%, and a PAT of ₹75 crore, reflecting a 4% year-over-year growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

TTK Healthcare Ltd is actively pursuing growth across its diverse business segments, including pharmaceuticals, medical devices, consumer products, and animal welfare. The company is focusing on expanding its medical devices division, particularly in heart valves and orthopedic implants, leveraging the "Make in India" initiative to reduce reliance on imports and enhance export potential. In the consumer products sector, TTK Healthcare is revitalizing its Woodward's Gripe Water brand through targeted media campaigns and new packaging to boost sales.

Name: Amanta Healthcare Ltd
Overview:
Amanta Healthcare Limited is a pharmaceutical company engaged in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of sterile liquid pharmaceutical products. The company primarily focuses on products such as intravenous (IV) fluids, sterile injectables, irrigation solutions, and other hospital-based pharmaceutical formulations used in medical treatments. These products are widely used in hospitals, clinics, and healthcare institutions for patient care and critical medical procedures.

The company operates advanced manufacturing facilities designed to produce sterile formulations that meet strict quality and safety standards required in the pharmaceutical industry. Amanta Healthcare supplies its products across domestic and international markets and works with healthcare providers, distributors, and institutional buyers.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its product portfolio in sterile formulations and strengthen its presence in hospital-focused pharmaceutical segments. It aims to increase manufacturing capacity and upgrade its production facilities to meet rising demand for sterile injectable and infusion products. The company is also focusing on expanding its reach in international markets by obtaining regulatory approvals and building stronger distribution networks.

Name: Lincoln Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
Lincoln Pharmaceuticals Ltd is an Indian pharmaceutical company, established in 1979, engaged in the manufacturing, marketing, and export of generic drugs, branded formulations, and over-the-counter medicines. The company operates multiple GMP-compliant manufacturing facilities and offers a diverse portfolio covering antibiotics, analgesics, cardiovascular, gastrointestinal, and respiratory therapies, among others.

With a focus on research and development, Lincoln Pharmaceuticals invests in advanced infrastructure and technology to ensure high-quality products that meet international standards. Serving both domestic and international markets through a strong distribution network, the company is committed to providing safe, effective, and affordable healthcare solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Lincoln Pharmaceuticals Ltd is actively pursuing expansion to strengthen its position in the global pharmaceutical market. The company has completed the expansion of its Cephalosporin plant in Mehsana, Gujarat, and commenced commercial production, with plans to register the product for export to multiple countries. Additionally, Lincoln Pharmaceuticals aims to achieve ₹1,000 crore in revenue within the next three years through business expansion and new market entries, including recent approvals from the EU and TGA, and entry into the Canadian market.

Name: Ind-Swift Laboratories Ltd
Overview:
Ind-Swift Laboratories Ltd, established in 1995, is a prominent Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in the production of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), intermediates, and formulations. The company has a strong presence in the global pharmaceutical industry, focusing on high-value therapeutic segments. Ind-Swift Laboratories operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in India, adhering to stringent quality standards and regulatory requirements.

The company's research and development (R&D) center, located in Mohali, Punjab, plays a pivotal role in its growth strategy. Spanning over 40,000 square feet, the R&D center is dedicated to the development and optimization of new and existing process technologies supporting the manufacturing of APIs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Ind-Swift Laboratories aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing its manufacturing capabilities. The company plans to invest in research and development to develop new and complex APIs, catering to the evolving needs of the global pharmaceutical market. With a focus on quality and regulatory compliance, Ind-Swift Laboratories is poised to strengthen its position in the international pharmaceutical industry.

Name: Bajaj Healthcare Ltd
Overview: Bajaj Healthcare Ltd is a pharmaceutical company established in 1993, specializing in the manufacture of bulk drugs, nutraceuticals, and food additives. The company offers a wide range of products, including amino acids, vitamins, and minerals, catering to various industries such as pharmaceuticals, nutraceuticals, and food. Bajaj Healthcare Ltd is committed to quality and innovation, ensuring compliance with international standards and regulations.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bajaj Healthcare Ltd is strategically positioning itself for sustained growth through several key initiatives. The company has entered into a development and supply agreement for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) with a European entity, signaling its commitment to expanding its international footprint and enhancing its product offerings.

Name: Anuh Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Anuh Pharma Ltd. is an Indian bulk drugs manufacturer and a part of the SK Group, specializing in APIs, medicinal chemicals, botanical products, and pharmaceutical formulations. The company focuses on therapeutic segments such as Macrolides, Anti-TB, Anti-Bacterial, Anti-Malarial, and Corticosteroids, serving over 350 customers across 57 countries and establishing a strong global presence.

Anuh Pharma operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Tarapur, accredited by EUGMP, USFDA, and WHO, with a capacity of 1,800 MTPA, and maintains an advanced R&D center in Mahape. Its diversified product portfolio includes Erythromycin, Higher Macrolides, Chloramphenicol, Pyrazinamide, Sulphadoxine, Corticosteroids, Ambroxol HCL, and Gliclazide, supporting both domestic and export markets while emphasizing innovation and regulatory compliance.
Key Takeways:
Growth Oulook

Looking forward, the company aims for a 15–20% annual growth, supported by capacity expansion and market penetration. Anuh Pharma's strategic focus on expanding its product offerings and enhancing manufacturing capabilities positions it well for sustained growth in the pharmaceutical sector.

Name: Sanofi Consumer Healthcare India Ltd
Overview:
Sanofi Consumer Healthcare India Ltd is the over-the-counter (OTC) and consumer healthcare division of the global Sanofi group, focused on manufacturing and trading non-prescription healthcare products. It operates independently after being demerged from its parent Sanofi India, with a mission to bring science-backed, accessible consumer health solutions across categories like allergy relief, digestive health, vitamins, and pain relief.

The company leverages strong R&D capabilities, a legacy portfolio of trusted brands, and both domestic and export markets. It emphasizes product innovation, relaunch of key products, and expanding its reach via partnerships and its own manufacturing base to serve a broader customer base.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sanofi Consumer is aiming to accelerate its product pipeline, planning to introduce new OTC and wellness products regularly and strengthen its launch cadence. It intends to boost both domestic and export sales through renewed focus on innovation, relaunches, and entry into premium segments. Leveraging operational efficiencies, the company plans to scale up its manufacturing base and enhance its partner network to improve market reach. Cost management and supply-chain optimization are expected to support margin improvement. I

Name: Hester Biosciences Ltd
Overview:
Hester Biosciences Ltd is a leading player in the animal healthcare sector, specializing in the manufacture and export of vaccines and health products for both poultry and large animals. As India’s second-largest poultry vaccine manufacturer, the company also provides complementary services such as seroprofiling kits, diagnostic labs tailored for poultry flocks, and mastitis control programs for cattle. 

Its operations span over 30 countries, including key markets like India, Nepal, and Tanzania, supported by collaborations with organizations such as the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, GALVmed, and Novapharma.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Hester is well positioned for continued growth through FY26, backed by a diversified portfolio, improving cost discipline, and rising demand for veterinary healthcare products and services. Its focus on expanding diagnostic offerings and strengthening export markets especially through global partnerships could drive both volume and margin expansion in upcoming quarters.

Name: Jubilant Pharmova Ltd
Overview:
Jubilant Pharmova Ltd. is a diversified global pharmaceuticals company with a strong presence across radiopharma, allergy immunotherapy, CDMO sterile injectables, CRDMO services, generics, and a proprietary novel drug pipeline. Its operations span the US, Canada, and India, with an integrated manufacturing and R&D footprint supported by a wide radiopharmacy network in the US.

The company’s strategic focus is on high-growth, high-margin segments, leveraging strong compliance standards, advanced technologies, and differentiated capabilities to build long-term customer partnerships.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management targets 2x revenue growth and 23–25% EBITDA margins by FY30, driven by radiopharma product launches, sterile injectables capacity scale-up, expanded PET network, and increased biologics outsourcing. Execution on compliance upgrades and strategic capex will be key to sustaining momentum through FY26 and beyond.

Name: Alivus Life Sciences Ltd
Overview:
Glenmark Pharmaceuticals is a global, research-driven pharmaceutical company operating in generics, specialty, and OTC segments across over 80 countries. The company has a diversified portfolio including branded products, respiratory and dermatology therapies, and a strong pipeline of innovative medicines. Glenmark balances growth between emerging and developed markets while investing substantially in R&D to support its specialty pipeline and global launches.

The business is organized regionally with India, North America, Europe, and Rest of World segments contributing to consolidated revenues. Glenmark’s R&D investment underpins new product launches in key therapeutic areas like respiratory, oncology, and immunology. Their global brand Ryaltris has seen significant traction with further geographic expansion planned, alongside upcoming launches in specialty segments.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management targets mid-teens revenue growth in FY26 with margin expansion driven by specialty launches and pipeline commercialization. The transition in diabetes portfolio is underway with new drugs launching to restore growth. Regulatory and market risks remain, but strong product pipeline and global expansion provide confidence in sustained growth and profitability.

Name: Blue Jet Healthcare Ltd
Overview:
Blue Jet Healthcare, incorporated in 1968, is a pharmaceutical and healthcare ingredient company with expertise in contrast media intermediates, high-intensity sweeteners, and pharma intermediates & APIs. It was the first manufacturer of saccharin and its salts in India and has evolved into a trusted global partner for innovator companies, leveraging long-term contracts and niche product positioning.

The company has built its reputation around specialized capabilities, strong R&D, and value-added manufacturing. With capacity expansions, backward integration, and a sustainable energy mix, Blue Jet is strategically positioned to capitalize on global demand, China+1 tailwinds, and opportunities in the fast-growing CDMO and contrast media markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is confident of strong revenue growth and margin trajectory, driven by contrast media launches, sustained PI/API momentum, and sweetener recovery. Backward integration and CDMO expansion provide visibility for long-term growth, with new launches expected to materially contribute from FY27 onward.

Name: Sanofi India Ltd
Overview:
Sanofi India Limited is one of the leading multinational pharmaceutical companies in the Indian market, with operations spanning seven therapeutic areas, including diabetes, cardiology, thrombosis, central nervous system, and anti-histamines. It is majority-owned by Sanofi Global and its wholly owned subsidiary Hoechst GmbH, holding a combined 60.40% stake. The company follows a calendar-year financial cycle in line with its parent. Its portfolio includes 32 brands, five of which are ranked among the top 300 in the Indian Pharmaceutical Market (IPM), and it serves over 100,000 pharmacies via 3,000 distributors, reaching 28 countries.

The company operates a manufacturing facility in Goa with over 5 billion tablets annual capacity, of which 61% is exported. This facility produces key drugs such as paracetamol-codeine combinations, Depakine, Ramipril, Fexofenadine, and Glimepiride, and is the only plant outside Frankfurt authorized to manufacture Ramipril PC granules. Sanofi India has recently undertaken significant restructuring, including divesting its nutraceuticals business, demerging its consumer healthcare arm into a wholly owned subsidiary, and forging strategic partnerships with Cipla and Emcure to expand reach in CNS and cardiovascular therapies, respectively.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Sanofi India plans to expand its renewable product pipeline in diabetes and cardiovascular therapy, deepen market penetration in consumer healthcare through modern trade and e-commerce, and further localize manufacturing to enhance supply chain resilience. The India for India strategy is expected to drive operational efficiency and sustained double-digit growth in key focus brands.

Name: Strides Pharma Science Ltd
Overview:
Strides Pharma Science Ltd. is a global pharmaceutical company engaged in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of niche pharmaceutical products across regulated and emerging markets. Its operations span more than 100 countries, supported by a portfolio that covers generics, branded generics, and institutional supplies for critical therapies.

The company operates 7 manufacturing facilities across India, the US, Italy, and Kenya, with 4 sites approved by the USFDA. Strides has a strong presence in the US, Europe, Australia, and Africa, with a focus on soft-gelatin capsules, specialty formulations, and high-volume generics. Its integrated global model and broad pipeline underpin sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Strides Pharma Science Ltd is strategically enhancing its global presence by focusing on regulated markets such as the U.S., Europe, Australia, and Canada, while also expanding into growth markets in Africa, Asia-Pacific, and Latin America. The company is transitioning towards a technology-led specialty Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) model, emphasizing complex injectables, biologics, and drug-device combinations.

Name: OneSource Specialty Pharma Ltd
Overview:
OneSource Specialty Pharma Ltd is a biopharma CDMO (Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization) providing end-to-end solutions for biological drug products, injectables, drug-device combinations, soft gelatin capsules, and complex modalities like cell & gene therapy and RNA products. It operates multiple globally compliant manufacturing facilities, with leadership in regulatory approvals and technical‐commercial expertise, enabling customers to take products from development through commercial supply. 

The company was formed by merging several business units (from Strides, SteriScience, Stelis) under a scheme of demerger to consolidate capabilities in biologics, injectables, soft gels and drug-device formats. It is based in Bengaluru and focuses on quality, compliance and scale to serve both domestic and global pharma & biotech customers.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

OneSource is aiming to grow strongly, riding high on global demand for weight-loss and diabetes drugs (especially GLP-1 types), and expanding its contract development & manufacturing services across biologics, injectables, softgel capsules, and drug-device combinations. It plans to invest heavily (about USD 100 million) to build capacity in drug-device formats, helping it win new customer contracts and scale operations.

Name: Caplin Point Laboratories Ltd
Overview:
Caplin Point Laboratories Ltd is a diversified pharmaceutical company engaged in manufacturing APIs, finished formulations, R&D, and clinical research, with a significant presence across Latin America, Africa, and the USA. The company offers a broad portfolio of over 650 formulations in 36 therapeutic areas, including many drugs on the WHO essential medicines list. Its business model emphasizes an asset-light approach, strategic front-end control, and global expansion through registered products and direct sales channels.

Caplin Point’s growth is supported by steady innovation in peptide injectables, insulin, and oncology, as well as the ramp-up of its US-based sterile injectable subsidiary. The company is focused on sustainable, high-quality growth with strong margins, balancing capital investment with operational efficiency. Recent investments in new manufacturing lines, backward integration of APIs, and expansion in emerging markets underpin its long-term strategy.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management anticipates a transitional phase of 18–24 months with steady revenue similar to FY25, before growth accelerates from US front-end expansion, oncology approvals, and LatAm market penetration. The company targets high-quality, margin-accretive growth rather than rapid topline expansion, with a long-term horizon of 25–30 years. Potential inorganic growth via M&A remains an option once cash reserves accumulate further.

Name: Procter & Gamble Health Ltd
Overview:
P&G Health Ltd, formerly known as Merck Limited, is a subsidiary of The Procter & Gamble Company and a leading player in India’s vitamins, minerals, and supplements (VMS) segment. The company markets trusted pharmaceutical brands such as Neurobion, Evion, Livogen, Seven Seas, Nasivion, Polybion, and Cosome, serving both domestic and international markets.

Its operations are anchored by a single manufacturing facility in Goa and a robust super distributor-led go-to-market model, which ensures deep penetration across rural and urban channels, as well as strong e-commerce presence. With an integrated growth strategy focusing on portfolio superiority, productivity, and constructive disruption, the company continues to expand market share while reinvesting in product innovation and brand building.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
PGHL aims to leverage its integrated growth strategy combining product innovation, superior retail execution, and GTM transformation to expand penetration in underserved markets. Future growth will be supported by rural expansion, broader e-commerce adoption, and high-impact brand activations. With a resilient operating model and consistent reinvestment in superiority, management remains confident in sustaining market share gains and category leadership.

Name: Gland Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Gland Pharma, established in 1978 and headquartered in Hyderabad, is among India's leading injectable-focused pharmaceutical companies. Initially operating as a contract manufacturer of small-volume liquid parenterals, it has evolved into a global player with a presence in over 60 countries, including key markets like the U.S., Europe, Canada, and Australia. The company primarily follows a B2B business model, supported by its robust development, manufacturing, and marketing capabilities in complex injectables.

The company offers a diverse range of injectables including vials, ampoules, pre-filled syringes, and lyophilized products across over 15 therapeutic areas. It is promoted by Shanghai Fosun Pharma and has scaled rapidly through consistent product innovation, international expansion, and strategic acquisitions such as Cenexi. The acquisition significantly boosted its European footprint and manufacturing capabilities.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Gland Pharma is strategically enhancing its position in the global injectable market through significant capacity expansions and a focus on high-value, complex generics. The company is scaling its GLP-1 fill-finish capacity from 40 million to 140 million cartridges, aiming to capitalize on the growing demand for these therapies. Additionally, Gland Pharma has filed 19 complex injectable products in the U.S., targeting a combined market opportunity of $6.5 billion.

Name: Rubicon Research Ltd
Overview:
Rubicon Research Limited, established in 1999, is a Mumbai-based pharmaceutical company specializing in the development, manufacturing, and commercialization of differentiated formulations. The company focuses on complex generic and specialty products, including oral solids, liquids, nasal sprays, and drug-device combinations, primarily targeting regulated markets such as the United States. 

Rubicon operates US FDA-approved manufacturing facilities in Ambernath and Satara, India, and a research center in Thane, India, with a global development center in Canada. The company has built a strong portfolio of over 70 US FDA-approved products and maintains a robust pipeline of new drug applications (NDAs) and abbreviated new drug applications (ANDAs).
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Rubicon Research aims to expand its product portfolio by leveraging its strong research and development capabilities, focusing on complex formulations and drug-device combinations. The company plans to enhance its global presence through strategic acquisitions and partnerships, particularly in the US and other regulated markets. Investments in manufacturing infrastructure are expected to support increased production capacity and compliance with international standards.

Name: Corona Remedies Ltd
Overview:
Corona Remedies Ltd is an Indian branded pharmaceutical company that develops, manufactures and markets a wide range of medicine formulations across key therapeutic areas such as women’s healthcare, cardio-metabolic disorders, pain management, urology and other speciality segments. Its diversified product portfolio includes over 70 brands that are prescribed by healthcare professionals and distributed through a strong pan-India network of medical representatives. 

The company operates modern manufacturing facilities with regulatory certifications and produces oral solids, capsules and other dosage forms used in chronic and sub-chronic therapies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Corona Remedies aims to expand its product portfolio further by launching new formulations and strengthening its presence in high-growth therapeutic areas, supported by ongoing R&D initiatives and strategic licensing or brand acquisitions. It plans to enhance its manufacturing capabilities, including commissioning new facilities to broaden its production base and reduce dependence on external suppliers.

Name: Panacea Biotec Ltd
Overview:
Panacea Biotec Ltd is a leading Indian biotechnology company engaged in the research, development, manufacturing, and marketing of vaccines, pharmaceutical formulations, biosimilars, and natural products. Established in 1984 and headquartered in New Delhi, the company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Lalru (Punjab) and Malpur (Himachal Pradesh).

The company focuses on innovation in vaccine development, including fully liquid vaccines like the Pentavalent and Hexavalent vaccines. It also produces a diverse range of generic drugs and biosimilars, serving multiple therapeutic areas such as oncology, nephrology, gastroenterology, and endocrinology. Panacea Biotec aims to combine quality manufacturing with global distribution to strengthen its international presence.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Panacea Biotec Ltd is accelerating its transformation by channeling capital into a fortified vaccine-driven growth agenda, having divested its domestic pharmaceutical portfolio to eliminate debt and fund R&D investments. Their strategy centers on scaling production of innovative vaccines—such as the fully liquid hexavalent EasySix and the latest EasyFourPol—while advancing clinical trials for next-generation vaccines like NucoVac-11 (pneumococcal) and DengiAll (tetravalent dengue)

Name: Alembic Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1907, Alembic Limited is the flagship company of the Alembic Group, with diversified interests in real estate, active pharmaceutical ingredients (API), and renewable power assets. The company holds significant strategic investments, including a 28.41% stake in Alembic Pharmaceuticals Ltd and a 19.01% stake in Paushak Ltd. Headquartered in Vadodara, it has transitioned from its original formulations business—demerged in 2010—to a more diversified model emphasizing real estate growth and high-value asset ownership.

The company’s real estate business has emerged as the dominant revenue driver, capitalizing on robust demand and premium pricing in residential and commercial spaces. Its API segment focuses on niche bulk drugs and pharmaceutical intermediates, while its power assets—windmills and co-generation plants—support captive consumption. Strategic investments and recurring dividend income provide additional stability and long-term value creation.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Alembic aims to sustain growth in the real estate segment through premium and mixed-use projects while leveraging its stake in Alembic Pharma for dividend income stability. The company’s focus on high-value developments, diversification of revenue streams, and asset-backed expansion is expected to underpin medium-term performance.

Name: Indegene Ltd
Overview:
Indegene Ltd, incorporated in 1998, is a digital-first healthcare and technology company providing analytics, technology, and commercial, medical, regulatory, and safety services to life sciences and healthcare organizations. The company enables biopharmaceutical, emerging biotech, and medical device companies to develop products, navigate regulatory processes, and bring them to market efficiently. Leveraging domain expertise, proprietary digital platforms, and an agile operating model, Indegene delivers solutions that enhance patient and physician engagement globally.

Its service portfolio spans drug development and clinical trials, regulatory submissions, pharmacovigilance, complaints management, and commercialization. The company operates through key segments including Enterprise Commercial Solutions, Omnichannel Activation, Enterprise Medical Solutions, and Enterprise Clinical Solutions & Consultancy. With a global footprint across North America, Europe, and Asia, Indegene serves top-tier life sciences clients and continues to expand its offerings through strategic collaborations and technology adoption.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company is poised for sustained growth in FY25–26, driven by rising digital transformation in life sciences, adoption of AI-enabled solutions, and expansion of global delivery capacity. Its focus on scaling omnichannel capabilities, deepening client partnerships, and leveraging technology platforms positions it for strong revenue growth and margin stability.

Name: Astrazeneca Pharma India Ltd
Overview:
AstraZeneca Pharma India is a science-led pharmaceutical company with a broad therapeutic portfolio that includes oncology, cardiovascular, renal & metabolism, respiratory & immunology, vaccines, and rare diseases. It has earned a strong reputation in India for driving innovation through strategic approvals and launches, particularly in cancer therapies. The company also maintains a focus on expanding access to treatment via licensing, partnerships, and tiered pricing strategies to reach varied patient segments.

The company’s operations are supported by a modern infrastructure, including a robust R&D and commercial setup in India. It also continues to strengthen its global presence by investing in local capabilities such as AI-driven research, digital health, and global business services hubs—cementing India as a pivotal part of its global strategy.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook 
AstraZeneca Pharma India appears well-positioned for sustained growth through Continued momentum from oncology, particularly with expanding indications and new launches. A growing footprint in rare and biopharmaceutical segments. Operational strength backed by debt-free balance sheet and ongoing investments in innovation and digital infrastructure. Strategic moves like asset monetization may unlock capital for reinvestment.

Name: Concord Biotech Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1984, Concord Biotech Limited is a research-driven biopharma company specializing in fermentation-based Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) and finished formulations. Headquartered in Ahmedabad, the company is one of the few global players with expertise in niche fermentation APIs across therapeutic areas such as immunosuppressants, oncology, anti-infectives, and antifungals. It operates through three business segments—APIs, formulations, and Contract Development & Manufacturing Organisation (CDMO) services—leveraging its advanced R&D capabilities, backward integration to Key Starting Materials (KSMs), and stringent regulatory compliance.

Concord’s manufacturing footprint includes four facilities in Gujarat, with a total fermentation capacity of 1,250 m³ and formulation capacity of 802 million units annually, recently expanded with a new injectable formulations facility commissioned in March 2025. The company’s products are supplied to over 70 countries, serving 250+ customers, with a revenue mix in FY25 of ~78% from APIs and ~22% from formulations. Its strategy focuses on diversifying its portfolio, increasing customer penetration, expanding geographic reach, and scaling CDMO offerings.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Concord plans to launch 8–10 new products in the next three years, focusing on oncology and anti-infectives. The CDMO business is poised for growth, with commercial supplies to a large US customer already underway and multiple discussions in progress. The new injectable facility is expected to boost formulations revenue in coming quarters. With robust R&D capabilities, backward integration, and strong regulatory approvals, Concord is positioned to expand market share and sustain double-digit growth in the medium term.

Name: Piramal Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Piramal Pharma Limited (PPL), a part of the Piramal Group, operates across three primary pharmaceutical verticals: Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO), Complex Hospital Generics (CHG), and India Consumer Healthcare (PCH). The company has built a diversified global presence with 15 manufacturing sites and operations in over 100 countries. Its offerings span from on-patent and generic drug development to branded OTC consumer health products. Through strategic investments and acquisitions, PPL has scaled its scientific and manufacturing capabilities to become one of India's top three CDMO players and the 13th largest globally.
