The CDMO segment operates across North America, Europe, and India, serving over 500 clients and contributing 58% to FY24 revenue. The CHG business, which accounted for 30% of revenue, includes critical care products like inhalation anesthesia and intrathecal therapies, supported by manufacturing units in India and the US. The PCH division, comprising over 25 brands such as Little’s, Lacto Calamine, and Tetmosol, grew to ₹1,000 Cr in FY24, following an asset-light and self-funded model. PPL also holds strategic stakes in a JV with Abbvie for ophthalmology and in Yapan Bio to expand into biologics and vaccines.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Piramal Pharma Limited is strategically enhancing its global footprint through significant investments in its Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) segment. The company is expanding its U.S. facilities, including a $90 million investment to enhance commercial-scale manufacturing capabilities, particularly in the development of antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) and sterile injectables.

Name: Unichem Laboratories Ltd
Overview:
Unichem Laboratories Ltd is a pharmaceutical company focused on manufacturing and marketing generic formulations, active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), and contract manufacturing services. Its operations span global markets, leveraging manufacturing facilities with multiple international regulatory approvals and a robust R&D infrastructure.

The company’s strategic emphasis is on scale, through comprehensive integration of APIs and formulations, supported by contract manufacturing, custom synthesis, and innovation from its R&D base.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Unichem is poised to leverage its integrated model and strong export orientation to sustain growth. Continued momentum in global formulations, backed by its API supply chain and CMO capabilities, should support steady performance ahead despite short-term fluctuations.

Name: Sun Pharma Advanced Research Company Ltd
Overview:
Sun Pharma Advanced Research Company Ltd (SPARC) is a biopharmaceutical company engaged in the research and development of pharmaceutical products. The company primarily focuses on the development of new chemical entities and novel drug delivery systems. SPARC operates in the healthcare industry, characterized by rapid innovation and significant investment in research and development.

In FY25, SPARC reported a total income of ₹520.41 crore, a 9.0% increase from the previous fiscal year. Despite this growth, the company faced challenges with profitability, reporting a net loss of ₹51.87 crore in Q1 FY26. The decline in profitability was attributed to increased research and development expenses and other operational costs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
SPARC plans to advance ongoing clinical trials, enhance R&D capabilities, and strengthen collaborations. Cost-control measures and operational efficiency improvements are expected to enhance profitability, with the long-term goal of expanding its innovative product pipeline and shareholder value.

Name: Senores Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
Senores Pharmaceuticals Ltd is a pharmaceutical company engaged in the development, manufacturing, and marketing of generic medicines across multiple therapeutic segments. With strong capabilities in formulation development, it serves both regulated and semi-regulated markets worldwide.

The company has a growing presence in markets like the US, Europe, and Latin America, supported by its R&D strength and manufacturing facilities adhering to global regulatory standards. It focuses on cost-effective, high-quality generics catering to chronic and acute therapies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management has outlined three core growth pillars: expanding the ANDA pipeline in regulated markets, scaling the CDMO/CMO business with global partnerships, and enhancing penetration in emerging markets while improving profitability. The backward integration strategy in APIs, with capacity being enhanced from 25 MTPA to 169 MTPA, is expected to bring better cost control, supply security, and margin resilience in the coming quarters.

Name: Sai Parenteral's Ltd
Overview:
Sai Parenteral's Ltd. is a pharmaceutical formulations company engaged in the research, development, and manufacturing of a wide range of medicines. The company focuses on producing formulations such as injectables, tablets, capsules, and other dosage forms used across different therapeutic segments. Its operations are centered on delivering quality healthcare products that meet regulatory standards for both domestic and international markets.

The company operates manufacturing facilities supported by research and development capabilities to create and improve pharmaceutical formulations. It serves hospitals, healthcare institutions, and distribution networks by offering a diversified product portfolio. With a focus on quality control, innovation, and compliance, the company aims to cater to evolving healthcare needs across multiple regions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its product portfolio by focusing on complex and high-value formulations, especially in injectables and specialty segments. It aims to increase its manufacturing capacity and upgrade facilities to meet growing demand and regulatory requirements. The company is also working on strengthening its presence in international markets by obtaining approvals and building distribution partnerships.

Name: Sudeep Pharma Ltd
Overview:
Sudeep Pharma Limited is a leading manufacturer of pharmaceutical-grade excipients, food-grade minerals, and specialty nutrition ingredients, supplying to over 100 countries worldwide. The company’s product range includes mineral salts and actives such as calcium, iron, magnesium, zinc, potassium, sodium and others, along with high-value specialty ingredients used in medicines, dietary supplements, infant nutrition, fortified foods, beverages, and nutraceuticals. 

Sudeep Pharma operates multiple manufacturing facilities with globally recognised regulatory certifications (including USFDA, WHO-GMP, EXCiPACT, and CEP for certain products), enabling it to meet stringent quality standards for global markets. 'Over time, it has added advanced capabilities such as encapsulation, spray-drying, granulation, liposomal processing and blending to support infant formula, clinical nutrition and specialty ingredient needs.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sudeep Pharma plans to expand capacity significantly by building a new large-scale facility to meet rising global demand for its mineral-based and speciality ingredients. With its strong regulatory compliance and diverse product portfolio, the company aims to deepen exports to regulated markets in the US and Europe and increase direct supply rather than relying on distributors.

Name: Morepen Laboratories Ltd
Overview:
Morepen Laboratories Ltd specializes in manufacturing and marketing a wide array of healthcare products, including active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), branded and generic formulations, and home health devices. As a vertically integrated player in pharmaceuticals and healthcare, it serves both domestic and global markets.

The company positions itself as a trusted provider across the healthcare value chain—from API production and formulation development to medical diagnostics—leveraging a broad product portfolio to meet evolving patient and industry needs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With a strong base in API exports and expanding capacity in medical devices backed by a significant ₹200 crore QIP raise Morepen is primed for recovery. Sustained investments in backward integration and global expansion could help revive growth momentum in FY26 and beyond, potentially restoring profitability as demand improves

Name: Dishman Carbogen Amcis Ltd
Overview:
Dishman Carbogen Amcis Ltd. (DCAL) is a leading Contract Research and Manufacturing Services (CRAMS) provider in the pharmaceutical industry, offering integrated solutions from process research and development to commercial manufacturing. With a global presence and facilities across Europe, India, and China, the company collaborates closely with pharmaceutical innovators to supply Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs).

The company operates through two primary segments: Carbogen Amcis (Switzerland) and DCAL IndiaNCE (India and China). These divisions cater to a diverse clientele, including global pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies, by providing high-quality, cost-effective, and timely delivery of pharmaceutical intermediates and APIs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Dishman Carbogen Amcis Ltd. plans to enhance its capabilities in both research and manufacturing to meet the growing demands of the global pharmaceutical industry. The company aims to expand its service offerings, strengthen its global presence, and invest in advanced technologies to drive sustainable growth and profitability in the coming fiscal year.

Name: Innova Captab Ltd
Overview:
Innova Captab Ltd is an Indian pharmaceutical company engaged in the manufacturing and trading of drugs and pharmaceuticals. Established in 2005 and converted to a public limited company in 2018, it has a strong presence across the pharmaceutical value chain. The company is headquartered in Mumbai and operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Jammu, which commenced operations in FY25.

Innova Captab is committed to delivering high-quality pharmaceutical products and services, leveraging advanced manufacturing technologies and a skilled workforce. The company focuses on innovation and operational excellence to meet the evolving demands of its clientele and achieve sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Innova Captab Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the pharmaceutical industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, Innova Captab is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Aarti Pharmalabs Ltd
Overview:
Aarti Pharmalabs Ltd, established in 2019, is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in pharmaceuticals and nutraceuticals. The company focuses on the development and production of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), pharmaceutical intermediates, and xanthine derivatives. With a commitment to quality and innovation, Aarti Pharmalabs caters to a global clientele, ensuring the delivery of high-quality products. 

Aarti Pharmalabs has demonstrated significant growth since its inception, expanding its product portfolio and market presence. The company has been actively investing in capacity expansion and technological advancements to meet the increasing demand for its products. Aarti Pharmalabs' strategic initiatives focus on enhancing operational efficiencies and strengthening its position in the competitive pharmaceutical industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Aarti Pharmalabs is focusing on expanding its capacity in high-demand product segments and strengthening its position in the global pharmaceutical market. The company's strategic initiatives, including capacity expansions and technological advancements, are expected to drive continued growth and enhance its market position. Aarti Pharmalabs' commitment to sustainability and customer satisfaction positions it well for sustained success in the pharmaceutical industry.

Name: Gujarat Themis Biosyn Ltd
Overview:
Gujarat Themis Biosyn Ltd is an Indian pharmaceutical company specializing in the production of active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) and intermediates. Established in 1981 and headquartered in Vapi, Gujarat, the company operates under the GTP Group, with a strong presence in the pharmaceutical sector.

The company is committed to delivering high-quality pharmaceutical products and services, leveraging advanced manufacturing technologies and a skilled workforce. Gujarat Themis Biosyn focuses on innovation and operational excellence to meet the evolving demands of its clientele and achieve sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Gujarat Themis Biosyn Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the pharmaceutical industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, Gujarat Themis Biosyn is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Amrutanjan Health Care Ltd
Overview:
Amrutanjan Health Care Ltd is a prominent healthcare and wellness company in India, specializing in pain relief and over-the-counter healthcare products. The company enjoys strong brand recognition and an extensive distribution network across urban and rural regions.

Innovation in product development and customer engagement has enabled Amrutanjan to sustain growth in a competitive market. The company continually invests in expanding its product lines and increasing market penetration.

Amrutanjan Health Care leverages its heritage and operational expertise to explore new wellness segments, aiming for sustainable growth and enhanced consumer trust.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Amrutanjan Health Care Ltd is sharpening its focus on expanding its over-the-counter (OTC) offerings while deepening its reach in personal hygiene and beverages. The company is broadening its product range with variants like roll-ons and dental gels, and is ramping up rural distribution through extensive stockist networks to increase accessibility. Emphasizing innovation, it continues to refresh its flagship balm with modern formats and packaging that appeal to younger consumers.

Name: Novartis India Ltd
Overview:
Novartis India Ltd is a pharmaceutical company engaged primarily in the wholesale distribution of medicines and medical goods. It sources and imports a variety of pharmaceutical products from global suppliers, serving the Indian market through its established distribution network.

The company plays the role of a bridge between international pharmaceutical developments and the Indian healthcare system, helping bring advanced treatments and medicines into the country. Its operations focus on ensuring availability and accessibility of imported pharmaceutical goods across domestic channels.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Novartis India Ltd is actively enhancing its capabilities to meet the growing demand for high-quality pharmaceutical products in India. The company is expanding its portfolio in key therapeutic areas such as pain management, organ transplantation, and neuroscience. Through strategic initiatives, Novartis India aims to strengthen its position in the Indian market by improving access to essential medicines and fostering innovation in healthcare delivery.

Name: Aarti Drugs Ltd
Overview:
Aarti Drugs Ltd, established in 1984 and part of the Aarti Group, is a vertically integrated pharmaceutical company specializing in the production of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), pharmaceutical intermediates, specialty chemicals, and finished formulations. The company supplies a wide range of products across global therapeutic segments and operates exports to over 100 countries, positioning itself as a key player in the international pharma supply chain.

Its operations span manufacturing, backward integration, and R&D, supported by facilities including a new greenfield site in Sayakha, Gujarat. This strategic layout of operations helps the company enhance margins, de-risk supply chains, and broaden its product portfolio across APIs, specialty chemicals, and formulation segments.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Fueled by its robust performance, strong segmental diversity, and aggressive Capex on backward integration and greenfield capacities, Aarti Drugs is well-positioned for sustained growth in FY26. The company’s focus on value-added segments (like formulations and specialty chemicals), coupled with margin expansion initiatives, strengthens its long-term competitive edge.

Name: Medico Remedies Ltd
Overview:
Medico Remedies Ltd, incorporated in 1994, is engaged in manufacturing and selling pharmaceutical formulations. The company focuses on supplying a range of medicinal products that cater to both domestic and international markets, particularly through its export-oriented business.

It has recently expanded its manufacturing capabilities, adding facilities for topical products like ointments and creams and has achieved WHO GMP certification, which is expected to enhance its production standards and market credibility.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company’s upgraded manufacturing capabilities and WHO GMP accreditation could help accelerate revenue growth. Combined with a strengthening export business and profitability momentum, Medico Remedies is well-positioned to further improve FY26 results—potentially achieving double-digit growth in both sales and profit if trends hold steady.

Name: Prakash Pipes Ltd
Overview: Prakash Pipes Ltd is an India-based company primarily engaged in manufacturing polyvinyl chloride (PVC) pipes and fittings, along with flexible packaging products. Established in 1981 by Shri Ved Prakash Agarwal, the company has grown to become a trusted brand in the PVC industry. Prakash Pipes offers a wide range of products, including agri pipes, column pipes, plumbing pipes, casing pipes, SWR pipes, garden pipes, and their related fittings. These products find applications in irrigation, drainage, housing, and sanitation sectors. The company's commitment to quality and innovation has contributed to its strong market presence.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Prakash Pipes Ltd is actively expanding its flexible packaging division, with plans to increase its capacity to 36,000 MT over the next two years. This expansion focuses on extrusion-coated and laminated structures, supported by investments in high-output, high-performance machinery to meet growing demand. Additionally, the company is enhancing its product offerings by developing customized and value-added solutions for customers.

Name: APL Apollo Tubes Ltd
Overview:
APL Apollo Tubes Limited is a leading Indian manufacturer of branded steel products with a strong presence in multiple industrial and infrastructure sectors. Headquartered in Delhi NCR, the company operates 10 manufacturing plants producing over 1,500 varieties of steel tubes including MS Black Pipes, Galvanized and Pre-Galvanized Tubes, and Structural ERW Steel Tubes. These products serve applications in urban infrastructure, housing, irrigation, solar plants, and engineering industries.

The company emphasizes product innovation and premiumization, moving away from commoditized steel to value-added, coated, and specialty tubes. With ongoing capacity expansions and a growing export footprint, APL Apollo aims to capture higher market share domestically and globally, supported by a focus on sustainable operations including renewable energy usage.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

APL Apollo Tubes is executing an ambitious growth strategy to solidify its leadership in the Indian steel tube industry. The company plans to expand its annual production capacity to 6.8 million tonnes by 2028, involving both brownfield and greenfield projects, with an investment of ₹1,500 crore over the next three years. APL Apollo is also focusing on product innovation, having introduced ready-to-use square box sections to accelerate construction timelines and enhance structural efficiency

Name: Astral Ltd
Overview:
Astral Poly Technik Ltd, founded in 1996, is a leading Indian manufacturer in the plumbing and drainage systems industry. Over the years, the company has expanded beyond its core piping business through strategic acquisitions and diversification. Astral’s strong brand equity, extensive distribution network, and innovation-driven product range make it one of India’s most trusted names in plumbing solutions and adhesives.
 
 Business Segments
 Piping Division (77% of Revenue)
 Astral’s core business offers pipes and fittings for plumbing, drainage, agriculture, and industrial applications. Its acquisition of Rex Polyextrusion Pvt Ltd has strengthened its position in corrugated pipes and infrastructure piping.
 
 Adhesives Division (23% of Revenue)
 A robust portfolio of adhesives, sealants, construction chemicals, and related products, catering to both consumer and industrial markets.
 
 New Segments: Faucets, Sanitaryware, and Paints
 
 Recently ventured into Faucets & Sanitaryware, tapping into India’s expanding home improvement and lifestyle segment.
 
 Strategic stake in Gem Paints Pvt Ltd to enter the decorative and industrial paints market — a sizeable adjacent opportunity aligned with the building materials ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Astral Poly Technik is focusing on expanding its product offerings and enhancing manufacturing capabilities to drive future growth. The company plans to introduce new products in the adhesives segment, including tile jointing compounds, and aims to expand its product portfolio from six to 24 categories in the coming years. Astral is also working on increasing its presence in rural markets, targeting at least 20% of sales from these areas. The company is investing in new manufacturing facilities, including a plant near Kanpur, to support its growth initiatives. With a strong emphasis on innovation and market expansion, Astral Poly Technik is positioning itself for sustained growth in the building materials sector.

Name: Maharashtra Seamless Ltd
Overview:
Maharashtra Seamless Ltd (MSL) is a leading manufacturer of seamless and Electric Resistance Welded (ERW) pipes catering mainly to the oil & gas sector, alongside exports to multiple global markets. The company also operates renewable power generation and rig deployment businesses. With a focus on product quality and cost-effective operations, MSL serves a diversified client base but remains closely linked to cyclical capex trends in oil & gas.

MSL’s business has faced margin pressure and volume softness recently, impacted by industry-wide challenges like Chinese dumping and muted oil & gas spending. Despite this, MSL maintains a strong treasury reserve and conservative capital management. Current capex projects aim to improve operational efficiency and product range, but near-term order book visibility remains weak.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
MSL maintains a prudent stance focused on financial conservatism, market leadership, and operational efficiency. Near-term volume and margin improvements are uncertain given weak order inflows. However, management remains prepared to leverage cash reserves for selective growth or value-accretive opportunities, expecting market conditions to gradually improve over time.

Name: Responsive Industries Ltd
Overview:
Responsive Industries Limited, incorporated in 1982, is one of India’s leading manufacturers of PVC-based products. The company offers a wide product range including luxury vinyl planks, vinyl sheets, synthetic leather, ropes, and waterproof membranes, serving industries such as residential, healthcare, transportation, and commercial applications.

It is the largest vinyl flooring producer in India and among the top five globally, with a distribution footprint across more than 70 countries. The company services 25 end-user industries through 400+ distributors and maintains strong relationships with institutional clients such as Indian Railways, Narayana Health, Volvo, and Qatar University.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Responsive Industries has guided for a top line of ₹2,500 crore with EBITDA margins of 22–23% over the next three years. Expansion plans include setting up 100+ customer experience centers and targeting placement of Luxury Vinyl Plank products across 2,500 stores within 3–5 years. The company’s recent product launches in luxury vinyl tiles and hard interlocking tiles are expected to accelerate growth momentum.

Name: Hi-Tech Pipes Ltd
Overview:
Hi-Tech Pipes Limited, established in 1992 and headquartered in New Delhi, is a leading manufacturer and supplier of Electric Resistance Welded (ERW) steel pipes in India. The company specializes in producing a wide range of products, including mild steel hollow section ERW pipes, galvanized steel (GI) pipes, pre-galvanized (GP) pipes, crash barriers, cold-rolled coils and strips, color-coated coils, and roofing sheets. 

With an installed capacity of 1 million tonnes per annum (MTPA), Hi-Tech Pipes operates multiple manufacturing plants across India, including locations in Sikandrabad (Uttar Pradesh), Sanand (Gujarat), Hindupur (Andhra Pradesh), and Khopoli (Maharashtra) . The company has a strong distribution network and serves over 50 original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and more than 800 dealers and distributors across 10 states in India .
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Hi-Tech Pipes aims to expand its manufacturing capacity to 1 million tonnes per annum during FY26, up from the current 7.5 lakh tonnes per annum. The company is focusing on increasing its presence in the renewable energy sector, having secured orders worth ₹105 crore for the supply of ERW steel pipes to be used in wind farms, solar installations, and other green energy projects .

Name: Hariom Pipe Industries Ltd
Overview:
Hariom Pipe Industries Ltd, established in 2008, is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in high-quality steel products. The company operates an integrated facility in Telangana, encompassing sponge iron production, steel rolling, and pipe manufacturing. 

Hariom Pipe's product portfolio includes mild steel (MS) billets, hot-rolled (HR) coils, MS tubes and pipes, galvanized (GI) pipes, galvanized plain (GP) pipes, scaffolding systems, and color-coated roofing profiles. The company adheres to stringent BIS standards, ensuring the delivery of durable and reliable products.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Hariom Pipe Industries aims to achieve a 50% revenue growth, driven by increased demand for its value-added products and expansion into new markets. The ongoing 60 MW solar power project is expected to contribute to cost savings and enhance sustainability.

Name: Jindal Saw Ltd
Overview:
Jindal Saw Ltd. is a global leader in the manufacturing of pipes and related solutions, with a diversified portfolio including LSAW, HSAW, DI, and seamless pipes, along with pellets. It is the world’s third-largest producer of rust-free iron pipes and a key supplier of coated and bare pipe systems globally. The company’s offerings span large-diameter welded pipes, rust-resistant iron pipes, non-welded industrial pipes, stainless-steel pipes in multiple grades, as well as anti-corrosion coating solutions and essential fittings. Complementing its pipe business, Jindal Saw also operates in mining and pellet production, backed by a 50-year mining lease in Rajasthan for 180 Mn tons of low-grade iron ore reserves.

With manufacturing facilities across India, the UAE, and the US, Jindal Saw serves clients in both domestic and international markets, particularly in the MENA and Latin America regions. Its vertically integrated operations, diversified product mix, and strategic global presence have positioned it as a one-stop provider for complete pipe system solutions, while its mining operations ensure raw material security and cost advantages.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
With a robust order book, sustained domestic and export demand, and operational synergies from global facilities, Jindal Saw is positioned for stable growth. Expansion in high-value segments, strategic debt reduction, and secure raw material sourcing from captive mines will support profitability and market leadership in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Prince Pipes & Fittings Ltd
Overview:
Prince Pipes and Fittings Limited is a fully integrated piping solutions and multi-polymer manufacturer, offering products across CPVC, UPVC, HDPE, and PPR categories. Since its establishment in 1987, the company has expanded from PVC manufacturing to a diversified portfolio covering plumbing, sewage, irrigation, storage, cable ducting, and bathware solutions under multiple brands.

With strategically located manufacturing facilities across India, the company caters to agriculture, infrastructure, and building material markets. Its focus on product innovation, brand visibility, and capacity expansion particularly in underpenetrated regions supports its growth strategy while maintaining operational resilience and a strong distribution network.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Prince Pipes and Fittings Ltd is strategically focusing on expanding its manufacturing capacity and diversifying its product offerings to drive future growth. The company has commissioned Phase 1 of its new plant in Begusarai, Bihar, adding 24,000 metric tonnes to its annual capacity, with plans to reach 60,000 metric tonnes in the coming months. Additionally, Prince Pipes is enhancing its product mix by increasing the share of higher-margin CPVC pipes and venturing into the bathware segment, which contributed ₹30 crore in revenue in FY25

Name: Stylam Industries Ltd
Overview:
Stylam Industries focuses on making high-quality decorative laminates under the “STYLAM” brand. The company caters primarily to markets in Europe and Southeast Asia and offers a broad product portfolio including specialty surfaces, PU+ lacquer coatings, solid surfaces, and compact laminates.

In recent years, Stylam has also expanded into the acrylic solid surface segment and is investing in new, large-format manufacturing facilities. These strategic moves aim to bolster its export leadership and broaden its product range for long-term growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Stylam Industries is actively enhancing its manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand for decorative laminates. The company has undertaken a significant expansion project, increasing its laminate production capacity by an additional 1,000 metric tonnes per month. This expansion is supported by a capital expenditure of ₹150 crore, aimed at bolstering exports to European and Southeast Asian markets.

Name: Man Industries (India) Ltd
Overview:
Man Industries Ltd is a leading manufacturer and exporter of large-diameter steel pipes, particularly LSAW and HSAW types, used extensively in oil, gas, and infrastructure sectors. The company boasts a strong manufacturing capacity and over three decades of experience, making it a key player in India's steel pipe industry.

With a substantial installed capacity and a solid export footprint, Man Industries plays a crucial role in both domestic infrastructure projects and international pipeline engineering markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Man Industries Ltd is poised for significant expansion, targeting a 30–35% revenue growth from its existing facilities in the upcoming fiscal year, with projections reaching up to 40% as new plants commence operations. The company is investing approximately ₹1,100–1,150 crore in two major greenfield projects: a ₹600 crore facility in Saudi Arabia and a ₹550 crore plant in Jammu and Kashmir, both slated to begin production by the third quarter of the next fiscal year

Name: Rama Steel Tubes Ltd
Overview:
Rama Steel Tubes Ltd is an India-based company engaged in the business of manufacturing steel pipes, tubes, and galvanized iron pipes. The company offers a range of products, including scaffolding pipes and tubes, ERW galvanized steel pipes and tubes, hollow sections, structural steel products, and casing swaged poles. Rama Steel Tubes Ltd manufactures and exports electric resistance weld (ERW) steel tubes.

Incorporated in 1974, the company has established itself as a leading manufacturer in the steel pipe industry. Rama Steel Tubes Ltd caters to various sectors, providing high-quality steel products to meet diverse industrial needs.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Rama Steel Tubes Ltd is actively expanding its operations and diversifying its business portfolio to capitalize on emerging opportunities. The company is enhancing its product offerings in the steel pipes and tubes sector, aiming to meet the growing demand in construction, infrastructure, and real estate industries. Additionally, Rama Steel Tubes is venturing into the renewable energy sector by acquiring a 10% stake in a 225 MW solar power project under the PM-KUSUM scheme, signaling its commitment to sustainable energy initiatives.

Name: Hardwyn India Ltd
Overview:
Hardwyn India Ltd, established in 2017 and headquartered in New Delhi, specializes in the wholesale and retail trade of architectural hardware, glass fittings, kitchen hardware, accessories, and appliances. The company offers a wide range of products catering to both residential and commercial structures, under the Hardwyn brand. With a focus on quality and innovation, Hardwyn India aims to provide comprehensive solutions for modern living spaces. 

Hardwyn India reported annual revenue of ₹185 crore, reflecting a growth of 20.78% compared to the previous year. The company's net income improved by 9.72% to ₹11.23 crore during the same period. These financial results underscore Hardwyn India's commitment to expanding its market presence and enhancing shareholder value.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Hardwyn India aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing its market presence. The company plans to leverage its strong brand equity and operational efficiencies to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the architectural hardware and home improvement sectors.

Name: Apollo Pipes Ltd
Overview:
Apollo Pipes Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in the production and trading of CPVC, uPVC, and HDPE pipes, along with associated products like PVC taps, fittings, water storage tanks, and solvents. The company caters to a diverse range of industrial applications, including agriculture, water management, construction, infrastructure, and telecom ducting.

As one of India's top 10 piping solution providers, Apollo Pipes has established a strong presence in both domestic and international markets, focusing on delivering high-quality products to meet the growing demands of various sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Apollo Pipes is focusing on expanding its product portfolio with new additions like PLVs DWC pipe, P/E gas pipe, and PVCO pipe. The company is ramping up production at its West India plant and is on track to commence operations at a new plant in Varanasi, strengthening its presence in the eastern Indian market.

Name: Hindware Home Innovation Ltd
Overview:
Hindware Home Innovation Ltd is a leading provider of building products and consumer appliances in India. Its portfolio spans bathware, premium tiles, plastic pipes & fittings, and modern living appliances under the Hindware Smart Appliances brand. The company also operates a joint venture, Hintastica Pvt Ltd, for manufacturing advanced water heaters.

Leveraging its strong brand and wide product offerings, Hindware serves both residential and institutional markets, positioning itself as a comprehensive home solutions provider.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Hindware’s focus on repositioning into higher-end product segments and optimizing its distribution footprint signals better margins ahead. The ramp-up at the new Uttarakhand plant will enhance capacity in Northern India, supporting growth. If current strategies hold, FY26 should witness gradual improvement in both topline momentum and profitability metrics.

Name: Finolex Industries Ltd
Overview:
Finolex Industries Limited (FIL) is a leading manufacturer of PVC resin and one of India’s largest producers of PVC pipes and fittings, with a strong presence in agriculture, construction, and industrial applications. It is the only major backward-integrated player in the Indian market, producing most of its PVC resin requirements in-house. The company operates four manufacturing facilities across Ratnagiri, Urse, Masar, and Bhadalwad, supported by a wide distribution network of over 900 dealers and 30,000 retailers.

The company’s product portfolio covers over 2,000 SKUs, including ASTM, SWR, CPVC, and sewerage pipes, agricultural PVC-U pipes, and a range of fittings. Its operations are significantly tilted toward pipes and fittings, which now account for nearly all revenues, as the PVC resin segment caters mainly to internal consumption. FIL maintains a strong liquidity position with substantial free cash reserves, enabling it to fund capacity expansions and marketing initiatives without relying heavily on debt.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
With a robust order book, sustained domestic and export demand, and operational synergies from global facilities, Jindal Saw is positioned for stable growth. Expansion in high-value segments, strategic debt reduction, and secure raw material sourcing from captive mines will support profitability and market leadership in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Ratnamani Metals & Tubes Ltd
Overview:
Ratnamani Metals & Tubes Ltd. is a leading Indian manufacturer of stainless steel and carbon steel pipes and tubes. The company supplies a wide range of products, including seamless and welded tubes, pipe bends, titanium welded tubes, and spooling solutions. These products cater to critical sectors such as oil and gas, nuclear and thermal power, refineries, petrochemicals, and chemicals. The company has maintained its leadership position in the stainless steel seamless segment with a 61,500 TPA capacity, and it also operates 510,000 TPA capacity in carbon steel pipes.

Ratnamani has three major segments: steel tubes and pipes (93% of revenue), bearing rings (6%) through its subsidiary Ravi Technoforge, and pipe spooling (1%) via a JV with Technoenergy AG. The company has a strong domestic base, but has expanded globally, exporting to over 30 countries. In FY25, the company achieved record consolidated revenues and remains focused on high-margin, value-added products, including a significant push into spooling, stainless steel boiler tubes, and nuclear applications.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ratnamani Metals & Tubes Ltd is strategically enhancing its manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand in the stainless steel and carbon steel pipe sectors. The company is investing in capacity expansion projects across its facilities in Orissa, subsidiaries, and a new joint venture in Saudi Arabia, aiming for significant capital expenditures over the next one to two years.

Name: Cool Caps Industries Ltd
Overview:
Cool Caps Industries Ltd is a specialist manufacturer of plastic caps and closures, serving diverse packaging needs for industries such as beverages, pharmaceuticals, and consumer goods in India and abroad. The company focuses on technical precision and consistent quality, leveraging strong manufacturing capabilities to meet the evolving demands of its clientele.

Beyond just producing caps, Cool Caps also engages in trading allied products that complement its core offerings. Through a blend of domestic distribution channels and export outreach, the company ensures product accessibility while building its position within the packaging supply chain.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With a solid operational recovery and improving profitability, Cool Caps Industries is well-positioned for continued momentum in FY26. Should strong demand for packaged goods sustain and operational efficiencies improve further, the company could expand its margins and reinforce its leadership in the caps and closures segment.

Name: B&B Triplewall Containers Ltd
Overview:
B&B Triplewall Containers Ltd is a packaging solutions provider specializing in corrugated boxes and boards. It caters to industries like FMCG, e-commerce, pharma, and industrial products. The company has a strong footprint in Southern India with growing national reach.

Its focus on quality, customization, and sustainability has helped build a wide customer base. By leveraging modern manufacturing facilities, the company continues to expand its capacity and product mix.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With its early footprint in automation and commitment to precision, B&B is well-placed to benefit from continued demand for packaging solutions in FY26. If cost pressures are contained and revenue momentum sustains, the company could stabilize or improve margins in upcoming quarters, further strengthening its position in the packaging market.

Name: Wim Plast Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1988, Wim Plast Ltd is an Indian company specializing in the manufacturing of various plastic products. The company offers a diverse range of products, including plastic molded furniture, extrusion sheets, air coolers, dustbins, industrial pallets, and industrial and engineering molds. Wim Plast operates under the well-known brand 'Cello,' which is recognized for its high-quality plastic products. 

The company has a significant presence in the Indian market and is expanding its reach internationally. Headquartered in Daman, India, Wim Plast has established manufacturing facilities in key locations such as Daman, Baddi, and Chennai.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Wim Plast Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product range and enhancing its manufacturing capabilities. The company's focus on quality, innovation, and operational efficiency is expected to drive its performance in the coming years.

Name: NLC India Ltd
Overview:
NLC India Limited, a Navratna Public Sector Undertaking under the Ministry of Coal, is primarily involved in lignite mining and power generation, including both thermal and renewable energy sources. The company operates 5 lignite-based thermal power stations, 1 coal-based power plant through a subsidiary, and renewable installations including 1,380 MW of solar and 51 MW of wind capacity, making it the first CPSE to cross 1 GW of solar power generation.

In mining, NLC India runs 4 opencast lignite mines and 1 coal mine with a combined installed capacity of over 50 MTPA. Over time, it has diversified into critical minerals, clean energy technologies, and green hydrogen. Its operations span Tamil Nadu, Rajasthan, and Odisha, with ambitious expansion plans through FY30 across mining, thermal, and renewable segments, backed by significant investments and strategic subsidiary formations.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

NLC India is actively pursuing a comprehensive growth strategy to diversify its energy portfolio and enhance its market presence. The company is focusing on expanding its renewable energy capacity, aiming to increase its installed capacity from 1.4 GW to 10 GW by 2030, with a longer-term target of 32 GW by 2047. In addition to its renewable energy initiatives, NLC India is also exploring opportunities in critical mineral mining to support its energy projects

Name: Premier Energies Ltd
Overview:
Premier Energies Limited, incorporated in April 1995, is one of India’s leading integrated solar PV manufacturers, with expertise spanning solar cells, solar modules, monofacial and bifacial modules, as well as EPC and O&M solutions. The company holds a dominant share in India’s solar cell exports and was the first Indian manufacturer to produce TOPCon cells, positioning itself at the forefront of advanced PV technology.

The company has built a strong domestic market focus, supported by robust demand drivers, government policy tailwinds, and a disciplined execution track record. With ongoing capacity expansions across solar, battery energy storage systems (BESS), and inverters, Premier Energies is aligning its growth strategy with India’s clean energy transition goals while maintaining profitability and operational resilience.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Premier Energies expects strong demand growth in FY26 and beyond, driven by policy support, rising DCR adoption, and structural capacity constraints in cells. Management projects tight market conditions for at least the next two to three years, supporting stable pricing and margins, while expansion into new verticals positions the company for sustained leadership in India’s renewable energy ecosystem.

Name: Godawari Power & Ispat Ltd
Overview:
Godawari Power & Ispat Limited (GPIL) is an integrated steel manufacturer with operations spanning captive iron ore mining, pellet making, sponge iron, steel billets, wire rods, HB wire, and ferro alloys, supported by in-house power generation. The company’s strong backward integration through captive mines provides significant cost advantages and secures raw material supply for its steel operations.

Alongside its core steel and mining business, GPIL is expanding into value-added steel products and battery energy storage systems (BESS) to diversify revenue streams. Its strategic focus remains on high-margin products, technological collaboration, and capacity enhancement while maintaining a conservative leverage position.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to capitalize on India’s strong steel demand, ramp up value-added product volumes, and establish a leadership position in the nascent BESS market. Focus will remain on operational efficiency, capacity expansion, and disciplined capital allocation to ensure sustainable growth through FY26 and beyond.

Name: CESC Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1978, CESC Limited is India’s first fully integrated power utility, engaged in both electricity generation and distribution. Headquartered in Kolkata, the company operates five thermal power plants with a total installed capacity of 2,140 MW, catering to the majority of the power requirements in its licensed areas of Kolkata and Greater Noida. Through multiple distribution licenses and franchisees across Rajasthan, Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh, and Chandigarh, CESC serves over 4.7 million customers across 1,454 sq. km.

The company is part of the RP-Sanjiv Goenka Group, a diversified conglomerate with a strong presence across power, consumer & retail, education, infrastructure, sports, chemicals, IT-enabled services, and media & entertainment. Beyond thermal generation, CESC is expanding its renewable energy footprint through Purvah Green Power, targeting 3,200 MW of renewable capacity by FY29 under Phase 1, with the long-term goal of 10,000 MW.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
CESC is positioned to benefit from India’s growing electricity demand, driven by urbanization, data centers, and EV adoption. With regulated returns in core distribution areas, aggressive renewable capacity addition, and sustained efficiency gains, the company aims to strengthen its leadership in the integrated power utility space while diversifying into clean energy.

Name: Reliance Power Ltd
Overview:
Reliance Power Ltd. is a prominent Indian power generation company engaged in the development, construction, and operation of various power projects, both domestically and internationally. The company operates a diverse portfolio encompassing coal, gas, hydro, wind, and solar-based energy projects. Its flagship assets include the Sasan Ultra Mega Power Project (3,960 MW) in Madhya Pradesh and the Rosa Power Plant (1,200 MW) in Uttar Pradesh, which have demonstrated strong operational performance.

In FY25, Reliance Power achieved a significant financial turnaround, reporting a consolidated net profit of ₹2,947 crore, reversing the previous year's loss. This remarkable recovery was primarily driven by improved operational efficiency, higher power demand, and strategic initiatives. The company also achieved zero bank debt status, reflecting its strengthened financial position and commitment to reducing leverage.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Reliance Power aims to maintain its operational excellence and financial discipline. The company is exploring opportunities to expand its renewable energy portfolio, aligning with the global shift towards sustainable energy sources. Additionally, Reliance Power plans to leverage its strong balance sheet to fund future growth initiatives, ensuring long-term value creation for its stakeholders.

Name: Waaree Renewable Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1999, Waaree Renewables Technologies Ltd is engaged in generating power through renewable energy sources and providing consultancy services in the sector. A subsidiary of the Waaree Group, the company is a leading solar EPC solutions provider in India with a significant global footprint. Waaree Energy is among the largest vertically integrated new energy companies in the country, boasting India’s largest solar panel manufacturing capacity of 12 GW across its plants in Chikhli, Surat, and Umbergaon, Gujarat.

The company operates mainly through two segments—EPC, which accounts for the majority of revenues, and Power Sale. Its EPC solutions cover both on-site solar projects (rooftop and ground-mounted) and off-site solar farms, along with products like inverters, lithium-ion batteries, solar thermal systems, and more. Waaree serves prominent industrial, commercial, and utility clients, maintains a strong O&M portfolio, and exports to over 68 countries worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook

Management expects to execute 1.8–2 GW of projects in FY25, potentially doubling revenues to ₹2,500–2,800 crore. Growth will be supported by execution of the existing order book, robust bidding pipeline, and sector tailwinds from India’s renewable energy targets. Strategic focus areas include expanding BESS EPC orders, entering the Middle East EPC market, and building capabilities in green hydrogen and data centers. The company aims to maintain EBITDA margins in the 14–16% range, with upside potential from operational efficiencies and premium project mix.

Name: K.P. Energy Ltd
Overview:
K.P. Energy Ltd is a Gujarat-based renewable energy company specializing in the development and operation of wind and hybrid power projects. Established in 2010, the company has expanded its footprint across India, focusing on sustainable and clean energy solutions.

The company operates through multiple business segments, including Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC), Operations & Maintenance (O&M), and Independent Power Producer (IPP) models. K.P. Energy Ltd continues to explore opportunities in the renewable energy sector to drive growth and profitability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
K.P. Energy Ltd aims to expand its footprint in the renewable energy sector by focusing on the development of wind and hybrid power projects. The company plans to leverage its strong order book and expertise in EPC and O&M services to drive growth and profitability. With a commitment to sustainable energy solutions, K.P. Energy Ltd is poised to play a significant role in India’s transition to clean energy.

Name: Ujaas Energy Ltd
Overview:
Ujaas Energy Ltd, established in 1999, is a leading Indian company specializing in the generation of solar power and the manufacturing, sales, and services of solar power plants and electric vehicles (EVs). Headquartered in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, Ujaas Energy has been at the forefront of promoting renewable energy solutions across the country. The company has successfully commissioned over 105 MW of solar power plants, contributing significantly to India's renewable energy capacity 

In addition to its solar power initiatives, Ujaas Energy has ventured into the electric vehicle sector, offering a range of electric two-wheelers designed for efficiency and cost-effectiveness. These vehicles are tailored to meet the growing demand for sustainable transportation options in urban areas.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ujaas Energy Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company is focusing on expanding its solar power generation capacity by undertaking large-scale projects across various regions. Additionally, Ujaas Energy aims to strengthen its position in the electric vehicle market by introducing new models and expanding its distribution network. The company is also exploring opportunities for international expansion to tap into global markets.

Name: Jyoti Structures Ltd
Overview:
Jyoti Structures Ltd is a leading EPC company focused on transmission line towers, substation structures, and railway electrification solutions. Operating domestically and internationally, it delivers turnkey power infrastructure projects for high-voltage transmission and distribution networks.

Founded in 1975, Jyoti Structures has earned a strong reputation for executing complex projects on time and within budget. Its advanced manufacturing facilities, skilled workforce, and commitment to quality, safety, and sustainability make it a preferred partner for government and private clients across geographies.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company plans to expand into new geographies and diversify projects. Adoption of sustainable practices and advanced technologies is underway. Strategic partnerships and innovative project delivery models will support long-term growth and global market positioning.

Name: Gujarat Industries Power Co Ltd
Overview:
GIPCL is a government-promoted, publicly listed power generation company incorporated in 1985 and based in Vadodara, Gujarat. It generates electricity from a diversified mix of thermal (lignite- and gas-based) and renewable (wind and solar) assets. The company also operates captive lignite and limestone mines to fuel its Surat lignite plant 

Its current total installed capacity stands at around 1,184 MW, comprised of a 500 MW Surat lignite power plant (2 × 250 MW), a 310 MW gas-based combined cycle plant, plus approximately 442 MW of solar and 112.4 MW of wind capacity.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Gujarat Industries is moving ahead with bold steps for future growth by significantly expanding its clean energy footprint. The company is developing a massive renewable energy park in Kutch, featuring over 1,100 MW of solar capacity and plans for hybrid wind–solar installations, alongside exploring green hydrogen and pumped storage solutions. It’s also investing in modernizing its thermal assets with life-extension and renovation efforts at its lignite power facility, ensuring steady operations.

Name: Transrail Lighting Ltd
Overview:
Transrail Lighting Ltd is a prominent EPC (engineering, procurement, and construction) company specializing in power transmission, distribution, and lighting infrastructure. With decades of experience, it provides end-to-end solutions spanning design, manufacturing, installation, and commissioning.

The company also supplies steel poles, transmission towers, and hardware, ensuring integration of manufacturing capabilities with EPC services. Its strong presence in domestic infrastructure and overseas markets makes it a versatile player in the power and lighting ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Transrail Lighting Ltd is poised for sustained growth, underpinned by a robust order book exceeding ₹15,900 crore, reflecting a 44% year-on-year increase. The company is actively expanding its manufacturing capacities, including a significant brownfield expansion of tower, conductor, and pole production facilities, aimed at enhancing output to meet rising demand. Strategic investments are being made in emerging markets, smart lighting, and sustainable energy solutions, positioning Transrail for long-term success.

Name: RattanIndia Power Ltd
Overview:
RattanIndia Power Limited is among India’s largest private power producers, operating thermal power plants located in Maharashtra at Amravati and Nashik. With robust thermal generation capacity, it plays a significant role in the state's energy landscape, supplying the majority of electricity through long-term power purchase agreements (PPAs) while selling limited quantities on the open market.

The company integrates power production with trading, capitalizing on both regulated and merchant segments. Its business model emphasizes leveraging installed thermal capacity and securing stable revenue via PPAs, while also tapping into market opportunities for supplemental sales.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Recovery in power demand and better operational dispatch could support a return to profitability. While the PPA-backed core provides stability, improving electricity demand and optimizing merchant sales could bolster revenue. The company’s ability to manage fuel costs and leverage its installed capacity will drive performance in FY26.

Name: India Power Corporation Ltd
Overview:
India Power Corporation Ltd (IPCL), established in 1919, is a prominent player in India's power sector, focusing on electricity distribution, renewable energy generation, and smart grid solutions. 

Headquartered in Kolkata, the company operates in the Eastern and Northern regions, serving a substantial customer base through its distribution networks. IPCL's commitment to sustainable energy is evident in its significant investments in renewable energy projects, aligning with India's growing focus on clean energy.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
IPCL aims to strengthen its position in the renewable energy sector by increasing its capacity and exploring new markets. The company's focus on smart grid technologies is expected to improve service reliability and customer satisfaction.

Name: Torrent Power Ltd
Overview:
Torrent Power Ltd, part of the diversified Torrent Group, is one of India’s leading integrated power utility companies. The company is engaged across the entire power value chain, including generation, transmission, and distribution, serving millions of customers with reliable power supply. Torrent Power’s robust portfolio includes gas-based and coal-based thermal power plants with a total operational generation capacity of 4,100 MW, further supported by an aggregate installed capacity of 4,600 MW, which includes growing renewable assets.
  Business Segments
 Generation:
 Operates thermal power plants in Gujarat with significant capacity in gas and coal segments. Expanding aggressively into renewables, with 156 MW wind and 75 MW solar projects added recently, plus 565 MW renewable capacity under development.
 
 Distribution:
 Supplies electricity with high reliability (over 99.9% availability) in licensed regions including Ahmedabad, Gandhinagar, Surat, and Dahej SEZ. Also expanding its footprint through a dedicated distribution network in Dholera Special Investment Region (DSIR) spanning 920 sq. km.
 
 Transmission:
 Owns and operates transmission lines and associated infrastructure to support seamless supply from generation to distribution zones.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Torrent Power is focusing on expanding its renewable energy capacity, aiming to achieve 10 GW by 2030. The company is investing in large-scale projects, including a 1,600 MW thermal plant in Madhya Pradesh and a 300 MW wind project in Gujarat. Additionally, Torrent Power is exploring energy storage solutions, such as pumped storage projects and battery-integrated renewable energy systems, to enhance grid reliability. The company is also venturing into green hydrogen production, with plans for a 1 lakh KTPA green ammonia facility. These initiatives are part of Torrent Power's commitment to sustainable growth and contributing to India's renewable energy goals.

Name: Adani Energy Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Adani Energy Solutions Ltd (AESL) is India’s largest private sector power transmission and distribution company. It establishes, operates, and maintains power transmission systems with a strong footprint across western and northern India. The company also lays optical fibers along its transmission lines to offer telecom solutions for telcos and ISPs.
 
Business Segments
 Power Transmission: Contributes 31% of total revenue; operates over 14,100 circuit km of lines and 20,400 MVA of transformation capacity.
 
 Power Distribution: Contributes 62% of total revenue; distributes power to over 3 million customers, with operations extending into key urban clusters like Mumbai and parts of western Uttar Pradesh.
 
 Optical Fiber/Telecom Solutions: Small but growing diversification segment leveraging existing transmission infrastructure.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Adani Energy Solutions Ltd. is targeting major capacity expansion, aiming to scale its transmission assets to 30,000 circuit km and reach 4.5 MVA/customer distribution capacity by 2026. The company is making strategic investments in digital infrastructure, including the aggressive rollout of smart meters and telecom fiber solutions, diversifying its revenue base beyond power. A $700 million international funding secured for the Kudus–Aarey HVDC transmission project underlines its focus on supporting renewable energy integration.

Name: NTPC Ltd
Overview:
NTPC Limited, India’s largest power generation company, has an installed capacity of 69,134 MW, supplying about 24% of India’s total electricity demand. It primarily generates and sells bulk power to state utilities, while also diversifying into consultancy, energy trading, coal mining, and oil & gas exploration.
 
 Installed Capacity Mix
 Coal: 52.7% — mainstay of NTPC’s generation fleet.
 
 Renewables: 27.5% — rapidly growing share.
 
 Hydro: 11.7% — strengthening low-emission base.
 
 Gas: 6.2% — flexible capacity to meet peak demands.
 Strategic Vision & Growth Targets
 Capacity Expansion: Target to double capacity from 63 GW to 130 GW by 2032.
 
 Green Transition:
 
 Non-fossil fuels: Aim to raise share from 7% to 30% by 2032.
 
 Renewable Energy: Install 60 GW of RE capacity by 2032, with a medium-term goal of 35 GW by 2027.
 
 Energy Efficiency: Committed to a 10% reduction in net energy intensity by 2032.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
NTPC is focusing on significant capital expenditure to expand renewable energy capacity and modernize thermal plants, supported by favorable policy and regulatory initiatives for green energy and emissions reduction. Rising power demand across India, driven by economic growth and electrification, presents opportunities for sustained expansion. The company is also venturing into ancillary services, including e-mobility infrastructure, battery storage, and power trading, while investing in innovation and digitalization to optimize grid management and operational efficiency.

Name: Tata Power Company Ltd
Overview:
Tata Power Company Ltd is one of India’s largest integrated power companies, transitioning from conventional generation to a consumer-focused clean energy company. Its operations span generation (thermal, hydro, solar, wind), transmission and distribution, rooftop solar, solar pumps, microgrids, EV charging infrastructure, home automation, and energy services. With strong commitments to sustainability, the company aims for 100% clean and green energy before 2045, while playing a pivotal role in India’s renewable and electric mobility ecosystem.
 
Business Segments 
 
 Renewable Energy (solar, wind, hydro): ~37% of total capacity currently; target of 70% by 2030.
 
 Transmission & Distribution (power supply to cities, towns): Major share of revenue through distribution licenses in Mumbai, Delhi, Odisha, and other regions.
 
 Consumer-centric Solutions: Solar rooftops, solar pumps, microgrids, and battery storage are fast-growing segments.
 
 EV Charging & Energy Services: Emerging share; target to expand to 25,000 EV charging points by 2028–30.
 
 Off-grid Products & EPC: Includes solar EPC, off-grid solar pumps, inverters, and storage solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Tata Power is well-positioned for long-term growth through its ambitious clean energy and ESG roadmap, targeting 80% renewable energy by 2030 and carbon neutrality by 2045. Expansion of EV charging infrastructure, rooftop solar projects, and distributed energy solutions—including microgrids, solar pumps, and energy management services—supports steady revenue and strengthens its leadership in India’s electric mobility and green energy sectors.

Name: Power Grid Corporation of India Ltd
Overview:
Power Grid Corporation of India Limited (PGCIL) is India’s largest electric power transmission company, operating under the Ministry of Power. Established in 1989, it plays a pivotal role in the planning, construction, operation, and maintenance of the Inter-State Transmission System (ISTS), overseeing approximately 85% of India’s interstate transmission network and transmitting about 45% of the country’s total power generation.
 
 Business Segments and Revenue Contribution (FY2025)
 Transmission Operations:
 Constitutes the core business, contributing around 85-90% of total revenue. This includes interstate transmission services, operation and maintenance of transmission assets, and system strengthening projects. Revenues are primarily from regulated tariff queues set by the Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC).
 
 Telecommunication Services:
 Makes up roughly 5-7% of revenues, leveraging the extensive optic fiber network deployed along transmission lines to provide telecom bandwidth and connectivity services.
 
 Consultancy and Other Services:
 Account for about 3-5%, encompassing project consultancy, design, and engineering services both domestically and internationally.
 
 Total Revenue:
 Estimated to be between ₹18,500 crore to ₹20,000 crore, marking steady growth supported by commissioned projects and new capacity additions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
The company is set to expand its transmission infrastructure through a robust project pipeline, including ₹4,870 crore in renewable-focused and system-strengthening projects. Investments in energy storage aim to balance intermittent renewable generation and ensure grid stability as India targets 500 GW of non-fossil energy. Expansion of a nationwide fast EV charging network supports green mobility objectives, while technological upgrades such as smart grids, automation, and digital control rooms enhance operational efficiency and reduce transmission losses.

Name: Siemens Energy India Ltd
Overview:
Siemens Energy India Ltd is an energy technology company that delivers integrated solutions across the power value chain. Its offerings include products and services for power generation (gas turbines, steam turbines, hybrid/hydrogen-enabled plants), power transmission (transformers, high-voltage switchgear, etc.), energy storage, and related infrastructure. SEIL works with customers and partners to support the energy transition and to build sustainable, resilient energy systems.

SEIL was recently demerged from Siemens Ltd’s energy business, becoming a separately listed entity. It now focuses on scaling both its transmission and generation segments, with manufacturing expansion, order inflows, and strategic capacity building being key parts of its growth strategy.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Siemens Energy India is stepping up capacity expansion, especially for high-voltage switchgear, to meet rising demand both in India and abroad. The company is leveraging a strong order backlog and healthy new order inflows, which gives it good visibility of future revenue streams. Operating efficiencies and margin expansion are expected as utilisation increases in its factories that have room to scale up.  Also, “Make in India” and export-oriented strategies are being prioritised, with investment to localise manufacturing.

Name: JSW Energy Ltd
Overview:
JSW Energy Ltd is a key player in India’s power sector, with a diversified portfolio spanning coal, lignite, hydro, thermal, solar, and gas-based power generation. The company operates multiple plants across Karnataka, Maharashtra, Nandyal, and Salboni, and is rapidly expanding its green energy footprint.
 
 Business Segments
 Thermal Power: Major capacity share through coal and lignite-based plants.
 
 Hydropower: Operating large-scale hydroelectric plants as part of renewable mix.
 
 Solar & Wind Energy: Expanding blended renewable capacity via new projects.
 
 Battery Storage & Green Hydrogen: New ventures in storage solutions and hydrogen production.
 
 Module Manufacturing: Plans for solar module production under PLI scheme.
 
 Revenue Mix
 Currently, 81% of total capacity is secured under long-term Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), of which 98% are take-or-pay, ensuring predictable cash flows. The remaining capacity is sold on short-term or merchant basis. Renewable and hydro power make up 30% of installed capacity today, with a clear target to push this to 60–70% in the medium term.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

JSW Energy aims to double its capacity from 4.6 GW to 10 GW in the medium term, with a strategic shift toward clean energy. It plans to raise the share of renewables and hydro power to 60–70% of its total mix, supported by Rs 5,965 crore in upcoming green energy projects. The company is actively investing in energy storage and green hydrogen initiatives under India’s clean energy policies and PLI schemes, positioning itself to capitalize on the rising demand for sustainable power solutions and reduced emissions.

Name: Adani Power Ltd
Overview:
Adani Power Ltd., the largest private thermal power producer in India, operates a portfolio of thermal and expanding renewable energy assets. The company primarily sells power through long-term Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), short-term PPAs, and merchant sales, with a strategic focus on maximizing the stability and visibility offered by long-term contracts.
 
 Business Segments and Revenue (FY2025)
 Total Revenue: ₹56,203 crore for the fiscal year ended March 2025.
 
 Thermal Power Generation: Remains the core revenue generator, accounting for nearly all of the company's earnings, as the company’s installed and operational portfolio is still dominated by coal-based thermal power assets.
 
 Long-term PPAs: Approximately 78% of gross capacity is secured by long-term PPAs as of FY2025, ensuring revenue stability.
 
 Power Generated: Over 100 billion units (BU) of electricity generated in FY2025, a new record for any Indian private sector power producer.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Adani Power is set to expand its installed capacity beyond 24 GW by 2030 through brownfield expansions and new ultra-supercritical plants, enhancing efficiency and reducing emissions. The company is pursuing technological leadership, operational optimization, and digitalization to drive profitability and resilience. Plans for renewable energy investments and strategic acquisitions aim to diversify its portfolio and consolidate market presence. Internationally, projects like the Bangladesh export initiative underscore its ambitions to grow beyond India while balancing power demand with sustainability objectives.

Name: PTC India Ltd
Overview:
PTC India Limited, founded in 1999 as a government-backed public-private initiative, is a leading power trading company in India. It engages in long-term, medium-term, and short-term trading of electricity, including cross-border power exchanges with Nepal, Bhutan, and Bangladesh. Its portfolio spans both conventional and renewable energy, supported by strategic stakes in the Hindustan Power Exchange and PTC Financial Services.

The company plays a vital role in balancing supply-demand mismatches in the Indian power sector while exploring emerging opportunities such as market coupling, market-based economic dispatch, and virtual power purchase agreements. PTC continues to strengthen its position through increased trading volumes, cross-border collaborations, and active participation in energy market reforms.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
PTC aims to sustain trading volume growth above industry levels, capitalizing on reforms, renewable opportunities, and cross-border trade expansion. The company expects HPX and PFS to enhance long-term earnings, while new market mechanisms like MBED and virtual PPAs could significantly boost trading volumes and margins from FY27 onward.

Name: RattanIndia Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
RattanIndia Enterprises Limited is a technology-driven company focused on new age sectors including e-commerce, electric vehicles (EVs), fintech, and drone technology. The company aims to build scalable businesses leveraging digital innovation across its diversified portfolio. Its key ventures include Cocoblu and Neobrand in e-commerce, Revolt in electric vehicles, a fintech arm, and Neosky in drone technology, serving both domestic and international markets.

The company has witnessed significant growth in its key segments, driven by rapid expansion in vendor partnerships in e-commerce and dealership network growth in electric vehicles. It is actively exploring global opportunities in the drone business while fintech has achieved profitability, reflecting operational progress and a diversified business model aimed at long-term value creation.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
RattanIndia Enterprises is positioned for sustained growth, supported by continued expansion in EV dealerships, aggressive scaling in e-commerce platforms, and strategic forays into drone technology globally. The company’s innovation-driven approach and diversified portfolio are expected to unlock new opportunities, driving revenue and market share gains in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Indian Energy Exchange Ltd
Overview:
Indian Energy Exchange (IEX) is India’s first and largest power trading platform, providing a transparent and technology-driven marketplace for electricity, renewable energy certificates (RECs), and energy-saving certificates (ESCerts). The company plays a critical role in enabling real-time and day-ahead electricity trading, as well as facilitating renewable energy integration through its specialized market segments.

Alongside its core power exchange, IEX has diversified into natural gas trading via the Indian Gas Exchange (IGX) and carbon credit trading through the International Carbon Exchange (ICX). With a strong track record of market leadership, robust technology, and product innovation, the company continues to expand its offerings in line with evolving energy market needs and India’s clean energy transition goals.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
IEX is positioned to benefit from India’s projected 6% annual power demand growth until 2032, increased renewable energy penetration, and expansion of battery energy storage systems. Management expects sustained growth in RTM, Green Markets, and IGX volumes, supported by favorable policies, technology-driven solutions, and continued product innovation.

Name: Kalpataru Projects International Ltd
Overview:
Kalpataru Projects International Ltd. (KPIL), formerly Kalpataru Power Transmission Ltd., is a global EPC player with over four decades of experience in executing complex infrastructure and energy projects. The company operates across six high-growth segments—Power Transmission & Distribution (T&D), Buildings & Factories (B&F), Oil & Gas, Water, Railways, and Urban Infrastructure—serving diverse markets in more than 75 countries. Its strong in-house design, engineering, and manufacturing capabilities enable end-to-end project execution, backed by state-of-the-art facilities such as 240,000 MTPA tower manufacturing capacity.

KPIL has built a robust global presence with live projects in over 30 countries, handling 250+ ongoing projects. For FY25, the company reported consolidated revenue of ₹22,316 crore and an order book of ₹64,495 crore, while in Q1 FY26, the order book expanded to ₹65,475 crore with ₹9,899 crore in new order wins. Supported by a diversified customer base and strong execution credentials, KPIL continues to focus on scaling operations while maintaining financial discipline.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
FY26 growth will be supported by a record order book, strong execution momentum in T&D, B&F, and Oil & Gas, and continued focus on international expansion. Margin improvement is expected from better operating leverage, cost efficiencies, and high-value EPC wins, with management targeting sustained double-digit revenue growth and healthy profitability.

Name: Skipper Ltd
Overview:
Skipper Ltd is a major Indian manufacturer specializing in power transmission & distribution (T&D) towers, telecom and railway structures, along with polymer pipes and fittings. It combines engineering-heavy fabrication with polypropylene and PVC manufacturing, serving both infrastructure and plumbing sectors.

The company has diversified across engineering products, infrastructure projects, and polymer offerings, enjoying a strong presence in domestic and export markets. Skipper's evolution has been underpinned by its integrated footprint and a sustained order book reflecting long-term execution visibility.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
With capacity expansions underway and a robust order pipeline, Skipper is well-poised to sustain double-digit revenue growth. Focus remains on EPC execution, efficient inventory management, and cost optimization. Management maintains a 25% revenue CAGR guidance for FY26, backed by structural tailwinds in energy and infrastructure sectors.

Name: Om Power Transmission Ltd
Overview:
Om Power Transmission Limited is an engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) company focused on power transmission infrastructure. The company undertakes projects related to the construction of transmission lines, substations, and associated electrical infrastructure that are essential for the efficient movement of electricity from generation sources to distribution networks. Its services cover project design, procurement of materials, construction, and commissioning of power transmission systems.

The company serves utilities, government bodies, and private sector clients by delivering turnkey solutions in the power infrastructure segment. It has developed capabilities in executing projects across different terrains and conditions, ensuring timely completion and quality standards.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its order book by bidding for large-scale transmission and infrastructure projects across domestic and international markets. It aims to strengthen its execution capabilities and enhance operational efficiency to handle complex and high-value projects. Increasing investments in power infrastructure and grid expansion are expected to drive demand for its services.

Name: Reliance Infrastructure Ltd
Overview:
Reliance Infrastructure Ltd is one of India’s leading infrastructure conglomerates, developing and operating projects across sectors such as power generation, transmission and distribution, roads, metro rail, airports, and defence. The company undertakes these initiatives through special purpose vehicles (SPVs) and provides comprehensive engineering and construction services across its footprint.

It plays an integral role across the entire power value chain from generation and trading to distribution while also contributing to urban infrastructure and strategic defence development. Reliance Infra’s integrated model enables it to leverage synergies across various verticals, maintaining a diversified and resilient business profile.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Reliance Infrastructure is poised for gradual recovery with steady improvement expected in revenues and profitability. The power segment is projected to continue driving earnings, while infrastructure verticals like roads, metro, and defence could stage a comeback. Ongoing efforts to reduce debt and improve balance sheet strength bode well for long-term stability and growth.

Name: Quality Power Electrical Equipments Ltd
Overview:
