Quality Power Electrical Equipments Ltd is engaged in designing and manufacturing a range of power-conditioning products, including stabilizers, UPS systems, and energy management solutions. Its offerings are aimed at ensuring reliable and uninterrupted power supply for industries, commercial establishments, IT infrastructure, and households.

The company has built its reputation around innovation, reliability, and efficiency, with products that cater to both high-load industrial applications and smaller-scale commercial and residential users. With India’s growing demand for uninterrupted power in data centers, factories, and offices, Quality Power occupies a vital position in bridging gaps in energy reliability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Quality Power is expected to scale its presence by enhancing smart-grid-compatible products and expanding its service offerings. The growing demand for uninterrupted power across industries places it in a favorable growth trajectory.

Name: Timex Group India Ltd
Overview:
Timex Group India Ltd, part of the Netherlands-based Tanager Group B.V. (formerly Timex Group B.V.), is engaged in the manufacturing, trading, and distribution of watches, alongside after-sales services and IT support to group entities. The company entered India in 1988 and has since built a strong domestic and international presence in the watch industry.

Its brand portfolio spans across categories, from the flagship Timex brand to luxury labels like Versace and Ferragamo, and fashion brands like Guess, Ted Baker, and Nautica. The company also participates in the smart and connected watches segment, supported by its Baddi (Himachal Pradesh) facility, which has an annual assembly capacity of 3 million watches.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
Management has outlined a strategy centered on premiumization, omni-channel distribution, and luxury retail expansion. The SEEPZ facility in Mumbai is expected to enhance jewellery and accessory offerings under private labels, widening Timex’s play beyond traditional watches. With the integration of Just Watches and stronger e-commerce penetration, the company aims to expand consumer reach across Tier-1 and Tier-2 cities.

Name: H T Media Ltd
Overview:
HT Media Ltd, incorporated in 2002, is a prominent Indian media conglomerate engaged in print, radio broadcasting, and digital services. The company publishes leading newspapers such as Hindustan Times and Mint, catering to a diverse readership across India. In the radio segment, HT Media operates Fever FM, a popular radio station known for its engaging content. The digital arm encompasses various platforms, including OTTplay and Shine, focusing on delivering digital content and services to a wide audience.

Headquartered in New Delhi, HT Media has established a significant presence in the Indian media landscape. The company's operations span across multiple states, with a robust infrastructure supporting its diverse media offerings.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
HT Media aims to continue its growth by focusing on expanding its digital offerings and enhancing content across its platforms. The company plans to invest in technology and innovation to improve user engagement and monetization strategies.

Name: Linc Ltd
Overview:
Linc Ltd is an India-based company primarily engaged in the manufacturing and marketing of writing instruments and stationery products. Established in 1976, the company produces ballpoint pens, gel pens, rollerball pens, mechanical pencils, markers, and other office and school stationery. Linc Ltd serves both domestic and international markets, offering a combination of innovative designs, high-quality materials, and affordable pricing. The company has built a strong brand reputation for reliability, durability, and product variety.

Linc Ltd emphasizes research and development to introduce new product designs, improve ink quality, and expand its stationery offerings. The company has established a wide distribution network across India and exports to multiple countries, strengthening its market presence. With sustainable manufacturing practices and focus on customer satisfaction, Linc Ltd continues to maintain leadership in the writing instruments segment.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Linc Ltd, a prominent player in India's writing instruments and stationery sector, is targeting a 15–20% compound annual growth rate (CAGR) over the next four to five years. The company plans to achieve this through strategic expansion, including new manufacturing units in Gujarat and West Bengal, and by diversifying its product portfolio beyond traditional pens to include markers, sketch pens, and crayons.

Name: Navneet Education Ltd
Overview:
Navneet Education Ltd is a diversified educational products company providing syllabus-based learning solutions and stationery across India. Its portfolio includes textbooks, supplementary books, general interest titles, and branded stationery under names like Navneet, Target, Gala, YOUVA, and Indiannica.

With a footprint spanning publication, stationery (both paper and non-paper), and newer digital/ed-tech offerings, Navneet serves a wide spectrum of schools, students, and educational segments across domestic and export markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Navneet's near-term focus is on aligning product offerings with updated curricula, rejuvenating stationery demand via digital and non-paper segments, and expanding exports. With consumption patterns stabilizing, margin recovery, inventory normalization, and execution discipline are expected to support a return to sustained growth in the remainder of FY26.

Name: Cello World Ltd
Overview:
Cello World is a prominent Indian consumer products company with a diversified portfolio across consumer houseware, writing instruments and stationery, and moulded furniture and allied products. Its offerings span household essentials like cookware, cleaning aids, and insulated ware under the Cello brand, as well as a broad range of pens under brands like Unomax and Ultron 2X. The company also has a strong presence in moulded furniture, catering to both domestic and export markets.

The business operates through 14 manufacturing facilities across India, enabling a high level of in-house production, which contributed nearly 80% of revenue in H1 FY25. With a robust distribution network of over 3,500 distributors and 1.45 lakh retailers, Cello World maintains a strong presence in general trade while also expanding in modern trade, exports, and e-commerce. Recent strategic moves include an investment of ₹250 crore in a glassware facility in Rajasthan, making it the only domestic player with in-house glassware manufacturing capabilities.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Cello World is positioned for growth through capacity expansion in glassware and opalware, deepening its presence in modern trade and e-commerce, and leveraging its extensive distribution network to drive penetration. The strategic investment in in-house glassware manufacturing is expected to enhance margins and differentiate the brand, while expansion in higher-margin product categories is likely to support sustainable growth.

Name: Flair Writing Industries Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1976, Flair Writing Industries Ltd is a key player in the writing instruments segment, recognized among India's top three brands in the sector. The company designs, manufactures, and markets a broad portfolio of writing products ranging from pens to creative and houseware items under homegrown and partnered international brands.

Flair blends innovation with distribution, supplying products through expansive retail networks, institutional channels, and exports. It emphasizes brand diversification through segments like creativ­e, steel bottles, and houseware, alongside its core pen offerings.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Flair Writing Industries is steadily growing its business by expanding manufacturing capacity and strengthening distribution. A new large-scale plant is under construction to produce both pens and creative stationery items, leading to increased efficiency and product availability. The company continues to introduce appealing mid-premium and premium product lines, helping lift margins and brand perception. It is also broadening its product offerings branching into steel bottles and houseware leveraging its manufacturing expertise for diversification.

Name: D B Corp Ltd
Overview:
D.B. Corp Ltd, established in 1995, is a prominent Indian media conglomerate engaged in publishing newspapers, radio broadcasting, and providing integrated internet and mobile interactive services. The company operates major brands such as Dainik Bhaskar (Hindi daily), Divya Bhaskar, Saurashtra Samachar (Gujarati daily), and Divya Marathi (Marathi daily). These publications have a significant presence across India, catering to diverse linguistic audiences.

In addition to its print media operations, D.B. Corp Ltd has expanded into radio broadcasting through its MY FM network and offers digital services, including mobile applications and online platforms. The company's diversified portfolio enables it to reach a wide audience across various media channels, enhancing its market presence and revenue streams.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
D.B. Corp Ltd aims to enhance its digital presence and expand its reach in the radio broadcasting segment. The company plans to invest in technology and content to attract a broader audience and increase advertising revenues. By leveraging its established brand equity and exploring new revenue streams, D.B. Corp Ltd is positioned to maintain its leadership in the Indian media industry.

Name: S Chand & Company Ltd
Overview: S. Chand & Company Ltd is a leading Indian educational content company, established over 80 years ago. The company provides educational content, solutions, and services across the education lifecycle. It delivers content solutions and services through its presence in three segments: publishing, digital education, and learning solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
S Chand & Company is transitioning from a traditional textbook publisher to a comprehensive educational content provider, focusing on digital learning solutions. The company is expanding its digital footprint through platforms like S Chand Academy and partnerships with EdTech ventures such as ixamBee and Smartivity Labs. This digital shift is complemented by the adoption of the National Curriculum Framework (NCF), which is expected to drive growth in the K-12 market.

Name: Repro India Ltd
Overview:
Repro India Limited is an Indian company operating in the printing and content solutions industry, catering to publishing, education, and corporate sectors. The company provides end-to-end content services, including digitization, typesetting, editing, proofreading, printing, and distribution, covering textbooks, journals, magazines, packaging materials, and marketing collaterals.

Leveraging advanced technologies such as digital and variable data printing, Repro India also offers digital publishing solutions, e-learning platforms, interactive e-books, and content management systems. With a focus on sustainability, technology adoption, and customer satisfaction, the company serves a diverse domestic and international clientele, aiming to be a trusted partner for comprehensive content and printing solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Repro India Ltd is strategically enhancing its position in the printing and content management sector through significant investments in technology and infrastructure. At PrintPack 2025, the company ordered Bindwel's advanced Bindline system for its Surat export-oriented unit, aiming to improve binding efficiency and maintain high-quality standards . Additionally, Repro India is expanding its warehousing capabilities with new facilities in key locations, facilitating better demand forecasting and inventory planning

Name: Amagi Media Labs Ltd
Overview:
Amagi Media Labs Ltd. is a technology-driven company that operates in the cloud-based broadcasting and connected TV (CTV) segment. It provides solutions for content owners, broadcasters, and streaming platforms to manage, distribute, and monetize video content across digital and television networks. The company’s platform enables ad-supported streaming, channel creation, content delivery, and targeted advertising using cloud infrastructure, helping media companies shift from traditional broadcasting to digital streaming ecosystems.

The company offers a range of services including cloud playout, ad insertion, content monetization, and analytics for video streaming platforms. It works with global content creators, OTT platforms, and advertisers to streamline content distribution and improve audience engagement. By leveraging data analytics and automation, Amagi helps clients optimize advertising revenue and efficiently manage large-scale video operations across multiple geographies.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its global footprint in the fast-growing connected TV and ad-supported streaming market by strengthening its cloud-based technology platform. It aims to deepen partnerships with content creators, streaming platforms, and advertisers to increase adoption of its monetization and distribution solutions. The company is also focusing on enhancing its data analytics and targeted advertising capabilities to improve revenue outcomes for clients.

Name: Jagran Prakashan Ltd
Overview:
Jagran Prakashan Ltd is a leading media company in India, known for its flagship newspaper, Dainik Jagran, and a diverse portfolio of radio stations and digital platforms. The company has a significant presence in the media industry, catering to a wide audience across the country.

In FY25, Jagran reported a consolidated net sales of ₹1,824 crore, a 3.5% increase from the previous year. The company's EBITDA stood at ₹450 crore, with a margin of 24.7%, and a PAT of ₹250 crore, reflecting a 6% year-over-year growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jagran Prakashan Ltd is actively enhancing its digital presence and expanding its multi-platform reach. The company is focusing on increasing user engagement and diversifying revenue streams across its digital, print, radio, and outdoor advertising segments. Jagran New Media aims to transform into a ₹1,000 crore business by 2028, building on its profitable trajectory since 2019.

Name: Sandesh Ltd
Overview: Sandesh Ltd is a prominent Gujarati media and publishing company, known for producing daily newspapers, weekly magazines, and regional periodicals. The company has a strong footprint in Gujarat, with editions published from Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara, Rajkot, Bhavnagar, and Bhuj. Beyond media, Sandesh engages in ancillary businesses such as bullion trading and real estate projects. With a heritage dating back to 1923, the company has built substantial brand credibility and a loyal readership. Sandesh continues to invest in digital platforms and content diversification to capture younger audiences while maintaining dominance in regional print media.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sandesh Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to strengthen its position in the media sector. The company is focusing on expanding its digital presence, aiming to enhance its online platforms and reach a broader audience. Additionally, Sandesh is exploring diversification opportunities beyond traditional media, including ventures into real estate development through its subsidiary, Applewoods Estate Pvt Ltd, which is developing an integrated township in Ahmedabad.

Name: Kokuyo Camlin Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1946, Kokuyo Camlin Ltd is a prominent Indian company specializing in the manufacturing and trading of stationery and color products. The company offers a diverse range of products, including writing instruments, notebooks, marker pens, inks, fine-art colors, and accessories, catering to artists, students, designers, and office workers. 

With manufacturing plants in Maharashtra and Jammu and Kashmir, Kokuyo Camlin ensures nationwide reach through its extensive distribution network. As a subsidiary of Kokuyo Co. Ltd., Japan, Kokuyo Camlin benefits from global expertise and innovation in the stationery industry. The company's commitment to quality and customer satisfaction has established it as a trusted brand in the Indian market.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Kokuyo Camlin aims to enhance its product offerings and expand its market reach to drive growth in FY26. The company plans to focus on innovation and quality to meet the evolving needs of its customers. Additionally, Kokuyo Camlin intends to strengthen its distribution network and explore new market segments to achieve sustainable growth in the coming fiscal year.

Name: Doms Industries Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 2006, DOMS Industries Limited is a leading Indian stationery and art product manufacturer, designing, developing, and selling a broad range of products under its flagship DOMS brand. It has established itself as a dominant player with extensive product lines spanning scholastic stationery, scholastic art material, paper stationery, office supplies, hobby and craft products, fine art products, and hygiene offerings through acquisitions.

The company’s operations are supported by a fully backward-integrated manufacturing setup, encompassing 18 facilities across five locations, covering more than 2 million sq. ft. DOMS has a pan-India presence via 5,725+ distributors and a growing global footprint across 55+ export countries. It also benefits from a strategic partnership with Italy’s FILA Group, enabling access to global markets, product expertise, and exclusive rights to distribute premium brands in South Asia.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
DOMS aims to accelerate capacity expansion, deepen product penetration, and strengthen both domestic and export channels. Leveraging its FILA partnership, it plans to introduce more premium products, expand its footprint in untapped geographies, and capitalize on inorganic opportunities in manufacturing and distribution. Continued investments in R&D, product innovation, and omnichannel strategies are expected to sustain growth momentum in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Hindustan Media Ventures Ltd
Overview:
Hindustan Media Ventures Ltd (HMVL) traces its roots to 1918, originally incorporated as The Behar Journals Ltd. Today, as part of HT Media and ultimately the KK Birla Group, it is a prominent Hindi media publishing house. It publishes the widely read daily newspaper Hindustan, along with magazines such as Nandan (children’s) and Kadambini (socio-cultural), and engages in digital media through LiveHindustan.com and OTTPlay platform.
Its print operations span 21 editions and 144 sub-editions across Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Uttarakhand, and Delhi-NCR, supported by a multi-tiered printing and distribution infrastructure. HMVL remains a key voice in Hindi print media, blending legacy print presence with evolving digital offerings.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Hindustan Media Ventures Ltd (HMVL), publisher of the Hindi daily Hindustan and operator of the OTT platform OTTPlay, is actively expanding its digital footprint. The company is focusing on enhancing user engagement through strategic content partnerships and technological advancements. HMVL aims to strengthen its position in the digital media landscape by diversifying its content offerings and improving user experience across various platforms.

Name: MPS Ltd
Overview:
MPS Limited is a B2B learning and platform solutions company that empowers education and research for corporate clients. It offers a wide range of services including content creation, digital transformation, and technology solutions. The company leverages advancements in AI/ML to cater to evolving corporate learning needs.

MPS serves a diverse clientele across sectors such as education, research, and corporate training. Its platforms and solutions integrate technology with learning, positioning the company as a key player in delivering high-quality, scalable B2B learning and research solutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
MPS plans to expand AI/ML capabilities via MPS Labs and launch a dedicated Data and AI practice in FY26. By scaling its STAR account strategy and focusing on high-value long-term partnerships, the company is well-positioned for sustained growth in the B2B learning and platform solutions market.

Name: IDFC First Bank Ltd
Overview:
IDFC First Bank, formed from the 2018 merger of IDFC Bank and Capital First, is a full-service bank offering a wide range of retail and corporate banking services. In recent years, the bank has successfully transitioned from a corporate-focused model to a retail-centric strategy, aiming to boost profitability through higher Net Interest Margins (NIM). The bank emphasizes digital services, customer convenience, and product diversification to drive growth and strengthen its position in the Indian banking sector.

Business Segments and Estimated Revenue Contribution:


Retail Banking 
Includes savings/current accounts, home loans, personal loans, car loans, credit cards, and digital services.
➤ Key driver of NIM at 5.98% and CASA ratio of 50%.

Corporate Banking 
Comprises legacy corporate lending, trade forex, and cash management solutions.
➤ Now a smaller focus post-retail transformation.

Other Services 
Includes wealth management, FASTag & toll acquiring, prepaid cards, and fee-based services.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

IDFC First Bank is executing a comprehensive growth strategy to enhance its position in the Indian banking sector. The bank is focusing on expanding its retail banking operations, aiming for a 20% annual growth in its loan book. To support this expansion, IDFC First Bank is raising capital through investments from Warburg Pincus and the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, which will bolster its capital adequacy ratio and enable further growth. Additionally, the bank is enhancing its digital banking services, including enabling UPI transactions for NRIs in 12 countries, to improve customer accessibility and convenience.

Name: HDFC Bank Ltd
Overview:
HDFC Bank is India’s largest private-sector bank, renowned for its comprehensive range of banking and financial services. It operates across retail banking, wholesale banking, and treasury, while maintaining leadership in payments, middle-market banking, and rural banking outreach.
 
 Business Segments
 Retail Banking: Individual banking products and services — deposits, loans, cards, and payments.
 
 Wholesale Banking: Corporate and institutional banking solutions.
 
 Treasury: Treasury operations and trading activities.
 
 Other Banking Operations: Fees, third-party product distribution, and allied services.
 
 Revenue Mix:
 Retail Banking: 46%
 
 Wholesale Banking: 27%
 
 Treasury: 14%
 
 Other Banking Operations: 13%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
HDFC Bank plans to continue expanding its branches and distribution channels into new geographies, leveraging its strong brand and customer trust to drive cross-selling opportunities. The bank aims to maintain its leadership in payment systems and middle-market banking while ensuring superior asset quality and operational efficiency. Integration and scaling of merged HDFC Ltd. branches will further enhance its reach and diversify the product portfolio, supporting sustainable growth.

Name: Karur Vysya Bank Ltd
Overview:
Karur Vysya Bank (KVB) is a long-standing Indian private sector bank that offers a comprehensive range of banking and financial services, including retail, corporate, and treasury operations. Established with a focus on customer-centric solutions, KVB has steadily expanded its presence across India, serving a broad base of retail and institutional customers. Its diversified operations encompass lending, deposits, wealth management, and para-banking services, supported by a growing digital banking infrastructure.

The bank maintains a balanced approach between traditional branch expansion and technological adoption, with a network spanning urban, semi-urban, and rural areas. It has strengthened strategic partnerships with insurance and wealth management firms to diversify revenue streams, while focusing on asset quality improvement and operational efficiency. KVB’s lending portfolio is well-diversified across commercial, retail, agricultural, and corporate segments, reflecting a prudent risk management strategy.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
KVB aims to sustain healthy credit growth, supported by branch expansion and deeper penetration in semi-urban and rural markets. Focus areas include scaling up co-lending, enhancing fee-based income through wealth management and insurance distribution, and maintaining asset quality below industry averages. The bank’s strategic priority for FY26 is to leverage technology-driven lending and digital channels to improve efficiency and customer engagement while targeting double-digit business growth.

Name: AU Small Finance Bank Ltd
Overview:
AU Small Finance Bank is a diversified banking institution offering retail and wholesale banking services, along with treasury operations. It focuses on retail secured assets including wheels, mortgages, and gold loans, complemented by a commercial banking segment and a controlled unsecured lending portfolio. The bank operates in a competitive environment but has shown strong deposit growth and branch profitability improvements.

The bank’s strategy emphasizes disciplined risk management, digital product offerings, and distribution expansion to support growth. Despite a muted macroeconomic environment and pressure on margins, the bank is leveraging merger synergies, improving operational efficiency, and expanding its footprint in key segments, aiming for sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects macro conditions to improve, aiding credit growth and demand revival. Loan growth is targeted at 2-2.5x nominal GDP, with wheels, commercial banking, and gold loans driving volumes. Credit cost is expected to peak and normalize, supported by risk management enhancements. NIM pressure is forecasted to ease from Q3, and ROA target for FY27 is set at 1.8%. The bank remains focused on disciplined growth, cost efficiency, and asset quality improvement.

Name: ICICI Bank Ltd
Overview:
ICICI Bank is one of India’s largest private-sector banks, providing a comprehensive range of banking and financial services to retail, SME, and corporate clients. It has a strong domestic footprint and a selective international presence in over 15 countries, serving customers through subsidiaries, branches, and representative offices.
 
 Business Segments
 Retail Banking: Savings & current accounts, cards, personal loans, mortgages, and digital banking solutions.
 
 Corporate & SME Banking: Loans, working capital finance, trade services, and specialized sector solutions.
 
 International Banking: Deposits, NRI services, and select lending operations — international advances contribute ~11% of total advances.
 
 Digital & Payment Services: Online banking, mobile banking, specialized digital tools for businesses and retail customers.
 
 Revenue Mix (Indicative Approximate Percentages)
 
 Retail Banking & Loans: ~52.2%

 Rural Banking: 5.6%
 
 Corporate & SME Banking: ~39.8%
 
 International Banking: ~2.4%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
ICICI Bank is focused on expanding its physical branches and digital banking footprint to enhance accessibility and customer convenience. The rollout of next-generation digital solutions is expected to boost engagement and operational efficiency. With a targeted strategy on NRI banking and select international markets, the bank aims for diversified growth. Strong risk management, low NPA ratios, and robust capital adequacy support financial resilience, while collaborations with fintechs and a sector-focused approach help broaden the customer base and increase cross-selling opportunities.

Name: RBL Bank Ltd
Overview:
RBL Bank Ltd., incorporated in 1943, operates across five verticals: Corporate Banking, Commercial Banking, Branch & Business Banking, Retail Assets, and Treasury & Financial Markets. Headquartered in Mumbai, the bank has developed a strong presence in both retail and wholesale banking, with a particular focus on secured retail assets and commercial banking.

As of FY25, the bank has a network of 561 branches, 412 ATMs, and ~1,472 business correspondent branches, serving customers across 600+ districts and over 18,000 pin codes in India. Its loan book stood at ₹92,618 crore with a 40:60 retail-to-wholesale mix, and total deposits were ₹1,10,944 crore, with granular deposits accounting for 49.8% of the base.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

RBL Bank is focusing on expanding its secured retail loan portfolio, targeting a 60% share of its overall retail loans by increasing its presence in Tier-3 and Tier-4 cities. The bank plans to introduce higher-yielding assets such as commercial vehicle and used car loans to enhance profitability. Additionally, RBL Bank is aiming to achieve a net interest margin (NIM) of 5.1% by focusing on expanding its retail business through higher-yielding assets.

Name: Axis Bank Ltd
Overview:
Axis Bank is India’s third-largest private sector bank, serving a wide customer base including large corporates, MSMEs, agriculture, and retail segments. The bank is also a significant player in India’s payments ecosystem:
 
 4th largest credit card issuer in India.
 
 2nd largest merchant-acquiring bank with 30% market share in installed capacity.
 
 Branch Network & Digital Strength
 Operates 4,750 branches, with a balanced urban-rural footprint (30% metro, 30% semi-urban, 23% urban, 17% rural).
 
 Extensive ATM and cash management network.
 
 Strong digital adoption:
 
 70% of new savings accounts,
 
 78% of new credit cards,
 
 68% of fixed deposits sourced digitally 
 
 Revenue Mix
 Retail fees: Cards contribute 32%, third-party products 26%, retail assets 24%, retail liabilities & others 18%.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
HDFC Bank plans to expand its sustainable finance portfolio to ₹30,000 crore by FY26, focusing on green lending and ESG-linked bond issuances to strengthen its global leadership in responsible banking. The bank continues to diversify products and segments, emphasizing retail cards, affordable housing, women-focused lending, and deep MSME outreach. Ongoing investments in digital platforms and AI-driven banking aim to enhance customer acquisition and product innovation.

Name: Yes Bank Ltd
Overview:
Yes Bank Limited is a private sector bank in India offering a comprehensive suite of banking and financial services. The bank serves corporate, institutional, and retail customers through an extensive network of 1,098 branches, 56 BC banking outlets, and 1,400 ATMs spread across India, with a strong presence in metro cities.
 
 Business Segments
 Corporate & Institutional Banking: Offers working capital finance, term loans, trade finance, and structured lending to corporates across various sectors.
 
 Branch & Retail Banking: Provides savings, current accounts, retail loans, and wealth management services to individual customers and small businesses.
 
 Financial Markets & Investment Banking: Includes treasury operations, debt capital markets, and advisory services.
 
 Business & Transaction Banking: Services include cash management, digital payments, and transaction processing for businesses.
 
 Wealth Management: Caters to high-net-worth individuals through customized investment solutions and advisory services.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Yes Bank is focusing on expanding its retail presence by adding new branches and increasing its lending to small and mid-sized businesses (SMEs). The bank is diversifying its revenue streams through innovative products and services, such as customized loan products and wealth management solutions. Strategic partnerships and collaborations are central to Yes Bank's expansion plans, aiming to increase its digital customer base and digital transactions. These initiatives are crucial for improving Yes Bank's financial performance and achieving its long-term goals.

Name: IndusInd Bank Ltd
Overview:
IndusInd Bank is a well-diversified private sector bank offering a full suite of banking and financial services to both corporate and retail clients across urban, semi-urban, metro, and rural India. The bank combines a strong physical presence with expanding digital channels to deepen customer reach.
 
Key Business Segments & Revenue Drivers
 Retail Banking: Major contributor, driven by deposits (CASA ratio at 40%) and loan products like vehicle finance, personal loans, and new home loans.
 
 Corporate Banking: Provides working capital, trade finance, and treasury solutions to businesses.
 
 Treasury & Forex: Adds to fee-based income through foreign exchange and investment services.
 
 Fee-Based Services: Cross-selling investment products, insurance, forex, and real estate advisory.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

IndusInd Bank is focused on expanding its well-diversified banking model by leveraging both physical and digital channels to deepen customer engagement across retail and corporate segments. Retail banking remains a major growth driver, particularly in vehicle, personal, and new home loans, while corporate banking and fee-based services like forex and investment advisory contribute to revenue diversification.

Name: Kotak Mahindra Bank Ltd
Overview:
Kotak Mahindra Bank is a leading Indian financial services group with over 2,148 branches and 2,700+ ATMs, serving 32 million customers nationwide and internationally via its Dubai branch. The bank offers a wide range of banking and financial products, generating most of its revenue from general banking fees. It has strongly embraced digital transformation, with over 97% of savings account transactions done digitally, boosting efficiency. Kotak maintains strong financial health with a 23% capital adequacy ratio, a 5% net interest margin, a low 0.6% net NPA, and a 58% CASA ratio. Its growth strategy focuses on expanding unsecured lending, improving margins through loan repricing, and growing its wealth management business via both branch expansion and digital channels.
 
 Business Segments
 General Banking Services: ~78% of fee income
 
 Distribution and Syndication Services: ~19% of fee income
 
 Other Sources: ~3% of fee income
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
The bank aims to expand its unsecured lending portfolios, including personal loans and credit cards, to capture higher interest margins and reach new customer segments. Growth in the wealth management business is expected to boost low-risk fee income, while continuous digital innovation leveraging AI, cloud, and data analytics will enhance customer experience and operational efficiency. Branch expansion, aligned with digital channels, will balance physical presence with digital convenience. A strong capital base and focus on asset quality support sustainable loan book growth, while cross-selling, data-driven insights, and targeted penetration in semi-urban and rural markets further strengthen customer engagement and long-term resilience.

Name: Federal Bank Ltd
Overview:
The Federal Bank Limited, one of Kerala’s largest private sector banks, operates a broad suite of retail and corporate banking services alongside para banking activities, treasury, and foreign exchange operations. The bank has shown steady growth with a diversified revenue base driven by advances, deposits, and expanding fee income streams. Its focus on granular deposit franchise, digital initiatives, and retail segments positions it well in a competitive banking environment.

The bank is also actively investing in branch expansion, brand refresh, and operational modernization to sustain growth and customer engagement. Asset quality remains robust with improved coverage ratios, while capital adequacy provides ample headroom for growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Loan growth is expected to accelerate beyond current levels, driven by mid-yield and retail segments, while CASA ratio is targeted to reach 36% over the medium term. NIM pressure from policy rate cuts will be mitigated via tactical product repricing and asset-liability management.

Name: Bandhan Bank Ltd
Overview:
Bandhan Bank, incorporated in 2014, is a pan-India commercial bank targeting underbanked and underpenetrated regions. It began as Bandhan Financial Services Pvt. Ltd (BFSL), which was India’s largest NBFC-MFI before transitioning into a bank in 2015. Following the receipt of a banking license from the Reserve Bank of India, Bandhan transferred its microfinance portfolio to the newly formed bank. The bank focuses on offering both asset and liability products, catering to microfinance, retail, housing, and SME segments.

Bandhan Bank is part of a larger ecosystem under Bandhan Financial Holdings Ltd., which includes verticals such as mutual funds, life insurance, and financial services. With a strong presence across 615 districts and a growing network of 1,715 branches and 4,594 banking units, the bank aims to shift its loan portfolio toward secured lending, while reducing reliance on microfinance loans. The bank is also pushing digital transformation, with 98% of retail transactions conducted digitally and 93% of savings accounts opened digitally in FY25.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Bandhan Bank is focusing on expanding its presence in underbanked and underserved markets across India. The bank aims to increase its assets under management (AUM) by 15–17% in the upcoming fiscal year, with a particular emphasis on secured lending products such as home and small business loans. This strategy includes enhancing its digital banking capabilities to improve customer access and operational efficiency.

Name: KSB Ltd
Overview:
KSB Ltd is a leading manufacturer of industrial pumps, valves, and related services, catering to sectors such as energy, water, wastewater, construction, oil & gas, and various industrial applications. Founded in 1960 and headquartered in Pune, the company has built a strong domestic footprint supported by six manufacturing facilities, multiple service centers, and warehouses, along with a growing export presence.

The company operates through four main segments — Standard Products, Valves, Engineered Products, and SupremeServ — offering a mix of mass-produced and highly customized solutions. Its offerings include submersible and monoblock pumps, high-pressure multistage pumps, specialized valves, and aftermarket services. KSB also maintains a strong market presence in the renewable energy sector through its solar pump business, and in the nuclear energy space with advanced pump systems for primary and secondary circuits.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company is positioned to benefit from India’s nuclear expansion and renewable energy adoption. Ongoing execution of large nuclear orders, participation in upcoming PM-KUSUM solar tenders, and continued investment in high-efficiency, localized manufacturing are expected to support growth. Strong demand from domestic infrastructure and industrial sectors, combined with export market penetration, provides multi-year revenue visibility.

Name: Blue Star Ltd
Overview:
Blue Star Limited is a leading player in the Indian air conditioning and refrigeration industry with a diversified portfolio ranging from air purifiers and coolers to turnkey mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) solutions. The company has also expanded into water purification and specialty electronics under its Professional Electronics and Industrial Systems (PE&IS) division. Blue Star is India’s largest after-sales service provider for air conditioning and commercial refrigeration equipment and has built a strong brand reputation over its 80+ years of operations.

The company operates seven manufacturing facilities across India and has a significant presence in over 18 countries, including the Middle East, Africa, SAARC, and ASEAN regions. Through its wholly owned subsidiaries (WOS) and joint ventures (JVs), Blue Star is also involved in MEP projects across global residential and commercial infrastructure sectors. The business is backed by strong R&D, a wide retail footprint, and steady revenue growth across core segments like unitary products, commercial air conditioning, and electro-mechanical projects.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Blue Star is poised for significant growth, driven by strategic investments and favorable market conditions. The company plans to invest ₹400 crore in FY26 to expand its manufacturing capacity across room air conditioners, commercial refrigeration, and commercial air conditioning segments. This expansion aims to increase room air conditioner production from 1.4 million to 1.8 million units, targeting a 14.3% market share by FY26

Name: Elgi Equipments Ltd
Overview:
Elgi Equipments Limited, incorporated in 1960, is a global player in the manufacturing and distribution of air compressors and automotive equipment, with a strong presence in after-sales services. The company is recognized as the 6th largest air compressor manufacturer globally and the 2nd largest in India, marking its dominance in both domestic and international markets.

Its compressor product portfolio spans oil-lubricated and oil-free screw compressors, piston-based reciprocating compressors, portable and medical variants, and specialized solutions like railway air compressors. With operations in over 120 countries and a direct presence in 28, Elgi has strategically built an international footprint supported by three global manufacturing units and 400+ distributors. The company has also backward integrated critical components like motors and continues to invest in R&D and breakthrough technologies like the “Stabilisor,” which improves compressor efficiency.
Key Takeways:
Cash Position and Capital Allocation
Elgi’s net cash position more than doubled to ₹4,188 Cr in Mar 2025, up from ₹2,140 Cr in Mar 2024. Capex incurred till Dec 2024 was ₹78 Cr, within the announced ₹250 Cr Capex cycle. Additionally, a major investment of ₹696 Cr has been committed for the MK2 manufacturing facility project over the next five years.

Operational Efficiency and Backward Integration
The company is shifting its motor manufacturing and assembly operations to its main facility to streamline costs and boost efficiency. As of FY25, Elgi manufactures 60% of its motor requirements and aims to achieve 100% in-house production by Sep 2025. This would reduce lead time from 3–6 months to just 3 days.

Subsidiaries and Divestment
Elgi maintains a strong international footprint with 26 subsidiaries and 9 JVs. In Feb 2025, the company divested its entire stake in G3 Industrial Solutions LLC, a U.S.-based JV, through its wholly owned subsidiary, Elgi Compressors USA Inc.

Name: Kirloskar Pneumatic Company Ltd
Overview:
Kirloskar Pneumatic Company Limited, part of the Kirloskar Group, is a leading engineering solutions provider operating primarily in the Compression and Transmission segments. It caters to diverse industries such as oil & gas, steel, cement, food & beverages, railways, and marine, with offerings ranging from compressors, refrigeration systems, and gearboxes to operation and maintenance services.

The company is a market leader in several niche segments, including CNG systems, oil and gas refrigeration, and ammonia refrigeration compressors, supported by a strong focus on indigenization and product innovation. With strategic backward integration, an expanding product portfolio, and capability-driven investments, KPCL is positioned to serve both domestic and global markets efficiently.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Kirloskar Pneumatic Company Ltd (KPCL) is strategically enhancing its product portfolio and expanding into new markets to drive future growth. The company is focusing on increasing its presence in key international markets, including Europe and North America, through targeted investments in sales and distribution channels.

Name: Voltas Ltd
Overview:
Voltas is a well-established engineering company specializing in air conditioning, refrigeration, electro-mechanical projects, and engineering products across domestic and international markets. With a strong legacy and over 5,000 active customer sites in India, it serves industries like construction, mining, water management, and textiles, along with consumer appliance segments through Voltas Beko.

The company balances project execution with product innovation, emphasizing premium, energy-efficient cooling solutions and expanding manufacturing capacity. Despite short-term macro and seasonal challenges, Voltas maintains a leadership position in the RAC market and continues to invest in backward integration and capacity expansion, particularly in its Chennai plant.
Key Takeways:
Future Growth
Management remains optimistic about double-digit growth across product lines in FY26, driven by demand recovery and government infrastructure spending. The focus is on regaining RAC market leadership, operational efficiency, and capturing growth in cold chain and commercial AC segments, alongside continued expansion of Voltas Beko’s footprint.

Name: Roto Pumps Ltd
Overview:
Roto Pumps Ltd, founded in 1968, is a pioneer in manufacturing positive displacement pumps. The company provides pumping solutions to a range of industries, including wastewater, sugar, paper, paint, oil & gas, chemicals & process, ceramics, food & beverages, renewable energy & power, mining & explosives, and marine.

Roto Pumps Ltd offers progressive cavity pumps, twin screw pumps, and retrofit spare parts, catering to diverse industrial applications.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Roto Pumps Ltd aims to expand its product range and enhance manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand in domestic and international markets. The company is also focusing on technological advancements to stay competitive in the industry.

Name: RHI Magnesita India Ltd
Overview:
RHI Magnesita India Ltd, formerly Orient Refractories Ltd, is a market leader in manufacturing and marketing high-grade refractory products, systems, and services for the steel industry in India and abroad. The company also caters to cement, non-ferrous, and glass sectors, offering end-to-end solutions with a strong focus on innovation and technology transfer from its global group.

Backed by a diversified product portfolio and strong customer relationships, RHI Magnesita maintains a 30% share of the Indian special refractories market. Its strategy revolves around operational excellence, localization of high-value products, cost competitiveness, and selective market participation to sustain profitability amidst industry overcapacity and global trade headwinds.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to achieve steady growth in FY26, The focus will be on innovation, cost control, and improving operational efficiency while carefully choosing profitable markets. Growth is expected to come from stronger demand in the steel and cement sectors, higher export sales, and benefits from ongoing plant modernization and productivity improvements..

Name: Amber Enterprises India Ltd
Overview:
Amber Enterprises India Ltd, incorporated in 1956, is a prominent integrated solutions provider for India’s Room Air Conditioner (RAC) OEM/ODM industry, with a strong market presence across consumer durables, electronics, and railway & defense segments. It offers a wide spectrum of products, including complete RAC units, critical components, electronics, and specialized systems for transportation and defense applications.

The company’s strength lies in its diversified product portfolio, strategic customer relationships, and increasing backward integration. Through organic expansion and acquisitions, Amber is evolving into a multi-segment engineering and manufacturing powerhouse with a growing presence in high-value electronics, power systems, and industrial automation, while maintaining leadership in consumer durables.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management remains bullish across segments, expecting to outperform industry growth in consumer durables by 10–12% in FY26. Electronics is targeted to reach $1 billion revenue within three years, driven by high-value verticals and global market access. Railways & Defense is on track to double revenue over two years. With deepening customer engagement, backward integration, and inorganic expansion, Amber is positioning for sustained double-digit growth beyond FY26.

Name: GK Energy Ltd
Overview:
GK Energy Ltd is an engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) firm focused on solar-powered agricultural water pump systems. It provides end-to-end services including survey, design, installation, commissioning, and maintenance of solar pumps under government subsidy programmes. The company is empanelled under key central and state government solar schemes and has built a strong presence in several agricultural states by executing a large number of installations. It operates with a relatively asset-light model, maintains warehouses, and works with both in-house and third-party contractors to scale its operations.

Over recent years, GK Energy has demonstrated sharp financial growth, with revenue and profit rising multiple times over, largely driven by execution under subsidy-led schemes. Its margins have improved as it takes on higher-margin orders and gains efficiencies in procurement (bulk purchases) and execution. The strength of its order book, combined with government support for renewable agriculture infrastructure, positions it as a leading player in its niche.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

GK Energy is likely to benefit from continuing and possibly expanding subsidy-led programmes that promote solar irrigation and renewable energy use in agriculture, driving demand for its EPC services. The company aims to scale its operations across additional states, diversify into rooftop solar systems, and increase non-scheme revenues to reduce its dependence on a single government programme.

Name: Kirloskar Brothers Ltd
Overview:
Kirloskar Brothers Ltd (KBL), a flagship company of the Kirloskar Group, is a leading engineering solutions provider specializing in fluid management systems. Its portfolio spans large infrastructure projects in water supply, irrigation, and power plants, alongside a vast range of pumps, valves, motors, and hydro turbines. The company caters to diverse industries through over 250 product categories and 100,000+ SKUs.

With a strong brand legacy and international presence through subsidiaries like SPP Pumps and Rodelta, KBL leverages technological innovation, strategic contracts, and a diversified customer base to maintain leadership in domestic and global pump markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
KBL aims for sustained double-digit revenue growth, margin expansion, and increased service revenue contribution. Growth will be supported by infrastructure investments in India, strong international order execution, and diversification into strategic sectors like FGD pumps and rotating equipment services. The company also sees long-term opportunities from urbanization, renewable energy, and industrial modernization both domestically and globally.

Name: Vesuvius India Ltd
Overview:
Vesuvius India Ltd, incorporated in 1991 and part of the global Vesuvius plc group, is a leading manufacturer and trader of refractory products and flow control systems primarily serving the steel industry. Its portfolio includes advanced refractories, crucibles, nozzles, stoppers, shrouds, castables, taphole clays, and a wide range of casting and refractory-related services. The company also caters to non-steel sectors like aluminium, cement, lime, mineral processing, hydrocarbons, refineries, and power generation, though steel and foundry remain the primary focus.

Vesuvius has strategically expanded its production footprint in India with major facilities in Kolkata and Visakhapatnam, both certified for ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and ISO 45001 standards. The company has invested heavily in capacity expansion, advanced manufacturing technologies, and R&D, positioning itself as a market leader in niche segments, with strong partnerships with major steel producers.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management remains confident of doubling turnover in the next 5–10 years, driven by rising steel demand in India, expansion into non-steel sectors, and a strong product innovation pipeline. The company is pursuing measured diversification beyond steel while maintaining cost discipline to protect margins. Future growth will be underpinned by capacity expansions, advanced technology adoption, and increasing TRMS penetration.

Name: Ingersoll-Rand (India) Ltd
Overview:
Ingersoll Rand India Limited is a leading manufacturer and seller of industrial air compressors, complemented by related services such as installation, commissioning, and maintenance. The company markets products under globally recognized brands, including Ingersoll Rand, NASH, CompAir, Gardner Denver, ARO, Thomas, Milton Roy, and EMCO Wheaton, serving industries such as automotive, metals, pharmaceuticals, and textiles. Backed by strong technological support from its parent, Ingersoll Rand Ltd, the company also fulfills a portion of global small compressor demand from India.

Its operations are supported by a manufacturing facility in Ahmedabad and branch offices across major Indian metros, with exports to the USA, Europe, Bangladesh, Nepal, and Sri Lanka. A new ₹170 crore plant in Gujarat is slated to enhance capacity to 5,000 compressors per month. The company enjoys a dominant market share of over 45% in the centrifugal compressor segment and focuses on energy-efficient products and leveraging technology for growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Ingersoll Rand India aims to leverage its enhanced production capacity, energy-efficient product portfolio, and global brand network to drive growth. The focus will be on scaling exports, meeting increasing domestic demand, and pursuing inorganic opportunities to strengthen its industrial solutions portfolio. The new plant is expected to be a key growth catalyst in this period.

Name: Shakti Pumps (India) Ltd
Overview:
Shakti Pumps (India) Ltd is a leading manufacturer specializing in pumps and motors, offering advanced water pumping solutions across various applications such as irrigation, horticulture, domestic water supply, and industrial uses. The company's flagship brand, "Shakti," is well-regarded in both domestic and international markets.

The company has a robust order book, reflecting steady inflows and active participation in tenders across various states. Shakti Pumps is also investing in capacity expansion and technological advancements to enhance its manufacturing capabilities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Shakti Pumps aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing its global presence. The company's focus on innovation and sustainability positions it well to meet the evolving needs of its diverse customer base.

Name: IFGL Refractories Ltd
Overview:
IFGL Refractories Ltd designs and produces specialized refractories and related operating systems tailored for the iron and steel industry. The company supplies critical components such as slide gate systems, ladle refractories, tundish linings, and pouring systems, supporting the high-temperature processes in steel-making.

Promoted by the S.K. Bajoria Group in India and Japan’s global refractory leader Krosaki Harima Corporation, IFGL bridges advanced international expertise with domestic manufacturing strength to serve India’s core industrial sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, IFGL has embarked on significant capacity enhancements: the Visakhapatnam plant’s new mag‑carbon brick line is now operational, the non‑ferrous refractory segment is underway, and a greenfield JV facility is planned in Gujarat’s Kutch region

Name: Nexus Select Trust
Overview:
Nexus Select Trust (NST) is India’s first publicly listed Retail REIT with a portfolio of high-quality consumption centers, primarily consisting of Grade-A malls, commercial offices, and hospitality assets. The REIT, registered under SEBI REIT Regulations 2014, is sponsored by Wynford Investments Ltd, an affiliate of Blackstone. Since its listing in May 2023, NST has rapidly expanded its footprint and currently operates 19 consumption centers across 15 Indian cities.

As of FY25, NST manages 10.6 million sq. ft. of retail assets, 1.3 million sq. ft. of office spaces, and 450 hotel keys. It serves over 130 million shoppers annually and hosts around 3,000 stores featuring 1,000+ brands. The REIT has maintained a strong operational performance with FY25 tenant sales reaching ₹12,400 Cr and a trading occupancy of 96.6%. It is actively pursuing growth through acquisitions, tenant premiumization, sustainability measures, and innovation in consumer experience.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Nexus Select Trust is actively expanding its portfolio of Grade-A urban consumption centres across India. The trust is focusing on strategic acquisitions, including the recent addition of the MBD Complex in Ludhiana, which comprises a shopping mall and a hotel. This acquisition is expected to generate additional rental income, enhancing the trust's revenue streams. Furthermore, Nexus Select Trust is exploring opportunities to acquire more malls in North India, aiming to strengthen its presence in key markets.

Name: Brookfield India Real Estate Trust
Overview:
Brookfield India Real Estate Trust REIT (BIRET) is a leading commercial real estate platform in India, focused on owning, operating, and managing large-scale, campus-format office parks in prime business hubs. Its portfolio spans Mumbai, Gurgaon, Noida, and Kolkata, with a strong presence in both SEZ and non-SEZ segments. 

BIRET follows a growth-led strategy through active asset management, acquisitions, and portfolio enhancements, backed by a strong sponsor and access to institutional capital. With a focus on leasing momentum, operational efficiency, and sustainability, it continues to strengthen occupancy and rental yields while positioning for inorganic expansion across key Indian office markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
BIRET targets 1.5–2 msf of leasing and occupancy in the 92–94% range, with potential to exceed 95% in FY27. Supported by strong tenant demand, GCC expansion, and ₹3,500 crore available for acquisitions, the REIT is well-positioned to scale its AUM toward its medium-term target of ₹60,000 crore while sustaining growth in NOI and distributions.

Name: Mindspace Business Parks REIT
Overview:
Mindspace REIT is a Real Estate Investment Trust in India that owns and manages income-generating commercial real estate assets, mainly premium office spaces. The trust primarily invests in Grade-A business parks, standalone office buildings and modern data centre infrastructure that generate rental income from large corporate tenants. 

The REIT is sponsored by the K Raheja Corp Group and is structured as a registered trust listed on Indian stock exchanges. Its portfolio consists of integrated business campuses and high-quality commercial office buildings leased to multinational corporations and domestic companies across different industries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Mindspace REIT plans to expand its commercial real estate portfolio by acquiring additional high-quality office assets and developing new spaces within its existing campuses. The trust aims to strengthen its presence in key business districts while maintaining high occupancy levels through long-term leases with multinational and technology companies.

Name: Embassy Office Parks REIT
Overview:
Embassy Office Parks REIT is India’s first publicly listed Real Estate Investment Trust that owns, operates and invests in income-generating commercial real estate assets. The trust primarily focuses on premium office parks and business campuses that generate stable rental income from large corporate tenants. Its portfolio mainly consists of Grade-A office parks located in major technology and business hubs.

The REIT structure allows investors to participate in commercial real estate through publicly traded units without directly owning property. Embassy Office Parks REIT manages large integrated office campuses that include office buildings along with amenities such as hotels, retail spaces and recreational facilities.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Embassy Office Parks REIT plans to expand its portfolio by acquiring additional high-quality commercial assets and developing new office spaces within its existing campuses. The trust aims to strengthen relationships with multinational tenants and attract new companies seeking modern and sustainable workspaces in major business hubs.

Name: Godrej Properties Ltd
Overview:
Godrej Properties Limited (GPL) is one of India’s leading real estate developers, leveraging the strong legacy, trust, and values of the Godrej Group. The company focuses on high-quality residential and mixed-use projects across major cities like Pune, MMR, Bangalore, and Delhi NCR, with a growing presence in peripheral markets and tier 2 cities through plotted developments. GPL combines innovation, sustainability, and timely delivery to maintain its strong brand positioning in the real estate sector.
 
 Business Segments 
 
 Residential Developments: ~90% of total revenue
 
 Commercial/Office/Other Projects: ~10% of total revenue
 
 Key Geographies: Major share from MMR, NCR, Pune, Bangalore
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Godrej Properties is set to sustain strong growth by leveraging its trusted brand, robust urban presence, and aggressive land acquisition strategy. With new project launches and a pipeline worth over ₹47,000 crore in sales potential, the company is capitalizing on urban housing demand, especially in premium and township formats. Its geographic diversification into tier-2 cities and plotted developments provides additional upside.

Name: DLF Ltd
Overview:
DLF Ltd is a leading Indian real estate developer, actively engaged in residential and commercial property development, leasing, hospitality, maintenance, and ESG-focused initiatives. The company is recognized for its sustainability leadership, consistently featuring in the Dow Jones Sustainability Index for Emerging Markets.
 
 Business Segments with Revenue Breakdown (FY2025)
 Development Business (Residential & Commercial Sales):
 This is the primary revenue driver, contributing about 78% of consolidated revenue. In FY2025, development revenue amounted to around ₹7,018 crore out of total revenue of ₹8,996 crore.
 
 Leasing/Annuity Business (Rental Income):
 The annuity business (DLF Cyber City Developers Ltd and rental assets) contributed approximately 20% to overall revenues, estimated at ₹1,800 crore.
 
 Other Income & Services (Maintenance, Hospitality, Power):
 Other auxiliary services and income streams, such as maintenance, hospitality, and power, contributed about 2% of the revenue, around ₹178 crore.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
DLF’s growth is supported by strategic launches of high-value luxury residential projects and sustained momentum in key metro and Tier-1 markets. Elevated demand for Grade A office and retail spaces, driven by the technology sector and global captives, provides additional revenue potential. Focused investments in R&D and technology, including smart buildings and customer-centric digital platforms, enhance operational efficiency and customer experience.

Name: Oberoi Realty Ltd
Overview:
Oberoi Realty Ltd, headquartered in Mumbai, is a diversified real estate developer with operations across residential, commercial, retail, hospitality, and social infrastructure segments. The company has built a strong reputation for premium developments, strategic land acquisition, and prudent financial management, with a core presence in Mumbai and selective expansion into NCR.

Its strategy focuses on creating long-term value through high-quality design, superior amenities, and curated environments around its projects. With a balanced portfolio across mid-income, upper-middle, and luxury housing, alongside commercial and hospitality assets, Oberoi Realty maintains a disciplined, profitability-first approach while leveraging urban infrastructure growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
FY26 is expected to be a high-activity year with multiple project launches, revenue recognition from completed inventory like Three Sixty West, and continued strong demand in Mumbai and NCR. Infrastructure upgrades, a robust pipeline, and disciplined capital allocation position Oberoi Realty for sustained profitability and growth while maintaining its premium brand positioning.

Name: Phoenix Mills Ltd
Overview:
Phoenix Mills Ltd is India’s leading retail-led mixed-use real estate developer and operator, with a diversified portfolio spanning malls, commercial offices, residential projects, and hospitality assets. The company operates iconic retail destinations across major gateway cities, with a strong focus on premiumization, brand curation, and enhancing trading densities.

Over the years, Phoenix Mills has strategically expanded beyond retail into offices and hotels, creating integrated urban ecosystems. The recent acquisition of full ownership in ISMDPL strengthens its control over a high-growth platform, enabling it to leverage operational synergies, accelerate leasing, and unlock future development potential across multiple asset classes.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is confident of double-digit EBITDA growth driven by retail rental uplift, office leasing ramp-up, and hospitality expansion. Strategic control of ISMDPL enables faster execution, monetization options, and diversification into a retail-office-hotel mix, positioning the company to capitalize on rising demand for premium real estate in India’s top cities.

Name: Knowledge Realty Trust
Overview:
Knowledge Realty Trust (KRT) is India's largest Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT), specializing in owning and managing high-quality office properties across major cities. The trust's portfolio includes 29 Grade A office assets totaling 46.3 million square feet, with a committed occupancy rate of 91.4% and a weighted average lease expiry of 8.4 years. Its assets are strategically located in key markets such as Mumbai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai, Gurugram, and GIFT City, hosting over 450 tenants, including leading Indian corporations and global multinationals

KRT's operations are bolstered by sponsorship from Blackstone, the world's largest alternative asset manager, and Sattva Group, a prominent Indian real estate developer.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Knowledge Realty Trust is poised for continued growth, with plans to expand its portfolio through strategic acquisitions and developments in high-demand office spaces. The trust aims to enhance its rental income by attracting long-term tenants and optimizing operational efficiencies.

Name: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd
Overview:
Prestige Estate Projects Ltd, headquartered in Bengaluru, is one of India’s leading real estate developers with a pan-India presence and a diverse portfolio covering residential, commercial, retail, and hospitality segments. Over the years, Prestige has built a strong reputation for quality developments, landmark projects, and timely delivery across major cities.
 
 Business Segments
 Residential:
 The core revenue driver, with a strong pipeline of premium, mid-income, and luxury housing projects across Tier-I and Tier-II cities.
 
 Commercial Offices:
 Develops and leases Grade A office spaces in top business hubs, including large-scale commercial complexes in Bengaluru, Mumbai, and Delhi-NCR.
 
 Retail:
 Operates shopping malls and retail hubs; expanding presence in Tier-II cities with new mall launches.
 
 Hospitality:
 Owns and operates hotels, convention centres, and allied hospitality assets, adding diversification and annuity income streams.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Prestige Estates Projects is actively expanding its footprint across major Indian cities, including Bengaluru, Delhi-NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Hyderabad, and Goa. The company plans to launch multiple residential projects, aiming to double its residential launches compared to previous years. In the Delhi-NCR region, Prestige Estates is developing a 62.5-acre integrated township, which will include residential units, retail spaces, and educational facilities. Additionally, the company has acquired significant land parcels in cities like Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Chennai, and Mumbai, with plans to develop housing projects with a potential gross development value exceeding ₹20,000 crore.

Name: Aditya Birla Real Estate Ltd
Overview:
Century Textiles and Industries Ltd, originally a textile manufacturer, has diversified into three key sectors—real estate, pulp and paper, and textiles—becoming a prominent player in each domain. Its real estate arm, Birla Estates, has established itself as a strong brand in premium and luxury housing, with projects spread across key metropolitan markets.

The company follows a strategy of combining brand equity, prime locations, and customer trust to deliver value-driven projects. With a significant development pipeline and partnerships with reputed global investors, it is poised to leverage long-term growth opportunities while maintaining a disciplined capital structure and focus on sustainable development.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to achieve ₹15,000 crore in annual sales over the next three years, with growth back-ended towards FY26. The focus will be on executing the robust launch pipeline, driving sales in luxury and premium housing, expanding redevelopment projects in Mumbai, and leveraging strategic partnerships to ensure consistent growth.

Name: Lodha Developers Ltd
Overview:
Macrotech Developers Ltd, widely recognized through its Lodha brand, is a leading real estate developer with a dominant presence in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR) and Pune. Holding over 10% market share in MMR, the company has delivered projects spanning over 85 million sq. ft. and continues to expand using a capital-light Joint Development Agreement (JDA) model.
 
Business Segments
 Residential Development: Core revenue driver, contributing ~90-95% of total revenue through large-scale housing projects across MMR and Pune.
 
 Commercial Rental Assets: Small but growing segment with four assets, contributing ~5-10% of revenue, including Xperia Mall and other developments.
 
 New Markets: Plans to enter Bengaluru’s residential market using the same asset-light JDA strategy.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Lodha is targeting significant market share growth, with plans to raise its presence to 15% in key regions like MMR and Pune. The company is executing a robust launch pipeline, including 22 new projects across MMR, Pune, and Bengaluru, and has committed Rs 3,500 crore by March to strengthen execution. Lodha's strategic entry into the Bengaluru market, coupled with its capital-light JDA model, supports rapid and scalable expansion.

Name: Ganesh Housing Corporation Ltd
Overview:
Ganesh Housing Corporation Ltd, incorporated in 1991, is a leading Gujarat-based real estate developer engaged in residential, commercial, and infrastructure projects. The company is known for its organized housing and construction solutions, with several million square feet of completed developments and a significant pipeline under execution. Certified under multiple international quality and safety standards, it has built a strong reputation for timely delivery and sustainable practices.

Its portfolio spans premium residential projects, large-scale commercial complexes, integrated townships, and Special Economic Zones. With a substantial unencumbered land bank and a flexible monetization model, the company strategically aligns project launches with market demand to optimize returns. Ganesh Housing is also expanding into annuity-yielding assets, positioning itself for both steady cash flows and value accretion over the medium to long term.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ganesh Housing Corporation Ltd is strategically enhancing its market presence by expanding into tier-II cities, capitalizing on the growing demand for Grade A office spaces and affordable housing. The company is focusing on developing large-scale townships and integrated mixed-use projects, such as the 450-acre township in Ahmedabad, to cater to the evolving needs of urban residents. Additionally, Ganesh Housing is investing in technology-driven initiatives like the Million Minds Tech City, aimed at fostering innovation and attracting IT and ITeS industries.

Name: Kolte Patil Developers Ltd
Overview:
Kolte Patil Developers Ltd is a prominent real estate development company based in India, specializing in residential, commercial, and township projects. The company has a strong presence in key markets, including Pune, Bengaluru, and Mumbai, and is known for its commitment to quality and timely delivery.

With a diversified portfolio and a focus on customer satisfaction, Kolte Patil Developers aims to be a leading player in the Indian real estate sector, contributing to the development of sustainable and innovative urban spaces.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company plans to accelerate project completions, expand market presence, and optimize costs to enhance profitability. Focus remains on strengthening cash flows, reducing debt, and sustaining growth across core and new markets, positioning the company for long-term resilience in the real estate sector.

Name: Ajmera Realty & Infra India Ltd
Overview:
Ajmera Realty & Infra India Ltd. (ARIIL), established in 1985, is a prominent real estate developer with a diversified portfolio encompassing residential and commercial properties. The company operates across key Indian cities, including Mumbai, Bengaluru, and Ahmedabad, and has expanded its presence internationally in Bahrain and the UK. ARIIL is recognized for its commitment to quality construction, timely delivery, and sustainable development practices.

ARIIL's operations are primarily segmented into residential development, commercial leasing, and redevelopment projects. The residential segment focuses on mid-to-premium housing, while the commercial segment involves leasing office spaces.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
ARIIL aims to achieve a revenue target of ₹1,600 crore for FY26. The company is focusing on accelerating project execution and obtaining necessary approvals to enhance pre-sales and collections in the upcoming quarters. With a strong project pipeline and strategic initiatives, ARIIL is poised for continued growth and value creation for its stakeholders.

Name: Indiqube Spaces Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 2015, IndiQube Spaces Ltd is a leading provider of managed, tech-driven workplace solutions in India. The company offers a range of flexible office spaces, including private cabins, dedicated workstations, and shared coworking environments, catering to businesses of all sizes. With over 115 centers across 15 cities, IndiQube has established a strong presence in the Indian market, serving a diverse clientele. Their offerings are designed to enhance employee experience through modern interiors, amenities, and a host of value-added services.

IndiQube's commitment to sustainability is evident in its eco-conscious designs and operations. The company integrates technology into its services, providing seamless management of workspace operations and employee engagement.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

IndiQube Spaces Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to significantly expand its assets under management (AUM), targeting a footprint of 11.47 million square feet by the end of the financial year 2027-28. Additionally, IndiQube is focusing on enhancing its technological infrastructure to offer integrated workplace solutions that streamline business processes and improve employee engagement.

Name: Raymond Realty Ltd
Overview:
Raymond Realty Ltd, established in 2017, is the real estate development arm of the Raymond Group, a renowned Indian conglomerate. The company specializes in developing premium residential and mixed-use projects, primarily in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR). Raymond Realty aims to deliver high-quality living spaces characterized by modern design, sustainable practices, and a commitment to enhancing urban lifestyles.

In May 2025, Raymond Realty became an independent entity following the demerger from Raymond Ltd. This strategic move allows the company to focus solely on its real estate ventures, leveraging its extensive land bank and development expertise. Raymond Realty's portfolio includes notable projects like "The Address by GS" and "TenX Habitat," reflecting its dedication to excellence in the real estate sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Raymond Realty is poised for significant growth, aiming to launch six new residential projects in the MMR, with a combined revenue potential of ₹14,000 crore. The company targets a 30% increase in sales bookings, aiming for ₹3,000 crore, driven by strong housing demand. With a focus on delivering projects with profit margins exceeding 20%, Raymond Realty plans to maintain a net-debt-free status and achieve a return on equity of 16.2% by FY28.

Name: Capacite Infraprojects Ltd
Overview:
Capacit’e Infraprojects Limited is an Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) company specializing in turnkey solutions for high-rise and super high-rise buildings, commercial complexes, and urban infrastructure projects. Established in 2012, the company offers comprehensive services across residential, commercial, and institutional sectors, catering to both public and private sector clients in India.

With a strong presence in major cities including Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai, and the National Capital Region, Capacit’e Infraprojects has successfully executed numerous projects, establishing itself as a trusted partner in the construction industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Capacit’e Infraprojects aims to achieve a 20% revenue growth in FY26, targeting a total income of approximately ₹2,888 crore. The company expects significant execution ramp-up in the second half of FY26, as activities pick up post-monsoon. Management remains confident of hitting or exceeding the full-year revenue guidance, supported by a healthy order book and strong bidding activity.

Name: AWFIS Space Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Awfis Space Solutions Limited, founded in 2014, is a leading provider of flexible workspace solutions in India. The company caters to a diverse clientele, including startups, SMEs, and large enterprises, offering a range of services from individual flexible desks to customized office spaces. With a presence across multiple cities, Awfis aims to meet the evolving needs of businesses seeking adaptable and cost-effective workspace solutions.

In Q1 FY25, the company added 5,368 seats and nine new centers, reflecting its robust expansion strategy. The company's diverse offerings and strategic locations position it well to capitalize on the growing demand for flexible workspaces in India.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
