Karnataka Bank, incorporated in 1924 and headquartered in Mangaluru, is a leading private sector bank in India offering a comprehensive suite of retail, corporate, SME, and agricultural banking services. With a network of 952 branches and 1,228 ATMs/e-lobbies, it maintains a strong footprint across the country, with a particular presence in southern states. The bank serves customers through physical branches, digital banking platforms, and a growing array of fintech partnerships.

The institution is currently undergoing a multi-year transformation journey aimed at diversifying its loan book, expanding into high-yield retail and SME segments, and strengthening its digital capabilities. This includes launching new products, enhancing its branch-led and digital acquisition channels, and executing capacity-building initiatives to improve operational efficiency and profitability while maintaining a strong capital adequacy profile.
Key Takeways:
Growth and Outlook
For FY26, the bank targets 11–12% net advances growth (14–18% gross), NIM between 3.2–3.4%, cost-to-income near 55%, and ROE in the range of 12–14%. Dividend for FY25 was set at ₹5 per share. Focus remains on scaling high-yield retail and SME portfolios, deepening digital penetration, and sustaining asset quality with credit costs below 0.5%.

Name: Quadrant Future Tek Ltd
Overview:
Quadrant Future-Tek Ltd (QFT) is a technology-driven company engaged in developing advanced train control and signalling systems while also manufacturing specialty cables for critical industries. Certified under ISO 14001:2015, ISO 9001:2015, IRIS, and TS Quality Management Systems, QFT plays a central role in the Indian Railways’ KAVACH project, India’s indigenous train collision avoidance system. Its cable portfolio serves diverse applications across railway rolling stock, naval defence, solar energy, and electric vehicles, supporting safety, reliability, and sustainability in infrastructure.

The company’s product portfolio spans control and power cables for railway coaches and locomotives, signalling cables, TUV-certified solar cables, irradiation cross-linked automotive cables, and a wide range of interconnect products such as electrical connectors and wiring harnesses. With manufacturing facilities in Mohali and engineering centres in Bangalore and Hyderabad, QFT combines production scale with design capabilities. In FY24, its revenue was predominantly cable-driven, with 77% from railway-related wires and cables, 22% from defence cables, and 1% from other sources, backed by an installed capacity of 1,887.6 MT of specialty cables and large-scale signalling systems.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management remains highly confident in scaling both Kavach and cables businesses, expecting multi-fold growth over the medium term. Solar, EV, and exports are expected to provide incremental opportunities in cables, while Kavach orders (locomotive and trackside) will drive transformational growth from FY26 onwards.

Name: Servotech Renewable Power System Ltd
Overview:
Servotech Renewable Power System Ltd, established in 2004 and headquartered in New Delhi, is a prominent Indian company specializing in renewable energy solutions. The company is listed on the NSE under the ticker symbol SERVOTECH and operates under the leadership of CEO Raman Bhatia.

Over the years, Servotech has expanded its product portfolio to include a range of high-end solar products and exceptionally efficient EV chargers. The company's commitment to revolutionizing the energy sector is evident in its continuous efforts to provide sustainable and technologically advanced solutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Servotech aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing its manufacturing capabilities. The company is focused on strengthening its presence in the electric vehicle charging infrastructure and solar energy sectors.

Name: Swelect Energy Systems Ltd
Overview:
Swelect Energy Systems Ltd is an Indian solar and renewable energy solutions provider, engaged in solar power projects, EPC services, and manufacturing of solar components. The company serves commercial, industrial, and utility-scale projects.

Headquartered in Ahmedabad, Swelect Energy Systems emphasizes technology, efficiency, and quality in renewable energy. Its integrated capabilities allow project execution from design to operation, ensuring value for clients and investors.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Swelect Energy Systems Ltd is actively expanding its footprint in the renewable energy sector, focusing on scaling its solar energy capabilities. The company has secured over 150 MW of orders for its high-efficiency TOPCon bifacial solar modules, underscoring its commitment to quality and innovation. Additionally, Swelect has raised ₹290 crore through Non-Convertible Debentures, which will be utilized to expand its Independent Power Producer (IPP) portfolio to 1 GW by 2026-27

Name: Alpex Solar Ltd
Overview:
Founded in 2008 and headquartered in Greater Noida, Alpex Solar Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in photovoltaic (PV) modules and comprehensive solar energy solutions. The company operates a state-of-the-art, vertically integrated facility spanning 10,000 square meters, employing over 250 professionals. 

Alpex Solar offers a diverse range of high-quality products, including bifacial, mono PERC, and half-cut solar modules, as well as in-house manufactured aluminum frames and junction boxes. These offerings cater to various applications, from residential rooftop installations to large-scale utility projects. Alpex Solar's commitment to innovation and quality has earned it certifications such as ALMM, IEC, and BIS, ensuring compliance with international standards.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Alpex Solar Ltd is poised for continued growth, focusing on expanding its manufacturing capacity and enhancing product offerings. The establishment of a second manufacturing facility in Mathura will bolster production capabilities, enabling the company to meet the increasing demand for solar energy solutions. Alpex Solar plans to diversify its product portfolio by incorporating advanced technologies and introducing new product lines to cater to emerging market needs.

Name: Australian Premium Solar (India) Ltd
Overview:
Australian Premium Solar Ltd (APS), established in 2013 by Mr. Nikunj Patel, is an Indian solar technology company headquartered in Ahmedabad, Gujarat. APS specializes in manufacturing high-efficiency monocrystalline and polycrystalline solar panels, grid-tied inverters, and solar water pumps. 

The company caters to a diverse clientele across residential, commercial, and agricultural sectors, providing tailored solar solutions to meet varying energy needs. In addition to its manufacturing capabilities, APS offers comprehensive Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) services for solar installations.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

APS is committed to expanding its production capacity to 1.2 GW by 2025, aiming to enhance its manufacturing capabilities and meet the growing demand for solar energy solutions. The company plans to diversify its product offerings, focusing on advanced technologies such as TopCon solar panels and smart inverters.

Name: Spectrum Electrical Industries Ltd
Overview:
Spectrum Electrical Industries Ltd is an Indian company engaged in the design and manufacturing of electrical components and systems. The company caters to various industries, providing customized solutions to meet specific requirements.

In FY25, Spectrum Electrical Industries reported significant growth in its financial performance, driven by increased demand and operational efficiencies.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Spectrum Electrical Industries plans to expand its product offerings and enhance its manufacturing capabilities. The company aims to increase its market share in the electrical components industry and improve profitability through operational efficiencies.

Name: Ideaforge Technology Ltd
Overview:
Ideaforge Technology Ltd is a prominent Indian drone manufacturer specializing in Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS) for defense, industrial, and commercial applications. Established in 2007, the company is headquartered in Mumbai and operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities.

The company focuses on delivering high-quality drone solutions, leveraging advanced technologies and a skilled workforce. Ideaforge is committed to innovation and operational excellence to meet the evolving demands of its clientele and achieve sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Ideaforge Technology Ltd aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and enhancing manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on innovation and quality to meet the evolving demands of the drone manufacturing industry. With a strong financial performance and strategic initiatives, Ideaforge is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Solex Energy Ltd
Overview:
Solex Energy Ltd, founded in 1995 and headquartered in Surat, Gujarat, is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in solar photovoltaic (PV) modules and providing comprehensive Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) services in the solar energy sector. The company offers a diverse range of products, including solar home lighting systems, street lights, lanterns, power plants, solar inverters, and water heating systems. Solex Energy also provides turnkey solutions for rooftop systems and large-scale solar power plants, serving both domestic and international markets. 

With a strong commitment to innovation and sustainability, Solex Energy has established itself as a trusted partner in the renewable energy industry. The company is engaged in Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) and Original Design Manufacturer (ODM) partnerships with Indian and international entities for module production under their brand names.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Solex Energy Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to expand its operations by increasing its annual solar module manufacturing capacity from 700 MW to 1.5 GW by the end of the year, with a further expansion to 4.5 GW by 2026. Additionally, Solex Energy aims to establish a 1 GW annual solar cell production facility by mid-2027, with plans to expand to 2 GW.

Name: Indosolar Ltd
Overview:
Founded in 2005, Indosolar Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in solar photovoltaic (PV) cells and modules. The company operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Noida, Uttar Pradesh, with an annual production capacity of 1.3 gigawatts (GW), focusing on advanced N-Type modules in M10R and G12 formats.

Indosolar Ltd is a subsidiary of Waaree Energies Limited, one of India's largest solar module manufacturers. The company serves a global clientele, delivering over 450 megawatts (MW) of solar modules to more than 260 customers across various regions, including India, Europe, the United States, the Middle East, and Asia
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Indosolar Ltd is poised for significant growth, aiming to leverage government incentives to expand its production capacity to 10 gigawatts by 2026. The company plans to enhance its manufacturing capabilities by incorporating advanced technologies and expanding its product portfolio to meet the increasing demand for renewable energy solutions. Strategic initiatives include strengthening its global presence and fostering partnerships to support the transition to a sustainable energy future.

Name: Danish Power Ltd
Overview:
Founded in July 1985, Danish Power Limited is a Jaipur-based manufacturer specializing in transformers and power equipment. The company offers a diverse product range, including inverter duty transformers designed for renewable energy projects such as solar and wind farms, as well as oil and dry-type power and distribution transformers, control relay panels, and substation automation services.

Danish Power operates two manufacturing facilities in Jaipur—one in Sitapur Industrial Area and another in Mahindra World City, which commenced operations in November 2022. The company is expanding its capacity with plans to develop additional manufacturing units in these locations.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Danish Power Limited is strategically focusing on expanding its manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand in the renewable energy sector. The company plans to enhance its production capacity for inverter duty transformers, which are essential for integrating renewable energy sources into the grid.

Name: Bhageria Industries Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1989, Bhageria Industries Ltd is an Indian company engaged in the manufacturing and trading of dyes and dye intermediates, as well as the generation and distribution of solar power. The company operates through two primary segments: Chemicals and Solar Power. Its chemical products include H-acid, gamma acid, and vinyl sulphone, which find applications in various industries such as textiles, paints, and coatings.


The company serves global textile, leather, and specialty chemical industries, with exports contributing significantly. The company generates and distributes solar power, contributing to India's growing renewable energy capacity.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Bhageria Industries Ltd is poised for continued growth, driven by its strong performance in the Chemicals segment and strategic expansion in the Solar Power sector. The company's focus on international markets and renewable energy projects positions it well to capitalize on global trends towards sustainability and clean energy.

Name: TGV Sraac Ltd
Overview:
TGV Sraac Ltd is a prominent Indian chemical manufacturing company specializing in the production of chlor-alkali products, chloromethanes, castor derivatives, and fatty acids. Established in 1981, the company operates in two primary segments: Chemicals and Oils & Fats. With a strong presence in the chemical industry, TGV Sraac Ltd has established itself as a key player in the production of essential chemical products. 

The company is publicly listed and has demonstrated a strong operational and financial profile. TGV Sraac Ltd maintains a commitment to transparency and stakeholder engagement, as evidenced by the release of its Q1 FY26 earnings call transcript. Additionally, the company is actively involved in corporate social responsibility through its initiative, the TGV Foundation, which focuses on providing skill training to underprivileged youth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

TGV SRAAC Ltd is actively enhancing its production capabilities and sustainability initiatives. The company is expanding its caustic soda production capacity by 50%, aiming to reach an annual output of approximately 500,000 tons. This expansion is facilitated by the implementation of thyssenkrupp nucera’s advanced eBiTAC® v7 electrolyzer technology, which offers high energy efficiency and operational safety.

Name: DMCC Speciality Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
DMCC Speciality Chemicals Ltd (formerly Dharamsi Morarji Chemical Company), founded in 1919 and headquartered in Mumbai, is India's pioneering producer of sulfuric acid and phosphate fertilizers. It has evolved into a fully integrated specialty and commodity chemical manufacturer, offering a range of base acids, boron chemicals, functional intermediates, and life science chemicals. Over its century-long journey, DMCC has commercialized downstream sulfur-based chemistries and expanded exports to over 25 countries across five continents.
The company emphasizes custom manufacturing services—including sulphonation, chlorosulphonation, and esterification—supported by century-old expertise in plant design, operation, and debottlenecking. With ISO 9001:2015 accreditation, DMCC also commits to sustainable development and minimal environmental footprint.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

DMCC Speciality Chemicals Ltd is actively pursuing growth through strategic initiatives aimed at expanding its product offerings and enhancing operational efficiency. The company is focusing on increasing the utilization of its existing capacities, with plans to invest approximately ₹10–15 crore in smaller projects to optimize operations and drive growth.

Name: Ganesh Benzoplast Ltd
Overview:
Ganesh Benzoplast Ltd is an India-based manufacturer of industrial adhesives, resins, and plastic products, catering to sectors such as construction, furniture, packaging, and automotive. Established in 1978, the company has developed a strong reputation for high-quality, durable products and a wide portfolio of adhesives, laminates, and resins. Its operations focus on both domestic and international markets, serving OEMs, distributors, and industrial users. The company’s manufacturing facilities utilize modern technology and quality systems to meet both domestic and global standards.

Ganesh Benzoplast also emphasizes innovation and R&D, continuously improving formulations to enhance product performance, durability, and environmental compliance. Over the years, it has expanded its distribution network across India and globally, strengthening its market presence. The company actively focuses on sustainability and eco-friendly manufacturing practices to reduce its carbon footprint and adhere to regulatory standards.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

GBL's strategic positioning at key Indian ports and its diversified business model provide a solid foundation for future growth. The company's extensive experience in liquid storage and handling, coupled with its robust infrastructure, enables it to cater to a wide array of industries, including chemicals, petroleum, and food processing. Additionally, GBL's commitment to quality and safety standards, as evidenced by its ISO certifications, enhances its reputation and reliability among clients.

Name: Anlon Healthcare Ltd
Overview:
Anlon Healthcare Limited is a pharmaceutical chemical manufacturing company engaged in the production of pharma intermediates and active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). These products act as key raw materials used in the manufacturing of medicines such as tablets, capsules, ointments, and syrups, and also find applications in nutraceuticals, personal care products, and animal health solutions. The company operates in the specialty chemical segment and focuses on manufacturing high-purity chemical compounds used in drug formulations.

The company operates manufacturing facilities supported by in-house laboratories and research capabilities to develop and produce pharmaceutical intermediates and APIs. Its product portfolio includes several commercialized products along with multiple products under development and testing, helping the company expand its pipeline.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its manufacturing capacity and strengthen its product portfolio to meet the growing global demand for pharmaceutical intermediates and APIs. It aims to focus on developing complex and high-value chemical compounds while increasing its presence in regulated international markets. The company is also working on expanding its research and development capabilities to support new product development and faster commercialization.

Name: Gem Aromatics Ltd
Overview:
Gem Aromatics Limited, established in October 1997, is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in essential oils, aroma chemicals, and value-added derivatives. With over two decades of experience, the company has developed a diverse product portfolio that caters to various industries, including oral care, cosmetics, nutraceuticals, pharmaceuticals, wellness, and personal care. 

Gem Aromatics offers a range of products such as mint and its derivatives, clove and its derivatives, phenol-based compounds, and other synthetic and natural ingredients. The company operates manufacturing facilities in Budaun, Silvassa, and Dahej, ensuring efficient production and supply to meet global demand.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Gem Aromatics is focused on expanding its manufacturing capacities and diversifying its product portfolio to meet the growing demand in the fragrance and flavor industry. The company is enhancing its Dahej facility to strengthen its citral product category and introduce high-value products such as safranal and damascene.

Name: Ultramarine & Pigments Ltd
Overview:
Ultramarine & Pigments Ltd (UPL) is an Indian company specializing in the manufacture and sale of pigments, surfactants, and IT-enabled services, including business process outsourcing (BPO). With over 60 years of expertise, UPL serves both domestic and international markets from its manufacturing units in Tamil Nadu and Gujarat. 

The company operates in various segments, including pigments, surfactants, detergents, and BPO activities, catering to diverse industries such as personal care, construction, and publishing. UPL's pigment division has a capacity of 7,500 metric tons per annum (MTPA), while its surfactant division boasts a capacity of 62,000 MTPA.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
UPL aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product portfolio and increasing its presence in international markets. The company's focus on innovation and operational efficiency is expected to drive sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Indo Borax & Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Indo Borax & Chemicals Ltd specializes in the manufacturing of boron-based chemicals, primarily borax, boric acid, and related compounds. These chemicals find applications across industries such as glass, ceramics, agriculture, and pharmaceuticals. The company has carved a niche as one of India’s leading players in this specialized segment.

Its manufacturing operations are based on proprietary processes and stringent quality standards, allowing it to serve both domestic and global clients. Strong backward linkages and efficient cost management help the company maintain its competitive position.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook 
Expansion into high-purity boron derivatives for pharmaceuticals and specialty industries is expected to strengthen margins. However, its growth remains linked to cyclical demand trends in end-user industries.

Name: Amines & Plasticizers Ltd
Overview:
Amines & Plasticizers Ltd (APL), established in 1973, is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in over 60 varieties of organic chemicals. The company produces a diverse range of products, including amines, solvents, and fertilizers, serving various industries such as agriculture, pharmaceuticals, and chemicals. 

APL operates multiple manufacturing units across strategic locations, ensuring scalability and efficiency in production. With a strong focus on research and development, the company continuously innovates to meet the evolving needs of its customers. Headquartered in Mumbai, APL has established a robust presence in the chemical manufacturing sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
APL aims to reach a revenue target of ₹1,000 crore by FY26. Growth will be driven by expanding its product portfolio, enhancing manufacturing capabilities, and increasing export contributions. The focus on innovation and operational efficiency positions the company for sustainable growth.

Name: Sigachi Industries Ltd
Overview:
Sigachi Industries Ltd is a leading manufacturer of microcrystalline cellulose (MCC) and other excipients, catering to the pharmaceutical, food, and personal care industries. The company has established a strong presence in the global market, known for its high-quality products and commitment to innovation.

In June 2025, Sigachi faced a tragic incident at its Hyderabad plant, resulting in significant loss of life and operational disruptions. Despite this setback, the company reported a 34% increase in revenue to ₹128 crore in Q1 FY26. However, the operational challenges led to a net loss of ₹101 crore for the quarter, contrasting with a profit of ₹13 crore in the same period the previous year.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sigachi Industries Ltd is strategically expanding its global footprint and product portfolio to drive sustainable growth. The company is enhancing its Microcrystalline Cellulose (MCC) production capacity at its Dahej SEZ facility, aiming to increase total installed capacity to 30,000 MTPA. This expansion is expected to bolster supply reliability for both domestic and international clients. Additionally, Sigachi is diversifying its offerings by integrating advanced intermediates and Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) through the acquisition of Trimax Biosciences.

Name: Naperol Investments Ltd
Overview:
National Peroxide Ltd, incorporated in 1954, is primarily involved in the investment and leasing sector. The company earns income by deploying its capital into financial instruments or leasing assets to generate steady returns. As a legacy participant in its industry, National Peroxide draws on decades of experience to manage its financial positioning and asset deployment strategy.

Beyond its financial operations, National Peroxide also owns and manages assets that are leased out, suggesting a dual focus on generating revenue through both investment returns and lease contracts. This diversified approach aims to provide balanced and stable sources of income in a capital-intensive business landscape.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
National Peroxide’s near-term growth will hinge on stabilizing its investment yields and leasing occupancy. If the company can reverse the downtrend and lift quarterly sales back toward previous levels—say toward ₹74–75 crore—and restore margins, it may reclaim profitability later in FY26. However, without specific guidance or new initiatives, expectations for recovery remain tentative.

Name: Kronox Lab Sciences Ltd
Overview:
Kronox Lab Sciences Ltd manufactures and supplies high-purity specialty chemicals used across pharmaceuticals, nutraceuticals, biotech, agrochemicals, and research applications. Its strength lies in producing chemicals that meet stringent purity requirements, making it a trusted partner for critical industries.

The company’s portfolio includes over 100 products across laboratory and industrial chemicals, serving both domestic clients and global customers. With emphasis on quality, compliance, and R&D, Kronox has established a strong niche in high-purity chemical manufacturing.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company aims to expand its product portfolio, strengthen its R&D, and enhance its export footprint. Growing global demand for high-purity specialty chemicals supports a positive outlook.

Name: Primo Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Primo Chemicals Ltd, established in 1975 and formerly known as Punjab Alkalies & Chemicals Ltd, is a prominent chemical manufacturing company based in India. The company specializes in producing a wide range of chemicals, including caustic soda lye, hydrochloric acid, liquid chlorine, sodium hypochlorite, hydrogen gas, stable bleaching powder, caustic soda flakes, and aluminum chloride. 

These products serve various industries such as paper and pulp, soap and detergents, aluminum, water treatment, and textiles. Primo Chemicals operates in a single business segment, namely chemicals, and has a manufacturing unit located in Naya Nangal, District Ropar, Punjab, India.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Primo Chemicals is poised for continued growth, driven by its strategic initiatives and strong market demand for its products. The company's focus on expanding its product portfolio and enhancing production capabilities positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the chemical industry.

Name: Sree Rayalaseema Hi-Strength Hypo Ltd
Overview: Sree Rayalaseema Hi-Strength Hypo Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer of chemical products such as calcium hypochlorite, stable bleaching powder, and sulphuric acid. The company leverages locally sourced raw materials and specialized chemical processes to deliver cost-effective, high-quality solutions. Its products serve multiple industries including water treatment, textiles, pulp and paper, and sanitation, making it a critical supplier in essential industrial processes. The company maintains stringent quality control, adhering to environmental and safety standards across its production units. With growing demand for water purification and industrial chemicals in India and abroad, Sree Rayalaseema continues to expand its production capacity while ensuring operational efficiency and sustainable practices.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sree Rayalaseema Hi-Strength Hypo Ltd is actively enhancing its production capabilities to meet increasing demand. The company has expanded its Sodium Methoxide solution production from 900 MT to 1,100 MT, reflecting its commitment to scaling operations. Financially, Sree Rayalaseema Hi-Strength Hypo Ltd has demonstrated a robust earnings growth, with an impressive 16% annual increase in earnings per share over the past five years, indicating strong operational performance.

Name: Transpek Industry Ltd
Overview:
Transpek Industries Ltd. is a specialty chemicals manufacturer with a strong presence in domestic and international markets. The company’s product portfolio includes sodium hydrosulfite, hydrogen peroxide, para dichlorobenzene, and optical brightening agents, serving industries such as textiles, dyes and pigments, plastics, paper, and agrochemicals. Its research-driven approach enables continuous innovation, supporting customer needs across multiple applications.

The company exports to over 70 countries, backed by a wide distribution network that enhances its global footprint. With a strong emphasis on sustainability, Transpek integrates environmentally responsible practices into operations while maintaining high quality standards. This approach has helped it build trust and long-standing relationships with its diverse customer base, ensuring relevance in a highly competitive specialty chemicals market.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Transpek Industry Ltd is strategically expanding its global footprint by entering new markets such as South America, Eurasia, and Japan. The company is also focusing on launching complex chemical products, with several awaiting client approvals, which are expected to contribute to future business growth. Despite facing some technical challenges and a lack of analyst coverage, Transpek's strategic initiatives and financial performance indicate a cautious but steady growth trajectory in the global chemicals sector.

Name: Nitta Gelatin India Ltd
Overview:
Nitta Gelatin India Ltd is an India-based manufacturer of gelatin, collagen peptides, and related products for food, pharmaceutical, and nutraceutical applications. The company is part of the global Nitta Gelatin Group and supplies products to pharmaceutical, food, confectionery, and cosmetic industries. Nitta Gelatin India Ltd emphasizes high-quality production using advanced manufacturing techniques and adheres to stringent quality standards.

The company continuously invests in research and development to innovate new products, improve functionality, and cater to the evolving demands of the global market. Its products are exported internationally and distributed domestically, creating a strong presence in both markets. With sustainable production practices and focus on compliance, Nitta Gelatin India Ltd is positioned as a trusted supplier in the gelatin and collagen industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Nitta Gelatin India Ltd is actively pursuing expansion to meet the growing global demand for gelatin and collagen peptides. The company has initiated a ₹60 crore expansion project at its Kakkanad facility in Kerala, aiming to increase collagen peptide production capacity from 600 metric tonnes per year to 1,150 metric tonnes per year.

Name: Vishnu Chemicals Ltd
Overview:
Vishnu Chemicals Ltd is an Indian chemical manufacturing company specializing in agrochemicals, intermediates, and specialty chemicals. The company caters to the agriculture, pharmaceuticals, and industrial sectors.

Headquartered in Hyderabad, Vishnu Chemicals emphasizes R&D, quality control, and sustainable manufacturing practices. Its integrated production facilities ensure consistent quality, cost efficiency, and timely delivery.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company continued to strengthen its operational mix by focusing on specialty chemicals, agrochemicals, and industrial intermediates. Higher contribution from the specialty chemicals division supported steady revenues, while efficient production planning and cost management improved operational margins. Expansion in contract manufacturing and consistent demand from domestic and export markets reinforced business stability during the quarter.

Name: Meghmani Organics Ltd
Overview:
Meghmani Organics Ltd is a leading Indian player in the agrochemical and pigment industries, producing a wide range of crop protection chemicals and pigments. The company serves both domestic and international markets, providing products to diverse industries. It is recognized for delivering high-quality, reliable, and innovative solutions to meet evolving customer needs.

In FY25, Meghmani focused on enhancing production capacity, modernizing manufacturing processes, and optimizing operational efficiency. The company actively pursued sustainability initiatives, such as adopting renewable energy and eco-friendly manufacturing practices, while expanding its research and development capabilities.

Meghmani has strengthened its distribution and supply chain networks to ensure timely delivery across markets. With a strong market presence and robust product portfolio, the company is well-positioned for future growth and long-term value creation.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Meghmani Organics Ltd is aggressively expanding its footprint in the global pigments and agrochemicals markets. The company is investing ₹1,000 crore in capacity expansion, including a ₹375 crore Phase II project to double its Titanium Dioxide (TiO₂) production to 33,000 metric tonnes per annum, complemented by a captive power plant. This move positions Meghmani as a significant player in India's TiO₂ market, which is expected to grow at a 4.2% CAGR

Name: IOL Chemicals & Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Overview:
IOL Chemicals & Pharmaceuticals Ltd is a leading manufacturer of bulk drugs and intermediates in India, specializing in active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) and specialty chemicals. The company caters to both domestic and international markets, with a strong emphasis on quality and compliance.

With a diverse product portfolio and state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities, IOL Chemicals is committed to delivering high-quality pharmaceutical and chemical products to meet global standards.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking ahead, IOL Chemicals aims to expand its product offerings and enhance its manufacturing capabilities. The company is focusing on increasing its export footprint and exploring new markets to drive growth. Additionally, IOL Chemicals is investing in research and development to introduce new products and technologies.

Name: Kiri Industries Ltd
Overview:
Kiri Industries Limited (KIL), established in 1999, is a leading Indian manufacturer and exporter specializing in dyes, dye intermediates, and basic chemicals. The company operates from its headquarters in Ahmedabad, Gujarat, and has a significant presence in the global chemical industry. Kiri Industries is known for its extensive product portfolio, which caters to various sectors, including textiles, automotive, and construction. The company has built a reputation for quality and reliability, making it a preferred supplier for many international clients.

Over the years, Kiri Industries has expanded its operations and product offerings to meet the growing demand in the chemical sector. The company's commitment to innovation and sustainability has driven its growth and enabled it to maintain a competitive edge in the market.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Kiri Industries is optimistic about its growth prospects. The company has signed a Share Purchase Agreement with Zhejiang Longsheng Group to sell its entire 37.57% stake in DyStar for approximately ₹5,765 crore. This transaction, once completed, is expected to provide a significant cash infusion, enabling Kiri Industries to reduce debt and invest in expanding its core chemical business. The company aims to leverage this opportunity to strengthen its financial position and drive future growth.

Name: Jubilant Agri & Consumer Products Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 2008, Jubilant Agri and Consumer Products Ltd (JACPL) is a diversified Indian company engaged in the manufacturing and sale of performance polymers, chemicals, fertilizers, and agri-nutrient products. Operating under the umbrella of the Jubilant Bhartia Group, JACPL serves a wide array of industries, including agriculture, construction, and consumer goods. The company's product portfolio encompasses synthetic latex, food polymers, adhesives, wood finishes, and Single Super Phosphate (SSP) fertilizers, catering to both residential and commercial applications.

Headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana, JACPL has established a strong manufacturing and distribution network across India. The company's agri-nutrient segment, marketed under the brand "RAMBAN," focuses on crop nutrition and plant growth regulators, positioning JACPL as one of the top three preferred brands for SSP in northern India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jubilant Agri and Consumer Products Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to expand its product offerings in the performance polymers and agri-nutrient sectors to cater to the evolving needs of its customers. Additionally, JACPL aims to strengthen its distribution network across India, ensuring wider accessibility of its products.

Name: Shree Pushkar Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd
Overview:
Shree Pushkar Chemicals & Fertilizers Ltd is engaged in the manufacture of dyes, dye intermediates, fertilizers, and basic chemicals. The company has built an integrated business model that allows it to utilize by-products from one process as raw material for another, ensuring efficient operations and reduced environmental impact.

The firm serves both domestic and international markets, with a wide product portfolio catering to textiles, agriculture, and chemical sectors. Its zero-waste philosophy and strong backward integration provide competitive cost advantages while aligning with sustainability goals.
Key Takeways:
Future Growth 
Its focus on expanding value-added dye products and deeper penetration in fertilizer markets is expected to drive long-term growth. Strong exports presence also provides a hedge against domestic market fluctuations.

Name: Pondy Oxides & Chemicals Ltd
Overview: Pondy Oxides & Chemicals Ltd (POCL) is India's leading producer of secondary lead, lead alloys, and plastic additives. Established in 1995, the company specializes in recycling lead from used batteries and other lead-bearing scrap to produce pure lead and lead alloys. POCL also manufactures zinc oxide and other non-ferrous metals. The company serves various industries, including battery manufacturing, rubber, and glass industries, both domestically and internationally.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Pondy Oxides and Chemicals Ltd (POCL) is actively enhancing its lead production capabilities to meet growing demand. The company has initiated a two-phase expansion at its Thervoykandigai plant, targeting a total increase of 72,000 metric tonnes per annum (MTPA). In addition to lead capacity expansion, POCL is focusing on value-added products and forward integration. The company aims to achieve a 20%+ revenue compound annual growth rate (CAGR), 20%+ return on capital employed (ROCE), and 8%+ EBITDA margins by 2030.

Name: Advanced Enzyme Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Advanced Enzyme Technologies Limited is a leading Indian company specializing in the research, development, manufacturing, and marketing of enzymes and probiotics. With a diverse product portfolio, the company serves various industries, including human healthcare and nutrition, animal nutrition, food processing, and non-food processing. Advanced Enzyme Technologies operates globally, offering over 400 products developed from more than 68 enzymes and probiotics. 

The company is committed to providing eco-safe solutions to a wide variety of industries. Its offerings aim to replace traditionally used chemicals with eco-friendly enzymatic solutions. Advanced Enzyme Technologies' dedication to innovation and sustainability underscores its position as a global leader in the enzyme manufacturing sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Advanced Enzyme Technologies aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing its global presence. The company's focus on innovation and sustainability positions it well to meet the evolving needs of its diverse customer base. With a strong foundation and strategic initiatives, Advanced Enzyme Technologies is poised for sustained growth in the enzyme manufacturing sector.

Name: DDev Plastiks Industries Ltd
Overview:
DDev Plastiks Industries Ltd is a manufacturer of specialized polymers serving automotive, electrical, and construction sectors. The company offers over 200 polymer compounds, catering to both domestic and export markets. Its focus on precision engineering and consistent quality has helped it establish a strong brand in the polymer industry.

In FY25, DDev Plastiks focused on product diversification, capacity expansion, and operational efficiency improvements. Continuous R&D and innovation in polymer formulations enabled the company to meet the changing requirements of clients across multiple industries.

The company has maintained long-term partnerships with major customers and is actively exploring export opportunities to expand its international footprint. Its ability to adapt to market trends and technological advancements positions it well for sustainable growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ddev Plastiks Industries Ltd is poised for significant growth, driven by strategic capacity expansions and diversification into high-demand sectors. Additionally, Ddev Plastiks aims to expand its overall polymer compounding capacity from approximately 28,000 MT to 200,000 MT by FY30, positioning itself as a leader in the Indian market. Ddev Plastiks is also focusing on sustainability initiatives, including waste reduction and energy efficiency, to align with global environmental standards. With a low debt-to-equity ratio and a commitment to reinvesting profits, the company is well-positioned for long-term growth.

Name: Kalyani Steels Ltd
Overview:
Kalyani Steels Ltd is a leading manufacturer of high-quality alloy and special steels in India. The company specializes in producing forged and rolled products for various industries, including automotive, engineering, and defense. With a strong presence in both domestic and international markets, Kalyani Steels is recognized for its commitment to quality and innovation.

The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities and adheres to stringent quality standards to meet the diverse needs of its customers. Kalyani Steels' product portfolio includes a wide range of steel products catering to various industrial applications, ensuring its position as a key player in the steel manufacturing sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Kalyani Steels aims to expand production capacity, invest in advanced technologies, and strengthen R&D to develop new steel grades. The company also plans to broaden its market reach, targeting revenue growth and reinforcing its position in the domestic and international steel markets.

Name: Interarch Building Solutions Ltd
Overview:
Interarch Building Solutions Ltd is a leading provider of pre-engineered steel buildings and structures, serving sectors such as industrial, commercial, and infrastructure. The company's offerings include design, manufacturing, and installation of steel buildings, providing turnkey solutions to clients. Interarch is known for its innovative designs and commitment to quality.

In FY25, Interarch Building Solutions reported a total income of ₹1,474.48 crore, up from ₹1,306.31 crore in the previous year. The company posted a net profit of ₹107.83 crore, reflecting a 25% increase compared to FY24. This growth was driven by robust demand and efficient project execution.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Interarch plans to capitalize on the growing demand for pre-engineered buildings by expanding its production capacity and exploring new market opportunities. The company is focusing on innovation and sustainability to drive long-term growth.

Name: Shyam Metalics & Energy Ltd
Overview:
Shyam Metalics is a diversified steel and metals producer engaged in manufacturing pellets, sponge iron, ferro alloys, finished steel products, stainless steel, aluminum foil, and generating captive power. It is India’s sixth-largest integrated steel producer, a leading ferro alloys manufacturer, the fourth-largest sponge iron producer, and a significant player in the pellet segment. The company’s operations are spread across seven manufacturing plants in West Bengal, Odisha, and Madhya Pradesh, with an aggregate installed capacity of 15.13 MTPA, supported by 476 MW of captive power generation, meeting 83% of its power needs at a substantial cost advantage.

Its product portfolio is well-diversified across intermediates, ferro alloys, finished steel, stainless steel, and aluminum foil, with new product lines including ductile iron pipes, hot flat products, and CRM stainless in the pipeline. Geographically, the company serves primarily the domestic market, with exports to 25 countries. Alongside organic growth, Shyam Metalics is undertaking significant capacity expansions and strategic diversification into high-value segments such as wagon manufacturing, with the aim to strengthen market presence and enhance revenue and EBITDA potential in the coming years.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Shyam Metalics is actively expanding its production capabilities to meet the growing demand for steel products. The company has initiated a ₹1,160 crore capital expenditure plan over the next three years to enhance its stainless steel (SS) production.

Name: Indian Metals & Ferro Alloys Ltd
Overview:
Indian Metals & Ferro Alloys Ltd, established in 1961, is India’s largest integrated ferrochrome producer with operations spanning mining, smelting, and captive power generation. Its products cater mainly to stainless steel and alloy industries, where ferrochrome is a critical raw material.

The company exports a major share of its production to international markets including China, Japan, and Taiwan. With a vertically integrated model, IMFA benefits from cost efficiencies and consistent quality across its value chain.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
IMFA has begun with a sequentially stronger first quarter, signaling recovery trends. Management focus remains on maintaining healthy margins, leveraging captive power for cost efficiencies, and maximizing export opportunities. As stainless steel demand improves globally, IMFA is expected to benefit from both domestic stability and international recovery.

Name: Welspun Corp Ltd
Overview:
Welspun Corp Ltd (WCL) is one of the world’s largest manufacturers of large-diameter pipes, ranking among the top three global line pipe producers. Its product portfolio spans SAW/ERW steel line pipes, DI pipes, stainless steel bars and pipes, TMT rebars, and BIS-certified billets, catering to oil & gas, water infrastructure, and industrial applications.

The company has also expanded into the building materials segment through the acquisition of Sintex-BAPL, a market leader in water storage tanks, and Weetek Plastics, gaining entry into CPVC, UPVC, and SWR pipes and fittings. With a manufacturing footprint across India, the US, and Saudi Arabia, WCL operates multiple large-scale expansion projects aimed at strengthening its global presence in both core pipe manufacturing and the building materials sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Jai Balaji Industries is actively enhancing its production capabilities, with plans to increase ductile iron pipe capacity.The company is also expanding its ferro-alloy production by 14% to 190,000 tonnes per annum, aiming to boost revenue from this segment to 30–35% of total sales.

Name: Technocraft Industries (India) Ltd
Overview:
Technocraft Industries (India) Ltd is a diversified manufacturing company with a global presence, operating across five primary sectors: Drum Closures, Scaffolding Systems, Cotton Yarn, Fabric, Garments, and Engineering Services. Established in 1992, the company has manufacturing facilities in India and China, catering to both domestic and international markets. Its scaffolding and formwork solutions are particularly prominent in the construction and infrastructure sectors.

The company is also expanding its footprint in the formwork segment, with its Mach One brand gaining traction in affordable housing projects. Additionally, Technocraft has ventured into international markets, including Saudi Arabia, South America, and the U.S., for its formwork products
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Technocraft anticipates a 20–21% revenue growth in FY26, driven by increased demand in the formwork and scaffolding segments. The company is also exploring opportunities in international markets to further bolster its growth trajectory.

Name: NMDC Steel Ltd
Overview:
NMDC Limited, founded in 1958, is a fully government-owned enterprise under the Ministry of Steel, specializing in the exploration and production of minerals. It is India’s largest iron ore producer and the sixth-largest globally, operating highly mechanized mining complexes in Chhattisgarh and Karnataka with a combined annual capacity of 51 MnT. The company’s product portfolio includes high-grade iron ore (64% Fe), pellets, diamonds, and sponge iron, alongside wind power generation. NMDC also manages India’s only mechanized diamond mine at Panna, Madhya Pradesh, which resumed operations in FY24 after a three-year halt.

The company’s operations extend to the mining of copper, rock phosphate, limestone, magnesite, and tungsten. Beyond mining, NMDC is advancing beneficiation, pelletization, and eco-friendly ore transport infrastructure. It has a significant international footprint through majority ownership in Legacy Iron Ore (Australia) and a stake in International Coal Ventures Ltd (Mozambique). With its integrated R&D centre in Hyderabad and a portfolio of patented technologies, NMDC emphasizes innovation in mineral processing and sustainable mining practices.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
NMDC’s growth trajectory is anchored in capacity expansion to 100 MnT, increased beneficiation and pelletization, and integration of sustainable transport infrastructure. Coal mining operations will diversify revenue streams, while overseas projects in Australia and Mozambique add resource security. The company’s sustained investment in R&D and technology, combined with its low-cost production model, positions it to strengthen global competitiveness and maintain leadership in India’s iron ore sector

Name: Shivalik Bimetal Controls Ltd
Overview:
Shivalik Bimetal Controls Ltd is a specialized engineering firm focused on designing and manufacturing precision components such as thermostatic bimetal/trimetal strips and shunt resistors—critical for applications in automotive, industrial, smart meters, and consumer electronics. The company operates through a vertically integrated model supporting R&D, tooling, and manufacturing.

Backed by a strong global presence spanning India, Europe, and North America, it’s recognized for its high ROCE, asset-light structure, and focus on innovation—making it a niche player in the precision-engineering space.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Shivalik is well-positioned to capitalize on secular trends like smart meter rollouts, EV growth, and industrial automation—boosting demand for its precision components. With improved margin profile, financial strength, and a lean operating model, it aims to scale further through continued innovation and disciplined execution in FY26 and beyond.

Name: Lloyds Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
Lloyds Enterprises Ltd (formerly Shree Global Tradefin Ltd) is primarily engaged in trading iron and steel products—ranging from hot-rolled coils and cold-rolled sheets to structural components like channels, angles, and beams. The company has diversified its activities through subsidiaries operating in real estate and engineering, enabling it to build a broader revenue base. 

Lloyds has augmented its profile via strategic moves beyond core trading, notably entering real estate development and engineering products—a diversification strategy enhancing long-term resilience.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With its dominant position in steel trading, supplemented by engineering and real estate ventures, Lloyds Enterprises is well-positioned for sustainable growth in FY26. Strategic investments like the gold mine and warehouse development not only diversify revenue sources but also offer high-value long-term returns. These developments, combined with strong operating metrics in early FY26, provide a solid foundation for continued expansion and value creation.

Name: Usha Martin Ltd
Overview:
Usha Martin Ltd is a leading manufacturer of steel wire ropes, strands, cords, and related accessories, also producing wire drawing machines and specialty cables. The company serves diversified sectors including oil & offshore, construction, elevator, mining, and telecommunications, with a strong international presence contributing over half its revenue. It continues to focus on expanding value-added products and enhancing digital and operational efficiencies through its “One Usha Martin” transformation program.

The company undertook capacity expansion and restructuring initiatives, especially in its European operations, while maintaining a strong balance sheet and improving cash flow. The focus remains on enhancing margins through product mix improvements, global restructuring, and leveraging emerging market opportunities.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Usha Martin expects sustained topline and volume growth of 12–15% in FY26 driven by value-added product ramp-up, capacity expansions, and strong market demand in key regions. The EBITDA margin is projected to improve above 18% as restructuring benefits mature. The company will continue focusing on digitalization, working capital optimization, and leveraging domestic infrastructure growth, aiming for stable international/domestic revenue mix and long-term profitability enhancement.

Name: Sambhv Steel Tubes Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 2018, Sambhv Steel Tubes Ltd is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in electric resistance welded (ERW) steel pipes and structural tubes, including black pipes and galvanized iron (GI) pipes. Sambhv's backward integration strategy encompasses the production of intermediate products such as sponge iron, blooms/slabs, and hot-rolled (HR) coils, ensuring quality control and cost efficiency across its supply chain

Sambhv Steel Tubes Ltd offers a diverse product portfolio catering to various industries, including construction, infrastructure, and water supply. The company's product range includes ERW black pipes (round, square, and rectangular), GI pipes, CRFH pipes, and steel door frames.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Sambhv Steel Tubes Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. Additionally, Sambhv aims to diversify its product offerings and strengthen its distribution network to cater to the growing infrastructure needs across the country. With a robust growth strategy and a focus on operational excellence, Sambhv Steel Tubes Ltd is well-positioned to capitalize on the expanding opportunities in the steel manufacturing sector.

Name: Gallantt Ispat Ltd.
Overview:
Gallantt Ispat Limited is a diversified industrial player founded in 2005, with operations spanning iron and steel manufacturing, agro-based food products, power generation, and real estate development. It is headquartered in Eastern Uttar Pradesh and markets products such as sponge iron, MS billets, TMT bars, and wheat-based food items under the “Gallantt” brand across multiple Indian states. In real estate, the company has executed group housing ventures like the Shalimar Gallantt project in Lucknow.

The company has shown strong earnings growth and operational resilience. In the year ended March 2025, it delivered robust net income growth and margin improvement. In the first quarter of FY26, despite a slight dip in sales, profitability surged significantly, indicating improved cost efficiency and favorable market dynamics.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management Continue focuses on cost optimization and lean operations that can sustain elevated profitability even amid cyclical revenue shifts. Expansion in agro and real estate may offer additional revenue buffers; real estate in particular can unlock significant returns over the medium term. Strong cash flows paired with controlled investment suggest the company can deploy capital judiciously whether into capacity expansion or enhanced shareholder value.

Name: JTL Industries Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1991, JTL Industries Ltd (formerly known as JTL Infra Ltd) is one of India’s leading manufacturers of Electric Resistance Welded (ERW) steel pipes and tubes. The company offers a diverse range of products, including MS black pipes, hollow sections, hot-dipped galvanized tubes, pre-galvanized pipes, and value-added products. These products cater to various sectors such as construction, infrastructure, oil & gas, solar energy, water supply, and telecommunications.

JTL Industries operates multiple manufacturing facilities across India and has a significant presence in both domestic and international markets. The company's commitment to quality and innovation has positioned it as a key player in the steel tube and pipe industry.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
JTL Industries is focusing on expanding its product offerings and enhancing production capabilities. The company is investing in capacity expansion and technological advancements to meet the growing demand in various sectors. With a strong product portfolio and strategic initiatives, JTL Industries aims to strengthen its market position and achieve sustainable growth in the coming years.

Name: Nava Ltd
Overview:
Nava Ltd is a diversified Indian multinational conglomerate with operations spanning metals, energy, mining, commercial agriculture, and healthcare. The company is headquartered in Hyderabad and has a significant presence in both domestic and international markets.

In FY25, Nava Ltd achieved its highest-ever revenue and net profit, reflecting strong performance across its business segments and strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing operational efficiency and expanding market reach.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company aims to grow through renewable energy expansion, operational efficiency improvements, and diversification into agriculture and healthcare. Strategic investments in infrastructure and technology are expected to drive sustainable revenue growth and shareholder value over the next few years.

Name: Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Ltd
Overview:
Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Ltd (KFIL), part of the Pune-based Kirloskar Group, is a leading domestic manufacturer of pig iron, ferrous castings, steel products, and seamless tubes. Its product range caters to diverse sectors, including automotive, tractors, construction equipment, oil & gas, mining, and defense. With the merger of ISMT Limited, KFIL now has an expanded product portfolio, including PQF and Assel mill–produced seamless tubes, enabling deeper penetration into high-value industrial applications.

The company operates three manufacturing units with an integrated captive power capacity of 56 MW, complemented by significant renewable energy investments. KFIL’s market presence is reinforced by strong segmental shares—22–25% in foundry-grade pig iron and 19–20% in castings—supported by backward integration into raw materials via its recently commenced captive iron ore mines.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
KFIL targets $2 billion in revenue by 2030, underpinned by backward integration, cost optimisation, and capacity expansion. While near-term margins in pig iron and steel remain under pressure due to commodity price declines and import competition, the castings and tubes segments are expected to drive structural margin improvement over the next 2–3 years, aided by high-value product ramp-ups and captive resource benefits.

Name: Raghav Productivity Enhancers Ltd
Overview:
Raghav Productivity Enhancers Ltd (RPEL) is a specialized manufacturer of quartz-based ramming mass, quartz powder, and tundish boards. Operating under the brand name "Raghav," the company caters to the steel and foundry industries, providing essential refractory materials that enhance productivity and reduce energy consumption. RPEL's products are integral to secondary steel producers and foundries, offering customized lining solutions that enable non-linear productivity growth and significant reductions in power and overhead costs for its clients.

Established as a small-cap entity, RPEL has positioned itself as a leading player in its niche market. The company is recognized for its commitment to quality and innovation, ensuring that its products meet the stringent requirements of the industries it serves.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
RPEL aims to continue its growth trajectory by expanding its product offerings and enhancing its market presence. The company's strategic initiatives focus on innovation and quality to meet the evolving needs of the steel and foundry industries.

Name: Bansal Wire Industries Ltd
Overview:
Bansal Wire Industries Limited, incorporated in 1985, is among India’s leading steel wire manufacturers and holds the top position in stainless steel wires by volume. The company caters to a large and diversified customer base of over 5,000 clients across 50+ countries, reflecting strong brand recall and customer loyalty.

Its portfolio spans a wide spectrum of wire products, with a strategic focus on specialty wires for high-growth applications such as automotive, infrastructure, and industrial uses. With a strong emphasis on capacity expansion, backward integration, and customer-centric execution, Bansal Wire is positioned as a key beneficiary of India’s industrial and infrastructure growth.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management is optimistic about achieving 30% volume growth, supported by capacity expansions and specialty wire ramp-up. While EBITDA margins are expected to see a temporary decline in FY26–27.

Name: Aeroflex Industries Ltd
Overview:
Aeroflex Industries Limited, incorporated in 1993 and part of Sat Industries Limited, is a leading Indian manufacturer of metallic flexible flow solutions. Its product portfolio spans stainless steel hoses, assemblies, fittings, metal bellows, composite hoses, and specialized industrial hoses, serving diverse industries such as oil & gas, petrochemicals, steel, mining, aerospace, robotics, and semiconductors. With over 2,700 SKUs, the company has established itself as a critical supplier for controlled flow applications in high-pressure and extreme-temperature environments.

The company operates a large-scale manufacturing facility in Taloja, Navi Mumbai, with an installed capacity of 13.5 million meters as of FY24 and plans to expand to 20 million meters by March 2026. Exports form the backbone of its revenue, contributing 77% in 9M FY25, with a strong presence in the Americas and Europe. Aeroflex continues to invest in R&D with an in-house NABL-accredited lab and over 70 products in development, while also diversifying through acquisitions such as Hyd-Air Engineering to strengthen its presence in railways, shipbuilding, and heavy industries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Management remains optimistic about recovery, supported by improving export demand and strong domestic momentum. The company is targeting ₹500 crore in revenues for FY26 with >20% EBITDA growth. Long-term EBITDA margins are guided at 25%, supported by scale-up of metal bellows and data center solutions. While tariff risks persist, Aeroflex believes its cost competitiveness versus US and EU peers provides resilience, even under higher duty scenarios.

Name: Goodluck India Ltd
Overview:
Goodluck India Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in engineering solutions, including ERW steel pipes, hollow sections, CR coils, CDW tubes, and structural components. Established in 1986 and headquartered in Ghaziabad, the company serves diverse sectors such as automotive, aerospace, oil & gas, transmission & distribution, defence, solar, and infrastructure.

With a robust manufacturing footprint across Uttar Pradesh and Gujarat, Goodluck India has a combined annual capacity of 500,000 metric tonnes. The company exports to over 100 countries, catering to more than 600 customers globally.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company targets double-digit revenue growth in FY26, aiming for ₹4,500 crore, leveraging capacity expansions and sectoral growth in defence, aerospace, and infrastructure. Strategic investments in technology and production capabilities are expected to improve profitability and market positioning.

Name: Steelcast Ltd
Overview:
Steelcast Limited is a leading manufacturer of steel and alloy steel castings, supplying to global OEMs across industries such as earthmoving, mining, railroads, construction, and defense. With expertise in both sand and shell molding processes, the company produces over 300 parts ranging from 2.5 kg to 2,500 kg, serving Fortune 500 customers in India and abroad.

The company operates a 30,000 TPA facility in Bhavnagar, Gujarat, supported by advanced R&D, machining capabilities, and renewable power plants that meet ~80% of captive power needs. It holds a Two-Star Export House status, with ~54% of revenues from exports, and aims to expand its presence to 18 countries by FY26 while diversifying across railways, defense, and ground engaging tools.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
Margins are expected to remain resilient, supported by higher value-addition, cost savings, and renewable energy initiatives. Long-term strategies include expanding product range, scaling defense and GET contributions, entering new geographies, and investing in automation to boost productivity by 25%. Despite global uncertainties and tariffs, the MD emphasized that Steelcast is structurally positioned for sustained growth and global competitiveness.

Name: Sarda Energy & Minerals Ltd
Overview:
Sarda Energy & Minerals Limited (SEML) operates primarily in steel, ferro alloys, and power production, establishing itself as a significant player in India’s energy and mineral sectors. The company has recently witnessed a shift in its earnings mix, with a growing contribution from its power business, supported by strong operational performance in hydropower and thermal plants.

SEML continues to invest aggressively in coal mining and power projects, including hydropower and captive solar capacity expansion. Its steel and ferro alloys segments remain focused on sustaining volumes and margins amid a dynamic industry environment, while the power segment is poised for steady growth backed by improved plant efficiencies and upcoming capacity additions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
SEML aims to sustain power segment momentum through operational efficiency and capacity expansions, while maintaining stable steel volumes and margins amid market volatility. The company’s disciplined capital allocation towards energy and mining projects, coupled with ongoing modernization efforts, positions it well for medium-to-long-term growth and enhanced shareholder value.

Name: Mishra Dhatu Nigam Ltd
Overview: Mishra Dhatu Nigam Ltd (MIDHANI) is a Defence PSU established in 1973 under the Ministry of Defence, specializing in superalloys, titanium alloys, and special-purpose metals for strategic sectors such as aerospace, energy, and defence. Headquartered in Hyderabad, MIDHANI possesses unique manufacturing capabilities (vacuum arc furnaces, precision mills) and a 4 MW solar power plant to support its operations.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook 
Management anticipates FY26 exports to rise to ₹100–120 crore. EBITDA margins are expected to remain in the 20–25% range, driven by ongoing operational efficiencies. The company is targeting ~20% annual revenue growth, led by defense, aerospace, and alloy expansion opportunities.

Name: Jai Balaji Industries Ltd
Overview:
Jai Balaji Industries Limited, incorporated in 1999, is a fully integrated steel manufacturer and one of Eastern India’s largest private producers of value-added steel products. Its core product portfolio includes ductile iron (DI) pipes and specialized ferro alloys, alongside sponge iron, pig iron, billets, TMT bars, coke, and captive power. The company also holds a Three Star Export House status, with its products reaching over 40 countries worldwide.

With four strategically located manufacturing units across West Bengal and Chhattisgarh, Jai Balaji has built substantial installed capacities in DI pipes, ferro alloys, blast furnaces, coke, and rolling mills. The company is actively executing a ₹1,000 crore brownfield expansion plan, fully funded via internal accruals, aimed at strengthening its DI pipe and ferro alloys segments, alongside debottlenecking and green energy initiatives.
Key Takeways:
Capacity Expansions & Operational Updates
In Q4 FY25, Jai Balaji commissioned 2.04 lakh tons of additional DI pipe capacity, raising the total to 5.04 lakh tons. TMT bar capacity increased from 2.6 lakh to 3 lakh tons via debottlenecking. One blast furnace was revamped, boosting hot metal capacity, with the second scheduled for completion in FY26. Sinter plant capacity also began scaling from 9 lakh to 12 lakh tons.

Capex & Funding
Out of the planned ₹1,000 crore expansion program, ₹822 crore had been spent by FY25-end, all through internal accruals. Capex for FY26 is guided at ₹175 crore. The company also approved entry into OPVC pipes on a pilot basis, with initial capex being less than $2 million.

Production & Order Book
FY25 DI pipe production was 2.82 lakh tons, with an order book of 1.45 lakh tons heading into FY26. Management targets 4 lakh tons production in FY26. DI pipe realizations in FY25 were ₹19,000/ton, with expectations to recover to ₹23,000–₹24,000/ton as demand revives.

Working Capital & Raw Materials
Receivables and inventory levels rose in FY25 due to delayed payments to government contractors and slower order execution. Iron ore prices remained stable, while coking coal prices are projected to decline by $10 YoY. Q4 margins were impacted by inventory losses following price corrections.

Growth or Future Outlook
Management guided for 25–30% revenue growth and 16–17% EBITDA margin in FY26, driven by higher DI pipe volumes, improved realizations, and better operating leverage. The demand outlook is supported by major government water infrastructure projects such as JJM and AMRUT 2, along with expected post-budget rebound in order inflows. Debt reduction remains a key priority, with plans to repay most term debt by FY26-end.

Name: Jayaswal Neco Industries Ltd
Overview:
Jayaswal Neco Industries Limited (JNIL), established in 1976, has evolved from a small-scale iron foundry into a prominent player in India's iron and steel casting industry. The company operates integrated manufacturing facilities encompassing steel production, pig iron, sponge iron, pellets, billets, and iron & steel castings. These operations are supported by strategically located captive iron ore mines, ensuring a consistent supply of raw materials and enhancing cost efficiency. 

JNIL's commitment to quality and innovation has positioned it as a trusted partner for diverse industries requiring high-quality castings. The company has consistently achieved record-breaking production and dispatch figures across its units, reflecting its manufacturing resilience and operational excellence. Additionally, JNIL has undertaken significant capital repairs and upgrades to its blast furnace and related facilities, strengthening its operational backbone for sustained productivity.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
JNIL is poised for sustained growth, driven by its diversified product portfolio and operational enhancements. The company's focus on reducing debt and optimizing operations positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the iron and steel casting industry. Continued investments in infrastructure and technology are expected to further bolster its market position.

Name: Sunflag Iron & Steel Company Ltd
Overview:
Sunflag Iron & Steel Company Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer of special alloy and stainless steel products, primarily serving the automotive, engineering, and defense sectors. Established in 1984, the company operates an integrated plant near Nagpur, Maharashtra, with a production capacity exceeding 4 lakh metric tonnes per annum.

The company specializes in high-quality rolled products, including bright bars, wire rods, and sections, catering to critical applications in aerospace, automotive, and industrial machinery. Sunflag has achieved ISO/TS 16949 and ISO 9001 certifications, underscoring its commitment to quality and precision.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company aims to expand production capacity, diversify into specialized alloys, and grow exports. Sustainability initiatives, including clean steel technology, are expected to enhance operational efficiency and strengthen Sunflag’s leadership in the steel sector.

Name: Rajputana Stainless Ltd
Overview:
Rajputana Stainless Ltd is engaged in the manufacturing and processing of stainless steel products used across various industrial and commercial applications. The company primarily deals in stainless steel sheets, coils, and related products, which serve as key raw materials for industries such as construction, infrastructure, engineering, and fabrication. Its operations focus on delivering quality steel products that meet the requirements of durability, corrosion resistance, and strength.

The company operates processing facilities that handle cutting, finishing, and distribution of stainless steel products to a wide customer base. It caters to both domestic and industrial clients, supplying materials used in sectors like kitchen equipment, automotive components, and architectural structures.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its processing capacity and strengthen its distribution network to cater to rising demand for stainless steel products. It aims to focus on value-added processing and customized solutions to improve margins and customer retention. Increasing demand from infrastructure, construction, and industrial sectors is expected to support business growth. The company is also working on improving operational efficiency through better sourcing and cost management.

Name: Kamdhenu Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1994, Kamdhenu Ltd is one of India’s largest branded players in the TMT and structural steel segment, with a strong retail presence through 8,500+ dealers, 250+ distributors, and 80+ franchise manufacturing units. Its portfolio includes TMT bars, structural steel products, color-coated profile sheets, and innovative grades such as Kamdhenu Nxt and Kamdhenu PAS 100.

The company operates through a capital-light franchisee model supported by its own steel division in Bhiwadi, Rajasthan. Kamdhenu has also diversified into the paints business, which is housed under Kamdhenu Ventures Ltd, focusing on decorative paints, emulsions, and premium coatings. Both businesses leverage the company’s wide distribution and brand equity in urban, semi-urban, and rural markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
For FY26, Kamdhenu guided for 14% growth in volumes and royalty income, 20% higher brand spends (₹74 crore in FY25), and capacity expansion of 20% (10–12% operational in FY26). In paints, management expects demand revival, margin recovery, and share gains from premium products and distribution strengthening. With a debt-free balance sheet, robust franchisee model, and strong brand pull, Kamdhenu targets sustained double-digit growth, stronger profitability, and deeper market penetration across both steel and paints.

Name: Manaksia Coated Metals & Industries Ltd
Overview:
Established in 2010, Manaksia Coated Metals & Industries Ltd (MCMIL) is a prominent manufacturer and exporter of coated metal products and fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG). The company operates a state-of-the-art facility in Kutch, Gujarat, with an annual production capacity of 120,000 metric tons, which is set to increase to 180,000 metric tons following ongoing expansion efforts. MCMIL's product portfolio includes color-coated galvanized steel and plain galvanized steel in both coil and sheet forms, catering to industries such as construction, automotive, and home appliances.

In addition to its metal products, MCMIL manufactures mosquito repellent coils and ultramarine blue powder. The company operates manufacturing units for mosquito coils in Bhopal, Guwahati, and Hyderabad, and has initiated contract manufacturing operations with Reckitt Benckiser to produce ultramarine blue powder in Bhopal
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Manaksia Coated Metals & Industries Ltd is focused on expanding its manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand for its products. The company plans to enhance its production capacity for coated metal products and mosquito repellent coils, aiming to strengthen its position in both domestic and international markets.

Name: Nelcast Ltd
Overview:
Nelcast Ltd, incorporated in 1982, is India’s largest jobbing foundry specializing in ductile and grey iron castings. The company manufactures complex parts such as axle housings, clutch housings, bogie suspension brackets, and brake disc friction rings, catering to multiple industries including commercial vehicles, tractors, off-highway equipment, passenger vehicles, and railways.

With three fully automated manufacturing facilities located in Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu, Nelcast has a combined installed capacity of 1,60,000 MTPA. Its diversified customer base includes top OEMs such as TAFE, Ashok Leyland, Tata Motors, and international Tier-1 suppliers, with exports contributing significantly to its revenue. The company is also increasing its renewable energy usage to drive efficiency and reduce costs.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Looking forward, Nelcast expects growth to be led by export orders, off-highway equipment, and railways, where customer engagement is deepening. Management reiterated that the export share is set to rise further, structurally improving margins. They see strong visibility over the next 2–3 years from secured orders and increasing demand from global OEMs, particularly in North America and Europe.
