Name: Safe Enterprises Retail Fixtures Ltd
Overview:
Safe Enterprises Retail Fixtures Limited, established in 1976 and incorporated as a public limited company in 2024, is a leading Indian provider of customized retail fixtures. The company specializes in designing, manufacturing, supplying, and installing bespoke shop fittings tailored to the unique needs of various retail segments, including fashion, electronics, and department stores.

The company's product offerings encompass a wide range of modular fixtures, such as storage racks, cabinets, partition systems, digital display screens, touch-enabled monitors, display tables, full glass counters, and cash counters.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Safe Enterprises Retail Fixtures Ltd is poised for significant growth, focusing on expanding its manufacturing capabilities and enhancing its product portfolio to meet the evolving demands of the retail industry. The company plans to leverage its expertise in customized retail fixtures to cater to a broader range of retail segments, including emerging sectors such as e-commerce and experiential retail.

Name: MSP Steel & Power Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1968, MSP Steel & Power Ltd is a prominent player in India's steel industry, headquartered in Kolkata. The company specializes in manufacturing a diverse range of iron and steel products, including TMT bars, billets, structural steel, and sponge iron. 

With a manufacturing facility located in Raigarh, Chhattisgarh, MSP Steel & Power is well-positioned to cater to both domestic and international markets. The company's commitment to quality and innovation has earned it recognition as one of India's best workplaces in manufacturing for 2025.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
MSP Steel & Power aims to expand its production capacity to 1.5 million tons by 2030. This ambitious goal reflects the company's commitment to scaling its operations and enhancing its market presence. The strong financial performance in Q1 FY26 positions MSP Steel & Power well to capitalize on growth opportunities in the steel industry.

Name: EPack Prefab Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1999 and headquartered in Greater Noida, EPACK Prefab Technologies Ltd specializes in providing turnkey pre-engineered steel buildings and prefabricated structures. The company offers comprehensive solutions encompassing design, manufacturing, installation, and erection of pre-engineered steel buildings, prefabricated structures, and their components. EPACK Prefab serves various industries, including agriculture, logistics, construction, railways, and automobiles, both domestically and internationally.

Additionally, EPACK Prefab manufactures expanded polystyrene (EPS) sheets and blocks, also known as EPS block molded and EPS shape molded products. These products are utilized in construction, packaging, and consumer goods industries in India. The company operates manufacturing facilities in Greater Noida, Ghiloth, and Mambattu, with a combined capacity of 8,400 metric tons per annum
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

EPACK Prefab Technologies Ltd is focusing on expanding its manufacturing capacity and geographic presence to meet the growing demand for prefabricated building solutions. The company is investing in new facilities and enhancing existing ones to increase production capabilities. By leveraging its expertise in pre-engineered steel buildings and EPS products, EPACK Prefab aims to strengthen its position in both domestic and international markets.

Name: Steel Exchange India Ltd
Overview:
Steel Exchange India Ltd (SEIL), established in 1999, is a leading integrated steel manufacturer based in Andhra Pradesh, India. The company operates an integrated steel plant and power unit in Vizianagaram District, near Visakhapatnam. SEIL is recognized for its flagship brand, SIMHADRI TMT rebars, which are widely used in infrastructure projects across South India.

SEIL's operations encompass the entire steel production value chain, including sponge iron production, billet manufacturing, rolling mills, and power generation. This integration allows the company to maintain control over quality and costs.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Steel Exchange India Ltd (SEIL) is strategically expanding its operations beyond steel manufacturing into infrastructure and logistics. The company has partnered with Vizag Profiles Logistics and Hind Terminals to develop a cargo terminal and multi-modal logistics park in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. This initiative aims to enhance logistics efficiency, reduce transit times, and support the long-term competitiveness of SEIL's core steel business.

Name: Prakash Industries Ltd
Overview:
Prakash Industries Ltd is an integrated steel and power company engaged in the manufacturing and sale of steel products and the generation of power. Headquartered in Haryana, India, the company operates an advanced steel plant in Champa, Chhattisgarh, and owns captive power plants to support its manufacturing operations.

Prakash Industries Ltd’s business spans two primary segments: steel products and power generation. The steel segment produces wire rods, HB wires, TMT bars, and structurals for various industrial applications, while the power segment ensures a reliable energy supply for both captive use and commercial distribution. This vertical integration enhances operational efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Prakash Industries Ltd aims to grow revenue by 20–25% over the next 2–3 years and maintain EBITDA margins around 35–40%. The company plans to expand steel production capacity, enhance product offerings, and optimize operations to strengthen its market position.

Name: Electrotherm (India) Ltd
Overview:
Electrotherm Ltd, established in 1983, is a diversified Indian company specializing in engineering technologies, special steel production, and electric vehicles (EVs). The company operates through three primary divisions: Steel, Engineering, and Electric Vehicles. In the steel sector,

Electrotherm manufactures a range of products, including induction melting furnaces and ductile iron pipes. The engineering division focuses on providing solutions for various industrial applications, while the EV division is dedicated to the development and production of electric vehicles, contributing to the growing demand for sustainable transportation options in India.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Electrotherm Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive future growth and enhance its market presence. The company plans to expand its operations by increasing its annual solar module manufacturing capacity from 700 MW to 1.5 GW by the end of the year, with a further expansion to 4.5 GW by 2026. Additionally, Electrotherm aims to establish a 1 GW annual solar cell production facility by mid-2027, with plans to expand to 2 GW.

Name: Mukand Ltd
Overview:
Mukand Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer of special alloy steel, billets, bars, rods, and steel castings. The company also produces industrial machinery such as EOT cranes, material handling equipment, and provides comprehensive engineering services.

The company caters to a variety of industries including automotive, infrastructure, and heavy engineering. With a focus on innovation and high-quality products, Mukand continues to strengthen its position in the Indian steel and industrial machinery sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Mukand plans to expand its product range and invest in technology upgrades to improve efficiency. The company is also exploring export opportunities, aiming to increase revenue and strengthen its position in the global steel and industrial machinery market.

Name: Venus Pipes & Tubes Ltd
Overview:
Venus Pipes & Tubes Ltd is a leading manufacturer and exporter of stainless-steel pipes and tubes in India. Its product portfolio spans seamless and welded pipes covering high-precision, heat-exchanger, hydraulic & instrumentation, and box pipes. Known for supplying critical industries, the company has strengthened its global footprint alongside strong domestic operations.

The company’s growth strategy has centered around enhancing product offerings and scaling value-added capabilities. In FY25, it expanded production capacity, reinforced its product lineup, and enhanced its export performance setting a robust foundation as it entered FY26 with a solid order book.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With robust on-order visibility, strong export traction, targeted capacity build-up, and product diversification underway, Venus is well-positioned to accelerate growth in FY26 and beyond. Its strategic investments and improved value-added mix aim to keep margins resilient and drive sustained value creation.

Name: Salasar Techno Engineering Ltd
Overview:
Salasar Techno Engineering Ltd is a leading engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) firm specializing in galvanized steel structures for telecom towers, transmission trusses, substation frameworks, and solar mounting systems. It serves infrastructure, energy, and telecom sectors across India from its modern manufacturing facilities.

Founded in 2006, it’s known for its integrated verticals—from design and engineering to fabrication and galvanization—providing end-to-end deployment capabilities and maintaining strong OEM relationships across critical infrastructure segments.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Amid cyclical slowdowns, Salasar remains focused on securing fresh orders, optimizing project execution, and leveraging its manufacturing edge. With EPC cycles likely to recover and its integrated model enhancing cost efficiencies, the company is expected to regain growth talent through FY26.

Name: Bharat Wire Ropes Ltd
Overview:
Bharat Wire Ropes Limited (BWRL) is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in steel wire, wire ropes, strands, and slings. With a robust presence in the iron and steel sector, the company caters to diverse industries, including infrastructure, construction, and mining. BWRL operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Chalisgaon and Atgaon, Maharashtra, ensuring high-quality production standards. The company is committed to delivering durable and reliable products that meet the stringent requirements of its clientele.

As a prominent player in the wire rope manufacturing industry, Bharat Wire Ropes has established a strong reputation for its engineering excellence and customer-centric approach. The company's products are integral to various applications, from lifting and rigging to safety and structural support systems.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Bharat Wire Ropes aims to enhance its product portfolio and expand its market presence. The company is focusing on increasing its export capabilities and exploring new applications for its products. With a strong emphasis on quality and innovation, BWRL is poised for sustainable growth in the coming years.

Name: Scoda Tubes Ltd
Overview:
Scoda Tubes Ltd is an Indian manufacturer of high-quality stainless-steel tubes and pipes—including seamless, welded, instrumentation, and U-tubes. Founded in 2008 and headquartered in Gujarat, the company operates a fully integrated facility near Mundra Port, with extensive backward integration via its own hot piercing mill. It produces a diverse range of stainless steels—including Austenitic, Duplex, and Super-Duplex variants—meeting global standards like ISO and PED.

With one of the most capacity-expanding operations in its sector, Scoda has grown revenue from under ₹200 crore to close to ₹400 crore in just a few years. It exports to multiple sectors like oil & gas, chemicals, power, pharmaceuticals, railways, and automotive, serving a diversified client base and maintaining strict quality control norms that ensure low rejection rates.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
With its expansion plans, strong execution, and broad industrial applications, Scoda is positioned to continue scaling sustainably. As orders increase from sectors needing stainless-steel infrastructure, Scoda’s margins and market presence should improve further.

Name: Gandhi Special Tubes Ltd
Overview:
Gandhi Special Tubes Ltd (GSTL), established in 1959, is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in seamless and welded steel tubes, nuts, and wind power generation. The company caters to diverse industries, including automotive, commercial vehicles, hydraulic systems, and general engineering, providing tailor-made tubular solutions and components. 

Operating in the power sector through windmills installed in Maharashtra and Gujarat, GSTL diversifies its revenue streams beyond traditional manufacturing. The company's products are supplied to original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) in the automotive sector, farm equipment manufacturers, construction equipment manufacturers, and other engineering industries.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
GSTL aims to sustain its growth trajectory by leveraging its diversified product portfolio and expanding its presence in the wind power sector. The company's focus on innovation and quality is expected to drive demand across its target industries.

Name: Rajratan Global Wire Ltd
Overview:
Rajratan Global Wire Ltd is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in tire bead wire and high-carbon steel wire. Established in 1988, the company operates globally with manufacturing facilities in India and Thailand, serving major tire manufacturers and other industries requiring high-strength wire products.

The company operates in two primary segments: tire bead wire and high-carbon steel wire. Rajratan Global Wire Ltd has been expanding its client base and product offerings, aiming to strengthen its position as a leader in the wire manufacturing sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Rajratan Global Wire Ltd aims to continue expanding its footprint both domestically and internationally. The company plans to enhance its product offerings and operational efficiencies to meet the growing demand for high-strength wire products. With a focus on innovation and customer satisfaction, Rajratan Global Wire Ltd is well-positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the wire manufacturing sector.

Name: Pennar Industries Ltd
Overview:
Pennar Industries Ltd is a diversified engineering company headquartered in Hyderabad, India. Established in 1988, the company specializes in manufacturing precision-engineered products across various sectors, including automotive, infrastructure, power, general engineering, and building & construction. Pennar Industries operates through multiple business verticals, offering a wide range of solutions to meet the evolving needs of its customers. 

The company's product portfolio encompasses steel products and profiles, systems and projects, tubes, industrial components, and pre-engineered building systems (PEBS).
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Pennar Industries aims to continue its growth trajectory, supported by a solid domestic order book and a steadily expanding global presence. The company plans to leverage its operational capabilities, widen market reach, and advance innovation-led solutions to capture emerging opportunities.

Name: Vardhman Special Steels Ltd
Overview:
Vardhman Special Steels Limited is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in billets, steel bars and rods, and bright bars. Established as part of the Vardhman Group, the company has built a strong reputation for producing high-quality special and alloy steels. Its products cater to various industries, including automotive, engineering, and construction, meeting the diverse needs of its clientele.

The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities equipped with advanced technology to ensure precision and efficiency in production. Vardhman Special Steels is committed to maintaining stringent quality standards and continuous innovation to stay competitive in the dynamic steel industry. With a focus on sustainability and operational excellence, the company aims to contribute significantly to the growth of India's industrial sector.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Vardhman Special Steels is focusing on expanding its production capacity to meet the growing demand in the steel industry. The company has announced plans to establish a greenfield steel plant in Punjab, aiming to enhance its billet production capacity from 300,000 metric tonnes per annum to 500,000 metric tonnes per annum.

Name: Dhampur Bio Organics Ltd
Overview: Dhampur Bio Organics Ltd is an integrated sugarcane processing company in India. The company manufactures refined and raw sugar, bio-fuels, biomass-based renewable power, and other related products. It emerged as a separate entity following a demerger from Dhampur Sugar Mills Ltd in September 2022.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Dhampur Bio Organics Ltd is actively pursuing growth through strategic expansions and diversification. The company has undertaken significant capacity enhancements at its sugar units, notably increasing the crushing capacity at its Meerganj plant from 5,000 TCD to 9,000 TCD, contributing to an overall capacity boost from 22,000 TCD to 26,000 TCD. In addition to its core sugar and ethanol operations, Dhampur is exploring new product lines, including the launch of additional offerings to broaden its market presence

Name: Balrampur Chini Mills Ltd
Overview:
Balrampur Chini Mills Ltd is one of India’s largest integrated sugar manufacturers, headquartered in Uttar Pradesh. In addition to sugar, the company operates significant distillery and cogeneration (power) businesses, leveraging by-products like molasses and bagasse.

Recognized for scale and diversification, Balrampur is progressing into biotech with its upcoming polylactic acid (PLA) biopolymer project and is expanding its ethanol and power footprint, aligning with sustainability and regulatory incentives.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Balrampur Chini Mills is strategically expanding its operations by increasing ethanol production capacity to 1,050 kilolitres per day (KLPD) and establishing India's first industrial-scale polylactic acid (PLA) bioplastics plant with an annual capacity of 80,000 tonnes. These initiatives align with the government's push for renewable energy and sustainable materials. The company is also enhancing its sugarcane crushing capacity and cogeneration power output to meet growing demand.

Name: Triveni Engineering and Industries Ltd
Overview:
Triveni Engineering & Industries Ltd is a diversified industrial conglomerate operating across sugar & allied products, alcohol distillation, power transmission (gearboxes/engineering), and water solutions. With integrated operations spanning upstream sugar production to downstream engineered products, the company serves industrial and consumer markets with scale and technological spread.

Headquartered in Noida, its portfolio includes sugar mills, distilleries, power co-generation, engineered gearboxes, water treatment systems, and value-added retail offerings like premium packaged alcohol—reflecting a balanced mix of traditional agro-driven businesses and engineering services.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Triveni is strategically focused on improving sugar profitability through varietal substitution and agronomic efficiencies, along with ethanol blending tailwinds. Engineering vertical remains a bright spot with robust order inflows, providing earnings diversification. As cost pressures ease and sugarcane recovery improves, return to margin expansion and profit recovery is expected for FY26 and beyond.

Name: Uttam Sugar Mills Ltd
Overview:
Uttam Sugar Mills Ltd is among the leading producers of double-refined sugar in India. The company started production in 2001 and has since grown to become a market leader, with a capacity of 20,000 TCD (tons per day) as of 2011.

Uttam Sugar Mills Ltd offers a range of sugar products under the Uttam brand, including bulk and retail packaging options, catering to various consumer needs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Uttam Sugar Mills Ltd aims to expand its product offerings and enhance manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand in the sugar industry. The company is also focusing on sustainable practices and technological advancements to stay competitive.

Name: Dwarikesh Sugar Industries Ltd
Overview:
Dwarikesh Sugar Industries Ltd is a prominent integrated sugar and bio-energy company with operations in sugar manufacturing, ethanol production, and power co-generation. The company operates three fully automated sugar mills in Uttar Pradesh, along with two distilleries and cogeneration facilities, making it a well-diversified player in the sugar-ethanol value chain.

With a focus on value addition and sustainability, the company has shifted its strategy towards ethanol-led growth. Its distillery division, supported by cane syrup diversion and government incentives for bio-fuel blending, is increasingly becoming a key revenue driver. Reduced debt levels and operational efficiencies further enhance financial flexibility as it transitions into a biofuel-centric enterprise.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
The company is positioning itself as a biofuel-first business, with ethanol expected to drive long-term growth. The distillery division, supported by government blending mandates, remains the key profit contributor, while sugar will continue as a supporting segment. Management reiterated its focus on higher-margin ethanol over sugar and expects stronger performance once the next crushing season begins.

Name: Andrew Yule & Company Ltd
Overview:
Andrew Yule & Company Ltd is a diversified Indian public sector company engaged in engineering, electrical, and tea businesses. Headquartered in Kolkata, the company provides electrical equipment, industrial solutions, and operates tea estates.

The company emphasizes operational efficiency, quality manufacturing, and sustainability. Its diversified operations provide balanced revenue streams and stable market positioning across industrial and agricultural sectors.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company plans to enhance technological capabilities, expand tea operations, and explore industrial market opportunities. Strategic diversification and operational improvements will drive sustainable growth.

Name: Magadh Sugar & Energy Ltd
Overview:
Magadh Sugar & Energy Ltd. operates in the sugar and energy sectors, focusing on the production and distribution of sugar and sugar-related products, including molasses and bagasse. Its sugar products serve industries such as food and beverage, confectionery, and pharmaceuticals. The company also generates renewable energy by converting bagasse and other biomass into electricity, which is used internally or supplied to the grid.

Emphasizing sustainable practices, Magadh Sugar & Energy adopts efficient energy management, water conservation, and responsible waste management. The company invests in modernizing its facilities, incorporating advanced technologies, and diversifying its product portfolio to meet evolving market demands while maintaining quality, efficiency, and customer satisfaction.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Magadh Sugar & Energy Ltd is strategically expanding its operations to strengthen its position in the sugar and renewable energy sectors. The company has approved the establishment of a 100 KLPD Greenfield Multi-feed Distillery at its Hasanpur Sugar Unit, with an investment of approximately ₹206.87 crore. This initiative aligns with the government's Ethanol Blending Program and leverages the availability of feedstock in the region.

Name: GRP Ltd
Overview:
GRP Ltd, established in 1974, is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in the production of reclaimed rubber derived from used tyres, upcycled polyamide from nylon waste, and engineered products die-cut from end-of-life tyres. 

The company operates in the auto tyres and rubber products sector, focusing on sustainable solutions and contributing to the circular economy. GRP Ltd's product portfolio includes reclaimed rubber, engineering plastics, polymer composites, and custom die forms, catering to various industrial applications.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
GRP Ltd aims to recover from the challenges faced in Q1 FY26 by leveraging its strong domestic market presence and expanding export opportunities. The company plans to enhance its EPR initiatives and explore new markets to drive revenue growth.

Name: Dhampur Sugar Mills Ltd
Overview:
Dhampur Sugar Mills Ltd is a leading integrated sugarcane processor with operations encompassing sugar manufacturing, co-generation of power, and the production of ethanol, chemicals, and related by-products. Focusing on innovation and in-house R&D, the company has established one of India's largest and most efficient sugarcane-crushing capacities, along with significant ethanol and renewable energy production.

With a diversified product mix that includes renewable power, fuel ethanol, ethyl acetate, alcohol derivatives, and biofertilizers, Dhampur caters to agriculture, energy, and industrial sectors. Its integrated business model supports sustained operations and resilience across commodity cycles.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects a recovery in sugarcane crushing and ethanol production going forward, which could help margins improve somewhat in FY26. While no firm targets are given, rising sugar prices and policy tailwinds in ethanol blending offer potential upside. Dhampur’s focus on operational resilience and product diversification could support better performance in the next few quarters.

Name: Dalmia Bharat Sugar & Industries Ltd
Overview:
Dalmia Bharat Ltd. is a leading Indian cement manufacturer, recognized for its commitment to sustainability and innovation. The company operates a diverse portfolio of brands, including Dalmia Cement, Konark Cement, and Dalmia DSP, catering to both retail and institutional segments.

With a focus on low-carbon technologies, Dalmia Bharat has emerged as a frontrunner in the green cement space, aiming to achieve a carbon footprint of 465 kg CO₂ per tonne of cement. The company has also been expanding its capacity, with plans to increase its installed cement capacity to 49.5 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) by the end of FY25.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Dalmia Bharat plans to enhance its production capacities and invest in technological advancements to meet the increasing demand for quality cement. The company aims to capitalize on the growing trends in infrastructure development and sustainable construction practices to drive future growth. With a focus on operational excellence and customer satisfaction, Dalmia Bharat is well-positioned to maintain its leadership in the Indian cement industry.

Name: Bannari Amman Sugars Ltd
Overview:
Bannari Amman Sugars Ltd is a diversified player in the Indian agribusiness sector, primarily engaged in sugar manufacturing, distillery operations, power generation, and granite processing. The company operates integrated facilities across Tamil Nadu, ensuring a robust supply chain from cane cultivation to finished products.

With a strong presence in both domestic and export markets, Bannari Amman Sugars leverages its integrated operations to maintain cost efficiencies and capitalize on synergies across its various business segments.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Looking ahead, Bannari Amman Sugars plans to enhance its ethanol production capacity in line with the government's push for renewable energy sources. The company is also exploring opportunities in the export market to mitigate domestic market fluctuations. With a focus on operational efficiencies and strategic investments, Bannari Amman Sugars aims to achieve sustainable growth in the coming fiscal year.

Name: Apcotex Industries Ltd
Overview:
Apcotex Industries Ltd manufactures specialty chemicals and synthetic latex products for industrial applications, including adhesives, coatings, textiles, and construction. Its product portfolio focuses on high-performance solutions tailored to client requirements.

In FY25, Apcotex enhanced production capacity, strengthened R&D capabilities, and optimized operations to drive efficiency. The company emphasizes sustainable manufacturing practices and innovation in product development.

Apcotex maintains long-term relationships with industrial clients and is exploring expansion in export markets. Its focus on quality, innovation, and operational excellence provides a solid foundation for future growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Apcotex Industries Ltd is actively expanding its production capacities to meet the increasing demand for synthetic rubber and latex products. The company has invested over ₹200 crore in recent years to enhance its manufacturing facilities, including the establishment of India's first Nitrile Latex plant at Valia, Gujarat, and a multi-purpose emulsions plant at Taloja, Maharashtra. These expansions aim to add approximately 85,000 MT of emulsions capacity, potentially contributing around ₹600–650 crore to the company's topline in the coming years.

Name: Shree Renuka Sugars Ltd
Overview:
Shree Renuka Sugars Ltd, incorporated in 1995, is a leading Indian manufacturer and refiner of sugar, with operations spanning ethanol production, ethyl alcohol, and power generation. It is the fourth-largest sugar producer in India and operates the country’s largest sugar refinery. The flagship “Madhur” brand commands about one-third of the branded sugar market, with branded sales forming half of total volumes.

The company has eight sugar mills in Karnataka with a crushing capacity of 37,500 TCD, two refineries in Gujarat and West Bengal totaling 5,500 TPD, distillery capacity of 1,250 KLPD, and a 259 MW co-generation capacity. Its operations are export-driven, with refining forming a major revenue contributor. The company continues to focus on refining profitability, white sugar premium optimization, and distribution expansion, while also pursuing organic and inorganic growth avenues.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Management’s focus in FY25 remains on refining profitability, leveraging favorable white sugar premiums, scaling distribution, and capitalizing on export opportunities. The Anamika acquisition is expected to drive geographical diversification and cane security. Capacity enhancements in the distillery segment and a continued push in branded sugar should support margin stability. However, high debt levels and volatility in cane availability remain key watchpoints for the year ahead.

Name: Pix Transmission Ltd
Overview:
Pix Transmissions Ltd. is a leading Indian manufacturer specializing in belts and related mechanical power transmission products. Established in 1981 and headquartered in Mumbai, the company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Hingna and Nagalwadi, Nagpur. Pix Transmissions offers a diverse range of products, including timing belts, raw edge cogged belts, poly-V belts, and automotive belts under the "PIX Force" brand. The company serves various sectors such as automotive, agriculture, industrial, and infrastructure.

With a strong presence in both domestic and international markets, Pix Transmissions has established subsidiaries and joint ventures in Europe and the Middle East, including PIX Europe Limited, PIX Germany GmbH, and PIX Middle East Trading LLC.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Pix Transmissions aims to achieve a revenue target of ₹5,700–5,800 crore in FY26, representing a growth of 17–19% compared to the previous year. The company plans to continue expanding its product offerings and strengthen its presence in both domestic and international markets. With a focus on innovation and quality, Pix Transmissions is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

Name: Davangere Sugar Company Ltd
Overview:
Davangere Sugar Company Ltd, incorporated in 1970, is a major sugar producer in India. The company is engaged in crushing sugarcane and producing sugar, along with by-products such as molasses and bagasse. It also operates co-generation plants that use bagasse to produce renewable energy, supporting sustainable practices. Davangere Sugar focuses on optimizing operations, improving yields, and adhering to environmental norms. 

The company serves domestic markets while exploring opportunities for export. Its integrated approach to sugar production and renewable energy generation provides stable revenue streams and a competitive advantage in the agro-industrial sector. Its Karnataka-based facility has a sugar-crushing capacity of 4,750 TCD, a multi-fuel co-generation unit with 24.45 MW capacity, and a distillery unit producing 63 KLPD.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Davangere Sugar Company Ltd is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to bolster its growth trajectory. The company is expanding its ethanol production capacity from 65 KLPD to 85 KLPD, with an additional grain-based distillery unit under construction at a cost of ₹54 crore. Furthermore, Davangere Sugar is enhancing its sugarcane cultivation by increasing the area under production by 15,000 acres, aiming to support local farmers and ensure a steady supply of raw materials

Name: EID Parry (India) Ltd
Overview:
EID Parry Limited operates primarily in sugar manufacturing, nutraceuticals, and ethanol production, with a strong presence in farm inputs including bio-pesticides through its subsidiary Coromandel International. As part of the Murugappa Group, the company leverages its diverse business interests and expertise to maintain a leadership position in these sectors.

The company’s sugar operations are integrated with refining, ethanol distillation, and power co-generation, serving both domestic and international markets. EID Parry focuses on sustainable agricultural practices and value-added product expansion, navigating industry dynamics such as fluctuating sugar prices and evolving ethanol policies.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
EID Parry anticipates improvement in refinery margins as global white sugar supply tightens in FY26. Ethanol distillery operations are expected to maintain high utilization rates, with dynamic product mix management. The consumer products segment will focus on aggressive distribution growth, primarily in South India, with plans for pan-India expansion.

Name: Ugar Sugar Works Ltd
Overview: Ugar Sugar Works Ltd is a leading sugar manufacturing company in Southern India, established over 75 years ago. The company operates two sugar plants located in Ugar and Jewargi, with a combined daily cane crushing capacity of 18,000 tonnes. Ugar Sugar Works Ltd also produces industrial and potable alcohol, contributing to the diversified revenue streams of the company.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ugar Sugar Works Ltd is pushing forward with its integrated sugar business by expanding ethanol and power operations, aligning with rising demand for ethanol blending and renewable energy. It has ramped up ethanol production capacity significantly, deploying efficient distillery technology to make more biofuel from sugarcane juice and grains while minimizing energy and water usage.

Name: Avadh Sugar & Energy Ltd
Overview:
Avadh Sugar & Energy Ltd is a leading Indian company engaged in the production of sugar, ethanol, and co-generation of power. Established in 1991, the company operates modern sugar mills and distilleries in Uttar Pradesh, utilizing advanced cane processing technologies. Its diversified business model integrates sugar production with ethanol distillation and power generation, optimizing resource utilization and profitability.

The company focuses on sustainable sugarcane procurement, renewable energy production, and expansion into ethanol for biofuel applications. Avadh Sugar & Energy has strengthened its market presence with robust distribution channels and strategic contracts with ethanol and power buyers. Investments in modern technology, operational efficiency, and quality standards ensure consistent performance in both domestic and export markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Avadh Sugar & Energy Ltd is actively enhancing its production capabilities to meet growing demand in the sugar and renewable energy sectors. The company is expanding its distillery capacity from 100 KLD to 150 KLD, focusing on producing ethanol, and increasing its co-generation power plant capacity from 3 MW to 6.5 MW at its Hargaon facility in Uttar Pradesh.

Name: Bajaj Hindusthan Sugar Ltd
Overview:
Bajaj Hindusthan Sugar Ltd is one of India’s oldest and largest integrated sugar producers, operating multiple sugar mills in Uttar Pradesh with extensive capacity to refine sugarcane. The company also produces ancillary products like ethanol, alcohol, and cogenerated power, aligning its business with both agro-processing and energy sectors.

Its integrated operational model enables it to efficiently process sugarcane into sugar and by-products, while leveraging ethanol and power generation to diversify revenue streams and improve financial resilience amid market fluctuations.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company is expected to benefit from the government’s ethanol blending program, which provides a stable demand avenue. Its diversified operations in sugar, ethanol, and cogeneration strengthen long-term growth potential, though performance remains sensitive to policy support and cyclical sugar price movements.

.

Name: DCM Shriram Industries Ltd
Overview:
DCM Shriram Ltd is a diversified conglomerate with a strong presence in the Indian industrial landscape. The company operates across multiple sectors, including agriculture, chemicals, and building materials. With a commitment to innovation and sustainability, DCM Shriram has established itself as a key player in these industries, delivering quality products and services to its customers.

Headquartered in New Delhi, DCM Shriram has a significant operational footprint across India, with manufacturing facilities in various states. The company's diverse portfolio encompasses a wide range of products and services, catering to the needs of various sectors and contributing to its robust business performance.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
DCM Shriram plans to invest in capacity expansion and strategic acquisitions to drive growth. The company has acquired a 53% stake in DNV Global to strengthen its Fenesta business and signed an agreement to acquire 100% of Hindusthan Specialty Chemicals Ltd, enhancing its chemicals portfolio.

Name: Godavari Biorefineries Ltd
Overview:
Incorporated in 1939 and part of the Somaiya Group, Godavari Biorefineries Ltd (GBL) operates as an integrated bio-refinery across sugar, ethanol, bio-based chemicals, and co-generation power. The company is a pioneer in green chemistry, being India’s first producer of Ethyl Vinyl Ether (EVE), the largest domestic manufacturer of MPO, and one of only two global manufacturers of natural 1,3 Butylene Glycol. Its diversified portfolio caters to FMCG, pharma, fragrances, agrochemicals, and clean energy sectors, with operations spanning Karnataka and Maharashtra.

GBL is undergoing a strategic pivot to increase its share of high-value bio-based specialty chemicals and multi-feedstock ethanol, thereby reducing dependence on sugar and seasonal businesses. It is investing over ₹325 crore in expansion projects, including a 200 KLPD grain-based distillery and capacity upgrades in specialty chemicals, while advancing its oncology R&D pipeline with patents across Europe and China.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company is exploring new verticals, particularly in advanced materials and sustainable agriculture, to diversify its portfolio. With a commitment to sustainability, GBL operates zero-liquid-discharge facilities, utilizes biomass for over 80% of its energy needs, and recycles nearly all water used in its processes. These initiatives position GBL for sustained growth in the evolving bio-economy landscape.

Name: Century Enka Ltd
Overview:
Century Enka Ltd, part of the Aditya Birla Group, is a leading manufacturer of synthetic yarns and related products in India. The company produces Nylon Filament Yarn (NFY) and Nylon Tyre Cord Fabric (NTCF), catering to various industries, including automotive and textiles.

With a production capacity of 30,000 tons per annum for NFY and 32,000 tons per annum for NTCF, Century Enka Ltd is one of the largest producers in India. The company's products are known for their quality and durability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Century Enka Ltd plans to expand its product offerings and enhance manufacturing capabilities to meet the growing demand in domestic and international markets. The company is also focusing on sustainability and technological advancements to stay ahead in the industry.

Name: Pashupati Cotspin Ltd
Overview:
Established in 2017, Pashupati Cotspin Ltd is a prominent Indian company specializing in the processing of raw cotton and manufacturing of cotton yarn. The company operates a fully automated ginning plant with a production capacity of over 2.5 lakh cotton bales (approximately 40,000 metric tons) per annum, utilizing 112 double roller gins.

As part of the Pashupati Group, the company adheres to a "Farm to Fabric" model, integrating various stages of production from ginning to spinning. With over 25 years of experience in the textile industry, Pashupati Cotspin Ltd exports its products to approximately 35 countries worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Pashupati Cotspin Ltd is strategically expanding its production capacity to meet the growing global demand for sustainable textile products. The company plans to enhance its manufacturing capabilities by incorporating advanced technologies and expanding its product portfolio to include a wider range of yarns and fabrics.

Name: RSWM Ltd
Overview: RSWM Ltd, the flagship company of the LNJ Bhilwara Group, is one of India's leading manufacturers and exporters of synthetic, cotton, and blended spun yarns. The company has a diverse product range, including yarns suitable for suitings, shirtings, hosiery, carpet, denim, technical textiles, and industrial applications. RSWM Ltd exports its products to over 70 countries globally, establishing a strong international presence. With a commitment to quality and innovation, RSWM Ltd has become a prominent player in the textile industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

RSWM Ltd is actively pursuing growth through strategic investments and modernization initiatives. The company has committed approximately ₹410 crore to expand its denim, cotton melange yarn, and knits business, alongside modernizing equipment across all its facilities. Additionally, RSWM is focusing on enhancing its product mix by incorporating value-added offerings, such as specialty, functional, technical, and eco-friendly yarn and fabric.

Name: GHCL Textiles Ltd
Overview: GHCL Textiles Ltd is one of India's largest home textile manufacturers, with an integrated setup commencing from the spinning of yarn to weaving, dyeing, and finishing. The division produces 100% cotton, blended, and synthetic yarns. With a capacity of 225,000 ring spindles, 3,320 rotors, 480 vortex, and 5,760 TFO spindles, GHCL Textiles adheres to global standards and international best practices, recognized for customer focus and quality products
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

GHCL Textiles Ltd is embarking on a bold transformation from traditional yarn manufacturing to becoming a fully integrated textile powerhouse. The company is significantly expanding its spinning and knitting operations, including the addition of tens of thousands of spindles and circular knitting machines, supported by major investments in green energy—now accounting for over 70% of its power needs.

Name: Mafatlal Industries Ltd
Overview: Mafatlal Industries Ltd, part of the Arvind Mafatlal Group, has a rich legacy of over 100 years in the textile industry. The company manufactures textiles including fabrics, yarns, and apparel materials, serving both domestic and international markets. It has diversified business operations, including textiles trading and limited financial services, enabling stable revenue streams. Mafatlal Industries emphasizes quality, innovation, and customer service, maintaining competitive positioning in the textile sector. The company has modern production facilities and supply chain networks that ensure timely delivery and product reliability. Its long-standing brand reputation supports continued growth in both B2B and B2C segments.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Mafatlal Industries Ltd is committed to enhancing its position in the textile sector through strategic investments and modernization initiatives. The company has undertaken significant upgrades at its manufacturing units in Nadiad and Navsari, Gujarat, focusing on integrating advanced technologies to improve efficiency and product quality.

Name: Rajapalayam Mills Ltd
Overview:
Rajapalayam Mills Ltd is a well-established textile company specializing in spinning, weaving, and production of high-quality cotton and blended yarns. The company caters to apparel, home textile, and industrial sectors, providing reliable and consistent products for domestic and international markets. With decades of experience, Rajapalayam Mills has built strong relationships with clients, leveraging its reputation for quality, timely delivery, and operational reliability.

The company operates integrated textile facilities that enhance efficiency and ensure stringent quality control. By adopting sustainable production practices and modern technology, Rajapalayam Mills is able to optimize resource use while maintaining high production standards. Its diversified product portfolio, including cotton, polyester, and blended yarns, positions the company to respond to changing market demands and capitalize on export opportunities.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Rajapalayam Mills Ltd is actively enhancing its capabilities in the textile industry through strategic investments and technological advancements. The company has recently commissioned a Fabric Expansion and Processing Unit, featuring 166 looms and a processing capacity of 50,000 meters per day, enabling the production of ready-to-stitch apparel fabrics.

Name: Nahar Spinning Mills Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1980 and headquartered in Ludhiana, Punjab, Nahar Spinning Mills Ltd is a prominent player in India's textile industry. The company specializes in the production of cotton and blended yarns, as well as hosiery knitwear. It operates a substantial manufacturing infrastructure, including a spinning division with over 93,000 spindles and a dyeing plant in Patiala, catering to high-value markets in the U.S. and European Union. 

As part of the Nahar Group, the company benefits from a diversified portfolio that spans textiles, garments, and other sectors like soaps and vegetable oils. With a global footprint, Nahar Spinning Mills exports a significant portion of its products, reflecting its strong presence in international markets.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Nahar Spinning Mills aims to capitalize on growing demand in international markets, particularly in the U.S. and European Union, by leveraging its enhanced product offerings and manufacturing capabilities. The company's focus on high-value yarn production positions it well for sustained growth in the global textile industry.

Name: Sutlej Textiles and Industries Ltd
Overview: Sutlej Textiles and Industries Ltd, established in 2005, is a prominent player in the textile industry in India. The company specializes in manufacturing a wide range of yarns, including cotton, polyester, and blended yarns. Sutlej is recognized as the largest producer of melange yarn in India and one of the few exclusive spinners for specialty yarns such as modal, lyocell, and Tencel. With a strong presence in over 65 countries, Sutlej caters to a diverse global clientele, offering high-quality products that meet international standards.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Sutlej is positioned to recover through continued cost discipline and potential stabilization in textile demand markets. If current cost efficiencies hold, the company could gradually return toward profitability and restore stakeholder confidence over the coming quarters.

Name: Ambika Cotton Mills Ltd
Overview:
Ambika Cotton Mills Ltd is a leading textile manufacturer focused on producing cotton yarn and fabrics for a wide range of domestic and international clients. The company operates modern spinning and weaving facilities, ensuring high-quality output and operational efficiency. Ambika Cotton has established a strong presence in the textile market, serving apparel, home textiles, and industrial sectors. Its emphasis on innovation, product consistency, and customer service has enabled it to maintain a competitive edge over peers.

In addition to quality manufacturing, Ambika Cotton actively invests in process optimization, modern machinery, and technology-driven production methods. These investments not only enhance productivity but also enable the company to expand its product portfolio to meet evolving market trends. With strong distribution channels and export capabilities, Ambika Cotton is positioned to capitalize on growing demand in global textile markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ambika Cotton Mills Ltd is focusing on expanding its spinning and knitting capacities to meet the growing demand for specialty cotton yarn. The company has planned an investment of approximately ₹20 crore to enhance its knitting segment, aiming to increase capacity to 16,000 kg per day. Additionally, Ambika Cotton Mills is exploring opportunities to expand its presence in the export markets, leveraging the increasing global demand for high-quality cotton yarn.

Name: Donear Industries Ltd
Overview:
Donear Industries Ltd is a textile manufacturer specializing in suiting fabrics, apparel, and related textile products. The company is a well-known brand in the Indian textile space, with a wide distribution network spanning across domestic and export markets.

Its integration across textile manufacturing processes ensures quality consistency and cost efficiency. With a focus on brand-building and premiumization, Donear has established itself as a recognized name in menswear and suiting segments.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Donear Industries Ltd is poised for growth in FY26, with a strong start in Q1. The significant increase in net profit indicates effective cost management and operational efficiency. If this positive trend continues, the company is well-positioned to achieve sustainable growth and enhance shareholder value in the coming quarters.

Name: Indo Rama Synthetics (India) Ltd
Overview:
Indo Rama Synthetics (India) Ltd., established in 1986, stands as one of India's largest dedicated polyester manufacturers. The company specializes in producing a comprehensive range of polyester products, including Polyester Staple Fibre (PSF), Polyester Filament Yarn (PFY), 

Draw Texturized Yarn (DTY), specialty fibers, and chips. Headquartered in Butibori, Maharashtra, Indo Rama operates an integrated manufacturing facility that enables large-scale production efficiency and technological integration to maintain competitive quality standards
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Indo Rama Synthetics is poised for continued growth, driven by its strategic initiatives and robust product portfolio. The company's focus on technological advancements and sustainable practices positions it well to capitalize on emerging market opportunities in the polyester manufacturing sector.

Name: Raj Rayon Industries Ltd
Overview:
Raj Rayon Industries Ltd is engaged in the manufacturing and marketing of polyester chips, polyester filament yarn, and partially oriented yarn. The company caters to the domestic and international textile industry, with its products serving as essential raw materials for garments, home furnishings, and industrial textiles. With a focus on both volume and quality, the company has strategically positioned itself in the competitive textile value chain.

The company’s integrated manufacturing capabilities allow it to address diverse demand segments across apparel, fashion, and technical textile applications. By leveraging domestic growth in polyester consumption and tapping into export opportunities, Raj Rayon aims to strengthen its market positioning. The company continues to focus on efficiency, technology upgrades, and financial stability as it rebuilds operations after restructuring in past years.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Raj Rayon is focusing on expanding its product portfolio into value-added yarns to cater to higher-margin markets. Export orders are expected to grow, particularly from Southeast Asian and Middle Eastern buyers. Rising polyester adoption in India, driven by substitution of cotton, is likely to boost growth in FY26.

Name: AYM Syntex Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1985, AYM Syntex Ltd is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in synthetic yarns, including Polyester Filament Yarn (PFY), Nylon Filament Yarn, and Bulk Continuous Filament Yarn (BCF). 

The company operates state-of-the-art facilities in Silvassa and Palghar, equipped with advanced machinery for spinning, texturizing, draw twisting, and dyeing of polyester and nylon yarns. AYM Syntex is recognized as one of the largest producers of BCF yarns in India and a leading multipolymer yarn dyeing house in Asia
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

AYM Syntex Ltd is focused on expanding its global footprint by establishing new markets and enhancing its product portfolio. The company plans to increase production capacity and introduce innovative yarn solutions to meet the evolving demands of the textile and industrial sectors. Investments in sustainable practices and advanced manufacturing technologies are central to AYM Syntex's strategy, aiming to improve efficiency and reduce environmental impact.

Name: Cheviot Company Ltd
Overview:
Cheviot Company Ltd, part of the Cheviot Group and headquartered in Kolkata, is among India’s leading jute product manufacturers with a legacy spanning over a century. The company produces a wide range of traditional and diversified jute goods such as hessian cloth, sacking bags, decorative fabrics, and high-value industrial jute products. Its vertically integrated operations include spinning, weaving, finishing, and packaging.

Cheviot emphasizes both traditional and modern applications of jute, supplying to domestic industries as well as exporting globally. With a strong focus on sustainability and natural fibre adoption, the company caters to markets in Europe, Asia, and the Americas, capitalizing on the rising demand for eco-friendly alternatives to synthetic packaging.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Cheviot Company Ltd is strategically diversifying its product portfolio to include non-traditional jute applications, such as geotextiles, home décor, and sustainable packaging solutions. The company is investing in state-of-the-art infrastructure to enhance manufacturing capabilities and meet the growing demand for eco-friendly products.

Name: Himatsingka Seide Ltd
Overview:
Himatsingka Seide Ltd is a vertically integrated global textile company, engaged in the design, development, manufacturing, and distribution of home textile products. Its scale of operations spans spinning, sheeting, terry towels, and drapery fabrics, making it one of the world’s largest integrated players in bedding, bath, and cotton yarn categories. The company operates four global-scale plants in Karnataka, with state-of-the-art infrastructure and patented DNA cotton traceability technology.

The company manages a large portfolio of global brands under license agreements, including Calvin Klein, Tommy Hilfiger, Kate Spade, and Disney, while also building its own brands such as Himeya, Liv, Atmosphere, and Bellora. With a strong North American presence and expanding operations across Europe, Asia-Pacific, and India, Himatsingka is pursuing a dual strategy of reducing US dependency and strengthening its domestic branded and private-label businesses.
Key Takeways:
Outlook & Strategic Focus
Management reaffirmed its 18–22% EBITDA margin guidance and is focused on diversification into India and other geographies, optimizing costs, and scaling domestic brands. ESG initiatives and energy rationalization remain a medium-term priority. While near-term growth is constrained by tariffs and slower customer replacement, the company’s large asset base, brand portfolio, and India-focused strategy position it for long-term scale and profitability.

Name: Ruby Mills Ltd
Overview:
Ruby Mills Ltd is an established Indian textile company specializing in spinning, weaving, and manufacturing cotton and blended yarns. The company produces high-quality yarns for apparel, home textiles, and industrial applications, serving clients in domestic and international markets. Ruby Mills emphasizes operational efficiency, quality control, and timely delivery, maintaining a competitive edge in the textile industry.

In addition to its traditional textile operations, Ruby Mills invests in modern machinery and production technology to enhance productivity and expand product offerings. The company’s strategic focus on customer satisfaction, sustainable production practices, and diversification enables it to respond effectively to changing market trends.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Ruby Mills Ltd is strategically positioning itself for sustainable growth by focusing on two key areas: textile innovation and real estate development. In the textile segment, the company is enhancing its product offerings with advanced finishes such as anti-odour, water-repellent, and antimicrobial treatments. Additionally, Ruby Mills is expanding its real estate portfolio, including the development of 'The Ruby' tower in Dadar, Mumbai, which is expected to increase occupancy and rental income.

Name: Borana Weaves Ltd
Overview:
Borana Weaves Ltd is a fast-growing textile manufacturer focused on high-quality greige (unfinished) fabrics, serving both domestic and export-oriented markets. Since 2021, the company has dramatically scaled its manufacturing capacity from 55 million meters to 220 million meters annually, powered by an advanced facility with 700 high-speed water-jet looms. Boasting nearly all processes—98% texturizing and twisting—captured in-house, the company maintains tight control over quality, consistency, and efficiency.

From its strong repeat order ratio to innovative manufacturing practices, Borana has carved out a niche in superior greige fabric production, catering to varied textile applications while emphasizing sustainable operations, including water recycling to reduce environmental impact.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Future expansion could involve value-added segments such as dyed or finished fabrics, enhancing margins. The company’s growing scale and commitment to quality make it well-positioned to capture incremental opportunities in both exports and domestic consumption.

Name: Gloster Ltd
Overview:
Gloster Limited specializes in the production and distribution of jute and jute-blended products, catering to both domestic and international markets. The company offers a diverse range of products including Hessian & sacking, yarn, and various jute-based items, serving industries such as FMCG, agriculture, and fashion textiles. Government sacking contributes approximately 30% of total revenue, reflecting a regulated and steady business segment. Gloster continuously innovates its product portfolio to address evolving customer needs and maintain a competitive edge in the jute industry.

The company has a strong domestic revenue base, accounting for around 72% of total income, while exports contribute to markets in the Americas, Europe, Asia, and Australia. To diversify operations, Gloster is entering the industrial cable sector through Fort Gloster Industries (FGIL), expanding its manufacturing capabilities and product offerings.

These strategic initiatives position Gloster for growth, diversification, and strengthened market presence in both domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Gloster Ltd is committed to expanding its production capacity and enhancing product offerings to meet growing global demand for sustainable materials. The company is investing in a greenfield jute mill, aiming to increase its production capacity by 130 tonnes per day, with the project being implemented in phases. Additionally, Gloster is focusing on research and development initiatives in collaboration with regulatory bodies to develop a water-free jute retting process, which will address challenges posed by erratic precipitation patterns.

Name: Faze Three Ltd
Overview:
Established in 1985, Faze Three Limited is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in home textile products such as bathmats, rugs, blankets, throws, and cushions. The company operates with a strong export orientation, serving leading retailers across the United States, United Kingdom, and Europe. 

Faze Three Limited has built long-standing relationships with top retail chains, contributing to its robust market presence. The company operates from multiple manufacturing facilities located in Panipat, Silvassa, and Vapi, ensuring efficient production and timely delivery of its products.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Faze Three Limited's future outlook remains positive, with expectations of continued growth in the home textile export market. The company's focus on quality, innovation, and customer relationships positions it well to capitalize on emerging opportunities.

Name: XT Global Infotech Ltd
Overview:
XT Global Infotech Ltd is an IT services provider offering digital transformation, enterprise solutions, and staffing services. It serves clients across industries with technology-enabled consulting and delivery models. The company operates with both domestic and overseas customers.

The business emphasizes innovation, automation, and cloud solutions to remain competitive. With scalable offshore operations, XT Global has built a strong presence in emerging technology solutions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
XTGlobal appears well-positioned for continued momentum through FY 26. If it manages costs while scaling operations, the company could further improve profit margins and sustain revenue growth.

Name: HCL Infosystems Ltd
Overview:
HCL Infosystems Ltd, part of the HCL Group, has long been a recognized name in India’s technology sector, providing IT hardware, software, and services. Historically known for its PC and systems integration business, the company has gradually shifted its focus towards distribution of technology products, system integration projects, and managed services. Its role as a distributor for leading global technology brands has ensured steady market presence.

Over the years, the company has transformed its portfolio to align with changing industry dynamics, moving away from commoditized hardware towards value-added services and distribution. While legacy businesses continue to be phased out, HCL Infosystems has built a base of enterprise clients and government contracts, positioning itself in niche segments of the IT services and products ecosystem.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
HCL Infosystems is focusing on stabilizing operations, improving margins, and building a sustainable services portfolio. Its future growth depends on scaling managed services and leveraging partnerships with global technology companies.

Name: GTL Infrastructure Ltd
Overview:
GTL Infrastructure Ltd is a leading provider of telecom infrastructure services in India. The company offers a range of services, including tower leasing, network rollout, and maintenance, to telecom operators and other service providers. GTL Infrastructure Ltd is known for its extensive network and commitment to service excellence.

The company has a pan-India presence, with a large portfolio of telecom towers and infrastructure assets. GTL Infrastructure Ltd leverages technology to optimize network performance and enhance service delivery. The company's focus on quality and customer satisfaction has earned it a strong reputation in the telecom infrastructure industry.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
GTL Infrastructure Ltd plans to expand its service offerings and geographic presence to tap into new markets. The company is investing in technology to enhance service delivery and operational efficiency. Additionally, GTL Infrastructure Ltd aims to strengthen its infrastructure assets to meet the growing demand for telecom services.

Name: Vindhya Telelinks Ltd
Overview:
Vindhya Telelinks Ltd, established in 1986, is a leading Indian manufacturer and supplier of telecommunication cables, including optical fiber cables (OFC), copper cables, and specialty cables. The company also offers turnkey solutions in the Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) sector, undertaking infrastructure projects across various domains such as telecom, water supply, power distribution, and solar energy.

The company operates through two primary segments: Cable manufacturing and EPC services. It has established a strong presence in both sectors, leveraging its technical expertise and experience to deliver quality products and services.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Vindhya Telelinks is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for infrastructure development and renewable energy projects in India. The company plans to expand its capacity in the Cable segment, particularly for solar PV cables, to meet the increasing requirements of the renewable energy sector.

Name: Suyog Telematics Ltd
Overview:
Suyog Telematics Ltd is an India-based technology company specializing in telematics solutions, fleet management systems, and IoT-based monitoring services. Established in the early 2000s, the company caters to logistics, transportation, and industrial clients by providing real-time tracking, monitoring, and analytics solutions. Suyog Telematics Limited is engaged primarily in the business of installing, commissioning and servicing of poles, towers and optical fibre cable (OFC ) systems, catering to the telecommunication industry with operators across 12 telecom circles in India.
The company is registered as Infrastructure Provider Category-I (IP-I) with the Department of Telecommunications (DoT).

The company’s offerings include vehicle tracking devices, GPS-based monitoring systems, and software platforms that provide operational insights, improve efficiency, and ensure safety and compliance. Suyog Telematics Ltd focuses on innovation, technological upgrades, and seamless integration with client systems to provide end-to-end solutions for fleet and asset management.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
 Suyog Telematics Ltd aims to expand its product portfolio, strengthen its presence in domestic and international markets, and leverage emerging technologies such as AI, IoT, and cloud-based analytics to drive future growth.

Name: NELCO Ltd
Overview:
Nelco Ltd, established in 1940, is a Tata Group company specializing in satellite communication services. The company primarily provides Very Small Aperture Terminal (VSAT) solutions to close user group networks, catering to sectors such as banking, defense, and enterprise communications. Nelco's offerings enable secure and reliable data transmission across remote and challenging terrains.

In recent developments, Nelco has partnered with global satellite operator Eutelsat to introduce OneWeb's low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite connectivity services in India. This collaboration aims to deliver high-speed, low-latency internet access across the Indian subcontinent, including remote areas, thereby enhancing connectivity for various sectors .
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Nelco's strategic partnership with Eutelsat to provide OneWeb's LEO satellite connectivity services positions the company to tap into the growing demand for high-speed internet in remote areas of India. This initiative is expected to bolster Nelco's service offerings and expand its market reach.

Name: Mahanagar Telephone Nigam Ltd
Overview:
Mahanagar Telephone Nigam Ltd (MTNL) is a state-owned telecom operator providing landline, broadband, and mobile services in Delhi and Mumbai. Established in 1986, MTNL has played a crucial role in India's urban telecommunications landscape.

Despite its long-standing presence, the company has faced challenges from increased competition and declining subscriber bases. MTNL continues to maintain legacy operations while attempting to modernize its service offerings.

Government support and strategic restructuring remain central to its efforts to stabilize financial health and improve operational efficiency. The company seeks to balance debt management with service quality improvements to maintain relevance in the telecom sector.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

MTNL is gearing up for a major revival by expanding its 4G network with strong support from the government. It has entered a long-term collaboration with BSNL for shared deployment of thousands of 4G sites, which will improve connectivity in its core markets. MTNL is also set to unlock value from its real estate holdings by selling or leasing surplus land and buildings, and is exploring asset monetization through a new public framework.

Name: Bharti Hexacom Ltd
Overview:
Bharti Hexacom Ltd offers mobile, fixed-line telephone, and broadband services mainly in Rajasthan and Northeast India. The company is part of the larger Airtel group and focuses on expanding its wireless and broadband networks in these regions. It serves a growing customer base with emphasis on upgrading to smartphones and expanding 5G coverage.

The business also includes digital TV and B2B enterprise services, with investments in cloud, data centers, and emerging technologies like AI.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Bharti Hexacom expects continued growth driven by 5G adoption, broadband expansion, and B2B digital services. Margins are likely to remain strong with ongoing cost discipline. The company aims to capitalize on market opportunities while maintaining financial prudence and adapting to evolving customer needs.

Name: HFCL Ltd
Overview:
HFCL Ltd (Himachal Futuristic Communications Limited) is a leading telecom infrastructure and technology company engaged in manufacturing high-end telecom equipment, optical fiber, optical fiber cables (OFC), and providing turnkey network services. It caters to telecom operators, defense, and railways with a diverse portfolio including Wi-Fi access points, UBR radios, fiber connectivity solutions, and defense electronics. The company has expanded its product mix to focus more on telecom products, improving margins and reducing working capital needs.

With operations in over 45 countries and clientele including Jio, Airtel, BSNL, Nokia, and Bharat Electronics, HFCL maintains a strong domestic presence (89% revenue) and a growing export base (11%). Its 5 manufacturing plants have a combined capacity of 14 Mn fkm optical fiber and 25 Mn fkm OFC annually, with planned expansion to 33.9 Mn fkm and 35 Mn fkm respectively. Strategic partnerships with Qualcomm, Wipro, and others bolster its innovation in 5G, Wi-Fi 7, and AI-powered network solutions.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
HFCL’s growth in FY26 will be driven by its pivot to high-margin telecom products, capacity expansions, and rising export opportunities, especially in Europe post anti-dumping duty exemption. Enhanced R&D spend (5% of revenue) and strong technology partnerships are expected to accelerate new product launches in 5G, defense, and AI-driven network solutions, positioning the company for sustained global competitiveness.

Name: Tata Teleservices (Maharashtra) Ltd
Overview:
Tata Teleservices Ltd (TTSL) operates in the wired and wireless telecommunication domain, holding a Unified License with Access Service Authorisation in Maharashtra and Goa, along with an Internet Service Provider Category A license. The company provides a comprehensive suite of enterprise-grade services including voice, data, managed telecom, cloud communication, IoT, cybersecurity, and collaboration solutions. Its service portfolio is aimed at enhancing business connectivity, digital transformation, and operational efficiency for large enterprises, SMEs, and government bodies.

Over the years, TTSL has transitioned from a pan-India consumer telecom player to a focused enterprise telecom solutions provider. The company has invested in optical fiber infrastructure spanning ~17,000 km in its licensed circles and launched innovative solutions such as Smartflo, Smart Internet Lease Line, Ultra-Lola 3.0, and Hub Connect. Collaborations like the Verified Business Caller ID Solution with Truecaller further strengthen its positioning in the enterprise telecom market.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The company is targeting growth through SME penetration, expansion of its unified communications and security portfolios, and scaling SD-WAN, fiber broadband, and omni-channel solutions. FY25 industry tailwinds include BharatNet rural broadband expansion, the PLI scheme for domestic telecom manufacturing, and increased AI-driven adoption in enterprise operations, all of which are expected to support medium-term growth.

Name: ITI Ltd
Overview: ITI Limited specializes in manufacturing, trading, and servicing telecommunications equipment and related services, with a focus on telephone communication solutions. It handles turnkey projects like BharatNet, ASCON, Net for Spectrum, and FTTH rollouts, and serves major public sector clients such as BSNL, MTNL, and defence agencies. The company operates six manufacturing units across India and maintains an R&D presence in several locations. It benefits from government backing under a revival plan and has historically depended heavily on state and PSU clients.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
The sharp rise in revenues and narrowed losses indicate that ITI made significant operational improvements in FY25. The consistent reduction in quarterly losses and rising revenues suggest path to stabilization.

Name: Astra Microwave Products Ltd
Overview:
Astra Microwave Products is a specialized technology company engaged in the design, development, and manufacture of high-value RF and microwave components, subsystems, and systems for defense, space, meteorology, and telecommunication applications. The company caters to mission-critical programs in radars, electronic warfare, missile electronics, space payloads, and counter-drone solutions, serving both domestic and export markets.

With decades of indigenous design capability and in-house manufacturing infrastructure, Astra has positioned itself as a diversified defense electronics player. Its operations span from MMICs and T/R modules to complete radar and EW systems, backed by strong R&D expertise, strategic partnerships, and growing participation in next-generation defense and space programs.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Astra Microwave Products Ltd is poised for sustained growth, driven by its expanding presence in defense, space, and telecommunications sectors. The company anticipates a 15–20% revenue increase in the upcoming fiscal year, bolstered by a robust order book exceeding ₹1,800 crore. Key projects include the Virupaksha and Uttam radar systems, missile seeker development, and anti-drone technologies.

Name: Black Box Ltd
Overview:
Black Box Ltd, a subsidiary of the Essar Group, is a global leader in digital infrastructure solutions. The company specializes in providing end-to-end IT services, including data center solutions, enterprise networking, and cybersecurity, to a diverse clientele across various industries.

With a strong presence in over 35 countries, Black Box Ltd serves Fortune 500 companies and other large enterprises. The company's commitment to innovation and customer-centric approach has established it as a trusted partner in the digital transformation journey of businesses worldwide.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Black Box aims to achieve $2 billion in revenue by FY29, driven by a robust order book and growth in data center and networking services. Focus remains on expanding within top global clients, enhancing operational efficiency, and exploring new markets, positioning the company for sustainable growth and stronger shareholder value.

Name: Sterlite Technologies Ltd
Overview:
Sterlite Technologies Limited (STL), established in 2001, is India’s largest optical fiber and optical fiber cable manufacturer with a strong international presence. Over the years, the company has expanded into power transmission cables and digital solutions, catering to telecom operators, data centers, and enterprises worldwide.

Positioning itself at the center of converging trends in FTTx, data centers, and 5G, STL provides essential connectivity infrastructure. With a diversified global footprint, technology-led solutions, and an innovation-driven portfolio, it is strategically placed to capture long-term demand for fiber and digital connectivity.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management maintains strong confidence in  growth, driven by rising utilization, margin expansion towards 18–20%, and robust order flows from telcos and data centers. With increasing fiberization for 5G and AI-era data centers, STL is well positioned to sustain double-digit growth while improving profitability and balance sheet strength.

Name: Tejas Networks Ltd
Overview:
Tejas Networks Ltd, incorporated in 2000 and part of Tata Sons’ subsidiary Panatone Finvest Ltd, designs and manufactures high-performance wireline and wireless networking products for telecom service providers, utilities, government, and defence networks across 75+ countries. The company’s portfolio spans 4G/5G wireless solutions based on 3GPP and O-RAN standards, optical transmission systems, broadband (GPON/XGS-PON), IP/MPLS routers, and satellite communication equipment. It is a leading domestic supplier for strategic national projects such as BharatNet, Smart Cities, and BSNL’s large-scale 4G/5G deployment.

In FY25, Tejas delivered one of the world’s largest single-vendor RAN rollouts for BSNL, boosting revenue by 261% YoY and expanding operating margins to 14% from 11% in FY24. The company is backed by strong R&D capabilities with over 445 patents filed and top-tier collaborations, including NEC (advanced wireless technologies), Rakuten Symphony (5G Open RAN), and Intel (D2M chipsets). Its growing global reach, coupled with strategic partnerships, positions it as a competitive player in next-generation telecom infrastructure markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Tejas plans to leverage its expanded FY25 portfolio—covering multi-band 5G radios, 4G/5G core, advanced optical systems, FTTx upgrades, and IP/MPLS routers—to tap large domestic and global opportunities. Partnerships with NEC and Rakuten, along with new wins in both government and private sectors, are expected to accelerate revenue recovery and international market penetration in FY26.

Name: Vodafone Idea Ltd
Overview:
Vodafone Idea Ltd (Vi) is one of India’s prominent telecom service providers, formed by the merger of Vodafone Group’s India operations and Idea Cellular in 2018. The company offers mobility services, long-distance and broadband connectivity, digital content, enterprise solutions, and value-added services to a large customer base across urban and rural India.
 
 Business Segments
