 Consumer Mobility: Voice, data, and value-added mobile services.
 
 Broadband & Long-Distance: Fixed broadband and national/international connectivity solutions.
 
 Enterprise Solutions: Cybersecurity, cloud services, IoT, and managed services for businesses.
 
 Internet of Things (IoT): Vi holds a dominant 52% market share in India’s IoT connectivity market.
 
 Revenue Mix (Indicative Approximate Percentages)
 Consumer Mobility: ~85% — Majority from prepaid and postpaid mobile services.
 
 Broadband & Long-Distance: ~5% — Includes fixed broadband and leased lines.
 
 Enterprise & IoT Solutions: ~10% — Growing share driven by IoT connectivity, cybersecurity, and managed services.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Vi (formerly Idea), with the country’s largest spectrum portfolio and extensive network infrastructure, is pursuing a revival via a Rs 14,000 crore equity infusion to strengthen its competitive position. It is promoting unlimited data plans and expanding enterprise solutions in cybersecurity, cloud, and IoT. Supported by its IoT Solution Lab, Vi is focusing on technological upgrades and strategic partnerships to navigate and overcome industry challenges.

Name: Indus Towers Ltd
Overview:
Indus Towers maintains its position as India’s largest telecom tower infrastructure provider, with leadership in both tower and tenancy counts. Following the Bharti Infratel merger and Vodafone’s exit, it is now majority-owned by Bharti Airtel (~50%).Total Towers: 219,736 towers in FY24 Co‑locations: ~342,831 (sharing ratio ~1.81x per tower) Tenancies: ~289,000 tenancies — highest globally outside China 
 
 Business Segment:
First pan-India tower company with large-scale presence.
 
Benefited from the merger of Bharti Infratel and Indus Towers in Nov 2020, boosting scale and equity capital.
 
Provides robust infrastructure to major telecom operators.
 
Efficient sharing model optimizes tower utilization and improves margins.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

IndusTower continues to capitalize on growth opportunities in the telecom space, diversifying into FTTH, small cells, smart cities, and active network solutions to tap into 5G demand. Its future growth is anchored in rising tenancy ratios, next-gen infrastructure monetization, and smart city deployments. Long-term financial stability is contingent on VIL dues recovery, while cost control remains central to sustaining profitability.

Name: Pace Digitek Ltd
Overview:
Pace Digitek Ltd, established in 2003 and headquartered in Bengaluru, is a prominent provider of telecom infrastructure and renewable energy solutions. The company specializes in power management systems, optical fiber laying, and energy management services, catering to clients across India, Africa, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, Myanmar, and the Philippines. 

With a workforce exceeding 4,000 professionals, Pace Digitek has serviced over 100,000 products and manages more than 25,000 operation and maintenance (O&M) service sites. The company operates two world-class manufacturing facilities spanning over 250,000 square feet and has a significant presence in the telecom and energy infrastructure sectors.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Pace Digitek is focusing on expanding its battery energy storage systems (BESS) portfolio, aiming to enhance its offerings in the energy storage sector. The company plans to diversify its revenue streams by increasing its renewable energy segment's contribution to 50%, addressing margin volatility concerns in this area.

Name: Railtel Corporation of India Ltd
Overview:
RailTel Corporation of India Ltd, a Navratna PSU, is a key ICT and telecom infrastructure provider in the country, leveraging its pan-India optic fiber network to deliver broadband, VPN, and project-based ICT solutions. The company plays a strategic role in modernizing railway operations and enabling digital connectivity across urban and rural India.

Its portfolio spans telecom services, project execution for railways and state governments, and emerging segments such as data centers and edge computing. Backed by a strong order pipeline and leadership in railway ICT projects, RailTel is positioned to benefit from government digitalization initiatives and railway infrastructure upgrades.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims for ~25% revenue growth in FY26 with stable margins of 11–12%. Growth will be driven by railway projects (especially Kavach), state government ICT orders, and expanding data center capacity. The company is also cautiously exploring international markets while maintaining execution focus on high-margin railway contracts.

Name: Tata Communications Ltd
Overview:
Tata Communications, incorporated in 1986 and part of the Tata Group since 2002, is a leading global digital ecosystem enabler with an extensive infrastructure footprint and a strong presence in emerging markets. The company serves a wide range of multinational corporations and service providers, offering advanced communication, collaboration, cloud, mobility, connected solutions, and data center services.

It partners with hundreds of Fortune 500 companies, delivering end-to-end managed solutions that integrate multiple digital platforms and technologies. Through continuous innovation, strategic customer engagement, and expansion into high-growth digital areas, Tata Communications positions itself to meet the evolving connectivity, AI, and cybersecurity needs of global enterprises.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The company targets ₹28,000 crore data revenues by FY28, with digital services contributing 65% and EBITDA margins returning to 23–25%. Growth is planned to be fully organic, with global markets driving momentum despite competitive pressures in India.

Name: Bharti Airtel Ltd
Overview:
Bharti Airtel Ltd is a leading global telecommunications provider operating in 18 countries, offering a broad suite of services, including mobile, voice, broadband, fixed-line, digital TV, and enterprise connectivity solutions. Airtel is also driving financial inclusion through Airtel Payments Bank and Airtel Money, delivering mobile-based banking and payment services across India and 14 African markets.
 
 Business Segments
 Mobile Services: 2G, 3G, 4G wireless services, expanding into 5G.
 
 Home Services: Fixed-line broadband and digital TV.
 
 Enterprise Solutions: National and international connectivity, integrated telecom solutions, cloud, and data centers.
 
 Airtel Payments Bank / Airtel Money: Mobile-based payments, remittances, and digital banking services.
 
 Revenue Mix 
 
 Mobile Services (India & South Asia): ~65%
 
 Airtel Africa Operations: ~25%
 
 Homes (Broadband, DTH): ~5%
 
 Enterprise / B2B & Payments Bank: ~5%
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook:
Bharti Airtel is well-positioned for 5G-led growth, with continued investments in advanced network technologies, towers, fiber, and data centers to support scalability and future revenue streams. Strategic partnerships, notably with Google and Ericsson, are enhancing its digital, cloud, and enterprise service offerings. The company aims to leverage rising digital adoption, enterprise demand, and value-added services to sustain strong financial performance. With disciplined execution and infrastructure expansion, Bharti Airtel is expected to maintain industry leadership while driving innovation in telecom and digital services.

Name: Alok Industries Ltd
Overview:
Alok Industries is a vertically integrated textile manufacturer with operations spanning the full value chain—from fiber processing and yarn production to fabric manufacturing and finished garments in both cotton and polyester segments. The company retails its products through strategic channels, including its parent’s retail network, and exports to multiple international markets. Its integration and broad geographic reach underpin its positioning in the textile industry.

With strong backing from Reliance Industries and a structured offtake arrangement for polyester products, Alok maintains operational linkage to upstream supply of raw materials and downstream retail distribution. This enhances working capital stability and creates synergies across petrochemical and textile business lines.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook 
With improving quarterly trends, operational efficiencies, and strategic support from Reliance, Alok appears to be on a turnaround path. Management’s focus on cost control, debt reduction, and incremental margins may help resume recovery. Enhanced market conditions and strengthened retail channels could further bolster performance in FY 26.

Name: Arvind Fashions Ltd
Overview:
Arvind Fashions Ltd is a leading player in the branded apparel, footwear, and beauty segments, with a strong portfolio of owned and licensed international labels. Its presence spans across premium, mid-market, and mass categories, allowing it to address diverse consumer needs in India’s fast-growing fashion market.

The company has strategically focused on scaling high-potential brands, investing in omni-channel retail capabilities, and expanding into adjacent categories such as kidswear, innerwear, womenswear, and footwear. Its asset-light retail expansion and disciplined working capital management support both growth and profitability.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects double-digit revenue growth, with medium-term guidance of 12–15% CAGR. EBITDA margins are expected to expand by 50–60 bps despite higher advertising investments. With scale-driven operating leverage, focus on premium brands, and expansion into adjacencies, Arvind Fashions is positioned to sustain profitable growth and strengthen market share.

Name: Indo Count Industries Ltd
Overview:
Indo Count Industries Ltd is one of India's foremost home textiles manufacturers, offering a vast portfolio of bed linen, sheets, pillowcases, and utility bedding. The company serves global markets and has scaled production and distribution across geographies, maintaining a reputation for quality and design.

Pursuing a balanced “volume + value” strategy, Indo Count invests in brand building, acquisitions in the U.S. (Fluvitex and Wamsutta), and the expansion of its utility bedding and D2C platforms, seeking to strengthen its premium and international footprint.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Indo Count’s growth trajectory is underpinned by its expanding branded utility bedding and U.S. distribution network, where new acquisitions are expected to drive earnings from mid-FY26. While margins have felt cyclical pressure, recovery in global demand, revitalized brand momentum, and supply chain efficiencies should support a comeback in profitability through FY26–FY27.

Name: Vardhman Textiles Ltd
Overview:
Vardhman Textiles is a leading integrated textile company engaged in manufacturing yarn, fabric, acrylic fiber, and garments, with a significant presence in India and over 75 countries globally. The group ranks among the top three woven fabric manufacturers in India and caters to a broad retail and brand customer base, especially in the US, Europe, and Asia.

The company’s product portfolio includes specialty and dyed yarns, fancy and hand-knitting yarns, and a wide range of fabrics for apparel, with a focus on expanding capacity and product value-addition. Vardhman emphasizes innovation, sustainability initiatives, and strategic geographic diversification to navigate global market volatility and rising raw material costs.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management aims to navigate ongoing raw material inflation and trade uncertainties with cautious optimism. Growth is expected to be driven by capacity expansions in fabric and specialty yarns, coupled with sustainability initiatives that will reduce power costs in the near term.

Name: K P R Mill Ltd
Overview:
K.P.R. Mill Ltd is among India’s largest vertically integrated apparel manufacturing companies, with operations spanning yarn production, knitted fabric, readymade garments, sugar, ethanol, and renewable energy generation. The company serves both domestic and international markets, exporting to over 60 countries, and has a diversified product mix catering to multiple industries.

Its operations are supported by large-scale manufacturing capacities, advanced processing infrastructure, and a diversified customer base that includes leading global apparel brands. Beyond textiles, K.P.R. Mill has also established a presence in the automotive retail space and clean energy generation, making it a multi-sector enterprise with multiple revenue streams.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
K.P.R. Mill aims to leverage capacity expansions in garments and solar power to enhance operational scale and margins. The ethanol business, with its upgraded 250 KLPD capacity, is expected to benefit from strong demand driven by the government’s ethanol blending program. With a diversified business model and a secured order pipeline, the company is positioned for steady volume growth and improved operational efficiency in the coming years.

Name: Welspun Living Ltd
Overview:
Welspun Living Limited (WLL), part of the $3.6 billion Welspun Group, is a global leader in home textiles with a strong presence in over 60 countries. It operates fully integrated manufacturing facilities in India and partners with top global retailers. The company’s product portfolio spans home textiles, advanced textiles, and flooring solutions, supported by a strategic focus on branding, innovation, and sustainability.

In FY25, WLL continued to strengthen its market leadership, ranking No.1 globally in towels and bath rugs and among the top two in bed sheets. It has been expanding its emerging businesses—flooring and advanced textiles—while increasing domestic market penetration. Sustainability remains a core focus, with significant investments in renewable energy and sustainable cotton farming.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Near-term caution due to tariff headwinds and muted export demand, but domestic business showing resilience. Focus on growing emerging segments, enhancing branded and retail presence, and executing capacity expansions. Target to reach ₹15,000 Cr revenue and raise non-core business share to 45% by FY27, supported by sustainability-driven competitiveness.

Name: Sheela Foam Ltd
Overview:
Sheela Foam Limited is a prominent leader in India’s mattress and polyurethane (PU) foam industry, holding significant market shares across India and Australia. With a diverse product range including mattresses under Sleepwell, Furlenco, and Kurlon brands, the company has a strong presence in both urban and rural markets. Sheela Foam also caters to technical foam needs in automotive and other specialized sectors, maintaining a nationwide manufacturing footprint.

The company has effectively integrated the Kurlon acquisition, achieving operational synergies and expanding its distribution network substantially. With a focus on innovation, brand strengthening, and cost optimization, Sheela Foam is well positioned to leverage growth opportunities in the evolving mattress market and related segments, both domestically and internationally.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management targets 15% topline growth over the next three years, driven by expanding distribution, new product launches, and increased penetration in rural markets. EBITDA margin is expected to improve to 14-15% as operating leverage kicks in. Fixed costs are expected to rise moderately, with synergy benefits partially reinvested for growth. No major capacity expansion is anticipated before FY30, supporting a lean investment profile.

Name: Garware Technical Fibres Ltd
Overview:
Garware-Wall Ropes is a prominent technical textiles company renowned for its specialized solutions across industries such as cordage, infrastructure, aquaculture, and agriculture. Its product range includes high-performance polymer ropes, fishing and sports nets, aquaculture cages, coated fabrics, agricultural netting, and geosynthetics. It serves both domestic and global clients, anchoring its reputation on innovation and quality.

The company’s operations span manufacturing and distribution across multiple geographies, with particular strength in geosynthetics and international fisheries. Garware's strategic expansion efforts include increasing its global footprint through new subsidiaries and tapping emerging markets, reflecting agile execution and forward-looking management.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
With robust performance in geosynthetics and international fisheries, coupled with operational expansion into new global regions, Garware is well-positioned for growth. Continued execution in its high-growth segments, mitigation of geopolitical risks, and strengthening of global infrastructure are expected to support sustained performance going forward.

Name: Trident Ltd
Overview:
Trident Ltd, incorporated in 1990, is a diversified manufacturer and exporter engaged in producing yarn, terry towels, bed sheets, paper, and chemicals. The company has established itself as a leading integrated home textile manufacturer and is the world’s largest wheat-straw-based paper producer. With a robust infrastructure and global presence, Trident caters to both domestic and international markets, supplying to top-tier clients like Amazon, Walmart, and IKEA.

The business operates across three core segments—Home Textiles, Yarn, and Paper & Chemicals. It derives 55% of its revenue from Home Textiles, 29% from Yarn, and 16% from the Paper & Chemicals segment. With manufacturing facilities located in Punjab and Madhya Pradesh, Trident has built a strong energy-efficient ecosystem, fulfilling over half its power needs via captive generation. The company continues to strengthen its retail and digital presence, while executing major capacity expansions and ESG initiatives.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Trident aims for 3X revenue growth by FY27, supported by a ₹1,000 Cr capex plan focused on sustainability, luxury segment expansion (LUXEHOME), and process digitization. The company is also investing ₹3,000 Cr in Madhya Pradesh to scale textile capacity and create 3,000 jobs. Despite a sequential revenue decline in Q1 FY26, strong margin expansion and robust demand from global markets set a positive trajectory for the remainder of FY26 and beyond.

Name: Nahar Industrial Enterprises Ltd
Overview:
Nahar Industrial Enterprises Ltd operates across textiles, sugar, and yarn production. Its textile segment remains the primary contributor, serving both domestic and international clients with a diverse product portfolio. The company is also engaged in agro-based activities.

With its integrated operations, Nahar Industrial has developed a strong reputation in yarn and fabric manufacturing. The firm emphasizes exports and continues to strengthen its presence in premium textile categories.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
If Nahar sustains its revenue growth and manages costs effectively through the year, it may steadily return to stronger profitability. Maintaining or improving on current quarterly income levels and profit margins into the coming quarters could help the company deliver meaningful FY26 results and steadily regain investor confidence.

Name: Shree Ram Twistex Ltd
Overview:
Shree Ram Twistex is a textile manufacturing company engaged in the production of cotton yarns used across the fabric and garment industry. The company manufactures a variety of yarn types such as compact ring spun yarn, combed yarn, and carded yarn, which are essential raw materials for textile production. These yarns are widely used in both knitting and weaving applications, catering to different quality and strength requirements in fabric manufacturing.

The company operates spinning facilities equipped with modern machinery to ensure consistent quality and efficient production. It supplies its yarns to textile mills, fabric manufacturers, and exporters, serving both domestic and international markets. By focusing on product quality, process efficiency, and a diversified yarn portfolio, the company supports a wide range of end-use applications in the textile value chain.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

The company plans to expand its production capacity and upgrade its spinning technology to meet the rising demand for quality cotton yarns. It aims to focus on higher-value products such as compact and combed yarns, which offer better margins and growing demand in premium textiles. The company is also looking to strengthen its presence in export markets by building long-term relationships with global textile players.

Name: Banswara Syntex Ltd
Overview:
Banswara Syntex Ltd, incorporated in 1976, is a vertically integrated textile company specializing in the production of yarns, fabrics, and garments. The company operates in both domestic and export markets, rooted in a manufacturing legacy that spans synthetic yarns and woven fabric solutions.

In addition to its textile operations, Banswara maintains captive power generation facilities that support its energy needs and enhance operational efficiency. This integration helps stabilize production and contributes to the company’s overall business resilience.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The positive momentum in the garment segment—especially a strong 42% growth—combined with operational efficiency enhancements from captive power and process modernization, may help drive recovery. If Banswara maintains these trends across the year, it could close FY26 with improved revenue and narrowed losses, potentially moving back toward profitability.

Name: Voith Paper Fabrics India Ltd
Overview: Voith Paper India Ltd, a subsidiary of the German Voith Group, is a leading provider of paper manufacturing equipment, services, and automation solutions in India. The company designs, installs, and maintains complete paper production systems, including machinery for pulp preparation, paper forming, and finishing processes. Voith Paper India leverages global technology and engineering expertise to offer energy-efficient and high-performance solutions tailored to the Indian paper industry. It serves clients across large-scale paper mills, specialty paper manufacturers, and integrated pulp and paper units. With a focus on sustainability, Voith Paper India promotes eco-friendly solutions, water and energy conservation, and process optimization in line with global best practices.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Voith Paper Fabrics India Ltd (VPFV) is strategically enhancing its position in the paper machine clothing sector. The company is expanding its product range to include forming fabrics, positioning itself as the sole Indian manufacturer capable of supplying the entire spectrum of machine clothing to paper manufacturers. This expansion aims to cater to both domestic and international markets, strengthening its global presence.

Name: Axita Cotton Ltd
Overview:
Axita Cotton Ltd is engaged in the business of processing and trading raw cotton, cotton seeds, and cotton bales, with a strong presence in both domestic and export markets. The company sources raw cotton directly from farmers and ginners, processes it into bales, and markets it to textile mills and global buyers. With India being one of the largest cotton producers globally, Axita has built a reliable supply chain that ensures consistent quality.

Over the years, the company has established strong export relationships, supplying cotton to markets such as China, Bangladesh, Vietnam, and Turkey. Its focus on operational efficiency, farmer linkages, and quality standards enables it to compete effectively in the international cotton trade.
Key Takeways:
Growth or Future Outlook
Axita Cotton aims to strengthen its global footprint and diversify into value-added cotton products. With rising global textile demand, the company’s focus on exports and efficient procurement is expected to drive future growth.

Name: Shankar Lal Rampal Dye-Chem Ltd
Overview:
Shankar Lal Rampal Dye-Chem LtdShankar Lal Rampal Dye-Chem Ltd manufactures and trades in chemicals, dyestuffs, and textile auxiliaries. Its products cater to textile processing, leather, and other allied industries.

The company’s strength lies in its distribution reach and ability to provide tailored chemical solutions across industries.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
With the revival of textile exports and robust domestic consumption trends, demand for dye-chem products is expected to remain strong in FY26. The company is positioned to leverage this opportunity by widening its product offerings and enhancing relationships with large textile manufacturers, ensuring consistent volume growth.

Name: MRF Ltd
Overview:
Madras Rubber Factory Limited (MRF) is India’s leading tyre manufacturer, holding a 29% market share in the Indian tyre industry. It offers a wide range of products including tyres for all vehicle categories, as well as tubes, flaps, paints, sports goods, and toys under the Funskool brand. With 91% of its revenue generated domestically and 9% from exports, MRF is heavily focused on the Indian market. The company is known for product quality, brand strength, and ongoing innovation—especially in launching tyres tailored for luxury vehicles, off-road use, and electric vehicles (EVs).

Business Segments and Estimated Revenue Contribution:
Based on the product range and information provided:

Tyres 
Includes tyres for passenger cars, two-wheelers, trucks, tractors, and EVs. This is the primary revenue driver.

Tubes & Flaps 
Ancillary products used alongside tyres.

Other Products
Includes paints, coats, sports goods (including Funskool toys), and pre-treads.

Geographic Revenue Contribution:
Domestic
Exports
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

MRF is focusing on expanding its presence in the electric vehicle (EV) tire segment, aiming to capture a larger share of the growing EV market in India, which is expected to reach a valuation of USD 206 billion by 2030. The company is investing in capacity expansion and technology upgrades to sustain long-term competitiveness. MRF is also enhancing its product development strategy, aiming to develop new products to ensure continuous growth and development.

Name: JK Tyre & Industries Ltd
Overview:
JK Tyre & Industries is one of India’s leading tyre manufacturers and among the top 25 global players, with a diverse product portfolio catering to commercial vehicles, passenger cars, multi-utility vehicles, and tractors. The company is recognized for innovation with products such as Smart Tyres equipped with TPMS and eco-friendly “UX Green” tyres made from sustainable materials.

JK Tyre has a strong presence in domestic replacement and OEM markets and operates internationally through its JK Tornel subsidiary in Mexico.  Its extensive distribution and service network supports market penetration, and it is poised to capitalize on emerging opportunities in electric vehicle tyres and growing automotive demand in India and abroad.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management expects stable raw material costs with no sharp increases and continued focus on margin improvement. With disciplined capex and strong liquidity, JK Tyre aims to maintain operational momentum, expand premium products, and strengthen its market leadership through FY26 and beyond.

Name: TVS Srichakra Ltd
Overview:
TVS Srichakra Ltd is a prominent Indian manufacturer specializing in two- and three-wheeler tires, as well as tires for industrial and agricultural vehicles. With a significant market presence in India, the company also exports its products to over 70 countries globally. Recognized for its innovation and quality, TVS Srichakra Ltd operates within the broader automotive sector, which includes the production and sale of vehicle components.

The company operates two main business segments: Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) and Replacement Market. TVS Srichakra Ltd has been expanding its client base and product offerings, aiming to strengthen its position as a leader in the mobility and automotive sectors.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
TVS Srichakra Ltd plans to continue expanding its footprint both domestically and internationally. The company aims to enhance its operational efficiencies and product offerings to meet the growing demand for automotive tires. With a focus on quality and customer satisfaction, TVS Srichakra Ltd is well-positioned to capitalize on increasing demand in the OEM and replacement tire markets.

Name: Goodyear India Ltd
Overview:
Goodyear India is a leading manufacturer and trader of tyres, tubes, and flaps, serving key segments like automotive and agricultural machinery. As a prominent player in the farm tyre market, the company plays a vital role in supplying reliable mobility solutions across rural and urban markets.

With manufacturing facilities located in Ballabgarh and Aurangabad, Goodyear India supports diverse customer segments—from tractor OEMs to replacement tyre markets—ensuring widespread availability and technical support across regions.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
The parent company is currently reviewing strategic alternatives for Goodyear India's farm tyre business, exploring a potential sale valued up to ₹2,500–2,700 crore. Despite near-term challenges, the replacement tyre segment—especially in premium categories like SUV and EV—offers growth potential as the company aims to reposition its brand and expand margins

Name: Tolins Tyres Ltd
Overview: Tolins Tyres Ltd, founded in 1982 as a small-scale industry unit, has emerged as a leading producer in the tire industry, specializing in a diverse range of products. Its portfolio includes two and three-wheeler tires, light commercial vehicle (LCV) tires, and agriculture tires. Additionally, Tolins Tyres takes pride in its innovative Precured Tread Rubber and other retreading products, showcasing its commitment to cutting-edge technology.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook
Tolins Tyres is set to accelerate its presence in both domestic and international markets by scaling up production capabilities and rolling out the latest additions to its agricultural tyre lineup—such as the recently introduced heavy-duty tractor rear tyres targeting a complete portfolio for farming needs. It is also focused on boosting operating efficiency by ramping up capacity utilization from its expanded manufacturing base, unlocking greater economies of scale

Name: Apollo Tyres Ltd
Overview:
Apollo Tyres, established in 1972, is one of India’s largest tyre manufacturers with a strong domestic and international footprint. The company produces a wide range of tyres for trucks, buses, passenger vehicles, and off-highway applications, with significant market shares in the TBR and PCR segments. Its manufacturing operations span across India and Europe, supported by a robust R&D infrastructure and premium brand portfolio.

The company operates under two key brands: Apollo, which serves the full spectrum of commercial and passenger segments, and Vredestein, a premium European brand. It serves both OEMs and the replacement market, with the latter contributing the majority of its revenue. With facilities in India and Europe, Apollo focuses on premiumization, exports, and capacity expansion to drive future growth.
Key Takeways:
Growth Outlook

Apollo Tyres is strategically enhancing its manufacturing capabilities and expanding its global presence to drive future growth. The company is investing ₹1,500 crore in the fiscal year 2025–26 to expand its passenger car radial (PCR) tyre capacity through a brownfield expansion and to upgrade existing facilities. Additionally, Apollo Tyres is focusing on the U.S. market, aiming to increase sales from $120–130 million to $500 million by strengthening its distribution network and brand presence

Name: Balkrishna Industries Ltd
Overview:
Balkrishna Industries Limited (BKT) is a global leader in the off-highway tyre segment, catering to niche applications such as agriculture, construction, industrial, earthmoving, mining, port, ATV, and turf care. The company offers a diversified portfolio of over 3,200 SKUs in both cross-ply and radial construction, with a strong brand presence across more than 160 countries.

Its operations are strategically integrated, with manufacturing facilities in India and a significant export footprint. BKT’s global market share in the specialty tyre segment is estimated at 5–6%, supported by continuous capacity expansion, innovation-driven product development, and a strong dealer network. The company remains focused on both international markets and the rapidly growing domestic opportunity.
Key Takeways:
Future Outlook
Management has maintained its EBITDA margin guidance of 24–25% despite near-term headwinds from tariffs and a higher India mix. Volumes for FY26 remain uncertain due to macroeconomic and geopolitical factors, but medium-term prospects are supported by the ramp-up of new capacity, entry into TBR and PCR segments, and growth in carbon black and rubber tracks. The company aims for a 5% PCR market share by 2030 while retaining its leadership in the off-highway segment.

Name: CEAT Ltd
Overview:
CEAT Ltd, established in 1958 and part of the RPG Group since 1982, is among India’s largest and fastest-growing tyre manufacturers, producing over 2000 SKUs across segments. The company caters to commercial vehicles, passenger cars, two- and three-wheelers, off-highway vehicles, and light commercial vehicles, with a presence in both OEM and replacement markets. CEAT has a strong domestic and global presence, exporting to over 110 countries, and is recognized for its innovation and sustainable practices.

The company operates six large manufacturing facilities and three smart facilities across India, with an annual installed capacity of 3.5 crore tyres. CEAT continues to expand production capacity, make strategic acquisitions, and strengthen its brand through sports partnerships, new product launches, and increased market penetration in both domestic and international markets.
Key Takeways:
Growth & Future Outlook
CEAT’s focus for the next few years is on capacity expansion, global market penetration, and product diversification, especially in the EV tyre category and off-highway segments. The Michelin CAMSO acquisition and Indonesian entry are expected to significantly enhance global market share and product portfolio breadth.
